title: "Glenedyth" Christmas Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 lbs. breadcrumbs
- 1/2 lb. flour
- 2 lbs. chopped suet
- 2 lbs. raisins, chopped
- 2 lbs. currants
- 2 lbs. sugar
- 18 eggs
- 1 claret glass brandy
- 1 wine glass maraschino
- 2 oz. almonds, blanched and sliced
- 2 oz. candied peel
- 2 oz. citron
- 2 oz. preserved ginger finely chopped
- 2 small nutmegs
- 2 limes, juice and finely chopped rind
- 1 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Mix all to a stiff paste, moistening with a little milk if necessary, but be careful, for milk will make the pudding heavy.
2. The eggs and milk should be stirred into the ingredients after they have been thoroughly mixed together, and last of all the brandy and liqueur.
3. This pudding will take ten hours to boil, and is large enough for a party of sixteen.
4. For sauce: Put ten yolks of eggs in a stew-pan, four ounces of sugar, one pint of milk.
5. Stir over the fire in a "bain marie" (double boiler) till a rich custard has been formed; add a claret glass of Noyeau; strain the sauce and serve hot.
6. Instead of Noyeau three-quarters of a pint of Madeira may be used.

title: "My Devil"
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of Worcestershire sauce
- 1 small dessertspoonful anchovy sauce
- 3 teaspoonfuls made mustard
- 1 saltspoonful of salt
- 1 saltspoonful pepper
- 0.5 saltspoonful cayenne
- 1 tablespoonful of vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful brown sugar
- one squeeze of lemon
- one glass port wine

## Directions

1. Mix together and heat in saucepan, and pour over grilled turkey legs, or any cold meat grilled for breakfast or lunch.

title: A Bread And Milk Poultice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- a tablespoonful of the crumbs of stale bread
- a gill of milk
- glycerine or sweet oil

## Directions

1. Put a tablespoonful of the crumbs of stale bread into a gill of milk, and give the whole one boil up
2. Or, take stale bread-crumbs, pour over them boiling water and boil till soft, stirring well
3. Take from the fire and gradually stir in a little glycerine or sweet oil, so as to render the poultice pliable when applied

title: A Brine to Preserve Butter
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- muslin cloth
- salt
- egg
- white sugar
- saltpetre

## Directions

1. Work your butter into small rolls, wrapping each one carefully in a clean muslin cloth, tying them up with a string
2. Make a brine, say three gallons, having it strong enough of salt to bear up an egg
3. Add half a teacupful of pure, white sugar, and one tablespoonful of saltpetre
4. Boil the brine, and when cold strain it carefully
5. Pour it over the rolls so as to more than cover them, as this excludes the air
6. Place a weight over all to keep the rolls under the surface

title: A Dainty Dessert
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of sponge cake or ladies' fingers
- sliced bananas
- a quart of blackberries
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Line a glass bowl with slices of sponge cake or ladies' fingers
2. fill the bowl with sliced bananas
3. squeeze the juice and pulp from a quart of blackberries, sweeten it well
4. pour it over the bananas
5. stand on ice until ice-cold
6. cover with a deep layer of whipped cream and serve

title: A Favorite Dish
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- 3 pounds beef or lean mutton

## Directions

1. Cut potatoes in halves and boil sufficient amount
2. Drain potatoes
3. Put into a baking tin
4. Add a little butter
5. Place beef or lean mutton in centre of potatoes
6. Bake 1 hour
7. Turn potatoes over occasionally until they are a golden brown

title: A Favorite Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts cabbage
- equal parts cold boiled beets
- equal parts celery
- 1 cup vinegar per quart
- ¾ cup sugar per quart
- ⅝ cup grated horseradish per quart
- spice to suit taste

## Directions

1. Chop fine equal parts of cabbage, cold boiled beets and any pieces of celery
2. Boil until cabbage is tender
3. Add for every quart 1 cup vinegar, ¾ cup sugar, ⅝ cup grated horseradish, spice to suit taste
4. Let boil a few minutes longer
5. Use as cold pickle when wanted

title: A Ginger Poultice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- ground ginger
- vinegar

## Directions

1. This is made like a mustard poultice, using ground ginger instead of mustard
2. A little vinegar is sometimes added

title: A Good Sponge Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- Weight of 10 in sugar
- Weight of 6 in flour
- Flavor with lemon extract

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks stiff, then add the sugar
2. beat with the yolks until very light
3. now essence
4. now gently stir in whites which have been beaten stiff, and last put in flour
5. Stir it in, do not beat it in
6. have buttered pans ready
7. Bake in moderate oven

title: A Hop Poultice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful of dried hops
- half a pint of water
- Indian meal

## Directions

1. Boil one handful of dried hops in half a pint of water, until the half pint is reduced to a gill
2. Stir into it enough Indian meal to thicken it

title: A Jonathon
body:
## Ingredients

- lard or butter
- apples
- 1 cup cream
- 1 egg
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Grease a deep pie plate with lard or preferably butter
2. cut apples in small pieces, not thin slices, and spread over the bottom of plate
3. make a batter of following: 1 cup cream, 1 egg, 1 cup flour, 1 teaspoon baking powder, a pinch of salt
4. pour this over the apple and bake in a moderate oven till nicely browned
5. Serve with a dressing after the Jonathon has been turned upside down on a plate

title: A Mustard Poultice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one gill of boiling water
- one tablespoonful of Indian meal
- one teaspoonful of mustard flour
- muslin

## Directions

1. Into one gill of boiling water stir one tablespoonful of Indian meal
2. Spread the paste thus made upon a cloth, and spread over the paste one teaspoonful of mustard flour
3. If you wish a mild poultice, use a teaspoonful of mustard as it is prepared for the table, instead of the mustard flour
4. Equal parts of ground mustard and flour made into a paste with warm water, and spread between two pieces of muslin, form the indispensable mustard plaster

title: A Nice Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 3 cups flour
- lemon

## Directions

1. Flavor with lemon and bake

title: A Nice Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1½ cups sugar
- ½ cup butter
- ¾ cup milk
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar
- ½ teaspoon soda
- 3 cups flour
- lemon flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Flavor with lemon

title: A Nice Dish Of Salmon
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cans red salmon
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 1 large spoonful flour
- pepper
- salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3 or 4 cold boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Drop unopened cans of salmon in boiling water and boil 20 minutes
2. Put a pint of sweet milk in a double boiler
3. Mix a large spoonful of flour in a little cold milk and season with pepper and salt
4. When the milk boils add a tablespoon of butter, then the flour
5. Cook till thick and smooth
6. Peel cold boiled eggs and cut in thin slices
7. Turn out the fish on a large dish and spread out
8. Lay the sliced eggs over the fish
9. Put the sauce over all

title: A Nice Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups bread crumbs
- 2 cups suet
- 1 cup sugar
- 4 eggs
- sufficient milk to make all nice and soft
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon allspice
- 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg

## Directions

1. mix bread crumbs, suet, sugar, eggs, and sufficient milk to make all nice and soft
2. season with cinnamon, allspice, and nutmeg
3. Bake in a moderate oven for 1 hour or more

title: A Nice Supper Dish
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs or more
- pepper
- salt
- chopped onion
- sage
- teacup milk
- good tablespoon of butter
- can of tomatoes

## Directions

1. Break eggs into a bowl, beat lightly and season with pepper, salt, chopped onion, and sage.
2. Add teacup milk.
3. Turn into a hot frying-pan in which you have heated a good tablespoon of butter.
4. Proceed as for scrambled eggs, only do not stir too much, as the larger the pieces of egg are, the nicer the dish will look.
5. Take a can of tomatoes and let it heat in a granite pan while your eggs are cooking, and season well with pepper and salt.
6. Turn the eggs out on a hot platter and pour the tomatoes over them.
7. Serve hot.

title: A Nice Way to Cook Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Prepare chicken as for stewing
2. Roll each piece in flour
3. Place in a small dripping pan
4. Sprinkle with pepper and salt and some butter
5. Fill the pan half full of sweet milk
6. Cook in the oven for 1 hour
7. The milk makes a nice gravy

title: A Nice Way to Roast Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- young chicken
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a young chicken and prepare as for cooking in the kettle and roll each piece in flour till your pan is full, or till you have plenty
2. Sprinkle with pepper and salt and a liberal sprinkling of butter
3. Set in the oven and roast till done, which takes about 1 hour

title: A Peanut Brittle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup unshelled peanuts

## Directions

1. Shell peanuts and remove brown skin
2. Roll nuts until broken fine
3. Put sugar in frying-pan and heat, stirring constantly until melted
4. Add rolled nuts, mix thoroughly and turn at once on an unbuttered pan inverted
5. Roll to desired thickness and mark in squares before it hardens

title: A Quick Relish of Smoked Salmon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of smoked salmon cut into narrow strips
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- Juice of half a lemon
- Cayenne pepper
- Dry toast

## Directions

1. Parboil the salmon ten minutes
2. Lay in cold water for the same length of time
3. Wipe dry
4. Broil over a clear fire
5. Butter while hot
6. Season with cayenne and lemon-juice
7. Pile in a log-cabin square upon a hot plate
8. Send up with dry toast

title: A Quickly Prepared Supper Dish
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound of cheese
- a small piece of butter
- 1 tablespoon milk
- pepper
- 2 slices of bread
- 2 eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Melt in the oven 3/4 pound of cheese, a small piece of butter, with 1 tablespoon milk, and pepper to taste.
2. While the cheese is melting, toast 2 slices of bread and poach two eggs.
3. Butter the toast.
4. When in a thick cream, pour the melted cheese over it and place an egg on each slice.

title: A Quickly Prepared Supper Dish
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound of cheese
- a small piece of butter
- 1 tablespoon milk
- pepper to taste
- 2 slices of bread
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Melt in the oven 3/4 pound of cheese, a small piece of butter, with 1 tablespoon milk, and pepper to taste
2. While the cheese is melting, toast 2 slices of bread and poach two eggs
3. Butter the toast
4. When cheese is in a thick cream, pour the melted cheese over it and place an egg on each slice
5. Serve very hot

title: A Spice Cake Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups light brown sugar
- ½ cup dark brown molasses
- 2 cups sour milk
- ½ cup butter
- 4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon allspice
- ½ cup stoned raisins

## Directions

1. Mix well
2. Oven the cake quickly
3. Any cake made with soda should be promptly baked
4. Makes 2 good-sized cakes if wished

title: A Stramonium Poultice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoonful of Indian meal
- a gill of boiling water
- one tablespoonful of bruised stramonium seeds

## Directions

1. Stir one tablespoonful of Indian meal into a gill of boiling water, and add one tablespoonful of bruised stramonium seeds

title: A Tasty Dish of Liver
body:
## Ingredients

- liver of any kind
- onions
- sage
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- water
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Cut liver in thin slices, dip in flour, place in shallow baking dish.
2. Slice onions on top, add sage, pepper and salt, and 3/4 pint water.
3. Bake in moderate oven 1 hour.
4. Tomatoes sliced improves this dish.

title: A Tasty Way of Doing Liver
body:
## Ingredients

- beef dripping
- onion, large or several small ones
- liver
- boiling water
- brown gravy
- pepper
- salt
- cornstarch

## Directions

1. Place in a stewpan enough beef dripping to fry either a large onion or several small ones.
2. Cut your liver in nice squares, not too thin, brown them nicely in the stewpan.
3. Add a little boiling water and a little brown gravy if you have it.
4. Stew till tender, and season to taste.
5. Before dishing, thicken the gravy with cornstarch.

title: A Tasty Way of Doing Liver
body:
## Ingredients

- beef dripping
- onion
- liver
- boiling water
- brown gravy
- cornstarch

## Directions

1. Place in a stewpan enough beef dripping to fry either a large onion or several small ones
2. Cut your liver in nice squares, not too thin
3. Brown them nicely in the stewpan
4. Add a little boiling water and a little brown gravy if you have it
5. Stew till tender and season to taste
6. Before dishing, thicken the gravy with cornstarch

title: A Tomato Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 6 peppers
- 6 large onions
- 4 cups celery
- 1 cup horseradish
- 1 quart vinegar
- 3/4 cup salt
- 2 ounces celery seed
- 2 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Chop peppers and onions fine
2. Let drain for several hours
3. Mix with celery, horseradish, vinegar, salt, celery seed, and sugar
4. This is not cooked
5. Bottle tight

title: A Tomato Relish 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 ripe tomatoes
- 12 sour apples
- 10 onions
- 6 peppers
- 5 tablespoons salt
- 1 pint vinegar
- sugar and cinnamon to taste

## Directions

1. Scald and skin tomatoes
2. Pare and core apples
3. Peel onions
4. Remove seeds of peppers
5. Chop all fine, mix thoroughly and stew slowly 1.5 hours

title: A Very Good Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup molasses
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 1/2 to 2 cups flour
- 1/2 cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients

title: Ada Ford's Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup melted butter
- 1 1/2 teacupfuls flour
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 teaspoonful baking powder
- 1/2 cup milk (large)
- 1 cup jam
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Butter a mould, cover the bottom with jam, fill with batter, and steam one hour and a quarter.
2. Serve with sauce.

title: Adirondack Cornbread
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs beaten separately
- 1 pt. milk
- ½ pt. cornmeal
- 2 oz. butter, melted
- ½ pt. flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful sugar

## Directions

1. Eggs beaten separately.
2. Add to milk, flour and cornmeal well mixed, the beaten yolks and melted butter; let it stand ten minutes.
3. Then add whites, salt and sugar, baking powder.
4. Mix thoroughly and bake in long biscuit tins.

title: Agra Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1.5 pts. milk
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- Bread-and-butter marmalade
- 1 pt. boiled custard
- Vanilla to taste
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of bread-and-butter
2. Spread them with the marmalade
3. Fit them neatly into a buttered pie-dish until it is half full
4. Pour over them gradually a hot custard made of the milk heated almost to boiling, then taken off the fire, and the beaten eggs and sugar stirred in with the flavoring
5. Place a small plate on the top to prevent the bread from rising
6. Let it soak for half an hour
7. Grate a little nutmeg on the top
8. Bake
9. When done turn it out of the dish
10. Pour over it a pint of boiled custard

title: Aikin's Honey-Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup extracted honey
- 1 pint sour cream
- scant teaspoon soda
- flavoring if desired
- flour to make a soft dough

## Directions

1. Mix honey, sour cream and soda
2. Add flavoring if desired
3. Mix with flour to make a soft dough

title: Alabama Marble Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups white sugar
- 3 cups flour
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 heaping tablespoons grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Mix cake batter
2. Take out 1 soup plateful of batter and stir into it 2 heaping tablespoons grated chocolate
3. Fill your cake mould about 2 inches deep with first batter
4. Drop in 3 or 4 places, a spoonful at a time, of the chocolate, and so on until all is used

title: Alexandra Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 crusts
- 1 egg
- 1 small cup sugar
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- a little nutmeg
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Stir all well together

title: Alexandra Roll
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Cold game or chicken, pounded
- Fresh butter, about a third of its bulk of the chicken
- A few capers
- A washed and boned anchovy for every ounce or so of chicken
- Cayenne pepper
- Thin brown bread
- Finely minced parsley
- Lobster coral or coralline pepper

## Directions

1. Butter sparingly some thin brown bread.
2. Have ready some cold game or chicken pounded, about a third of its bulk of fresh butter, a few capers, a washed and boned anchovy for every ounce or so of chicken, and a good seasoning of cayenne pepper.
3. Spread the bread and butter with this mixture, roll up cigar fashion.
4. Butter the top very lightly and roll half of these (cigars) in finely minced parsley and the other half in lobster coral or coralline pepper.

title: Almond Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs, whites beaten
- 1 pound icing sugar
- 1/2 pound almonds, chopped

## Directions

1. Beat whites of 2 eggs
2. Add 1 pound icing sugar and beat together
3. Reserve 1/6 cup mixture to ice the cake
4. Chop 1/2 pound almonds and add to the mixture
5. Cut in narrow strips and brown slightly
6. Bake on inverted tins in a slow oven

title: Almond Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups butter
- 8 eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 teaspoonfuls ratafia
- 4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls vanilla
- 1 cup chopped almonds
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking-powder
- 2 cups raisins

## Directions

1. Add the flour and baking powder mixed last of all

title: Almond Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound sugar
- 6 eggs
- 6 small cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 pound citron
- 1 pound almonds
- small cup sweet cream

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pound butter, 1 pound sugar, 6 eggs, 6 small cups flour, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 1/2 pound citron, 1 pound almonds, and small cup sweet cream together and bake

title: Almond Custard Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- delicate sponge cake
- blanched almonds
- wine
- rich vanilla custard

## Directions

1. Make a delicate sponge cake and fill it with blanched almonds
2. pour over it a little wine and then a rich vanilla custard

title: Almond Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound almonds blanched
- yolk of 1 egg
- whites of 3 eggs beaten stiff
- 3 cups icing sugar

## Directions

1. Roll the nuts to a fine powder
2. Add the yolk of 1 egg and stir well
3. Add the whites of 3 eggs beaten stiff
4. Add 3 cups icing sugar

title: Almond Icing for Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup blanched almonds ground very fine
- 2 tablespoons milk
- 1 egg
- butter size of an egg
- rose water
- white icing

## Directions

1. Beat sugar, butter, egg and milk to a cream
2. Stir in the nuts
3. Boil 8 minutes in a double boiler
4. Flavor with rose water
5. Spread on cake
6. After it sets, spread white icing over

title: Almond Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ounces sweet almonds blanched and pounded fine
- whites of 2 eggs
- 12 ounces powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch and pound fine 6 ounces sweet almonds
2. Mix in a large bowl the whites of 2 eggs and 12 ounces powdered sugar
3. Beat with a wooden spoon for 5 minutes
4. Drop through pastry tube in pieces about the size of a quarter on sheets of buttered paper, in baking pans
5. Bake in a slow oven until cakes are golden brown

title: Almond Paste Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound ground almonds
- 1 pound fine sifted white sugar
- whites of 2 eggs
- few drops essence almonds

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and ground almonds well together
2. Add little by little whites of eggs beaten to a froth, until the mixture is a thick paste
3. Add essence of almonds
4. Mix all thoroughly

title: Ambrosia
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pineapple chopped quite fine
- one-half box strawberries
- six bananas sliced and the slices quartered
- six oranges sliced and the slices quartered
- one lemon cut fine
- sugar
- one wineglassful sherry

## Directions

1. Chop pineapple quite fine
2. Slice bananas and quarter the slices
3. Slice oranges and quarter the slices
4. Cut lemon fine
5. sweeten to taste
6. Add one wineglassful sherry and set away until cold

title: American Fritters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of undercooked roast beef or mutton
- one-half pound flour
- one large or two small eggs
- salt
- milli
- lard or dripping for frying
- fried apples for garnish

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into moderately thick slices, and as neat a shape as possible, pepper and salt each piece
2. then make a batter in the above proportions, taking care that it is not very thin
3. Have ready a pan of boiling lard or dripping, dip each piece of meat into the batter, and fry quickly to a light brown
4. Serve on a hot dish, and garnish with a thick border of fried apples

title: American Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart sliced cucumbers (not too large)
- 1 large onion
- 1 green pepper
- salt
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon turmeric
- 1 tablespoon grated horseradish
- 1 tablespoon mustard seed
- 1 teaspoon celery seed
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice cucumbers
2. Chop onion and green pepper
3. Sprinkle with salt and let stand 3 hours
4. Rinse in cold water
5. Add brown sugar, turmeric, grated horseradish, mustard seed and celery seed
6. Cover with vinegar
7. Bring to a scald

title: American Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- one cup of sweet milk
- a little salt
- Light bread
- Butter

## Directions

1. Beat one egg thoroughly
2. Put one cup of sweet milk to the beaten egg, and a little salt
3. Slice light bread and dip into the mixture, allowing each slice to absorb some of the milk
4. Brown on a hot, buttered griddle or thick-bottom frying-pan
5. Spread with butter, and serve hot

title: Ammonia Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint granulated sugar
- 1 pint good sweet cream
- 1 ounce baking ammonia
- ¾ teaspoon salt
- lemon essence or vanilla
- flour enough to roll out and cut

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients and roll out and cut

title: Ammonia Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sweet milk
- 1 pint sugar
- 1 small pint lard
- 2 heaping teaspoons ammonia
- ¾ teaspoon salt
- flour to suit

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients

title: An Economical Dish
body:
## Ingredients

- remnant of cold boiled leg of mutton or roast of beef
- onion
- sweet herbs
- gravy or water
- butter
- flour
- hot boiled potatoes
- milk or butter

## Directions

1. Shave the meat into thin slices, season and add, if you like, an onion chopped fine, or a pinch of sweet herbs.
2. Put this on a baking dish and pour over it the gravy, if you have any, if not a little water will do, with a little butter and flour.
3. Take hot boiled potatoes, mash fine, add a little milk or butter to soften them into a smooth paste and lay over the meat.
4. Put the dish in the oven and bake a nice brown.

title: An Old English Recipe for Orange Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 bitter or Seville oranges
- 4 lemons
- 12 pounds granulated sugar
- 6 quarts water

## Directions

1. On a platter cut oranges and lemons into quarters
2. Take out pith and pips into bowl and cover with water
3. Slice or shred thin as possible oranges and lemons
4. Put into a vessel with remainder of water
5. Allow it to stand 24 or 36 hours
6. Boil 2 hours, but first strain water off the pips into the whole
7. Have sugar warmed before adding
8. Boil 1/4 an hour, or until it jellies

title: Anchovy Eggs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 oz. butter
- 1 teaspoonful anchovy paste
- cayenne
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the eggs in half and take out the yolks very carefully.
2. Cut a small slice from the end of the whites.
3. Pound together the yolks, butter and anchovy paste.
4. Add seasoning and fill the whites with the mixture.

title: Angel Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs, whites only
- 1½ cups sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoonful cream tartar
- Vanilla or almond extract

## Directions

1. Sift sugar, flour, and cream tartar well together and beat in the whites of eggs — already well beaten
2. Bake in slow oven sixty minutes, in a pan not greased

title: Angel Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 11 eggs, beaten stiff
- 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar, sifted once
- 1 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon cream tartar
- lemon flavoring
- vanilla flavoring
- pineapple flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat whites of 11 eggs stiff on a platter
2. Add 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar, sifted once
3. Take 1 cup flour with 1/2 teaspoon cream tartar and sift 4 times
4. Gradually add to the cake
5. Mix lemon, vanilla and pineapple flavoring in equal quantities and add 1/2 teaspoon of mixture
6. Use a pan with tube up the centre and do not grease it
7. Bake 40 minutes
8. When baked, invert the pan on 2 cups and let it stand until cool

title: Angel Cake (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 cups flour
- 2 cups white sugar
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix whites of 3 eggs, 2 tablespoons butter, 3 cups flour, 2 cups white sugar, 1 cup sweet milk, and 2 teaspoons baking powder together and bake

title: Angels on Horseback
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve oysters
- twelve round croutons
- twelve pieces of bacon two inches long and one-half inch wide
- lemon juice
- cayenne
- watercress

## Directions

1. Beard and trim each oyster and put one on each piece of bacon
2. Squeeze on each a drop of lemon juice and a very little cayenne
3. Roll it up in the bacon
4. Cook it in a brisk oven long enough to cook the bacon
5. Serve very hot, dished on watercress

title: Angels on Horseback
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon
- oysters

## Directions

1. Take a slice of bacon, roll it up
2. Take an oyster, and skewer it on the roll of bacon
3. Repeat until the ashet is full
4. Bake in the oven

title: Apfel Kuchen (Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 teaspoons butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- apples, peeled and cut in sixths
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup sweet milk, 1 cup sugar, 3 teaspoons butter, 2 teaspoons baking powder, and 2 cups flour together
2. Pour dough into jelly tins
3. Press apples peeled and cut in sixths into the dough as close together as possible, covering the top of the cake with them
4. Cover with sugar and a little cinnamon and bake

title: Apple and Bread Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 dozen apples
- bread crumbs
- sugar
- nutmeg
- 1 egg
- 1 1/2 cups sweet milk

## Directions

1. Chop apples
2. put a layer in a baking dish, then a layer of bread crumbs, sugar and nutmeg sprinkled on
3. continue until the dish is full
4. take egg beaten in sweet milk, and pour over it
5. bake 1/4 hour

title: Apple Bird's Nest Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 pint milk
- 1 egg
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Pare and core apples
2. Fill cavity with sugar
3. Pour over batter made with 1 tablespoon flour, 1/2 pint milk, 1 egg, and sugar to taste
4. Bake 1 hour

title: Apple Butter
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 gallons sweet cider
- 1 peck tart apples
- 2 pounds brown sugar

## Directions

1. Pare and core the apples, then quarter, and cut across to shorten the fibre
2. Boil the cider down to about half its original quantity
3. Add the apples and sugar, and continue to stir the mixture well from the bottom
4. Boil until the apples and cider form one solid mass and do not separate
5. Simmer without burning

title: Apple Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3 teaspoons butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- apples peeled and cut in sixths
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pour dough into jelly tins
2. Have ready some apples peeled and cut in sixths
3. Press them into the dough as close together as possible, covering the top of the cake with them
4. Cover with sugar and a little cinnamon and bake

title: Apple Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts apple sauce
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup vinegar
- 2 onions
- 1 teaspoon each of pepper corns, mustard seeds, ginger, cloves and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients

title: Apple Charlotte
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of bread and butter, with the crust trimmed off
- Six apples
- Grated rind of a lemon and the juice
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Butter a pie-dish
2. Place a layer of bread-and-butter at the bottom
3. Layer apples, peeled, cored and cut into slices
4. Sprinkle over with sugar, a little of the lemon peel, and a few drops of the juice
5. Repeat until the dish is full
6. Cover well over with the peel of the apples to prevent it burning
7. Bake in a quick oven about three-quarters of an hour
8. When done, remove the peel
9. Turn it out on a dish
10. Sprinkle with white sugar
11. Serve at once

title: Apple Charlotte
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound breadcrumbs
- one-quarter pound brown sugar
- one pound apples
- two ounces suet, chopped
- grated rind of one lemon

## Directions

1. Butter a pie dish and sprinkle it with sugar.
2. Mix suet and bread-crumbs together.
3. Put a layer of apple in small pieces, sugar and rind of lemon, then suet and crumbs.
4. Repeat until dish is full.
5. Bake thirty or forty minutes.
6. Turn out.

title: Apple Charlotte
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint stewed apples
- one cup sugar
- one-quarter box gelatine
- whites of three eggs
- juice and rind of one lemon
- lady fingers

## Directions

1. Mix apples with one cup sugar, the grated rind and juice of lemon
2. Soak gelatine in one-third of a cup of cold water twenty minutes
3. add one-third of a cup of boiling water to dissolve the gelatine
4. when cool add it to the apples
5. When beginning to stiffen, add the beaten whites of three eggs
6. pour into moulds lined with lady fingers
7. Serve with soft custard poured round the base of the charlotte

title: Apple Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup water
- apple jelly
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Core and peel as many apples as wanted and cook slowly in a syrup made by boiling 1 cup sugar to 1 cup water
2. When done lift to a dish and fill the spaces where the cores were with apple jelly and sprinkle with granulated sugar
3. Pour the syrup around them
4. Nice red apples can be quartered and cored and the skins left on them and cooked slowly in the same way, turning them in order that both sides may be cooked alike

title: Apple Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint stewed apples sweetened and cooled
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 4 eggs beaten well
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix the apples, milk and eggs
2. Put in baking dish
3. Grate a little nutmeg over the top
4. Bake 3/4 an hour

title: Apple Custard Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 pint sweet milk
- 4 grated apples
- sugar to taste
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients
2. Serve with whipped cream

title: Apple Filling for Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 grated apples
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Boil until thick

title: Apple Float
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- eggs

## Directions

1. Make the old-fashioned apple sauce by stewing the apples until soft
2. Sweeten and beat
3. Add the beaten whites of eggs
4. Pile on nice white dish
5. Serve with a soft custard made from the yolks of the eggs

title: Apple Frittees
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- 2 eggs, whites and yolks beaten separately
- Milk for thin batter
- 1 oz. butter
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- Apples
- Boiling lard or clarified dripping
- Sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare some apples, as for a pie, or mince them if preferred
2. Add these to the batter
3. Drop a large tablespoonful at a time into a pan of boiling lard or clarified dripping
4. Fry till of a light brown, turning when required
5. When done, lay them on a sheet of blotting-paper before the fire to absorb the grease
6. Dish them, piled high, one above the other
7. Strew with sifted sugar
8. Serve as hot as possible

title: Apple Gems
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 sour apples
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup molasses
- 1 1/2 cups cornmeal
- 1 1/4 cups flour
- 1/2 teaspoonful soda

## Directions

1. Chop apples very fine
2. Stir into them beaten egg, molasses, cornmeal and sifted flour
3. Dissolve soda in warm water and add it, using enough water to thin batter
4. Bake in buttered gem-pans in a moderate oven

title: Apple Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- good ripe tart apples
- sugar
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Take some good, ripe, tart apples
2. Cut them all up fine
3. Sweeten with sugar
4. Pour sweet cream over them
5. Eat while fresh

title: Apple Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- white of 1 egg
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- one apple

## Directions

1. Grate apple
2. Beat all together for 1/2 hour
3. Flavor with almond

title: Apple Icing for Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- white of 1 egg
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 medium-sized apple grated
- almond or lemon flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat together for 20 minutes
2. Flavor with almond or lemon

title: Apple Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Windfall apples
- Sugar
- Water
- Thin rind of two lemons

## Directions

1. Wash apples and boil whole in a preserving kettle until quite pulpy
2. Strain through a jelly bag without squeezing
3. Pass through a silver wire sieve first, will run easier
4. Put sugar into a kettle with a little water, enough to melt
5. Boil quickly for ten minutes
6. Add strained apple juice; to every five pints, four pounds of sugar is allowed
7. Add thin rind of two lemons
8. Boil all together one hour
9. Measure apple juice carefully before putting on sugar and gauge sugar exactly

title: Apple Jelly for Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large or 2 small apples grated
- rind and juice of 1 lemon
- one cup sugar

## Directions

1. Grate 1 large or 2 small apples
2. Add the rind and juice of 1 lemon
3. Add one cup sugar
4. Boil 3 minutes

title: Apple Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound apples
- 1 pound sugar
- 3/4 a lemon
- 1/8 ounce whole ginger

## Directions

1. Peel and core apples
2. Slice into eighths
3. Put in a basin of water
4. Lift apples dripping into the preserving pan
5. Boil with sugar, ginger and rind of lemon until quite clear
6. Add lemon juice about 20 minutes before the preserve is done

title: Apple Meringue Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. stewed apples
- 3 eggs, whites and yolks beaten separately
- Little cinnamon
- 1 teacup sugar
- 1 teaspoonful butter
- 1 teaspoonful essence of almond
- two tablespoonfuls of white sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the apples as for a pie, and stew till almost a pulp
2. Sweeten and spice
3. While hot stir in the yolks of the eggs gradually
4. Beat very light
5. Pour into a buttered dish and bake for ten minutes
6. Cover, without drawing it from the oven, with a meringue made of the beaten whites, two tablespoonfuls of white sugar, and the almond flavoring
7. Spread it over with a tablespoon, evenly and quickly
8. Close the oven again and brown very slightly
9. Serve either hot or cold, as preferred

title: Apple Porcupine
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- sugar
- water
- blanched almonds
- jelly, preserved fruit or marmalade
- cream

## Directions

1. Pare and core the apples.
2. Make a syrup by boiling sugar and water in equal proportions.
3. As soon as the fruit is pared, before it is discolored by standing, immerse it in the syrup and cook until it is easily pierced with a straw.
4. Draw the apples from the liquid and ornament the sides of each by piercing them with blanched almonds.
5. Fill the cavities with jelly, preserved fruit or marmalade and serve hot or cold with cream.

title: Apple Pot Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 apples
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup rich milk
- a pinch of salt
- enough flour to make a stiff paste
- butter the size of a small egg
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Select 8 apples, cut in 1/8's, remove core but not skin
2. take 1 egg, 3/4 cup rich milk, a pinch of salt, enough flour to make a stiff paste
3. roll out very thin, and cut into 2-inch squares
4. put butter the size of a small egg into a granite kettle, allow it to become a delicate brown, remove to back of stove
5. put in a layer of apples, a layer of the rolled paste, a little brown sugar, a pinch of cinnamon, repeat until your apples and paste are all used
6. Over this pour boiling water and cook for 20 minutes
7. Serve hot

title: Apple Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantities of good brown sugar and apples
- 1 pint of water to 3 pounds of sugar
- 1 or 2 lemons
- a little whole white ginger

## Directions

1. Weigh equal quantities of good brown sugar and apples
2. Peel, core and cut the apples into small square pieces
3. Make syrup of 1 pint of water to 3 pounds of sugar
4. Boil until pretty thick
5. Add the apples, the grated peel of a lemon or two, and a little whole white ginger
6. Boil until the apples are clear and begin to fall

title: Apple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 4 level teaspoons baking powder
- 1 egg
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- a little salt
- 3 large apples, peeled and sliced

## Directions

1. Cream the butter, add sugar, then egg, and beat well
2. Add flour and milk alternately, stirring apples

title: Apple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/8 teaspoon salt
- good cream
- 2 cups chopped apples
- 1/2 cup sugar
- any ordinary spice or flavoring
- 1 cup sugar
- cold water

## Directions

1. Mix dry ingredients thoroughly
2. Moisten with good cream after mixing dry ingredients
3. Have the dough quite soft, as for biscuit
4. Roll to the size of a large pie pan
5. Place in a large, well-buttered pudding dish
6. Spread on the dough 2 cups chopped apples, 1/2 cup sugar and any ordinary spice or flavoring
7. Moisten the edge of dough and press together to form a covering for the filling
8. Over the top spread a cup of sugar
9. Pour into the dish enough cold water to completely cover the pudding
10. Place in a moderately hot oven and bake 1 3/4 hour

title: Apple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen medium-sized apples
- 3/4 cup sugar
- a little water
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- butter size of small egg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- a heaping teaspoon of baking powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Peel, core and cut apples in quarters
2. Put apples with 3/4 cup sugar and a little water in saucepan and stew until they are about half done
3. Make a batter of 1 egg, 1 cup sugar, butter size of small egg creamed together
4. Add 1 cup sweet milk, a heaping teaspoon of baking powder and flour to thicken
5. Put apples in pudding dish
6. Pour batter over them and bake
7. When done spread butter over the pudding and serve with cream

title: Apple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 dozen cooking apples
- grated bread crumbs
- sugar
- nutmeg
- cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pare and slice 1/2 dozen cooking apples
2. Grease a pudding dish
3. Put a thick layer of grated bread crumbs
4. Then a layer of apples, with sugar and nutmeg
5. Repeat until dish is full
6. Bake thirty minutes
7. Serve with cream and sugar

title: Apple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced apples
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Fill a buttered baking dish with sliced apples
2. Pour over the top a batter made of 1 tablespoon of butter, 1/2 cup sugar, 1 egg, 1/2 cup sweet milk and 1 cup flour, in which has been sifted 1 teaspoon baking powder
3. Bake in a moderate oven from 30 to 45 minutes
4. Serve with cream and sugar or pudding sauce

title: Apple Puff
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 apples
- yolks of 2 eggs
- tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 pint milk
- vanilla
- whites of 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- jelly

## Directions

1. Bake 6 apples
2. Let cool
3. Take yolks of 2 eggs, tablespoon cornstarch, 1 pint milk
4. Cook till thick
5. Flavor with vanilla
6. Cool
7. Scrape white part of apple into beaten whites of eggs
8. Add 1 cup sugar
9. Beat hour
10. Put custard in dish with a little jelly
11. Cover with beaten apple

title: Apple Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 good tart apples
- 1 onion
- 3/4 cup vinegar
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- butter size of an egg

## Directions

1. Chop 6 good tart apples fine
2. Chop 1 onion fine
3. Put together
4. Boil vinegar, mustard, pepper, salt, cornstarch, sugar, and butter size of an egg on the stove till thick
5. Put by till cool
6. Put sauce on apples when cold

title: Apple Sauce Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup unsweetened apple sauce
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup melted butter and lard
- 1/4 cup raisins
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- ginger or cinnamon to taste

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 teaspoon soda in apple sauce
2. Mix all ingredients together
3. Bake in slow oven

title: Apple Sauce Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup shortening
- 1 saltspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- nutmeg
- 1 cup raisins seeded and cut up
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup sour apple sauce
- 2 1/2 cups sifted flour
- boiled cider or vinegar

## Directions

1. Cream together 1 cup sugar and 1/2 cup shortening
2. Add 1 saltspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon cloves, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, a little nutmeg and 1 cup raisins seeded and cut up
3. Dissolve 1 teaspoon soda in a bit of warm water, then stir it in a cup of sour apple sauce, letting it foam over the ingredients in the bowl
4. Beat all thoroughly and add 2 1/2 cups sifted flour
5. Bake in a loaf tin 45 minutes or more, until it is well baked
6. If the apple sauce is not very sour add a little boiled cider or vinegar

title: Apple Short Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Apple sauce seasoned with a little butter, and pinch of salt
- One pint of flour sifted with two teaspoonfuls baking powder
- a little salt
- butter half size of an egg
- one coffee-cup sweet milk
- melted butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Rub butter half size of an egg into the flour, and mix into a soft dough with one coffee-cup sweet milk
2. Divide dough into two parts, roll out one-half, put in pan
3. brush surface with melted butter
4. Roll out the rest and put on top
5. Bake in very hot oven
6. Divide, butter, and spread with sauce
7. Serve with cream

title: Apple Snow
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Six or eight tart, juicy apples
- A little water
- Sugar to suit the taste
- One pint of milk
- Yolks of three eggs
- Sugar to sweeten
- Three heaping tablespoons of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Pare, core and slice six or eight tart, juicy apples
2. Stew them in a little water until soft enough to press through a colander
3. Sweeten to suit the taste
4. Turn into a deep glass dish
5. Let the apples get cold
6. Cover with a soft custard made with a pint of milk, the yolks of three eggs and sugar to sweeten
7. Flavor with lemon or orange
8. When cold pour over the apples
9. Whip the whites to a stiff froth with three heaping tablespoons of powdered sugar
10. Heap lightly over the custard

title: Apple Snow
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large apples
- granulated or powdered sugar
- salt
- whites of 3 eggs
- custard made with yolks of eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 cups milk
- jelly

## Directions

1. Wipe, pare and core 4 large apples
2. Steam until soft
3. Press through sieve
4. Sweeten with granulated or powdered sugar
5. Add a few grains of salt
6. Beat very stiff the whites of 3 eggs
7. Pour in the apples, beating constantly
8. Chill
9. Serve with custard made with yolks of eggs, 3/4 cup sugar and 2 cups milk
10. If wished to look especially nice, add a few dots of bright jelly

title: Apple Snow (Second Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups apple sauce
- white of 1 egg
- whipped cream or boiled custard

## Directions

1. Cook apple sauce as dry as possible
2. Press through sieve
3. Sweeten
4. Add the white of 1 egg, beaten stiff
5. Beat briskly
6. Heap in a dish
7. Pour around it whipped cream or boiled custard

title: Apple Snow Frosting
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large sour apple
- 1 cup white pulverized sugar
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Peel and grate the apple
2. Add sugar and beaten egg white
3. Continue beating 3/4 hour
4. Use as a filling

title: Apple Souffle Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 7 fine apples
- 1 cup fine breadcrumbs
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- Nutmeg and a little grated lemon peel

## Directions

1. Pare, core, and slice the apples
2. Stew in a covered double saucepan, without a drop of water, until they are tender
3. Mash to a smooth pulp
4. While hot, stir in the butter and sugar
5. Let it get quite cold
6. Whip in first the yolks of the eggs, then the whites beaten very stiff, alternately with the breadcrumbs
7. Flavor and beat quickly three minutes, until all the ingredients are reduced to a creamy batter
8. Bake in a buttered dish, in a moderate oven
9. It will take about an hour to cook it properly
10. Keep it covered until ten minutes before you take it out

title: Apple Sponge
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. apples
- 0.5 oz. gelatine
- 1 lb. sugar
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Rind of 1 lemon
- 0.25 pt. boiling water
- Whites of three eggs
- Custard

## Directions

1. Boil the water and sugar in a saucepan until dissolved
2. Peel and slice the apples thinly, and add to the syrup
3. Stew until tender
4. Add gelatine, and strain all through a sieve
5. Add lemon juice and rind and beat until cool
6. Beat the whites of three eggs stiffly and add to the mixture
7. Beat all until cold
8. Put into a mould
9. Serve with custard

title: Apple Stuffing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. tart apple sauce
- 1 cup breadcrumbs
- 1 onion, chopped
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. For roast goose, duck and game.

title: Apple Tapioca
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup tapioca
- 7 sour apples
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- cold water
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 2 1/2 cups boiling water

## Directions

1. Soak tapioca 1 hour in cold water to cover
2. Add boiling water and salt
3. Cook in double boiler until transparent
4. Pare and slice apples
5. Place in a buttered pudding dish
6. Sprinkle sugar over apples
7. Pour tapioca over apples
8. Bake in a moderate oven until apples are soft

title: Apple Tapioca or Sago
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup pearl or minute tapioca
- cold water to cover
- 4 cups water
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 square inches lemon rind
- 7 sour apples
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. soak tapioca 1 hour, drain and add water and salt and lemon rind
2. cook in double boiler until transparent
3. Core and pare apples, arrange in buttered baking dish
4. fill cavities with sugar and bake in moderate oven until apples are soft
5. serve with sugar and cream

title: Apple Whip
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 3 eggs
- 1 pint stewed and sweetened apple
- 1 cup chopped nuts
- 1 pint candied cherries

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of eggs until stiff and perfectly dry
2. Fold lightly into the stewed apple
3. Add chopped nuts and candied cherries
4. Beat until firm
5. Chill through

title: Apples and Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large apples
- sugar
- butter
- water
- 1 pint milk
- yolks 2 eggs
- 1 level teaspoon cornstarch
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Pare and core 6 large apples, filling the cavities with sugar and a bit of butter
2. Put a little water in a dish, place in the apples and bake
3. When nearly done, pour a custard around made of 1 pint milk, yolks 2 eggs, 1 level teaspoon cornstarch, sugar and flavor to taste
4. Put back in the oven and bake
5. Make a meringue of the 2 whites, and put a tablespoon over each
6. Brown lightly and eat cold

title: Apples in Bloom
body:
## Ingredients

- large red apples
- hot water
- 1 cup sugar
- orange or lemon rind
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Select large red apples, wash or wipe clean as many as will set in bottom of pudding dish or saucepan
2. Pour in hot water until about half-way up the apples
3. Boil on top of range, taking care to turn often, pricking with a fork to keep the skin as whole as possible
4. When cooked, take from fire, and when slightly cooled remove the skins carefully and arrange in a pretty dish
5. To the water add 1 cup sugar and boil down to about 1 cup
6. If an orange or lemon is handy some of the rind grated is an improvement
7. Pour over apples
8. Serve when cold with whipped cream

title: Apples with Dunfillan Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons flour
- 3 ounces butter
- 1 egg
- 1 eggspoon baking powder
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 3/4 pint milk
- flavoring to taste
- 1 1/2 pounds apples

## Directions

1. Have ready some cold stewed apples
2. Rub butter into flour
3. Add baking powder
4. Beat up egg with sugar
5. Add milk, and stir all into the flour
6. Mix well
7. Pour on the top of the apples and bake in moderate oven for hour
8. Sift sugar over

title: Apples with Dunfillan Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons flour
- 3 ounces butter
- 1 egg
- 1 eggspoon baking powder
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 3/4 pint milk
- flavoring to taste
- 1 1/2 pounds apples
- cold stewed apples

## Directions

1. Have ready some cold stewed apples
2. Rub butter into flour
3. Add baking powder
4. Beat up egg with sugar
5. Add milk
6. Stir all into the flour
7. Mix well
8. Pour on the top of the apples
9. Bake in moderate oven for hour
10. Sift sugar over

title: Apples à la Princess Maude
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds of good cooking apples
- three-quarters of a pint of water
- four to six ounces of loaf sugar
- bay-leaves
- finely cut peel of one lemon
- three-quarters of an ounce of leaf gelatine
- liquid carmine
- thick cream
- lemon jelly
- angelica
- liquid jelly
- thickly whipped cream
- vanilla essence
- chopped pistachio nuts

## Directions

1. Peel one and a half pounds of good cooking apples, cut them up and cook them in three-quarters of a pint of water with four to six ounces of loaf sugar, according to the sweetness of the apples, and bay-leaves, and the finely cut peel of one lemon.
2. When the apples are perfectly soft, dissolve with them three-quarters of an ounce of leaf gelatine and pass the whole through a sieve.
3. Divide the puree into two parts, and redden one of them with liquid carmine, and whiten the other with a little thick cream.
4. Put them in separate sauce pans to about a quarter of an inch thick, and let them set on broken ice.
5. Cut out in rounds with a plain cutter about the size of a shilling for ornamenting round the mould, and in leaf shapes for the bottom and in the centre of each of the rounds set a little round of angelica with a little liquid jelly.
6. Line a plain round mould with lemon jelly to about one-eighth of an inch thick.
7. Set the cut leaves of apple puree regularly on the bottom of the mould with the stock ends at the centre and the rounds regularly round the sides in alternate colors.
8. Fix these in their places with a little more of the lemon jelly.
9. Separately dissolve the odds and ends of the cuttings of the puree with two tablespoonfuls of lemon jelly, and let them stand till somewhat cool.
10. Add to each a quarter of a pint of thickly whipped cream and pour them into the mould in alternate layers and put to set on broken ice.
11. When required turn out on a dish.
12. Place on the top a ball of stiffly whipped cream sweetened and flavored with vanilla essence, and lightly sprinkle with a little chopped pistachio nuts.

title: Apricot Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiling water
- 2 squares in lemon rind
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook flour until somewhat thick
2. Add butter and lemon juice just before removing from the stove
3. Remove rind

title: Arrowroot Charlotte
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 3 tablespoonfuls arrowroot
- 1 qt. milk
- 3 or 4 stale sponge cakes
- Vanilla flavoring
- Sherry
- Jelly or jam

## Directions

1. Line a mould with the sponge cakes, cut thin
2. Sprinkle with sherry
3. Mix the flour and arrowroot with enough cold milk to make it smooth
4. Put the remainder of the milk into a saucepan
5. Stir in the mixture just before it boils
6. Boil a few minutes, stirring all the time
7. Pour it boiling into the mould
8. Stand it aside till quite cold
9. Turn it out of the mould
10. Spread it with jelly or jam

title: Arrowroot Creams
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. arrowroot
- 3 oz. powdered sugar
- 1 egg, yolk only
- 1 qt. milk
- A little lemon peel and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix the arrowroot with a little of the milk to a smooth paste
2. Add to it the egg
3. Boil the remainder of the milk with the sugar and flavoring
4. Pour it boiling hot on the arrowroot
5. Keep it well stirred till nearly cold

title: Arrowroot Creams
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. arrowroot
- 1 qt. milk
- 3 oz. powdered sugar
- 1 egg yolk
- A little lemon peel and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix the arrowroot with a little of the milk to a smooth paste and add to it the egg.
2. Boil the remainder of the milk with the sugar and flavoring, and pour it boiling hot on the arrowroot, keeping it well stirred till nearly cold, when it may be set aside in custard glasses.

title: Arrowroot Milk Porridge
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cup new milk
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 teaspoonful arrowroot
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Put the sugar into the milk, the salt into the boiling water, which should be poured into a farina-kettle
2. Add the arrowroot, make into paste with cold water, and boil, stirring constantly until it is clear
3. Put in the milk, and cook ten minutes, stirring often
4. Give while warm, adding hot milk should it be thicker than gruel

title: Artificial Honey
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 pounds white sugar
- 3 pounds clear bees' honey
- 1 quart hot water
- 3/8 ounce cream of tartar
- 2 or 3 drops otto roses
- clear yellow honeycomb

## Directions

1. Mix together 10 pounds white sugar, 3 pounds clear bees' honey, 1 quart hot water, 3/8 ounce cream of tartar
2. When cool flavor with 2 or 3 drops otto roses and sprinkle in a handful of clear yellow honeycomb broken up

title: Asparagus in White Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus stalks
- salt
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the asparagus stalks into pieces about an inch long, and as far down as tender
2. Cook them in salted boiling water
3. Drain and stir into them just enough white sauce to well cover them

title: Asparagus on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- water
- toast
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the tough, hard end from the asparagus
2. Wash carefully
3. Tie in bunches
4. Set the bunches on end in a saucepan of boiling, salted water, having the tender ends above the water
5. Cover and boil gently for 1 hour
6. Drain
7. Slightly moisten pieces of toast with a little of the water in which asparagus was cooked
8. Spread with butter
9. Arrange these on a hot dish
10. Untie the bunches of asparagus and place them on the toast
11. Put a little butter over the tender ends of the stalks and serve very hot

title: Asparagus Patés
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread
- butter
- asparagus tips
- white sauce
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut rounds of stale bread an inch and a half thick
2. Press a small cutter an inch deep into each, and dig out the inside, leaving a round saucerlike cavity
3. Butter these well and set upon the grating of a hot oven to crisp and to color lightly
4. Fill them with asparagus tips prepared as in the last recipe
5. Serve hot

title: Asparagus Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 pounds of veal cut fine
- a little salt pork
- 2 or 3 bunches of asparagus
- 3 quarts of water
- 1 pint of milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Boil one-half of the asparagus with the meat and salt pork
2. Leave the rest of the asparagus in water until about 20 minutes before serving
3. The soup should boil about 3 hours before adding the last half of the asparagus
4. Add the rest of the asparagus and boil just before serving
5. Add 1 pint of milk
6. Thicken with a little flour and season

title: Asparagus Tips
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the asparagus stalks into pieces about an inch long, and as far down as tender.
2. Cook them in salted boiling water.
3. Drain and stir into them just enough white sauce to well cover them.

title: Asparagus Tips
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus tips
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- white sauce
- milk

## Directions

1. Use only the delicate tips of asparagus, less than two inches long
2. Boil in hot, salted water until tender
3. Drain, turn into a deep dish, pepper, salt, butter
4. Pour a good white sauce over them — half a cupful to one cupful of the tips

title: Asparagus à la Vinaigrette
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus stalks
- salt
- three tablespoonfuls salad oil
- one tablespoonful vinegar
- one teaspoonful French mustard
- salt
- cayenne
- one saltspoonful sugar

## Directions

1. Boil asparagus as directed
2. While the stalks are hot pour over them a dressing made of three tablespoonfuls of salad oil to one of vinegar, a teaspoonful of French mustard, a little salt and cayenne, and a saltspoonful of sugar
3. Set away in a closely covered dish
4. When cold put upon the ice for some hours before serving

title: Aspic Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. good stock
- whites and shells of 2 eggs
- 1 cup lean raw beef
- 1 oz. gelatine
- 1 tablespoonful vinegar
- 2 tablespoonfuls sherry
- 20 peppercorns
- 10 cloves
- 2 stalks celery
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 0.5 saltspoonful ground mace
- sprig of parsley

## Directions

1. Mix the beef and eggs thoroughly together and add to the stock before it gets hot
2. When hot add gelatine (previously soaked in cold water)
3. Boil for about five minutes
4. Strain and add vinegar, sherry, twenty peppercorns, ten cloves, two or three stalks of celery, one teaspoon salt, one-half saltspoon of ground mace, sprig of parsley

title: Aunt Fannies Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup icing sugar
- 1 egg white beaten stiff
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3/4 cup chopped walnuts
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Aunt Julia's Ice Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 3 eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 cup cream
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Scald milk
2. Beat eggs, sugar and flour together
3. Add to milk when hot, stirring constantly
4. Let cool, add cream and flavoring
5. For the freezing, use 3 of ice to 1 of salt; have ice fine and mix thoroughly with salt in a pan before packing

title: Austin Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/4 teaspoon spice (cloves and nutmeg)
- flour

## Directions

1. Cream together 1 1/2 cups brown sugar and 3/4 cup butter
2. Add 2 eggs, 1 cup sweet milk, 1 teaspoon soda, and 1/4 teaspoon spice (cloves and nutmeg)
3. Add flour enough to make a thick batter
4. Bake in a long pan

title: Austrian Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- A pinch of salt
- 1 large teaspoonful baking-powder
- 1/2 lb. suet, chopped
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 1 breakfast cup of milk
- 1 breakfast cup of treacle

## Directions

1. Mix the dry ingredients together, then warm the milk, stir it into the treacle, and add it to the pudding.
2. Mix well and boil slowly but continuously in a well-buttered basin for three hours.

title: Bachelor's Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One egg
- Its weight in minced apple
- Flour
- Sugar
- Currants
- Breadcrumbs
- Suet
- A little milk

## Directions

1. Mix these with the egg and add a little milk.
2. Boil in a mould from one and a half to two hours.

title: Bacon Fraze
body:
## Ingredients

- breakfast bacon or salt pork
- 1 cup milk
- 1 egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook either breakfast bacon or salt pork in frying pan till nearly done
2. Pour over it a batter made of 1 cup milk, 1 egg, and flour to thicken
3. Cook till set, then put in oven to brown
4. Turn over on flat dish
5. Serve on a cold winter night with hot biscuits

title: Baked Apple Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large apples (grated)
- 3 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1/4 lb. sugar
- 2 eggs (whites and yolks beaten separately)
- Juice of 1 lemon and half the rind grated
- Pastry

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar into a cream
2. Stir in the yolks, the lemon, the grated apple, and lastly the whites of the eggs
3. Line a dish with pastry
4. Pour in the mixture
5. Bake till nicely browned
6. This is best cold

title: Baked Apple Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- large apples
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Pare, quarter and core large apples
2. Pack in an earthen jar with brown sugar
3. Cover closely
4. Bake slowly in a moderate oven until the contents have been shrunken to about half their original bulk and are rich, red and luscious

title: Baked Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- apples
- egg
- sugar
- water
- fine bread crumbs
- lemon

## Directions

1. Butter a deep tin pan
2. Cover the bottom with a layer of apples
3. Beat egg, put in enough sugar to sweeten apple, add a little water
4. Thicken with fine bread crumbs
5. Flavor lightly with lemon
6. Pour this mixture over apples and bake until the apples are done, which will take about an hour

title: Baked Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- sugar
- water
- nuts
- jelly
- cream
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Pare and core, leaving whole as many apples as required
2. Fill the centres with sugar
3. Pour a little water in the dish
4. Cover and cook in a hot oven
5. As a variation add some finely chopped nuts to the sugar or a lump of jelly in each apple
6. Serve hot or cold with cream

title: Baked Batter Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 pints milk
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 2 ounces butter
- 4 eggs
- a little salt
- sweet sauce or stewed fruit
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with a little cold milk
2. Make the rest hot (but not too hot)
3. Pour it on the flour
4. Keep the mixture well stirred
5. Add butter, eggs and salt
6. Beat the whole well and put the pudding into a buttered pie dish
7. Bake 3/4 hour
8. Serve with a sweet sauce or stewed fruit, or add sugar to taste before cooking and serve with cream

title: Baked Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart beans (Lima or white beans)
- 1/4 pound salt pork
- 2 tablespoons molasses
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1/2 tablespoon mustard
- 1/8 teaspoon pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and soak beans over night
2. In the morning pour off the water and rinse well
3. Cover with fresh water
4. Bring to boiling point and simmer until skins will burst; this is best determined by taking a few in the hand and blowing on them; if skins burst, beans are cooked
5. Drain beans and throw away the water
6. Scald rind of pork, scrape and cut in two
7. Half of it put in bottom of bean crock, the other half reserve for the top
8. Put beans in the crock and over them pour the seasoning mixed with 1 cup boiling water
9. Add enough more boiling water to cover beans
10. Bake in covered bean pot in a slow oven 6 or 8 hours
11. Uncover the pot the last hour that pork rind may become brown and crisp

title: Baked Beef Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups chopped cooked cold beef
- 3 cups mashed potatoes
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 2 tablespoons sweet milk
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Pack in granite pudding dish
3. Cook in the oven for 3 1/2 hours

title: Baked Berry Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- rich biscuit dough
- berries
- butter
- spices
- sugar
- hot water
- sweet sauce

## Directions

1. Roll rich biscuit dough thin
2. Cut it into little squares four inches wide and seven inches long
3. Spread over with berries
4. Roll up the crust
5. Put the rolls in a dipping pan, just a little apart
6. Put a piece of butter on each roll, spices if you like
7. Strew over a large handful of sugar a little hot water
8. Set in the oven and bake like dumplings
9. Serve with sweet sauce

title: Baked Berry Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- rich biscuit dough
- berries

## Directions

1. Roll rich biscuit dough thin
2. Cut it into little squares four inches wide and seven inches long
3. Spread over with berries
4. Roll up the crust

title: Baked Black Bass
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- black bass
- eight good sized onions
- bread-crumbs
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- anchovy sauce
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Chop eight good sized onions fine
2. mix with half that quantity of bread-crumbs
3. mix thoroughly with anchovy sauce until quite red
4. stuff your fish with this compound and pour the rest over it
5. sprinkle it with a little red pepper
6. bake

title: Baked Brown Bread
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups Indian meal
- 2 cups rye or Graham flour
- ½ cup molasses
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- ½ teaspoonful salt
- Sour milk to make batter like cake

## Directions

1. Have moderate oven; bake slowly four or five hours

title: Baked Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cabbage
- pepper
- salt
- a little cream

## Directions

1. Boil a cabbage
2. Put in a colander and drain until perfectly dry
3. Chop fine
4. Add pepper, salt, and a little cream
5. Put in an earthen baking pan
6. Bake 1 hour

title: Baked Cauliflower
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- boiling, salted water
- white sauce
- grated cheese
- paprica

## Directions

1. Cut into clusters and stew tender in boiling, salted water
2. Or, if you have a couple of small cauliflowers, boil them whole and dish together
3. Drain and lay in a bake-dish
4. Pour over it a good white sauce (hot), sprinkle with grated cheese and paprica, and bake, covered, twenty minutes

title: Baked Celery
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups celery cut in 1-inch pieces
- 1 cup stale bread crumbs buttered
- 3/4 cup grated cheese
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Cook celery in boiling water until tender
2. Drain and save the liquid
3. Make a sauce

title: Baked Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- fowls
- flour
- salt and pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the fowls open and lay them flat in a pan
2. Break down the breast and the back bones
3. Dredge with flour and season well with salt and pepper and bits of butter
4. Put in a very hot oven until done, basting frequently with melted butter
5. Or when half done take out the chicken and finish by broiling it upon a gridiron over bright coals
6. Pour over it melted butter and the juices in the pan in which it was baked

title: Baked Cod
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Middle part of large fresh cod, or small whole one
- 1 teacup of breadcrumbs peppered and salted
- 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped salt pork
- 1 tablespoonful of herbs (parsley, marjoram and thyme)
- A suspicion of onion
- 1 tablespoonful of Harvey's sauce, or anchovy
- 1/2 teacup of melted butter
- Juice of half a lemon
- 1 beaten egg

## Directions

1. Lay the fish in cold, salted water for half an hour
2. Wipe it dry
3. Stuff it with a force-meat, made of crumbs, pork, herbs, onions and seasoning, bound with the beaten egg
4. Lay it in the baking-dish, and pour over it the melted butter, which should be quite thin, seasoned with the sauce, lemon-juice, pepper, and a pinch of parsley
5. Bake in a moderate oven for an hour, or longer, if the piece is large, basting frequently, lest it should brown too fast
6. Add a little butter and water if the sauce thickens too much
7. When the fish is done, remove it to a hot dish, strain the gravy over it, and serve
8. A few capers or chopped green pickles are considered a pleasant addition to the sauce

title: Baked Cold Roast Beef
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of roast beef
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- cold gravy or butter
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Place a layer of the slices in the bottom of a shallow pudding dish
2. Put pepper and salt, and a small slice of onion, on each slice
3. Add cold gravy or little pieces of butter
4. Put in another layer of meat until all is used
5. Cover the top with a layer of mashed potatoes
6. Bake for half an hour, or until the top is nicely browned

title: Baked Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- pinch salt
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat the egg just enough to mix it thoroughly
2. Stir in the sugar and salt
3. Add milk and flavoring
4. Stir until the sugar is dissolved
5. Turn into a baking dish
6. Set the baking dish in a pan and pour boiling water around it
7. Bake in a hot oven until the point of a knife will come out of the centre with nothing on it

title: Baked Dumplings
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 tablespoons lard
- 2 tablespoons butter
- milk
- apple, peach or any desired fruit
- 1 cup sugar
- cinnamon
- butter
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Wet with milk and roll out into 8 dumplings, putting apple, peach or any desired fruit inside
2. Lay in bake dish and sprinkle 1 cup sugar and a little cinnamon over top
3. Add little bits of butter
4. Cover with boiling water and bake

title: Baked Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 7 eggs
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Break 6 or 7 eggs into a buttered dish, taking care that each is whole and does not encroach upon the others so much as to mix or disturb the yolks
2. Sprinkle with pepper and salt
3. Put a bit of butter upon each
4. Put into an oven and bake until whites are set
5. Nice for breakfast served on toast or alone

title: Baked Fish
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- fish of three or four pounds
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- lemon
- pork
- melted butter
- lard
- brown paper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Procure a fish of three or four pounds.
2. Season with one heaping tablespoonful of salt and one teaspoonful of pepper.
3. Rub the seasoning well in and outside the fish.
4. Place the fish with two sliced onions on a large dish.
5. Sprinkle over the juice of one large lemon.
6. Cover and set aside for one hour.
7. Lay the fish in a baking-pan with four thin slices of pork under it and three slices of pork on top.
8. Pour one tablespoonful of melted butter over.
9. Bake forty-five minutes.
10. Serve in a hot dish garnished with lemon cut into quarters and parsley.

title: Baked Fish
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- any cold boiled fish, free from bones
- a cup or more of cream or milk
- a little flour
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- Cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Take any cold boiled fish, free from bones (canned salmon will do)
2. Pour over it a cup or more of cream or milk mixed with a little flour, butter, pepper, and salt
3. Put small bits of butter on top
4. Bake one-half hour
5. Cracker crumbs are an improvement

title: Baked Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- A fresh fish of any kind
- A piece of butter the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Cut fish in pieces about 2 inches square
2. Place these in a bean crock with a piece of butter the size of an egg
3. Cover and place in the oven
4. The fish will cook in its own liquor

title: Baked Ham
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- onion
- carrot
- fresh tomato
- hot water
- flour
- Madeira or sherry or other pale wine
- browned cracker-crumbs

## Directions

1. Soak, wash, and parboil the ham, twelve minutes to the pound.
2. Skin as soon as you can handle it, and staunch the flow of juices by rubbing flour into it.
3. Put into a good oven; slice an onion, mince a carrot and a fresh tomato, and lay about the meat, pour in half a cupful of hot water to prevent burning, cover closely, and bake twelve minutes to the pound.
4. During this time baste the ham four times with Madeira or sherry or other pale wine, using two glasses in all, and four times with the pan-gravy.
5. Have ready some browned cracker-crumbs and sift them thickly over the ham when done.
6. Leave it in the oven until firm and evenly colored.

title: Baked Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh ham
- dressing

## Directions

1. Get a fresh ham and make a pocket in it by cutting the fat from between the rind and lean part
2. Make a dressing the same as for a turkey for it, and fill the pocket
3. Skewer the rind and bake for 4 hours

title: Baked Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chopped meat (fresh)
- 2 cups chopped cold potatoes
- 2 cups bread crumbs
- 1 small onion or little sage
- pepper and salt
- 1 cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. season with pepper and salt
2. pour over 1 cup sweet milk
3. cover and bake slowly 1 hour

title: Baked Hash 2
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat
- potatoes
- two slices of bread
- cold water or milk
- salt and pepper
- a little sliced onion
- butter size of an egg

## Directions

1. Chop cold meat and potatoes, twice as much potatoes as meat
2. dip two slices of bread in cold water or milk
3. add salt and pepper with a little sliced onion, and butter size of an egg
4. Put in baking pan and cover tightly when cooking
5. Bake 3/4 hour and turn out in a cake on a hot dish

title: Baked Onions
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- 1 cupful of stock
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Cook tender in two waters — the second salted and boiling.
2. Drain well, pressing each onion in a coarse cloth, gently, not to break it, and when they are dry, lay all together, side by side, in a bake-pan.
3. Pepper, salt, and butter, and add a cupful of stock.
4. Brown in a quick oven; take out the onions and keep them hot in a deep dish while you thicken the gravy left in the pan with browned flour.
5. Pour over the onions, set in the oven for two minutes, and serve.

title: Baked Oyster Plant (Salsify)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify
- pepper
- salt
- one tablespoonful butter
- half a cup milk
- bread-crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the salsify until perfectly tender, then mash through a strainer
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Add a tablespoonful of butter and half a cup of milk
4. Put in a bake-dish, cover with bread-crumbs and bits of butter, and bake fifteen minutes

title: Baked Parsnips
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil parsnips until tender
2. Drain and mash
3. Add a generous lump of butter, pepper and salt to taste
4. Butter a baking dish
5. Cover the bottom with a layer of bread crumbs
6. Put in parsnips
7. Cover with a layer of crumbs
8. Brown in oven

title: Baked Parsnips (with Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- eggs
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Mash boiled parsnips
2. Add beaten egg
3. Pile on buttered dish
4. Cover with buttered crumbs
5. Bake in the oven until the crumbs are browned

title: Baked Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups mashed potatoes
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup cream or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat potatoes and butter
2. Add whipped eggs, cream or milk, and salt to taste
3. Beat well
4. Pour into a buttered dish
5. Bake in a quick oven until well browned

title: Baked Rice Curry
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful raw rice
- 1 onion
- 1 quart water
- salt
- 1 heaping teaspoonful curry-powder
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Wash a cupful of raw rice in three waters, and let it soak fifteen minutes in water enough to cover it.
2. Boil an onion in a quart of water with a little salt until the onion is very soft.
3. Strain the water, squeezing the onion hard in a bit of cloth.
4. Throw it away, put the water over the fire with a heaping teaspoonful of curry-powder, and when it boils again pour upon the rice and the water in which it was soaked.
5. Turn all into a jar with a close top, or a casserole dish with a cover, and set in a moderate oven until the rice has soaked up the liquid and is swollen and soft, but not broken.
6. Serve in a deep, open dish, and pour over it a few spoonfuls of melted butter, loosening the rice gently with a fork to allow the butter to penetrate to the bottom.
7. Serve with roast chicken, veal, or fish.

title: Baked Saddle of Venison
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- saddle of venison of about ten pounds
- salt pork in strips about two inches long, and an eighth of an inch thick
- two carrots
- one onion
- salt pork in thin slices
- two bay leaves
- two cloves
- four kernels of allspice
- half a lemon, sliced
- salt and pepper
- a quart of good stock
- a small piece of butter
- jelly or a wine sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the saddle carefully; see that no hairs are left dried on to the outside.
2. Cut some salt pork in strips about two inches long, and an eighth of an inch thick, with which lard the saddle with two rows on each side.
3. In a large dripping-pan cut two carrots, one onion, and some salt pork in thin slices; add two bay leaves, two cloves, four kernels of allspice, half a lemon, sliced, and season with salt and pepper.
4. Place the saddle of venison in the pan, with a quart of good stock, boiling hot, and a small piece of butter, and let it boil about fifteen minutes on top of the stove.
5. Put it in a hot oven and bake, basting well every five minutes, until it is medium rare, so that the blood runs when cut.
6. Serve with jelly or a wine sauce.
7. If the venison is desired well done cook much longer, and use a cream sauce with it, or stir cream into the venison gravy.

title: Baked Salmon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 1/2 cup bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon vinegar or juice of 1 lemon
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Drain the salmon and pick to pieces
2. Work in the melted butter, egg, seasoning and crumbs
3. Moisten with liquid drained from salmon
4. Turn into a well-buttered pan in boiling water
5. Cover closely
6. Bake 1 hour

title: Baked Salmon With Cream Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- A middle cut of salmon
- 4 tablespoonfuls of butter, melted in hot water
- 1 cup of cream
- 1 teaspoonful of cornflour
