- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- Pepper
- Salt
- Parsley
- Butter for paper
- Soda

## Directions

1. Butter a sheet of foolscap paper on both sides
2. Wrap the fish up in it, pinning the ends securely together
3. Lay it in the baking-pan
4. Pour six or seven spoonfuls of butter-and-water over it
5. Turn another pan over all
6. Steam in a moderate oven from three-quarters of an hour to an hour
7. Lift the cover from time to time to baste and assure yourself that the paper is not burning
8. Meanwhile have ready in a saucepan a cup of cream
9. Dissolve a bit of soda a little larger than a pea in the cream
10. Heat this in a double boiler
11. Thicken with a heaping teaspoonful of cornflour
12. Add a tablespoonful of butter, pepper and salt to taste
13. Add a liberal pinch of minced parsley
14. When the fish is unwrapped and dished pour half slowly over it
15. Send the rest to table in a boat
16. If you have no cream use milk and add a beaten egg to the thickening

title: Baked Sliced Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- sweet milk
- 5 or 6 slices of pork

## Directions

1. Pare raw potatoes and slice very thinly enough to fill a 2 quart pudding dish
2. Season with salt and pepper over the top
3. Pour over sweet milk 3/4 full
4. Cut 5 or 6 slices of pork and lay over the top
5. Bake about 2 hours
6. If liked, the pork may be left off and a little more milk added

title: Baked Squash
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one squash
- two tablespoonfuls butter
- one egg
- one cup milk
- bread-crumbs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Boil and mash the squash
2. Stir in butter, and egg beaten light
3. Add milk, and pepper and salt to taste
4. Fill a buttered pudding-dish with this
5. Strew fine bread-crumbs over the top
6. Bake to a nice brown

title: Baked Squash
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut squash in halves
2. Remove seeds and stringy portion
3. Place in a dripping pan and cover
4. Bake 2 hours, or until soft, in a moderate oven
5. Remove from shell and mash
6. Season with butter, salt and pepper

title: Baked Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- left-over meat
- onion
- sage
- pepper and salt
- sliced raw potatoes
- gravy or water

## Directions

1. Cut meat in pieces
2. lay in baking dish
3. slice an onion
4. add a sprinkling of sage, pepper and salt to taste
5. fill dish to top with sliced raw potatoes
6. add gravy or water
7. bake in hot oven 1 1/2 hours

title: Baked Suet Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cupful of sugar
- half a cupful of suet
- one egg
- one cup of milk
- two heaping cups of flour
- three teaspoonfuls of baking powder

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients and bake
2. Eat with maple syrup

title: Baked Tamale
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small chicken or tender meat
- 2 tablespoons lard or drippings
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 cup broth
- 2 tablespoons chili powder
- 1 small cupful raisins
- about 1 cupful olives
- 3 cups cornmeal
- 1 cup flour
- salt
- 3/4 cup lard or drippings
- 1 beaten egg
- 1 cup broth

## Directions

1. Boil until tender one small chicken, or that amount of any tender meat
2. Strain off the broth, remove the bones, and break meat into small pieces
3. Put into a frying-pan 2 tablespoons of lard or drippings, and when hot add 2 tablespoons flour, and let brown a little
4. Then add the meat, salted to taste, with 1 cup broth and 2 tablespoons of chili powder
5. Stir and cook 10 minutes
6. Set off to cool, and add a small cupful raisins (whole) and about the same of olives
7. Make a stiff batter of 3 cups of cornmeal and 1 of flour, salted, into which is rubbed 3/4 of a cup of lard or drippings, moistened with 1 beaten egg and 1 cup broth
8. Line a deep pan with the batter, and bake 1 hour

title: Baked Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- pepper
- salt
- sugar
- onion-juice
- salad oil
- paprika
- Graham-bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel with a sharp knife.
2. Cut a piece from the top and gouge out most of the pulp, leaving the walls intact.
3. Season what you have removed with pepper, salt, sugar, a few drops of onion-juice, and twice as much salad oil when you have chopped the pulp rather coarsely.
4. Put it back into the tomatoes, replace the top, sprinkle with oil, paprika, and salt, and arrange upon a baking-pan.
5. Bake, covered, for twenty minutes, and uncovered for five, and serve upon buttered Graham-bread toast.

title: Baked Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash and wipe tomatoes, then cut in two
2. Place them in a baking dish with the skin side down
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Place in a hot oven
5. Take up carefully when done and put bits of butter on each piece of tomato

title: Baked Veal Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds cold roast veal
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 pepper
- nutmeg
- 6 rolled crackers
- 3 eggs
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Chop the veal fine, add salt, pepper, nutmeg, and rolled crackers.
2. If veal is thin, add a piece of butter half as large as an egg, and a tablespoon of cream.
3. Form all into a large roll and spot the roll over with bits of butter, then strew over it the pounded crackers remaining after mixing meat to proper stiffness.
4. Put in the oven and from time to time add a little water.
5. Cook slowly 2 hours and slice when cold.

title: Baker's Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- white of 1 egg
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- flour

## Directions

1. Scald 1 pint sweet milk
2. While hot add tablespoon butter
3. When milk is lukewarm add the well-beaten white of 1 egg, 3/4 cup granulated sugar, 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
4. Stir in flour and mould into a loaf
5. Let rise over night and mould three times
6. Roll out to about an inch in thickness
7. Cut into biscuits
8. Butter on half and fold over
9. Let rise and bake in a quick oven

title: Bakers' Buns
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup bread dough
- 1/2 cup sugar
- lard the size of an egg
- 1 cup lukewarm water
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix all together and add flour to make a stiff dough, but not as stiff as for bread
2. Take the cup of dough out when putting bread on the pans
3. Put it on a plate and set it away to rise for about 4 or 5 hours
4. Then add these ingredients and let rise about 3 hours more
5. Make into buns, and let it rise 9 hours more
6. Bake in a slow oven

title: Bakers' Buns (No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 mashed potatoes
- 1 teaspoon coffee sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup boiling water
- 3/4 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 3/4 cup warm water
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 1 cup sugar
- butter size of an egg
- flour

## Directions

1. At noon mix together mashed potatoes, coffee sugar, and flour
2. Scald with 1 cup boiling water
3. When cool add White Swan Yeast Cake which has been dissolved in 3/4 cup warm water
4. Let stand till night
5. Take 1 pint sweet milk, 1 cup sugar, butter size of an egg, scald together and let cool
6. Stir in yeast and flour enough to make a stiff batter
7. Let rise until morning
8. In the morning mix down as for bread
9. Let stand until light
10. Mould into buns and let rise
11. Bake 20 minutes

title: Bakers' Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 heaping teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- flour to make stiff

## Directions

1. Scald molasses and when cold add sugar, egg, cold water, vinegar, soda, and ginger
2. Add flour to make stiff
3. Roll thin and bake in a quick oven

title: Baking Powder Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 dessertspoons butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- cold water or milk

## Directions

1. Sift together 2 cups flour, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 2 dessertspoons butter, 1 tablespoon sugar
2. Mix with cold water or milk to a stiff dough with a spoon
3. Do not work much with the hands, just enough to roll out
4. Roll quickly and bake in a hot oven

title: Bales Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chopped cooked raisins
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 cup light brown sugar
- juice of 1 small lemon
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- 1 crust
- egg white

## Directions

1. mix raisins, milk, egg yolk, brown sugar, lemon juice, and cornstarch smoothly
2. add raisins, and bake in 1 crust
3. Make frosting of egg white

title: Baltimore Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon green tomatoes
- 10 large onions
- 1 head cabbage
- 1 cup salt
- 1 teaspoon tumeric powder
- 1 teaspoon celery seed
- 2 teaspoons mustard seed
- 13/4 pounds brown sugar
- 3 pints vinegar
- 1 cup horseradish grated

## Directions

1. Peel and slice tomatoes
2. Cut onions fine
3. Sprinkle 1 cup salt and let stand over night
4. Drain off water
5. Add onions, cabbage cut fine, tumeric powder, celery seed, mustard seed, brown sugar, and vinegar
6. Mix well and boil 1 hour
7. Just before bottling add grated horseradish

title: Banana Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- whites of 3 eggs
- 3/4 cup butter
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 1/4 cup cornstarch
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup flour
- sliced bananas

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Separate eggs and beat yolk, add to butter
3. Sift flour, baking powder and cornstarch, add to butter, mix and add milk
4. Add whites of eggs well beaten
5. Bake in layers
6. Just before serving put a layer of sliced bananas between the layers

title: Banana Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon banana flavoring
- sliced banana

## Directions

1. Mix 1 egg, 1 cup granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon butter, 3/4 cup sweet milk, 2 cups flour, and 2 teaspoons baking powder
2. Bake in 3 layers
3. For filling: Mix 1/4 cup granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon flour, yolk of 1 egg, 1 cup sweet milk, and 1 teaspoon banana flavoring
4. Cook filling till thick, put between layers of cake
5. Beat the white of the egg and add sugar enough to make icing
6. Cover the cake with the icing and slice banana over the top
7. Best while fresh

title: Banana Cream Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 coffee cup of milk
- 1 crust
- 2 bananas
- beaten whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a custard from the yolks of 3 eggs, 1 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon butter, 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
2. beat well and add 1 coffee cup of milk
3. bake in 1 crust
4. when done slice 2 bananas thin over the top
5. cover with the beaten whites of 2 eggs and serve fresh

title: Banana Cream Pie (Second Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sweet milk (large cups)
- 2 large tablespoons cornstarch
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 3 eggs
- little piece of butter
- little salt
- few drops oil of lemon or vanilla
- bananas

## Directions

1. cook until it thickens in double boiler
2. add few drops oil of lemon or vanilla, also a little in the icing
3. slice bananas in bottom of pie dish, and cover with the custard, with icing on top
4. let get perfectly cool before using

title: Banana Croquettes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- small yellow bananas
- pepper
- salt
- eggs
- cracker-crumbs
- cottolene

## Directions

1. Select small, yellow bananas; strip off the skins and cut off the ends, so as to make them look like croquettes.
2. Pepper and salt, roll in egg, then in cracker-crumbs, set on the ice for one hour to stiffen them.
3. Fry in hot, deep cottolene to a golden brown.
4. Serve dry and hot.

title: Banana Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- plain boiled custard

## Directions

1. Make a plain boiled custard
2. When cold, pour it over bananas sliced very thin

title: Banana Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces flour
- 1 ounce cornstarch
- 1 ounce sugar
- little milk
- bananas

## Directions

1. Make a very thin paste with flour, cornstarch, sugar and milk
2. Cut bananas into small pieces
3. Dip pieces in batter
4. Fry in plenty of smoking fat, very hot
5. Drain on paper
6. Serve hot on a napkin with sugar dusted over

title: Banana Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- a small tablespoon butter
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 1 3/4 teaspoons baking powder
- bananas
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Slice bananas thin
2. Sprinkle granulated sugar over them and put between layers

title: Banana Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- tart pie crust
- bananas
- sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Fill a tart pie crust with bananas sliced very thin
2. add a sprinkling of sugar, and cover with whipped cream

title: Banana Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 box gelatine
- 6 small bananas
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 small cupful preserved ginger, chopped
- 1 cupful walnuts, chopped fine
- 1 pt. milk
- 1/2 pt. whipped cream

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine for fifteen minutes in a cupful of cold milk.
2. Mash the bananas till smooth.
3. Mix in the chopped ginger and walnuts, add the sugar.
4. Pour the milk (boiling) on the gelatine, stir till dissolved, to which add the bananas, ginger, and walnuts.
5. Set in a bowl in a can of crushed ice or cold water.
6. Stir occasionally till the mixture begins to grow firm, then very gently fold in the whipped cream and pour into a mould which has been rubbed inside with sweet oil.
7. When firm turn out and serve with whipped cream.

title: Banana Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- boiled dressing
- finely chipped walnuts
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Peel bananas and cut in two lengthwise
2. Cut across in quarters
3. Roll each quarter in boiled dressing
4. Roll in finely chipped walnuts
5. Serve on lettuce leaves

title: Banana Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ripe bananas
- 3 oranges
- 1 apple thin sliced
- 1 cup water
- 2 teaspoons cornstarch
- ½ cup sugar

## Directions

1. Pass pulp of bananas through a colander, then a sieve
2. Add apple and water
3. When boiling, add cornstarch and sugar
4. Simmer 10 minutes
5. Add orange juices
6. Strain

title: Banana Whip
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cut-up banana
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Whip together with a silver fork about 15 minutes
2. Continue until firm and white
3. Use as a substitute for whipped cream

title: Bananas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- white wine
- custard

## Directions

1. Slice and pour over them a little white wine, leave them to soak for two hours.
2. Cover with custard made as for chocolate cream, without flavoring.

title: Banbury Tart Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup currants
- juice and rind of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat egg
2. Add juice and rind, sugar and currants
3. Bring to a boil

title: Banbury Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup sugar
- grated rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 2 tablespoons hot water

## Directions

1. Boil
2. Fill puff paste
3. Cook

title: Batter for Meat Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 6 tablespoons melted butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix egg, melted butter, and sweet milk
2. Sift flour with 3 teaspoons baking powder and add salt
3. Combine ingredients

title: Batter For Timbales
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1 egg
- 1 pt. milk
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Put the flour into a basin; drop in yolk of egg.
2. Stir in the milk by degrees.
3. Whip the white to a stiff froth and add lightly.
4. Dip your hot iron into this and fry in deep boiling lard.

title: Batter Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1/2 cup white sugar
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- any kind of fruit
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put any kind of fruit in bottom of dish
2. Pour over it batter made with 1 tablespoon butter, 1/2 cup white sugar, 1 egg, 3/4 cup sweet milk, 2 cups flour, 2 teaspoons cream tartar and 1 teaspoon soda
3. Add sugar on fruit as desired
4. Steam 1 1/2 hours
5. Serve with cream or with any sauce, as you please

title: Batter Sausage
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- milk
- salt
- baking powder
- flour
- sausages

## Directions

1. Mix together 3 eggs, 1 1/2 pints milk, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon baking powder
2. Add 1 cup flour and eggs, then add other ingredients
3. Put in pan and spread sausages on top of batter
4. Cook 1 hour

title: Bavarian Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups any fruit syrup
- 2 teaspoons Knox gelatine or 1 ounce sheet gelatine
- 1/4 cup cold water
- 2 cups cream

## Directions

1. Put gelatine to soak in cold water
2. When thoroughly softened, dissolve over hot water
3. Stir into the fruit syrup
4. Stand the syrup bowl in a pan of cold water
5. Stir occasionally while whipping the cream
6. When syrup begins to thicken, fold in whipped cream
7. Turn into a wet mould
8. Set away to get perfectly cold before serving

title: Bay Rum
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- French proof spirit, one gallon
- Extract bay, six ounces
- Caramel for coloring

## Directions

1. Mix French proof spirit and extract bay
2. Color with caramel
3. No filtering needed

title: Bean Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck butter beans
- slightly salted water
- 1 cup mustard
- 1 cup flour
- 2 tablespoons turmeric
- cold vinegar
- 2 3/4 pints boiling vinegar
- 2 1/2 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Take ends off beans
2. cook 1 hour in slightly salted water
3. drain
4. prepare dressing: 1 cup mustard, 1 cup flour, 2 tablespoons turmeric, mix with a little cold vinegar to a batter
5. add 2 3/4 pints boiling vinegar in which has been dissolved 2 1/2 pounds sugar
6. mix all together, mix 5 minutes
7. put in glass jars and seal
8. Pick nice yellow beans when tender

title: Bean Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint white beans
- salt and pepper to taste
- left-over meat scraps
- 1 small cup vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash a pint of white beans
2. boil in soft water till tender, but not done
3. take any left-over meat scraps, mince fine
4. add salt, pepper to taste, a small cup of vinegar
5. put all in a bake dish and bake 1 hour
6. serve hot

title: Bean Polenta
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- 3 tablespoons molasses for every 2 quarts
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- egg and flour

## Directions

1. Wash beans, cook 3 hours until soft and mealy
2. for every 2 quarts add 3 tablespoons molasses, 1 teaspoon salt, 3/4 teaspoon pepper, 1 teaspoon mustard, 1 tablespoon butter, 2 tablespoons vinegar
3. cook 10 minutes
4. stir good, do not scorch, should be dry and mealy
5. The above is nice mashed, mixed with egg and flour and fried

title: Bean Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 onions
- 2 pints cold cooked beans
- 1 teaspoonful pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls salad oil
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 1 scant cup vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- Olives
- Two hard-boiled eggs
- Lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Peel and slice onions very fine. Pour cold water over them and press hard to remove the strong taste. Drain well.
2. Have ready in the salad bowl cold boiled or baked beans, preferably the latter.
3. Mix with them the minced onion, salt, pepper, salad oil, sugar and vinegar.
4. Toss and mix with a fork lightly, but thoroughly, and garnish with olives, two hard-boiled eggs sliced and lettuce leaves.

title: Bean Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup beans
- luke-warm water
- 2 eggs
- flour
- salt
- milk
- biscuits

## Directions

1. Wash beans, soak in luke-warm water all night
2. Drain water off in morning, boil until tender
3. Beat eggs, add flour to make a thin paste
4. Drop from spoon, let come to a boil
5. Add salt, milk and biscuits to taste

title: Bean Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of beans
- 1 quart of cold water
- one slice of onion
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- 1 stalk of celery
- 1 cup white sauce
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup hot milk
- salt
- pepper
- a bit of mustard
- a very thin slice of lemon

## Directions

1. Soak 1 cup of beans over night, drain and put on to boil in 1 quart of cold water
2. Fry one slice of onion in 1 tablespoon of butter until slightly brown, and add, with 1 stalk of celery, to the beans
3. Let simmer till very soft, adding a little cold water occasionally to check the boiling and help soften the beans
4. Rub through a soup strainer, put over fire and when hot, add 1 cup white sauce (made of 1 tablespoon each of flour and butter, cooked together and thinned with 1 cup hot milk)
5. Season with salt, pepper and a bit of mustard, and serve with a very thin slice of lemon in each plate

title: Bean Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup beans
- 2 eggs
- flour
- salt
- milk
- biscuits

## Directions

1. Wash a cup of beans, soak in lukewarm water all night
2. Drain water off in morning, boil until tender
3. Take 2 eggs, beat, add flour to make a thin paste
4. Drop from spoon, let come to a boil
5. Then add salt, milk and biscuits to taste

title: Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups common beans
- melted butter
- pepper
- salt
- liquid from a can of tomatoes
- ground peanuts
- bread or cracker crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil 2 cups common beans until they crack
2. Drain
3. Add melted butter, pepper and salt
4. Mash with potato masher
5. Put in your pudding pan or baker
6. Add the liquid from a can of tomatoes
7. Cover thickly with ground peanuts
8. Then a layer of bread or cracker crumbs with dots of butter
9. Bake in oven until brown
10. Good either hot or cold

title: Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint or more of beans
- water
- celery tops
- parsley
- onion
- celery salt or table salt

## Directions

1. Cover beans with water
2. Heat and boil from 10 to 30 minutes
3. Drain after this parboiling
4. Now cover well with water
5. Flavor with celery tops, parsley or onion, using one or all to suit taste
6. Season with celery salt (table salt may be used)
7. If desired for soup, boil till soft
8. If for baking, when the skins curl back they are ready for the oven

title: Beef Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds raw beef
- suet
- salt
- pepper
- a handful of flour
- hot drippings

## Directions

1. Chop very fine the beef with some suet
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Mix in a handful of flour
4. Mould into balls
5. Fry in hot drippings to a nice brown on both sides

title: Beef Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. under-cooked roast beef
- Salt and pepper
- 1/2 lb. of ham or bacon
- 1 large egg
- 1 teaspoonful of sweet herbs
- white of egg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Mince the beef and ham, add herbs
2. Mix with the egg, which must be previously well beaten
3. Brush each cake over with a little white of egg
4. Cover with breadcrumbs
5. Fry quickly for five minutes

title: Beef Cecils
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups cold beef
- 2 cups bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- a small onion
- a little sage
- pepper
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Chop cold beef
2. Add bread crumbs
3. Add egg
4. Add small onion chopped fine
5. Add a little sage, pepper and salt to taste
6. Add enough milk to moisten the mixture so it will hold together in balls
7. Flatten into balls
8. Fry in drippings or butter

title: Beef Cottage Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- old beef scraps
- small onion
- salt and pepper
- gravy or butter
- boiling water
- mashed potatoes
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop beef scraps fine with a small onion, salt and pepper
2. Place meat in a deep dish moistened with gravy or a little butter dissolved in boiling water
3. Put enough mashed potatoes over the top to make a thick cover over the meat
4. Put a few bits of butter over the potatoes before putting into the oven
5. Bake a light brown

title: Beef Croquettes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Minced cold beef
- 1/2 as much mashed potato
- Gravy enough to moisten them, in which an onion has been cooked
- Season with catsup, pepper, salt and a pinch of marjoram
- Fine breadcrumbs
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mash the potatoes, while hot, very smooth, or if cold potatoes be used, see they are free from lumps
2. Mix in the meat, gravy, and seasoning
3. Bind all together with the beaten egg and form into the desired shapes
4. Roll them in fine breadcrumbs
5. Fry quickly to a light brown
6. Drain on soft paper before the fire till free from fat
7. Serve hot

title: Beef Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- cold beef
- cracker crumbs
- 1 egg
- pepper
- salt
- onion
- hot water or meat gravy

## Directions

1. Chop the bits of cold beef very fine
2. To 1 cup of chopped meat add 1 cup of cracker crumbs
3. Add 1 egg, pepper and salt
4. Add a little chopped onion if desired
5. Add a little hot water or meat gravy if needed to make crumbs stick together
6. Make into little cakes
7. Fry in hot buttered pan

title: Beef Croquettes (Method 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint cold cooked beef
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon onion juice
- a dash of cayenne
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- a grating of nutmeg
- 1/2 pint milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Chop sufficient cold cooked beef to make 1 pint
2. Add to it 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon onion juice, a dash of cayenne, 3/4 teaspoon pepper, and a grating of nutmeg
3. Put half a pint of milk over the fire
4. Rub together 1 tablespoon butter and 2 tablespoons flour
5. Add to the hot milk and stir until you have a smooth, thick paste
6. Take from the fire and mix with it the meat
7. Let cool
8. When cool, form in croquettes
9. Beat 1 egg and add to it

title: Beef Loaf
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of beefsteak
- fifteen soda biscuits
- half a cupful of sweet milk
- half a cupful of butter
- one teaspoonful of salt and pepper
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the beefsteak fine.
2. Roll the soda biscuits well.
3. Mix all ingredients well in a loaf and bake two hours.

title: Beef Loaf
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of beefsteak, chopped fine
- Fifteen soda biscuits, well rolled
- Half a cupful of sweet milk
- Half a cupful of butter
- One teaspoonful of salt and pepper
- Two eggs

## Directions

1. Mix well in a loaf
2. Bake two hours

title: Beef Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 3/4 pounds beefsteak
- 2 eggs
- 2 soda biscuits
- 1/2 cup cream
- 6 large chopped onions
- small piece of suet chopped fine
- 1/2 cup butter
- chopped parsley
- sage
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Put beefsteak through grinder
2. Crumble soda biscuits
3. Chop parsley and sage
4. Mix all ingredients together
5. Bake in oven about 2 hours

title: Beef Loaf (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds raw beef
- 1/2 pound suet
- 1 pound bread crumbs
- 4 tablespoons sweet cream
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 2 teaspoons summer savory
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Grind beef and suet
2. Mix with bread crumbs, sweet cream, and eggs
3. Add butter, summer savory, pepper, and salt
4. Work into a loaf, using flour to bind the ingredients together
5. Bake in a pan for 2 hours
6. Baste frequently with melted butter or hot water

title: Beef Loaf (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds beef
- 3/4 pound lean pork
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- water
- 1 small onion
- pepper
- salt
- sage

## Directions

1. Put beef through grinder
2. Put pork through grinder
3. Soak bread crumbs in water
4. Mix beef, pork, eggs, bread crumbs, onion, pepper, salt, and sage
5. Bake 1 1/2 hours

title: Beef Loaf (4)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lean beef
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- 2 tablespoons dripping

## Directions

1. Chop beef fine
2. Put egg into a bowl
3. Beat egg just enough to bring yolk and white together
4. Add dripping
5. Mix together and pour it over the meat
6. Mix the whole well together
7. Wet the hands and form the meat into a roll
8. Bake in a hot oven 30 minutes
9. Baste every 10 minutes

title: Beef Loaf (5)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds beef
- 4 biscuits
- 1 egg
- 1 cup milk
- butter size of egg
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon pepper

## Directions

1. Chop beef fine and uncooked
2. Crush biscuits fine
3. Mix all ingredients
4. Shape into loaf
5. Bake in slow oven 2 1/2 hours
6. Baste often
7. Serve cold for lunch

title: Beef Olives
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of undercooked roast beef
- breadcrumbs
- sweet herbs
- pepper and salt
- gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the slices of meat very thin, spread upon each slice a stuffing made from the above ingredients, roll up tightly, and tie with string
2. Have ready in a saucepan some good brown gravy, lay in the olives, and let them simmer for about half an hour
3. Take up, remove the string carefully that the shape may not be spoiled, pour the gravy over, and serve hot

title: Beef Olives
body:
## Ingredients

- chunk steak 1/2 inch thick
- stuffing
- brown sauce
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut chunk steak into pieces 4 inches square
2. Season both sides
3. Put stuffing on pieces and roll
4. Tie pieces
5. Put in double roasting pan with brown sauce underneath
6. Cook in aladdin or slow oven 2 hours
7. Season with salt and serve

title: Beef Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds round beef (uncooked) chopped fine
- 6 eggs beaten together
- 6 soda crackers rolled fine
- little butter and suet
- pepper
- salt
- sage

## Directions

1. Mix chopped beef, eggs, crackers, butter, suet, pepper, salt, and sage
2. Make 2 loaves
3. Roll in crackers
4. Bake about an hour
5. Slice when cold

title: Beef Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dessertspoon dripping
- 1 onion sliced
- 3/4 pound beefsteak
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 2 pints water
- salt and pepper
- potato and carrot
- 3 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Put dripping in a pan and heat until smoking hot
2. Add sliced onion
3. Cut beefsteak into bits and brown
4. Shake in flour and brown
5. Add water and stir slowly
6. Season and cover
7. Simmer gently for 2 hours
8. Add bits of potato and carrot if liked
9. For biscuits: sift 3 cups flour with 2 teaspoons baking powder and 1/2 teaspoon salt
10. Rub in 1 tablespoon butter and make into dough with sweet milk
11. Divide dough in two and pat out (do not knead or roll)
12. Cut as you would pie and bake
13. Break open biscuits on a platter and pour meat mixture over
14. Serve

title: Beef Pie (Cold Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast beef
- cold potatoes and onions
- 1 cup rich gravy
- 1 cup boiling water
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Slice meat in small pieces and also potatoes
2. Put a layer of meat, then a layer of potatoes, then a little onion, pepper and salt
3. Continue until dish is full
4. Add one cup rich gravy and 1 cup boiling water
5. Make a crust as you would for biscuits, only a little richer
6. Cover pie and bake in medium oven for 3/4 hour

title: Beef Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds beef
- 1 quart flour
- 0.5 pound suet
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- salt and pepper
- flour
- water
- chopped onion
- sage herbs

## Directions

1. Cut beef into small pieces
2. Chop suet very fine and add to flour and salt
3. Mix into a stiff paste with a knife
4. Roll out and line a bowl with the paste
5. Fill in the beef with salt and pepper to taste
6. Dust with flour and add a little water
7. Cover the top with paste and join the edges well
8. Tie down with a cloth and plunge into boiling water
9. Keep boiling for 4 hours
10. Optional: put in a chopped onion and a little sage herbs if liked

title: Beef Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound beef
- 1 quart flour
- 1/2 pound suet
- 1 teaspoon salt
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- a little water

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into small pieces
2. Chop suet very fine, and add to flour, and salt and mix into a stiff paste with a knife
3. Roll out and line a bowl with the paste
4. Fill in the beef, with salt and pepper to taste
5. Dust with flour
6. Put in a little water
7. Cover the top with paste, and join the edges well
8. Tie down into a cloth and plunge into boiling water
9. Keep boiling for 4 hours

title: Beef Roll
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce lean beef from shoulder or shin
- 2 ounces sausage meat
- crumbs
- salt
- pepper
- 1 slice onion
- teaspoon Worcestershire sauce
- 2 teaspoons catsup
- flour
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix sausage meat with equal quantity of crumbs
2. Cut meat in slices 1/4 inch thick and spread on sausage
3. Roll up and tie firmly
4. Salt, pepper and dredge thickly with flour
5. Put into earthenware dish with other ingredients
6. Cover with boiling water and cook 5 hours in aladdin pail

title: Beef Tea
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of lean beef
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut beef into small pieces
2. Put into a glass canning-jar without a drop of water
3. Cover tightly, and set in a pot of cold water
4. Heat gradually to a boil, and continue this steadily for three or four hours, until the meat is like white rags, and the juice all drawn out
5. Season with salt to taste, and when cold, skim

title: Beef, Oatmeal and Tomato
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound shin beef
- 2 sausages
- 1 cup tomatoes
- 1 cup oatmeal
- 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce
- salt and pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut beef in small pieces and season with salt and pepper
2. Cut sausages in 1-inch pieces and roll in flour
3. Put sausages and beef in earthen dish
4. Add tomatoes, oatmeal, and Worcestershire sauce
5. Cook 5 hours in aladdin or slow oven

title: Beefsteak And Mutton Chops
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- tenderest cuts of beefsteak and mutton chops
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Select the tenderest cuts, and broil over a clear, hot fire
2. Let the steak be rare, the chops well done
3. Salt and pepper
4. Lay between two hot plates three minutes, and serve to your patient
5. If he is very weak, do not let him swallow anything except the juice, when he has chewed the meat well

title: Beefsteak Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup baking syrup
- 1/2 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/2 cup buttermilk
- 1 good teaspoon soda
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix 1/2 cup butter and 3/4 cup brown sugar
2. Add 2 eggs, 1/4 cup baking syrup, 1/2 cup currants, 1 teaspoon cloves, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 1/2 cup buttermilk, 1 good teaspoon soda, a little salt
3. Add flour enough to make not too stiff a batter
4. Bake

title: Beefsteak Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pound steak
- 3 or 4 medium-sized potatoes
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon flour
- butter
- 1 quart flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 0.75 cup lard
- salt
- 1 cup sour milk

## Directions

1. Cut steak in small pieces and stew till tender
2. Cut potatoes in small pieces and add to steak
3. Cook together until done and remove
4. Make a thickening for broth of 1 cup milk and 1 tablespoon flour, a little butter and season to taste
5. Make a crust of 1 quart flour, 1 teaspoon baking powder, 1 teaspoon soda, 0.75 cup lard, a pinch of salt and 1 cup sour milk
6. Mix and line a dish with crust
7. Fill with mixture, having plenty of broth to cover beef and potatoes
8. Cover the top with a crust and cut a hole in top crust
9. Bake to a golden brown in a moderate oven

title: Beefsteak With Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound chopped raw beef
- 1 chopped onion
- pepper
- salt
- butter, size of walnut

## Directions

1. Make into small balls
2. Flatten these
3. Fry in butter until brown on both sides

title: Beentwood Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. breadcrumbs
- 3 oz. butter
- 3 oz. sugar
- Yolks of 3 eggs
- Juice of 2 lemons and grated rind of one

## Directions

1. Line the dish with pastry, put a layer of jam at the bottom, over which pour the above mixture.
2. Bake forty minutes.
3. When it has been in the oven thirty minutes pour over it the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth.

title: Beet and Cabbage Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart boiled beets chopped fine
- 2 quarts raw cabbage cut fine
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup ground horseradish
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon black pepper
- 3/4 tablespoon red pepper
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Cover with cold vinegar and use

title: Beet Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart beets, boiled and chopped fine
- 1 pint celery, chopped fine
- 1 small head cabbage, chopped fine
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- 1 cup grated horseradish
- 3/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Combine beets, celery, and cabbage
2. Add sugar, salt, black pepper, horseradish, and cayenne pepper
3. Cover with cold vinegar
4. Keep from the air

title: Beet Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart beets
- 1 pint celery
- 1 small head cabbage
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- 1 cup grated horseradish
- 3/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil and chop fine the beets
2. Chop fine the celery
3. Chop fine the cabbage
4. Mix all ingredients
5. Cover with cold vinegar

title: Beet Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart chopped cabbage
- 1 quart boiled beets
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 cup grated horseradish
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix 1 quart chopped cabbage, 1 quart boiled beets, 2 cups brown sugar, 1 tablespoon salt, 1 teaspoon pepper, 1 cup grated horseradish
2. cover with cold vinegar, and keep from the air

title: Beet Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a dozen young beets
- Vinegar
- Sugar
- A couple of bunches of chives
- Three stalks of celery
- Celery leaves
- Salad dressing

## Directions

1. Boil half a dozen young beets, chop fine and pour over them warm vinegar with a little sugar in it.
2. Slice very thin a couple of bunches of chives, and chop fine three stalks of celery.
3. In serving make a foundation of celery leaves, and on it place first the thin slices of chives, then a layer of beets, then chives, and sprinkle over them the chopped celery.
4. Pour over all a salad dressing.

title: Beet Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen young beets
- warm vinegar
- a little sugar
- a couple of bunches of chives
- three stalks of celery
- celery leaves
- salad dressing

## Directions

1. Boil half a dozen young beets, chop fine and pour over them warm vinegar with a little sugar in it.
2. Slice very thin a couple of bunches of chives, and chop fine three stalks of celery.
3. In serving make a foundation of celery leaves, and on it place first the thin slices of chives, then a layer of beets, then chives, and sprinkle over them the chopped celery.
4. Pour over all a salad dressing.

title: Beet Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 stalk celery to 6 beets
- beets
- celery
- 2 teaspoons mustard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 cup vinegar
- 1 tablespoon Worcester sauce
- 3 eggs
- 3/4 cup milk
- butter
- 1 teaspoon pepper

## Directions

1. Boil beets
2. chop celery and beets both fine
3. prepare dressing: 2 teaspoons mustard, 1 teaspoon salt, 3/4 cup vinegar, 1 tablespoon Worcester sauce, 3 eggs, 3/4 cup milk, a little butter, 1 teaspoon pepper
4. mix dressing with beets and celery

title: Beet Salad (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart boiled beets
- 1 pint celery
- 1 pint cabbage
- 1 cup horseradish
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon red pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop all ingredients
2. cover with vinegar and keep from air

title: Beet Salad (Variation 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart chopped cabbage
- 1 quart boiled beets chopped
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup grated horseradish
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon red pepper
- 1 tablespoon salt
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix 1 quart chopped cabbage, 1 quart boiled beets chopped, 2 cups sugar, 1 cup grated horseradish, 1 teaspoon black pepper, 3/4 teaspoon red pepper, 1 tablespoon salt
2. cover with cold vinegar, put in a crock and cover
3. Will keep all winter

title: Beet-Tops (German)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound cold boiled ham
- salad oil
- 1/2 grated onion
- 2 tablespoonfuls hot vinegar
- beet greens
- salt
- water
- poached eggs

## Directions

1. Cut half a pound of cold boiled ham into dice and fry in a little salad oil with half a grated onion.
2. Add two tablespoonfuls of hot vinegar, and set in hot water while you wash, pick over, and boil the greens in hot, salted water.
3. Fifteen minutes should make them tender.
4. Chop fine, drain well, and mix with the fried ham and vinegar.
5. Dish hot with poached eggs on top of the greens.

title: Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean nicely, but do not pare them, leaving on a short piece of its stalk
2. put over, boil in hot water
3. Young beets will cook tender in an hour, old beets require several hours boiling
4. When done, skin quickly while hot
5. slice thin into your vegetable dish
6. put on salt, pepper and a little butter
7. put over a little vinegar and serve hot or cold

title: Belfast Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 egg beaten
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 cups Graham flour
- 1/4 cup white flour
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix 3/4 cup butter and 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
2. Add 1 cup raisins and 1 egg beaten
3. Add 1 cup sour milk with 1 teaspoon soda
4. Add 2 cups Graham flour, 1/4 cup white flour, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, and 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg
5. Bake

title: Belfasts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1/4 nutmeg
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup seeded raisins or 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 7 1/2 cups Graham flour

## Directions

1. Mix 1 egg, 1 cup white sugar, 1/2 cup butter, 1 cup buttermilk, 1/4 nutmeg, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 cup seeded raisins or 1 cup currants, 1 teaspoon soda, and 7 1/2 cups Graham flour
2. Bake in gem rings

title: Bengal Chutney
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. garlic, chopped
- 1 lb. onions, chopped
- 1 lb. raisins, chopped
- 1 lb. brown sugar
- 1 lb. salt
- 1 lb. ground mustard
- 1 lb. ground ginger
- 1 oz. cayenne pepper
- 3 pts. vinegar
- 13 large sour apples peeled and cored
- 13 large tomatoes

## Directions

1. Cook apples and tomatoes together and mash.
2. Mix all together.
3. Cook till thoroughly soft, bottle.

title: Bengal Chutney
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1/2 pound salt
- 3/4 pound mustard seed
- 3/4 pound onions chopped fine
- 3/4 pound stoned raisins chopped fine
- 3/4 teaspoon red pepper
- heaping teaspoon ginger
- 2 ounces ground mustard
- 1 ounce tumeric
- 13 large apples
- 9 large tomatoes
- 3 pints good vinegar

## Directions

1. Pare, quarter, and core apples
2. Peel tomatoes
3. Cook and put through colander
4. Boil mustard seed in vinegar
5. Strain out seed and add vinegar
6. Add spices to pulp and boil all together
7. Stir constantly to prevent burning
8. Lastly add tumeric
9. When cold, bottle and cork

title: Berkshire Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup cornmeal
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1/2 cup cooked rice
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 cup scalded milk
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 3 tablespoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Turn milk on meal, let it stand 5 minutes
2. Add rice and dry material
3. Add beaten yolk, butter and white of egg, beaten stiff

title: Berlin Kidney
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lamb's kidneys
- 1 cup green peas
- 1/2 onion, chopped
- 2 tablespoonfuls Worcestershire
- 1 cup gravy with stock
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut lamb kidneys, skin and fry in butter for a few minutes
2. Mix all together until thoroughly heated

title: Berliner Lobster
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fresh lobster
- Butter size of egg
- Yolks 3 eggs
- 1/2 pt. cream
- 1 small glass sherry
- Salt and paprica to taste

## Directions

1. Cut lobster into small pieces, put in butter, melt quickly in double boiler
2. Beat up yolks eggs with cream
3. Beat slowly while standing in another dish of hot water till creamy, then take off the fire
4. Add salt and paprica to taste and small glass of best sherry
5. Pour over lobster and serve quickly and hot

title: Bermuda Onions (Stuffed)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- large Bermuda or Spanish onions
- force-meat of minced meat, veal, ham, or chicken
- fine crumbs
- salt
- cayenne
- butter
- crumbs
- weak stock

## Directions

1. Peel large Bermuda or Spanish onions, and parboil them for ten minutes.
2. Drain, and let them get perfectly cold.
3. With a sharp knife dig out the centre from each and fill with a force-meat of minced meat, veal, ham, or chicken, well seasoned, and mixed with one-third as much fine crumbs.
4. Season with salt and cayenne and a little butter.
5. Set the stuffed onions close together in a dish, fill the interstices with crumbs, and scatter more over the top.
6. Pour about them enough weak stock to keep them from burning — about an inch in the bottom of the dish will do — and cook, covered, half an hour.
7. Uncover and brown lightly.

title: Berry Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 cup raspberries
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix 2 eggs, 1/2 cup sugar, 1/4 cup butter, 1 cup raspberries, 1 teaspoon soda, and 1 1/2 cups flour
2. Bake in layers

title: Berry Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 cup cooked berries
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix 2 eggs, 3/4 cup granulated sugar, 3/4 cup butter, 1 cup cooked berries, 1 1/2 cups flour, and 1 teaspoon soda
2. Bake in loaf

title: Berry Pudding (Steamed)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint flour
- 1 teaspoon Magic baking powder
- a pinch of salt
- milk
- berries

## Directions

1. Make into a soft batter with milk
2. Put into well-buttered cups a spoonful of batter, then one of berries, then another of batter
3. Steam

title: Bessie Molasses Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup dark molasses
- 3/4 cup shortening
- 1 cup raisins chopped fine
- 1 cup hot water
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 4 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup brown sugar, 1 cup dark molasses, and 3/4 cup shortening
2. Add 1 cup raisins chopped fine
3. Dissolve 1 teaspoon soda in 1 cup hot water and add to mixture
4. Add 4 cups flour
5. Bake in long pan
6. Ice with icing

title: Betsy Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of milk
- Three ounces breadcrumbs
- One egg
- One tablespoonful of white sugar
- Jam
- A little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Spread a good layer of jam in the bottom of a pie-dish.
2. Pour the milk nearly boiling on to the breadcrumbs.
3. When cool stir in the egg, which should be beaten.
4. Pour this gently on the preserve.
5. Grate a little nutmeg over the top, and bake for half an hour.

title: Beverage
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lemons
- 1 oz. tartaric acid
- 3 lbs. sugar
- 2 bottles ginger ale

## Directions

1. Pare rind of lemons as thin as possible, put rinds, white sugar and tartaric into a jar and cover with quart boiling water, and stir till sugar is melted.
2. When cold add the juice of the lemons and two quarts of cold water.
3. When about to serve add two bottles of ginger ale.

title: Birthday Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 2 cups flour
- whites of 3 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- small teaspoon salt
- nutmeg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1/4 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup cocoanut
- 2 teaspoons cornstarch

## Directions

1. Beat 1 cup sugar and 1 cup sweet cream together first
2. Add 2 cups flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder next
3. Add small teaspoon salt and a little nutmeg
4. Add whites of 3 eggs well beaten and mix quickly
5. Bake in 3 layers
6. For the filling: boil 1 cup sweet milk, 1/4 cup sugar, and 3/4 cup cocoanut together
7. When boiling, stir in 2 teaspoons cornstarch wet with cold milk

title: Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 dessertspoons sugar
- 1 small teaspoon salt
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 3/4 cup currants
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar

## Directions

1. Sift flour, cream tartar, soda and salt together
2. Add sugar and currants
3. Mix with a spoon stiff enough to roll out
4. Leave for 5 minutes
5. Cut into cakes, put in the pan
6. Leave 5 minutes more
7. Bake in a hot oven; if the fire is good will cook in 5 or 10 minutes

title: Biscuits 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 large tablespoons butter
- about 2 cups sweet milk

## Directions

1. Sift together flour, cream tartar, soda and salt
2. Work into the flour 2 large tablespoons of butter
3. Add about 2 cups of sweet milk (mixing with a knife) until you have a soft dough
4. Turn out on the mixing board but do not knead
5. Pat into shape, roll out, cut into large biscuits
6. Bake in a very hot oven

title: Bitter Orange Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve Seville oranges
- three pints of cold water per pound of fruit
- one pound granulated sugar per pound of fruit and juice

## Directions

1. Slice twelve Seville oranges into a large bowl, leaving out pips.
2. To each pound of fruit add three pints of cold water and let stand twelve or eighteen hours.
3. Then boil gently till soft, about thirty minutes.
4. Let stand again till next day.
5. Then weigh and to every pound of fruit and juice add one pound granulated sugar.
6. Boil till clear and thick.

title: Black and White Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Light Part: 1 cup white sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- whites of 3 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 2 small spoons baking powder
- 1 cup chopped nuts, or 5 cents' worth of preserved cherries
- Dark Part: 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons syrup
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 2 3/4 cups flour
- 1 cup seeded raisins
- spices and vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Pour the dark part in a pan and the light part over it

title: Black Hill Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- white of egg
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour
- yolk of egg
- 1 tablespoon blackstrap
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 teaspoon flour

## Directions

1. Cream together 1 cup sugar and 1 tablespoon butter
2. Add white of egg beaten to a froth
3. Add 1/4 cup sweet milk, 2 teaspoons baking powder and enough flour to stiffen
4. Leave 3 tablespoons of light part, add yolk of egg, 1 tablespoon blackstrap, 1 teaspoon vanilla, 1 teaspoon flour
5. Bake in hot oven

title: Black Pepper Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup blackstrap
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 3/4 cup sour milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup currants, 1 cup blackstrap, 1 cup brown sugar, 1/4 cup butter, 3/4 cup sour milk, 2 eggs, 1 teaspoon pepper, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 teaspoon cloves, 1 teaspoon soda, 2 teaspoons cream tartar, and 2 cups flour
2. Bake in layers

title: Black Pepper Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound suet
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- a little salt
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- cold water

## Directions

1. Mix stiff with cold water
2. Tie in a floured cloth
3. Boil 2 hours
4. Serve with all kinds of hot meat dinners

title: Black Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup molasses
- 3/4 cup cold water
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon

## Directions

1. Steam for 2 hours

title: Black, or After-Dinner Coffee
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cupful of freshly ground coffee
- three large cupfuls of freshly boiled water

## Directions

1. Make as directed in last recipe, running through the filter three times.
2. Serve in small cups, and give the drinkers their choice of sugar or no sugar. Black coffee is a good digestive agent and is far more wholesome than coffee mixed with cream or milk.

title: Black-Eyed Peas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- black-eyed peas
- fat bacon
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. They are boiled always with a bit of fat bacon, to give them richness.
2. Drain well, pepper, salt, and serve with the bacon on the top of the peas; or, after they are boiled they are drained and turned into a frying-pan in which slices of fat bacon have been cooked and then taken out, leaving the fat in the pan.
3. Saute the peas in this until dry, hot, and well seasoned by the fat.
4. Serve dry, and lay the fried bacon on or about the peas.
5. Dried black-eyed peas must be soaked over night.

title: Blackberry and Apple Jam
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- blackberries
- ½ lb. sliced apple per pound of berries
- 1 lemon per 6 pounds of berries, very thinly sliced
- equal weight of fruit and sugar

## Directions

1. Pick over your blackberries and weigh them
2. To each pound of berries put half a pound of sliced apple
3. To every six pounds of berries add one lemon, very thinly sliced
4. Put the fruit on and let it boil steadily nearly half an hour
5. Add the sugar by degrees, and boil about twenty minutes

title: Blackberry Cordial
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart blackberry juice
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 teaspoonfuls grated nutmeg
- 2 teaspoonfuls cinnamon
- 2 teaspoonfuls allspice
- 1 teaspoonful cloves
- 1 pint good brandy

## Directions

1. Pound and squeeze enough blackberries through a coarse muslin bag to make a quart of juice
2. Put this into an agate-iron or porcelain-lined kettle, with a pound of sugar
3. Tie the spices up in little thin muslin bags
4. Stir the sugar until dissolved
5. Set over the fire and cook together, after the boil begins, fifteen minutes
6. Take off the scum
7. Turn into a jar, and cover closely while it cools
8. When perfectly cold strain out the spices
9. Add a pint of good brandy
10. Bottle and seal

title: Blackberry Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of fruit
- 3/4 pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Mash fruit and sugar separately
2. Put together
3. Boil 3/4 to 1 hour

title: Blackstrap Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup blackstrap
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 3/4 cup sour milk (sweet will do)
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- a little over 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Bake in layers

title: Blanquette of Turkey
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. cooked turkey cut in small pieces
- 1 large cup stock
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 heaping tablespoonful of flour
- 1 teaspoonful lemon juice
- 1 cupful milk or cream
- yolks of four eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put the butter in the sauce-pan and when hot add the flour.
2. Stir until smooth, but not brown.
3. Add the stock, and cook two minutes.
4. Add the seasoning and cream.
5. As soon as this boils up add the turkey.
6. Cook ten minutes.
7. Beat the yolks of the eggs with four tablespoonfuls of milk.
8. Stir into the blanquette.
9. Cook about half a minute longer.
10. This can be served in a rice or potato border.

title: Blanquette of Veal
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls chopped cooked veal
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 cupful of stock
- 1 cupful of cream
- Yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 12 button mushrooms
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Melt the butter, add flour, stir until smooth, add the liquid, and when the sauce thickens add meat and mushrooms.
2. Cook all together for a few minutes.
3. When ready to serve add yolks of eggs and parsley, cooking for a minute; garnish with whole mushrooms.

title: Blueberry Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 2 large tablespoonfuls sugar
- A little salt
- 1 cup milk (sour)
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- Flour to make stiff batter
- 1 large pint blueberries

## Directions

1. Stir in a large pint blueberries
2. Mix and put in buttered basin
3. Steam one hour and fifteen minutes
4. Serve hot with whipped cream

title: Boiled and Baked Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- raw egg white
- sugar
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil your ham tender
2. Cover it with the white of a raw egg and sprinkle sugar or bread crumbs over it
3. Put it in the oven and brown
4. It is also delicious covered with a regular cake icing and browned

title: Boiled Artichokes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- salt
- water
- drawn butter or sauce piquante

## Directions

1. Pare off the stems and the lower and coarser leaves
2. With a sharp knife trim the tops evenly, and take out the hard core
3. Wash and lay in cold water ten minutes
4. Shake off the wet and cook in boiling, salted water for thirty-five minutes, or until the bottoms are tender
5. If large, cut into halves; if of moderate size, serve whole with drawn butter or sauce piquante poured over them

title: Boiled Asparagus
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt

## Directions

1. Scrape the stalks and lay them in cold water for half an hour.
2. Tie into rather loose bundles with soft string, and cook in hot, salted water for half an hour.
3. If desired, undo the strings and arrange the stalks upon a hot dish.

title: Boiled Asparagus
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus stalks
- salt
- white sauce or Hollandaise sauce or melted butter

## Directions

1. Scrape the stalks and lay them in cold water for half an hour
2. Tie into rather loose bundles with soft string
3. Cook in hot, salted water for half an hour
4. Undo the strings and arrange the stalks upon a hot dish
5. Pour white or Hollandaise sauce over it, or pass this separately, or serve melted butter with it

title: Boiled Bass
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- vinegar
- salt
- onion
- black peppers
- blade of mace
- fish
- butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Put enough water in the pot for the fish to swim in easily.
2. Add half a cupful of vinegar, a teaspoonful of salt, an onion, a dozen black peppers, and a blade of mace.
3. Sew up the fish in a piece of clean mosquito-netting, fitted to its shape.
4. Heat slowly for the first half hour, then boil twelve minutes to the pound, quite fast.
5. Unwrap, and pour over it a cup of drawn-butter, based upon the liquor in which the fish was boiled, with the juice of half a lemon stirred into it.
6. Garnish with sliced lemon.

title: Boiled Bass
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Bass
- Water
- Half a cupful of vinegar
- Teaspoonful of salt
- An onion
- A dozen black peppers
- A blade of mace
- Clean mosquito-netting
- Drawn-butter

## Directions

1. Put enough water in the pot for the fish to swim in easily
2. Add half a cupful of vinegar, a teaspoonful of salt, an onion, a dozen black peppers, and a blade of mace
3. Sew up the fish in a piece of clean mosquito-netting, fitted to its shape
4. Heat slowly for the first half hour, then boil twelve minutes to the pound, quite fast
5. Unwrap, and pour over it a cup of drawn-butter, based upon the liquor in which the fish was boiled

title: Boiled Brains
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's or bullock's brains
- pepper and salt
- marjoram or sage
- sippets of toasted bread
- melted butter sauce

## Directions

1. Steep the brains in lukewarm water for two hours to draw the blood
2. then tie in muslin, put into boiling water and boil for twenty minutes
3. take them up, drain in a colander, then turn into a basin and beat thoroughly with a fork
4. season with plenty of pepper and salt and a little marjoram or sage — sage is best
5. Put on a hot dish, pour over a good melted butter sauce, and garnish with sippets of toast

title: Boiled Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup water
- 1/4 cup lard
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 3/4 teaspoon cloves
- salt
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Boil it and then cool
2. add 2 cups flour, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 3/4 teaspoon cloves, salt, 1 teaspoon soda

title: Boiled Cauliflower
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- hot, salted water
- a tablespoonful of vinegar
- a cupful of drawn butter
- lemon-juice
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower, tied in a net, in plenty of hot, salted water, in which has been stirred a tablespoonful of vinegar
2. When done, drain and dish, the flower upward
3. Pour over it a cupful of drawn butter seasoned with lemon-juice, pepper, and salt
4. Serve very hot

title: Boiled Cauliflower with Tomato Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- hot, salted water
- vinegar
- a cupful of strained tomato sauce
- butter
- sugar
- salt
- paprica

## Directions

1. Cook as directed in the boiled cauliflower recipe
2. When dished pour over it, instead of the white sauce, a cupful of strained tomato sauce, seasoned with butter, sugar, salt, and paprica

title: Boiled Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- cheesecloth
- boiled rice
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- celery
- white sauce
- chopped parsley
- oyster sauce or Bearnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Truss the chicken firmly.
2. Tie it in a piece of cheesecloth, to keep it in good shape.
3. It may be stuffed or not.
4. Put the chicken into boiling salted water and simmer, allowing twenty minutes to the pound.
5. When done, remove the cloth and cords carefully.
6. Spread a little white sauce over the breast, and sprinkle it with chopped parsley.
7. Garnish with parsley, and serve with it oyster, or Bearnaise sauce.

title: Boiled Coffee With Boiling Water
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces coffee
- 1 pint water
- 1 raw egg
- 4 tablespoons cold water

## Directions

1. Beat dry coffee well into 1 raw egg mixed with 4 tablespoons of cold water
2. Add boiling water
3. Boil 3 minutes
4. Place where it will keep hot for 10 minutes
5. Serve

title: Boiled Coffee With Cold Water
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces coffee
- 1 pint water
- 1 raw egg
- 4 tablespoons cold water

## Directions

1. Beat dry coffee well into 1 raw egg mixed with 4 tablespoons of cold water
2. Add remainder of water
3. Heat slowly to boiling
4. Let boil 3 minutes
5. Let settle 5 minutes
6. Serve

title: Boiled Corn
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- salt

## Directions

1. Husk, clearing the ear of every strand of silk, and trim off stem and top neatly
2. Boil fifteen or twenty minutes, according to the age of the corn
3. Drain, sprinkle the corn with salt, and serve upon a hot napkin upon a platter
4. Fold the corners of the napkin over the corn

title: Boiled Cucumbers
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Pare, cut in pieces
2. cook until soft in boiling salted water
3. drain, mash and season with salt and pepper and butter, or add to cream sauce

title: Boiled Custard Dessert
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints milk
- 2 heaping tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 eggs
- 1 small cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Let milk come to a scald
2. Wet cornstarch with a little milk
3. Beat eggs very light
4. Pour in cornstarch when milk is scalding, but before it boils
5. Add sugar and beaten eggs
6. Add vanilla
7. Serve warm

title: Boiled Fish
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- drawn-butter
- hard-boiled eggs
- parsley
- lemons
- tomato catsup

## Directions

1. Weigh the fish.
2. Carefully bind up the fish in thin muslin or coarse cheese-cloth.
3. Use a large kettle that will accommodate the fish easily.
4. Salt the water well first.
5. Heat the water to boiling point, but not boiling when the fish is put in.
6. Ensure sufficient water to fully cover the fish, but not in excess.
7. For a large fish, add three tablespoonfuls of vinegar to the water.
8. Keep the water boiling and allow six minutes to each pound; if the fish is large, add six minutes to the computation.
9. Never stab the fish with a fork or skewer to find if it is done.
10. Serve with drawn-butter and hard-boiled eggs sliced.
11. Garnish with parsley and sliced lemons.
12. Some like tomato catsup poured over the fish, without the eggs and lemons.

title: Boiled Frosting
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- just enough water to moisten well
- white of 1 egg beaten

## Directions

1. Boil 1 cup white sugar with just enough water to moisten well, until it hairs from the spoon
2. Pour into the well-beaten white of 1 egg
3. Beat until cool or stiff enough to spread

title: Boiled Halibut
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- halibut
- salt
- saltpetre
- horseradish
- parsley
- egg
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a small halibut, or what you require from a large fish.
2. Put it into the fish-kettle, with the back of the fish undermost, cover it with cold water, in which a handful of salt, and a bit of saltpetre the size of a hazelnut, have been dissolved.
3. When it begins to boil, skim it carefully, and then let it just simmer till it is done.
4. Four pounds of fish will require nearly thirty minutes to boil it.
5. Drain it, garnish with horseradish or parsley — egg sauce or plain melted butter are served with it.

title: Boiled Ham
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- ham weighing from eight to ten pounds
- cold water
- bay-leaf
- cloves
- blades of mace
- teaspoonful of sugar
- fresh sweet hay
- beaten egg
- very fine bread crumbs or fine cracker-dust
- watercress or parsley

## Directions

1. Soak all night; wash it carefully before you put it on to boil, removing rust or mould with a small, stiff scrubbing-brush.
2. Lay it in a large boiler and pour over it enough cold water to cover it.
3. Add a bay-leaf, half a dozen cloves, a couple of blades of mace, a teaspoonful of sugar, and, if you can get it, a good handful of fresh, sweet hay.
4. Let the water heat very gradually, not reaching the boil under two hours.
5. It should never boil hard, but simmer gently until the ham has cooked fifteen minutes to every pound.
6. It must cool in the liquor, and the skin should not be removed until the meat is entirely cold, taking care not to break or tear the fat.
7. Brush over the ham with beaten egg, strew it thickly with very fine bread crumbs, or fine cracker-dust, and brown in a quick oven.
8. Arrange a frill of paper around the bone of the shank, and surround the meat with watercress, or garnish the dish with parsley.

title: Boiled Ham (Virginia Style)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- A twelve-pound ham
- 1 cup of molasses
- 1 cup of vinegar
- A few pieces of stick cinnamon
- Water
- Breadcrumbs
- 1 cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a cup of molasses, one cup of vinegar and a few pieces of stick cinnamon, and stir these ingredients into the water in which the ham is to be boiled
2. Put in the ham and boil slowly three hours
3. Leave the ham in the water until it is lukewarm
4. Take it out and skin
5. Cover with breadcrumbs and put in a pan in the oven, with one cup of vinegar
6. Bake one hour

title: Boiled Macaroni
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ cup macaroni broken in inch pieces
- 2 quarts boiling water
- 1 tablespoon salt

## Directions

1. Boil 20 minutes in 2 quarts boiling water with 1 tablespoon salt
2. Boil until soft
3. Rinse with cold water

title: Boiled Mutton
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- salted water
- minced carrot
- turnip
- small onion
- stick of celery
- parsley
- sprig of mint
- butter
- white roux
- great spoonful of capers

## Directions

1. Plunge the meat into a kettle of salted water that is boiling hard; lift it for fifteen minutes to the side of the range.
2. After this cook slowly fifteen minutes to the pound.
3. Half an hour before you are ready to serve it, drop in a minced carrot, a turnip, a small onion — both sliced — a stick of celery and a little parsley, also a sprig of mint, and let all cook together.
4. Take up the meat, wash over with butter and keep covered and hot.
5. Strain out enough of the liquor to serve as a foundation for a white sauce, and set away the rest for soup stock.
6. Set the reserved liquor in cold water to throw up the fat, skim, and thicken with a white roux; stir in a great spoonful of capers and serve in a boat.

title: Boiled Pigeons or Squabs
body:
## Ingredients

- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Young pigeons, or squabs, are cleaned, washed and dried carefully with a clean cloth
2. Split down the back, and broil like chickens
3. Season with pepper and salt and butter liberally in dishing them

title: Boiled Rice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- rice

## Directions

1. Have a large saucepan three parts full of water, let the water boil very fast, drop the rice in through the fingers, stirring with a fork; keep it boiling very fast for ten minutes, stirring all the time.
2. Put it into a wire sieve and let cold water run through for three minutes, put back into a dry sauce-pan; separate the grains with a fork; stand it on the stove to dry, stirring occasionally.
3. Do not cover rice while drying.

title: Boiled Rice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 half cupful rice
- water
- 1 half teaspoonful salt
- 1 cupful milk
- 1 well-beaten egg
- cream
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil half a cupful of rice in just enough water to cover it, with half a teaspoonful of salt
2. When the water has boiled nearly out and the rice begins to look soft and dry, turn over it a cupful of milk
3. Let it simmer until the rice is done and nearly dry
4. Take from the fire and beat in a well-beaten egg
5. Eat it warm with cream and sugar
6. Flavor to taste

title: Boiled Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1/4 tablespoonful flour
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls lemon juice
- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- Yolks 2 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups vinegar
- Dash of cayenne

## Directions

1. Mix dry ingredients and add to yolks of eggs slightly beaten
2. Beat butter and flour to a paste and put on stove till it bubbles
3. Add vinegar, stir in egg, take from stove
4. Beat in as much whipped cream as is necessary to make desired consistency
5. Add lemon last, slowly

title: Boiled Salmon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Thin muslin or mosquito-netting
- Salted boiling water
- Two tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- Egg sauce
- One tablespoonful of capers
- Nasturtiums, parsley, or cresses

## Directions

1. Sew up the fish in a piece of thin muslin, or mosquito-netting, fitted well to it
2. Boil in salted boiling water to which two tablespoonfuls of vinegar have been added
3. Take off the cloth carefully when the fish has boiled twelve minutes to the pound
4. Lay upon a hot platter
5. Pour over it a few spoonfuls of egg sauce into which has been stirred a tablespoonful of capers, and serve the rest in a gravy-boat
6. Garnish with nasturtiums, or parsley, or cresses

title: Boiled Squash
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- squash
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoonful of butter
- one tablespoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Pare off the outer shell
2. Take out the seeds
3. Cut into small pieces
4. Boil in hot, salted water until tender (twenty minutes if young, longer for full-grown squash)
5. Drain well
6. Rub through a vegetable-press
7. Return to the saucepan
8. Mix with salt, pepper, and a tablespoonful of butter made into a roux with a tablespoonful of flour
9. Stir and beat for a whole minute, until you have a creamy, smoking mass
10. Pour out

title: Boiled Turkey
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- oyster stuffing
- salted boiling water
- pickled salt pork
- oyster or celery sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare as you would for baking or roasting
2. Fill with an oyster stuffing, made as the above
3. Tie the legs and wings close to the body
4. Place in salted boiling water with the breast downward
5. Skim it off and boil about two hours, but not till the skin breaks
6. Serve with oyster or celery sauce
7. Boil a nicely pickled piece of salt pork, and serve at table a thin slice to each plate

title: Boiled Whitefish
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- whitefish
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- sliced eggs
- flour
- boiling water
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Lay the fish open
2. put it in a dripping pan, with the back down
3. nearly cover with water
4. to one fish put two tablespoonfuls of salt
5. cover tightly and simmer (not boil) one-half hour
6. dress with gravy, butter and pepper
7. garnish with sliced eggs
8. for sauce use a piece of butter the size of an egg, one tablespoonful of flour, one-half pint boiling water
9. boil a few minutes, and add three hard-boiled eggs, shred

title: Bon Accord Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 good sized apples
- 1/4 lb. breadcrumbs
- 4 oz. sugar
- 4 oz. raisins
- a little salt and nutmeg
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Pare, core and mince apples quite fine.
2. Beat up and add eggs to the other ingredients, beating all well.
3. Put into a buttered mould, tie down with a cloth, and boil for an hour and a half.
4. Serve with sweet sauce.

title: Boned Turkey
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- fat bacon
- salt
- water
- butter
- port wine
- tomato sauce
- stuffing

## Directions

1. Clean the fowl as usual.
2. With a sharp and pointed knife, begin at the extremity of the wing, and pass the knife down close to the bone, cutting off the flesh from the bone, and preserving the skin whole.
3. Run the knife down each side of the breast bone and up the legs, keeping close to the bone.
4. Split the back half way up, and draw out the bones.
5. Fill the places whence the bones were taken with a stuffing, restoring the fowl to its natural form.
6. Sew up all the incisions made in the skin.
7. Lard with two or three rows of slips of fat bacon on the top, basting often with salt and water, and a little butter.
8. Carve across in slices, and serve with tomato sauce.

title: Boneless Birds
body:
## Ingredients

- veal or leaf steak
- pepper
- salt
- bacon
- string
- flour
- butter
- dripping
- water

## Directions

1. Cut up veal, or leaf steak, in pieces about 2 inches square, and pound each piece until it is quite flat.
2. Dust all with pepper and salt, and lay on each piece a strip of bacon, roll it up and tie with string.
3. Dip each piece in flour and brown in a mixture of butter and dripping.
4. When birds have become brown pour on enough water to cover them.
5. Let them simmer gently about 2 hours.
6. Thicken gravy with a little flour and serve hot.

title: Bordeaux Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes
- 2 small heads cabbage
- 10 large onions
- 5 hot peppers
- 1 ounce celery seed
- 1/2 ounce mustard seed
- 1 ounce tumeric powder
- 3 pounds granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup salt
- 3 quarts vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop tomatoes and cabbage fine
2. Let stand in salt over night
3. Add onions, hot peppers, celery seed, mustard seed, tumeric powder, sugar, and vinegar
4. Boil slowly 2 or 3 hours

title: Bordeaux Sauce 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon green tomatoes
- 1 large cabbage
- 10 onions
- 4 green peppers
- 2 heads celery
- 1 teacup salt
- 1 ounce tumeric
- 3/4 pound mustard seed
- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 1 gallon vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop all fine and mix with salt
2. Let stand 1 hour
3. Drain and put in a kettle
4. Add tumeric, mustard seed, brown sugar, and vinegar
5. Boil and seal

title: Bordeaux Stewed Rabbit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbit
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Skin, clean, and joint
2. Heat a tablespoonful of butter in a sauce-pan and fry in it a sliced onion
3. When it is slightly browned (recipe continues on next page)

title: Bordeaux Stewed Rabbit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 sliced onion
- rabbit, jointed, salted and peppered
- flour
- cold water or weak stock
- parsley
- sweet marjoram
- pepper
- salt
- 1 great spoonful brown roux
- 1 teaspoonful Worcestershire sauce
- 1/2 cupful chopped champignons
- 1 glass claret

## Directions

1. Skin, clean, and joint the rabbit.
2. Heat a tablespoonful of butter in a sauce-pan and fry in it a sliced onion.
3. When it is slightly colored put in the pieces of hare, salted, peppered, and dredged with flour, and cook five minutes, turning over and over that all parts may be seared.
4. Cover with cold water or weak stock, add parsley, sweet marjoram, pepper, and salt, and stew gently until tender.
5. Take up the meat with a skimmer and pile upon a dish.
6. Add to the gravy in the sauce-pan a great spoonful of brown roux, a teaspoonful of Worcestershire sauce, and, if you like, half a cupful of chopped champignons.
7. Boil two minutes, take from the fire, add a glass of claret, pour over the meat, cover and set in an open oven for five minutes before serving.

title: Boston Baked Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart white beans
- 2 quarts boiling water
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup cream
- 2 cups sweet milk
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons molasses
- salt and pepper to taste
- a few slices of fresh pork

## Directions

1. Pick over and wash 1 quart white beans
2. put on with 2 quarts boiling water and 1 teaspoon soda
3. cook until soft, adding water from time to time while cooking
4. drain and add 1 cup cream, 2 cups sweet milk, 1/2 cup brown sugar, 2 tablespoons molasses, salt and pepper to taste
5. put in a dripping pan and place on top of the beans a few slices of fresh pork
6. bake until a rich brown

title: Boston Baked Beans (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound pork sliced
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1/2 cup sugar
- pepper and salt
- 1 quart beans
- water

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart beans until skins break open
2. place 2 or 3 slices of meat in bottom of bean pot
3. add a layer of beans, a little mustard, sugar, pepper and salt if pork is fresh
4. continue until bean pot is full, leaving a little pork for top
5. fill bean pot with water
6. bake all day in a moderate oven, adding water occasionally
7. Serve hot

title: Boston Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg
- 2 1/4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- nutmeg or lemon to taste

## Directions

1. Flour with nutmeg or lemon

title: Boston Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1½ cups sugar
- 3 eggs well beaten
- 1 teaspoon soda dissolved in 1½ tablespoons hot water
- 3¾ cups flour
- ¾ teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup chopped walnuts
- 1 cup chopped raisins

## Directions

1. Cream butter
2. Add gradually sugar and eggs well beaten
3. Add soda dissolved in hot water
4. Sift together flour, salt, and cinnamon
5. Add ¾ of flour mixture to butter mixture
6. Add chopped walnuts and chopped raisins
7. Put in the rest of flour and stir well
8. Drop by teaspoons 1 inch apart on buttered pans
9. Bake in moderate oven

title: Boston Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds white sugar
- 2 quarts water
- 2 ounces tartaric acid
- 10c. bottle vanilla
- whites of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water 10 minutes
