2. When cold, add tartaric acid
3. Add vanilla
4. Add beaten egg whites

title: Boston Pork and Beans
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- cold water
- warm water
- hot water
- boiling salted water
- pork
- boiling water
- dry mustard
- extract of celery or celery salt
- molasses

## Directions

1. Soak the beans over night in cold water, changing this in the morning for warm, an hour later for hot.
2. Put over the fire half an hour afterwards, in boiling salted water, and cook until tender, but not broken.
3. Drain them then, and put into a deep dish or bean-pot, bury a piece of pork (parboiled) in the centre.
4. Stir into a large cupful of boiling water half a teaspoonful of dry mustard, half as much extract of celery or celery salt, and a tablespoonful of molasses.
5. Pour this over the pork and beans.
6. Cover closely, set in the oven and bake slowly from four to six hours according to size of the pot.
7. Serve Boston brown bread with it always.

title: Boston Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint new milk (warm)
- 1/2 cup sugar
- butter size of an egg
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 pint new milk (warm), 1/2 cup sugar, butter size of an egg, 1 White Swan Yeast Cake and flour enough to make a thick paste with 1/2 teaspoon salt
2. Let rise over night
3. Knead in the morning the same as bread
4. Keep in a warm place
5. When ready to rise the last time, roll out lightly on the breadboard quite thick
6. Dip in melted butter and lard
7. Let rise till very light
8. Bake 3/4 of an hour

title: Boston Short Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups flour
- 4 teaspoons baking powder
- a little shortening
- handful white sugar
- 1 egg
- sweet milk or cream
- a little salt
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup flour

## Directions

1. Beat 1 egg well and fill the cup with sweet milk or cream
2. For filling: beat 1 egg, 1 cup brown sugar, 1/2 cup flour
3. Roll dough out and spread with filling
4. Roll up and cut in pieces the same as jelly cake and bake

title: Bottled Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- small cucumbers
- boiling water
- 1 gallon vinegar
- 1 cup salt
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon alum
- spices to suit taste

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over cucumbers
2. Let stand for four hours
3. Mix vinegar, salt, brown sugar, alum, and spices
4. Let come to a boil
5. Put cucumbers in bottles
6. Pour boiling vinegar over them
7. Seal

title: Bouchees a la Reine
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 chicken, minced
- 2 oz. ham
- 6 mushrooms, minced
- A gill cream
- Yolks 2 eggs
- Truffle

## Directions

1. Make some caisses of puff paste, or line little moulds with the paste.
2. Put into a stewpan white sauce, add the chicken, ham, etc., when it is hot add the cream and lastly stir in the yolks of eggs.
3. Fill the caisses and serve.

title: Boys' Delight
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped cold roast meat
- stewed or canned tomatoes
- roast gravy or stock
- mashed potatoes
- rolled biscuit
- butter

## Directions

1. place in a granite pan and cover with stewed or canned tomatoes and a little of the roast gravy or stock
2. cover with mashed potatoes and rolled biscuit and dot with butter
3. Bake 3/4 of an hour in oven

title: Braised Boned Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken prepared as above
- cheesecloth
- bones of the chicken
- slice of carrot
- slice of onion
- parsley
- bay leaf
- three cloves
- twelve peppercorns
- celery
- knuckle of veal
- water

## Directions

1. Roll the chicken lightly in a piece of cheesecloth, tying the ends well.
2. Put in a sauce-pan the bones of the chicken, a slice of carrot and onion, a bouquet containing parsley, one bay leaf, three cloves, twelve peppercorns, celery if convenient, and a knuckle of veal.
3. Add enough water to cover the bed of vegetables and bones.
4. Lay in the chicken.
5. Cover the pot, and let it simmer for four hours.

title: Braised Boned Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken bones
- slice of carrot
- slice of onion
- parsley
- one bay leaf
- three cloves
- twelve peppercorns
- celery
- knuckle of veal
- water
- cheesecloth

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken and roll it lightly in a piece of cheesecloth, tying the ends well.
2. Put in a saucepan the bones of the chicken, a slice of carrot and onion, a bouquet containing parsley, one bay leaf, three cloves, twelve peppercorns, celery if convenient, and a knuckle of veal.
3. Add enough water to cover the bed of vegetables and bones.
4. Lay in the chicken and cover the pot.
5. Let it simmer for four hours.

title: Braised Breast of Lamb
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 breast of lamb, or two scrags
- 1 sliced turnip
- 1 sliced onion
- 2 sliced tomatoes
- pepper and salt
- less than a cupful of stock

## Directions

1. Lay a breast of lamb, or two scrags, in a broad pot, meat downward.
2. Scatter over this a sliced turnip, a sliced onion, and two sliced tomatoes, with a little pepper and salt.
3. Add less than a cupful of stock, and cook slowly one hour.
4. Turn

title: Braised Breast of Lamb
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- breast of lamb or two scrags
- sliced turnip
- sliced onion
- two sliced tomatoes
- pepper
- salt
- less than a cupful of stock
- butter
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Lay a breast of lamb, or two scrags, in a broad pot, meat downward.
2. Scatter over this a sliced turnip, a sliced onion, and two sliced tomatoes, with a little pepper and salt.
3. Add less than a cupful of stock, and cook slowly one hour.
4. Turn the meat then and cook one hour longer, very slowly.
5. When tender, but not ragged, brown, rub with butter and keep hot.
6. Strain the gravy; thicken with browned flour; season, boil up, and pour over the meat.

title: Braised Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- fowl
- carrot
- turnip
- onion
- celery
- salt pork
- parsley
- bay-leaf
- cloves
- peppercorns
- salt
- hot water
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Prepare the fowl as for roasting, trussing it into good shape.
2. Cut into dice a carrot, turnip, onion, and stalk of celery.
3. Put them in a pot with a few slices of salt pork, and on them place the fowl, with a few pieces of salt pork laid over the breast.
4. Add a bouquet of parsley, one bay-leaf, three cloves, six peppercorns, also a teaspoonful of salt, and a pint of hot water.
5. Cover the pot closely and let simmer for three hours.
6. If any steam escapes, a little more water may have to be added.
7. When done, rub a little butter over the breast, dredge with flour, and place in the oven a few minutes to brown.
8. Strain the liquor from the braising pot, season to taste, and if necessary thicken with a little butter and flour browned.
9. Serve it with the chicken as sauce.

title: Braised Duck
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- onion
- sage

## Directions

1. Proceed as with braised goose, omitting the apple from the bed and adding onion and sage very sparingly

title: Braised Lamb Hearts
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb hearts
- salt
- pepper
- Worcestershire sauce or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Wipe each cut of extra fat and take out partition walls
2. Stuff using recipe and tie up and sew
3. Make 3/4 cup brown sauce in dish in which heart was seared
4. Put in double roasting pan and cook in aladdin or slow oven 3 to 5 hours
5. Season with Worcestershire or tomato sauce and serve

title: Braised Pigeon With Mushrooms
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon
- crumbs
- chopped fat pork
- onion-juice
- salt
- pepper
- minced carrot
- onion
- celery
- parsley
- stock
- butter
- water
- fresh mushrooms
- brown roux

## Directions

1. Drain, wash, and stuff with a force-meat of crumbs and chopped fat pork, seasoned with onion-juice, salt, and pepper
2. Prepare the usual bed of vegetables — minced carrot, onion, celery, and parsley
3. Lay the pigeons upon it
4. Add a cupful of stock, or of butter and water
5. Cover and cook gently one hour, or until tender
6. Dish the birds and keep hot
7. Rub the gravy through a colander into a sauce-pan, season to taste
8. Add a dozen fresh mushrooms cut into small pieces, simmer five minutes
9. Thicken with a tablespoonful of brown roux, boil up and pour over the pigeons

title: Bran Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup shortening
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3/4 cup currants
- salt
- 1 cup flour
- bran

## Directions

1. Mix stiff with bran

title: Bran Cake No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 heaping tablespoon butter
- 2 cups bran
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- sweet milk enough to make a batter

## Directions

1. Bake in a quick oven

title: Bran Gems
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups sour milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups bran
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Beat butter, sugar, and eggs together
2. Add the sour milk and bran
3. Add last the soda sifted in the flour

title: Bran Gems 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups sour milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups bran
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. beat butter, sugar, and eggs together
2. add the sour milk and bran
3. add the soda sifted in the flour

title: Bran Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup of butter and lard mixed
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 2 cups bran
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 cup chopped raisins

## Directions

1. Bake in muffin tins

title: Bran Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda dissolved in a little hot water
- 2 cups bran
- flour to make thick batter

## Directions

1. Bake in hot oven

title: Bran Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg beaten to stiff froth
- 3/4 cup bran
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 4 tablespoons sweet cream
- the remainder of the cup filled with buttermilk
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 1 even teaspoon of soda

## Directions

1. Add last of all the beaten whites and soda

title: Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pints of lukewarm water
- flour
- one cup of yeast
- salt

## Directions

1. Add flour enough to lukewarm water to make a thick batter
2. Add one cup of yeast, let rise till morning
3. Add salt to taste
4. Mix in flour enough to make into loaves
5. Let rise, then put in pans

title: Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of yeast per loaf
- flour
- salt
- sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a pint of the yeast for every loaf required
2. Set on the stove and stir with the hand until warm (not hot)
3. Warm a quantity of flour, add a little salt, sugar and butter (about a tablespoon of each for three loaves)
4. Make into a dough, let rise about 1 hour
5. Then knead, when light again mould into loaves, let rise
6. Bake in a hot oven about 1 hour or maybe more, according to size
7. Sugar and butter may be omitted
8. Do not put salt in upon the yeast, as it kills yeast germs

title: Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups potato water
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- yeast
- 3 cups warm water
- 3 cups flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon salt

## Directions

1. Save 3 cups potato water and add 1/2 cup granulated sugar; pour in the yeast.
2. At night take 3 cups of this liquid and to it add 3 cups warm water (always cup for cup).
3. Allow 3 cups of flour to 1 of liquid; add 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon salt.
4. Knead well and put in pan to rise.
5. In morning make into loaves.
6. Grease loaves with melted butter just before placing in oven.

title: Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quarts warm water
- 3 level tablespoons salt
- 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes
- flour
- 1 cup sugar

## Directions

1. At night put in the bake dish 4 quarts warm water, 3 level tablespoons salt, 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes, mix all together with enough good flour to make a thin batter.
2. Next morning, when nice and light, put in 1 cup sugar, mix stiff and knead a few minutes, let rise and make into loaves.
3. Let them rise and bake in moderate oven for one hour.

title: Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon lard
- 2 cups mashed potatoes
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- hot potato water
- 1/2 cup warm water
- flour

## Directions

1. At noon mix sugar, salt, lard and potatoes in crock, pouring over this the hot potato water.
2. When cool add yeast cake which has been dissolved in a half cup warm water.
3. By night this should look foamy.
4. In the morning warm the mixture and mix stiff with flour.
5. When light knead and put in pans.

title: Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 tablespoons mashed potato
- 2 tablespoons salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- cold water
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- warm water
- flour

## Directions

1. Take 5 tablespoons mashed potato, 2 tablespoons salt, 1 tablespoon sugar and cold water enough to make mixture lukewarm; beat thoroughly together and add 1 White Swan Yeast Cake.
2. Let stand until night, then measure the yeast into a quart measure and add enough warm water to make four quarts of liquid.
3. Into this stir the flour gradually until too stiff for the spoon, then mix with hands to a medium stiff dough.
4. Put in warm place till morning, then work down in the pan, let stand until light again.
5. Mould into loaves.
6. Let rise one hour, put into medium hot oven, and bake from 1 to 1 3/4 hours.

title: Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Allow 2 cups of yeast for every loaf of bread.
2. Warm the yeast and flour, add a little salt, mix stiff and let rise.
3. Knead and let rise again.
4. Put in pans and rise again and bake.
5. Keep at an even temperature while rising.

title: Bread and Almond Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 2 cups hot milk
- 1/2 cup chopped almonds
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- juice and rind of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Soak crumbs in milk
2. Beat yolks with sugar
3. Add to milk
4. Add nuts and lemon
5. Fold in beaten whites
6. Bake in a small mould 20 minutes
7. Serve with orange sauce

title: Bread and Butter Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of home-made bread
- butter
- currants
- three eggs
- a pint of milk
- sugar
- grated lemon-peel or cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut bread about half an inch thick, butter and lay in a pudding-dish, sprinkle with currants.
2. Put in another layer of buttered bread and currants.
3. Beat three eggs light and stir into a pint of milk, sweeten to taste.
4. Flavor with a little grated lemon-peel or cinnamon.
5. Pour over the bread and butter and bake in a moderate oven until the custard is set.
6. Test with a knife; if the knife comes out clean the pudding is done.
7. Serve cold in the dish in which it is baked, with a simple sauce.

title: Bread and Butter Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter
- raisins
- two eggs
- one pint and a half of milk
- sugar
- about a dessertspoonful of butter

## Directions

1. Spread your bread with a little butter and cut into slices about half an inch thick
2. Put into a flat buttered pudding dish, put one layer on the bottom of the dish and then put some raisins over it
3. Do this until the dish is full
4. Make a little custard with two eggs to a pint and a half of milk
5. Pour the custard over the bread, first sprinkling sugar over the bread
6. Then put a piece of butter, about a dessertspoonful, in the custard, so that it will float on the top
7. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Bread Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups scalded milk
- 4 cups flour
- 2 yeast cakes
- 6 to 8 tablespoons butter
- 8 tablespoons sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 eggs beaten

## Directions

1. Set to rise 2 cups scalded milk, 4 cups flour, 2 yeast cakes
2. When light, add 6 to 8 tablespoons butter, 8 tablespoons sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, creamed together
3. add 2 eggs beaten and flour to form
4. let rise again

title: Bread Fritters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. milk
- 1 egg
- 1/2 lb. flour
- Bread and jam

## Directions

1. Make a batter with the milk, egg, and flour.
2. Cut some slices of bread rather thin, in squares or three-cornered pieces, spread half of them with jam and cover with the other slices.
3. Dip them into the batter, and fry in boiling lard till of a light brown color.
4. Serve very hot, piled on a dish, and sprinkled with white sugar.

title: Bread from Manitoba Flour
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- 3 quarts warm water
- 1 quart yeast
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 3 tablespoons salt

## Directions

1. In the morning sift flour into your bread pan
2. Take 3 quarts of warm water to 1 quart of yeast, 2 tablespoons sugar, 3 tablespoons salt
3. Mix to consistency of a nice soft dough
4. Punch for half an hour, gradually sifting flour till you have a nice, smooth, spongy dough
5. Let rise and mix down twice
6. Put in the pans and let rise for an hour
7. Bake in oven

title: Bread Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of sweet milk
- 1 1/2 pints bread crumbs
- 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon salt
- flour to thicken

## Directions

1. Mix sweet milk, bread crumbs, eggs, baking powder, salt, and flour to thicken. Fry, turning carefully as they are very tender.

title: Bread Panada
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 gills water
- 1 tablespoonful white sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls crumbs of stale white bread
- 1 glass white wine
- 1 grated lemon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put three gills of water and one tablespoonful of white sugar on the fire
2. Just before it boils add two tablespoonfuls of the crumbs of stale white bread
3. Stir it well, and let it boil three or four minutes
4. Add one glass of white wine, a grated lemon and a little nutmeg
5. Let it boil up once
6. Remove it from the fire, and keep it closely covered

title: Bread Panada
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three gills of water
- one tablespoonful of white sugar
- two tablespoonfuls of the crumbs of stale white bread
- one glass of white wine
- a grated lemon
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put three gills of water and one tablespoonful of white sugar on the fire
2. Just before it boils add two tablespoonfuls of the crumbs of stale white bread
3. Stir it well, and let it boil three or four minutes
4. Then add one glass of white wine, a grated lemon and a little nutmeg
5. Let it boil up once, then remove it from the fire, and keep it closely covered until it is wanted for use

title: Bread Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 slices of bread
- 1 egg
- pinch salt
- 1 teaspoon brown sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour to make batter
- butter and lard mixed

## Directions

1. Soak bread slices for 1/2 hour in cold water. Pour water off dry and beat up bread fine. Add egg, pinch salt, brown sugar, milk, and baking powder. Add flour to make batter. Fry in butter and lard mixed.

title: Bread Pancakes (Variant 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of dry bread
- fresh buttermilk
- 1 beaten egg
- teaspoon baking soda
- pinch of salt
- flour (wheat) enough to make a batter

## Directions

1. Cover dry bread with fresh buttermilk and let stand until soft. For 1 quart of mixture, add 1 beaten egg, teaspoon baking soda, pinch of salt, and flour enough to make a batter.

title: Bread Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- stale pieces of bread
- sweet milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- little salt
- flavoring
- jelly or fresh fruit

## Directions

1. Pour milk over bread to cover nicely
2. Put a small plate on top to keep bread under milk
3. Let stand a couple of hours
4. Beat up 2 eggs separate
5. Stir in sugar, little salt and flavoring
6. Stir into soaked bread
7. Bake in oven
8. When done spread a few spoonfuls of jelly or fresh fruit on top
9. Cover with beaten whites of eggs
10. Return to oven to brown
11. Serve with a good sauce or cream

title: Bread Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- Pieces of bread or toast
- 2 eggs
- sugar to taste
- milk to fill dish

## Directions

1. Put milk in dish
2. Add beaten eggs
3. Add sugar to taste
4. Put in toast or bread
5. When done, sprinkle with white sugar and nutmeg

title: Bread Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups bread crumbs
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3/4 cup butter
- 3/4 cup seeded and chopped raisins
- 3/4 teaspoon cinnamon
- 3/4 teaspoon cloves
- 3/4 teaspoon allspice
- 1 1/2 tablespoons flour
- 4 tablespoons cold water
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 tablespoon butter
- grated rind and piece of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Stir all together
2. Steam 1/2 hour
3. For sauce: stir flour and cold water until smooth
4. Add 1 cup boiling water
5. Sweeten to taste
6. Add 1 tablespoon butter
7. Add grated rind and piece of 1 lemon

title: Bread Pudding 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart finely-powdered bread crumbs
- water
- 3/4 teaspoon cloves
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons flour
- a little nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 pint boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix bread crumbs, cloves, molasses, flour, raisins, and soda well
2. Steam 1 hour
3. For pudding sauce: mix sugar with 2 tablespoons flour
4. Add a little nutmeg and 1 tablespoon butter
5. Pour over this 1 pint boiling water
6. Stir till smooth

title: Bread-and-Butter Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs well beaten
- One ounce of lump sugar
- One-quarter pound of currants
- One quarter of a teaspoonful of salt
- Butter
- 1 quart of milk

## Directions

1. Butter a basin well, put in a sprinkle of currants all round, then a layer of bread-and-butter, and so on, until the basin or mould be nearly full.
2. Add to the eggs a quart of milk with the sugar.
3. Boil for an hour and a half gently.

title: Breaded Chops
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chops
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- cracker-dust
- boiling fat

## Directions

1. Trim and flatten, sprinkle with salt and pepper, dip in egg and then in cracker-dust, and fry to a fine brown in deep boiling fat.
2. Drain and serve dry and hot.

title: Breakfast Dish of Beef
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold roast beef
- 3 tablespoonfuls of walnut catsup
- 1 teaspoonful of vinegar
- A little salt and pepper
- A dessertspoonful of currant jelly
- A little warm water

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices of cold roast beef
2. Lay them in a tin saucepan set in a pot of boiling water
3. Cover them with gravy made of the walnut catsup, vinegar, salt, pepper, currant jelly, and warm water
4. Cover tightly, and steam for half an hour, keeping the water in the outer vessel on a hard boil

title: Breakfast Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg well beaten
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder in the flour
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Bake in muffin tins

title: Breakfast Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1/2 cup water
- 1/2 cup milk

## Directions

1. mix the liquid and add the flour gradually
2. beat until light
3. pour into hot buttered gems
4. Bake 30 to 35 minutes in hot oven

title: Bridesmaid's Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. butter
- 1/2 lb. sugar
- Whites of 14 eggs
- 1/2 lb. almonds
- 1 glass sherry
- A little rose water and mace
- 3/4 lb. flour

## Directions

1. Butter and sugar beaten to a cream, the whites of eggs beaten to a stiff froth, almonds pounded fine, with rose water and sherry, and a little mace, are made into a paste; rub this into three-quarter pounds of flour, and add lightly and quickly to the other ingredients already mixed.
2. The almonds, paste, and flour should be prepared first.
3. Bake in moderate oven.

title: Broiled Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young spring chickens
- salt
- pepper
- soft butter
- maitre d'hotel butter
- parsley or watercress
- lemon

## Directions

1. Split them down the back, remove the entrails and the breast bone, and wipe them clean.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, and rub them with soft butter.
3. Place them on a broiler over a slow fire, the inside down.
4. Cover with a pan, and let cook for twenty minutes to twenty-five minutes.
5. Turn, to let the skin side brown when nearly done.
6. Place them on a hot dish, and spread them with maitre d'hotel butter.
7. Garnish with parsley or watercress and thin slices of lemon.

title: Broiled Chicken on Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- toast
- cream or milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Broil the usual way, and when thoroughly done take it up in a square tin or dripping pan, butter it well, season with pepper and salt, and set in the oven a few minutes.
2. Lay slices of moistened toast on a platter, take the chicken up over it.
3. Add to the gravy in the pan part of a cupful of cream, if you have it; if not, use milk.
4. Thicken with a little flour and pour over the chicken.

title: Broiled Egg-Plant
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- egg-plant
- three tablespoonfuls of olive oil
- juice of half a lemon
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into rather thin slices
2. Lay in salted ice-water for an hour
3. Spread upon a soft towel and cover with another, patting and pressing the slices until they are entirely dry
4. Leave them for ten minutes in a mixture of three tablespoonfuls of olive oil and the juice of half a lemon
5. Sprinkle them with salt and pepper
6. Broil quickly upon a wire broiler for twelve minutes to cook both sides

title: Broiled Fish
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- suet
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean and split the fish.
2. Rub a double broiler with suet.
3. Lay the fish, flesh side down, on the broiler and set over the fire.
4. Turn until both sides are brown.
5. When done take up carefully on a heated dish.
6. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
7. Spread with butter and serve.

title: Broiled Grouse (Larded)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- grouse
- fat salt pork
- lemon-juice
- oil
- pepper
- salt
- toasted bread or fried hominy
- butter

## Directions

1. Singe, clean, wipe well, split down the back
2. Lard the breasts with narrow strips of fat salt pork, drawn through the skin for an inch and out the other side with a larding-needle
3. Or if they are decidedly tough, steam for half an hour and lay until cold in a marinade of lemon-juice and oil
4. Pepper and salt and broil for fifteen minutes
5. Serve upon squares of toasted bread, or upon oblongs of fried hominy
6. Butter well before sending to table

title: Broiled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- melted butter
- fine crumbs
- salt

## Directions

1. Clean oysters, dry
2. Drop in melted butter
3. Roll in fine crumbs, season
4. Broil in buttered broiler

title: Broiled Pork Tenderloin
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tenderloin
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Split the tenderloin through lengthwise
2. Put on a wire broiler and cook over a clear fire
3. When done, put in a pan with a lump of butter and let the juice of the meat and butter come to a boil
4. This makes a delicious gravy
5. Season with salt and pepper

title: Broiled Quail
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- salad oil
- lemon-juice
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- buttered toast
- hot stock

## Directions

1. Draw, wipe, and split down the back
2. Leave them in a marinade of salad oil and lemon-juice for half an hour
3. Without wiping, broil on a wire bird-broiler for ten minutes, turning twice
4. Butter, salt, and pepper them
5. Serve on squares of buttered toast, upon each of which has been poured a teaspoonful of hot stock

title: Broiled Salt Mackerel
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- salt mackerel
- water
- salad oil
- vinegar
- lemon-juice
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape the fish.
2. Soak all night, changing the water at bed-time for tepid, and again early in the morning for almost scalding.
3. Keep this hot for an hour by setting the vessel containing the soaking fish on the side of the range.
4. Wash, now, in cold water with a stiff brush or rough cloth, wipe perfectly dry, rub all over with salad oil and vinegar, or lemon-juice, and let it lie in this marinade for a quarter of an hour before broiling it over clear coals.
5. Lay on a hot dish and spread with a mixture of butter, lemon-juice, and minced parsley.

title: Broiled Squab
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- squab
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- buttered toast
- hot stock or gravy

## Directions

1. Split down the back
2. Rub all over with butter, salt and pepper them
3. Broil over red coals
4. Serve upon buttered toast wet with a little hot stock or gravy

title: Broiled Steak
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Rump steak, about an inch thick
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Butter a sheet of white paper and twist the four corners so as to form a little tray
2. Lay the steak in this and broil quickly from five to ten minutes, turning it once in the paper while cooking
3. When done lay it on a hot dish, season with pepper and salt
4. Add a little bit of butter, and serve at once

title: Broiled Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- oil
- maitre d'hotel sauce
- hot Mayonnaise
- Bearnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the tomatoes horizontally in two; leave the skins on.
2. Place them on a broiler with the skin side down; dust with salt and pepper, and broil without turning, over a moderate fire, fifteen to twenty minutes, or until tender.
3. Lay them on a hot dish, and spread each piece with either butter, oil, maitre d'hotel sauce, hot Mayonnaise or Bearnaise.
4. Alternatively, the tomatoes may be cut into thick slices, covered with oil, and then broiled, turning frequently.

title: Broiled Tomatoes with Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 fine tomatoes, pared and sliced thick
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls melted butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- 2 raw eggs, beaten light
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 1/4 teaspoonful made mustard
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- pinch of cayenne

## Directions

1. Rub butter, pounded yolks, pepper, salt, mustard, and sugar together.
2. Beat hard, add vinegar, and heat to a boil.
3. Put this gradually upon the beaten eggs and whip to a smooth cream.
4. Set in hot water while you broil the tomatoes in an oyster-broiler over clear coals.
5. Lay this upon a hot-water dish and pour the scalding dressing upon them.

title: Broiled Venison Steak
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- venison steak
- salt
- pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls currant jelly
- butter

## Directions

1. Broil venison steak over a clear fire, turning often.
2. It requires more cooking than beef.
3. When sufficiently done, season with salt and pepper, pour over two tablespoonfuls of currant jelly, melted with a piece of butter.
4. Serve hot on plates.

title: Broiled Whitefish—Fresh
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- whitefish
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash and drain the fish
2. sprinkle with pepper and lay with the inside down upon the gridiron, and broil over fresh bright coals
3. when a nice brown, turn for a moment on the other side
4. take up and spread with butter

title: Brook Trout
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- brook trout
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- fat

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and dry the fish, handling tenderly, not to mar its beauty or flavor, roll in salted and peppered flour, and fry in deep fat to a delicate brown.
2. Serve up on folded tissue-paper in a hot-water dish, if you have one.

title: Broth Made Quickly for Invalids
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of lean meat, chopped to a fine mince
- One pint of cold water
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Chop lean meat to a fine mince
2. To a pound of meat add one pint of cold water
3. Let soak for fifteen minutes
4. Let slowly boil for half an hour
5. Season and strain

title: Brown Betty
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 dozen cooking apples
- butter
- bread crumbs
- sugar
- nutmeg
- 1 cup cold water

## Directions

1. Pare and slice fine cooking apples
2. Butter a pudding dish
3. Put in a layer 1/2 inch thick of bread crumbs
4. Add bits of butter
5. Put in a layer of chopped apples, sugar and nutmeg
6. Repeat till the dish is full
7. Pour over the whole a cup of cold water
8. Bake for 30 minutes
9. Serve with cream

title: Brown Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 3 tablespoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 generous tablespoons butter or lard
- 1 cup of fine bran
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Sift 2 cups flour, 3 tablespoons baking powder and 1 teaspoon salt into a mixing bowl
2. Rub in thoroughly 2 generous tablespoons butter or lard
3. Add 1 cup of fine bran
4. Wet with sweet milk
5. Roll to 1/2 inch in thickness
6. Cut as tea biscuits
7. Bake in a quick oven

title: Brown Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups white flour
- 3 cups Graham flour
- almost 3 cups milk
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup molasses
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt

## Directions

1. Mix all together
2. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Brown Bread 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups warm water
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- a little salt
- coarse Graham flour
- bread flour

## Directions

1. Add 1 cup molasses, 1 White Swan Yeast Cake, and a little salt to 4 cups warm water
2. Add enough coarse Graham flour to make a thick batter
3. Set to rise
4. When light, stir in enough of bread flour to knead on board
5. Shape in loaves
6. Put in pans
7. When light bake about one hour in a medium hot oven

title: Brown Bread 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup oatmeal
- 1/2 cup molasses
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 pint of boiling water
- 1/2 White Swan Yeast Cake
- flour

## Directions

1. Put 1 pint of boiling water over the oatmeal
2. Let stand till warm
3. Mix all together with 1/2 White Swan Yeast Cake, and flour enough to make a stiff batter with a spoon
4. Let rise over night
5. In the morning stir down and put in bake pan
6. When light bake

title: Brown Cake No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs, save white of 1 for frosting
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup molasses
- 1 teaspoon mixed pastry spice
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3/4 cup sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Bake in layers in moderate oven

title: Brown Celery Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head celery
- 4 potatoes
- 1 carrot
- 1 Spanish onion
- 2 quarts water
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop the onion small and fry in butter until brown
2. Add to the water and boil for 15 minutes, then strain out the onion
3. Add the vegetables, and boil 1 hour, seasoning with pepper and salt

title: Brown Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups brown sugar
- 1 cup butter
- ¾ cup currants
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sweet milk
- very small teaspoon soda
- cloves and nutmeg to taste
- flour to roll

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients and roll

title: Brown Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- ½ cup lard
- 2 cups molasses
- 3 cups hot water
- 2 tablespoons soda
- flour
- cloves
- cinnamon
- ginger to suit

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients and bake

title: Brown Crystallized Corn
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 0.75 cup water
- 0.75 cup vinegar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- previously popped corn

## Directions

1. Boil sugar, water, vinegar, and butter together without stirring until it stands in cold water, as for white sugar syrup
2. Stir this over the previously popped corn
3. This colors the corn a golden brown

title: Brown Jelly Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 1/2 cup molasses
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake

title: Brown Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Weight of 2 eggs in flour
- Weight of 2 eggs in butter
- Weight of 1 egg in sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls raspberry jam
- 1 teaspoonful baking soda

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and eggs to a cream
2. Beat jam till all bubbles
3. Add flour and half a teaspoonful carbonate of soda
4. Steam one hour and three-quarters
5. For sauce: Whip yolks of three eggs with two tablespoonfuls castor sugar and one cup cooking sherry in a double saucepan, just a minute

title: Brown Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- sugar
- flour
- butter
- half a teaspoonful of soda
- four tablespoonfuls of raspberry jam

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients with sugar, flour and butter equal to the weight of eggs, or a little less butter.
2. Steam two hours.
3. Serve with cream sauce.

title: Brown Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup cornmeal
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup suet
- 2 eggs
- 1 small teaspoon soda
- milk

## Directions

1. Mix flour, cornmeal, molasses, suet, eggs, soda with enough milk to make a nice batter
2. Steam 2 hours
3. Serve with sweet sauce

title: Brown Stock
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. shin of beef or other meat and bones
- 4 carrots
- 1 turnip
- 4 onions
- 1 small head of celery
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 4 teaspoonful of peppercorns
- 6 cloves
- 5 pints cold water

## Directions

1. Cut up the meat and bones and place in the stock pot
2. Pour over the water and skim when boiling
3. Prepare the vegetables and add
4. Cover closely and simmer four hours
5. The spices should be added with the vegetables

title: Brown Stone Front Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons grated chocolate
- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- pinch salt

## Directions

1. boil till it thickens
2. cool, add 1 cup brown sugar, 1/4 cup sweet milk, 1 teaspoon soda, 1 teaspoon cream tartar, 1 1/2 cups flour, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 teaspoon vanilla, pinch salt

title: Brown Sugar Mustard Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cup of vinegar
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- 1 tablespoon of mustard
- 1 of brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1/2 head of cabbage cut up fine
- pepper and salt
- a little chopped onion

## Directions

1. Let 1/4 cup of vinegar and a tablespoon of butter come to a boil
2. After thoroughly mixing a tablespoon of mustard, 1 of brown sugar, an egg and a cup sweet milk, stir in slowly with the vinegar and keep stirring till it boils
3. Cut up fine half head of cabbage, season with pepper and salt, and put into the dressing; a little chopped onion could be added if wished

title: Browned Mince of Beef
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of cold roast beef
- Breadcrumbs
- 1/2 as much potato, mashed
- 1 cup of gravy
- Seasoning of salt, pepper, mustard and catsup
- butter

## Directions

1. Mince the meat very fine, mix with it the potato, and season well
2. Add the cup of gravy, work all together and make very hot in a saucepan
3. Pile upon a dish, cover with fine breadcrumbs
4. Put bits of butter over the top
5. Brown quickly in the oven
6. Serve in the dish it is baked in

title: Browned Mince of Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of cold roast beef
- 3/4 as much mashed potatoes
- 1 cup gravy
- pepper
- salt
- mustard
- catsup
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Make all very hot in saucepan
2. Pile on dish
3. Cover with bread crumbs
4. Put little bits of butter over
5. Brown quickly in oven

title: Browned Sweet Potatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut cold boiled potatoes into slices one-quarter of an inch thick.
2. Sprinkle them with salt and pepper.
3. Spread with butter, and sprinkle with sugar.
4. Place them in a hot oven to brown.

title: Brownies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup melted butter
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons buttermilk
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 2 1/2 cups Graham flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- fruit

## Directions

1. Roll thin and cut in squares
2. put pieces together with fruit between

title: Brownies No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 2 cups bran
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup melted butter
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 2 eggs slightly beaten
- 2 tablespoons sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- dates

## Directions

1. mix all together like pie crust and cut in rounds and place dates between

title: Brunswick Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- 6 large tomatoes
- 3 large potatoes
- 1 pint sweet corn
- 1/2 pint shell beans, green
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put the chicken over and cook till nearly done
2. Add tomatoes and potatoes and beans
3. Let cook until about 15 minutes before taking up
4. Then add corn, salt and pepper to taste
5. The stew should cook 3 hours altogether

title: Brussels Sprouts
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- salt
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Pick over and remove wilted leaves
2. Soak in cold water 15 minutes
3. Cook in boiling water 20 minutes, add salt when partly cooked
4. Drain and add to white sauce

title: Bubble and Squeak
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast or boiled beef
- pepper
- salt
- cabbage
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut cold roast or boiled beef into pieces convenient for frying
2. Add pepper and salt and fry them
3. Boil and chop cabbage small
4. Fry cabbage with a little pepper and salt
5. Keep stirring so all may be equally done
6. Sprinkle over cabbage when taken from the fire a very little vinegar
7. Place cabbage in centre of dish and arrange meat neatly round it

title: Buns
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup bread dough
- 1/2 cup shortening
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 cup water
- a few raisins and currants if desired

## Directions

1. Mix into a stiff dough
2. Set into a cool place to rise until bed-time
3. Make into buns and place in a cool place until morning
4. Bake in oven when ready

title: Buns (No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- warm water or new milk
- candied lemon peel, caraway seeds, or raisins

## Directions

1. Beat eggs in a quart basin
2. Add butter, sugar, and White Swan Yeast Cake
3. Fill basin with warm water or new milk
4. Make into batter
5. In morning knead stiff
6. Add candied lemon peel, caraway seeds, or raisins
7. When risen, knead into buns
8. Bake in a quick oven
9. When nearly done wash with sugar and water

title: Buns (No. 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 3/8 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 pint hot water
- 1 pint sweet milk
- flour
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- warm water
- currants if desired

## Directions

1. Pour hot water over butter, sugar, and salt
2. When butter and sugar are melted, cool by adding 1 pint of sweet milk
3. Add flour enough to make a rather stiff batter
4. Soak White Swan Yeast Cake for 10 minutes in warm water
5. Stir in yeast cake
6. Let it stand in warm place over night
7. In the morning add flour enough to knead into a stiff dough
8. Let it rise and when light roll out an inch thick
9. Spread lightly over the top with butter
10. Cut with small cake cutter
11. When light, bake in a moderate oven
12. A cup of currants may be added if desired

title: Burnt Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart milk
- four tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- one pound brown sugar
- one-half pint cream, whipped
- a few walnut meats

## Directions

1. Boil milk, stir in cornstarch, add a few broken walnuts.
2. Put in a saucepan one pound of brown sugar, let it brown as dark as possible, then add the milk.
3. After the milk is thick beat well together and turn into a mould.
4. Decorate with half walnuts around, or solitaire moulds with a half walnut on top, whipped cream around dish.

title: Burnt Cream Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups milk
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Brown 1 cup sugar and 3 tablespoons butter together
2. Dissolve 2 tablespoons cornstarch in part of the milk
3. Mix well together, add to the sugar and butter
4. Set on stove again and cook until thick as filling for cream pie
5. Beat whites of eggs to a stiff froth
6. Add 1 teaspoon sugar spread over the pie and brown lightly

title: Butter Frosting
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup pulverized sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter creamed
- teaspoon vanilla
- milk

## Directions

1. Cream the butter
2. Add teaspoon vanilla
3. Add milk enough to spread

title: Butter Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter size of an egg
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- Pinch of salt
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix butter, flour, and salt well on stove
2. Add boiling water, stirring all the time

title: Butter Scotch
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls milk
- Butter size of egg
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil until it hardens on a spoon

title: Butter Scotch
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 3 tablespoons molasses
- 2 tablespoons water
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 saltspoon soda
- 1 small teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil sugar, molasses, water, and butter together without stirring till it hardens in water
2. Add vanilla and pour at once on a buttered platter
3. When hard break into squares

title: Butter Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat eggs
2. Mix with brown sugar, butter, currants and vanilla
3. Fill tart shells
4. Bake

title: Butter Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 egg
- 1 cup currants
- nutmeg or flavor of choice

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients
2. This quantity will fill a dozen shells
3. Fill into uncooked shells
4. Brown well

title: Butter Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup currants

## Directions

1. Cream sugar and butter together
2. Add eggs and currants
3. Bake in medium hot oven in tart shells

title: Butter Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup currants
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat whites and yolks of eggs separately
2. Mix with butter, brown sugar, currants and nutmeg
3. Fill and bake

title: Butter Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon water
- 2 teaspoons butter
- 2 teaspoons flour
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Stir well
2. Put in tart shells
3. Bake in oven

title: Butter Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter melted
- 1 cup currants
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients
2. Fill tart shells
3. Bake

title: Buttered Parsnips
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil tender and scrape
2. Slice lengthwise and saute in a little butter heated in a frying-pan and seasoned with pepper, salt, and minced parsley
3. Shake and turn until the parsnips are well coated and hot through
4. Dish, and pour the butter over them

title: Buttermilk Biscuit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. sour milk or buttermilk
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- ½ teaspoonful salt
- ½ cup soft lard
- Flour to make dough

## Directions

1. Take buttermilk and stir in it a rounding teaspoonful of baking soda
2. Pour into the flour bowl where there has been made a hole in the middle of the flour
3. Add salt and lard
4. Mix with the fingers into a soft dough
5. Do not get in too much flour — it must be quite soft
6. Roll out one inch thick and cut
7. Place not too close together on a tin and bake in a very hot oven

title: Buttermilk Biscuits or Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups of flour
- 1 teaspoon of soda
- 1 tablespoon of butter or lard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- buttermilk

## Directions

1. Sift flour and soda together three times
2. Mix 1 tablespoon of butter or lard and 1 teaspoon salt well with flour
3. Add enough buttermilk to make a soft dough
4. Roll and bake in one large cake, or cut like biscuits
5. Bake in very quick oven

title: Buttermilk Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups buttermilk
- 2 cups raisins
- 2 cups currants
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 tablespoons shortening
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 4 cups flour
- spice to suit taste

## Directions

1. Beat all together, put it in your pan ready for the oven, then let it rise over night

title: Buttermilk Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 1/2 cups buttermilk
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 2 teaspoons spices according to taste
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Buttermilk Fruit Cake No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup currants and raisins
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour
- 1 nutmeg grated

## Directions

1. Mix fruit and soda with flour, 1 nutmeg grated
2. This makes a nice pudding if baked first, then steamed
3. Serve with sauce

title: Buttermilk Pancakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart flour
- one egg
- half cup of brown sugar
- a little salt
- one teaspoonful baking powder
- four cups buttermilk

## Directions

1. Mix well
2. Drop with a spoon on a hot greased pan or griddle

title: Buttermilk Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups buttermilk
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 tablespoons butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, buttermilk, eggs, flour, and butter
2. flavor with lemon, bake on lined plate same as custard

title: Buttermilk Pie (Second Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups buttermilk
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- a small piece of butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup sugar, 2 cups buttermilk, 2 eggs, 2 tablespoons flour, a small piece of butter
2. flavor with lemon
3. This makes 2 pies

title: Butternut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 1/4 cup raisins
- 1 cup butternut meats
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake until done

title: Cabbage and Celery Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts fine cut celery
- shredded cabbage
- one cup salad dressing
- half tablespoon salt
- half tablespoon mustard
- one cup sugar
- one egg slightly beaten
- two and a half tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Mix equal parts of fine cut celery and shredded cabbage together with one cup salad dressing
2. Mix half tablespoon each of salt and mustard and one cup sugar
3. Add one egg slightly beaten and two and a half tablespoons butter

title: Cabbage Dressing with Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 quarts cabbage
- 1 cup vinegar
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
- 3 teaspoons mustard
- a pinch of cayenne
- 1 cup cream or rich milk

## Directions

1. Beat all together and stir while cooking
2. Add 1 cup cream or rich milk and beat thoroughly

title: Cabbage Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cabbage
- pepper
- salt
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 3 well-beaten eggs
- ¼ cup thin cream
- 1 tablespoon butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil a small cabbage until tender
2. Drain and let stand until quite cold
3. Chop fine and season with pepper and salt
4. Add melted butter, eggs and cream
5. Melt butter in a frying-pan
6. Turn in the mixture
7. Stir until nicely browned
8. Turn out on a hot dish
9. Garnish with parsley

title: Cabbage Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 7 tablespoons vinegar
- 12 tablespoons cream
- small piece of butter
- cabbage

## Directions

1. Take 1 spoonful vinegar and rub the mustard well so as not to leave any lumps
2. Add all together and cook
3. When it begins to thicken it is cooked
4. Let cool
5. Chop your cabbage
6. Mix together

title: Cabbage Salad (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts cabbage chopped fine
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup vinegar
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- ½ teaspoons mustard

## Directions

1. Boil until thick as cream
2. Pour over cabbage while hot
3. Mix well

title: Cabbage Salad (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart boiled beets chopped fine
- 2 quarts raw cabbage cut fine
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup ground horseradish
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon black pepper
- ¾ tablespoon red pepper
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Cover with cold vinegar
3. Use

title: Cabbage Salad (4)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1½ cups sweet cream
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 3½ cup vinegar
- chopped cabbage

## Directions

1. Cook in a double boiler
2. Stir until thick
3. When cold or ready to use, add vinegar
4. Serve on chopped cabbage

title: Cabbage Salad Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup vinegar
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon salt
- about 1 teaspoon mustard
- about 1 teaspoon flour
- 2 tablespoons cream
- butter size of an egg

## Directions

1. Mix flour fine with 2 tablespoons cream
2. Stir until it boils

title: Cabbage Slaw
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- water
- cream
- pepper and salt
- 2 tablespoons vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut cabbage fine, put it on with just enough water to cook it
2. When done add a little cream, season with pepper and salt, then add two tablespoons vinegar
3. Stir until vinegar is all through the cabbage

title: Cabbage Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of meat, either pork or beef
- water
- cabbage
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- 1 cupful of good sour cream
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Add enough water to cover meat and bring to a boil
2. Skim it
3. Cut cabbage fine, cover with water, bring to a boil, and drain and add it to the boiling meat
4. Season with salt, pepper and onion
5. When meat and cabbage are done, add a cupful of good sour cream, bring to boiling again
6. Take out the meat and serve the soup
7. Tomatoes may be added and it will be all the nicer

title: Cabbage With Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cabbage
- salt
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 even tablespoon flour
- 1 pint milk

## Directions

1. Cut a cabbage into quarters
2. Put into boiling salt water and cook 20 minutes
3. Remove the pieces and put into cold water till cool
4. When cold, chop fine and season with pepper and salt
5. Mix butter with flour and make into a sauce by adding milk
6. Put all together and let simmer half an hour
7. Serve

title: Cabbage, Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cabbage
- salt

## Directions

1. Take off outside leaves
2. Cut in quarters
3. Remove tough stalk
4. Soak in cold salt water
5. Cook in uncovered vessel in boiling water
6. Change water every 10 minutes
7. Drain

title: Cafe au Lait
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup ground coffee
- 2 cups boiling water
- 1 1/2 cups fresh milk

## Directions

1. Make the coffee in the usual way
2. Strain into a coffee-pot or pitcher
3. Add the milk, scalding hot
4. Set for five minutes, closely covered, in boiling water
5. When allowed to cool and then iced this is a favorite beverage at hot-weather luncheons and picnics

title: Cafe Au Lait
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup coffee
- 2 cups boiling water
- 1 1/2 cups milk

## Directions

1. Make the coffee in the usual way.
2. Strain into a coffee-pot or pitcher, add the milk, scalding hot, and set for five minutes, closely covered, in boiling water.
3. When allowed to cool and then iced this is a favorite beverage at hot-weather luncheons and picnics.

title: Cafe Frappe
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. strong coffee
- white of 1 egg
- sugar to taste
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. To coffee sweetened, add the beaten white one egg and freeze.
2. Serve in glasses with whipped cream on top, or with vanilla ice cream.

title: Cafe Mousse
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of thick cream
- 3 tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of vanilla
- one-third of a cup of very strong coffee
- ice and salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix well together one pint of thick cream, three tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar, one teaspoonful of vanilla and one-third of a cup of very strong coffee.
2. Chill thoroughly, then whip, setting the bowl in a pan of ice water.
3. Take off the froth as it rises and lay on a sieve.
4. When no more froth will rise turn the drowned whip carefully into a mould.
5. Cover lightly, binding the edges with a strip of muslin dipped in melted butter.
6. Bury in ice and salt as for freezing, let stand for two or three hours.
7. Wipe off the mould and turn out on a serving dish.

title: Cake Made in a Hurry
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 egg
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 cup milk or water
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 tablespoons melted butter

## Directions

1. Put all in a bowl together and beat until light

title: Cake Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1/2 cup of sugar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking-powder
- a little preserve

## Directions

1. Butter the mould
2. Put in a little preserve and then your batter
3. Steam one hour

title: Calf's-Head or Mock-Turtle Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoonful of butter
- Two tablespoonfuls of flour
- One and a half or two quarts of water in which a calf's head has been boiled
- White wine instead of vinegar in the boiling
- Three or four strained tomatoes
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Pieces of boiled calf's head
- Egg balls
- Two or three tablespoonfuls of sherry
- A few very thin slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Make a brown roux by putting in a saucepan one tablespoonful of butter, let it brown, add two tablespoonfuls of flour, and let that brown
2. Add slowly at first, one and a half or two quarts of water in which a calf's head has been boiled
3. Add three or four strained tomatoes and simmer for one-half hour
4. Skim off any fat and season with salt and pepper
5. Add some pieces of boiled calf's head cut in pieces one-half inch square, a few egg balls, two or three tablespoonfuls of sherry, and a few very thin slices of lemon

title: California Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks of 4 eggs
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup shortening
- 1 cup cold water
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of eggs and brown sugar together
2. Add shortening, cold water, cinnamon
3. Mix soda and cream of tartar in a little water
4. Add to mixture
5. Add flour
6. Bake until done

title: Calves-Foot Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- four calves' feet
- one-third of a box of gelatine
- juice of three lemons
- grated rind of one lemon
- one stick of cinnamon
- sugar to taste
- whites of four eggs
- one pint of sherry

## Directions

1. Get the butcher to clean the calves' feet thoroughly and remove the hoof-horns
2. If not sufficiently white, pour boiling water over them and scrape with a knife
3. Divide each foot in half
4. Place over the fire in a preserving pan, three parts full of cold water, adding a pinch of salt
5. Boil till the meat comes to shreds, adding water occasionally, and the bones separate easily
6. Strain, set aside to cool (the liquid should measure about two quarts)
7. When cool skim off every particle of fat, rejecting the sediment beneath the jelly
8. Add one-third of a box of gelatine dissolved in the boiling stock
9. Take off the fire, add the juice of three lemons and grated rind of one, a stick of cinnamon, and sugar to taste
10. Beat up with these the whites of four eggs and broken shells slightly beaten
11. Set on the fire again and boil a few minutes till a thick scum rises to the top
12. Set the pan on the back of the stove one minute, then pour slowly through a flannel jelly bag, returning till the jelly is clear
13. When finished add a pint of sherry
14. Suspend the bag in front of the oven and do not move it, or the jelly will be cloudy
15. Exclude cold air

title: Canadian Coffee
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons ground coffee
- cold water
- 1 cup boiling water
- equal quantity of milk

## Directions

1. Wet ground coffee with a little cold water
2. Pour over this a cup of boiling water
3. In another pan have an equal quantity of milk and allow it to scald, or until it begins to bubble around the edges of the pan
4. When the coffee boils, add the scalded milk
5. Allow to boil until it foams up
6. Add a few drops of cold water to settle the grounds
7. Serve

title: Canadian Tart
body:
## Ingredients

- short paste
- raspberry jam
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Cover pie plate with short paste
2. Cover with raspberry jam
3. Beat 1 tablespoon butter with 3 tablespoons sugar
4. Add 2 eggs, 1/2 teaspoon baking powder, and flour, and make soft paste
5. Spread on jam

title: Canapees Of Sardines
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Sardines
- yolks of three or four hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- mustard
- pepper
- vinegar
- slices of toast

## Directions

1. Take some slices of roll and cut them neatly into oval or octagon shapes
2. Toast them slightly, or fry them in oil or butter till they are of a nice yellow color
3. Take some sardines and strip them from the bones
4. Lay one-half of them aside, and pound the other to a smooth paste with the eggs and butter
5. Add the mustard, pepper and vinegar
6. When these ingredients are well mixed, spread the paste over the prepared slices of toast
7. On the top lay the other half of the sardines, cut into small strips
8. Stand them in a Dutch oven before the fire and serve very hot

title: Candied Citron
body:
## Ingredients

- citron
- alum
- sugar (3/4 pound to 1 pound citron)
- ginger root
- lemon (1)
- citrons (2 medium-sized)

## Directions

1. Boil citron in water with a small piece of alum until tender and clear
2. Make a syrup, allowing 3/4 pound sugar to 1 pound citron
3. Boil a piece of ginger root in the syrup
4. Put the citron in and boil for a few minutes
5. Put in 1 lemon with 2 medium-sized citrons

title: Candied Citron
body:
## Ingredients

- citron
- white sugar (1 pound to each pound of citron)
- sugar (10 pounds to 1 pint water)
- lemons (2)
- root ginger (2 ounces)

## Directions

1. Pare the citron and cut into slices about 1.5 inches thick, then into strips
2. Take out the seeds and weigh
3. Make a syrup by allowing 10 pounds of sugar to 1 pint water
4. Simmer gently for 20 minutes
5. Put in citron and boil for 1 hour till tender
6. Shortly before taking off the fire put in 2 lemons sliced thin, and 2 ounces root ginger
7. Don't stir while boiling

title: Canned Cherries
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries, stoned
- sugar (0.5 pound to a pound of stoned cherries)
- cherry stones

## Directions

1. Take 0.5 pound sugar to a pound of stoned cherries
2. Make syrup and put in the fruit
3. Let it scald, not boil hard, for 10 or 15 minutes
4. Can and seal
5. Optionally put some of cherry stones in a muslin bag and put into the syrup to scald, but they should not be put into the jars with the fruit

title: Canned Corn
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quarts corn
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 cup salt (scant)
- water

## Directions

1. Cover with water
2. Let boil 15 minutes
3. Put in glass jars and seal

title: Canned Corn 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 cups of corn cut from the cob
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup salt

## Directions

1. Mix together corn, sugar, and salt
2. Put in a kettle at the back of the stove until enough of the milk of the corn has exuded to cook it without adding water
3. Cook for 1 hour, watching carefully that it does not scorch
4. Can and seal
5. When you wish to use the corn take the required amount and put in cold water for 3 1/2 hours
6. Turn off the salty water and prepare with milk and butter

title: Canned Corn 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 cups sweet corn scraped off cob
- 3 cups water
- 1/2 cup salt
- 1 cup granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Mix well
2. Let come to a boil and boil for 20 minutes
3. Seal hot
4. The jars, new rubbers, tops and rings must be scalded before using
5. To prepare for table, put on in cold water and let come to a boil
6. Drain off, put on fresh water again, and let come to a boil
7. Drain off, fix with cream or milk, thicken with flour

title: Canned Corn 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 pints corn
- 1 pint sugar
- 3/4 pint salt
- 1 1/2 pints water

## Directions

1. Cook 20 minutes
2. Seal in glass jars

title: Canned Peaches
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sugar and water in equal quantities

## Directions

1. Make syrup of equal quantities of sugar and water
2. Pare peaches, remove stones
3. Cook till tender

title: Canned Peas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- canned peas
- cold water
- salted boiling water
- white sugar (a lump)
- mint (a small sprig)
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Drain and leave in cold water for ten minutes, put on in salted boiling water, cook fifteen minutes; drop in a lump of white sugar and a small sprig of mint, and cook five minutes longer.
2. Drain, butter, pepper and salt, and serve.

title: Canned Pineapple
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. pineapple
- 4½ lbs. sugar
- 3 pts. water
- brandied paper

## Directions

1. Peel and remove all dark spots on the pineapples
2. Shred with a fork
3. Boil sugar and water ten minutes, skim and add the shredded pineapple
4. Let it boil up, then seal in self-sealers with a brandied paper between the fruit and glass tops

title: Canned Pineapple
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds shredded pineapple
- 2 pounds granulated sugar
- 3 pints water

## Directions

1. Make a syrup of 2 pounds granulated sugar and 3 pints water
2. Boil 5 minutes, then skim
3. Add the fruit
4. Boil 5 minutes more and seal while hot

title: Canned Quinces
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- water
- 1/2 pound sugar to pound of fruit

## Directions

1. Peel, core and quarter, or cut in 8 pieces if large
2. Weigh your quinces
3. Put them in the kettle, cover with water and cook till soft
4. Drain off the water all but a very little
5. Add 1/2 pound sugar to pound of fruit
6. Cook till sugar is melted

title: Canned Raspberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds sugar
- 1 quart water

## Directions

1. To every 3 pounds sugar allow 1 quart water, and boil for 1/4 hour
2. Draw kettle to back of stove, where mixture will not boil
3. Let it remain till you have jars filled with fruit
4. After jars are filled stand on a wet cloth and fill with the hot syrup
5. Seal and put a sack or cloth in a tub
6. Put jars in and pour boiling water in until jars are covered
7. Let stand until cool

title: Canned Strawberries
body:
## Ingredients

- stemmed berries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place quantity of stemmed berries, which will fill preserve kettle, on a platter and add 3/4 pound sugar to each pound of fruit
2. Let them stand 2 or 3 hours, till the juice is drawn from them
3. Pour it in the kettle and let come to a boil, and remove the scum which rises
4. Then put in the berries very carefully
5. As soon as they come thoroughly to a boil, put them in warm jars and seal while boiling hot
6. Be sure cans are airtight

title: Canned Vegetables
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 cups of vegetables, peas, beans or corn
- 3/4 cup salt
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 3 cups water

## Directions

1. Boil for 20 minutes
2. Then fill jars and seal
3. On opening jar put on in cold water and let them come to a boil
4. Then drain it off

title: Canning Green Peas or Corn
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cups green peas
- 2 cups water
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup salt

## Directions

1. Put all in a kettle
2. Boil 20 minutes
3. After they start to boil put in sealers boiling hot

title: Canning Green Peas or Corn 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 cups corn or peas
- 1 cup white sugar
- 2 cups water
- 3/4 cup salt

## Directions

1. Boil 20 minutes
2. Put in jars

title: Canope De Luxe
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Round pieces of toasted brown bread
- Hard-boiled egg
- Oil
- Vinegar or lemon juice
- Coralline pepper
- Salt
- Caviar
- Shelled prawn or stuffed olive

## Directions

1. Lightly butter some round pieces of toasted brown bread and place on each a slice of hard-boiled egg seasoned with oil, vinegar (or lemon juice), coralline pepper and salt.
2. On this place a small teaspoonful of caviar seasoned with oil and lemon juice and top of all a shelled prawn or a stuffed olive.

title: Caramel
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups white sugar
- one-half cup water
- one-half large cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Put on a hot fire in a frying-pan, and stir constantly until it burns a dark brown color and becomes liquid.
2. Remove from the fire and add one-half large cup of boiling water.
3. Set away when cool in a jar for use.

title: Caramel Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cake chocolate
- Yolk of 1 egg
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Take chocolate, yolk of egg, 1/2 cup sugar, and 1/4 cup milk
2. Let boil until it thickens
3. Set aside until cool
4. Beat 2 eggs with 1 cup sugar and 1/2 cup butter
5. Add 1 cup milk
6. Mix in 2 cups flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder
7. Put in the chocolate mixture
8. Mix thoroughly
9. Bake in layers
10. Join with icing

title: Caramel Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup shortening
- 3 teaspoons sour cream
- 3 teaspoons syrup
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- flour to roll out

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients and roll out

title: Caramel Custard
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart milk
- yolks of two eggs
- one cup white sugar
- two tablespoons cornstarch
- one-half large cup caramel
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Stir all together carefully, cooking in a double boiler.
2. Serve cold, with whipped cream.

title: Caramel Custard
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half cup sugar
- Two tablespoonfuls water
- One quart milk
- Six eggs
- One-half teaspoonful salt
- One teaspoonful vanilla

## Directions

1. Put sugar in pan
2. Stir until it melts and is light brown
3. Add the water
4. Stir into the warm milk
5. Beat eggs lightly
6. Add salt and vanilla and part of the milk
7. Strain into the remainder of the milk
8. Pour into a buttered two-quart mould
9. Set the mould in a pan of warm water
10. Bake from thirty to forty minutes, or till firm
11. Serve with cream and fruit sugar

title: Caramel Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups sugar
- scant 1/2 cup cream
- vanilla flavoring to taste

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together

title: Caramel Icing (Variation)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 3 tablespoons cream
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 teaspoons vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil until thick enough to spread on the cake

title: Caramel Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls white sugar
- 1 pt. milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Put sugar on stove and stir until brown
2. Put on stove again to simmer one pint milk, two tablespoonfuls corn starch, and vanilla flavoring
3. When all are ready mix together and stir until thick
4. Put in a mould to cool

title: Caramel Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- 1 pt. milk
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Let milk boil, then add the cornstarch moistened with milk
2. boil sugar and scorch on a tin pie-dish
3. Then pour into the cornstarch, stirring very quickly
4. Pour into mould

title: Caramel Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 pt. cream
- yolks of 8 eggs
- 1 glass rye whisky
- rind of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Melt sugar to a light brown
2. Line a mould with it by putting some in and turning the mould till cool
3. Sweeten cream to taste and put in the grated rind of one-half a lemon
4. Put all into a double boiler
5. When it comes to a boil pour into a basin and let cool
6. Add the yolks of eggs and whisky
7. Stir all together and strain into the prepared mould
8. Tie kitchen paper over mould and steam three-quarters of an hour
9. Serve with whipped cream

title: Caramel Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 pint milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- vanilla
- almonds

## Directions

1. Scald milk
2. Brown 1 tablespoon butter and 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
3. When melted, add to milk
4. Add cornstarch
5. Add vanilla
6. Add almonds if desired
7. Put in delf mould
8. Serve with cream and sugar when cold

title: Caramel Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- water
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- milk
- whites of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Put brown sugar in pudding dish with just enough water to keep from burning
2. Put on stove to brown
3. Beat yolks of 3 eggs well
4. Stir in 2 tablespoons cornstarch
5. If too thick, add a little milk
6. Pour in the sugar and cook
7. Have the whites of the eggs beaten stiff
8. Pour over the rest, stirring briskly
9. Serve with or without cream, as preferred

title: Caramel Pudding Custard
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs (yolks)
- 2 oz. sugar
- 2 oz. castor sugar
- 1/2 pt. cream
- Enough water to moisten sugar

## Directions

1. Put sugar and water into a mould and cook until sugar is of a dark color.
2. Allow it to run all over the mould.
3. Dip in cold water to set it.
4. Pour in your custard.
5. Put into a saucepan with boiling water half way up the mould.
6. Cook about twenty minutes, cover mould with a buttered paper, and put cover on saucepan while the custard is steaming.

title: Caramel Sauce For Puddings
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- boiling water
- flour
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place 1 cup brown sugar in frying pan
2. Let brown, stir frequently to prevent burning
3. Pour on boiling water
4. Thicken with a little flour
5. Season with a dash of nutmeg

title: Caramels
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup molasses
- Butter size of egg
- 1 cup brown sugar
- Vanilla flavoring
- 1 cup cream or milk

## Directions

1. Beat all together
2. Boil until it thickens in water
3. Turn into flat tins
4. When nearly cold cut into small squares

title: Caramels
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. brown sugar
- Butter size of egg
- 1 cake chocolate
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla
- 1 cup milk

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate and dissolve
2. Then add the sugar and butter and boil until they will harden in cold water
3. Put into a buttered pan
4. Work off in squares when half cold

title: Carita Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. figs
- 3 eggs, slightly beaten
- 2 cups milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- Vanilla

## Directions

1. Grease a plain-sided oval mould with butter, and line it with figs, which are split through the centre lengthwise
2. Place the skin side next to the mould
3. Make a custard of milk, eggs and sugar
4. place the bowl containing this custard in a saucepan of boiling water and stir gently until it thickens to the consistency of cream (it must on no account boil)
5. add three drops vanilla and set it aside to cool
6. When cool pour it into the lined mould and steam till it is set, which will probably be in twenty minutes
7. Take care that the top of the mould is covered to prevent the steam settling
8. Turn out of the mould to serve
9. To be eaten with cream and sugar

title: Carmel Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter the size of an egg
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup hot water
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 spoon vanilla
- yolks of 2 eggs
- crust
- whites of eggs

## Directions

1. put Butter the size of an egg and 1 cup brown sugar in pan and let brown
2. thin with 1 cup hot water
3. mix 1 tablespoon cornstarch, 1 spoon vanilla, yolks of 2 eggs, with water, and stir in
4. have crust ready and pour filling in, using the white of eggs on top

title: Carmel Pie (Second Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 large tablespoon flour
- 1 pint milk (not skimmed)
- 2 tablespoons of white sugar

## Directions

1. take yolk of egg, stir it, add a little of the milk, in which stir the flour until thoroughly smooth, then add rest of milk
2. Place frying pan on stove; when hot, put on cup of sugar, stirring so as not to burn
3. when nicely boiling, pour on the mixture, stirring it, and let boil until sugar is all dissolved
4. pour in pie shell, which has previously been baked in oven
5. Beat white of egg to a stiff froth, and gradually add 2 tablespoons of white sugar, or a little more if required, put on pie and nicely brown

title: Carnation Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolk of 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon allspice
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 3/4 cup chopped almonds
- 8 dessertspoons icing sugar

## Directions

1. Mix egg yolks, sugar, and butter
2. Add flour, raisins, sour milk, soda, nutmeg, and allspice
3. Bake in layers
4. For icing: Mix sweet cream, chopped almonds, and icing sugar
5. Spread between layers

title: Carrot Filling For Pies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups mashed boiled carrots
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 3 well-beaten eggs
- 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon ground ginger
- pinch salt

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in a hot oven without an upper crust

title: Carrot Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled, mashed or grated red carrots
- salt
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 lemon juice and grated rind or sliced
- 1 orange
- water

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients and prepare as marmalade

title: Carrot Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow carrots
- milk
- sugar
- eggs
- ginger
- cloves
- cinnamon or allspice

## Directions

1. Cook yellow carrots until soft, then sift and make as pumpkin pie with milk, sugar and eggs
2. season with ginger, cloves, and cinnamon or allspice

title: Carrot Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup grated carrots
- 1 cup water
- 2 eggs
- a small piece of butter
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- vanilla
- salt
- pie shell or light cake

## Directions

1. Boil same as lemon pie, adding pinch of salt
2. Whip egg whites for top
3. Can be put in pie shell or light cake for bottom

title: Carrot Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup grated carrots
- 1 cup water
- 2 eggs
- a small piece of butter
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- vanilla
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Boil same as lemon pie, adding pinch of salt
2. Can be put in pie shell or light cake for bottom

title: Carrot Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. grated breadcrumbs
- 1 lb. currants
- 1/2 lb. suet
- 1/2 lb. flour
- 1/2 lb. sultanas
- 1 large tablespoonful treacle
- Grated rind of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Mix well together with a little milk, and boil in a basin, or mould, for an hour and a half.

title: Carrot Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup grated carrots
- one large cup currants
- one cup grated potato
- one egg
- one cup brown sugar
- a little chopped peel
- one cup suet
- one teaspoonful soda
- one large cup raisins
- salt and spice to taste
- brandy sauce

## Directions

1. Steam in mould three hours
2. Serve with brandy sauce

title: Carrot Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 cups flour
- 1 cup suet
