- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup grated potatoes
- 1 cup grated carrots
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup raisins
- a little salt
- pinch of soda

## Directions

1. Mix all together
2. Pour into a buttered basin
3. Steam 3 hours
4. Eat with sweet melted butter

title: Carrot Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup grated potatoes
- 1 cup grated carrots
- 1 cup suet
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup yellow sugar
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients
2. Place in a mould
3. Steam 4 hours

title: Carrot Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of stock
- 12 or 13 ounces of pulped carrot
- salt
- cayenne
- 4 good-sized carrots per quart
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Boil as many carrots as required (about four good-sized ones to each quart) till quite tender
2. Cut up the red part and rub it through a sieve
3. Weigh it and add gravy soup or good stock in the proportions of twelve or thirteen ounces of pulped carrot to each quart of stock
4. Mix it gradually and season with salt and a little cayenne
5. Let it boil up, and serve very hot, with a dish of fried bread cut into small squares

title: Carrots and Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- water
- onions
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil as many carrots as are required in water until done
2. Drain the carrots
3. Fry as many onions as there are carrots in butter
4. Put carrots into the pan with the onions
5. Serve hot
6. Season with salt and pepper

title: Carrots, Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- salt
- pepper
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Wash, scrape, cut in cubes
2. Cook in boiling salted water until tender, drain
3. Serve with salt and pepper or white sauce

title: Casserole of Rice and Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups chopped meat
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon onion juice
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup fine bread crumbs
- 4 cups boiled rice
- stock
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Season the meat and mix with the crumbs and beaten egg
2. Add enough stock to make it pack easily
3. Line a buttered dish with 3 cups rice
4. Fill with the meat
5. Cover with the remainder of rice
6. Cover tightly and steam 45 minutes
7. Serve with tomato sauce

title: Castle Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- butter
- sugar
- flour
- 3/4 teaspoon baking powder
- jam

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar
2. Add the eggs well beaten
3. Stir in the flour, having the powder well mixed with it
4. Bake in buttered darioles for 20 minutes
5. Turn out and serve with jam

title: Castle Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- their weight in butter
- their weight in sugar
- their weight in flour
- 3/4 teaspoon baking powder (Royal)

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar
2. Add the eggs well beaten
3. Stir in the flour, having the powder well mixed with it
4. Bake in buttered darioles (long-shaped tins) for 20 minutes
5. Turn out and serve with jam and jee poured round

title: Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 peck ripe tomatoes
- 1 cup grated horseradish
- 1 small cup salt
- 1 cup mustard seed
- 2 tablespoons black pepper
- 2 red peppers
- 1 ounce celery seed
- 1 cup chopped onions
- 1 teaspoon ground cloves
- 1 teaspoon mace
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 quart best cider vinegar

## Directions

1. Skin and chop tomatoes fine
2. Cut red peppers fine without seeds
3. Mix all well together without boiling
4. Put away in small jars
5. Can be used at once

title: Cauliflower
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- milk and water
- salt
- drawn butter

## Directions

1. Remove leaves
2. Cut the main stock close to the flower
3. Lay it in boiling milk and water, slightly salted, with the stalk down
4. When done, take out carefully and drain in a colander
5. Place in the vegetable dish and pour over it a rich drawn butter dressing

title: Cauliflower (Parisian Style)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- a good-sized cauliflower
- a tablespoonful of butter
- flour
- a pint of strained tomato-juice
- half an onion
- three or four tablespoonfuls of cracker-dust
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil a good-sized cauliflower until tender
2. Chop it coarsely, and press it hard in a bowl or mould, so that it will keep its form when turned out
3. Put the shape thus made upon a dish that will stand the heat, and pour over it a tomato sauce
4. Make the sauce by cooking together a tablespoonful of butter and flour in a saucepan, and pouring upon them a pint of strained tomato-juice in which half an onion has been stewed
5. Stir until smooth, and thicken still more by the addition of three or four tablespoonfuls of cracker-dust
6. Salt to taste, turn the sauce over the moulded cauliflower, set it in the oven for about ten minutes, and serve in the dish in which it is cooked

title: Cauliflower au Gratin
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two cauliflowers
- grated cheese
- white sauce
- fried bread-crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil one or two cauliflowers (after removing leaves) until tender
2. Strain off the water and place in a dish
3. Cover with grated cheese, some white sauce and some fried bread-crumbs
4. Add some small pieces of butter and bake until a nice brown

title: Cauliflower au Gratin
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- pepper
- grated cheese
- dried crumbs
- milk

## Directions

1. Cook cauliflower; drain well and remove the flowerettes
2. Tear the rest to pieces with a fork, lay it in a deep dish and sprinkle over it a little salt, pepper and grated cheese and a few dried crumbs moistened with milk
3. Then add the top layer of the flowerettes and sprinkle with the salt, pepper and cheese, and bake until slightly brown

title: Cauliflower au Gratin (Italian)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- hot, salted water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- Parmesan cheese
- cracker-crumbs
- milk
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil in hot, salted water and divide into tiny clusters, a flower or two on each
2. Butter a deep dish and put in a layer of these, sprinkling with butter, salt, and pepper, and covering first with Parmesan cheese, then with cracker-crumbs
3. Wet each layer with milk, and fill the dish in this order, finishing with a layer of crumbs dotted with butter-bits, and dusted with cayenne
4. Bake, covered, half an hour, then brown
5. Serve in the dish

title: Cauliflower Broth (Without Meat)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One fine cauliflower
- Two tablespoonfuls butter rubbed into one of cornstarch
- One onion
- Bunch of parsley
- Two blades of mace
- Two quarts water
- Two cups milk
- Pepper and salt
- A pinch of soda in the milk

## Directions

1. Cut the cauliflower into bunches, reserving about a cupful of small clusters to put whole into the soup
2. Chop the rest, also the onion and herbs, and put on in the water, with the mace
3. Cook an hour, and rub through a colander
4. Return the puree thus obtained to the pot, and season with pepper and salt
5. As it boils, stir in the whole clusters, previously boiled tender in hot, salted water, and left to cool
6. When the soup is again hot, put in the butter and cornstarch
7. Stir until this has thickened
8. Pour into the tureen, and add the boiling milk
9. Pass sliced lemon and cream-crackers with it

title: Cauliflower Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk (sweet)
- 1 cup flour
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- a little salt
- flowerets of cauliflower
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Mix into a batter
2. Dip flowerets of cauliflower previously boiled tender in a little salted water
3. Fry in hot fat till brown

title: Cayenne Cheese Straws
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. butter
- 2 oz. Parmesan cheese
- 1/4 lb. flour
- a pinch of salt
- a little cayenne
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Mix into a paste with the yolk of an egg.
2. Roll out to the thickness of a silver quarter, about four or five inches long; cut into strips about a third of an inch wide, twist them as you would a paper spill, and lay them on a baking-sheet slightly floured.
3. Bake in a moderate oven until crisp, but they must not be the least brown.
4. If put away in a tin, these cheese straws will keep a long time.
5. Serve cold, piled tastefully on a glass dish. You can make the straws of remnants of puff-pastry, rolling in the grated cheese.

title: Cecils
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cold cooked meat chopped fine
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 tablespoon bread crumbs
- a few drops onion juice
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper
- 1 cup stock soup

## Directions

1. stir all together over fire until warm
2. then cool
3. form in ball
4. roll in crumbs
5. then egg
6. fry in hot lard
7. serve with bechamel sauce

title: Celeried Chicken (Entree)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups cold cooked chicken, minced
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 heaping tablespoonful flour
- 1 teaspoonful celery salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 4 dashes pepper
- 3 slices of toast, cut lengthwise

## Directions

1. Put butter and flour in saucepan, stir till butter is melted and smooth
2. Add milk, salt and pepper, stir till it comes to a boil
3. Add parsley and celery salt, which have been mixed with a little of the butter mixture
4. Add chicken
5. Arrange toast in strips (log-cabin style)
6. Place the chicken in centre and serve

title: Celery and Cheese Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cheese
- celery
- mayonnaise
- thin buttered slices

## Directions

1. Chop cheese and celery together fine
2. Toss in a little mayonnaise
3. Spread between thin buttered slices

title: Celery Chowder
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads cabbage
- 1/2 peck onions
- 3 1/2 peck tomatoes (green)
- 3 heads celery
- 1 ounce turmeric powder
- 3/4 pound mustard
- 2 pounds sugar
- 3 pints vinegar
- 2 teaspoons cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Boil well together

title: Celery Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup chopped celery
- 1 large potato
- 2 tablespoons chopped nuts
- butter
- salt and pepper
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Pare and boil potato and celery together until tender
2. Remove potato, mash, drain the celery
3. Mix together chopped nuts, butter, salt and pepper
4. Cool, form into croquette shapes
5. Dip in egg and bread crumbs and fry

title: Celery Lobster
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lobster meat
- 1 cupful celery, minced
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- Juice 1 lemon
- 1/2 pt. milk
- Yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 saltspoonful cayenne
- 1 dessertspoonful flour

## Directions

1. Cook together the butter and flour over hot water, add the milk, stir until smooth, put in the celery and cook three minutes, add the lobster, seasoning, and yolk of egg
2. stir until thick, and serve

title: Celery Oysters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 oysters
- 1 gill oysters liquor
- 1/2 cup celery minced
- 1 gill of cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 gill sherry or Madeira
- 1 teaspoonful each of salt and paprica

## Directions

1. Put the oyster-liquor, celery, and paprica in the chafing-dish over hot water, and when it comes to a boil simmer three or four minutes
2. Add the butter and the cream, and when these are boiling hot put in the oysters
3. Cook until the edges curl, stir in the wine and salt, and serve at once on toast

title: Celery Oysters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 oysters
- 1 gill oysters liquor
- 1/2 cup celery minced
- 1 gill of cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 gill sherry or Madeira
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 teaspoonful paprica

## Directions

1. Put the oyster-liquor, celery, and paprica in the chafing-dish over hot water, and when it comes to a boil simmer three or four minutes
2. Add the butter and the cream, and when these are boiling hot put in the oysters
3. Cook until the edges curl, stir in the wine and salt, and serve at once on toast

title: Celery Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen bunches celery cut fine
- 2 quarts vinegar
- 1 small tin mustard (1/2 pound)
- 2 ounces curry powder
- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 2 red peppers
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Let vinegar come to the boil and keep simmering
2. Mix mustard, curry powder, and flour separately in a little vinegar
3. Be sure they are very smooth
4. Put the flour in the hot vinegar first
5. Then add the mustard and curry powder and chopped peppers
6. Add the celery and boil all for 10 minutes
7. Be sure it is not lumpy before adding the celery
8. Put away in jars

title: Celery Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- two heads of celery
- two sour apples
- seven olives
- mayonnaise dressing
- chopped walnuts
- capers
- hot-house cress

## Directions

1. Chop celery, apples, and olives together, not too fine
2. Mix a little mayonnaise dressing with it
3. Pour over the top more mayonnaise dressing
4. Put chopped walnuts and capers over top
5. Arrange hot-house cress around salad

title: Celery Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 bunches celery
- salt and pepper
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons mustard
- celery tops
- hard-boiled egg

## Directions

1. Chop celery fine
2. Sprinkle over with salt and pepper
3. Beat 1 egg in a saucepan, add 1/2 cup vinegar, 2 tablespoons sugar, 2 tablespoons mustard, stir all together
4. When boiling put in celery, let boil five minutes, stir constantly
5. Put in dish, garnished with celery tops and cut up a hard-boiled egg and lay over tops

title: Celery Salad 2
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- 1 sweet apple
- white Malaga grapes
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup water
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1/2 tablespoon mustard
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut celery in dice
2. Pare 1 sweet apple, cut in dice
3. Take a bunch of white Malaga grapes cut in two
4. Make a dressing of 2 eggs, 1/2 cup water, 1/2 cup vinegar, 2 tablespoons sugar, 1/2 tablespoon mustard, salt and a piece of butter
5. Boil until thick, pour over the above

title: Celery Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- white tender celery
- two eggs
- mayonnaise dressing
- bread
- butter

## Directions

1. With a sharp knife cut white tender celery into bits a quarter of an inch long until you have a cupful.
2. Mix with it two minced eggs that have been boiled twenty-five minutes, then left in cold water until they have cooled to the heart.
3. Chop them fine and rub through a coarse sieve.
4. Work up well with the celery and beat in two tablespoonfuls of mayonnaise dressing.
5. Spread between thin slices of buttered bread.

title: Celery Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 ripe tomatoes
- 6 heads celery
- 6 small onions
- 1 red pepper
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 qt. vinegar
- 1 cup salt
- 1 tablespoonful mixed spice

## Directions

1. Peel tomatoes and onions
2. Chop very finely the celery, pepper, tomatoes and onions
3. Add the vinegar, sugar, spice, etc.
4. Boil gently for three hours

title: Celery Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 large tomatoes
- 2 bunches celery
- 4 large onions
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup vinegar
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- red pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon mustard

## Directions

1. Skin and slice tomatoes
2. Chop celery and onions fine
3. Drain off juice
4. Add sugar, vinegar, cinnamon, red pepper, and mustard
5. Boil about 2 hours
6. Bottle

title: Celery Sauce 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds tomatoes
- 2 1/2 pounds celery
- 9 teaspoons salt
- 6 teaspoons ground pepper
- 2 1/4 teaspoons cinnamon
- 6 teaspoons mustard
- 3/8 pound white onions
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1/8 teaspoon cayenne pepper
- 1 quart vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop celery and onions
2. Peel tomatoes
3. Cook until tender
4. Strain and add all the other ingredients
5. Cook until celery is tender

title: Celery Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads celery
- 1 quart of milk
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 cup whipped cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut 2 heads celery into finger lengths, simmer in a quart of milk for 3½ an hour
2. Remove celery
3. Thicken with tablespoon cornstarch blended with milk and cup whipped cream
4. Season and serve

title: Cereal Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- White of 1 egg
- 1/4 cup granulated sugar
- 5 tablespoons wheat
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat white until stiff
2. Add gradually the sugar
3. Add wheat, then vanilla
4. Beat constantly
5. Drop on buttered sheet
6. Bake 12 to 15 minutes in slow oven

title: Charity Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- Butter size of an egg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Stir sugar and butter to a cream
2. Add sweet milk
3. Add flour, cream of tartar, and soda
4. Mix together
5. Bake until done

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. cream (whipped stiff)
- 1 oz. gelatine
- 1/4 pt. milk
- whites of 2 eggs
- 1 teacupful powdered sugar
- flavor to taste
- lady fingers
- icing

## Directions

1. Dissolve gelatine in one-quarter pint of hot milk.
2. Fasten lady fingers with icing.
3. Mix cream, whites of eggs and sugar together.
4. Add gelatine last, a little at a time.
5. Beat quickly with a spoon.

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint cream
- one-quarter cup sugar
- whites of two eggs
- one teaspoonful granulated gelatine
- lady fingers

## Directions

1. Dissolve gelatine in as little boiling water as possible.
2. Whip cream and beat eggs very light.
3. Add all together and pour into mould lined with lady fingers.

title: Charlotte Russe
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint cream
- one-quarter cup sugar
- whites of two eggs
- one teaspoonful granulated gelatine
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Dissolve gelatine in as little boiling water as possible
2. whip cream and beat eggs

title: Charlotte Russe
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups cream
- 3/4 cup sugar
- lady fingers or stale sponge cake
- flavoring to taste
- 1 teaspoon Knox gelatine, or 1 ounce sheet gelatine
- 3/4 cup cold water or milk

## Directions

1. Line a mould or dish holding about 6 cups with lady fingers or strips of sponge cake
2. Trim the cake to fit into the dish
3. Stick lady fingers together with white of egg or dissolved gelatine to keep them in place
4. Put the gelatine to soak in cold water or milk
5. Whip the cream
6. Beat in the sugar and the flavoring
7. Stand the gelatine over hot water and dissolve it
8. Rapidly mix the dissolved gelatine with the whipped cream
9. Turn it into the cake-lined dish
10. Set away to get perfectly cold before turning out to serve

title: Cheap and Good Spice Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg beaten
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 tablespoons molasses
- 2 small teaspoons mixed spice
- small ½ cup shortening
- large half cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix egg, sugar, molasses, spice, shortening, and sour milk
2. Dissolve soda in sour milk
3. Add flour to make thick batter
4. Bake in a small dripping pan
5. Ice and cut in square pieces

title: Cheap Marble Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar and milk
- 2¾ cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup lard or butter
- 2 tablespoons dark molasses
- 2 tablespoons chocolate
- spice

## Directions

1. Sift flour with baking powder
2. Take ½ the mixture, add 2 tablespoons dark molasses and 2 tablespoons chocolate, spice to taste
3. Pour light and dark at one time in cake pan
4. Bake in moderate oven

title: Cheap Plum Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of flour
- One teaspoon of baking-powder
- One-half teaspoon of cinnamon
- One-half cup of suet
- One-half cup raisins
- One egg
- One-half cup molasses
- One-half cup of milk

## Directions

1. Steam three hours.

title: Cheap Plum Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of suet
- one cup of molasses
- one cup sweet milk
- one cup of seeded raisins
- three cups of flour
- a little salt
- soda
- one egg

## Directions

1. Boil three or four hours.

title: Cheese and Honey
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cream cheese
- honey
- buttered slices

## Directions

1. Spread cream cheese on daintily cut and buttered slices
2. Then spread scantily run honey on the cheese, or mix cheese and honey and spread together

title: Cheese and Mustard Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of broken cheese
- 1 teaspoonful of made mustard
- a tiny drop of cream
- salt
- pepper
- thin buttered slices

## Directions

1. Pound and mix together cheese and mustard
2. Add a tiny drop of cream if not soft enough
3. Add salt and a dash of pepper
4. Spread on thin buttered slices

title: Cheese Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/4 cups grated cheese
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- speck cayenne
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Fold in 3 egg whites beaten stiff
2. Shape in small balls
3. Roll in fine crumbs
4. Fry
5. Drain

title: Cheese Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of curd of sour milk
- 1 cup of currants
- 1 cup of sugar
- 1/2 a nutmeg grated
- cinnamon and cloves, 1/2 teaspoon each
- 2 eggs well beaten
- 2 ounces of lemon peel
- good paste

## Directions

1. Add to curd 1 cup of currants, 1 cup of sugar, 1/2 a nutmeg grated, cinnamon and cloves, 1/2 teaspoon each, 2 eggs well beaten, and 2 ounces of lemon peel
2. Mix all together
3. Line some patty pans with good paste and fill with the curd
4. Bake in a slow oven

title: Cheese Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup thick white sauce
- 2 yolks
- 1 3/4 cups cheese
- salt
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Make the sauce
2. Add yolks and cheese
3. When fairly beaten, add seasoning
4. Spread in shallow saucepan
5. Cool
6. Cut in shapes
7. Roll in crumbs
8. Fry

title: Cheese Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon mustard
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup milk
- 3/4 cup grated stale cheese

## Directions

1. Put the seasonings and the eggs into a saucepan and beat thoroughly
2. Add the milk and stir in the cheese
3. Put over the fire and stir constantly until it begins to thicken and coat the spoon
4. Take at once from the fire, and pour over soda biscuits, strips of buttered toast, or shredded wheat biscuits arranged on a hot platter

title: Cheese Fondu
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- Gill hot cream
- 3 eggs
- 6 tablespoonfuls grated cheese
- Saltspoonful salt
- Pinch cayenne

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan butter and when melted stir in flour and hot cream.
2. Stir constantly and when thick and smooth stir in grated cheese, salt and cayenne.
3. Turn out into a bowl and beat in the beaten yolks of two eggs.
4. Beat the whites of three eggs as stiff as possible; have the baking dish heated and buttered, and just fifteen minutes before the fondu is wanted mix in the whites very quickly and lightly and bake.
5. The oven should be hot, but not over hot, and the fondu should rise to twice its original height.

title: Cheese Fondu
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. butter
- 1 pt. boiling milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1/4 teaspoonful pepper
- 5 oz. grated cheese

## Directions

1. Melt butter, and whisk into it the boiled milk
2. Dissolve flour in a gill of cold milk, add it to the boiled milk and let it cool
3. Beat the yolks of eggs with salt, pepper, and grated cheese
4. Whip the whites of the eggs and add them
5. Pour the mixture into a deep tin lined with buttered paper, and allow for the rising, say four inches
6. Bake twenty minutes and serve the moment it leaves the oven

title: Cheese Fondu
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cracker crumbs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup chopped cheese
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Add whites well beaten last
2. Bake 15 minutes

title: Cheese Fourdeue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup stale crumbs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1/2 pound grated cheese
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- speck of cayenne pepper
- 1 yolk of egg
- toast

## Directions

1. Soak crumbs 6 hours in 1 cup milk
2. Cook over hot water
3. Melt butter, add soaked crumbs mixed with egg, then seasonings and cheese
4. Stir constantly
5. When smooth and hot serve on toast

title: Cheese Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of milk
- one tablespoonful of flour
- half a teaspoonful of baking powder
- one tablespoonful of grated cheese
- four eggs
- one tablespoonful of melted butter
- salt
- pepper (cayenne)

## Directions

1. Half cook in the spider, then spread on the whites
2. Cook on the top of the stove for about ten minutes
3. Put in the oven to brown

title: Cheese Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- custard pie crust
- cheese shaved very fine
- One large cup milk
- two eggs
- one tablespoon melted butter

## Directions

1. Prepare crust for custard pie.
2. Fill crust half full of cheese shaved very fine.
3. Pour over this a custard made of one large cup milk, two eggs, and one tablespoon melted butter.
4. Bake in a very hot oven.

title: Cheese Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- 3/4 cup grated cheese
- 1 egg
- 1 pint sweet milk

## Directions

1. Cut stale bread in inch squares
2. Butter evenly
3. Put 1 layer in pudding dish
4. Add a little salt and pepper and 3/4 cup grated cheese
5. Repeat layers, but for top layer use 3/4 cup cheese
6. Beat 1 egg and add 1 pint sweet milk
7. Pour over bread
8. Bake 3/4 hour
9. Serve hot in place of potatoes

title: Cheese Ramakins
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- light puff-paste
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Take the remains or odd pieces of any light puff-paste left from pies or tarts; gather up the pieces of paste, roll it out evenly, and sprinkle it with grated cheese of a nice flavor.
2. Fold the paste in three, roll it out again, and sprinkle more cheese over; fold the paste, roll it out, and with a paste-cutter shape it in any way that may be desired.
3. Bake the ramakins in a brisk oven from ten to fifteen minutes, dish them on a hot napkin, and serve quickly.
4. The appearance of this dish may be very much improved by brushing the ramakins over with yolk of egg before they are placed in the oven. Where expense is not objected to, Parmesan is the best kind of cheese to use for making this dish.

title: Cheese Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hard-boiled egg
- 1/4 lb. cheese, grated
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 1/4 teaspoonful pepper
- 1/4 teaspoonful mustard
- 1 tablespoonful melted butter
- 1 tablespoonful vinegar
- biscuits or oat-cake

## Directions

1. Take the yolk of the egg and put it into a small bowl and crumble it down
2. Put into it the butter and mix it smooth with a spoon
3. Add the salt, pepper, mustard, and the cheese, mixing each well
4. Put in the tablespoonful of vinegar, which will make it the proper thickness
5. If the vinegar is not relished, then use cold water instead
6. Spread this between two biscuits or pieces of oat-cake

title: Cheese Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 3/4 teaspoon mustard
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- a little pepper
- 3/4 cup grated cheese

## Directions

1. Put the butter, flour, mustard, salt and pepper into a saucepan
2. Stir over the fire until it froths
3. Add the milk and stir constantly until it thickens and boils
4. Stand over hot water, or on a cooler part of the stove
5. Add the cheese and stir until it is well mixed in

title: Cheese Scallop
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup dry bread crumbs
- fresh milk
- 3 eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3½ pound grated cheese
- sifted bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Soak 1 cup dry bread crumbs in fresh milk
2. Beat into this 3 eggs
3. Add 1 tablespoon butter and 3½ pound grated cheese
4. Strew sifted bread crumbs upon the top
5. Bake in the oven a delicate brown

title: Cheese Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. boiling milk
- 1 teaspoonful pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 5 oz. grated cheese
- 4 eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Melt butter, and whisk into it the boiled milk.
2. Dissolve flour in a gill of cold milk, add it to the boiled milk and let it cool.
3. Beat the yolks of eggs with salt, pepper, and grated cheese.
4. Whip the whites of the eggs and add them, pour the mixture into a deep tin lined with buttered paper, and allow for the rising, say four inches.
5. Bake twenty minutes and serve the moment it leaves the oven.

title: Cheese Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cup milk
- 1/2 cup breadcrumbs
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- salt and cayenne
- 4 tablespoonfuls grated cheese
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Set milk and breadcrumbs over boiling water, stir till smooth.
2. Take from fire and add butter, grated cheese, salt, cayenne, yolks eggs beaten well.
3. Fold in the whites of eggs well beaten.
4. Bake in buttered dish for twenty minutes in a pan of hot water.
5. Serve at once.

title: Cheese Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. butter
- 1 pt. milk
- 1 oz. flour
- 3 oz. Parmesan cheese
- a pinch cayenne
- a pinch salt
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Melt butter in a saucepan; mix smoothly with it flour, salt and cayenne and milk; simmer the mixture gently over the fire, stirring it all the time, till it is as thick as melted butter.
2. Stir into it finely-grated cheese.
3. Turn it into a basin, and mix with it the yolks of two well-beaten eggs.
4. Whisk three whites to a solid froth, and just before the souffle is baked put them into it, and pour the mixture into a small round tin.
5. It should be only half filled, as the fondu will rise very high.
6. Pin a napkin around the dish in which it is baked, and serve the moment it is baked.
7. Have a metal cover strongly heated.
8. Bake twenty minutes. Sufficient for six persons.

title: Cheese Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls flour
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- pinch of cayenne
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1/4 cup old cheese
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Make same as white sauce; add yolks and cheese, set to cool, then add whites beaten stiff.
2. Put into a greased pudding dish in medium oven, bake fifteen or twenty minutes.

title: Cheese Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls grated cheese
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 2 oz. bread
- 2 teaspoonful mustard
- 1 gill milk
- pepper and salt to taste
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the bread and milk on to boil, stir and boil until smooth, add the cheese and butter, stir over the fire for one minute.
2. Take off, add seasoning and the yolks of two eggs.
3. Beat the whites of three eggs to a stiff froth and stir them in carefully.
4. Pour into a greased baking-dish and bake fifteen minutes in a quick oven.

title: Cheese Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup grated cheese
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Heat butter, stir in the flour, add the hot milk.
2. Cook two minutes.
3. Set away to cool; when cold stir in the cheese and yolks of eggs.
4. Last the whites of the eggs beaten to a stiff froth.
5. Turn into a buttered dish and bake from twenty to twenty-five minutes.
6. Serve in the same dish.

title: Cheese Souffle
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons butter
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup grated cheese
- ⅜ teaspoon salt
- pinch of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Mix butter and flour
2. Add salt, pepper and milk
3. Cook and stir until thick as good cream
4. Remove from stove and beat in 1 cup grated cheese and yolks of 3 eggs
5. Beat in lightly the stiffly beaten whites of the eggs
6. Steam for 1 hour

title: Cheese Souffle (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce melted butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- pinch pepper
- 1 cup milk
- ¾ cup grated cheese
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix together in a saucepan 1 ounce melted butter and 1 tablespoon flour
2. When quite smooth, add 1 teaspoon salt and a pinch pepper and 1 cup milk
3. Cook and stir this till thick as good cream
4. Remove from stove and beat in ¾ cup grated cheese and yolks of 2 eggs
5. Beat in lightly the stiffly beaten whites of the eggs
6. Pour the mixture into a buttered pudding dish
7. Bake in a very hot oven for about 25 minutes
8. Serve as soon as taken from oven

title: Cheese Souffle (Variation 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 1/16 teaspoon mustard
- ⅜ teaspoon salt
- cayenne pepper
- ½ pound cheese cut fine
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Make white sauce
2. Add yolks
3. Add cheese
4. Fold in beaten whites
5. Cover in blazer and let stand 25 minutes over hot water without lifting the lid

title: Cheese Souffle (Variation 4)
body:
## Ingredients

- ⅜ cup butter
- 1⅓ cup flour
- ¾ cup grated cheese
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon salt
- ¾ teaspoon mustard
- pepper

## Directions

1. Melt the butter
2. Add the flour and stir over the fire until frothy
3. Add the milk and stir constantly until it thickens and boils
4. Stir in the seasonings and cheese
5. Stir in the beaten egg-yolks and take from the fire at once
6. Stand the saucepan aside and let the mixture cool
7. Beat the egg whites stiff
8. Add a little to the cheese mixture and stir it in
9. Add remainder and fold it in lightly but thoroughly
10. Turn the whole into a buttered baking dish
11. Bake in a moderate oven until golden (usually about an hour)
12. If the oven is too hot, stand the baking dish in a pan of boiling water while baking

title: Cheese Straws
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. butter
- 2 oz. flour
- 2 oz. breadcrumbs
- 2 oz. cheese
- salt and cayenne

## Directions

1. Grate the cheese and mix the ingredients into a paste; season with the pepper and salt; roll out very thin and cut into strips quarter of an inch wide and six inches long, then twist several times and lay on a buttered tin dish.
2. Bake about five minutes.

title: Cheese Straws
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. flour
- 4 oz. grated cheese (Parmesan is best)
- 1/4 tablespoonful salt
- Dash of cayenne
- 1/4 lb. butter

## Directions

1. Mix flour with grated cheese, add salt, cayenne, and butter.
2. Work this to a smooth paste sufficiently stiff to roll; add a very little water, if necessary.
3. Roll out in very thin strips and cut into straws, place on a greased tin and bake ten minutes in a moderate oven.
4. They must be straw color and very crisp.

title: Cheese Straws
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces each of butter, flour, bread crumbs and grated cheese
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Mix these ingredients into a paste, roll it a quarter of an inch thick, cut into narrow strips.
2. Bake until a light brown color.
3. Serve cold.

title: Cheese Straws
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. flour
- 2 oz. butter
- 3 oz. cheese
- 1 egg
- A little salt and cayenne

## Directions

1. Grate the cheese and mix all together; roll out and cut into thin strips with a pastry cutter and bake in a flat tin.

title: Cheese Straws
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup grated cheese
- One cup flour
- One-half cup butter
- Water

## Directions

1. Rub together and wet with water like pie-paste, roll thin and cut in strips; quick oven; just cut enough at one time for oven.

title: Cheese Straws
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup grated cheese
- 1 cup flour
- ½ teaspoon salt
- 2 tablespoons butter
- pinch cayenne pepper
- cold water

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly the cheese, flour, salt, pepper and butter
2. Add enough cold water so that the paste can be rolled out thin
3. Cut in stripes seven inches long and ⅜ inch wide
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Cheese Straws (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1 saltspoon salt
- 1 saltspoon paprika
- 1 saltspoon baking powder
- 1 cup grated cheese
- ¾ cup butter
- ice-water

## Directions

1. Sift into a bowl 1 cup flour, 1 saltspoon each of salt, paprika and baking powder
2. Add 1 cup grated cheese
3. Mix thoroughly
4. Add ¾ cup of butter and chop the ingredients together until of the consistency of meal
5. Mix to a paste with ice-water (use as little as possible)
6. Turn on to the pastry board and roll into a sheet about a quarter of an inch thick
7. Cut with a sharp knife into narrow strips about six inches long
8. Bake to a pale yellow
9. Arrange cob-house fashion on a doiley-covered plate

title: Cheese Tartlets
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce butter
- two ounces flour
- one yolk of an egg
- water
- salt
- two ounces grated cheese
- yolks of two eggs
- pepper
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Make a paste of one ounce butter, two ounces flour, one yolk of an egg, a little water and salt.
2. Line some patty pans with the paste.
3. Beat up two ounces grated cheese in a basin with yolks of two eggs.
4. Add pepper, salt, etc.
5. Work in a little cream or milk.
6. Fill each patty with mixture and bake in moderate oven.

title: Cheese Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 cup currants
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients
2. Use whites for frosting
3. Fill and bake

title: Chelsea Buns
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 2 teaspoons shortening
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- salt
- milk
- 1 tablespoon granulated sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Mix flour, baking powder, shortening, cinnamon, and salt with milk
2. Roll out a little over 3/4 of an inch thick
3. Spread over mixture of granulated sugar and butter beaten to a cream
4. Roll up
5. Cut off in lengths for buns
6. Set on ends and bake in quick oven

title: Chelsea Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 2 large teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 4 tablespoons lard
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 large teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup currants
- milk and sugar for glaze

## Directions

1. Make biscuit dough with 1 quart flour, 2 large teaspoons baking powder, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 4 tablespoons lard and 1 cup sweet milk
2. Roll out to about 1/4 inch thick
3. Spread with 2 tablespoons butter, 1 cup sugar, mixed with 1 large teaspoon cinnamon, 1 cup currants
4. Give a light roll with rolling-pin to keep the currants from dropping out
5. Roll as you would a jelly roll
6. Cut in slices about 1 inch thick
7. Place in pan and bake a light brown
8. Glaze with a spoonful of milk and sugar

title: Cherry Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup cherry preserves without pits
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg
- 2 heaping cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix brown sugar and butter
2. Add cherry preserves
3. Add eggs, sour milk, soda, cinnamon, and nutmeg
4. Add flour
5. Bake until done

title: Cherry Pickles
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries
- Salt
- Water
- Allspice
- Cloves
- Cinnamon
- Sugar
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Put cherries in cold salt and water for eighteen hours
2. Season vinegar with allspice, cloves, cinnamon and sugar
3. Pour hot over the cherries
4. Do this for three days in succession

title: Chestnut Stuffing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of large French chestnuts
- hot water
- one tablespoonful of butter
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one quarter of a teaspoonful of pepper
- one tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- stock
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Shell a quart of large French chestnuts.
2. Put them in hot water and boil until the skins are softened.
3. Drain off the water and remove the skins.
4. Replace the blanched chestnuts in water, and boil until soft.
5. Take out a few at a time, and press them through a colander or a potato press.
6. Season the mashed chestnuts with a tablespoonful of butter, a teaspoonful of salt, and a quarter of a teaspoonful of pepper.
7. Some cooks add a tablespoonful of chopped parsley, and moisten it with a little stock, or add a few breadcrumbs.

title: Chicken And Corn Broth
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart stock
- 1 can corn, chopped (or 8 ears)
- 1 tablespoonful butter mixed with 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 tablespoonful minced parsley
- 1 tablespoonful green onion tops
- 1 cup boiling milk
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Clear stock of fat after it is cold
2. Boil corn and liquor slowly together one hour after they begin to bubble
3. Rub thoroughly through a colander
4. Season and add herbs
5. Heat to boiling and stir in the floured butter
6. Simmer five minutes
7. Pour into the tureen and add the boiling milk

title: Chicken Broth
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fowl
- 4 quarts cold water
- salt and pepper
- 1 small cupful rice
- 1 cup milk (optional)
- 1 or 2 beaten eggs (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut up the fowl and put it into the pot with four quarts of cold water
2. Stew until there are but three quarts left
3. Take out the chicken
4. Season the liquor and add a small cupful of rice
5. Cook rice tender
6. May add a cup of milk and one or two beaten eggs just before serving

title: Chicken Broth
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fowl
- 4 quarts cold water
- 1/2 cupful rice
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the fowl carefully and wash it with a wet cloth
2. Cut it into pieces and remove the fat
3. Place the joints in a saucepan with a quart of water to each pound of fowl
4. Let it simmer until the meat is tender
5. Remove the breast after four hours and strain through a sieve
6. Let the soup stand until the grease rises and carefully remove it
7. Put the soup again in the saucepan
8. Add the breast of the chicken cut into dice and half cupful of rice
9. Salt and pepper to taste and cook until the rice is tender

title: Chicken Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- a plump chicken weighing about 4 or 5 pounds when dressed
- salt and pepper
- 1 small loaf of baker's bread
- a lump of butter, cut fine, size of an egg
- a little sage
- 2 beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Put chicken on and boil till tender and well done
2. Remove all the bones and grisly parts and chop fine, with a little salt and pepper
3. Boil down the water it was cooked in to a pint, and it jellies, and stir it in with the chicken
4. Put bread through the meat grinder with a lump of butter, cut fine, size of an egg, a little sage, salt and pepper
5. Stir in 2 beaten eggs to hold it together
6. Put half of the chicken chopped in a deep earthen or granite dish, spread level
7. Put on the dressing, spread level, then the rest of the minced chicken
8. Set in the oven till hot, then set in a cool place over night
9. When cold slice and serve

title: Chicken Croquettes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 1 pt. milk
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- pepper, salt and a dash of cayenne
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil chicken, remove skin and chop fine
2. Cook the sauce and add the chopped chicken
3. Mix well, then set aside to cool
4. When cool mould into shape
5. Dip in egg and bread-crumbs and boil in hot fat

title: Chicken Croquettes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 solid pt. chopped chicken
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful pepper
- 1 cup cream or chicken stock
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of onion juice
- 1 tablespoonful lemon juice
- 1 pt. bread-crumbs
- 3 tablespoonfuls butter

## Directions

1. Put the cream or stock on to boil
2. Mix the flour and butter together and stir into the boiling cream
3. Add the chicken and seasoning
4. Boil for two minutes, and add two of the eggs well beaten
5. Take from the fire immediately and set away to cool
6. When cool, shape, roll in egg and crumbs and fry

title: Chicken Croquettes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups finely chopped chicken
- 1 cup chicken stock
- 1/2 cup cream or milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- a little mace, nutmeg, pepper, and salt

## Directions

1. Mix the butter and flour together
2. When the milk and stock have come to a boil add them to the butter and flour
3. Add the meat, and let all simmer together ten minutes
4. Add eggs pretty well beaten
5. Leave on the back of stove for a few minutes, but do not allow it to simmer
6. Pour in a dish to cool
7. When quite cool form into small rolls and roll in bread-crumbs, then in egg, and again in bread-crumbs
8. Cook in boiling lard

title: Chicken Croquettes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Four cups minced chicken
- One cup bread crumbs
- three eggs
- Tablespoonful butter and seasoning

## Directions

1. Mix and make into balls, dip into beaten egg and bread crumbs
2. Fry a nice brown

title: Chicken Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 tablespoon summer savory
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix butter well through bread crumbs
2. Add 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon pepper, 1 tablespoon summer savory
3. Mix all together with 2 eggs
4. Put in chicken and roast

title: Chicken Fricassee
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- butter or drippings
- boiling water
- bouquet of herbs
- salt
- pepper
- salt pork
- flour
- cream or milk
- egg yolks
- sherry
- mushrooms
- rice or toast

## Directions

1. Cut a chicken into eleven pieces: two drumsticks, two second joints, two wings, two breasts, three back pieces.
2. Put the pieces in a saucepan with two tablespoonfuls of butter or drippings.
3. Let them brown slightly on both sides, but use care that they do not burn.
4. When a little colored, add enough boiling water to cover them.
5. Add a bouquet of herbs, salt and pepper, and a few slices of salt pork.
6. Simmer until tender.
7. Arrange the pieces neatly on a dish, using the best ones outside.
8. Strain the liquor from the pot and take off the fat.
9. Make a white sauce of one tablespoonful of butter and two of flour and a cupful of the liquor from the pot.
10. Season to taste.
11. Remove from the fire, and when a little cool add a cupful of cream or milk beaten up with two or three yolks of eggs.
12. Place again on the fire until the eggs are a little thickened, but do not let it boil, or they will curdle.
13. A tablespoonful of sherry may be added, if liked, or a half can of mushrooms.
14. Pour the gravy over the chicken pieces.
15. A border of rice may be placed around the chicken, or sippets of toast used.

title: Chicken Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- peppercorn
- water

## Directions

1. Cut chicken into joints and put into a pan or stew-jar
2. Put in a very little salt and a peppercorn, and just cover with water
3. Let it stew gently for two or three hours or more, adding a little water if required
4. When the meat falls from the bones take off the meat and pound up the bones, and give them an extra boil
5. Strain the liquid from the meat (and bones) and when cold take off any fat

title: Chicken Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook a chicken in enough water to little more than cover it
2. Let it stew gently until the meat drops from the bones, and the broth is reduced to about a pint
3. Season it to taste, with a little salt and pepper
4. Strain and press, first through a colander, then through a coarse cloth
5. Set it over the fire again, and cook a few minutes longer
6. Turn it into an earthen vegetable dish to harden
7. Set it on the ice in the refrigerator
8. Eat cold in slices
9. Nice made into sandwiches, with thin slices of bread, lightly spread with butter

title: Chicken Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens
- pepper
- salt
- sage
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Boil 2 chickens in as little water as possible to cook them without burning, until the meat will drop from the bones
2. Remove the meat from the bone and cut it up fine
3. Put it back in the kettle with plenty of water, pepper and salt and sage
4. Heat it thoroughly
5. Slice hard-boiled eggs in the bottom of a dish, then pour the chicken over it while hot
6. Place a weight upon it, and set in a cool place
7. When cold it will come out in a loaf and may be sliced

title: Chicken Mousse
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. cooked ham
- 1 pt. cream
- 1 pt. aspic jelly (liquid)
- A very little glaze
- 1 liqueur of brandy
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Have ready a saucepan with the aspic just warm, mince the ham and pass through a wire sieve, add it to the aspic with the glaze brandy and pepper.
2. Whip the cream a little thick and add half a pint.
3. Whisk over a slow fire until the ingredients are well mixed (do not allow it to get too hot).
4. Take it off the fire and slowly stir in the remaining half pint of cream.
5. Put a little cochineal to make the mousse a nice pink (the color of ham).
6. Pour the whole into a souffle dish and let it stand to get quite cold.
7. When it is quite set, pour over the top a little aspic; let this also set, then ornament with truffles or white of egg.

title: Chicken Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 part ice-cold water
- 1 part thick sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
- 1 quart flour
- chicken
- rolled crackers
- broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Line a deep biscuit pan with pastry made with 1 part ice-cold water and one part of thick sour cream, with 1 teaspoon soda and 2 of cream of tartar to 1 quart of flour
2. Bone the chicken and distribute white and dark meat evenly over crust
3. Sprinkle rolled crackers 1/2 inch in depth over meat when pan was half filled
4. Simmer broth to good strength, thicken and season as for gravy
5. Pour in pan enough to nearly cover second layer of meat
6. Leave opening in top crust for escape of steam
7. Bake 40 minutes

title: Chicken Pie 2
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- 2 medium-sized potatoes
- 2 large onions
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Boil the chicken until tender, having first put in two medium-sized potatoes and 2 large onions sliced very thin, pepper and salt, or any seasoning one may wish
2. Thicken gravy nicely with little flour and water, having enough to cover chicken
3. Pour all into granite pan until crust is prepared
4. Prepare crust for top: Same as for pie crust, but should be at least half an inch thick, and not too rich
5. Bake in quick oven from 20 minutes to 1/2 an hour

title: Chicken Pie 3
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- chicken broth
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Cook chicken until tender, add salt and pepper to taste
2. Place the chicken flat in a stone baking dish
3. Make a gravy of chicken broth, not enough to cover
4. For Crust: Mix 2 cups of flour, sifted, with 1 teaspoon baking powder, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 3 tablespoons butter mixed with flour
5. Beat 1 egg, add to it 1 cup sweet milk, stir into flour
6. Dip in spoonfuls over chicken
7. Bake in moderate oven from 1/2 to 3/4 hour

title: Chicken Pie 4
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- milk
- 2 cups cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- butter
- 1/2 cup sweet cream

## Directions

1. Stew a nice chicken till tender, remove the larger bones, salt and pepper
2. Thicken gravy with flour made smooth with milk and have plenty of gravy or the pie will be dry
3. For Crust: Use one pint or 2 cups cream; if sour add 1 teaspoon soda, if sweet 1/2 teaspoon soda
4. Add 1 teaspoon baking powder in flour enough to roll
5. Line 3 quart pan around sides, put in chicken and piece of butter, with 1/2 cup sweet cream, butter size of an egg
6. Cover with remainder of crust dough, cut a slit in top for steam escape and bake
7. When nice and brown remove from oven, cover for ten minutes and serve

title: Chicken Pot-Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- large chicken
- cold water
- salt
- pepper
- flour, two tablespoonfuls
- butter
- bread-dough

## Directions

1. Cut and joint a large chicken, cover with cold water, and let it boil gently until tender.
2. Season with salt and pepper, and thicken the gravy with two tablespoonfuls of flour, mixed smooth with a piece of butter the size of an egg.
3. Have ready nice light bread-dough; cut with the top of a wineglass about half an inch thick; let them stand half an hour and rise.
4. Drop these into the boiling gravy.
5. Put the cover on the pot closely, wrap a cloth around it, in order that no steam shall escape; and by no means allow the pot to cease boiling.
6. Boil three-quarters of an hour.

title: Chicken Pot-Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of fine fowls
- pepper
- light biscuit dough
- cold ham
- potatoes
- water, a quart

## Directions

1. Take a pair of fine fowls; cut them up, wash the pieces, and season with pepper only.
2. Make a light biscuit dough, and plenty of it, as it is always much liked by the eaters of pot-pie.
3. Roll out the dough not very thin, and cut most of it into long squares.
4. Butter the sides of a pot, and line them with dough nearly to the top.
5. Lay slices of cold ham at the bottom of the pot, and then the pieces of fowl, interspersed all through with squares of dough and potatoes, pared and quartered.
6. Pour in a quart of water.
7. Cover the whole with a lid of dough, having a slit in the centre, through which the gravy will bubble up.
8. Boil it steadily for two hours.

title: Chicken Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 qts. cooked chicken (cut in dice)
- 1 qt. celery (cut in 1/2-inch pieces)
- 4 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- 2 tablespoonfuls oil
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1/4 teaspoonful pepper

## Directions

1. Place chicken in an earthen bowl, and season with vinegar, oil, salt and pepper
2. Set away in a cold place for two or three hours
3. Put minced celery in the ice-box until serving time
4. Make the mayonnaise dressing
5. Mix the chicken and celery together, and add half the dressing
6. Arrange in a salad bowl or on a flat dish, and pour the remainder of the dressing over it
7. Garnish with white celery leaves

title: Chicken Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- white meat of cold boiled or roasted chicken or turkey
- celery (chopped), same bulk as meat
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 raw egg well beaten
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 3 teaspoons salad oil
- 2 teaspoons white sugar
- 1/8 teacup vinegar

## Directions

1. Mince the meat, removing all fat gristle and skin
2. Mix with celery
3. Pour dressing over when cold

title: Chicken Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- white meat of cold boiled or roasted chicken or turkey
- 3/4 same bulk celery (chopped)
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 raw egg
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 3 teaspoons salad oil
- 2 teaspoons white sugar
- 3/4 teacup vinegar

## Directions

1. Mince the meat, removing all fat gristle and skin
2. Mix with celery
3. Pour dressing over when cold

title: Chicken Salad (Second Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold left-over chicken
- celery, same quantity as chicken
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- cream

## Directions

1. Chop chicken and celery fine
2. Add salt, pepper and a little vinegar and cream
3. Mix together

title: Chicken Salad (Third Version)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 chickens chopped coarsely
- 3 bunches celery chopped
- 2 heads of lettuce
- 2 hard-boiled eggs chopped fine
- a few pickled cucumbers
- salt to taste
- mayonnaise dressing
- lemons for garnish

## Directions

1. Chop chickens coarsely
2. Chop celery and lettuce
3. Chop hard-boiled eggs fine
4. Mix with mayonnaise dressing
5. Garnish with lemons just before sending to table

title: Chicken Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled or roasted chicken
- gravy, water or cream
- butter
- pepper
- buttered bread slices

## Directions

1. Mince up fine any cold boiled or roasted chicken
2. Put it into a saucepan with gravy, water or cream enough to soften it
3. Add a good piece of butter and a pinch of pepper
4. Work it very smooth while it is heating until it looks almost like a paste
5. Spread it on a plate to cool
6. Spread it between slices of buttered bread

title: Chicken Saute, a la Webb
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- nice plump young chicken
- flour
- good butter
- half clove of garlic
- quarter pod green peppers
- strong chicken broth
- carrots
- turnips
- salt
- Saratoga chips
- French peas

## Directions

1. Secure a nice plump young chicken, clean and cut into pieces, not too small.
2. Flour them and saute in good butter until of a nice golden brown color.
3. Mince half clove of garlic very fine, also quarter pod green peppers and cook in butter for five minutes.
4. Mix sufficient flour to absorb butter, and moisten with half a pint of strong chicken broth.
5. Stew with a few carrots and turnips cut into small diamond shapes until tender.
6. Season with salt, and serve on platter with Saratoga chip border sprinkled with French peas.

title: Chicken Stewed with Biscuit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- soda or baking-powder biscuits

## Directions

1. Take chickens, and make a fricassee
2. Just before you are ready to dish it up, have ready two baking-tins of rich soda or baking-powder biscuits
3. Take them from the oven hot, split them apart by breaking them with your hands
4. Lay them on a large meat platter, covering it
5. Pour the hot chicken stew over all
6. Send to the table hot

title: Chiffonade Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large green peppers
- 1 grape fruit
- 3 large tomatoes
- 1 head lettuce
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Cut open peppers, remove the seeds and veins and cut into shreds; drop into boiling water for one minute; drain and cool.
2. Cut a grape fruit. Take out the pulp with a spoon and cut into bits.
3. Peel tomatoes, and cut into small pieces.
4. Shred lettuce finely.
5. Marinade each article separately with a French dressing.
6. Put the grape fruit pulp in the centre of the salad dish and arrange around it the prepared peppers, tomatoes and lettuce in such a way as to display the different colors to the best advantage.

title: Children Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons syrup
- 3 tablespoons melted butter
- 3/4 cup raisins
- 3/4 cup currants
- 1 teacup buttermilk
- 1 small teaspoon soda
- 4 cups flour

## Directions

1. Boil in a cloth for 2 hours
2. Serve with sauce

title: Children's Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 teaspoons vanilla
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix butter, sugar, and milk
2. Add flour and baking powder
3. Add vanilla and eggs
4. Bake in layer cake or sponge cake form

title: Children's Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or more good-size potatoes
- 2 onions
- water
- 1 cup of cream
- 2 cups milk
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Pare potatoes, slice in thin slices
2. Cut 2 onions fine, add water enough to cook
3. When done add a cup of cream and 2 cups milk
4. Let come to a boil, season with pepper and salt, and butter

title: Chili Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 48 good-sized tomatoes
- 8 onions
- 16 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 4 cups vinegar
- 4 tablespoonfuls salt
- 6 green peppers
- 2 tablespoonfuls allspice
- 2 tablespoonfuls cloves
- 2 tablespoonfuls cinnamon
- 2 tablespoonfuls mustard

## Directions

1. Have spices ground.
2. Peel tomatoes, and onions, stem peppers; chop together and boil till onions are soft.

title: Chili Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ripe tomatoes
- 2 large onions
- 4 green peppers
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 4 teaspoonfuls salt
- 1 teacup vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop onions and peppers very fine.
2. Boil one hour and a half.
3. Add one teacup of vinegar half an hour before taking from the fire.
4. Bottle, cork closely.
5. Keep in the coolest part of the cellar.

title: Chili Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large onions
- 3 peppers, chopped
- 6 ripe tomatoes
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful nutmeg
- 1 tablespoonful cloves
- 1 tablespoonful ginger
- 1 tablespoonful horseradish

## Directions

1. Cook slowly for two hours.

title: Chili Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen large ripe tomatoes
- 6 large sour apples
- 6 onions
- 1 large head of celery
- 1 pint cider vinegar
- 2 cups brown sugar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Scald and skin tomatoes and cut into quarters
2. Cut apples, onions and celery into thin slices
3. Put all into a kettle together with cider vinegar and brown sugar
4. Add salt and pepper to taste
5. Cook until tender (about 1 1/2 hours)
6. Seal hot

title: Chili Sauce 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 ripe tomatoes
- 10 large onions
- 8 red peppers
- 6 cups vinegar
- 20 tablespoons sugar
- 5 tablespoons salt

## Directions

1. Chop fine
2. Boil 1 hour

title: Chili Sauce 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quarts tomatoes
- 2 pounds sugar
- 3/4 pound raisins
- 2 cups stewed apples
- 2 tablespoons allspice
- 1/2 tablespoon cloves
- 3/4 tablespoon cinnamon
- 4 tablespoons ginger
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop raisins
2. Boil 1 hour

title: Chili Sauce 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large ripe tomatoes
- 4 apples
- 2 onions
- 2 red peppers
- 4 tablespoons brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons salt
- 3 cups vinegar
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- ginger
- cinnamon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop fine
2. Boil till soft

title: Chip Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- long-shaped potatoes
- boiling fat

## Directions

1. Peel and boil some long-shaped potatoes till nearly done
2. Strain
3. Cut each potato lengthwise into 4 pieces
4. Put into a baking dish in boiling fat
5. Bake in oven till nicely browned

title: Chispe Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup white sugar
- 3 small teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup sweet cream
- butter size of an egg
- flour enough to mix in a soft dough
- granulated sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients in a soft dough
2. Roll to whatever thickness you want and sprinkle with granulated sugar
3. Roll once and cut out
4. Bake in a hot oven

title: Choca
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. milk
- 1 pt. boiling water
- 4 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 1 scant cup grated chocolate
- 1 pt. strong coffee
- 10 drops vanilla
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Scald one pint of milk and add the same amount of boiling water.
2. Mix together sugar and grated chocolate.
3. Add sufficient of the hot liquid to mix to a smooth paste, gradually dilute and turn into the milk and water, then cook gently for five minutes.
4. Add a pinch of salt, coffee, and vanilla, and take from the fire.
5. Serve with whipped cream for luncheon or 5 o'clock tea.

title: Chocolate
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoonfuls grated chocolate
- 1 pt. boiling water
- 1 pt. milk
- Sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Rub the chocolate to a paste with a little cold water
2. Stir into the hot water
3. Boil twenty minutes
4. Add the milk and boil ten minutes longer, stirring often
5. Sweeten in the cups
6. It is improved by laying upon the surface of each cup a teaspoonful of cream

title: Chocolate
body:
## Ingredients

- chocolate
- cold water
- yolks of eggs
- milk
- water

## Directions

1. Scrape chocolate fine
2. Mix with a little cold water and the yolks of eggs well beaten
3. Add this to equal parts of milk and water
4. Boil well, being careful that it does not burn
5. Sweeten to taste
6. Serve hot

title: Chocolate and Almond Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1/2 cup grated chocolate (3 ounces)
- 1/2 cup milk
- 5 eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 cup chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Cook butter, flour, chocolate and milk over fire, stirring constantly
2. Set it to cool
3. Beat yolks with sugar until creamy
4. Add it to cold mixture, a spoonful at a time
5. Mix thoroughly
6. Fold in beaten whites
7. Turn into a buttered mould
8. Steam 2 to 3 hours
9. Serve with hard sauce

title: Chocolate Bread Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup stale bread crumbs
- 2 cups milk
- a heaping tablespoon butter
- grated Cowan's Chocolate or Cocoa
- 1/2 cup sugar
- a teaspoon vanilla
- yolks of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Put bread crumbs and milk on the fire
2. Heat to the boiling point
3. Add a heaping tablespoon butter
4. Add grated Cowan's Chocolate or Cocoa
5. Add 1/2 cup sugar and a teaspoon vanilla
6. Take from the fire and cool
7. Stir in the beaten yolks of 3 eggs
8. Last, fold in the stiff whites
9. Put in a buttered dish and bake
10. Serve with cream

title: Chocolate Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1/2 teacup melted butter
- Milk enough to make batter
- 2 teacups flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- A pinch of salt
- 1 cake chocolate and 1 teacup water
- Confectioner's sugar

## Directions

1. Beat whites and yolks separately first, and then together; beat into this sugar, and then melted butter.
2. Sift flour into which you have stirred two teaspoons baking powder and a pinch of salt.
3. Beat it gradually into the eggs, etc., and then add enough milk to make a moderately stiff batter.
4. Bake in two layer cake tins in quick oven.
5. Melt chocolate in teacup boiling water on stove; add sugar enough to stiffen it into a thick paste; put this between layers while they are hot and ice top of cake.

title: Chocolate Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 slice of butter, one inch thick, off a print
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 heaping teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla
- 5 heaping tablespoonfuls chocolate
- 3 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls milk
- 1 pint cream whipped very stiff
- sugar to taste
- 1 teaspoonful coffee extract
- chopped walnuts

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar together
2. add the beaten yolks of eggs
3. then add the above melted mixture
4. then the flour, which must be sifted five times, with the baking powder in it
5. Add alternately with the milk until both are used up
6. Then the vanilla, and lastly the beaten whites of the eggs
7. This can be baked in one good-sized tin as a loaf, or made into three layers, when the following filling is used: One pint cream whipped very stiff, sugar to taste and flavor with one teaspoonful coffee extract
8. Put a layer of chopped walnuts, then the cream, between the layers, and on top
9. Bake in a medium oven

title: Chocolate Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/4 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 1/4 pound grated Cowan's Perfection Chocolate
- 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/4 teaspoon soda
- 5 tablespoons sugar
- 3 tablespoons boiling water

## Directions

1. To the grated chocolate add 5 tablespoons sugar, 3 tablespoons boiling water and stir over fire until smooth and glossy
2. Cream butter and sugar together
3. Stir chocolate mixture into the creamed butter and sugar
4. Add well beaten eggs
5. Add milk
6. Mix flour with soda and cream tartar
7. Add flour mixture to other ingredients
8. Bake 20 minutes in a moderate oven

title: Chocolate Cake (Simple)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 oz. chocolate, melted
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat butter to a cream and gradually beat into this one cupful of sugar, chocolate melted, also two unbeaten eggs
2. Beat vigorously for five minutes
3. then stir in milk, flour, with a generous teaspoonful of baking powder, flavor with vanilla
4. pour into a buttered shallow cake-pan and bake for half an hour in a moderate oven
5. Any frosting can be used

title: Chocolate Caramel Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 13.5 cups brown sugar
- butter size of walnut
- 3 tablespoons sweet cream
- 5 cent cake of chocolate grated or about 1/2 cup cocoa

## Directions

1. Boil till it threads
2. Beat in a 5 cent cake of chocolate that has been grated, or about 1/2 cup cocoa

title: Chocolate Caramels
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. brown sugar
- 1/4 lb. chocolate
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 small cup cold water
- 2 teaspoonfuls vanilla

## Directions

1. Put on the fire brown sugar, chocolate broken into small pieces, and a small cupful of cold water
2. Boil this until a little of it hardens in water
3. Stir into it butter and vanilla
4. Turn into buttered pans and cut into squares

title: Chocolate Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oz. chocolate, grated
- 6 oz. sugar
- 1 pt. cream
- Yolks of 6 eggs
- 2 oz. gelatine

## Directions

1. Grate finely six ounces chocolate; put chocolate into a saucepan with cream, sugar, and yolks of eggs.
2. Stir over fire until it thickens, run through sieve; add dissolved gelatine.
3. Mix and fill mould and place on ice.

title: Chocolate Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. chocolate
- 1 pt. milk
- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls sugar

## Directions

1. Melt chocolate.
2. Heat one pint milk; add yolks of eggs, and sugar.
3. Stir until creamy; add the chocolate.
4. It must not boil.

title: Chocolate Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- six ounces chocolate, grated
- six ounces sugar
- one pint cream
- yolks of six eggs
- two ounces gelatine

## Directions

1. Grate finely six ounces chocolate
2. put chocolate into a saucepan with cream, sugar, and yolks of eggs
3. Stir over fire until it thickens, run through sieve
4. add dissolved gelatine
5. Mix and fill mould and place on ice

title: Chocolate Cream Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cake chocolate
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1/8 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil gently until thick

title: Chocolate Creams
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- White of 1 egg
- 6 tablespoonfuls sweet chocolate
- Equal quantity of water
- Sugar (confectioner's) to make stiff

## Directions

1. To the white of an egg, mixed with as much water, add enough confectioner's sugar to make a dough-like paste that can be worked with the fingers into small balls
2. Grate sweetened chocolate, melt it, without water, in a cup on the stove, and when smooth and thick dip your balls of sugar-paste into it and then let them dry on waxed paper

title: Chocolate Eclairs
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- plain tube
- buttered sheet
- cream filling
- chocolate frosting

## Directions

1. Press paste from bag with plain tube, 3/8 inch in diameter on buttered sheet, in strips 3 1/2 inches long
2. When baked or cold, fill with cream filling
3. Spread smooth on under side with chocolate frosting

title: Chocolate Fudge
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cake chocolate
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups white sugar
- Butter size of walnut

## Directions

1. Cook all together
2. Test in cold water until you can roll well in fingers
3. Take off stove
4. Stir until cool
5. Pour into plates and cut into squares

title: Chocolate Fudge
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 ounces Cowan's Perfection Chocolate
- 1 tablespoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Heat sugar, milk and butter to boiling point
2. Add chocolate, stirring constantly until it is melted
3. Boil until mixture forms a soft ball in cold water
4. Remove from fire and cool slightly
5. Add vanilla and beat until creamy and until mixture begins to sugar slightly around edge of pan
6. Pour on buttered plate and when cool enough mark in squares

title: Chocolate Glace
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 square chocolate
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cupful milk
- 1.5 cupfuls flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat butter to a cream
2. Gradually work into this 1 cup sugar
3. Add chocolate, melted, and unbeaten eggs
4. Beat vigorously five minutes
5. Stir in milk
6. Stir in flour with which has been mixed 2 teaspoons of baking powder
7. Bake in a buttered, shallow cake-pan for half an hour in a moderate oven
8. Ice first with white icing, flavored with orange juice
9. When this is set, ice with thick chocolate icing

title: Chocolate Icing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- white of one egg
- one teaspoon vanilla
- one tablespoon water
- icing sugar
- one-sixth of a package of Baker's chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat white of one egg stiff
2. add one teaspoon of vanilla and one tablespoon of water, and enough icing sugar to make stiff
3. Melt one-sixth of a package of Baker's chocolate
4. beat into egg and sugar and spread it on the cake

title: Chocolate Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/8 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 ounce unsweetened chocolate
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar, milk and cream of tartar together for 6 minutes
2. Remove from stove and add butter, chocolate and vanilla
3. Beat until proper consistency to spread on cake

title: Chocolate Icing (Variation)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- butter size of egg
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 7/8 cake chocolate

## Directions

1. Let it boil till it begins to harden

title: Chocolate Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups white sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 3/4 cup chocolate
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Bake in 2 tins and ice with white icing

title: Chocolate Loaf Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 oz. chocolate
- 1 cup butter
- 1.5 cups sugar
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 4 eggs
- 0.75 cup milk
- 1.5 cups flour
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve chocolate in five tablespoonfuls boiling water
2. Beat butter to a cream
3. Add gradually sugar, beating all while
4. Add yolks of eggs, beat again
5. Add milk, then the melted chocolate, and flour
6. Beat the whites of the eggs to a stiff froth
7. Add to the mixture one teaspoon vanilla and baking powder sifted with flour

title: Chocolate Marble Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- 1¾ cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- whites of 4 eggs
- 5 tablespoons of grated Cowan's Chocolate
- milk
- vanilla
- pulverized sugar
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add milk and flour
3. Add whites of 4 eggs last
4. Take ½ of mixture and add 5 tablespoons of grated Cowan's Chocolate, wet with milk and flavored with vanilla
5. Drop in a pan in alternate spoonfuls
6. Bake and ice with chocolate icing made with pulverized sugar and grated chocolate

title: Chocolate Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon Cowan's Perfection Chocolate
- a little boiling water
- 2 small cups of new milk
- 3 tablespoons flour
- ½ cup white sugar
- 1 egg
- a little cold milk
- 2½ teaspoons of sugar
- ½ teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Grate Cowan's Perfection Chocolate
2. Dissolve in a cup with a little boiling water
3. Have in your dish 2 small cups of new milk, then add the melted chocolate
4. When this boils add 3 tablespoons flour, ½ cup white sugar, yolk of 1 egg
5. Make smooth with a little of the cold milk
6. Beat white of the egg for the top with 2½ teaspoons of sugar and ½ teaspoon vanilla
7. Place on pie and brown

title: Chocolate Pie No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ teacup Cowan's Perfection Chocolate (grated)
- 1½ teacups hot water
- ½ teacup sugar
- butter the size of an egg
- 1 tablespoon vanilla
- beaten yolk of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients well
2. Cook on top of stove until thick
3. Stir constantly
4. Pour into the shell
5. Let it cool
6. Beat the whites and spread on top
7. Brown in oven

title: Chocolate Pie No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons Cowan's Perfection Chocolate (grated)
- 1 cup sweet milk
- yolks of 3 eggs
- ¾ cup sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Let 2 tablespoons chocolate and 1 cup sweet milk come to a boil
2. When cold add yolks of 3 eggs
3. Add ¾ cup sugar
4. Beat to a cream
5. Flavor with vanilla
6. Bake with under crust
7. Save whites of eggs for icing

title: Chocolate Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup grated Cowan's Perfection Chocolate
- 1 1/2 pints sweet milk
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- butter size of walnut
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 teaspoons vanilla

## Directions

1. Dissolve chocolate in milk
2. Add cornstarch and eggs
3. Boil for 8 or 10 minutes
4. Put in buttered cups to cool
5. Eat with cream and sugar

title: Chocolate Pudding (Variant 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons chocolate or good cocoa
- 1 scant teaspoon salt
- cold water
- 1 quart boiling water
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Blend cornstarch, chocolate or cocoa, and salt with cold water
2. Add 1 quart boiling water
3. Boil 2 minutes
4. Add white sugar, butter, and vanilla
5. Serve warm or cold with cream

title: Chocolate Pudding (Variant 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups milk
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoons Cowan's Perfection Chocolate (grated)
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 2 eggs
- pinch of salt
- vanilla to flavor

## Directions

1. Let milk, bread crumbs, chocolate, and sugar come to a boil, stirring the while
2. When cool add eggs, pinch of salt, and vanilla to flavor
3. Pour into buttered dish
4. Bake 1/2 hour
5. Add the whipped whites of eggs

title: Chocolate Pudding (Variant 4)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces Cowan's Perfection Chocolate (grated)
- 1 pint milk
- 1/2 cup bread crumbs
- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants

## Directions

1. Steam 1 hour
2. Serve hot

title: Chocolate Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- two tablespoonfuls grated chocolate
- three tablespoonfuls cream
- three tablespoonfuls flour
- one cup sugar
- one-half teaspoonful butter
- one teaspoonful vanilla

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients and boil

title: Chocolate Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups milk
- 1½ teaspoons cornstarch
- 2 squares Cowan's Chocolate
- 2 tablespoons hot water
- 2 eggs
- 7 to 8 cups powdered sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Blend cornstarch with 1 pint milk
2. Add rest scalded
3. Cook in double boiler with chocolate
4. Add 4 tablespoons sugar and hot water
5. Stir until smooth
6. Add to cooked mixture
7. Beat whites until stiff
8. Add rest of sugar and unbeaten yolks
9. Stir into cooked part
10. Cook 1 minute
11. Add vanilla

title: Chocolate Souffle
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 cup powdered sugar
- 3 tablespoons Cowan's Perfection Chocolate (grated)
- Whites of 6 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks and powdered sugar with grated chocolate for 10 minutes
2. Beat whites of eggs with salt very stiff
3. Fold in whites
4. Turn into a buttered pan
5. Bake 10 minutes in a hot oven
6. Serve immediately

title: Chocolate Sponge
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls milk
- 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls grated chocolate
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- 1/4 cup flour

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of the eggs with the sugar
2. add the grated chocolate and milk by turns
3. put the baking powder into the flour and add them to the mixture
