4. and lastly the beaten whites of the eggs
5. Steam an hour and a quarter
6. Serve with whipped cream

title: Chocolate Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup milk
- 1/2 cup water
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- pinch soda
- 1/2 cake grated chocolate
- tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Add sugar, butter, soda, and grated chocolate to milk and water
2. When this boils add tablespoons cornstarch dissolved in a little water
3. Let this boil until thickened
4. Add 1 teaspoon vanilla
5. Place in tart shells and put icing on top

title: Chopped Pears
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 lbs. sickle pears
- 8 lbs. sugar
- 1/4 lb. candied ginger
- 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Chip or slice the pears, slice the ginger root and let them boil together with the sugar for one hour, slowly.
2. Boil the lemons whole in clear water until tender, then cut up in small bits, removing the seeds.
3. Add to the pears and boil an hour longer.
4. Put in glasses.
5. Use candied or crystallized ginger in preference to the green root.

title: Chopped Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cabbage
- 1 bunch celery
- 3 large green cucumbers
- 12 medium green tomatoes
- vinegar
- 1 pound mustard
- tumeric
- salt
- sugar to taste
- black and red pepper

## Directions

1. Chop cabbage, celery, cucumbers, and tomatoes fine
2. Cover with vinegar
3. Put on stove and simmer well
4. Add mustard, tumeric, salt, sugar to taste, and black and red pepper
5. Simmer 20 minutes

title: Chopped Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon green tomatoes, chopped fine
- 1 gallon cabbage
- 3/4 gallon onions
- 2 dozen large green peppers
- 1 dozen large red peppers
- salt
- 1 pint white mustard seed
- 1 gallon vinegar
- 1 1/2 pounds brown sugar

## Directions

1. Chop all vegetables fine
2. Put all together in a vessel
3. Cover with salt
4. Let stand over night
5. Next morning put all in a bag and drain 24 hours
6. After draining, add white mustard seed
7. Boil vinegar with brown sugar
8. While boiling hot pour over chopped ingredients
9. Put all on the stove together and cook a few minutes

title: Chow Chow
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads cabbage
- 2 heads cauliflower
- 2 qts. onions
- 2 qts. green tomatoes
- 1 small cup sugar
- 2 cucumbers
- 6 roots of celery
- 2 green peppers
- 2 qts. vinegar
- 1/2 lb. mustard
- 1 oz. turmeric
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Chop fine.
2. Boil in just enough water to cover until moderately soft.
3. Strain.
4. Take vinegar, mustard, turmeric, sugar and salt to taste.
5. Let this come to a boil and pour it over the mixture.

title: Chow Chow
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes
- 6 large onions
- 4 peppers
- 1 head cabbage
- 3 pounds sugar
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- cloves and allspice
- vinegar to cover

## Directions

1. Cook until tender

title: Chow Chow 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts small onions
- 2 quarts green tomatoes
- 2 quarts cucumbers
- 2 heads cauliflower
- 4 heads celery
- 1 dozen green peppers
- salt and water

## Directions

1. Cook all together in salt and water until tender, not soft

title: Chow Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 cup flour
- 3 eggs
- 3/4 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Mix as for cream puffs

title: Chow-Chow
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. green tomatoes
- 1 qt. gherkins
- 1 qt. ripe cucumbers
- 1 qt. celery
- 3 cups vinegar
- 3 cups brown sugar
- 1 qt. white onions
- 1 qt. string beans
- 1 large cauliflower
- 6 green peppers
- 6 tablespoonfuls mustard
- 4 tablespoonfuls flour
- 3 tablespoonfuls turmeric
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Chop all very fine and cover with a good sprinkling of salt and water over night
2. Bring to a boil and drain
3. Boil together vinegar, sugar, mustard, flour and turmeric
4. Pour boiling hot over vegetables and bottle

title: Christian Science Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 4 tablespoons blackstrap
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- A pinch of salt
- 2 cups flour (scant)
- Dates

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, sugar, blackstrap, cinnamon, sour cream, soda, salt, and flour
2. Bake in 2 layers
3. Cook the dates and spread between layers as filling

title: Christian Science Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 4 tablespoons blackstrap
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- a pinch of salt
- 2 cups flour (scant)
- dates for filling

## Directions

1. Bake in 2 layers
2. Cook the dates and spread between layers

title: Christmas Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 13 1/2 pounds flour
- 1 pound butter
- 12 ounces moist brown sugar
- 3 pounds currants
- 1/2 pound citron peel
- 5 eggs
- 2 teaspoons vinegar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 4 ounces ground almonds

## Directions

1. Warm the butter slightly to make it beat easily, but not oily
2. Put it into a bowl with the sugar and beat both to a cream
3. Sift the flour and mix it in, a tablespoon at a time
4. Add the currants washed and dried, the peel chopped fine, the ground almonds and the soda
5. When all these are mixed, add the yolks beaten to a froth
6. Mix for five minutes
7. Add the whites of the eggs beaten to a froth
8. Add lastly the vinegar

title: Christmas Cake No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 3 pounds raisins
- 3 pounds currants
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 1 cup blackstrap
- 1 dozen eggs
- 1 tablespoon soda
- 2 ounces lemon peel
- 2 ounces citron peel
- 2 ounces orange peel
- a little salt
- 4 cups sugar
- flour to make stiff

## Directions

1. This will make a 4-story cake

title: Christmas Cake No. 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 8 eggs well beaten
- 3 pounds currants
- 3 pounds raisins
- 1 pound mixed peel cut fine
- 1/2 pound blanched almonds chopped fine
- 5 cups sifted flour
- 2 nutmegs
- 2 teaspoons mixed spice
- vanilla and lemon
- 1/2 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Weigh your butter and cut in pieces, soften, but do not melt
2. Add the sugar
3. Beat the eggs and put into the sugar and butter
4. Sift the flour over your fruit, mixed well
5. Add to the other ingredients
6. Last of all, put in the mixed peel and add nuts

title: Christmas Cake No. 6
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds seeded raisins
- 3 pounds cleaned currants
- 1/2 pound lemon peel
- 2 pounds light brown sugar
- 2 pounds butter
- 4 nutmegs
- 15 eggs
- 1/8 teaspoon ground cloves
- 1/8 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/8 teaspoon ginger
- 1/8 teaspoon allspice
- 3 tablespoons lemon extract
- 2 tablespoons vanilla
- 2 teaspoons rose water
- 2 teaspoons soda dissolved in 2 tablespoons milk
- 2 3/4 pounds flour

## Directions

1. Bake for 3 hours slowly

title: Christmas Plum Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. raisins
- 2 lbs. currants
- 1 lb. citron or lemon peel
- 1 teacup sugar
- 2 lbs. beef suet
- Butter size of orange
- 2 lbs. flour
- 2 thick slices bread crumbed fine
- 7 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful ground cloves
- 1 teaspoonful cinnamon
- 1 nutmeg grated
- milk or water

## Directions

1. Mix it all with milk or water
2. Boil four hours in a bag
3. This makes two large puddings, and may be kept a month steamed when wanted, and eaten with a sauce

title: Christmas Plum Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound raisins
- 1 pound currants
- 1 pound suet
- 1 pound sugar
- 3/4 pound flour
- 1/2 pound bread crumbs
- 1/2 pound mixed peel
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 nutmeg grated
- 1/2 teaspoon spice
- 8 eggs
- 3/4 pint milk

## Directions

1. Stone raisins if not the dressed ones
2. Wash, dry and pick currants
3. Mince suet fine
4. Cut into thin slices the mixed peel
5. Grate fine the bread crumbs
6. Sift the flour
7. Add spices, sugar and salt
8. Beat the eggs and add the milk
9. Stir well that everything may be thoroughly blended
10. Press into buttered moulds or basins
11. Tie down tightly with a floured cloth
12. Boil from 6 to 8 hours
13. Serve hot with a good sweet sauce

title: Christmas Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. suet
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 lb. currants
- 1 lb. grated breadcrumbs
- 1 lb. raisins
- 1/2 lb. citron
- 1 lb. sultanas
- 1/4 lb. orange peel
- 1 lb. moist sugar
- 1/4 lb. lemon peel
- 1/2 pt. milk
- 1/4 lb. flour (browned)

## Directions

1. Mix well and boil five hours.

title: Christmas Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds currants
- 2 pounds stoned raisins
- 2 pounds suet
- 2 pounds flour (dry it)
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1/2 pound bread crumbs
- 1 pound candied fruit
- 2 grated nutmegs
- 1/2 ounce mixed spice
- 15 eggs
- juice of 2 lemons
- pinch of salt
- a little milk

## Directions

1. Mix stiff with a little milk
2. Boil 8 hours
3. Boil again when wanted
4. Will keep for months

title: Christmas Pudding (Variant 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds currants
- 2 pounds raisins
- 2 pounds sugar
- 2 1/2 pounds suet chopped
- 6 eggs
- 10 cents lemon peel
- 10 cents orange peel
- 10 cents citron peel
- 1 1/2 cups buttermilk
- 2 teaspoons soda
- a little salt and ginger
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 dessertspoon allspice and cloves mixed
- a little nutmeg
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 1/2 pounds bread crumbs
- 1 pound flour
- a wine glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Mix together and boil

title: Christmas Pudding (Variant 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint raisins
- 1 pint currants
- 1 pint suet
- 1 pint flour
- 1/2 pint bread crumbs
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 cup sugar
- 5 eggs
- a little candied orange or lemon peel
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix all together
2. Boil 3 hours
3. Eat with wine sauce

title: Chutney
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. apples
- 1 teaspoonful red pepper
- 8 oz. tomatoes
- 2 oz. ginger
- 1 oz. salt (or 1 oz.)
- 2 oz. garlic
- 8 oz. sugar
- 2 oz. onions
- 8 oz. raisins
- 3 pts. vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop apples, onions, tomatoes, and raisins; mix spices and vinegar with them; put in a warm place and stir twice a day for a fortnight.

title: Chutney
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 lbs. tomatoes
- 3 lbs. onions
- 3 pts. vinegar
- 2 doz. small peppers
- 1 lb. prunes, stoned
- 1 lb. raisins
- 2 lbs. sugar
- 2 oz. ground ginger
- 1 lb. mustard seed
- 1 cup salt

## Directions

1. Chop well, cook slowly, stir often and seal well.

title: Chutney
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- eight apples, chopped fine
- eight peppers (four red and four green)
- twelve tomatoes, peeled
- eight large onions
- 1/4 pound salt
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1/2 pound raisins, chopped
- 2 quarts vinegar
- 1 ounce ginger

## Directions

1. Chop all fine.
2. Boil till thick.

title: Cincinnati Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fat salt pork chopped fine and free from lean and rind
- 1 pint boiling water
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon nutmeg
- 2 ounces cloves
- 2 pounds raisins
- 1 pound currants
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teacup sifted flour

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the salt pork chopped fine and free from lean and rind
2. Let it stand until nearly cold
3. Add brown sugar, molasses, cinnamon, nutmeg, and cloves
4. Add raisins and currants
5. Add cream tartar and soda
6. Stir in sifted flour
7. Bake slowly 1 1/2 hours

title: Cinnamon Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large sifter full flour
- 1 large cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- a little salt
- good rich cream

## Directions

1. Sift all well together
2. Add a little salt and good rich cream to make into a dough
3. Roll out and bake in a hot oven

title: Cinnamon Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups white sugar
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 3 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon (scant)
- 1 cup chopped raisins

## Directions

1. Cream butter with sugar
2. Add sour milk
3. Add baking soda
4. Add flour and cinnamon
5. Add chopped raisins

title: Cinnamon Cake No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- butter the size of an egg
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1/2 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Take sugar, butter, eggs, milk, flour, and baking powder together
2. For the icing, mix butter the size of an egg, cinnamon, and sugar
3. Mix well and spread on each layer

title: Cinnamon Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- butter size of an egg
- brown sugar
- cinnamon
- a little butter

## Directions

1. Mix as little as possible
2. Roll thin
3. Spread with brown sugar, cinnamon and a little butter
4. Roll up and slice into 1/2-inch slices
5. Bake in medium oven

title: Citron Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- citron
- alum
- water
- 3/4 pound sugar to 1 pound citron
- ginger root
- 1 lemon
- 2 medium-sized citrons

## Directions

1. Cut the citron in dice
2. Boil in water with a small piece of alum until tender and clear
3. Make a syrup, allowing 3/4 pound sugar to 1 pound citron
4. Boil a piece of ginger root in the syrup
5. Put the citron in and boil for a few minutes
6. Put in 1 lemon with 2 medium-sized citrons

title: Clam Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 clams
- 1 pint milk
- 3 eggs
- clam liquor
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- hot lard

## Directions

1. Chop or leave clams whole
2. Add clam liquor, salt and pepper to milk and eggs
3. Add flour enough for a thin batter
4. Fry in hot lard

title: Clam Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Clams
- One cupful of hot milk per quart of juice
- One tablespoonful of butter
- One tablespoonful of flour
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Whipped cream

## Directions

1. Remove the clams from the shells as soon as they have opened
2. Put them in a warm place, until the juice is prepared
3. Add a cupful of hot milk to a quart of juice
4. Thicken it with a roux made of one tablespoonful of butter and one tablespoonful of flour
5. Add the clams, chopped fine, season, and bring the soup again to the boiling-point and serve
6. Two spoonfuls of whipped cream served on each plateful of soup is an improvement to the dish

title: Clam Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- salt pork
- 1 large or 2 small onions
- 2 quarts water
- 1 quart clams
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 pint milk
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- Worcester sauce

## Directions

1. Cut salt pork into very small squares and fry a light brown
2. Add 1 large or 2 small onions cut very fine, and cook about ten minutes
3. Add 2 quarts water and 1 quart clams
4. Mix 1 tablespoon flour with the water, put it with 1 pint milk and pour into the soup
5. Let it boil about five minutes
6. Add butter, pepper and salt, Worcester sauce to taste

title: Clam Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- hot water
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- flour
- toast

## Directions

1. Lay clams on a gridiron over hot coals
2. Take out of shells as soon as open
3. Save the juice
4. Add a little hot water, pepper, and a very little salt
5. Add butter rolled in flour sufficient for seasoning
6. Cook for 5 minutes
7. Pour over toast

title: Clams a la Financiere
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 doz. clams
- 1 slice mild ham
- 1 teaspoonful chopped parsley
- 1/4 teaspoonful chopped onion
- 1 tablespoonful chopped celery
- 1/4 can French mushrooms
- 1/4 can French peas
- Salt and red pepper
- Butter
- Mashed potatoes
- Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Open clams, taking care to retain all the liquor found in shell, also shells to serve up in
2. Stew in their own liquor for five minutes, cut into pieces the size of peas
3. Take ham, cut into small dice; place in a small saucepan on fire with sufficient butter to prevent burning
4. Fry to a light brown color, then add chopped onions, chopped celery; mushrooms, peas; parsley
5. Mix in the clams and their liquor
6. Season to taste with red pepper and salt; stew for fifteen minutes
7. Serve in their own shell made hot, with borders of mashed potatoes and garnished with parsley and lemon

title: Clams Saute
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 clams
- 2 slices salt pork
- A little white pepper
- Slices of Graham toast

## Directions

1. Fry the pork or bacon crisp in the blazer, and when the dice begin to brown push them to the side of the pan and lay in the clams
2. Saute them, turning once or twice, and serve on Graham or Boston brown bread toast

title: Claret Cup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 bottles claret
- 6 bottles soda water
- 4 lemons
- 6 wine glasses curagao
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Peel lemons quite thin, throw the rind in bowl and then squeeze in the lemons; put a lump of ice in bowl first.
2. Then pour claret on top of it, putting claret and soda in last. Sugar to taste. For about fifty people.

title: Claret Cup (Army Recipe)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart claret
- one bottle soda
- one-half pound crushed ice
- four tablespoons sugar
- one-quarter teaspoon grated nutmeg
- wineglass of brandy or maraschino

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients.

title: Clear Brown Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Stock
- Caramel
- Granulated sugar
- Boiling water
- Cloves
- Allspice
- Onion

## Directions

1. Clear the stock as directed in recipe for Amber Soup, and stir in enough caramel to color it to your liking, bearing in mind that too much will give a sweetish taste to the liquid.
2. The caramel is made by heating granulated sugar in a tin cup or agate iron saucepan until it bubbles brownly all over.
3. Add, at once, boiling water, a tablespoonful for each spoonful of the sugar — and stir until the sugar is dissolved.
4. It will keep well in the refrigerator for a week or more.
5. Some palates enjoy the flavor of cloves and allspice in browned soup.
6. The whole spices are used and strained out before the caramel goes in.
7. Allow six cloves and four allspice to a quart of stock.
8. Onion flavor should be imparted by grating a raw onion and squeezing the juice through a cloth into the heating stock.

title: Clear Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- A heaping tablespoonful of butter
- A medium-sized onion
- Two pounds of finely chopped lean raw beef
- One quart cold water
- The white and shell of an egg, well beaten, with a little cold water
- Four peppercorns
- Teaspoonful of salt
- Two cloves
- A blade of mace

## Directions

1. Heat a heaping tablespoonful of butter in a covered saucepan
2. Slice into it a medium-sized onion; stir until the onion is browned
3. Add two pounds of finely chopped lean raw beef, one quart cold water; cover closely and let it simmer three hours
4. Strain the soup, return to the kettle
5. Add the white and shell of an egg, well beaten, with a little cold water; add also four peppercorns, teaspoonful of salt, two cloves and a blade of mace
6. Boil five minutes, then drain and serve from a hot tureen

title: Clear Tapioca Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of pearl tapioca
- 1 large cup of cold water
- 1 quart of well-seasoned boiling clear stock

## Directions

1. Soak two tablespoonfuls of pearl tapioca in a large cup of cold water four hours.
2. Stir into a quart of well-seasoned boiling clear stock, and simmer ten minutes.

title: Clove Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/4 cup chopped raisins
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 1/4 teaspoonful soda
- 1 teaspoonful ground cloves

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. drop the eggs in (not beaten)
3. add soda to the milk
4. then part of the flour, then the fruit and the rest of the flour

title: Cocoa
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. water
- 3 tablespoonfuls grated cocoa
- 1 pt. hot milk
- a bit of soda

## Directions

1. Boil a pint of water
2. Rub three tablespoonfuls of grated cocoa to a smooth paste with cold water
3. Stir into the hot water
4. Boil ten minutes, hard
5. Pour upon it a pint of hot milk with a bit of soda in it
6. Boil for ten minutes longer, stirring and beating well
7. Sweeten in the cups

title: Cocoa
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 level teaspoons cocoa
- 2 level teaspoons sugar
- water
- 0.5 cup milk

## Directions

1. Stir cocoa and sugar together
2. Add water drop by drop, stirring constantly until a smooth, even paste has been secured
3. Place 0.5 cup milk over fire and allow it to heat until it begins to simmer around the sides of the pan
4. Add the cocoa and allow it to come to the boiling point
5. Remove from the fire and beat vigorously for 2 or 3 minutes

title: Cocoa Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 2 eggs well-beaten
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 3 1/2 cups sifted flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 1/4 cup cocoa
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 4 tablespoons boiling water

## Directions

1. Cream brown sugar and butter
2. Add well-beaten eggs and sour milk in which soda has been dissolved
3. Beat in gradually flour, baking powder and cocoa thoroughly mixed
4. Bake in 3 layers
5. For Caramel Filling: boil together milk, butter, brown sugar and granulated sugar until thickening slightly
6. Remove from fire and stir until thick enough to spread
7. For icing: boil together granulated sugar and boiling water until syrup hairs
8. Beat into frosting

title: Cocoanut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs (the whites of 2 to be used for frosting)
- ¾ cup sugar
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- 1⅜ cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- ½ teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Bake in layers
2. Beat the whites of 2 eggs well with 4 dessertspoons sugar
3. Spread frosting on cake and sprinkle with cocoanut

title: Cocoanut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound sugar
- ½ pound cocoanut
- whites of 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Bake in a greased pan, drop by spoonfuls

title: Cocoanut Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cocoanut
- 2 cups sugar
- milk of cocoanut or 1/4 cup water

## Directions

1. Put sugar and milk of cocoanut together, heat slowly until the sugar is melted
2. Boil five minutes
3. Add cocoanut (finely grated), boil ten minutes longer, stir constantly to keep from burning
4. Pour on buttered plates, cut in squares
5. Will take about two days to harden
6. Use prepared cocoanut when other cannot be had

title: Cocoanut Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup water
- shredded cocoanut
- 3 squares chocolate
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water to a syrup, or till it forms when dropped in water
2. Mix in cocoanut and chocolate and beat till it gets thick and creamy
3. Add vanilla

title: Cocoanut Candy (Second Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup water
- 1 teaspoon vinegar
- 1 tablespoon cocoanut

## Directions

1. Cook sugar, water and vinegar to soft ball stage
2. Add cocoanut and beat until creamy
3. Pour out on buttered plate

title: Cocoanut Candy (Third Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups white sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup cocoanut

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients in granite dish and boil 4 minutes, stirring constantly on stove
2. Continue stirring after removing from fire until it cools
3. Mark into squares

title: Cocoanut Cookies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of white sugar
- one cup of butter
- three eggs
- three teaspoons of baking powder
- one cup cocoanut
- flour enough to roll nicely

## Directions

1. Roll nicely
2. Bake

title: Cocoanut Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup cocoanut
- milk just enough to wet soda
- 2 teaspoons vanilla
- flour enough to roll

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients and roll

title: Cocoanut Cream Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls shredded cocoanut
- A little water

## Directions

1. Sugar and a little water boiled without stirring until it will spin a thread
2. Take from the fire
3. Add a large tablespoonful of shredded cocoanut and stir until creamy
4. Drop size of a penny on buttered paper
5. Slip off when cool and pack in boxes

title: Cocoanut Cream Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup cream
- ½ cup shredded cocoanut
- 3 cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs
2. Add the sugar gradually
3. Add the cocoanut, cream and flour with baking powder and salt

title: Cocoanut Drop Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 3/2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sour cream
- 3/2 teaspoon soda
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 tablespoons desiccated cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat 2 eggs
2. Add sugar, butter, sour cream, soda, flour, vanilla and cocoanut
3. Drop on buttered tins
4. Bake a delicate brown

title: Cocoanut Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 grated cocoanut
- half its weight of sugar
- the white of one egg

## Directions

1. Add sugar to grated cocoanut and the white of one egg
2. Beat to a froth and mix thoroughly
3. Drop on buttered white paper or tin sheets
4. Bake 15 minutes

title: Cocoanut Drops
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups cocoanut
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- white of 1 egg beaten stiff

## Directions

1. Drop on buttered paper and bake 15 minutes in a slow oven

title: Cocoanut Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white or yellow sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 tablespoons sweet cream
- 3 eggs well beaten
- 1 1/4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3/4 teaspoon fruit color
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- cold milk
- 1/2 cup sugar
- yolk of 1 egg
- white of 1 egg
- 1/2 cup white sugar
- water
- 1/2 cup cocoanut

## Directions

1. Make into 3 layers and put 3/4 teaspoon fruit color in centre layer
2. For filling, boil 1/2 cup sweet milk
3. Thicken with 2 tablespoons cornstarch dissolved in cold milk
4. Add 1/2 cup sugar
5. When cool, add yolk of 1 egg
6. For top of cake, beat white of 1 egg
7. Boil 1/2 cup white sugar dissolved in a little water a few minutes
8. Stir these two together
9. Add 1/2 cup cocoanut

title: Cocoanut Loaf Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup cocoanut
- 2 cups flour
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- 3 tablespoons melted butter

## Directions

1. Stir butter and sugar together well
2. Add egg, then milk
3. Sift flour and baking powder together and add them, beating vigorously
4. When all is smooth, add the cocoanut
5. Bake in a quick oven

title: Cocoanut Macaroons
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs, whites
- 1 heaping cup cocoanut
- 0.75 cup sugar, large
- 1 dessertspoonful cornstarch
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Stir into sugar one dessertspoon cornstarch
2. Stir this into whites of eggs beaten very stiff
3. Set over steam of kettle and stir till sugary around edges, about twenty minutes
4. Take off and stir in one heaping cup shredded cocoanut
5. Flavor with vanilla
6. Drop on buttered paper
7. Bake in rather quick oven

title: Cocoanut Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch
- 1 1/2 cups cocoanut
- 1/2 teaspoon pineapple flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat whites of 2 eggs stiff
2. Beat in 1 cup sugar with whites
3. Set in boiling water
4. Add 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch
5. Stir briskly until crust begins to form
6. Take out, add 1 1/2 cups cocoanut and 1/2 teaspoon pineapple flavoring
7. Have pan with buttered paper and drop in a teaspoon of the mixture
8. Bake 15 minutes in a moderate oven

title: Cocoanut Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 3 eggs
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 2 teaspoons cornstarch
- cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat stiff the whites of 3 eggs
2. Mix with 1 cup granulated sugar and 2 teaspoons cornstarch
3. Cook in a double boiler
4. Stir in cocoanut till very thick
5. Add 1 teaspoon of vanilla
6. Drop in spoonfuls on a pan
7. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Cocoanut Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 3 eggs beaten stiff
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 2½ cups grated cocoanut
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch

## Directions

1. Boil whites of eggs and sugar 3 minutes in double boiler
2. Add grated cocoanut and cornstarch mixed together
3. Drop on buttered paper and brown in oven

title: Cocoanut Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. milk
- 3 eggs, yolks
- 1/4 grated cocoanut
- 3 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla
- pie paste
- white of egg
- white of one egg for meringue

## Directions

1. Line a tin basin which is two inches deep with pie paste, and bake it.
2. Scald the milk and turn it on to the yolks and sugar beaten together.
3. Return it to the fire.
4. Add the cornstarch moistened with cold milk, and stir until well thickened.
5. Add vanilla, and the whites of two eggs whipped to a froth.
6. Cook one minute to set the egg, then remove.
7. When nearly cold and stiffened stir in the cocoanut.
8. Brush the bottom of the baked pie-crust with white of egg.
9. Cover it with a thin layer of grated cocoanut and turn in the thickened custard.
10. Cover the top with meringue made with the white of one egg.
11. Return it to the oven one minute to color the meringue.
12. Let the pie stand long enough to get firm and cold before serving.

title: Cocoanut Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 cup cocoanut
- 2 teaspoons cornstarch
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Beat sugar to a cream with the yolks
2. Add sweet milk, 3/4 cup sugar, cocoanut, cornstarch, and salt
3. Bake in crust

title: Cocoanut Pie 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup cocoanut
- 1 1/2 cups milk
- 1/4 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- butter size of an egg
- yolk of 2 eggs
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Beat the whites for the top

title: Cocoanut Pie 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rich sweet milk
- 1 egg
- 2/4 cup sugar
- 1 large tablespoon flour
- cocoanut
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Heat milk in double boiler
2. Beat egg, sugar, and flour together
3. Cook mixture in milk
4. Have a rich pie crust previously baked
5. Fill with layer of filling, then cocoanut with whipped cream on top

title: Cocoanut Pie 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons cocoanut
- 2 tablespoons flour
- about 1 1/2 cups sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, sugar, cocoanut, flour, and milk together
2. Bake with an under crust

title: Cocoanut Pie 5
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cup milk
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup white sugar
- vanilla to taste
- 1/2 cup shredded cocoanut

## Directions

1. Stir over fire until it thickens
2. Just before taking it off the stove add 1/2 cup of shredded cocoanut
3. Keep the white of egg for frosting pie

title: Cocoanut Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint milk
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons cocoanut
- 1/2 cup crackers
- 1 teaspoon lemon extract
- whites of 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Combine 1 pint milk, 1/2 cup sugar, 2 eggs, 2 tablespoons cocoanut, 1/2 cup crackers, and 1 teaspoon lemon extract
2. Bake 1.5 hours
3. For frosting, beat whites of 2 eggs with 3/4 cup sugar, put in oven and brown

title: Cocoanut Puffs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch
- vanilla
- cocoanut enough to make stiff

## Directions

1. Beat whites of eggs very light and stiff
2. Add sugar and cornstarch dissolved in a very little water and stirred into the eggs and sugar
3. Put on a double boiler and cook over water (boiling) for about twenty or twenty-five minutes
4. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking
5. Add cocoanut enough to stand up well when dropped on buttered tins
6. Flavor with vanilla
7. Drop on tins and bake eight or ten minutes in a moderate oven

title: Cocoanut Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 3 eggs
- 1 cup ground sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 2 cups shredded cocoanut

## Directions

1. Beat the whites well
2. Add the sugar and beat over steam until a crust forms on the bottom and sides of dish

title: Cocoanut Tapioca Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons tapioca
- cold water
- 1 quart milk
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 tablespoons cocoanut
- whites of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Soak 4 tablespoons tapioca 4 hours in cold water
2. Pour off water and stir tapioca into 1 quart milk
3. Boil together 10 minutes
4. Add yolks of 3 eggs, 1 cup sugar, and 3 tablespoons cocoanut
5. Cook 5 minutes longer
6. Use whites of eggs for meringue for top

title: Coconut Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- milk of cocoanut
- cocoanut (finely grated)
- butter

## Directions

1. Put sugar and milk of cocoanut together, heat slowly until the sugar is melted, then boil five minutes
2. Add cocoanut (finely grated), boil ten minutes longer, stir constantly to keep from burning
3. Pour on buttered plates, cut in squares
4. Will take about two days to harden
5. Use prepared cocoanut when other cannot be had

title: Coddled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Scald the milk, add egg slightly beaten.
2. Cook over hot water, stirring constantly.
3. Add seasoning and serve on hot toast.

title: Codfish And Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- codfish
- eggs
- butter
- butter and lard mixed

## Directions

1. Properly soak and pick the codfish in bits
2. To each cup add 2 eggs beaten well together
3. Drop from a spoon in hot butter or butter and lard mixed
4. Fry to a nice brown on both sides

title: Codfish Balls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- purified, shredded codfish
- vinegar
- mashed potato
- egg
- pepper
- flour
- fat for deep frying

## Directions

1. Soak codfish for two or three hours
2. Boil for fifteen minutes in water that has had a tablespoonful of vinegar stirred into it
3. Spread upon a sieve to get cold
4. Allow to each cupful of fish half as much mashed potato whipped to a soft cream
5. Mix them together well
6. Make very hot over the fire and beat in a frothed egg for every cupful of fish
7. Season with pepper
8. Let the mixture get quite cold
9. Make into balls
10. Roll in flour
11. Set in a cold place to stiffen
12. Roll again in flour
13. Fry in deep fat to a yellow-brown color

title: Codfish Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled potatoes and rice
- 1 cup soaked and shredded codfish
- 1 tablespoon butter
- curry powder
- minced onion
- paprika
- beaten egg
- cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Make into balls
2. Roll in beaten egg
3. Roll in cracker crumbs
4. Leave in a cool place
5. Fry quickly
6. Serve

title: Codfish Balls (Second Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 or 7 good-sized potatoes
- 1 large cup of boneless codfish
- boiling water
- egg
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Pare 6 or 7 good-sized potatoes
2. Quarter them
3. Cover with boiling water
4. Put a large cup of boneless codfish above the potatoes
5. Cook till tender
6. Drain
7. Mash and season to taste
8. Add an egg and beat well
9. Shape into balls or flat cakes (about 1/2 inch thick)
10. Fry in hot fat

title: Codfish in Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of salted codfish
- cold water
- one cup of rich milk
- one spoonful of flour
- one spoonful of butter
- one hard-boiled egg
- pepper
- one teaspoonful of minced parsley

## Directions

1. Shred and soak half a cup of salted codfish over night
2. In the morning drain, place in a stew-pan, cover with cold water
3. When it boils, drain
4. Cover again with water and simmer gently for fifteen minutes
5. Add one cup of rich milk
6. Rub one spoonful of flour smooth in one spoonful of butter
7. Add to the codfish
8. Mince one hard-boiled egg, stir into the mixture
9. Add a pinch of pepper and a teaspoonful of minced parsley
10. Boil up once

title: Coffee
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee
- cold water
- boiling water
- milk

## Directions

1. Wet coffee with a little cold water
2. Pour over this a cup of boiling water
3. In another pan have an equal quantity of milk and allow it to scald, or until it begins to bubble around the edges of the pan
4. When the coffee boils, add the scalded milk and allow to boil until it foams up
5. Add a few drops of cold water to settle the grounds
6. Serve

title: Coffee Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups bread sponge
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 beaten egg
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- flour
- butter size of walnut
- 1 tablespoon cream

## Directions

1. Mix 2 cups bread sponge, 1/2 cup butter, 1/2 cup sugar, 1/2 cup sweet milk, 1 beaten egg, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon with flour enough to mix stiff
2. Let rise a second time
3. Roll out over 1 inch thick
4. Cream butter size of walnut, 1/2 cup sugar, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, 1 tablespoon cream and mix
5. Spread over top
6. Let rise and bake

title: Coffee Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- ½ cup butter
- ½ cup sugar
- ½ cup currants
- ½ cup molasses
- ½ cup cold coffee
- ½ teaspoon each of cinnamon and cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda in the coffee

## Directions

1. Mix as thick as a jelly cake
2. Bake in layers

title: Coffee Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups bread sponge
- ½ cup melted butter
- ½ cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- grated rind of 1 lemon
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- ½ cup chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Mix as for buns, only put in loaf

title: Coffee Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- ½ cup molasses
- ½ cup cold coffee
- 1 egg
- ½ cup butter
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 cup raisins
- a little citron or lemon peel
- 2¾ cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar

## Directions

1. Bake in layers and ice

title: Coffee Cake (Kaffee Kuchen)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread dough
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Spread bread dough 3/4 inches thick upon buttered pans
2. Let rise
3. Brush with melted butter
4. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
5. Bake

title: Coffee Cake Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup molasses
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup cold coffee
- 1 cup lard
- 1 cup sour milk
- 3 teaspoons soda
- 2 cups raisins chopped fine
- 2 cups currants
- 1 lemon peel
- 2 teaspoons all kinds of spice
- 5 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix and bake

title: Coffee Dessert
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups strong coffee
- 1 cup milk
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon gelatine
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix coffee, milk, sugar, salt, and gelatine in double boiler
2. Reserve 1/4 cup sugar and beat with egg yolks
3. When ingredients are heating, stir in boiling mixture with egg yolks
4. Return to boiler until it begins to thicken
5. Remove from fire
6. Stir in stiffened egg whites and vanilla
7. Turn into a mould dipped in cold water

title: Coffee Sponge
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups strong cold coffee
- ¼ cup milk
- ½ cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon gelatine soaked for ½ an hour in ¾ cup water
- pinch salt
- yolks of 2 eggs
- ¼ cup sugar
- beaten whites of 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons vanilla
- whipped cream or cream

## Directions

1. Heat coffee, milk, ½ cup sugar, and soaked gelatine in a double boiler
2. When fully heated add ¼ cup sugar, pinch salt, and yolks of eggs
3. Cook until mixture thickens
4. Remove from fire and stir in the beaten whites of the eggs
5. Add vanilla
6. In summer set on ice to mould
7. Serve with whipped cream or cream

title: Cold Almond Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- quarter pound of ground almonds
- quarter pound of powdered sugar
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs until very light
2. Add the sugar and almonds gently
3. Beat till it bubbles
4. Put in a greased dish
5. Put blanched almonds on top
6. Bake in rather a moderate oven

title: Cold Cabinet Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon granulated gelatine
- 1/2 cup cold water
- 2 cups scalded milk
- 3 yolks eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/8 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon brandy
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 5 lady fingers
- 6 macaroons
- angelica
- candied cherries
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Make custard with scalded milk, egg yolks, sugar, and salt
2. Add gelatine soaked in cold water
3. Strain and let thicken slightly
4. Add brandy and vanilla flavoring
5. Place mould in ice water and decorate bottom with angelica and cherries
6. Cover with mixture
7. When firm, add layer of lady fingers soaked in the gelatine mixture, then layer of macaroons
8. Repeat until mould is full
9. Serve with cream sauce and garnish with candied cherries

title: Cold Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 10 onions
- 4 heads celery
- 3 red peppers
- 1 cup salt
- 10 cups coffee sugar
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon cloves
- 1 tablespoon allspice
- 1 tablespoon black pepper
- 1 quart vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop onions and tomatoes fine
2. Put in a cup of salt and let stand all night
3. Drain as dry as possible
4. Chop celery and peppers together
5. Add sugar, spices, and vinegar
6. Mix and bottle

title: Cold Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Oil of almonds, one ounce
- Spermaceti, half-ounce
- White wax, one drachm
- Rose-water, two ounces

## Directions

1. Melt oil of almonds, spermaceti, and white wax
2. Add two ounces of rose-water
3. Stir constantly until cold

title: Cold Fruit Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Hot stewed fruit
- Bread and butter
- Cream

## Directions

1. Pour hot stewed fruit over layers of bread and butter in a mould
2. When cold serve with cream

title: Cold Meat Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe peeled tomatoes
- 8 onions
- 3 ripe peppers
- 1¾ cups white sugar or 1½ cups horseradish
- ½ cup salt
- 2 tablespoons mustard seeds
- 2 tablespoons pepper
- 1 stalk of celery
- 1 quart vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop onions
2. Do not cook
3. Can right up

title: Cold Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-beaten yolks of 3 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch
- 1 cup cold water
- Juice and rind of 2 lemons
- thin stale cake
- three eggs (the whites)

## Directions

1. Boil together until thick the yolks of 3 eggs, 1 cup white sugar, 1 tablespoonful cornstarch, 1 cup cold water, and juice and rind of 2 lemons
2. Stir well and pour while hot into a pudding dish, lined first with thin stale cake
3. When cooked cover the top with three eggs (the whites) well whisked
4. Brown in the oven
5. Serve cold

title: Cold Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 6 large onions
- 3 red peppers
- 5 cups grated horseradish
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 4 cups vinegar
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- ginger
- mustard
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Peel tomatoes
2. Chop onions
3. Don't cook this sauce
4. Keep in open jars in a cool cellar

title: Cold Slaw
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup vinegar
- 1 well-beaten egg
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- a small lump butter
- pepper
- salt
- cabbage

## Directions

1. Add egg, mustard, sugar, butter to vinegar
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Let come to a boil
4. Pour over nicely chopped cabbage while hot

title: Colloped Halibut
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- halibut
- bones
- onions
- celery
- sweet herbs
- flour
- butter
- cream
- sauce
- wine
- capers
- lemon

## Directions

1. Cut the fish into nice cutlets, of about an inch thick, and fry them.
2. Put them into a broth made of the bones, four onions, a stick of celery, and a bundle of sweet herbs, boiled together for half an hour.
3. Strain this broth, thicken, then flour and lay them in a stew-pan with some good broth, and let them stew gently until perfectly tender.
4. Thicken the gravy with butter or cream, add a spoonful of sauce, half a glass of wine, and serve it up with capers strewed over the top, and garnished with slices of lemon.

title: Cologne Water
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Oil of lavender, two drachms
- Oil of rosemary, one drachm and a half
- Oil of orange, one drachm
- Oil of lemon, one drachm
- Oil of bergamot, one drachm
- Essence of musk, two drachms
- Attar of rose, ten drops
- Proof spirit, one pint

## Directions

1. Shake all together thoroughly three times a day for a week

title: Combination Fudge
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups coffee sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 cup broken English walnuts
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 square unsweetened chocolate
- butter size of a small egg

## Directions

1. In one pan put 3 cups coffee sugar and 1 cup milk, boil until it forms a soft ball in water
2. Add 1 teaspoon butter and let boil up
3. Take from the fire and allow it to cool so the hand may rest upon it
4. Beat with a spoon, adding after it begins to thicken 1 teaspoon vanilla and 1 cup broken English walnuts
5. In another pan put 2 cups granulated sugar, 3/4 cup milk, 1 square unsweetened chocolate
6. Cook 8 minutes, then add butter size of a small egg
7. When it makes a firm ball in water take from the fire and add a few drops of vanilla
8. Beat until like very cold molasses, then pour into the pan that contains the other fudge
9. It will harden over the other and can be cut in squares

title: Composition Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 cups table molasses
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1/2 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients
2. Fill tart shells
3. Bake in medium hot oven

title: Compote of Chestnuts
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 chestnuts
- 1 qt. milk and water
- 12 oz. sugar
- 3 oranges
- 0.5 pt. cream
- Maraschino
- Whipped cream
- Vanilla bean

## Directions

1. Remove husks and skin from the chestnuts
2. Boil gently in the milk and water until like floury potatoes, and strain them
3. Boil the sugar until it purls on the surface and flavor with vanilla bean
4. Add the chestnuts and work all together vigorously
5. Rub through a potato masher on to a dish
6. Pile up whipped cream in the centre of a dish
7. Gently strew the chestnuts on top of the cream in a conical form
8. Garnish with orange quarters soaked in maraschino at the base of the cream and nuts

title: Consomme
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. shin of beef
- 4 lbs. knuckle of veal
- 4 qts. of water (cold)
- 2 oz. lean ham or bacon
- 6 cloves
- 6 peppercorns
- 2 stalks of celery
- Bouquet of herbs
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 3 onions
- 1 carrot
- 1 turnip
- 2 sprigs of parsley
- 3 eggs (whites and shells)

## Directions

1. Wipe and cut the meat and bones into small pieces
2. Put the marrow, bones, and part of the meat in the kettle, with four quarts of cold water
3. Heat slowly; cut the onions and vegetables fine, and fry them in the ham fat or in drippings, then brown the remainder of the meat
4. Add onions, meat, herbs, spices and vegetables
5. Simmer until the meat is in rags; it will take about seven hours
6. Strain, and when cold remove the fat and add the whites and shells of the eggs, lemon and salt and pepper, if needed
7. When well mixed heat it, and boil ten minutes
8. Strain through fine strainer, and heat again to the boiling point before serving
9. Serve clear, or with wine or lemon

title: Cookies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of butter and lard mixed
- one cup brown sugar
- two eggs
- one and a half teaspoons baking powder
- flour enough to roll

## Directions

1. Bake in quick oven

title: Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- ½ cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour enough to roll
- nutmeg or lemon for flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake in a hot oven

title: Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups lard
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- 1 small nutmeg
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour enough to roll

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake in quick oven

title: Cookies 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup pork fryings
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 small teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- nutmeg for flavoring
- flour enough to roll out thin

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in sour milk
2. Add flour enough to roll out thin with baking powder
3. Flavor with nutmeg and bake in hot oven

title: Cookies 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- ½ cup butter
- ½ cup sweet milk
- 1 cup currants
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Roll dough in a roll about as large as a fifty-cent piece
2. Cut into pieces about ½ inch thick and bake

title: Cookies 5
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 3 eggs
- ½ teaspoon soda
- flour enough to make a stiff dough

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Roll out thin and bake in rather quick oven

title: Cookies Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 cup butter
- ¾ cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- ½ teaspoon salt
- flour to mix stiff

## Directions

1. Roll very thin
2. Bake

title: Cookies Without Eggs 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup sour cream
- soda
- flour enough to make it easy to roll

## Directions

1. Mix flour and butter together
2. Add sugar and then cream and soda

title: Cookies Without Eggs 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups sugar
- 2 cups lard
- 1 pint sour milk
- 1 tablespoon soda
- 1 teaspoon alum
- ginger to taste
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix in evening and let stand over night

title: Cooking Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 firm, medium cabbage
- salt
- pepper
- 1 tablespoon butter or salt fat pork
- 1 egg
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut cabbage in ½-inch strips using a very sharp knife or cabbage cutter
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Put butter or salt fat pork in a kettle
4. Put in cabbage and cook 10 minutes, stirring constantly
5. Remove to back of stove to steam until done
6. Break in an egg and beat well
7. Add vinegar to taste
8. Serve very hot

title: Corn a la Southern
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can chopped corn
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon salt
- ⅛ teaspoon pepper
- ¾ tablespoon melted butter
- 1 pint scalded milk

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. turn into buttered baking dish
3. Bake in slow oven until firm

title: Corn Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Large 3/4 cup corn meal
- 1 cup flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- A little salt
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoonful cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoonful soda

## Directions

1. Sift soda and cream tartar into flour
2. Bake twenty minutes

title: Corn Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup yellow cornmeal
- 1 egg
- butter size of English walnut
- 1 cup flour
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1 full teaspoon baking powder
- 1 pinch salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Sift meal, flour, sugar and baking powder and salt together in a large mixing bowl
2. Add enough milk to make a thick batter, the consistency between pancake and cake batter
3. Add 1 egg well beaten
4. Lastly add the butter melted hot
5. Bake in a hot oven

title: Corn Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon sour cream or butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 cup meal
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Corn Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen ears of corn grated and scraped
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoons salt
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Cook same as griddle cakes

title: Corn Chowder
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve ears of corn
- Two onions sliced
- Three large potatoes parboiled
- Six Boston crackers well buttered and soaked five minutes in hot water
- Three tablespoonfuls butter
- One cup milk
- One beaten egg
- One quart boiling water
- Parsley
- Pepper and salt
- A pinch of soda in the milk

## Directions

1. Fry the onions in two tablespoonfuls of butter in the soup-kettle
2. Remove this to the table and take out the onions with a skimmer, leaving the browned butter in the bottom
3. Put into this a layer of corn cut from the cob, then of crackers, next of sliced parboiled potatoes, seasoning as you go, until all the ingredients are in
4. Cover with the hot water, and cook gently for about forty minutes after it begins to boil
5. Heat the milk in a separate vessel, stir into it a tablespoonful of butter rolled in flour, and at last a beaten egg
6. Pour the milk into the tureen, then the chowder, stirring all the while

title: Corn Chowder
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen ears sweet corn
- 1 cabbage head
- 4 onions
- 2 red peppers
- 3 pints of vinegar
- 3/4 pound mustard
- 2 teaspoons curry powder
- 1 teaspoon turmeric
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Remove corn from cob
2. Chop together corn, cabbage

title: Corn Chowder (A Nice Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen ears sweet corn
- 1 cabbage head
- 4 onions
- 2 red peppers
- 3 pints of vinegar
- 3/4 pound mustard
- 2 teaspoons curry powder
- 1 teaspoon turmeric
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Remove corn from cob
2. Chop together corn, cabbage, onions and peppers
3. Add other ingredients
4. Boil until thick enough to bottle

title: Corn Chowder (A Nice Pickle) 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 ears of sweet corn
- 1 large head cabbage
- 3 red-hot peppers
- 2 red sweet peppers
- 1 large quart vinegar
- 4 small cups granulated sugar
- 3 dessertspoons mustard
- 2 dessertspoons turmeric
- 3/4 cup salt
- 7 large onions

## Directions

1. Put corn, cabbage, peppers and onions through the grinder
2. Put in kettle and add vinegar, salt and sugar
3. Mix your mustard and turmeric together dry and wet with a little cold vinegar
4. Stir all together and let boil 1/2 hour, then bottle

title: Corn Chowder 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can corn
- 4 cups potatoes cut in 1/2-inch slices
- 1 1/2-inch cube fat salt pork or butter
- 1 sliced onion
- 4 cups scalded milk
- 8 common crackers
- 3 tablespoons butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Try out pork, add onion and cook, stirring often that onion may not burn
2. Parboil potatoes 5 minutes in boiling water to cover, drain and add to onion and strained fat, then add 2 cups boiling water
3. Cook till potatoes are soft, add corn and milk, and heat to boiling point
4. Season with salt and pepper, add butter and crackers split and soaked in enough cold milk to moisten
5. Remove crackers, turn chowder into a tureen and put crackers on top

title: Corn Fritters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups fresh sweet corn or canned corn
- one tablespoonful salt
- one teaspoonful pepper
- two eggs
- one-quarter pound sifted flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Season corn with one tablespoonful of salt and a teaspoonful of pepper, add two eggs and a quarter of a pound of sifted flour
2. Do not beat the mixture, but stir vigorously with a wooden spoon for five minutes, till firm
3. Butter a frying pan and put in the preparation about a gill at a time
4. Be careful that the fritters do not touch one another, and fry a golden color
5. Serve hot in a folded napkin

title: Corn Fritters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups grated corn
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- a pinch of soda
- 1 tablespoonful melted butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Mix and fry as you would griddle-cakes, and send in hot, in relays.

title: Corn Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can corn
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- pepper
- 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
- 1/2 cup flour
- milk to make thick batter

## Directions

1. Mix the corn, yolks of eggs, salt, pepper together, and add the flour sifted with baking powder
2. Add milk, beat well
3. Fold in whites, drop by spoonful into hot fat

title: Corn Fritters (Canned Corn)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of corn
- two eggs
- seasoning to taste
- two tablespoonfuls of milk or cream
- one tablespoonful of butter
- thickening

## Directions

1. Beat eggs well; add corn by degrees, beating very hard; salt to taste; one tablespoonful of butter; stir in milk and thickening enough to hold together for frying.

title: Corn Fritters 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can corn
- 1 cup milk
- 1 egg well beaten
- salt
- rolled crackers

## Directions

1. Add milk and egg well beaten to corn
2. Season with salt and thicken with rolled crackers
3. Fry in hot butter

title: Corn Fritters 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can corn
- 3 eggs well beaten
- a little salt
- flour enough to make a rather stiff batter

## Directions

1. Mix corn, eggs, salt, and flour to make a rather stiff batter
2. Cook in deep hot lard same as doughnuts

title: Corn Gems
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 1/2 cups milk
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1 cup cornmeal
- 2 level teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat first 3 ingredients together
2. mix cornmeal and flour
3. add a little at a time, beating well for at least five minutes
4. add the stiffly beaten white of egg
5. bake in gem pans in moderate oven

title: Corn Muffins
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful melted butter
- 1 tablespoonful brown sugar
- 2 teacups cornmeal
- 1 heaping tablespoonful flour
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- 1 cup milk

## Directions

1. Beat eggs very light, add melted butter, brown sugar, cornmeal, and flour, to which add baking powder and a cupful of milk.
2. Mix thoroughly, pour into greased muffin tins and bake in a quick oven.

title: Corn or Apple Fritters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup corn
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Make into a thick batter and drop in spoonfuls into boiling lard.
2. May use chopped apples instead of corn.

title: Corn Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint canned corn chopped fine
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- 2 well-beaten eggs
- 2 cups rich sweet milk
- 1 small teaspoon sugar
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Mix corn, cornstarch, eggs, milk, sugar, pepper and salt together
2. Bake until set

title: Corn Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen ears of corn
- 1 large head cabbage
- 1 quart white wine vinegar
- 3 or 4 cups brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons salt
- 6 peppers (3 green and 3 red)
- 1 tablespoon mustard seed
- 1 tablespoon celery seed
- 5 cents turmeric and curry

## Directions

1. Boil 15 or 20 minutes
2. Add more vinegar if you think it too thick

title: Corn Rusks
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- ¾ cup brown sugar
- ¾ cup shortening
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- ginger enough to suit taste
- cornmeal enough to make batter

## Directions

1. Mix well and roll like round loaf bread
2. Cut off about 1 inch thick and lay in cookie pan to bake

title: Corn Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen ears corn
- 1 head cabbage
- 4 onions
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 large sweet pepper
- 1 pint vinegar
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 small teaspoon turmeric

## Directions

1. Boil corn and cut off
2. Chop all together: corn, cabbage, onions, and pepper
3. Cook, adding 1 pint vinegar
4. Mix mustard, turmeric with water and add to the mixture
5. Cook a few minutes

title: Corn Salad 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ears of corn
- 1 head cabbage
- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup dry mustard
- sweet pepper to suit taste
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut tops of the corn off, then scrape all off the cob
2. Put in enough vinegar to cover it and cook for 20 minutes

title: Corn Salad 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 13/4 dozen corn cut off cob
- 1 head cabbage
- 3 heads celery
- 2 red peppers (take out seeds)
- 5 large onions
- 2 quarts vinegar
- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 4 tablespoons mustard
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 5 cents' worth of turmeric
- 1/2 cup salt

## Directions

1. Chop cabbage, celery, and peppers
2. Put vinegar on to boil, add sugar and salt
3. Mix flour and mustard together, then add turmeric
4. Add corn, onions, peppers, cabbage and celery
5. Boil slowly until tender

title: Corn Salad 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head cabbage cut fine
- 1 dozen ears of corn cut off
- salt
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons mustard
- a little pepper
- vinegar enough to cover

## Directions

1. Mix cabbage and corn with a little salt and let stand a few hours
2. Add brown sugar, mustard, and pepper, and vinegar enough to cover
3. Cook until done and put in sealers

title: Corn Salad 5
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 ears of corn
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup salt
- 1/2 teaspoon celery seed
- 3/4 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon turmeric
- 4 onions chopped fine
- 1 large head cabbage
- 2 ounces mustard
- 2 quarts vinegar (strong)
- 2 green peppers chopped fine

## Directions

1. Boil corn until tender, then cut off cob
2. Mix corn, peppers, cabbage and onions together and pour 1 quart vinegar over this and let come to boil
3. Mix other ingredients together with the other quart of vinegar
4. Mix all well, and boil mixture 1 hour
5. Seal in jars

title: Corn Scallop
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of corn
- 1 pint milk
- 1 beaten egg
- 2 slices bread crumbled fine
- bits of butter
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Empty a can of corn into a baker
2. add to it 1 pint milk, and a beaten egg
3. add 2 slices bread crumbled fine
4. dot the top with bits of butter, pepper and salt
5. brown in a hot oven

title: Corn Table Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen ears corn
- 1 head cabbage
- 1½ quarts vinegar
- salt
- 3 cups sugar
- 3 red peppers
- ¾ tablespoon mustard
- 4 or 6 onions chopped fine

## Directions

1. Cook gently until tender

title: Corn Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of corn
- 1 pint of brown sugar or molasses
- 1 gallon of rain water

## Directions

1. Cut off the cob 1 pint of corn
2. Take 1 pint of brown sugar or molasses to a gallon of rain water
3. Add corn
4. Put into a jar and cover with cloth
5. Set in sun
6. In 3 weeks will be good vinegar

title: Corned Beef Hash
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled corned beef
- cold boiled potatoes
- onion
- butter
- pepper and salt
- rich milk or cream

## Directions

1. Chop fine cold boiled corned beef; to one pint meat add one pint and a little more of cold boiled potatoes, chopped, though not too fine.
2. A little onion can be used if liked.
3. Have ready a pan with a good piece of butter in it, put in hash, season with pepper and salt, then add rich milk or cream, enough to moisten.
4. Cover and make hot.

title: Corned Tongue
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups salt
- 1 teaspoon saltpetre
- tongue

## Directions

1. Put tongue in this brine and let lay for 3 weeks, turning often, nearly every day.

title: Cornmeal Gems
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters of a cup cornmeal
- two cupfuls flour
- half a cupful sugar
- half a cupful butter
- two eggs
- one cupful milk
- three tablespoonfuls baking powder
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Bake in gem-pans

title: Cornmeal Muffins
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three-quarters of a cup of butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup of milk
- Three-quarters of a cup of cornmeal
- 2 cups of flour
- 1/2 cup of sugar
- 3 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- A little salt

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake in muffin tins

title: Cornmeal Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sour milk
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 egg well beaten
- equal parts flour and cornmeal to make a soft batter

## Directions

1. Mix sour milk, soda, and well beaten egg. Add equal parts flour and cornmeal to make a soft batter. Fry on a hot buttered pan.

title: Cornstarch Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 0.75 cup cornstarch
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of eggs, butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add whites, starch, flour, baking powder and milk last

title: Cornstarch Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 4 eggs
- ¾ pound sugar
- 1 cup butter
- ¾ cup cornstarch
- 1 cup flour
- ½ cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- vanilla or lemon flavoring

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar together
2. Mix cornstarch with milk and add
3. Add eggs beaten stiff
4. Add flour in which the baking powder has been sifted
5. Add flavoring
6. Put in well-greased mould and bake

title: Cornstarch Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 4 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- ¾ cup cornstarch
- ¾ cup butter
- ½ cup milk
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix and bake

title: Cornstarch Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 3 tablespoons white sugar
- salt
- whites of 3 eggs
- 1 tablespoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil milk
2. Add sugar and cornstarch
3. Boil until thick
4. Beat the whites of 3 eggs to a stiff froth and add just before removing from stove
5. Flavor with vanilla

title: Cornstarch Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- yolks of 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons cornstarch
- a little milk
- whites of 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons white powdered sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart milk
2. Beat the yolks of 4 eggs with 4 tablespoons cornstarch and a little milk
3. Stir into the boiling milk and let it boil up once
4. Turn into a pudding dish
5. Beat the whites of the eggs to a froth and add 4 tablespoons white powdered sugar
6. Cover the pudding with the mixture, set in the oven and let brown lightly
7. Flavor with vanilla

title: Cornstarch Souffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 ounces cornstarch
- 3 ounces butter
- 3 ounces fine white sugar
- 3 large eggs
- 1 pint milk

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in a saucepan
2. Add to it the cornstarch and sugar and mix them together
3. Add milk heated to nearly boiling
4. Stir well with a wooden spoon until it boils and thickens
5. Let it cool and beat well into it the yolks of the eggs
6. Beat the whites to a stiff froth and stir gently into the mixture
7. Add any flavoring you like
8. Slightly butter a dish, pour in the mixture and bake in a slow oven
9. Serve quickly as Souffles will soon fall

title: Coronation Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- butter size of an egg
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup seeded raisins

## Directions

1. Bake in two layers
2. Make filling with 1 cup sugar, 2 tablespoons cream, 2 tablespoons water, 1½ tablespoons butter

title: Cosmetic Balm
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- German cologne, half a gill
- Alcohol, half a gill
- Glycerine, half a gill
- Gum tragacanth, one-eighth ounce
- Rain-water, one pint

## Directions

1. Put all except gum in bottle
2. Heat quarter of a pint of rain-water and add the gum
3. Let stand half a day
4. Mix all the ingredients and bottle for use

title: Cottage Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 2 cups flour
- 3/4 cup milk
- flavor to taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Cottage Cheese
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sour or loppered milk
- cloth or towel
- sieve
- salt
- butter
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Put a pan of sour or loppered milk on the stove or range, where it is not too hot
2. Let it scald until the whey rises to the top; be careful that it does not boil, or the curd will become hard and tough
3. Place a clean cloth or towel over a sieve, and pour this whey and curd into it
4. Leave it covered to drain two or three hours
5. Put it into a dish and chop it fine with a spoon
6. Add a teaspoonful of salt, a tablespoonful of butter and enough sweet cream to make the cheese the consistency of putty
7. With your hands make it into little balls flattened
8. Keep it in a cool place

title: Cottage Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- sour milk
- boiling water
- sweet cream
- celery salt

## Directions

1. Take sour milk, cream and all, and scald with boiling water poured over it until the milk separates from the whey
2. Strain off the water and let the milk drain in a cheesecloth bag for 10 hours
3. Remove and run through a grinder to make it fine and creamy
4. Mix with sweet cream and season to taste
5. Celery salt gives a tasty flavor to cottage cheese

title: Cottage Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- sliced or chopped meat
- gravy or sauce
- mashed potatoes

## Directions

1. Nearly fill a baking dish with sliced or chopped meat and gravy, or a sauce
2. Cover with mashed potatoes
3. Place in an oven

title: Cottage Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two tablespoonfuls butter
- One cup white sugar
- One egg
- One cup milk
- One pint flour
- Two tablespoonfuls baking powder

## Directions

1. Bake and serve with sauce

title: Cottage Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 pint sifted flour
- 1 cup raisins

## Directions

1. Combine 1 cup sugar, 1 egg, 3/4 cup butter, 1 cup milk, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 1 pint sifted flour, and 1 cup raisins
2. Steam 2 hours
3. Serve with sauce

title: Cottage Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 7/8 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Combine 7/8 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 egg, 1/2 cup sweet milk, 1 cup flour, and 1 teaspoon baking powder
2. Put in a round buttered tin and bake in a quick oven

title: Cough Mixture
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of tincture of benzoin
- two wine-glasses of whisky
- one cup of molasses

## Directions

1. Mix one ounce of tincture of benzoin, two wine-glasses of whisky, and one cup of molasses well
2. One teaspoonful whenever cough is troublesome

title: Crab-Apple Catsup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. apples (which have been cored)
- 1½ lbs. sugar
- 1 qt. vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful cloves
- 1 tablespoonful cinnamon
- 1 tablespoonful pepper
- 1 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Scald the fruit, run it through the sieve
2. Mix all ingredients together
3. Boil until it is almost as thick as jam

title: Crab-Apple Pickle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pk. apples
- 3 lbs. sugar
- 2 qts. vinegar
- 1 oz. cinnamon
- 1 oz. cloves

## Directions

1. Boil sugar, vinegar, spices, tying spices in muslin bag; prepare apples by sticking three or four cloves in each apple; steam until soft; then put them in syrup and let simmer for fifteen minutes; then take apples out and let syrup boil for ten minutes longer.

title: Crabapple Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit (4 pounds)
- sugar (1.5 pounds)
- vinegar (pints)
- cloves (1 tablespoon)
- cinnamon (1 tablespoon)
- black pepper (1 tablespoon)
- salt (1 teaspoon)
- ginger

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients and cook until thick

title: Crabapple Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit (3 pounds)
- water (about 1 cup)
- sugar (1.5 pounds)
- vinegar (1 quart)
- salt (1 teaspoon)
- spices to taste

## Directions

1. Quarter and core the fruit
2. Boil with about 1 cup of water
3. Rub through a colander
4. Add 1.5 pounds sugar, 1 quart vinegar, and 1 teaspoon salt
5. Add spice to taste and boil till thick

title: Crabapple Sauce for Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds crabapples
- 1½ quarts vinegar
- 3 pounds brown sugar
- 1 dessertspoon cinnamon
- 1 dessertspoon cloves
- 1 dessertspoon pepper
- 1 dessertspoon salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil 3 pounds crabapples in 1½ quarts vinegar
2. Strain through a colander
3. Add 3 pounds brown sugar, 1 dessertspoon each cinnamon, cloves, pepper and salt
4. Let boil about 5 minutes
5. Bottle hot
6. Add more vinegar if too thick

title: Cracker Panada
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 hard crackers baked quite brown
- 1 quart water
- sugar
- wine
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Break in pieces three or four hard crackers that are baked quite brown
2. Let them boil fifteen minutes in one quart of water
3. Remove from the fire, let them stand three or four minutes
4. Strain off the liquor through a fine wire sieve
5. Season it with sugar
6. For invalids recovering from a fever, add a little wine and nutmeg

title: Cracker Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 crackers rolled
- A piece of butter
- 5 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups stoned raisins
- 1 cup currants
- Citron, cinnamon, nutmeg and little flour (1 tablespoon)
- 1 quart milk

## Directions

1. Soak crackers in one quart milk over night
2. In the morning add well-beaten eggs, sugar, raisins, currants, citron, cinnamon, nutmeg, flour and salt
3. Steam four or five hours

title: Crackle Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups granulated sugar
- butter size of small egg
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil sugar, butter, water, and lemon juice together
2. When it hardens in cold water, remove from fire and flavor with vanilla
3. As soon as cool enough to handle, flour the hands and pull

title: Cranberry And Raisin Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups chopped cranberries
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 teaspoon butter
- ½ cup water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake between 2 crusts

title: Cranberry Frappe
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cranberries
- 1 quart of water
- 1 pint of sugar
- juice of 2 lemons
- ice and salt

## Directions

1. Boil cranberries and water for five or six minutes.
2. Strain through a coarse cheese cloth.
3. Add one pint of sugar and stir and boil until sugar is dissolved.
4. When cold add juice of two lemons strained.
5. Freeze to a mush, using equal parts of ice and salt.
6. Serve in glass cups.

title: Cranberry Frappe
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cranberries
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups water
- juice 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Cook cranberries and water 8 minutes
2. Force through a sieve
3. Add sugar and lemon juice
4. Freeze to a mush using equal portions of ice and salt

title: Cranberry Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of cranberries
- one scant cupful of water
- two cupfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. To one quart of cranberries add one scant cupful of water, and cook until the berries are tender
2. Remove from the fire and strain through a fine sieve
3. Return the juice to the saucepan, add two cupfuls of sugar, and cook just long enough to thoroughly melt the sugar
4. When cool, put in a mould and pack in ice for an hour, and serve in sherbet glasses

title: Cranberry Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries (1 quart)
- water (1 pint)
- white sugar (1 pound)
- raisins (0.5 pound)
- walnuts, chopped (10 cents' worth)
- oranges (2)

## Directions

1. Boil cranberries and water together until cooked
2. Add white sugar, raisins, chopped walnuts, grated rind and juice of 2 oranges
3. Boil for 20 minutes

title: Cranberry Or Raisin Pie, Or Tart
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups cranberries
- ¾ cup sugar
- 3 tablespoons cracker crumbs
- ¾ cup seeded raisins
- ½ cup water
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Chop cranberries and raisins together
2. Add water and cook slightly
3. Add sugar and crumbs
4. Cook until thoroughly mixed
5. When cool, add vanilla
6. Just before serving, put between an upper and under crust, previously baked or in a shell and cover with a lattice of baked crust

title: Cranberry Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cupful of cranberries
- a half cupful of seeded raisins
- a cupful of sugar
- a half cupful of water
- a tablespoonful of flour
- a teaspoonful of vanilla
- upper and under crust

## Directions

1. Chop one cupful of cranberries and a half cupful of seeded raisins together into small pieces.
2. Add to them a cupful of sugar, a half cupful of water, a tablespoonful of flour, and a teaspoonful of vanilla.
3. Bake with an upper and under crust.

title: Cranberry Tart
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 3/4 cups cranberries
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 3/4 cup water

## Directions

1. Pick over and wash cranberries
2. Add water, cover and cook over moderate fire until all the berries have burst
3. Add sugar and cook to thoroughly combine
4. Cool and put in previously baked lower crust just before serving
5. Cover with lattice of baked crust, or bake mixture in crust without previously cooking

title: Cream Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 2 cups sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 3/4 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Cream Biscuits (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- little salt
- good rich milk or cream

## Directions

1. Moisten flour with good rich milk or cream
2. Do not mix stiff
3. Bake in a hot oven

title: Cream Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups white sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 3 cups flour
- vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Cream the sugar and butter
2. Break in eggs and add milk
3. Put soda and cream tartar in flour
4. Beat all together

title: Cream Cake (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 pint milk
- 1/4 cup flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- vanilla flavoring
- butter

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add 3 eggs beaten in 1 cup milk
3. Add 3 cups flour, 2 teaspoons cream tartar, 1 teaspoon soda
4. For middle cream: let 1 pint milk come to a boil
5. Add 1/4 cup flour, 1 cup sugar, 2 eggs
6. Flavor with vanilla and a lump of butter

title: Cream Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound cheese
- 1 dessertspoon butter
- 2 teaspoons mustard
- 3/4 cup good sweet cream

## Directions

1. Mix cheese, mustard, and butter together till smooth
2. Add cream

title: Cream Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sour cream
- 3 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 3 teaspoons soda
- a nutmeg grated
- 1 teaspoon lemon
- flour enough to roll out

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake in a quick oven

title: Cream Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups butter
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1 cup thin sweet cream
- 1 egg
- 3 level teaspoons baking powder
- flour to roll soft

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll soft and bake

title: Cream Cookies 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup sour cream
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 2 spoons baking powder
- dash of salt

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll and bake

title: Cream Crullers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 2 cups cream
- 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, cream, eggs, and baking powder
2. Mix in enough flour to roll out soft
3. Fry in very hot lard

title: Cream Egg Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs well beaten
- 6 tablespoons cream
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 6 teaspoons vinegar
- a small piece of butter
- 1/2 head of cabbage chopped fine
- salt for sprinkling
- 2 tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. Put on the fire and cook, stirring constantly until quite thick, or cook in a double boiler
2. Have a half head of cabbage chopped fine, sprinkle with salt
3. Add to the dressing when cold 2 tablespoons cream and pour over the cabbage

title: Cream Fruit Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 2 eggs
- 1 cup berry jam
- 1/2 cup sugar
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Beat whites of 2 eggs, 1 cup berry jam, and 1/2 cup sugar together for 1/2 hour
2. Serve with sweet cream

title: Cream Mustard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup vinegar
- 1 tablespoon dry mustard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup sweet cream

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 cup vinegar on the stove to boil
2. Mix 1 tablespoon dry mustard, 1 teaspoon salt, 2 eggs, and 3/4 cup sweet cream
3. Beat this well
