4. Pour into boiling vinegar
5. Stir whole mixture over boiling water until like thick cream

title: Cream Of Asparagus
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bundle of asparagus
- salted water
- 1 quart of good stock
- 2 ounces of butter
- 2 ounces of flour
- 1 pint of milk
- 1 gill of cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean a bundle of asparagus, cut off tips and boil in salted water till soft
2. Boil the stalks twenty minutes in a quart of good stock
3. Put two ounces of butter in a stew-pan with two ounces of flour, mix smoothly and pour in the hot stock, having previously pulped the asparagus through a sieve
4. Add one pint of milk
5. Boil up and skim
6. Put the tips in a tureen with a gill of cream
7. Pour in the boiling soup
8. Season with salt and pepper to taste, and serve

title: Cream Of Barley Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 knuckle of veal or chicken or turkey bones
- 2 quarts cold water
- 1/2 cup pearl barley
- 1/2 pint boiling cream
- salt
- pepper
- 15 beef balls

## Directions

1. Boil knuckle of veal or bones in cold water very gently for three hours
2. Skim and strain
3. Wash pearl barley in cold water
4. Cover it with the white broth
5. Cover saucepan and cook very gently until barley is tender
6. Remove one-third of the barley and set aside
7. Rub remaining portion through a sieve
8. Place whole barley grains in saucepan with barley and stock passed through sieve
9. Add boiling cream and season to taste with salt and pepper
10. Throw 15 beef balls into soup
11. Boil up once and serve 3 balls to each person

title: Cream of Carrot Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 carrots
- 1 pint of water
- 1 slice of onion
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 quart milk
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- salt
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Grate 3 carrots, cover with a pint of water and add a slice of onion and a bay leaf
2. Simmer gently for 30 minutes
3. Remove onion and bay leaf and add 1 quart milk
4. Moisten 1 tablespoon cornstarch in a little cold water and add it to soup
5. Stir till smooth, add salt and pepper
6. Just at serving time add 2 tablespoons butter

title: Cream of Celery Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. milk
- 1 small onion
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- A little mace
- 1 cup of whipped cream
- Long stalks of 3 heads of celery
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil onion, celery and mace from thirty to forty minutes
2. Mix flour with two tablespoonfuls of cold milk and add to boiling milk; cook ten minutes
3. Mash the celery in the water it was boiled in and stir in boiling milk
4. Add butter and season with salt and pepper to taste
5. Strain, and serve immediately
6. Whip a cup of cream and add to soup after it is in the tureen

title: Cream of Celery Soup (Second Recipe)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Pint of milk
- Flour
- Cornstarch
- A dash of red pepper
- A slice of onion
- A blade of mace
- A head of celery
- Salt
- Lump of butter
- A cupful of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Boil together pint of milk thickened with flour and cornstarch, a dash of red pepper, a slice of onion, and a blade of mace
2. Add a head of celery previously cut in pieces, boiled and mashed, in salted water in which boiled
3. When well blended strain
4. Add lump of butter; stir over fire till blended
5. If very special add a cupful of whipped cream after soup is dished in very hot tureen

title: Cream of Lettuce Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads of lettuce
- 1 quart of good stock
- 2 tablespoonfuls of white roux
- 1 tablespoonful of cold boiled onion, minced fine
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley
- 1 cup of milk
- Pepper and salt
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Shred finely two heads of lettuce — the greener the better.
2. Cook for half an hour in a quart of good stock, rub through a colander.
3. Return to the fire; stir into a cup of this two tablespoonfuls of white roux and a tablespoonful of cold boiled onion, minced fine, and one of minced parsley.
4. Heat a cup of milk in another vessel, season with pepper and salt, stir in a well-whipped egg, and pour this mixture into the tureen, adding finally the lettuce soup.

title: Cream of Lilies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Best white castor oil
- Strong solution of sal tartar in water

## Directions

1. Pour strong solution of sal tartar in water into best white castor oil
2. Shake until it looks thick and white
3. Perfume with lavender

title: Cream of Oysters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of oysters in their own liquor
- One tablespoonful of butter
- One heaping tablespoonful of flour
- Pepper, salt and paprica to taste
- One half pint of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Scald a quart of oysters in their own liquor
2. Remove the oysters; chop and pound them in a mortar, then press as much of them as possible through a puree sieve
3. Make a roux of one tablespoonful of butter and a heaping tablespoonful of flour
4. Dilute it with the oyster juice
5. Add the oyster pulp; season it with pepper, salt and paprica, and keep it hot until ready to serve
6. Just before serving add a half pint of whipped cream, and beat it well into the soup

title: Cream of Potato
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of mashed potatoes
- 1 pint of hot milk
- 1 cup white sauce
- salt
- pepper
- celery salt
- onion juice

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup of mashed potatoes with 1 pint of hot milk
2. Add 1 cup white sauce
3. Flavor with salt, pepper, celery salt and onion juice

title: Cream of Roses
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Olive oil, one pound
- Attar of roses, fifty drops
- Oil of rosemary, twenty-five drops
- Alkanet root for coloring

## Directions

1. Mix olive oil, attar of roses, and oil of rosemary
2. Color it with alkanet root

title: Cream of Tartar and Soda Biscuit Without Milk
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. flour
- 2 heaping teaspoonfuls butter
- 2 cups cold water
- 2 teaspoonfuls cream tartar
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- ½ teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Mix cream tartar with flour and dissolve soda in water
2. Stir the dissolved soda in the cold water
3. Mix the dough very quickly, having it just stiff enough to handle and roll
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Cream of Tomato Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of tomatoes (1 quart)
- 3/4 teaspoon soda
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- 1 quart milk

## Directions

1. Stew the tomatoes slowly 1/2 hour
2. Strain, and add soda while hot
3. Melt butter, add flour, mix fine
4. Add milk gradually, then the seasoning
5. Add the tomatoes to this mixture and serve at once

title: Cream of Turnip Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of lamb or mutton broth
- 2 cups of turnip dice
- White, young turnips

## Directions

1. Cook turnip dice in the liquor half an hour after the boil begins.
2. When very tender, rub through a colander.
3. Return to the fire and proceed as with cream of celery soup, only putting in both white and yolk of the egg.

title: Cream Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cupful sugar
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- 1 cupful flour
- 2.5 cups milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 0.5 cupful sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Sift the flour and baking powder together.
2. Beat the yolks and sugar together.
3. Add the flour and lastly the whipped whites of the eggs.
4. Bake this cake mixture in two layers.
5. For the cream filling, scald the milk and turn it on to the beaten egg.
6. Return it to the fire and add the flour moistened with a little milk, and the sugar, and stir until thickened.
7. Let it cool before adding it to the cake.
8. Place between the cake layers when cold, and just before serving, a thick layer of whipped cream or the cream filling.
9. Have the top piece covered with a boiled icing.

title: Cream Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 small cup sugar
- Yolks of 3 eggs
- Butter size of egg
- 1 tablespoonful flour

## Directions

1. Boil until it thickens, stirring constantly
2. Fill the shell (which has previously been baked) with the mixture
3. Ice with the whites of eggs
4. Place in oven a few minutes until icing begins to brown

title: Cream Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- rich paste for shell
- 1 pint of sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons of flour
- a little milk
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 3 tablespoons of sugar
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- 2 whites of eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Make a shell of rich paste
2. Put 1 pint of sweet milk on in double boiler
3. Wet 2 tablespoons of flour with a little milk
4. Pour on it the boiling milk slowly
5. Add to this the yolks of 2 eggs beaten with 3 tablespoons of sugar and 1 of butter
6. Cook 5 minutes
7. Pour into the crust which has been already baked
8. Make meringue of the 2 whites whipped light with 2 tablespoons sugar
9. Brown slightly

title: Cream Pie No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- nice crust
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1¼ cups sweet milk
- a little piece butter
- 2 level tablespoons cornstarch
- vanilla
- a pinch of salt
- white of an egg or whipped cream

## Directions

1. Line a pie dish with nice crust and bake
2. Take 2 eggs, 1 cup sugar, 1¼ cups sweet milk, a little piece butter, 2 level tablespoons cornstarch, flavor with vanilla, a pinch of salt
3. Put on the stove and bring to a boil
4. Put this mixture into the crust
5. Top with either the white of an egg beaten for frosting, or whipped cream

title: Cream Pie No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint milk
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- five tablespoons sugar
- yolks of 2 eggs
- a little cold milk
- whites of eggs for icing

## Directions

1. Scald 1 pint milk
2. Wet 2 tablespoons starch with a little cold milk
3. Beat the eggs and sugar till light
4. Stir the whole into the scalding milk
5. Use the whites of eggs for icing

title: Cream Puffs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a cup of butter
- one cup of hot water
- one cup of flour
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Melt half a cup of butter in one cup of hot water, put on the stove to boil.
2. While boiling add one cup of flour.
3. Take off and let cool.
4. When cold stir in three eggs one after the other without beating.
5. Drop on buttered tins and bake thirty minutes.

title: Cream Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup hot water
- 1 1/2 cups butter
- 1 cup sifted flour
- 3 unbeaten eggs

## Directions

1. Boil water and butter together
2. While boiling stir in sifted flour
3. Take from stove and stir to a thin paste
4. After this cools stir in 3 unbeaten eggs
5. Stir 5 minutes
6. Drop in tablespoonfuls on buttered tins
7. Bake in quick oven 25 minutes, opening door not oftener than necessary
8. Be careful that they do not touch each other
9. Fill when cold

title: Cream Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup water
- 1 cup flour
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Put butter and water on the stove
2. While boiling add the flour, stirring all the time till cooked
3. When cool, add 4 eggs
4. Drop in small spoonfuls on a buttered pan
5. Bake 25 minutes in a hot oven
6. When cool, split 1 side and fill with cream filling or whipped cream

title: Cream Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup water
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup flour
- 3 unbeaten eggs

## Directions

1. Boil water and butter together
2. When boiling, stir in 1 cup flour
3. When almost cold stir in 3 unbeaten eggs
4. Drop on buttered tins
5. Bake 25 minutes
6. Fill with whipped cream

title: Cream Puffs 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup water
- 1 cup flour
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Put butter and water on the stove
2. While boiling add the flour, stirring all the time till cooked
3. When cool, add 4 eggs
4. Drop in small spoonfuls on a buttered pan
5. Bake 25 minutes in a hot oven
6. When cool, split 1 side
7. Fill with cream filling or whipped cream

title: Cream Puffs Filling
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of milk
- one egg
- half a cup of sugar
- two small tablespoonfuls of cornstarch
- vanilla
- a small lump of butter

## Directions

1. Thicken one cup of milk and one egg mixed with half a cup of sugar using two small tablespoonfuls of cornstarch.
2. Flavor with vanilla and add a small lump of butter.

title: Cream Puffs Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups milk
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil
2. Cool

title: Cream Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 oz. flour
- 1/2 oz. cornstarch
- 3 oz. powdered sugar
- vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Put milk to boil
2. Break eggs into a basin and add flour, cornstarch, powdered sugar, beating all well together
3. Add the boiling milk gradually, stirring well
4. Put all in the saucepan and stir till it comes to a boil
5. Remove and add vanilla

title: Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- a little pepper

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and flour together and stir until they froth
2. Add the milk and stir constantly until it thickens and boils
3. Add the salt and pepper
4. If it must stand any time before serving, it should be closely covered and stirred occasionally to prevent a skin forming on the top
5. When a large quantity is made, 3/4 of the milk may be scalded and added after the first third is stirred in

title: Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups milk
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons flour
- ¼ teaspoon salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Melt butter in saucepan
2. Add the flour and stir until frothy
3. Add milk
4. Stir constantly until it boils a minute
5. Add seasoning

title: Cream Sponge
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half box Knox's gelatine
- one-quarter cup cold water
- one pint cream
- one cup milk
- one cup sugar
- one egg
- one teaspoonful vanilla

## Directions

1. Soak gelatine in water for one-half hour
2. Stand it in boiling water to dissolve
3. Scald the milk, add sugar
4. Beat the egg until light, and add hot milk to egg, stirring constantly while adding, removing from fire for the purpose
5. Add gelatine and flavoring, and set aside to cool but not to set
6. Whip the cream, add the cooled custard, beat well
7. Put into moulds

title: Cream Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. milk
- Butter size of egg
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 cup rich cream
- Slices of toast

## Directions

1. Heat to boiling, and add butter
2. Stir flour smoothly into cream, and add some of the boiling milk to this
3. Heat it gradually and prevent the flour from lumping
4. Stir into the boiling milk, and let it cook a few moments
5. Salt to taste
6. After taking from the fire stir in a beaten egg
7. Strain the mixture on to toast lightly buttered

title: Cream Tomato Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can tomatoes
- Butter size of egg
- 1 qt. water
- 1 qt. milk
- Salt and pepper
- 1 teaspoonful soda

## Directions

1. Cook tomatoes thoroughly in water; have milk scalding in a double boiler to prevent scorching
2. When tomatoes are done, add a large teaspoonful of soda, which will cause a violent effervescence
3. Set vessel in a pan before adding soda, to prevent waste
4. When the commotion has ceased add milk and seasoning

title: Cream-Cheese Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- half a Philadelphia cream-cheese
- a tablespoonful of butter
- powdered yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
- salt
- paprica
- crackers

## Directions

1. Rub together half a Philadelphia cream-cheese, a tablespoonful of butter, and the powdered yolks of two hard-boiled eggs
2. Season with salt and paprica
3. Spread this between crackers — saltines, or water-thin biscuits, or sea foams
4. Home-made cottage cheese can be substituted for the Philadelphia

title: Creamed Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- milk
- flour
- butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil cabbage in salted water until tender
2. Drain and pour milk enough to almost cover
3. Mix flour and cold milk and add just as milk and cabbage boils
4. Cook five minutes
5. Add butter and nutmeg to taste

title: Creamed Carrots
body:
## Ingredients

- lard
- 1 onion
- carrots
- salt and pepper
- water
- 3/4 cup sweet cream
- flour

## Directions

1. Put in your pot a little lard; when hot slice in 1 onion
2. Put in carrots, salt and pepper, cover with water, let boil till tender
3. Add 3/4 cup sweet cream, mixed with a little flour
4. If too thick add a little hot water

title: Creamed Carrots with Butter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- salt
- water
- 2 medium teaspoons flour
- 2 medium teaspoons butter
- 1 cup water

## Directions

1. Scrape carrots, cut into cubes or slice thinly lengthwise
2. Cook in boiling salted water forty minutes, drain and pour the sauce over carrots in a warm dish
3. For Butter Sauce: mix 2 medium teaspoons flour, 2 medium teaspoons butter, 1 cup water and season to taste

title: Creamed Celery
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- salt
- water
- milk
- cream
- white roux

## Directions

1. Cut into inch-long pieces
2. Cook tender in boiling, salted water
3. Drain this off
4. Cover with a cupful of hot milk (half cream, if you have it) in which has been stirred a tablespoonful of white roux
5. Simmer five minutes and serve

title: Creamed Celery in Tomato Cups
body:
## Ingredients

- large tomatoes
- stewed celery
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- salt and pepper
- browned bread crumbs
- hot buttered toast

## Directions

1. Cut out circular pieces from stem end of large tomatoes
2. Remove part of pulp
3. Fill tomatoes with stewed celery to which has been added 1 cup sweet cream, 1 tablespoon melted butter, season to taste
4. Cover the openings with browned bread crumbs
5. Bake 3/4 hour in a moderate oven
6. Serve on rounds of hot buttered toast

title: Creamed Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ lbs. chicken, raw
- ½ lb. cooked sweetbreads
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 pt. cream
- ½ can mushrooms
- 1½ tablespoonfuls flour
- seasoning
- bread-crumbs
- parsley

## Directions

1. When chicken and sweet-breads have been cooked tender allow them to cool, chopping sweet-breads fine.
2. Put butter in a saucepan; when melted add flour, and when dissolved, cream, which has been heated.
3. When thick add seasoning and chicken.
4. Mix all well together; pour into a baking-dish, spread bread-crumbs and butter on top and bake twenty minutes.
5. Garnish with parsley.

title: Creamed Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 lbs. chicken, raw
- 0.5 lb. cooked sweetbreads
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 pint cream
- 0.5 can mushrooms
- 1.5 tablespoonfuls flour

## Directions

1. When chicken and sweet-breads have been cooked tender allow them to cool, chopping sweet-breads fine.
2. Put butter in a saucepan; when melted add flour, and when dissolved, cream, which has been heated.
3. When thick add seasoning and chicken.
4. Mix all well together; pour into a baking-dish, spread bread-crumbs and butter on top and bake twenty minutes.

title: Creamed Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken of four pounds, or two of six pounds
- four sweetbreads
- one can of mushrooms
- four coffee-cups or one quart of cream
- four large tablespoonfuls of butter
- five even tablespoonfuls of flour
- small half of a grated onion
- a very little nutmeg
- black and red pepper
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Boil chicken and sweetbreads and when cold cut up as for salad
2. In a sauce-pan put four coffee-cups or one quart of cream
3. In another sauce-pan put four large tablespoonfuls of butter and five even tablespoonfuls of flour
4. Stir until melted, then pour in the hot cream and stir until it thickens
5. Flavor with the small half of a grated onion and a very little nutmeg
6. Season highly with black and red pepper
7. Put the chicken and cream in a baking-dish
8. Add the mushrooms cut in pieces (if large), and cover with grated bread
9. Put a number of pieces of butter on the top and bake ten or twenty minutes

title: Creamed Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken of 4 1/2 pounds, or 2 of 6 pounds
- 4 sweetbreads
- 1 can of mushrooms
- 4 coffee cups cream
- 4 large tablespoons butter
- 5 even tablespoons flour
- a small half of grated onion
- a very little grated nutmeg
- black and red pepper
- bread crumbs
- pieces of butter

## Directions

1. Boil chicken and sweetbreads, and when cold cut up as for salad
2. In a saucepan put 4 coffee cups cream
3. In another put 4 large tablespoons butter and 5 even tablespoons of flour
4. Stir until melted, then pour on the hot cream, stirring until it thickens
5. Flavor with a small half of grated onion and a very little grated nutmeg
6. Season highly with black and red pepper
7. Put chicken and ingredients together with sweetbreads and mushrooms (which if large should be cut in 4 pieces) in a baking dish
8. Cover with bread crumbs and pieces of butter, and bake 20 minutes

title: Creamed Codfish
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of fish
- cold water
- one cupful of good cream
- one cupful of milk
- two tablespoonfuls of flour
- one tablespoonful of butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Pick one cup of fish fine, then freshen in cold water
2. Bring just to a boil, then drain
3. Take one cupful of good cream and one cupful of milk
4. Mix two tablespoonfuls of flour smooth with some of the milk
5. Add one tablespoonful of butter and more salt if necessary

title: Creamed Codfish (Salt)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- salted codfish
- water
- one tablespoonful of vinegar
- one cupful of milk
- one tablespoonful of butter
- one tablespoonful of flour
- two beaten eggs
- one tablespoonful of minced parsley
- lemon-juice
- cayenne or paprica

## Directions

1. Soak all night, changing the water several times and having the last bath quite hot
2. Boil tender in hot water with a tablespoonful of vinegar
3. Take out the bones while hot, and let it cool before picking or shredding it into fine flakes
4. Heat a cupful of milk
5. Stir into it a tablespoonful of butter rolled in one of flour
6. Cook until it thickens well
7. Take from the fire and add two beaten eggs
8. When these are well mixed, add the shredded fish, and cook two minutes, stirring steadily
9. Add a tablespoonful of minced parsley and a little lemon-juice
10. Season with cayenne or paprica
11. Serve hot

title: Creamed Cucumbers
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cucumbers
- salted water
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Pare, cut lengthwise
2. remove the seeds and cut pulp into sections
3. cook gently in salted water until soft
4. drain, and serve in a cream sauce

title: Creamed Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 fresh eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Boil 5 fresh eggs 15 or 20 minutes
2. Dip in cold water just an instant and shell
3. Cut lengthwise in 4 slices on a hot, shallow vegetable dish set in range closet to keep warm
4. Make a sauce: cook 1 tablespoon butter with 1 onion chopped fine
5. Add 1 tablespoon flour and rub smooth, may be browned if desired
6. Add 1 pint of sweet milk and 1/2 teaspoon salt, little pepper
7. Cook till like gravy and pour over the eggs and serve hot

title: Creamed Lima Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup dried Lima beans
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 cup cream
- 1 tablespoon butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak over night
2. drain and cook slowly in salted water until soft
3. drain, add cream, salt and pepper
4. Re-heat before serving
5. When cooked they may be popped from their skins before adding the cream

title: Creamed Lobster
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat of 1 lobster chopped fine
- 1/2 pt. whipped cream
- 1/2 oz. gelatine
- A little anchovy paste
- cayenne and salt

## Directions

1. Take the meat lobster, chop it fine, add whipped cream, gelatine which has been soaked in water, a little anchovy sauce, salt and cayenne
2. Stir it gently till nearly set, and pour into a slightly oiled border mould
3. When turned out, fill the centre with mayonnaise, garnish with mashed green peas put through a forcepipe, and the claws round the outside

title: Creamed Macaroni on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup thin white sauce
- 1 cup boiled macaroni
- 1/2 cup grated cheese
- 6 slices toast (buttered)

## Directions

1. Add macaroni to sauce
2. Cook for some minutes
3. Pour over toast and place on baking pan
4. Dust with cheese
5. Set on top shelf of oven for some time

title: Creamed Onions
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of medium-sized onions
- 1 teaspoonful of sugar
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1/2 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 cupful of hot milk
- whole pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel one quart of medium-sized onions, place them in a saucepan, cover with boiling water; add one teaspoonful of sugar, and boil until nearly done; add one teaspoonful salt; boil a few minutes longer, then drain in a colander.
2. Melt two tablespoonfuls of butter, add half a tablespoonful of flour.
3. Stir and cook two minutes; add one cupful of hot milk and cook two minutes longer, and season with whole pepper and salt.
4. Put the onions in a hot dish and pour the sauce over them.

title: Creamed Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup thick cream sauce
- salt
- black pepper
- cayenne
- celery salt
- 1 pint oysters
- toast

## Directions

1. Make 1 cup of thick cream sauce and season with salt, black pepper, cayenne and celery salt
2. Wash and pick over 1 pint oysters
3. Parboil until plump
4. Skim carefully, drain and add them to sauce
5. Serve on toast and garnish dish with points of toast

title: Creamed Oysters on Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of hot toast
- 1 pint boiling milk
- 1 pint oysters
- 2 dessertspoonfuls cornstarch or flour
- Salt
- cayenne
- lump of butter size of small egg

## Directions

1. Cover large hot platter with slices of hot toast
2. Turn in oysters to boiling milk, then add 2 dessertspoonfuls cornstarch or flour stirred smooth in a little milk
3. Add salt, cayenne, and lump of butter size of small egg
4. Pour over toast and serve instantly

title: Creamed Parsnips
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- butter
- flour
- cream

## Directions

1. Boil, scrape, and slice crosswise
2. Heat a tablespoonful of butter in a saucepan
3. Put in the parsnips and shake and turn until all are coated with the butter and very hot
4. Turn them into a deep dish
5. Pour over them a sauce made by adding to the butter left in the saucepan a teaspoonful of flour and thinning it with three or four tablespoonfuls of hot cream
6. Boil up once, and when you have covered the parsnips with it, serve

title: Creamed Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- baked potatoes
- cream or rich milk
- salt
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Slice baked potatoes
2. Heat sufficient cream or rich milk to moisten the potatoes well
3. Add salt, pepper and parsley
4. Let cream boil up and put in the potatoes
5. Leave them until well heated and serve at once

title: Creamed Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled potatoes
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- parsley
- milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Take some cold boiled potatoes and cut in dice
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Add a little butter and a sprig of parsley, cut up fine
4. Put milk into frying-pan and add potatoes
5. Add enough milk to make it creamy
6. Add a little thickening of flour
7. Add enough milk to make a nice gravy

title: Creamed Salmon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- A tin of salmon
- One pint of milk
- Butter size of an egg
- One teaspoonful of flour
- Salt and cayenne pepper to taste
- Two well-beaten eggs
- Cracker or bread-crumbs

## Directions

1. Take a tin of salmon, empty on a dish and flake with a fork
2. Have ready a sauce made in double boiler from one pint of milk, butter size of an egg, one teaspoonful of flour, with salt and cayenne pepper to taste
3. Melt butter and flour until smooth and add milk slowly
4. When it boils remove from fire and add two well-beaten eggs
5. Put alternate layers of salmon and sauce in baking dish until all is used
6. Cover with layer of cracker or bread-crumbs
7. Bake fifteen minutes and serve hot

title: Creamed Salmon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tin salmon
- 1 pint milk
- butter size of an egg
- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- 1 egg beaten
- biscuit crumbs

## Directions

1. Turn out salmon on a dish and flake with a fork
2. Make sauce in double boiler from 1 pint milk, butter size of an egg, and a tablespoon of flour, salt and cayenne pepper to taste
3. Melt butter and flour together until smooth
4. Add to milk slowly, also 1 egg beaten, and put in just like boiled custard
5. Put in a baking dish alternate layers of salmon, biscuit crumbs, and sauce until all is used
6. Put layer of crumbs on top
7. Bake 15 minutes and serve hot

title: Creamed Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large or 2 small turnips
- water
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup sweet cream or milk
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pare and slice 1 large or 2 small turnips
2. Cover with water to which has been added 1 tablespoon salt
3. Cook until easily pierced with a fork, then drain and mash
4. Add 1 tablespoon butter and 1 cup sweet cream or milk
5. Beat the turnips until all are smooth
6. Add a few dashes of pepper

title: Creamed Young Carrots
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young carrots
- one tablespoonful butter
- hot water
- salt and pepper
- four tablespoonfuls cream
- one teaspoonful chopped parsley
- beaten yolks of two eggs

## Directions

1. Scald for five minutes and rub off the skins with a rough cloth
2. Slice crosswise and thin
3. Heat in a saucepan butter, two tablespoonfuls of hot water, salt and pepper to taste
4. Put in the sliced carrots
5. Cook gently, covered, for half an hour
6. In another saucepan heat cream and chopped parsley
7. When the mixture boils take from the fire and pour upon the beaten yolks of eggs
8. Stir up well
9. Pour over the carrots
10. Cook one scant minute

title: Creamy Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/4 cup powdered sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls wine
- 2 tablespoonfuls cream

## Directions

1. Cream butter, add sugar slowly, then wine and cream.
2. Beat well and just before using, place bowl over hot water and stir till creamy, but not enough to melt the butter.
3. When the wine and cream are added, the sauce has a curdled appearance. This is removed by beating and heating enough to blend materials.
4. Sauce should be cold when served.

title: Crimson Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- dark-colored, cold boiled beet-root
- tabasco sauce
- very white buttered slices

## Directions

1. Take dark-colored, cold boiled beet-root and pass through a fine sieve
2. Flavor with a little tabasco sauce
3. Spread on very white buttered slices, so that the color shows well at edges

title: Croquettes of Lobster
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat of 1 fine lobster
- 2 tablespoons of butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 good teaspoonful of lemon juice
- 1/4 cup of fine breadcrumbs
- Pinch of mace and lemon peel
- 1 teaspoonful of anchovy sauce
- Yolks of 2 raw eggs beaten very light
- Yolks of 2 eggs, boiled
- Salt and cayenne pepper
- Cottolene for frying

## Directions

1. Mince the meat, work in the butter, melted, but not hot
2. Add the seasoning, the raw eggs, and lastly the breadcrumbs
3. Make into oblong balls, set on the ice for two hours
4. Fry quickly in deep cottolene
5. Drain them of every drop of fat by rolling each, for an instant, very lightly upon a hot, clean cloth
6. Be sure your dish is well heated

title: Crow's Nest
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sliced apples
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- flour for a nice stiff batter
- soft sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- a little butter

## Directions

1. Butter a pie plate and fill with sliced apples
2. Make a batter with 2 eggs, 1/2 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon butter, 1/2 cup sweet milk, 1 teaspoon baking powder, and flour for a nice stiff batter
3. Pour batter over apples and bake slowly so that apples will cook well
4. Turn out of the plate upside down
5. Sprinkle with soft sugar and 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon and a little butter in spots
6. Turn a plate over it and let steam about ten minutes
7. Serve with or without milk

title: Crullers
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a quarter pounds of white sugar
- one-half pound of butter
- eight eggs, well beaten
- one nutmeg
- flour enough to roll out

## Directions

1. Fry in very hot lard

title: Crumb Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 teaspoon each of cloves and allspice
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 cup raisins

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar, and butter together
2. Take 1/4 cup out to sprinkle on top of cake
3. Add spices to the remainder (1 teaspoon each of cloves and allspice)
4. Add 1 teaspoon soda and 1 cup sour milk to the remainder
5. Add 1 cup raisins
6. Sprinkle reserved portion on top

title: Crumb Cake (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup walnuts
- vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Crumble together 2 cups flour, 1 cup sugar, and 1/4 cup butter
2. Take out 1/3 of a cup and keep aside
3. To the balance add 1 egg, 1 cup sour milk, 1 teaspoon soda, 1 cup raisins, 1 cup walnuts, vanilla to taste
4. Put in pan and sprinkle the part kept out on top

title: Crumpets
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1/2 cup butter
- 2 small cups flour
- 2 tablespoons sour milk
- 1 egg
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1/4 teaspoon of all kinds of spices

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Crust for Meat Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 level teaspoon of soda
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 pint of flour
- 1 tablespoon lard
- sour milk

## Directions

1. Sift 1 level teaspoon of soda and 1 teaspoon salt in a pint of flour
2. Rub into flour 1 tablespoon lard
3. Wet with sour milk to a nice batter that will drop from the spoon
4. Have the meat prepared as for meat pie, with it hot in dish when crust is ready
5. Take a piece of white paper, make a tube with paper, place in centre of meat dish and spread batter over top of pie
6. Bake 1/2 hour

title: Cucumber and Onion Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ripe cucumbers
- 8 large onions
- salt
- vinegar
- 1 tablespoon white pepper
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon tumeric powder
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 3 cups white sugar

## Directions

1. Peel cucumbers and cut lengthwise, taking out all seeds
2. Slice very thin across each piece
3. Cut onions in slices
4. Put a layer of each and sprinkle with salt
5. Let stand 2 hours
6. Drain through a colander
7. Put in a kettle with barely enough vinegar to cover
8. Add 1 tablespoon white pepper, 1 tablespoon mustard, 1 tablespoon tumeric powder, 1 tablespoon flour, 3 cups white sugar
9. Mix all with a little vinegar
10. Boil slowly 1 hour

title: Cucumber Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart grated large cucumbers without seeds or peel
- 1 pint chopped onions
- 1 green pepper chopped
- 6 teaspoons salt
- 2 teaspoons black pepper
- 1.5 pints vinegar
- 4 or 5 small red peppers

## Directions

1. Drain grated cucumbers
2. Add onions, pepper, salt, black pepper, and vinegar
3. Mix all together and bottle
4. Do not drain too dry

title: Cucumber Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large cucumbers
- 6 large onions
- salt
- vinegar
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 3/4 teaspoon tumeric
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Peel and slice cucumbers and onions
2. Sprinkle with salt
3. Let stand over night
4. Drain
5. Put in kettle and cover with vinegar
6. Let boil until tender
7. Add sugar, mustard, and tumeric
8. Mix flour with cold vinegar
9. Stir into pickle

title: Cucumber Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 dozen small cucumbers
- 1 quart onions, sliced
- 5 cents mustard seed
- 5 cents celery seed
- 2 ounces olive oil
- 1 teaspoon tumeric
- 1 teaspoon cayenne
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon curry powder
- 3 cups brown sugar
- 3 pints vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice cucumbers and onions fine
2. Sprinkle 1 cup salt over and let stand over night
3. Drain
4. Add vinegar and all ingredients
5. Boil 10 minutes and bottle

title: Cucumber Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe cucumbers
- salt
- water
- vinegar
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 quart vinegar
- oil of cloves
- oil of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel cucumbers and cut lengthwise
2. Take out seeds
3. Soak in salt and water 24 hours
4. Take them out and wash them
5. Soak them in vinegar and water for 6 hours
6. Drain them
7. Make a syrup of 1 pound sugar and 1 quart vinegar
8. Flavor with oil of cloves and oil of cinnamon
9. Boil till tender

title: Cucumber Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 large green cucumbers peeled
- 4 large onions
- brine
- 1.5 pints vinegar
- 2.5 cups granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1 tablespoon celery seed
- 2 tablespoons mustard

## Directions

1. Let cucumbers stand in brine 4 hours
2. Chop fine with onions
3. Cook together as for salad dressing
4. Add cucumbers and onions and cook for 5 minutes

title: Cucumber Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large green cucumbers
- 3 large onions
- 1 pint vinegar
- 1 small cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon celery seed
- ½ teaspoon pepper
- 1 tablespoon white mustard seed
- 1 level teaspoon tumeric

## Directions

1. Pare, slice and salt over night
2. in the morning drain
3. pour over cucumbers and onions 1 pint vinegar, 1 small cup sugar, 1 teaspoon celery seed, ½ teaspoon pepper, 1 tablespoon white mustard seed, 1 level teaspoon tumeric, and let come to a boil
4. Put away in jars

title: Cup Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter washed in warm water
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 5 eggs
- 4 cups flour sifted
- 4 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix butter washed in warm water, sugar, and milk.
2. Add eggs mixed in one by one.
3. Sift in flour and baking powder.
4. Bake.

title: Cup Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter, creamed
- two cups of sugar
- four eggs, beaten separately
- one cup of milk
- three cups of sifted flour
- four even teaspoonfuls of baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix with the hand.

title: Cup Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup cold water
- 3 cups flour
- 3 tablespoons boiling water
- flavor to taste

## Directions

1. Beat butter and sugar to cream
2. Add the eggs well beaten
3. Take 3 tablespoons out of the cup of cold water and just before putting in pan to bake, add 3 tablespoons boiling water
4. Flavor to taste

title: Cup Cake (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Cream sugar with butter
2. Add the yolks of 2 eggs and nearly a cup of milk
3. Stir in 2 cups flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder
4. Add the beaten whites of the 2 eggs
5. Bake in cups or muffin tins

title: Cup Custard
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 2 teaspoonfuls sugar
- a pinch of salt
- a pinch of grated nutmeg
- good sweet milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Break an egg into a coffee-cup
2. Put in two teaspoonfuls of sugar
3. Beat it up thoroughly with a pinch of salt and a pinch of grated nutmeg
4. Fill up the cup with good sweet milk
5. Turn it into another cup, well buttered
6. Set it in a pan of boiling water, reaching nearly to the top of the cup
7. Set in the oven
8. When the custard is set it is done

title: Cup Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- a little salt
- 1 quart milk
- nutmeg for flavor

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs to a light froth
2. Add 1 cup granulated sugar, a little salt, and 1 quart milk
3. Flavor with nutmeg
4. Put the custard in 5 cups
5. Place cups in a pan of boiling water
6. Cook them until firm in the centre
7. When done stand away to cool
8. Serve it in the cups

title: Cup Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup flour
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1.5 teaspoons baking powder
- preserves

## Directions

1. Butter your cups
2. Pour in small quantity of preserves
3. Fill with batter half-way
4. Steam 20 minutes
5. This will make 8 medium-size cups

title: Cup Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- fresh or canned raspberries
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1.5 cups flour
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg if desired
- pinch of salt
- sweet milk
- 1 cup yellow sugar
- 1/2 cup butter

## Directions

1. Butter cups and fill about full of fresh or canned raspberries
2. Make a batter by sifting 2 teaspoons baking powder in 1.5 cups flour
3. Add 2 tablespoons sugar, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 egg if desired, and pinch of salt
4. Add enough sweet milk to make rather stiff
5. Fill cups 3/4 full of batter
6. Place in steamer over boiling water and steam from 20 to 30 minutes
7. Turn out on plates and serve hot with 1 cup yellow sugar creamed with 1/2 cup butter

title: Curds and Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- prepared rennet
- cloth
- cool water
- fresh cream
- powdered loaf-sugar
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. One gallon of milk will make a moderate dish
2. Put one spoonful of prepared rennet to each quart of milk
3. When you find that it has become curd, tie it loosely in a thin cloth and hang it to drain
4. Do not wring or press the cloth
5. When drained, put the curd into a mug and set in cool water, which must be frequently changed
6. When you dish it, if there is whey in the mug, ladle it gently out without pressing the curd
7. Lay it on a deep dish, and pour fresh cream over it
8. Have powdered loaf-sugar to eat with it; also hand the nutmeg grater

title: Cure For Coughs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three newly-laid eggs
- the juice of six lemons
- one-half pound of rock candy
- one pint of Jamaica brandy

## Directions

1. Pour the juice of six lemons over three newly-laid eggs, unbroken, and allow to stand for forty-eight hours
2. Pick out any bits of eggshell which are not dissolved
3. Add one-half pound of rock candy, and one pint of Jamaica brandy
4. Mix well and bottle
5. Dose: 1 tablespoonful three or four times a day

title: Cure For Ringworms
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow dock, root or leaves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Steep yellow dock, root or leaves, in vinegar, to cure the worst case of ringworm

title: Currant Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup new milk
- 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes
- flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup currants
- 2 well-beaten eggs
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scald 1 cup new milk and let it cool
2. Add 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes and flour enough to make soft batter
3. Set in a warm place to rise until light
4. Add 1 cup brown sugar, half a cup butter, 1 cup currants, 2 well-beaten eggs, and a little nutmeg
5. Add flour enough to knead soft
6. Let rise again
7. Tuck into tins and set in a warm place
8. Let rise about 2 hours
9. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Currant Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup washed currants
- white icing sugar

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, sugar, butter, milk, flour, and baking powder
2. Dredge currants with flour and add
3. Bake
4. When cooked, ice over the top with white icing sugar

title: Currant Conserve
body:
## Ingredients

- currants (5 pounds)
- granulated sugar (5 pounds)
- raisins (2 pounds)
- oranges (4)

## Directions

1. Stem currants and seed and chop raisins
2. Cook currants for 15 minutes
3. Add sugar warmed as for jelly, just set it in the oven while you cook currants
4. After you add sugar cook 5 minutes more
5. Then add raisins and cook 20 minutes
6. Then add the orange pulp and grated rind and cook 5 minutes
7. Put in pint cans

title: Currant Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 3 tablespoons sweet milk
- 1/2 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 teaspoon each of nutmeg, cloves, cinnamon
- 1 cup currants

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll and bake

title: Currant Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour to roll out

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out and bake

title: Currant Cookies 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup lard
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon salt
- flour enough to roll

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll and bake in hot oven

title: Currant Drops
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- currant-juice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Use currant-juice, instead of water, to moisten a quantity of sugar
2. Put it in a pan and heat, stirring constantly
3. Be sure not to let it boil
4. Then mix a very little more sugar, let it warm with the rest a moment
5. Then, with a smooth stick, drop on paper

title: Currant Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups flour
- 5 teaspoons baking powder or 1 teaspoon soda and 2 teaspoons cream of tartar
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- butter or shortening size of 3/2 egg
- a little nutmeg or spice to taste
- 1 cup currants or raisins
- 3/2 teaspoon ratafia
- cold water

## Directions

1. Sift flour with baking powder or soda and cream of tartar
2. Mix in sugar, salt, butter or shortening, nutmeg or spice, currants or raisins, and ratafia
3. Wet with cold water to make like a loaf of bread
4. Put in a greased pan
5. Let stand an hour to raise in a warm place
6. Bake an hour

title: Currant Loaf 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup cleaned currants
- grated rind of 1 lemon
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- cold milk

## Directions

1. Mix dry ingredients together
2. Rub in butter
3. Add currants and lemon rind
4. Mix to a thick batter with cold milk
5. Turn into a well-greased pan
6. Bake 1 hour in moderate oven

title: Currant Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup currants
- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 1 tablespoon flour
- yolks of 2 eggs
- beaten whites of 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Bake in 1 crust with currants, brown sugar, water, flour, and egg yolks
2. When baked cover with the beaten whites and 3/4 cup granulated sugar and brown

title: Currant Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups currants
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg
- a little salt
- 3/4 cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients
2. Fill tart shells
3. Bake

title: Currant Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- rich crust
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- butter size of egg
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Line tins with rich crust
2. Add well-beaten eggs mixed with sugar, butter, currants and vanilla
3. Bake light brown

title: Currie Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint vinegar
- 1 tablespoon currie powder
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon black pepper
- 1 teaspoon tumeric
- 3 cups sugar
- 1 dozen large cucumbers
- 1 dozen onions
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice cucumbers and onions
2. Sprinkle with salt
3. Let stand over night
4. Drain thoroughly in the morning
5. Add the sugar and spices
6. Let it cook until tender

title: Curried Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken weighing from a pound and a half to two pounds
- water
- salt
- two small onions
- butter
- curry powder
- flour
- egg yolk
- hot boiled rice

## Directions

1. Cut up a chicken weighing from a pound and a half to two pounds, as for fricassee, wash it well, and put it in a stewpan with sufficient water to cover it.
2. Boil it closely covered, until tender; add a large teaspoonful of salt, and cook a few minutes longer.
3. Remove from the fire, take out the chicken, pour the liquor into a bowl, and set it one side.
4. Cut up into the stewpan two small onions, and fry them with a piece of butter as large as an egg.
5. As soon as the onions are brown, skim them out and put in the chicken; fry for three or four minutes.
6. Sprinkle over two teaspoonfuls of curry powder.
7. Pour over the liquor in which the chicken was stewed, stir all well together, and stew for five minutes longer.
8. Stir into this a tablespoonful of sifted flour made thin with a little water.
9. Lastly, stir in a beaten yolk of egg.
10. Serve with hot boiled rice laid round on the edge of a platter, and the chicken curry in the centre.

title: Curried Lobster
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of lobster dice
- 2 cups weak stock
- 1 teaspoonful minced onion
- 2 teaspoonfuls of curry powder
- Saltspoon of salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Fry the onion in the butter, add the salt, the stock, the curry, and cook gently for five minutes, before putting in the lobster
2. Serve as soon as this is thoroughly heated
3. Pass plain boiled rice with this dish

title: Curried Mutton
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. raw or rare mutton
- 1 sour apple
- sufficient water to stew
- curry
- pepper
- salt
- 1 gill of cream
- 1 cupful of Patna rice
- 2 quarts of boiling water
- parsley

## Directions

1. Boil one sour apple with meat in sufficient water to stew
2. Add curry and pepper and salt to taste
3. When serving add a gill of cream
4. Boil a cupful of Patna rice in two quarts of boiling water twenty minutes
5. Strain through colander and shake well
6. Make a wall of the rice around the serving dish
7. Pour in the mixture and garnish with parsley
8. Serve very hot

title: Curried Oysters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen oysters
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoonful of curry
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 2 ounces of butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop the onion up quite fine
2. Mix the curry-powder, flour and butter together
3. Put all these ingredients into a stew-pan
4. Simmer till of a nice brown, stirring all the time
5. Add the liquor of the oysters and the lemon-juice
6. Boil together for five minutes
7. Put in the oysters
8. Boil up once
9. Serve with a dish of rice

title: Curried Pigeon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon
- crumbs
- chopped fat pork
- hard-boiled eggs
- celery
- onion-juice
- pepper
- salt
- boiling water
- browned flour
- curry-powder
- boiled rice
- ice-cold bananas

## Directions

1. Cook as directed in English Jugged Pigeon recipe
2. Dish and add to the gravy two teaspoonfuls of curry-powder
3. Boil one minute before pouring over the birds
4. Serve with boiled rice
5. Serve ice-cold bananas with this dish

title: Curry Sauce for Meats
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoon curry powder
- 1 tablespoon Chutney sauce
- 1 cup stock

## Directions

1. Brown butter
2. Add onion and Chutney sauce
3. Cook thoroughly before adding stock and curry
4. In this sauce, heat up cooked egg or meat cut in small pieces
5. Serve with cooked rice

title: Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 3 eggs
- 3/4 pint milk
- sugar to sweeten

## Directions

1. Cook yolks, milk, and sugar in double boiler until consistency of thick cream
2. Cool and serve with pudding

title: Custard Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1/8 teaspoon salt
- 2 cups milk
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat eggs slightly
2. Add sugar, salt and milk
3. Line plate with paste and build up a fluted rim
4. Strain in the mixture and sprinkle with nutmeg
5. Bake in quick oven to set rim, then decrease heat

title: Custard Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cream or rich milk
- 2 large spoonfuls of fine flour
- bitter almonds broken up
- 8 yolks and 4 whites of eggs
- 1 glass of brandy or white wine
- 1 teaspoonful of powdered nutmeg
- 1/2 cupful of sugar

## Directions

1. Take five tablespoonfuls out of a quart of cream or rich milk, and mix them with two large spoonfuls of fine flour.
2. Set the rest of the milk to boil, flavoring it with bitter almonds broken up.
3. When it has boiled hard, take it off, strain it, and stir it in the cold milk and flour.
4. Set it away to cool, and beat well eight yolks and four whites of eggs; add them to the milk, and stir in, at the last, a glass of brandy or white wine, a teaspoonful of powdered nutmeg, and half a cupful of sugar.
5. Butter a large bowl or mould; pour in the mixture; tie a cloth tightly over it; put it into a pot of boiling water, and boil it two hours, replenishing the pot with hot water from a tea-kettle.
6. When the pudding is done, let it get cool before you turn it out.
7. Eat it with butter and sugar stirred together to a cream and flavored with lemon.

title: Custard Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- ¾ cup milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- pinch salt
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Heat the milk in a double boiler
2. Beat the egg just enough to mix it thoroughly
3. Stir in the sugar and the salt
4. Stir the hot milk into the egg
5. Return to the double boiler
6. Stir constantly until it thickens and coats the spoon
7. Turn immediately into the bowl
8. Add the flavoring and stir a moment or two

title: Custard Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 scant tablespoonfuls butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 1 cup milk
- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar

## Directions

1. Rub butter to a cream
2. Add flour
3. Pour on gradually one cup of hot milk
4. Cook eight minutes in double boiler, stirring often
5. Separate yolks and whites of eggs
6. Put whites on ice
7. Beat yolks
8. Add sugar
9. Add to the milk
10. Set away to cool
11. Half an hour before serving beat the whites stiff
12. Cut them in lightly
13. Bake in buttered pudding dish in moderate oven thirty minutes
14. Serve at once with creamy sauce

title: Cutlets of Cod or Salmon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of fish cut in slices three-quarters of an inch thick
- a handful of fine breadcrumbs
- pepper and salt
- a little mixed parsley
- one egg, beaten light
- Enough butter, lard, or dripping to fry the cutlets

## Directions

1. Cut each slice of fish into strips, as wide as your two fingers
2. Dry them with a clean cloth
3. Rub lightly with salt and pepper
4. Dip in the egg, then the breadcrumbs
5. Fry in enough fat to cover them well
6. Drain away every drop of fat, and lay upon hot white paper in a heated dish

title: Dahl Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half pint of dahl, washed
- Three pints of cold water
- Two small heads of celery or one teaspoonful of celery seed
- One blade mace
- One-half dozen peppercorns
- One onion
- One ounce butter
- One ounce flour
- Pepper
- Salt
- Toasted dice-bread

## Directions

1. Wash dahl and let it stand two hours in three pints of cold water
2. Then add two small heads of celery, or one teaspoonful of celery seed tied in a muslin bag, one blade mace, one-half dozen peppercorns, one onion
3. Add one ounce butter and one of flour mixed together; season with pepper and salt
4. Serve with toasted dice-bread

title: Daisy Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup cornstarch
- 1 cup separator sweet cream
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Damson Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pounds Damsons
- lump sugar

## Directions

1. Place Damsons in oven
2. When soft take out stones, crack them and blanch the kernels
3. Add lump sugar
4. Boil 3 hours
5. Wet the moulds before using

title: Dandelion Wine
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Four quarts of dandelion flowers
- One gallon of water
- Six lemons
- Four pounds of white sugar
- Half royal yeast-cake

## Directions

1. Cover dandelion flowers with one gallon of water and boil
2. Strain, and when luke-warm add six lemons, four pounds of white sugar and half royal yeast-cake
3. Let it stand about ten days, or until done working
4. Then strain, bottle and seal

title: Dandelion Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- dandelion flowers (1 gallon)
- boiling water (2 gallons)
- lemons (4)
- sugar (6 pounds)
- White Swan Yeast Cake (1)
- toast

## Directions

1. To 1 gallon of dandelion flowers add 2 gallons of boiling water
2. Let stand 24 hours, then strain and see that there is 2 gallons of liquid
3. Add 4 lemons, sliced, and 6 pounds sugar
4. Soak 1 White Swan Yeast Cake and spread on a piece of toast and float on liquid, yeast side down
5. Let it stand 3 weeks, only stirring till the sugar is all off the bottom of the crock
6. Bottle

title: Dandelion Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- water (3 quarts)
- dandelion flowers (4 quarts)
- sugar (4 pounds)
- lemons (3)
- White Swan Yeast Cake (1)
- toast

## Directions

1. Boil water and dandelion flowers until soft
2. Strain and add 4 pounds sugar and 3 lemons sliced
3. Put it on again and boil 15 minutes
4. Set to cool and when about lukewarm set on top of it a slice of toast and spread 1 White Swan Yeast Cake on it
5. Let it stand 48 hours in a warm place
6. Strain again and bottle, but do not cork down
7. Let it stand 4 or 5 weeks, then cork and put away

title: Dark and Light Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 3 1/2 cups flour
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- whites of 4 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 cup molasses
- cloves
- cinnamon
- spices
- 1/2 cup raisins
- 1/2 cup flour
- 3 tablespoons sweet milk

## Directions

1. Take 1/2 this mixture, add to it 1/2 cup molasses, cloves, cinnamon and spices to taste
2. Add 1/2 cup raisins
3. Add 1/2 cup flour and 3 tablespoons sweet milk

title: Dark Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup sour milk
- 2 tablespoons syrup
- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon mixed spice
- a pinch of salt
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Dark Jelly Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter the size of an egg
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 2 well-beaten eggs
- 1/2 cup blackstrap
- 1 teaspoon mixed spice
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 cup buttermilk
- 1 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Cream butter with brown sugar
2. Add well-beaten eggs
3. Add blackstrap, mixed spice, soda, and buttermilk
4. Sift in flour
5. Bake in layers

title: Dark Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup blackstrap
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 cup raisins stoned
- flour

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in milk
2. Bake about 15 minutes in a moderate oven

title: Dark Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks of 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup molasses
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 3/4 cups sifted flour
- a pinch of salt
- 2 tablespoons cocoa or chocolate

## Directions

1. Bake in layers and put together with white filling and frost with chocolate

title: Dark Layer Cake Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup molasses
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1 large tablespoon shortening
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- salt
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in boiling water

title: Date Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rolled oats
- 2.5 cups flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 0.25 cup lard
- 0.75 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- 1 lb. dates
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup hot water

## Directions

1. Roll paste out quite thin
2. Spread date filling between two layers
3. Cook together
4. Let cool

title: Date Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ⅜ cup butter
- ⅜ cup currants
- ⅜ cup sour milk
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3 tablespoons molasses

## Directions

1. Make a filling of dates

title: Date Cake 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cup brown sugar
- butter size of an egg
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- pinch salt
- spice to taste
- 2½ cups flour
- 1 pound dates

## Directions

1. Bake in slow oven

title: Date Cake 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 1½ cups white sugar
- 1 pound dates (chopped)
- ¾ pound walnuts chopped fairly fine
- 3 cups flour
- 1 small dessertspoon soda dissolved in a little warm water

## Directions

1. Drop on a buttered pan in pieces about the size of a walnut

title: Date Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups Graham flour
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter and lard
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- enough sour milk to roll out thin
- 2 pounds chopped dates

## Directions

1. Mix flour, sugar, butter and lard with soda and enough sour milk to roll out thin
2. Spread over 2 pounds chopped dates
3. Put 1 layer on top of dates
4. Bake

title: Date Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups Graham flour
- 2 1/2 cups white flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup lard
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 pound dates
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup hot water

## Directions

1. Mix Graham flour, white flour, brown sugar, lard, milk and baking powder
2. Roll and bake
3. Cook 1 pound dates, 1 cup brown sugar and 1 cup hot water well
4. Put the date mixture between cookies

title: Date Cookies 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup soft butter
- 2 cups flour
- 4 tablespoons sweet milk
- 2 level teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 1/2 pounds dates
- 1 1/2 cups water
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1 1/2 teaspoons vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add eggs and other ingredients
3. Roll thin and bake
4. For filling: boil dates, water, sugar and vanilla for 7 minutes
5. Cool filling and spread between cakes

title: Date Cookies 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rolled oatmeal
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup cold water
- 1 teaspoon soda
- dates

## Directions

1. Mix rolled oatmeal, flour, brown sugar, butter, cold water and soda
2. Spread dates between layers
3. Bake like cookies

title: Date Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- a little water
- 1 cup dates, chopped fine
- white of 1 egg, well-beaten

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and a little water together until brittle when dropped into cold water
2. Remove from stove and stir quickly into the well-beaten white of an egg
3. Add 1 cup dates chopped fine

title: Date Filling for Toronto Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup dates
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup water

## Directions

1. Cook dates, sugar, and water on stove in saucepan till thick
2. Spread between layers
3. When cool this cake will keep for a month

title: Date Filling for Trilbys
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound dates chopped
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 cup water

## Directions

1. Boil chopped dates with sugar and water

title: Date or Fig Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. dates or figs
- 1/4 lb. suet
- 1/4 teaspoonful soda
- 5 oz. brown sugar
- 1/4 lb. breadcrumbs
- a little salt and nutmeg
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients with well-beaten eggs.
2. Put into buttered mould, and boil two and a half hours.
3. Serve with brandy sauce.
4. The pudding is improved by soaking the dates beforehand in a small cupful of sherry or whiskey.

title: Date Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound dates
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 teaspoons cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Stone and boil 1/2 pound dates
2. Put through colander
3. Add 2 eggs well beaten, 1 cup milk, 2 teaspoons cornstarch, and 2 tablespoons sugar
4. Boil in double boiler till thick
5. Have a nice rich pie shell baked and put filling in
6. Before using, cover with nicely flavored whipped cream

title: Date Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound dates
- 1/2 pound chopped suet
- a little salt
- 1.5 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- cold water

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pound dates, 1/2 pound chopped suet, a little salt, 1.5 cups flour, and 2 teaspoons baking powder
2. Mix with cold water very stiff
3. Steam 2.5 hours

title: Date Pudding (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup tapioca
- 4 cups cold water
- 1 pound dates stoned and chopped
- 1/4 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 dessertspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Soak 1 cup tapioca in 4 cups cold water over night
2. Add 1 pound dates stoned and chopped, 1/4 cup brown sugar, 1 teaspoon salt
3. Steam 1 hour
4. After taking from stove add 1 dessertspoon vanilla
5. Serve with cream

title: Date Pudding (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound suet
- 6 ounces bread crumbs
- 1 cup flour
- 1/4 pound dates
- 2 ounces sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk

## Directions

1. Stone and chop dates
2. Mix bread crumbs, flour and sugar, add dates
3. Beat eggs well and add to milk
4. Mix all well for 10 minutes
5. Put into a mould and steam for 3 hours
6. Serve with sweet sauce

title: Date Rocks
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter and lard mixed
- 1½ cups brown sugar
- 1 pound chopped dates
- ¾ pound chopped walnuts
- 3 eggs
- 1 level teaspoon soda dissolved in 1 tablespoon water
- 1 tablespoon cassia
- ¾ grated nutmeg
- flour to make a stiff batter

## Directions

1. Drop on buttered tin in large teaspoons

title: Date Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rolled oats
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ⅜ cup butter
- 1 cup lard
- 1 teaspoon soda wet with milk

## Directions

1. roll thin
2. cut with an oblong cake cutter
3. then bake

title: Date Wafers Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound stoned dates
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup boiling water

## Directions

1. boil for a few minutes
2. let cool a little
3. when cakes are cooked, put between

title: Date Whip
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound dates
- water
- whites of 4 eggs
- 1/2 cup white sugar
- whipped cream for serving

## Directions

1. Stone dates and take ends off
2. Boil in water, watching not to burn
3. Stir until it gets smooth and let cool
4. Beat whites of 4 eggs well
5. Add 1/2 cup white sugar
6. Stir all into dates
7. Put in oven until light brown
8. Serve with whipped cream

title: Day Yeast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen large potatoes
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 3 1/2 cup salt
- 1/8 cup sugar
- a couple of quarts of cold water
- 4 yeast cakes

## Directions

1. Take 1 dozen large potatoes and boil till well done
2. Drain water and mash fine
3. Drain the potatoes on the following ingredients: 2 cups flour, 1 teaspoon ginger, 3 1/2 cup salt, 1/8 cup sugar
4. Drain the water on while hot and if too thick put a couple of quarts of cold water on to thin it down to the consistency of cream
5. When lukewarm, add 4 yeast cakes
6. Let rise for 24 hours
7. Set away in a cool cellar. This will last a month

title: Dayton Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 3 cups flour
- 5 eggs
- scant teaspoonful cream of tartar
- scant 1/2 teaspoonful soda
- 2/3 cup milk

## Directions

1. Combine ingredients and bake. This recipe was used before baking powder was known. A heaping teaspoonful of baking powder could be used instead.

title: Deacon Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 peck green tomatoes
- 10 onions
- 1 head cabbage
- 1 cup salt
- 4 green peppers
- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 1/2 ounce celery seed
- 1/2 ounce tumeric powder
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Let stand 1/2 hour and drain well
2. Add green peppers, brown sugar, celery seed, and tumeric powder
3. Boil one hour in vinegar enough to cover

title: Deanery Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound flour
- 1/4 pound bread crumbs
- 3/4 pound suet
- 2 ounces sugar
- 2 ounces sultanas
- 3 1/2 nutmeg
- a pinch of salt
- 2 tablespoons treacle
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- rind and juice 1 lemon
- or a few drops essence lemon

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Steam 2 hours

title: Delicate Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- ½ cup butter and lard mixed
- ½ cup cornstarch mixed in ½ cup sweet milk
- 1 cup sifted flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- whites of 3 eggs
- flavor with almond

## Directions

1. beat stiff and added last
2. bake in a loaf

title: Delicate Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 1 egg
- 1 cup raisins
- butter size of an egg
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour to make a stiff batter

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients to make a stiff batter
2. Steam 1 hour

title: Delicious Gingerbread
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 tablespoon cloves
- 1 tablespoon mixed spices
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 3/4 cup lard
- 1 dessertspoon soda
- hot water
- flour
- 1 cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Beat eggs, sugar, molasses, cloves, mixed spices, and ginger together
2. Add lard
3. Dissolve soda in hot water and add
4. Stir in flour until stiff as sponge cake
5. Add 1 cup boiling water last

title: Delicious Gingerbread No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- 1 large nutmeg
- 1 cup sour milk
- 4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 2 tablespoons hot water

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add well-beaten eggs
3. Add sour milk
4. Add molasses and spices
5. Add flour
6. Stir all together
7. Just before putting into pan, stir in soda dissolved in hot water

title: Delicious Parsnips
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- water
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel parsnips and wash
2. Cut into cubes
3. Boil in sufficient water to cover them until tender
4. Place in the oven a dish in which put a good-sized lump of butter
5. Let gradually melt
6. When parsnips are cooked, drain
7. Season with pepper and salt
8. Place in a vegetable dish
9. Pour over them the melted butter
10. Serve hot

title: Delmonico Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- white milk sauce
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Take boiled potatoes left over from dinner and cut in dice
2. Put in pudding dish
3. Pour over this a white milk sauce
4. Cover the top with a layer of grated cheese
5. Bake for 1/2 hour
6. Serve hot

title: Derbyshire Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint milk
- butter size of walnut
- one heaping tablespoonful flour
- one cup white sugar
- one lemon
- one cup blanched almonds
- four eggs, beaten separately

## Directions

1. Boil milk; keep a wineglass milk cold and mix with flour
2. Pour hot milk on this and stir thoroughly until thick
3. Let cool
4. Add grated rind of lemon, butter, and white sugar, blanched almonds cut in about eight pieces, the yolks of eggs well beaten, and whites of two eggs
5. Mix all these ingredients well and pour into a buttered pudding dish
6. Bake until a light gold color
7. Ice with two whites left, juice of a lemon and a little white sugar, well beaten
8. Return to oven until icing is stiff
9. Allow pudding to become quite cold

title: Devil's Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ¼ cup butter creamed
- ½ cup grated chocolate
- ½ cup sour milk
- ½ cup boiling water
- 2½ cups flour
- ½ teaspoon cream tartar
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda in 1 tablespoon boiling water

## Directions

1. sour milk last

title: Devil's Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ cup butter
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 2 eggs
- ½ cake chocolate grated and stirred in ½ cup boiling water
- ½ cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon baking soda settled in 1 tablespoon hot water
- ½ teaspoon cream tartar
- 2½ cups flour

## Directions

1. cream butter with sugar
2. mix with sour milk
3. sift cream tartar with flour

title: Devil's Cake 2 Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups white sugar
- ½ cup butter
- ½ cup milk
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Let boil about 1 minute
2. when cool add vanilla and beat until cold

title: Devil's Food Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- ½ cup sour milk
- 2 eggs
- ½ cake chocolate dissolved in 1 cup boiling water
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Bake in layers

title: Devil's Food Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chocolate
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ½ cup sweet milk
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ⅜ cup butter
- 3 eggs 1 at a time
- ½ cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon soda in a little warm water
- 2½ cups flour

## Directions

1. boil and cool
2. Add first part to second part, stirring thoroughly

title: Devil's Food Cake Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ cup cream or fresh milk
- ½ cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 tablespoons chocolate

## Directions

1. cook until done
2. then stir until it cools
3. spread on cake

title: Deviled Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- lean boiled ham
- mustard
- flour
- boiling water
- egg
- sweet milk or cream
- sugar
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop lean boiled ham very fine
2. To each pint of ham add 1 teaspoon mustard, 1 tablespoon flour, 3/4 cup boiling water
3. Press into moulds to slice or for sandwiches
4. Beat 1 egg well with 1/2 cup sweet milk or cream, 1 tablespoon mustard, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 teaspoon salt
5. Cook all together till thick
6. When cold add 3/4 cup strong vinegar

title: Deviled Kidneys
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Kidneys
- One quart of water or stock
- One clove
- Four teaspoonfuls of onion-juice
- Salt and pepper
- One tablespoonful of butter
- One tablespoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys in thin round slices
2. Cover them with cold water and let stand half an hour
3. Wash them clean and put in stew-pan with one quart of water or stock, a clove, four teaspoonfuls of onion-juice, salt and pepper
4. Simmer two hours or longer if not tender
5. Set away, and for breakfast put one tablespoonful of butter in frying-pan
6. When hot add one tablespoonful of flour
7. Stir till brown and smooth

title: Deviled Kidneys
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Kidneys
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 even teaspoonful of mustard
- A pinch of paprica or cayenne
- Salt
- 1 teaspoonful of lemon juice
- Cracker-dust

## Directions

1. Slice and take out hard centres and fat
2. Have ready, beaten to a cream, a tablespoonful of butter, an even teaspoonful of mustard, a pinch of paprica or cayenne, a little salt, and a teaspoonful of lemon juice
3. Melt, without really heating the mixture
4. Coat each slice with it
5. Roll in cracker-dust
6. Broil, turning often
7. They should be done in eight minutes
8. Put a few drops of the deviled sauce upon each and send to table

title: Deviled Oysters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 oysters
- 1 gill oyster-liquor
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1/4 teaspoonful curry powder
- 1 teaspoonful Worcestershire sauce
- 10 drops tabasco sauce
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in the blazer, stir in the flour, and when this is blended, the oyster-liquor and all the seasoning except the lemon-juice
2. As soon as the sauce is boiling hot, drop in the oysters and cook three minutes or until they plump
3. Add the lemon-juice and serve them at once on Graham toast

title: Deviled Sardines
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box boneless sardines
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 tablespoonful lemon juice
- 1 teaspoonful paprica
- 1/2 saltspoonful cayenne
- 1 saltspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in the blazer and when hissing hot lay in the sardines
2. Cook until heated through, turning once, sprinkle with salt and paprica, add the lemon-juice, and serve on toast

title: Deviled Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. firm tomatoes
- 2 raw eggs, beaten light
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls melted butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful made mustard
- 1 teaspoonful powdered sugar
- 1 saltspoonful salt
- Pinch of cayenne

## Directions

1. Pound the boiled yolks; rub in the butter and seasoning.
2. Beat light, add the vinegar, and heat almost to a boil.
3. Stir in the beaten egg until the mixture begins to thicken.
4. Set in hot water while you cut the tomatoes in slices nearly half an inch thick.
5. Broil over a clear fire upon a wire oyster-broiler.
6. Lay on a hot-water dish, and pour the hot sauce over them.

title: Devilled Crabs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups crab meat (8-10 crabs)
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon paprika
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon mixed parsley
- 1/2 cup chopped mushrooms
- 2 yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Make white sauce, add seasoning, meat and yolk of egg
2. Cook to thicken
3. Cool slightly, put into shells
4. Cover with buttered crumbs and brown in oven

title: Devilled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs hard boiled
- 3/4 cup finely chopped ham or chicken
- 1 or 2 tablespoons butter
- 3/4 teaspoon mustard
- cayenne
- 3/4 teaspoon salt (omit with ham)
- 1 teaspoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the eggs in half transversely, remove the yolk.
2. Press yolk through strainer, add ham and seasonings, moistening the mustard in vinegar.
3. Mould into balls, fit into whites and serve on a thick bed parsley or lettuce.

title: Devilled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled eggs in halves.
2. Remove yolks and mix to a soft paste with mayonnaise dressing.
3. Replace in whites and serve on lettuce leaves.

title: Devilled Salmon
body:
## Ingredients

- canned or cold boiled salmon
- Yolks of 3 eggs boiled hard
- 1 pound salad oil or melted butter
- 2 teaspoons sugar
- 2 teaspoons mustard
- salt and cayenne to taste
- vinegar
- lettuce leaves
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Arrange fish in neat flakes
2. Rub egg yolks with salad oil or melted butter to a smooth paste
3. Add sugar and mustard, salt and cayenne to taste
4. Add a little vinegar
5. Arrange lettuce leaves around the plate
6. Pour the dressing over the fish
7. Garnish with the whites of eggs cut in rings
8. Note: This is nice prepared with shredded (not chopped) cabbage

title: Dewey Icing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup icing sugar
- Butter the size of a walnut
- White of an egg
- One teaspoonful of vanilla
- One teaspoonful of lemon

## Directions

1. Mix butter and sugar, and add the egg; then the other ingredients, and spread on the cake when cold.

title: Divinity Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups white sugar
- 1/4 cup corn syrup
- 1/4 cup cold water
- the beaten whites of 2 eggs
- vanilla
- nut meats

## Directions

1. Put sugar, corn syrup, and cold water into a saucepan and boil until it will harden in water
2. Have ready the beaten whites of 2 eggs
3. When the syrup has boiled sufficiently beat it into the whites of the eggs and add vanilla
4. Add nut meats just before turning out in the pan
5. This candy is better when a couple of days old than when first made

title: Dolly Varden Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- ⅜ cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3 eggs
- ⅜ teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 3 cups flour
- flavor with lemon
- 1 tablespoon molasses
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- ½ cup currants
- piece citron chopped fine
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- cloves and nutmeg

## Directions

1. Bake ⅔ of this in two pans
2. to the remainder add molasses, raisins, currants, citron, and spices
3. Bake in two pans and put in sheets, alternately, with a little jelly or white of an egg beaten to a froth
