title: Dominion Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 ounces butter
- 1 pound 2 ounces white sugar
- 1 pound 6 ounces flour
- 9 eggs
- 2 pounds seeded raisins

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and bake

title: Doughnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 pinch salt
- 3 tablespoons melted butter
- flour to make a soft dough
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Beat eggs and sugar until very light
2. Add sour milk, soda and salt
3. Add melted butter
4. Add enough flour to make a soft dough
5. Roll out thin
6. Cut with doughnut cutter
7. Fry in hot lard

title: Doughnuts (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out and cut with doughnut cutter
3. Fry in hot lard

title: Doughnuts (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 well-beaten eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- a pinch salt
- 1 nutmeg
- flour to roll out

## Directions

1. Beat eggs well
2. Mix in sugar, sour cream, soda, salt and nutmeg
3. Add enough flour to roll out
4. Cut with doughnut cutter
5. Fry in hot lard

title: Doughnuts (4)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups sweet milk or water
- 3 tablespoons melted lard
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- a little salt and nutmeg
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out and cut with doughnut cutter
3. Fry in hot lard

title: Doughnuts Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 1/2 cups sugar
- 4 cups sour buttermilk
- 6 teaspoons soda
- 4 tablespoons or more of lard
- flour enough to make a soft dough to roll out

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out with doughnut cutter
3. Fry in hot lard

title: Dover Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 pound flour
- 3/4 pound butter
- 5 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- flavoring to taste

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake 1 hour slowly

title: Dover Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup suet or butter
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- lemon peel to suit taste

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Steam 3 hours

title: Drawn Butter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 teaspoons of butter
- 2 teaspoons flour
- boiling water
- 1 chopped hard-boiled egg

## Directions

1. Let butter get soft
2. Mix with flour
3. Pour on boiling water until it is the right consistency
4. Do not boil it
5. Add a chopped hard-boiled egg

title: Dressed Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small teacup vinegar
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- butter half the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Beat the egg
2. Put other ingredients over the fire
3. Put in the egg
4. Stir until it boils
5. Cool and pour over chopped or shaved cabbage

title: Dressing For Fried Fish, Oysters Or Cutlets
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Soda biscuits
- seasoning
- eggs
- pepper and salt for fish and oysters
- thyme, sweet marjoram and summer savory for cutlets
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Roll biscuits (if a bottle is used for this it will be found to roll the biscuits as fine as flour)
2. Add seasoning, pepper and salt for fish and oysters; for cutlets, thyme, sweet marjoram and summer savory
3. Beat eggs
4. Dip oysters or any fry in them
5. Roll in seasoned cracker crumbs
6. Fry in butter or lard

title: Dressing for Fruit Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup water
- Yolks of 3 eggs
- Juice 2 lemons
- wine

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water five minutes
2. Pour on to the beaten yolks of eggs
3. Return to the fire and cook over hot water, stirring constantly until thickened
4. Cool and add juice of two lemons
5. One-half cup wine may be used in place of the lemon-juice, retaining one tablespoonful of lemon-juice
6. Pour over the fruit salad and serve after being thoroughly chilled

title: Dressing for Oyster Cocktail
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of four lemons
- two tablespoonfuls onion juice
- six tablespoonfuls tomato catsup
- four tablespoonfuls grated horseradish
- ten drops of tobasco
- one small teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together for use as dressing for oyster cocktail

title: Dressing for Oyster Cocktail
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of four lemons
- two tablespoonfuls onion juice
- six tablespoonfuls tomato catsup
- four tablespoonfuls grated horseradish
- ten drops of tobasco
- one small teaspoonful salt
- sufficient vinegar to make a thin sauce

## Directions

1. Mix juice of four lemons, onion juice, tomato catsup, horseradish, tobasco, and salt
2. Add sufficient vinegar to make a thin sauce

title: Dressing or Stuffing for Fowls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- brown crust from slices or pieces of stale bread
- tepid water
- pepper
- salt
- powdered summer savory
- sage
- melted butter
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Cut the brown crust from slices or pieces of stale bread until you have as much as the inside of a pound loaf.
2. Put it into a suitable dish, and pour tepid water over it.
3. Let it stand one minute, as it soaks very quickly.
4. Take up a handful at a time and squeeze it hard and dry with both hands, placing it in another dish.
5. Toss it all up lightly through your fingers.
6. Add pepper, salt (about a teaspoonful), also a teaspoonful of powdered summer savory, the same amount of sage or the green herb minced fine.
7. Add a little melted butter, and a beaten egg.
8. Work thoroughly all together, and it is ready for dressing either fowls, fish or meats.

title: Dried Apple Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups dried apples
- 2 cups molasses
- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 4 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 good spoon soda
- 1 cup sour milk
- spice of all kinds

## Directions

1. Soak dried apples over night in water
2. Chop fine and simmer in 2 cups of molasses
3. When nearly cold, add 1 cup sugar, 3/4 cup butter, 4 cups flour, 2 eggs, 1 good spoon soda, 1 cup sour milk, and spice of all kinds

title: Drop Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 4 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful whisky
- Flour enough to make stiff batter
- caraway seeds

## Directions

1. Beat butter to a cream, then add yolks well beaten, then part of the flour
2. whites beaten stiff
3. whisky
4. rest of flour
5. Bake in a quick oven, on a buttered paper

title: Drop Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 1 1/4 cups sugar
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- butter rubbed in to make like pie-crust
- currants
- chopped lemon peel
- a little milk

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, flour and baking powder together
2. Rub in butter to make like pie-crust
3. Add currants and chopped lemon peel
4. Mix to a very stiff batter with a little milk
5. Drop by spoonfuls on greased paper and bake

title: Drop Cakes (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 cup chopped walnuts
- 1 pound raisins or dates
- a little salt
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Drop in teaspoonfuls on greased paper
3. Bake

title: Drop Cakes (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 cups rolled oats
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1/2 cup granulated coconut
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/8 teaspoon soda
- 1 egg
- enough milk to wet nicely

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Drop off spoon in greased bake pan
3. Bake in moderate oven

title: Drop Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup currants or walnuts
- salt and flavor to taste

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Drop by spoonful on pan and bake

title: Drop Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- whites 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 a nutmeg
- flour enough to stir very stiff
- currants

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Drop in spoonfuls on a buttered tin
3. Sprinkle the top with currants
4. Bake quickly

title: Drop Cookies 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup cold water
- 3/4 cup lard
- 1 heaping teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon, cloves, allspice

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Drop in buttered pan
3. Bake

title: Drop Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup chopped English walnuts
- 1 cup dates or raisins
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all together and drop on a greased pan

title: Drop Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup each chopped English walnuts and dates or raisins
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all together
2. Drop on a greased pan

title: Drop Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 2 cups milk
- 1 tablespoon baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Mix together till quite stiff and foamy
2. Drop in hot greased muffin rings and cook quickly

title: Duchess Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint white stock
- One pint brown stock
- Salt and cayenne
- Four teaspoonfuls rice-flour
- Yolks of two eggs
- Half a cup of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Strain stock and pour over well beaten yolks of two eggs
2. Soup must not be allowed to boil after this
3. Before serving add half a cup of whipped cream

title: Duchess Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart white stock
- 2 slices carrot and onion
- 2 blades of mace
- 1 cup grated cheese
- 2 cups milk
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 cup flour
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Cook some of vegetables in some of butter 3 minutes
2. Add stock and mace
3. Simmer 15 minutes, strain into white sauce made of rest of butter, flour and milk
4. Season, add cheese

title: Ducks
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- dressing as for turkey
- slices of onion
- dripping from pan
- chopped giblets
- brown flour
- water
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. When roasted, use dressing as for turkey, with the addition of a few slices of onion
2. Many cooks lay over the game slice of onions, which take away the fishy flavor, removing the onion before serving
3. Make a sauce with the dripping in the pan in which the game is roasted, and to which are put the chopped giblets, which are previously well cooked
4. Thicken the gravy with brown flour moistened with water
5. Serve with currant jelly

title: Dumpling Dinner
body:
## Ingredients

- broth from soup bone
- a dozen or more potatoes
- 1 pint water or milk
- a pinch of salt
- 1 egg
- 1 heaping teaspoon baking powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Set about half the broth away in a cool place
2. Pare potatoes and drop in cold water, then in the boiling broth
3. Mix water or milk, salt, beaten egg, and baking powder
4. Sift in flour to make a very stiff batter
5. Drop from the spoon in round lumps into boiling broth
6. Let boil about fifteen minutes
7. Serve with some of the broth

title: Dumplings for Boiled Duck or Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 scant teaspoon soda
- 2 scant teaspoons cream tartar
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- flour for dusting

## Directions

1. Sift flour, soda, cream tartar, and salt twice
2. Add 3/4 cup sweet milk and mix light, handling as little as possible
3. Roll 1/4 inch thick and cut into small squares
4. Sift a little flour over them
5. When chicken is nearly cooked, lay dumplings on top
6. Cover tightly and let boil for fifteen minutes without moving the cover

title: Dumplings for Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix and sift flour, baking powder, and salt
2. Mix to a soft dough with milk
3. Turn on board and roll out 1 inch thick
4. Cut in small circles
5. Roll each in flour
6. Drop in simmering stew
7. Cover and cook 20 minutes

title: Dundee Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 ounces butter
- 5 ounces white sugar
- 10 ounces flour
- 2 ounces almond nuts
- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 5 ounces sultanas
- 5 ounces currants

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add yolks of eggs not beaten
3. Add flour and milk alternately
4. Add fruit, baking powder and lastly whites of eggs beaten stiff
5. Bake 1 1/2 hours

title: Durham Cutlets
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups cold left-over beef
- 1 small onion
- 1 cup milk
- 0.5 cup gravy
- 0.5 tablespoon flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Grind beef fine with onion
2. Heat milk and gravy
3. Thicken with flour
4. When cold, mix with the meat
5. Set away to cool in a mould
6. Cut in slices and fry

title: Dutch Apple Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup milk
- 4 sour apples
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Sift together the flour, baking powder and salt and rub in butter
2. beat egg, add to milk, stir into the above and put in a deep pie plate
3. core apples, quarter them and partly cube apples in the batter, sprinkle sugar on apples only
4. Bake 15 minutes in a hot oven and serve with lemon sauce

title: Dutch Apple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 egg
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 4 sour apples, cored and cut into eighths

## Directions

1. Mix flour, salt and baking powder
2. Rub in the butter
3. Beat the eggs and mix with the milk
4. Stir with the dry mixture
5. Turn the mixture into a shallow pan
6. Press the sharp apples into the dough slightly
7. Bake in a quick oven 20 or 30 minutes

title: Dutch Apple Pudding Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- milk
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Make milk to make thick as cream
2. Season with vanilla

title: Dutch Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 egg (if cream is very rich)

## Directions

1. Roll out and sprinkle with sugar

title: Dutch Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 large teaspoon baking powder
- butter size of an egg
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup milk
- 4 apples
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix 2 cups flour, 1 large teaspoon baking powder, and butter size of an egg well
2. Beat 1 egg light and mix with 3/4 cup milk, pour into dry mixture and stir
3. Spread 3 1/2 inch thick in baking pan
4. Peel 4 apples, cut and stick into dough and sprinkle with sugar
5. Bake
6. Serve with cream

title: Dutch Stew
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- A little onion
- laurel leaves
- 4 or 5 cloves
- 1 cupful of gravy or bouillon
- 1 beaten egg
- squares of cooked meat

## Directions

1. Fry together butter, vinegar, sugar, salt, onion, laurel leaves, and cloves.
2. Add a cupful of gravy or bouillon and a beaten egg.
3. When hot add squares of cooked meat.
4. A great improvement is a little red wine, about two tablespoons, in which case a little more sugar is needed.

title: Dye for White or Light Eyebrows
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Walnut bark, one ounce
- Water, one pint
- Alum, a lump the size of a filbert

## Directions

1. Boil an ounce of walnut bark in a pint of water for an hour
2. Add a lump of alum the size of a filbert
3. When cold apply with a camel's hair brush

title: Eagle Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- cloves and nutmeg
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3/4 teaspoon cream tartar
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and bake

title: East India Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ peck sliced green tomatoes
- ⅝ peck white onions
- 25 small cucumbers
- 2 heads cauliflower
- salt
- ½ cup grated horseradish
- ½ ounce tumeric
- ½ ounce ground cinnamon
- ½ ounce ground cloves
- 1 ounce pepper
- 1 pint ground mustard
- 1 pint brown sugar
- 2 bunches celery
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut up sliced green tomatoes, white onions, small cucumbers, and cauliflower
2. Sprinkle with salt
3. Let stand over night
4. Drain
5. Mix ½ cup grated horseradish, ½ ounce tumeric, ½ ounce ground cinnamon and cloves, 1 ounce pepper, 1 pint ground mustard, 1 pint brown sugar, 2 bunches chopped celery
6. Cover with vinegar
7. Boil slowly 2 hours

title: East Indian Ragout of Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cocoanut
- 2 cups fresh diced tomatoes
- 1 large green pepper, chopped
- A tiny pinch of chopped garlic
- 1 tablespoonful grated onion
- 1 teaspoonful curry
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1/2 teaspoonful soda

## Directions

1. Break the shell of a cocoanut, saving the milk if it be sweet.
2. Grate the meat when you have taken off the brown skin.
3. Heat the milk and pour over the grated cocoanut. (If the milk be not sweet use a cupful of boiling water, slightly sweetened with loaf-sugar.)
4. Set aside, covered, until perfectly cold, then strain through a muslin bag, squeezing out every drop of liquid.
5. Peel and cut tomatoes to make two cupfuls; add green pepper, chopped, chopped garlic, grated onion, and stew gently for twenty minutes.
6. Add the curry and draw to the side of the range, while you heat the cocoanut-milk and thin with it a roux of one tablespoonful of flour, stirred smooth into a larger spoonful of boiling butter.
7. Season with salt to taste, pour all together in a deep dish, stir in soda, and serve while frothing.

title: Easter Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 3 eggs
- 2 heaping teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon lemon flavoring

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar
2. Add the eggs and rest of ingredients
3. Roll thin and bake in a quick oven

title: Eastlawn Oyster Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- thin glutinous brown bread
- butter
- 1 cupful of oysters
- cayenne
- salt
- lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Butter the thin glutinous brown bread well
2. Beard and dry oysters on a napkin
3. Cut oysters fine with knife (do not chop)
4. Season with cayenne, a little salt and squeeze lemon-juice over
5. Spread sparingly on the brown bread
6. Set on the ice till wanted

title: Easy Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- piece of butter size of an egg
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 1 heaping teaspoonful baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat all together until light.
2. Add milk, flour and baking powder and beat again.
3. Bake in two layers.

title: Easy Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 eggs
- milk
- 4 tablespoons soft butter
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, flour and baking powder together
2. Break 2 eggs into a cup (not beaten) and fill up with milk
3. Put in eggs and milk to the dry ingredients
4. Last of all add soft butter and flavoring
5. Bake in loaf or layer

title: Easy Method of Canning Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts water
- 2 tablespoons salt
- tomatoes

## Directions

1. Put 2 quarts water and 2 tablespoons salt in kettle, let come to a boil
2. Peel tomatoes and put the whole tomatoes in liquid, cook 20 minutes
3. Skim out tomatoes and fill jars, seal, leaving liquid in kettle
4. The same liquid may be used for another kettle of tomatoes by adding a little more salt, but no more water

title: Eccles Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- very short paste
- currants
- brown sugar
- syrup

## Directions

1. Make a very short paste and roll lightly (always in one direction, never back and forth), and cut into squares about five inches.
2. Place on each square currants which have been heated in a syrup of brown sugar and fold over the sides so as to make a square cake with round opening.
3. Put a little syrup in each cake before baking in a very hot oven.

title: Economy Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 cup white sugar
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- a pinch of salt
- 1 cup milk
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Sift flour, sugar, baking powder and salt together four times
2. Scald milk and pour it while still hot on to the sifted ingredients
3. Stir thoroughly
4. Stir in the beaten whites of 2 eggs
5. Bake in a shallow, oblong tin and use an icing

title: Economy Cake (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 egg
- butter the size of an egg
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and bake

title: Eels Stewed à l'American
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds eels, skinned, cleaned, and fat removed from inside
- One young onion chopped
- Four tablespoonfuls of butter
- Pepper and salt
- Little chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the eels in pieces, about two inches in length
2. Season and lay in a saucepan containing the melted butter
3. Strew the onion and parsley over all
4. Cover the saucepan closely, and set in a pot of cold water
5. Bring this gradually to a boil, then cook very gently for an hour and a half, or until the eels are tender
6. Turn out into a deep dish

title: Egg and Bacon Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- short paste
- bacon
- 2 eggs
- milk

## Directions

1. Make a short paste and line pie plate
2. Cover with bacon cut into fine strips
3. Beat 2 eggs with a little milk, season to taste
4. Pour over bacon
5. Cover with crust and bake in moderate oven

title: Egg and Bacon Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- pastry
- cooked bacon or ham
- 1 or more eggs
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put a layer of pastry in a soup plate
2. Put small pieces of cooked bacon or ham in
3. Beat 1 or more eggs, season with pepper
4. Pour over the bacon
5. Cover with pastry and bake

title: Egg and Vinegar Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg beaten
- 1/4 cup vinegar
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- a piece of butter
- salt and pepper
- 1 egg boiled hard to garnish

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients and serve with cabbage

title: Egg Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- just enough flour to roll

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup butter, 1 cup sugar, 3 eggs together
2. Sift 2 teaspoons baking powder and 1 teaspoon cream tartar with just enough flour to roll
3. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Egg Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup butter, 1 cup sugar and 3 eggs together
2. Sift 2 teaspoons baking powder with other ingredients

title: Egg Gruel
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- yolk of 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 1 teacupful boiling water
- white of 1 egg
- seasoning or spice as desired

## Directions

1. Beat the yolk of an egg with one tablespoonful of sugar
2. Pour one teacupful of boiling water on it
3. Add the white of an egg, beaten to a froth, with any seasoning or spice desired
4. Take warm

title: Egg Gruel
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- yolk of an egg
- one tablespoonful of sugar
- one teacupful of boiling water
- white of an egg

## Directions

1. Beat the yolk of an egg with one tablespoonful of sugar
2. Pour one teacupful of boiling water on it
3. Add the white of an egg, beaten to a froth, with any seasoning or spice desired

title: Egg Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1/2 pint milk
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat whites and yolks separately.
2. Add the whites beaten to a stiff froth last.
3. Cook in a little butter.

title: Egg Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons milk
- salt and pepper
- tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Separate whites and yolks.
2. Beat whites to very stiff froth.
3. Beat yolks and 4 tablespoons milk with a sprinkle of salt and pepper.
4. Have ready a frying-pan with tablespoon butter in it.
5. When hot, stir both mixtures together quickly, turn into pan.
6. When a nice brown on bottom, turn 1/2 over on the other.
7. Let cook a minute longer, serve at once.

title: Egg Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon milk to every egg used
- 1 extra tablespoon
- 5 eggs (for 3 people)
- 6 tablespoons milk (for 3 people)
- salt and pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat eggs well, add milk, pinch salt and pepper.
2. Have pan hot, well greased with butter, and pour mixture in.
3. After it begins to set, put in hot oven for few minutes.
4. Loosen with knife and turn half over.
5. Serve to be eaten immediately.

title: Egg Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup cracker crumbs
- 3 eggs
- salt
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Soak the cracker crumbs in the milk.
2. Beat 3 eggs to a froth.
3. Stir eggs into crackers and add a little salt.
4. Turn all into the hot frying-pan that contains a little melted butter.
5. When the omelet is browned on one side turn and brown the other.

title: Egg or Lettuce Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 raw egg
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- ½ cup vinegar
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 hard-boiled egg chopped fine
- lettuce also chopped fine

## Directions

1. boil until thick like honey
2. remove from stove and add chopped egg and lettuce

title: Egg Rolls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 small cup milk
- 1 tablespoonful lard or lard and butter
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- enough flour to make stiff dough

## Directions

1. Roll out, cut desired size, bake in a hot oven.
2. If liked, add two tablespoonfuls of white sugar.

title: Egg Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- dressing
- celery salt
- chopped mace
- parsley
- lettuce leaves
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut hard-boiled eggs in half
2. Take out yolks and mash
3. Add dressing and moisten
4. Season with celery salt, chopped mace, or parsley
5. Stuff whites with this
6. Arrange in lettuce leaves and serve with mayonnaise

title: Egg Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- very fresh eggs
- bread and butter
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Hard boil some very fresh eggs
2. When cold, cut them into moderately thin slices
3. Lay them between some bread and butter cut as thin as possible
4. Season them with pepper, salt and nutmeg

title: Egg Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 slice bread
- hot water
- salt
- butter
- 1 egg
- salt

## Directions

1. Brown a slice of bread nicely over the coals
2. Dip it in hot water slightly salted
3. Butter it
4. Lay on the top an egg that has been broken into boiling water, and cooked until the white has hardened
5. Season the egg with a bit of butter and a crumb of salt

title: Eggless Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 5 tablespoons lard or butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 tablespoon boiling water
- flavoring to taste

## Directions

1. Sift baking powder in flour
2. Mix in all ingredients
3. Bake

title: Eggless Cake (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 cup raisins
- spice to taste

## Directions

1. Beat sugar and butter to a cream
2. Add milk
3. Add flour in which cream tartar and baking soda have been sifted
4. Add raisins and spice to taste
5. Bake

title: Eggless Cake (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- a piece of butter the size of a large egg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 3 cups flour (scant)
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Cream together sugar and butter
2. Add sweet milk
3. Add baking powder and flour
4. Use any kind of flavoring desired
5. Bake as a layer cake

title: Eggless Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup lard
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3/4 teaspoon soda
- 1 1/2 teaspoon salt
- flour to roll

## Directions

1. Bake in a quick oven

title: Eggless Gingerbread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup sour cream
- 3 cups flour
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 heaping teaspoon soda
- hot water

## Directions

1. Combine sugar, molasses, and sour cream
2. Add flour and butter
3. Add ginger and cinnamon
4. Dissolve soda in hot water and add last
5. Beat hard
6. Bake in a slow oven

title: Eggs in the Nest
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- pepper and salt
- butter
- 6 tablespoons nice cream

## Directions

1. Beat to a froth the whites of 6 eggs, a little pepper and salt.
2. Pour into a buttered baking tin.
3. Dip upon it 6 tablespoons nice cream, 1 only in a place.
4. Upon each spoonful of cream drop 1 of the yolks whole (being careful not to break them).
5. Place in moderately hot oven to cook, and serve hot as omelet.

title: Eggs on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- eggs
- bread
- butter
- flour
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Put as much milk in a saucepan as will just cover the number of eggs needed.
2. When on the point of boiling, drop in the eggs and let cook about 5 minutes.
3. Take out and put each egg on a slice of freshly made toast, lightly buttered.
4. Thicken the milk with a little flour, add pepper and salt.
5. Pour over toast and serve hot.

title: Eggs Stewed in Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint cream
- ten whole black peppers
- six eggs
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil one pint cream
2. Add ten whole black peppers
3. When boiling add six eggs
4. Let cook on top of stove five minutes
5. Bake in oven ten minutes
6. Add pinch of salt
7. Serve from same dish

title: Egyptian Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage outer green leaves
- 1 tablespoon chopped meat
- salted water
- cream sauce or English drawn butter

## Directions

1. Scald the outside green leaves, remove the midrbs
2. Put a tablespoon chopped meat in each and roll it up
3. Cook these in salted water and serve with a cream sauce or English drawn butter

title: Elliman's Embrocation
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One new-laid egg
- One gill turpentine
- One gill vinegar
- One-half ounce spirits of camphor

## Directions

1. Beat egg well
2. Add turpentine and vinegar by degrees, alternately putting in one-half ounce spirits of camphor
3. For rheumatism, lumbago, for sore throat, cold in chest, etc., rub in well with hand, night and morning
4. A flannel may also be soaked in embrocation and put on, covered with a cloth or flannel
5. Can be used also as a substitute for mustard plaster

title: Elliman's Embrocation
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two glasses turpentine
- One glass vinegar
- One teaspoon salad oil
- Two raw eggs

## Directions

1. Put all in a bottle and shake well for quarter of an hour, when it will be ready for use
2. Keep it well corked

title: English Barley Broth
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of strong stock made by boiling the bones of a rib-roast, or steak well broken, with a pound of underdone beef for six hours
- One pound of chopped lean beef and the cracked cooked bones
- Three pints of water
- One onion
- One carrot cut into dice
- One small turnip cut up small
- Half a cup of barley soaked for an hour
- Minced parsley
- Sweet marjoram
- Pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Parboil the vegetables, drain them and put into the soup-kettle with the barley and the cold stock
2. Bring to a slow boil and keep this up for an hour, before the parsley goes in

title: English Boiled Batter Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 2 eggs, well beaten
- 1 tablespoonful white sugar
- 1/2 pt. milk

## Directions

1. Mix well and pour into a mould well greased with dripping
2. Boil for one hour
3. Serve with wine sauce
4. The mould should be quite full to prevent water from entering

title: English Breakfast Muffins
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 level teaspoonfuls butter
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 3 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 pt milk
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder

## Directions

1. Soften the butter, add to it the yolks of two eggs and beat
2. Add milk and mix
3. Add flour, salt and baking powder and beat well
4. Stir in well-beaten whites
5. Bake in quick oven twenty-five minutes in well-greased rings

title: English Jugged Pigeon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon
- crumbs
- chopped fat pork
- hard-boiled eggs
- celery
- onion-juice
- pepper
- salt
- boiling water
- browned flour
- tomato catsup

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and stuff with a good force-meat of crumbs, chopped fat pork, the yolks of two hard-boiled eggs rubbed to powder, and a tablespoonful of celery boiled tender and chopped
2. Season to taste with onion-juice, pepper, and salt
3. Truss the birds; tie wings and legs close to the bodies and pack in an agate-iron pail with a close top
4. Plunge this into boiling water deep enough to cover the pail almost to the top, but not to float it
5. Put a weight on the top to keep the pail from turning over as the boiling becomes hard
6. Cook for three hours if the pigeons are tough
7. Dish the birds, thicken the gravy with browned flour
8. Add a tablespoonful of tomato catsup, boil up and pour over the pigeons

title: English Lemon Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice and rind of 3 lemons
- 3 eggs well beaten
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoon butter

## Directions

1. Beat together and boil 5 minutes
2. Keep in covered jelly glasses

title: English Lemon Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice and rind of 3 lemons
- 3 eggs
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoon butter

## Directions

1. Beat eggs well
2. Beat together juice and rind of 3 lemons, 3 eggs well beaten, 1 pound sugar, 1 cup water, 1 teaspoon butter
3. Boil 5 minutes
4. Keep in covered jelly glasses

title: English Monkey
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of stale breadcrumbs
- 1 cup of milk
- 1 heaping teaspoonful of butter
- 3/4 of a cup of cheese cut fine
- 1 beaten egg

## Directions

1. Soak breadcrumbs in milk fifteen minutes
2. Put butter into chafing-dish
3. Add cheese cut fine; stir until melted
4. Add crumbs with beaten egg

title: English Monkey
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup soft bread crumbs
- ¾ cup cheese

## Directions

1. Melt butter
2. Add cheese and stir until melted
3. Add crumbs soaked in the milk
4. Add egg lightly beaten
5. Cook until thickened considerable

title: English Plum Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 pound suet chopped fine
- 1 pound raisins
- 1 pound currants
- 2 3/4 pounds flour
- 1 ounce candied lemon peel
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 nutmeg
- 1/4 teaspoon cloves
- 1/4 teaspoon allspice
- 1 tablespoon salt

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients

title: English Plum Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound raisins well chopped
- 1 pound currants
- 3/4 pound suet finely chopped
- pinch salt
- 3/4 pound butter
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup molasses
- 3/4 pound mixed peel (lemon, orange and citron) grated
- 1/4 small nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon each ginger, cinnamon, allspice
- a very little ground cloves
- 6 eggs well beaten
- 3/4 pound blanched almonds cut fine
- 1 pound bread crumbs
- 1 cup flour

## Directions

1. Be sure and flavor your fruit well before adding
2. Place in a bag, tie tightly, and boil for not less than 6 hours
3. Add a little salt to water in which it is boiled
4. Serve with sauce seasoned with grated lemon, and a little of the juice

title: English Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large green cucumbers
- 1 cauliflower
- 6 large onions
- 2 green peppers
- salt water
- 1 quart of vinegar
- 1.5 cups sugar
- 1 dessertspoon tumeric
- 1 dessertspoon salt
- 1 dessertspoon celery seed
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 tablespoon mustard

## Directions

1. Cut all vegetables fine and let stand in salt water 5 hours
2. Take out and drain and put in a kettle with vinegar and sugar
3. Cook briskly for 15 or 20 minutes
4. Add tumeric, salt, celery seed, pepper, flour, and mustard for dressing

title: English Scones
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup raisins
- 4 cups flour
- 1 cup lard
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix with sweet milk to pack all together and cut with three-cornered cake cutter

title: English Tart Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon butter

## Directions

1. Keep the white of 1 egg out for icing

title: English Walnut Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- bread sponge
- 1 cup walnut meats
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 1 tablespoon caraway seed
- flour

## Directions

1. Make sponge same as for white bread
2. To each loaf add 1 cup walnut meats broken into four or five pieces, 2 tablespoons sugar, 2 tablespoons melted butter, and 1 tablespoon caraway seed
3. Mix in flour same as for white bread
4. Turn out on board and knead until the dough does not stick to hands
5. Let rise
6. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Entree (Bavarian Style)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. beef collops
- 1/4 lb. sausage
- 1 small onion
- 1 egg
- salt and pepper
- cabbage leaves
- lard and butter

## Directions

1. Form into croquettes.
2. Have some half-boiled leaves of cabbage, wrap each croquette in one large leaf, completely covering the meat, tie firmly and fry slowly in lard and butter (half and half) until brown on both sides.
3. Serve on a flat platter with gravy poured over, which may be increased by addition of a little boiling water.

title: Entree (Bavarian Style)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. beef collops
- 1/2 lb. sausage
- 1 egg
- 1 small onion
- salt
- pepper
- cabbage leaves
- lard

## Directions

1. Form beef collops and sausage into croquettes.
2. Have some half-boiled leaves of cabbage, wrap each croquette in one large leaf, completely covering the meat.
3. Tie firmly and fry slowly in lard.

title: Escalloped Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onions
- soda crackers
- salt
- pepper
- milk
- fine biscuit crumbs

## Directions

1. Butter baking dish of desired size
2. Put in a layer of sliced onions
3. Add a layer of soda crackers broken in small pieces
4. Sprinkle lightly with salt and pepper and dots of butter
5. Continue this until dish is full
6. Cover with milk
7. Sprinkle with fine biscuit crumbs
8. Bake in a moderate oven 1 hour

title: Escalloped Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint oysters
- 4 tablespoons oyster liquor
- 2 tablespoons cream
- 1 beaten egg
- 1/2 cup stale crumbs
- 1/8 cup cracker crumbs
- 1/4 cup melted butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mix crumbs in butter
2. Arrange alternate layers in buttered baking dish of crumbs, oyster seasonings, wetting
3. Bake 35 minutes

title: Escalloped Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- cracker crumbs
- butter
- tomatoes
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Put into an earthen baking dish a layer of cracker crumbs and small bits of butter
2. Then a layer of tomatoes with a little sugar sprinkled over them
3. Add another layer of cracker crumbs, seasoned with butter and a layer of tomatoes until your dish is full, with cracker crumbs on top
4. Pour over a little water to moisten
5. Bake for half hour

title: Everton Toffee
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound treacle
- 1 pound moist sugar
- 1 pound butter

## Directions

1. Put butter first and rub well over bottom of saucepan
2. Add treacle and sugar, stirring gently with a knife
3. Boil over a clear fire in a saucepan large enough to allow of fast boiling
4. After it has boiled about 10 minutes, ascertain if it is done by dropping a little into cold water from point of knife
5. If sufficiently done it will be quite crisp when taken from the water
6. Butter a shallow tin, pan or dish and pour toffee from saucepan into dish to cool

title: Excellent Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1/2 pound butter
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 3 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 pound raisins

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and bake

title: Excellent Cough Mixture
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of gum
- one cup of honey
- one cup of lemon juice
- one ounce of glycerine

## Directions

1. Mix well, bottle, and take one teaspoonful when cough is troublesome

title: Excellent Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 cups white sugar
- 2 cups rich sour cream
- 2 cups blackstrap
- 1 pound butter
- 1 1/2 teaspoons soda
- 2 tablespoons cloves
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 6 nutmegs
- 3 pounds currants
- 3 pounds raisins
- 2 ounces lemon peel
- 2 ounces orange peel
- 2 ounces citron peel
- 1 cup walnuts or butternuts
- 1/4 cup chopped suet
- flour enough to make stiff dough

## Directions

1. Beat thoroughly
2. Bake 3 hours in slow oven

title: Excellent Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 cups white sugar
- 2 cups rich sour cream
- 2 cups blackstrap
- 1 pound butter
- 1 1/2 teaspoons soda
- 2 tablespoons cloves
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 6 nutmegs
- 3 pounds currants
- 3 pounds raisins
- 2 ounces each lemon

## Directions

1. Beat thoroughly

title: Excellent Lunch Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One coffee-cup butter melted
- one cup sweet milk
- one and one-half cups of sugar
- three and one-half cups flour
- three eggs
- three teaspoons baking powder
- one small cup sugar
- one tablespoon cornstarch
- two cups cocoanut
- one teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Bake in a shallow meat tin
2. Beat whites of three eggs to a stiff froth
3. add slowly one small cup sugar and one tablespoon cornstarch
4. Place mixture in double boiler and cook fifteen minutes, stirring constantly
5. Then add two cups cocoanut and one teaspoon vanilla and drop on buttered tins in teaspoonfuls and bake a delicate brown

title: Extra Good Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter (2 cups)
- 1 pound sugar (2 cups)
- 12 yolks eggs
- 2 cups molasses
- 1 pound flour (4 cups)
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 2 teaspoons mace
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- whites of 12 eggs
- 2 pounds seeded raisins
- 2 pounds sliced citron
- 2 1/2 pounds currants
- 3/4 pound candied peel chopped
- 1/4 pound chopped blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Bake in 2 round sponge-cake pans, with tube, about 2 hours and 40 minutes
2. Each loaf weighs 5 pounds

title: Fadge
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup wheat flour
- 1 cup cold water

## Directions

1. mix and beat until light
2. pour into hot buttered gem pans
3. bake 30 or 35 minutes

title: Fairy Creams
body:
## Ingredients

- good pie paste
- red raspberries (fresh or preserved)
- sugar
- flour
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Line patty pans with good pie paste
2. Fill half full with red raspberries
3. Sprinkle on each a pinch of sugar and flour
4. Bake and cool
5. Before serving heap them full of whipped cream

title: Fancy Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sour milk
- 4 cups flour
- 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- 2 yolks or 2 whites beaten

## Directions

1. Set to rise 2 cups sour milk, 4 cups flour, 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes
2. Add 4 tablespoons butter, 4 tablespoons sugar, 3/4 teaspoon salt, creamed together
3. Add 2 yolks or 2 whites beaten
4. Let rise
5. Add flour to foam

title: Farmer's Daughter's Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- cinnamon to taste

## Directions

1. Mix soft
2. Roll thin
3. Bake

title: Farmer's Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups dried apples
- 2 cups molasses
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 4 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- spices
- 1/2 pound figs or dates chopped fine
- 3/4 cup water
- 1/4 cup white sugar

## Directions

1. Cook filling until it thickens

title: Farmer's Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 pint flour
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Favorite Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart raw cabbage
- 1 quart boiled beets
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon white or black pepper
- ½ teaspoon red pepper
- cold vinegar
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Chop cabbage fine
2. Chop beets fine
3. Add 2 cups sugar, 1 tablespoon salt, 1 teaspoon white or black pepper, ½ teaspoon red pepper
4. Cover with cold vinegar and keep from the air
5. Slice horseradish and place on top of pickles
6. Keep covered and serve as cold pickle

title: Favorite Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 stewed apples
- 1 tablespoon butter
- beaten yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup cream
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Mash 6 stewed apples and while hot stir in a tablespoon butter
2. When cool add the beaten yolks of 4 eggs, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup cream and juice of one lemon
3. Line pie tins with good crust and fill with above mixture
4. Bake in a quick oven and spread the beaten whites of the eggs sweetened a little over top
5. Place in oven and brown lightly

title: Feather Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup flour
- 2 tablespoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake in 3 layers
3. Put together with icing made from 1 cup sugar, 1 cup water, 1 even tablespoon cornstarch, 2 yolks of eggs, juice and grated rinds

title: Feather Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup flour
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup water
- 1 even tablespoon cornstarch
- 2 yolks of eggs
- juice and grated rinds of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Bake in 3 layers
2. For icing: Cook sugar, water, cornstarch, yolks of eggs, juice and grated rinds of lemons all together until thickened
3. Cool and spread on layers of the cake

title: Feather Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3/4 cup cornstarch
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon essence lemon
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- whites of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the sugar and butter together
2. Add the whites of eggs and sweet milk
3. Add cornstarch and flour
4. Mix in the baking powder

title: Feather Cake 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup water
- 1 even tablespoon cornstarch
- yolks of 2 eggs
- juice and grated rind of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Bake in 3 layers
2. For icing: Cook sugar, water, cornstarch, yolks of eggs, juice and grated rind of lemons all together until it thickens
3. When cool spread on the layers of cake

title: Fig Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup shortening
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- a little salt
- 3/4 cup oatmeal mixed in flour
- figs or other fruit

## Directions

1. Make into dough
2. Take half the dough and roll out and put on bottom of pan
3. Spread with figs or other fruit
4. Roll remainder of dough and put on top
5. Cook until done

title: Fig Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound figs, finely chopped
- 1/4 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup boiling water
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mix in order given
2. Cook in double boiler until thick enough to spread

title: Fig Paste Filling
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. figs
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup cold water

## Directions

1. Put figs into a bowl and pour boiling water over them, letting stand till soft, then cutting into small pieces with scissors; first cut off all the small hard pieces, and then chop figs until they become a thick paste
2. add sugar and water, and cook till thick and clear

title: Fig Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of figs
- one pound breadcrumbs
- one cup melted butter
- half pound sugar
- five eggs
- one nutmeg
- orange and lemon peel

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients
2. Steam three hours

title: Fig Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chopped figs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 large cups grated breadcrumbs
- 1 large cup brown sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup chopped suet
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Pour over figs boiling milk, grated breadcrumbs, brown sugar, eggs, salt, and suet
2. Either steam or boil for four hours and serve with sauce

title: Fig Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 pound suet
- 1 pound figs cut fine
- 1 pound bread crumbs
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 nutmeg
- 3/4 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup flour
- sour milk enough to moisten

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Steam 3 hours

title: Fig Pudding (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound figs chopped fine
- 1/8 pound suet
- 1/2 pound brown sugar
- 1 pound bread crumbs
- 2 ounces candied lemon
- 2 eggs well beaten
- a little nutmeg
- 1 tablespoon molasses mixed in milk

## Directions

1. Put in a mould
2. Boil 3 hours

title: Fig Roll
body:
## Ingredients

- light suet pastry
- self-raising flour
- figs
- golden syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare a light suet pastry using self-raising flour
2. Slice and soak the figs, then drain from the water
3. Lay upon the rolled-out sheet of paste
4. Spread with golden syrup
5. Roll up carefully, fastening the ends
6. Tie loosely in cloth
7. Boil or steam for 2 hours

title: Fig Trifle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 figs
- 3 oz. shelled walnuts
- 1 glass liquor
- 1 dessertspoonful sugar
- 1 pt. cream

## Directions

1. Cut the figs with a sharp knife into fine dice
2. Chop the walnuts coarsely
3. Whip the cream
4. Sweeten with sugar to taste
5. Lightly stir in the figs and walnuts
6. Flavor with the liqueur
7. Serve in custard glasses

title: Filled Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup lard or butter
- 2 eggs
- 3 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour to roll out soft
- 1 cup raisins chopped fine
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 large cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Make dough and roll very thin and cut out round or square
2. For filling: Put 1 cup chopped raisins, 1 cup boiling water, and 1 large cup sugar on the stove
3. When boiling stir in 2 tablespoons of flour
4. When cold, spread between the cookies
5. Bake

title: Filling and Icing for Walnut Cake 4
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 5 eggs
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 cup chopped walnuts

## Directions

1. Stir together egg yolks, sour cream, and sugar
2. Cook till it thickens, stirring all the time as it burns very quickly
3. When cold, add chopped walnuts

title: Filling for Any Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- enough water to dissolve it
- 1 cup stoned and chopped raisins
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Put 1 cup white sugar into a basin with enough water to dissolve it
2. Let boil till it will harden in cold water
3. Have 1 cup stoned and chopped raisins ready
4. Beat the white of 1 egg to a stiff froth
5. Mix with the raisins into boiling sugar
6. Stir briskly
7. While warm put between and on top of cake

title: Filling for Cream Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups milk
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoon flour
- pinch salt
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Put the milk on the stove
2. When hot, add the egg and sugar beaten with flour
3. Cook

title: Filling for Orange Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- grated rind and inside of 1 orange
- 1 egg
- 1 cup white sugar
- butter size of an egg
- 1 heaping tablespoon flour
- 1 teacup boiling water

## Directions

1. Take grated rind and inside of 1 orange, 1 egg, 1 cup white sugar, butter size of an egg, 1 heaping tablespoon flour, 1 teacup boiling water
2. Set it on stove, and let it boil, and let it cool before putting into the cake

title: Filling for Trilbys 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound dates
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup water

## Directions

1. Cook dates, sugar, and water together

title: Filtered Coffee
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces coffee
- 1 pint water

## Directions

1. Place coffee in strainer, place strainer in pot
2. Add boiling water to coffee, 0.5 cup at a time, at intervals of 3 to 5 minutes
3. If desired stronger, re-filter
4. Serve at once
5. Coffee pot may be placed in a kettle of boiling water

title: Finnan Haddie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Finnan haddie
- Butter
- Lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Wash thoroughly, leave in cold water half an hour, then for five minutes in very hot
2. Wipe, rub over with butter and lemon-juice and broil fifteen minutes

title: Fish Balls à la Noeuis
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One and one-half cups of minced fish
- Two-thirds of a tablespoonful of butter
- Two-thirds of a cup of milk
- One and one-half tablespoonfuls of flour
- One tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- One-half teaspoonful of celery salt
- Grating of nutmeg
- Salt and pepper
- Puree of peas and potatoes

## Directions

1. Butter and flour put in saucepan stirred until well blended
2. Add milk, cook, stirring constantly till it leaves bottom and sides of pan
3. Add flavorings, seasoning and fish
4. Mix well together
5. Form into balls without using flour
6. Arrange down the centre of a dish which has been garnished with a puree of peas and potatoes using pastry bag
7. Garnish with parsley

title: Fish Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover fish
- Mashed potatoes
- A lump of butter
- 2 beaten eggs
- 1 tablespoon cream
- Pepper and salt
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mince the fish fine
2. Add a few more mashed potatoes than you have fish
3. Mix thoroughly, add a lump of butter, 2 beaten eggs, 1 tablespoon cream, pepper and salt
4. Fry in lard

title: Fish Cutlet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of boiled fish
- One cup of milk or cream
- One large tablespoonful of butter
- Three tablespoonfuls of flour
- Yolks of two eggs
- One tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- Pepper and salt to taste
- Little onion juice and nutmeg, if you wish

## Directions

1. Put milk on to boil, rub butter and flour together well and add to milk, then parsley
2. Add yolks of eggs, then the fish and stir until well mixed, then season
3. When cold form into cutlets
4. Roll with a little flour, dip in egg and then in breadcrumbs
5. Fry in dripping
6. For the sauce: Tablespoonful of melted butter, one of flour, stir smoothly, add one cup of milk; salt and pepper

title: Fish Entree
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One can salmon
- Two eggs well beaten
- One cup breadcrumbs
- A small piece of butter
- Pepper and salt
- Two hard-boiled eggs
- Drawn-butter sauce

## Directions

1. Put in a bowl and steam two hours
2. Serve with a drawn-butter sauce, in which can be added two hard-boiled eggs, chopped fine

title: Fish Left Over
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoonful of minced onion
- one teaspoonful of butter
- one level tablespoonful of flour
- one cup of hot stewed tomatoes
- one part cold boiled fish
- one part macaroni
- one-quarter cup of cheese
- one cold egg
- one-quarter cup of fine cracker crumbs
- one-third cup of melted butter

## Directions

1. Cook minced onion in butter until yellow
2. Add flour and when well mixed and bubbling, stir it into a cup of hot stewed tomatoes
3. When it has cooked a little and is thick, season to taste
4. Take one part each of cold boiled fish and macaroni with one-quarter cup of cheese
5. Cut into small bits one cold egg
6. Strain the tomato sauce over them and one-quarter cup of fine cracker crumbs, moistened in one-third cup of melted butter on the top
7. Bake till brown

title: Fish Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one level tablespoonful flour
- one tablespoonful butter
- half a cup hot milk
- pepper
- half-cupful shredded codfish
- two cupfuls cold boiled potatoes
- one tablespoonful butter

## Directions

1. Cook together one level tablespoonful of flour and one of butter
2. add gradually half a cup of hot milk and a little pepper
3. pour boiling water on a half-cupful of shredded codfish, drain and mix with the thickened milk
4. add two cupfuls of cold boiled potatoes chopped fine
5. melt a tablespoonful of butter in a spider
6. when hot turn in fish and cook slowly until a thick crust has formed
7. fold over and serve on hot platter

title: Fish Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- cold fish
- cream
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a plain omelet
2. When ready to fold, spread over it fish prepared as follows
3. Add to a cupful of any kind of cold fish, broken fine, cream enough to moisten it
4. Season with a tablespoonful of butter, then pepper and salt to taste
5. Warm together

title: Fish on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups codfish or any cold leftover fish
- 1 pint milk
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons butter
- Pepper
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- Buttered toast

## Directions

1. Heat milk to boiling point
2. Add cornstarch, butter and pepper, cook for 5 minutes
3. Prepare buttered toast, place fish on it
4. Cover with the milk mixture and garnish with sliced egg

title: Fish Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- Yolks of 2 eggs
- Juice of half lemon
- 1 saltspoon salt
- A pinch cayenne pepper
- 1/2 cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Stir butter to a cream
2. Add yolks of 2 eggs, 1 at a time, beat well
3. Stir in the juice of half lemon, salt and cayenne pepper
4. When ready to serve, add 1/2 cup boiling water
5. Place the bowl in a pan of boiling water and cook until thick, stirring all the while

title: Fish Surbot
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whitefish (medium size)
- 1 pint milk
- 1/2 onion
- flour
- 2 eggs
- 3/4 pound butter
- cracker crumbs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Steam whitefish 20 minutes
2. Pick fine
3. Boil milk with 1/2 onion in it
4. Thicken with flour
5. When cold, add 2 eggs and 3/4 pound butter
6. Put in a dish a layer of fish and then a layer of dressing, till all is used
7. Cover with cracker crumbs
8. Add bits of butter and juice of a lemon

title: Five-Hour Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large potatoes
- 2 3/4 cups flour
- 2 tablespoons brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 quart boiling water
- potato water
- 1 quart of cold water
- 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes
- warm water

## Directions

1. Take 12 large potatoes, peel boil and mash fine.
2. Take 2 3/4 cups flour and 2 tablespoons brown sugar and 1 tablespoon salt, and scald this with 1 quart boiling water.
3. Mix well, then add the potato water, then 1 quart of cold water.
4. Put 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes to soak in a little warm water and when the yeast is lukewarm, put the yeast cakes in and let rise.
5. One cup of yeast and 1 of water makes 1 loaf.

title: Flaky Pastry
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1½ to ¾ cup butter
- Ice-cold water

## Directions

1. Rub 2 ounces of the butter into the flour
2. Cut the remainder into ¼-inch dice, and stir through the flour
3. Mix the water in with a broad-bladed knife, using only enough to hold the flour and butter together in shreds
4. Turn the mixture out on the board in a pile, pack it together with the hands, and roll out into a sheet
5. Use a broad-bladed knife, and fold it into three or four layers, tucking in all the loose shreds about the edges
6. Roll out again
7. Fold and roll out again
8. Fold in half and set away to chill, if necessary, before rolling it out to line pie-plates, etc.

title: Flax-Seed Lemonade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large tablespoonful flax-seed
- 1 tumbler and a half cold water
- 1 quarter pound pulverized sugar
- 1 ounce pulverized gum arable
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Boil flax-seed and cold water together till the liquid becomes very sticky
2. Strain it hot over the pulverized sugar and gum arable
3. Stir it till quite dissolved
4. Squeeze into it the juice of a lemon
5. Take a wine-glass of it as often as the cough is troublesome

title: Floating Island
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 cup sugar
- a little salt
- 1 quart of milk
- vanilla to taste

## Directions

1. Save the whites of 3 eggs for top
2. Beat together 2 tablespoons cornstarch and 1 cup sugar
3. Add yolks of eggs and a little salt
4. Take 1 quart of milk and let come to a boil
5. Stir in sugar, cornstarch, and other ingredients
6. Cool
7. Put in vanilla to taste
8. Beat the whites of eggs
9. Cook the beaten whites on boiling water

title: Fluff
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup water
- the white of 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil the sugar and water till it forms a ball when dropped into water
2. Beat the white of 1 egg and pour in the boiled sugar and water
3. Beat until thick and add vanilla
4. Put on buttered platter and let stand till cool

title: Foam Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Half cup butter
- one cup white sugar
- one egg
- six tablespoonfuls of milk
- one wineglass of brandy

## Directions

1. Beat the butter and sugar to a froth; add yolk of egg and milk.
2. Set on a slow fire; add brandy and white of egg well beaten just before serving.

title: Foam Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- half a cup of butter
- yolks of two eggs
- one wineglass of sherry
- beaten whites of two eggs

## Directions

1. Beat butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add the yolks of two eggs and a wineglass of sherry
3. Add the beaten whites
4. Stand in a bowl of hot water
5. Stir for one minute

title: Foamy Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of two eggs
- one cup sugar
- one cupful of hot syrup of preserved pears, apricots, peaches or anything of that sort, or a cupful of hot milk

## Directions

1. Beat whites of eggs and sugar together well
2. This may be done an hour or more before serving
3. Add last thing before sending to table one cupful of hot syrup or hot milk
4. Beat all together and serve

title: Foamy Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup powdered sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons wine, or 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream butter
2. add sugar gradually
3. add egg well beaten and wine
4. beat while heating over hot water

title: Fondant
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups water
- 1/4 teaspoon cream tartar

## Directions

1. Stir sugar, water, and cream tartar until dissolved
2. Let boil until it reaches soft ball stage
3. Stand at once in pan of cold water
4. When cooled enough that you can stand your finger in it stir vigorously with stick
5. Never jar saucepan after it starts to boil
6. Do not attempt on wet day
7. Keep crystals wiped off sides

title: Fondu of Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful minced chicken (turkey or veal)
- 1 cup breadcrumbs
- 1 cup of boiling milk
- 1/2 onion boiled in the milk and strained out
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 slice cold boiled ham, minced
- 2 eggs beaten
- Pinch of soda
- Pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Soak the crumbs in the milk
2. Stir in the butter and beat very light
3. Let the mixture cool while you mince the meat and beat the eggs
4. Stir in the meat when the bread and milk are nearly cold
5. Season, lastly adding the eggs
6. Beat well up
7. Put it into a well-greased baking dish
8. Set in a brisk oven
9. When the fondu is a light delicate brown puff send at once to the table in the same dish in which it has been baked

title: For Children Teething
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of a pound of wheat flour
- one quart of water
- one tablespoonful of grated boiled flour
- half a pint of new milk
- a pinch of salt
- a tablespoonful of cold water

## Directions

1. Tie a quarter of a pound of wheat flour in a thick cloth, and boil it in one quart of water for three hours
2. Remove the cloth and expose the flour to the air or heat until it is hard and dry
3. Grate from it, when wanted, one tablespoonful, which put into half a pint of new milk, and stir over the fire until it comes to a boil
4. When it comes to a boil, add a pinch of salt and a tablespoonful of cold water, and serve

title: Force Meat for Stuffing Boned Fowls
body:
## Ingredients

- meat of another fowl or veal, or a mixture of both
- 1 cup crumbs
- some chopped ham or tongue
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoon onion juice
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon thyme
- stock

## Directions

1. Chop meat fine and add to the mince meat 1 cup crumbs and if convenient, some chopped ham or tongue
2. Season with 1 tablespoon chopped parsley, 1 teaspoon onion juice, 1 teaspoon salt, 3/4 teaspoon pepper, 1 teaspoon thyme
3. Moisten with stock if veal is used; take it from knuckle, and use the bone in braizing pot, as it will leave a good jelly
4. In stuffing, be careful that too much is not used, as allowance must be made for the swelling

title: Force-Meat For Stuffing Boned Fowls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- meat of another fowl
- veal
- pork
- one cupful of bread or cracker crumbs
- chopped boiled ham or tongue
- lardoons of pork
- one tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- one teaspoonful of onion-juice
- one-quarter teaspoonful of pepper
- one teaspoonful thyme
- one teaspoonful of salt
- stock

## Directions

1. Use the meat of another fowl, or veal, or pork, or a mixture.
2. Chop them fine, and add to the minced meat one cupful of bread or cracker crumbs, and, if convenient, a little chopped boiled ham or tongue, and a few lardoons of pork.
3. Season with one tablespoonful of chopped parsley, one teaspoonful of onion-juice, one-quarter teaspoonful of pepper, one teaspoonful thyme, and one teaspoonful of salt.
4. Moisten the whole with stock.
5. If veal is used, take it from the knuckle, and use the bone in the braising pot, as it will give a good jelly.

title: Forcemeat Ball
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cooked meat
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 3/4 teaspoon thyme
- 1 teaspoon onion juice
- pepper
- yolk of 1 egg
- egg

## Directions

1. Mix in order given, adding enough egg to moisten meat
2. Form in balls size of a marble
3. Roll in flour and brown in hot fat

title: Forcemeat Ball
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cooked meat
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 3/4 teaspoon thyme
- 1 teaspoon onion juice
- pepper
- yolk of 1 egg
- flour

## Directions

1. mix in order given, adding enough egg to moisten meat
2. form in balls size of a marble
3. roll in flour and brown in hot fat

title: Forcemeat for Veal, Turkeys, Fowls, Hare
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liver
- 2 oz. ham or bacon
- 1 lb. suet
- rind of 0.5 lemon
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful minced parsley
- 1 teaspoonful minced sweet herbs
- salt
- cayenne
- mace
- 6 oz. breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Shred the ham or bacon and liver
2. Chop the suet, lemon peel and herbs very fine
3. Add the seasoning to taste, salt, cayenne and mace
4. Blend all thoroughly together with the breadcrumbs before wetting
5. Beat and strain the eggs
6. Work them up with the other ingredients and the forcemeat will be ready for use

title: Four-Hour Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- potato water
- 6 potatoes
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup salt
- warm water
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake

## Directions

1. On the day before the baking is to be done, drain the water in which the potatoes for dinner have been boiled into a small pail or crock, add 6 potatoes mashed fine, and when it has cooled add 2 cups flour, 1 cup sugar, about 1/2 cup salt, and enough warm water to make three quarts.
2. Add a White Swan Yeast Cake and set away in a warm place until the next day.
3. When ready to bake set this pail or crock in a pan of warm water until it is warm (not hot), then add flour enough to knead into a stiff dough.
4. Let it rise; when light, knead again and put in tins.
5. Let it rise again and bake.

title: Four-Hour Bread Yeast
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 medium-sized potatoes
- 4 heaping tablespoons flour
- 2 heaping tablespoons salt
- 2 heaping tablespoons sugar
- potato water
- 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes
- warm water

## Directions

1. Boil 12 medium-sized potatoes, have ready in a 2-gallon crock 4 heaping tablespoons flour, 2 heaping tablespoons salt, 2 heaping tablespoons sugar.
2. Scald with potato water, mash potatoes through colander, then add enough water to make about 1 1/2 gallons, lukewarm.
3. Add 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes which have been soaked in a little warm water.
4. Set in a warm place to rise.

title: Fowl Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- very stale bread
- finely chopped onions
- apples
- raw eggs
- salt
- pepper
- allspice

## Directions

1. Steam very stale bread according to size of fowl.
2. Add finely chopped onions and apples, raw eggs, salt, pepper and allspice to taste.
3. For a duck: use small bowl bread, one onion, two apples, three eggs, mixed well.

title: Fowls' Honey Fruit-Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup honey
- 3/4 cup apple jelly or boiled cider
- 2 eggs well beaten
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 teacup raisins
- 1 teacup dried currants
- flour enough to make a stiff batter

## Directions

1. Warm the butter, honey and apple jelly slightly
2. Add the beaten eggs
3. Add the soda dissolved in a little warm water
4. Add spices and flour enough to make a stiff batter
5. Stir in the fruit and bake in a slow oven
6. Keep in a covered jar several weeks before using

title: Fowls' Honey Layer-Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 cup honey
- 3 eggs beaten
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 1.5 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Cream the honey and butter together
2. Add the beaten eggs and milk
3. Add 2 cups flour containing 1.5 teaspoons baking powder previously stirred in
4. Stir in flour to make a stiff batter
5. Bake in jelly-tins
6. When the cakes are cold, spread finely flavored candied honey between layers

title: Fowls' Honey-Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 teaspoons soda
- 2 cups warm honey
- 1 cup shortening containing salt
- 2 teaspoons ginger
- 1 cup hot water
- flour sufficient to roll

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in warm honey
2. Add shortening containing salt and ginger
3. Add hot water
4. Mix with flour sufficient to roll

title: French Cream Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- 1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 quart milk
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup white sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- flavor to taste

## Directions

1. Bake in 2 tins and split while hot and fill in with cream filling
2. For cream filling: heat 1 quart milk
3. Mix 2 tablespoons cornstarch, 1 egg, 1/4 cup white sugar, 2 tablespoons butter
4. Add to milk and flavor to taste

title: French Fried Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash and pare potatoes
2. Cut in eighths lengthwise
3. Soak 1 hour in cold water
4. Dry and fry
5. Drain and sprinkle with salt

title: French Mustard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 good-sized onion
- cold vinegar
- cayenne pepper
- salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- mustard

## Directions

1. Slice up a good-sized onion
2. Cover it with cold vinegar
3. After it has stood for an hour, pour off the vinegar
4. Add a little cayenne pepper and salt
5. Add 1 teaspoon sugar
6. Add mustard enough to thicken
7. Mix and set on the stove
8. Stir until it boils

title: French Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- several slices of stale bread
- thick, sour milk
- 3 1/2 teaspoon soda scalded
- a little flour
- a little sweet milk
- an egg or two well beaten
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Cover several slices of stale bread with thick, sour milk and let stand over night. Stir well and add soda scalded, a little flour, and if needed, a little sweet milk. Add an egg or two well beaten and butter. Bake on buttered pans.

title: French Pickle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pk. green tomatoes
- 1 cup salt
- 3 qts. vinegar
- 2 qts. water
- 2½ lbs. brown sugar
- 2 small tablespoonfuls ground allspice
- 2 small tablespoonfuls mustard seed
- 2 small tablespoonfuls ginger
- 2 small tablespoonfuls mustard
- 2 small tablespoonfuls cloves
- 2 large tablespoonfuls cinnamon

## Directions

1. Slice green tomatoes, sprinkle with one cup of salt, and leave over night
2. Drain well and add two quarts of water and one of vinegar
3. Boil fifteen minutes
4. Drain again and add two quarts of vinegar, brown sugar, mustard seed, allspice, ginger, mustard, cloves, two large tablespoonfuls of cinnamon
5. Mix, and boil fifteen minutes

title: French Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes
- 6 onions
- 4 green peppers
- 1 teacup salt
- 1 cup vinegar
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon caraway
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon

## Directions

1. Chop green tomatoes, onions, and green peppers fine together
2. Add 1 teacup of salt
3. Let stand over night
4. Drain off all water
5. Add 1 cup vinegar, 2 pounds sugar, 1 teaspoon each of mustard, ginger, cloves, and caraway if desired, and 2 tablespoons cinnamon
6. Boil 1 hour

title: French Rolls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sweet milk
- 1/2 cup butter and lard mixed
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup yeast or 1/2 cake dissolved in 1/2 cup warm water
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- flour to make stiff dough

## Directions

1. Let rise over night.
2. In the morning add well-beaten eggs, knead and let rise again.
3. Make into balls the size of an egg.
4. Roll each one between the hands to make a long roll (about three inches long).
5. Place close together in even rows on well-buttered pans.
6. Cover and let rise again.
7. Glaze with sweet milk before baking.
8. Bake in a quick oven to a delicate brown.

title: French Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint scalded milk
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 3/2 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Scald 1 pint milk
2. Add 1 teaspoon salt, 3/4 White Swan Yeast Cake, 3/2 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon butter
3. Rub in flour to make a sponge
4. Let rise over night
5. Next morning mix stiff and set to rise again
6. Mix down twice
7. Cut with cake cutter and double over
8. Cook in a moderately hot oven

title: French Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread
- one egg
- two cupfuls milk
- Butter
- white sugar

## Directions

1. Slice bread as for ordinary toast
2. Beat one egg well, add to it two cupfuls milk
3. Soak a slice of bread in the egg and milk
4. Fry it in butter, turn until nicely toasted on both sides
5. Sprinkle white sugar over it
6. Serve it separately, or place one slice above another

title: Fricassee of Shrimp
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart tomatoes
- one quart water
- onion
- butter
- flour
- two cans shrimps
- rice

## Directions

1. stew one quart of tomatoes, one quart of water, and onion to suit the taste together until the tomatoes can be passed through a sieve
2. after steaming stew with seasoning (season highly), and a tablespoonful of butter creamed with a little flour, for fifteen minutes
3. add two cans of shrimps, carefully washed
4. heat thoroughly and serve with rice

title: Fricassee Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint oysters
- 1 cup white sauce
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Clean plump oysters
2. Add egg to white sauce
3. Add oysters

title: Fried Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- fair-sized apples
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take as many fair-sized apples as required
2. Cut them in two crosswise
3. Remove core
4. Fry the cut side until brown in butter
5. Turn over
6. Fill cavity with sugar
7. Brown the other side
8. Set pan in oven until thoroughly cooked

title: Fried Bananas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- flour
- butter
- eggs
- cracker dust
- cottolene

## Directions

1. Strip off the skins; cut each banana into three slices, and flour well.
2. Saute in hot butter in a frying-pan, or fry in deep fat.
3. Drain dry and serve hot.
4. Or, roll in egg, then in cracker dust; set on ice for one hour and fry in hot, deep cottolene.

title: Fried Bananas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- flour
- butter
- egg
- cracker dust
- cottolene

## Directions

1. Strip off the skins; cut each banana into three slices, and flour well.
2. Saute in hot butter in a frying-pan, or fry in deep fat.
3. Drain dry and serve hot.
4. Or, roll in egg, then in cracker dust; set on ice for one hour and fry in hot, deep cottolene.

title: Fried Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Wash without breaking the skin
2. boil tender
3. slice
4. fry in butter, season and serve very hot

title: Fried Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 1 small cup brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup cream
- 1/2 cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- lard or goose grease

## Directions

1. Mix all together
2. Fry in lard or goose grease

title: Fried Cakes 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 good-sized potatoes
- butter the size of a walnut
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon baking powder to each cup of flour
- flour enough so it will not stick to hands
- a little nutmeg
- pinch of salt
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Boil and mash potatoes fine
2. Add butter, sugar, and eggs beaten well
3. Add sweet milk
4. Add baking powder to flour
5. Use flour enough so it will not stick to hands, try not to get too stiff

title: Fried Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- tender chicken
- water
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- lard or butter
- milk or cream
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut a tender chicken in pieces.
2. Dip the pieces in water.
3. Sprinkle them with salt and pepper, and roll them in flour.
4. Saute them in a tablespoonful of lard or butter, browning both sides.
5. Remove and add to the pan a tablespoonful of flour.
6. Cook it for a minute without browning, stirring all the time, and add a cupful of milk or cream.
7. Stir until it is a little thickened.
8. Strain.
9. Mix into it a tablespoonful of chopped parsley.
10. Place the sauce on the serving-dish and arrange the pieces of chicken on it.

title: Fried Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- good plump chicken
- deep hot fat
- 1 large potato
- 1 large onion
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cut chicken in half lengthwise and cut each half across to make four pieces of the body
2. Cut off the legs and open them to the bone
3. Place the fowl in the frying pan in deep hot fat
4. Slice over it 1 large potato and 1 large onion
5. Season well with pepper and salt
6. Cover and fry gently for 20 minutes or 3/4 an hour, turning meat to brown on both sides

title: Fried Chicken 2
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut chicken up and boil 1 hour
2. Dip each piece in flour, then fry in butter till brown

title: Fried Cucumbers
body:
## Ingredients

- large cucumbers
- salt
- butter or lard
- flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take large cucumbers, peal and slice lengthwise
2. sprinkle with salt
3. have the pan ready with butter or lard and butter
4. have it hot
5. dip the cucumbers in flour
6. sprinkle with pepper and fry to a nice brown

title: Fried Egg Plant
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plant
- salt
- pepper
- batter or egg and crumbs

## Directions

1. Treat as for sauted egg plant
