7. Melt in one cup the soda, and in another the tartaric acid.
8. Dissolve them both in lukewarm water and see that both are quite melted.
9. First stir the soda into the mixture and then put in the tartaric acid.
10. On no account exceed the quantity of the two alkalies, as if too much is used they will destroy entirely the flavoring and communicate a very disagreeable taste instead.
11. Having buttered a large round or oblong pan, put in the mixture and bake it in a moderate oven till thoroughly done, keeping up a steady heat, but watching that it does not burn.

title: Lamb and Peas
body:
## Ingredients

- chopped lamb
- onion
- cold potatoes
- pepper
- salt
- stock
- buttered crumbs
- green peas

## Directions

1. Mix 2 cups chopped lamb (cold), 1 small onion, 1 cup cold potatoes, pepper and salt, 3 tablespoons stock, 3/4 cup buttered crumbs, 1 cup green peas lightly
2. Heap in mould or shallow butter dish
3. Cover with crumbs and bake until browned
4. Before serving, surround with seasoning and hot peas

title: Lamb Haricot
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. chops
- Pepper and salt
- 1 slice onion
- 1 cup hot water
- 2 carrots
- browned flour
- minced parsley

## Directions

1. Brown chops
2. Brown carefully a sliced onion
3. Add carrots cut in pieces, pepper, salt, and a cup of hot water
4. Cook slowly two hours
5. Add more water if necessary
6. Thicken slightly with browned flour when cooked
7. Add a little minced parsley

title: Large Cucumber Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart vinegar
- 3/4 cup flour
- 2 tablespoons mustard
- sugar to taste
- red pepper to taste
- celery seed to taste
- cucumbers
- onions
- small tender corn cobs
- salt and water

## Directions

1. Peel and slice cucumbers, onions, and corn cobs the night before in not too strong salt and water
2. Drain in the morning
3. Put vinegar on to boil
4. Mix mustard, flour and sugar to a paste
5. Add to hot vinegar and let boil up
6. Add onions, cucumbers, and corn cobs
7. Stir till the whole comes to a boil

title: Lavender Water
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Oil of lavender, two ounces
- Orris root, half an ounce
- Spirits of wine, one pint

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Keep two or three weeks
3. Strain through two thicknesses of blotting-paper and it is ready for use

title: Layer Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- Whites of 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 0.5 nutmeg
- Flour to make very stiff

## Directions

1. Drop in small spoonfuls on a buttered tin
2. Sprinkle the top with English currants and sugar
3. Bake quickly

title: Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Cream sugar and butter
2. Add eggs well beaten
3. Add last, milk and flour sifted with the baking powder
4. Bake in 3 layers and put together with lemon filling or icing
5. Ice top and side

title: Layer Cake Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- butter size of an egg
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon lemon
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 3 scant cups flour

## Directions

1. Cream together 1 cup sugar and butter size of an egg
2. Add 1 cup milk and 1 teaspoon lemon
3. Add 3 teaspoons baking powder sifted in 3 scant cups flour
4. Mix all together

title: Layer Cake Without Shortening
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3/4 cup boiling sweet milk

## Directions

1. Beat eggs, sugar, flour and baking powder together well
2. Add 3/4 cup boiling sweet milk

title: Layer Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 2 cups chopped seeded raisins or 1 pound chopped dates
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- a little nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon lemon extract
- flour to make a soft batter

## Directions

1. Bake in layers and use any kind of nice filling

title: Lea's Red Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 12 chopped apples
- 6 onions
- 4 peppers
- 1 pint vinegar
- 2 cups sugar
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients
2. Cook until thick

title: Left-Over Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- stale cake
- 1 large sweet orange
- 2 cups sweet milk
- 1 egg
- ½ cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 teaspoons vanilla

## Directions

1. Break up cake in small pieces in salad dish
2. Cut orange in small pieces and arrange over the cake
3. Make a cream of sweet milk, egg, sugar, and flour enough to make a thin cream
4. Let come to boil and take from stove
5. Add vanilla
6. Pour over the whole and let cool before serving

title: Left-over Potatoes Lyonnaise
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onion
- cold boiled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- brown stock
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook for 5 minutes in 3 tablespoons butter with 1 small onion, cut in slices
2. Add 3 cold boiled potatoes cut in 1/2-inch cubes
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
4. Let stand until well browned underneath
5. Fold and turn on hot plate
6. To this might be added 2 tablespoons brown stock and chopped parsley

title: Leg of Mutton for Venison
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- quart of rich gravy
- tomato sauce or catsup
- salt
- 2 tablespoons barberry or currant jelly

## Directions

1. Pick out a fine leg of mutton and let it hang twelve days in a cool place.
2. Wash it thoroughly and put it in a dripping-pan on a grate, so it will heat through thoroughly but will not cook.
3. Remove it from the pan and pour into the pan a quart of rich gravy.
4. Cut the mutton from the bone in slices not quite inch thick, add a little tomato sauce or catsup and salt to suit taste, then lay in the slices of meat and cook about 25 minutes, or until done.
5. When nearly done, add 2 tablespoons barberry or currant jelly.
6. Serve hot as soon as cooked.

title: Lemon Biscuit
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 cups sugar
- 1.5 cups lard
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 2 eggs
- five cents' worth of baker's ammonia
- five cents' worth of lemon oil
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Beat sugar, lard, and eggs together
2. Pulverize and dissolve ammonia in sweet milk
3. Put lemon oil in last
4. Mix in flour till stiff
5. Roll about as thick as pasteboard
6. Cut in squares or round shapes
7. Add a little salt
8. Bake in a quick oven

title: Lemon Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 2 cups sweet milk
- 1 cup lard
- 3/4 of 5c. worth oil of lemon
- 1/2 of 5c. worth baking ammonia
- sugar

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, milk, lard, oil of lemon and baking ammonia
2. Roll a little thicker than pie crust on bottom of tin
3. Cross with a fork
4. Sprinkle with sugar
5. Bake

title: Lemon Biscuits (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 1/2 cups white sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup lard
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 5 cents' worth baking ammonia
- 5 cents' worth oil of lemon
- hot water

## Directions

1. Mix white sugar, eggs, butter, lard and milk
2. Dissolve baking ammonia and oil of lemon with hot water
3. Add to mixture
4. Bake

title: Lemon Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- Grated rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 egg, the whites and yolks beaten separately

## Directions

1. Boil till it drips like honey

title: Lemon Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- Grated rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Beat the whites and yolks separately
2. Combine grated rind and juice of 1 lemon, 1 tablespoon butter, 3/4 cup sugar, 1 egg
3. Boil till it drips like honey

title: Lemon Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 3 eggs
- grated rind of a fresh lemon
- 1/2 pound flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1/2 cup milk

## Directions

1. Combine ingredients and bake.

title: Lemon Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon lard
- 3/4 cup white sugar
- 1 egg
- grated rind of lemon
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 3/4 cup currants
- flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix butter, lard and sugar
2. Add egg
3. Add grated rind of lemon
4. Add sweet milk
5. Add currants
6. Add flour enough to make a stiff batter and baking powder
7. Mix and bake

title: Lemon Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 pounds sugar
- 1.75 pints water
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon tartaric acid
- 1 teaspoon extract of lemon

## Directions

1. Put into a kettle sugar, water, and cream tartar
2. Let boil until it becomes brittle when dropped into cold water
3. When sufficiently done take off fire and pour into a shallow dish which has been greased with a little butter
4. When this has been cooled so that it can be handled, add tartaric acid and extract of lemon
5. Work them into the mass until evenly distributed

title: Lemon Champagne
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 ounces citric acid
- 2 quarts warm water
- 5 pounds white sugar
- 1 dram oil of lemon
- soda

## Directions

1. Dissolve 4 ounces citric acid in a little warm water
2. Pour 2 quarts warm water on 5 pounds white sugar
3. When dissolved add acid and 1 dram oil of lemon
4. Stir all well and bottle for use
5. Use 1 tablespoon to glass of water, and a pinch of soda

title: Lemon Cheese
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. fresh butter
- 3 lemons, juice
- 3 eggs well beaten
- 1 lb. sugar
- Peel of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Make in double boiler, boiling to the consistency of thick honey — stirring all the while.

title: Lemon Cheese Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. lump sugar
- Juice of 3 lemons
- 6 eggs (yolks)
- Rind of 2 lemons, grated
- Whites of 2 eggs
- 1/4 lb. butter

## Directions

1. Mix all together in double boiler and stir gently over a fire until the mixture becomes thick
2. put away in a bowl or crock and it will keep for weeks
3. Make a nice light paste and line little patty pans with it, and put a teaspoonful of the mixture into each
4. bake in a hot oven

title: Lemon Cheese Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. sugar
- 5 eggs
- Juice and grated rind of 2 lemons
- 1 lb. butter
- one rolled biscuit
- puff paste

## Directions

1. Place over a slow fire, stirring until dissolved
2. add one rolled biscuit
3. place in a jar for use
4. Make small shells of puff paste and fill with the lemon filling

title: Lemon Cheese Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- butter the size of an egg
- 1 well-beaten egg
- juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
- 1 cup currants
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- rich paste

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup sugar with butter the size of an egg
2. Add 1 well-beaten egg, juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
3. Add 1 cup currants and 3/4 cup sweet milk
4. Line tart pans with rich paste
5. Put a spoonful of mixture in each and bake

title: Lemon Cheese for Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound white sugar
- rind of 2 lemons
- juice of 3 or 4 small lemons
- 6 eggs
- 6 ounces of butter

## Directions

1. Boil until the thickness of honey
2. Put into sealers and keep in a cool place

title: Lemon Cheese For Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound white sugar
- rind of 2 lemons
- juice of 3 or 4 small lemons
- 6 eggs (leaving out the whites of 3)
- 6 ounces of butter

## Directions

1. Boil until the thickness of honey
2. Put into sealers and keep in a cool place

title: Lemon Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup lard
- 5c. oil lemon
- 5c. cooking ammonia
- 1 pint sweet milk
- flour to make a stiff dough

## Directions

1. Roll out and cut in medium size.

title: Lemon Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups granulated sugar
- 2 cups sweet milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup lard
- 5 cents baking ammonia
- 5 cents oil of lemon
- 3/4 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and bake.

title: Lemon Dessert
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 1 lemon
- 1 cup white sugar
- 2 cups water
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 teaspoon butter
- pinch of salt
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. When water comes to boil, add beaten yolks, cornstarch and lemon juice
2. beat the whites stiff, and add to mixture when it begins to bubble
3. Beat all briskly
4. Serve cold

title: Lemon Drops
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 coffee-cupful of finely powdered sugar
- lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Upon a coffee-cupful of finely powdered sugar, pour just enough lemon-juice to dissolve it
2. Boil it to the consistency of thick syrup, and so that it appears brittle when dropped in cold water
3. Drop this on buttered plates in drops
4. Set away to cool and harden

title: Lemon Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 1 lemon
- 1 egg
- 1 cup white sugar
- small lump butter

## Directions

1. Boil 10 minutes

title: Lemon Filling for Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole lemon, grated
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 beaten egg

## Directions

1. Grate a whole lemon and put it in a small saucepan with 1 cup sugar
2. Let it boil a few minutes
3. Add a beaten egg
4. Spread between layers

title: Lemon Filling for Cake (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon, grated
- 1 apple, grated
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 grated lemon, 1 grated apple, 1 egg, and 1 cup sugar
2. Boil 4 minutes

title: Lemon Filling for Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One lemon, juice and rind grated
- yolks of two eggs
- one cup of water
- one cup of sugar
- one heaping teaspoonful of cornstarch

## Directions

1. Put lemon juice and rind grated, yolks of two eggs, one cup of water, one cup of sugar, and one heaping teaspoonful of cornstarch in a double boiler and boil thick.
2. Save the whites for top.

title: Lemon Filling for Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice of 2 lemons
- rind of one
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 ounces butter
- 2 or 3 well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Boil till it thickens
2. Fill in baked shells

title: Lemon Filling for Tarts, Cakes, Etc.
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 egg
- 1 dessertspoonful cornstarch
- Grated rind and juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Beat sugar and egg to a cream
2. Add rind and juice of lemon, previously prepared, also cornstarch, and blend thoroughly
3. Pour in slowly cup of water, which must be boiling
4. Put on the fire in a double saucepan and allow it to simmer for ten minutes

title: Lemon Filling for Washington Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 egg
- 1 cup white sugar
- small lump butter

## Directions

1. Combine juice, egg, sugar, and butter
2. Boil 10 minutes

title: Lemon Honey
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces of fresh butter
- 1 cup of sugar
- 3 eggs, yolks beaten
- Grated peel of a large lemon
- Juice of the lemon

## Directions

1. Melt butter and sugar together
2. Stir the beaten yolks of 3 eggs into which have been mixed the grated peel of a large lemon
3. Stir over the fire till it begins to thicken
4. Add juice of the lemon
5. Continue stirring till as thick as honey
6. Pour into jelly tumblers and cover

title: Lemon Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box gelatine
- 1 pt. hot water
- 0.5 pt. wine
- 1 pt. sugar
- 2 lemons, juice and rind
- Whites of 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Soak gelatine for one hour in a pint of cold water
2. Add hot water, wine, sugar, lemons, juice and thinly pared rind
3. Boil for one-half a minute and strain
4. Beat whites of three eggs to a stiff froth and stir in before quite cold
5. Put in mould

title: Lemon Jelly Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3 eggs well beaten
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
- 1/2 cup cold water
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch

## Directions

1. Beat butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add well beaten eggs, milk, flour and baking powder sifted together
3. Bake in layers
4. For jelly: combine sugar, egg, lemon juice, rind and cold water with cornstarch
5. Place the dish in boiling water until it thickens
6. Spread between the layers and ice the top

title: Lemon Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 lemons
- 5 quarts of water
- 6 pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Slice 12 lemons very thin, and take out the seeds
2. Pour over them 5 quarts of water and let them stand all night
3. The next morning put them on in the same water, and let them boil until tender enough to mash with a spoon
4. Add six pounds of sugar and boil 30 minutes
5. Do not cover until cold

title: Lemon Mincemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large lemons
- 6 large apples
- 3/4 pound suet
- 1 pound currants
- 3 1/2 pound sugar
- 2 ounces candied lemon peel
- 1 ounce citron peel
- mixed spice to taste

## Directions

1. Pare the lemons and squeeze them
2. Boil the peel tender enough to mash
3. Add to the mashed lemon peel the apples minced, then the chopped suet, currants, sugar, peel and spice
4. Strain the lemon juice to these ingredients
5. Stir the mixture well, put in a jar with a closely fitting lid
6. Stir occasionally
7. In one week it will be ready for use

title: Lemon or Orange Filling for a Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange or lemon grated
- 1 egg beaten well
- 3/2 to 1 cup sugar according to taste

## Directions

1. Put the lemon in dish, set in a pot of boiling water
2. When nearly boiling, pour in the beaten egg and sugar
3. Let it boil until nicely thickened
4. Spread between layers of cake

title: Lemon or Orange Filling for a Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange or lemon grated
- 1 egg beaten well
- 1/2 to 1 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Put the grated lemon or orange in a dish and set in a pot of boiling water
2. When nearly boiling, pour in the beaten egg and sugar
3. Let it boil until nicely thickened

title: Lemon Peel Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup sweet cream and milk
- 1 cup lemon peel cut fine
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 3/4 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Lemon Pickle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lemons
- 0.5 pound salt
- 0.5 ounce cloves
- 0.5 ounce mace
- 0.5 ounce turmeric
- 3 pints vinegar
- Quarter ounce cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the lemons in halves
2. Squeeze out the juice
3. Cut each half into four pieces
4. Rub each piece with salt
5. Put them in a slow oven till they are quite brown and hard
6. Rub them with the turmeric and put them in a jar
7. Put the juice, spice and vinegar and quarter ounce of cayenne pepper over them
8. Cover them up and set the jar on a hot hearth or on the back of the stove till the pickles are soft

title: Lemon Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon, grated
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- 3/4 cupful sugar
- Yolks of 2 eggs
- Butter size of egg
- 1 cup hot water

## Directions

1. Cook in double boiler
2. Let it cool a little, then put in yolks last, after the other ingredients are well cooked
3. To prepare the lemon, grate off the outside, taking care to get only the yellow (the white is bitter), then squeeze out the juice
4. Bake your pie crust first, then add lemon filling
5. Keep the whites for the meringue
6. Whip up the whites stiff, add a little pulverized sugar
7. Spread on top of pie, and put in the oven for a few minutes

title: Lemon Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 2 eggs
- 2 cups warm water
- 2 dessertspoons cornstarch
- 1 cup sugar
- juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of 2 eggs
2. Mix with 2 cups warm water, 2 dessertspoons cornstarch, 1 cup sugar, juice and grated rind of 1 lemon, and pinch of salt
3. Beat whites of eggs for top

title: Lemon Pie 2
body:
## Ingredients

- pie crust
- yolk of 1 egg
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
- 2 heaping teaspoons cornstarch
- 1 cup boiling water
- white of 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- lemon drops

## Directions

1. Line a pie plate with crust and bake and set aside to cool
2. Mix yolk of egg and 1 cup granulated sugar together
3. Mix juice of lemon and 2 heaping teaspoons cornstarch
4. Mix all together and stir into 1 cup boiling water
5. Cook until thick and pour into pie shell
6. Frost with the white of egg beaten to a stiff froth with a teaspoon sugar and a few drops lemon
7. Set in oven until top is browned

title: Lemon Pie 3
body:
## Ingredients

- juice and grated rind of 1 large lemon
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 cup milk

## Directions

1. Put the sugar into the yolk and beat
2. Add flour and beat again
3. Add milk and fold in the stiffly beaten whites
4. Be careful not to beat the mixture after the whites are added
5. Bake so the whites rise to the top while baking and form a sort of meringue

title: Lemon Pie 4
body:
## Ingredients

- juice and grated rind of one lemon
- 2 1/2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- yolks of 4 eggs
- 3 cups boiling water
- whites of 4 eggs
- 1 tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Cook juice, rind, cornstarch, sugar, and egg yolks with 3 cups boiling water till thick, stirring all the time
2. This makes filling for 2 pies
3. Beat stiff the whites of the 4 eggs with 1 tablespoon sugar
4. Spread evenly over the tops and return to the oven to brown slightly

title: Lemon Pie and Tarts
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lemons
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups sugar, scant
- Butter size of egg
- 2 tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- 1 1/2 cups water

## Directions

1. Grate the rind and squeeze the juice of the lemons
2. Beat eggs light and melt the butter
3. Mix all together
4. Make a puff paste and line the tins with the pastry
5. Brush over with the white of an egg and fill with the lemon mixture and bake

title: Lemon Pie and Tarts
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lemons
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups sugar, scant
- Butter size of egg
- Puff paste
- White of an egg

## Directions

1. Grate the rind and squeeze the juice of the lemons
2. Beat eggs light and melt the butter
3. Mix all together
4. Make a puff paste and line the tins with the pastry
5. Brush over with the white of an egg
6. Fill with the lemon mixture and bake

title: Lemon Preserve for Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 cup butter

## Directions

1. Cook all together until thick

title: Lemon Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of water
- two tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- the juice and grated rind of two lemons
- a large cupful of white sugar
- the whites of three eggs
- the yolks of eggs

## Directions

1. Put one quart of water to boil
2. When boiling mix in two tablespoonfuls cornstarch, previously dissolved in a little water
3. Add the juice and grated rind of two lemons and a large cupful of white sugar
4. When quite thick beat in the whites of three eggs, beaten stiff
5. Set aside to cool
6. Serve with a thin custard made with the yolks of eggs, flavored with vanilla

title: Lemon Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound suet
- 3/4 pound bread crumbs
- 3/4 pound flour
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- grated rind and juice of 2 small lemons or one large one
- 2 eggs
- about 1/2 cup milk

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients with milk.
2. Boil 4 hours.

title: Lemon Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon (juice and rind)
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 2 1/2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 1/2 cups boiling water
- whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook lemon, sugar, cornstarch and water till thick.
2. Take from stove and when nearly cool, stir in the whites of 2 eggs beaten to a stiff froth.
3. Serve with sauce.

title: Lemon Rice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- 2 cups water
- grated rind of 1 lemon
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 1 pint milk
- white sugar to taste
- whites of 3 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Boil rice tender in water.
2. Make a custard of grated rind of lemon, yolks of eggs, and milk.
3. Add to rice and sweeten to taste.
4. Boil until the custard is set.
5. Beat whites of eggs to a stiff froth with 1 cup white sugar and juice of 1 lemon.
6. Put over the pudding and brown very slightly before serving.

title: Lemon Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 1/2 cups boiling water

## Directions

1. Beat together until light the yolks of eggs, sugar, butter, cornstarch, and lemon juice
2. Stir this into boiling water until sufficiently thick for the table

title: Lemon Sauce 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 1/2 tablespoons lemon juice
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. mix sugar and cornstarch
2. add nutmeg
3. add water gradually, stirring constantly
4. boil 5 minutes
5. Add other material

title: Lemon Sherbet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Six lemons
- Four eggs (the whites)
- Two pints sugar
- Water
- Vanilla
- Citric acid or cream tartar

## Directions

1. Make a thick syrup of one pint sugar and about one pint water
2. When cold, thin with the juice of six lemons, and water enough to make it a rich lemonade
3. When it is half frozen add boiled icing made as follows: One pint sugar moistened with water, and boiled until it is a soft candy
4. Whilst hot add the stiff beaten whites of four eggs
5. Flavor with vanilla and a little citric acid or cream tartar
6. Beat hard until thick and smooth
7. Add to the half frozen lemonade

title: Lemon Sherbet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of fresh milk
- 3 cups of sugar
- 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Grate a very little of the rind of one of the lemons, add the strained juice of all the lemons and the sugar to the milk.
2. Freeze at once.

title: Lemon Tapioca Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one small cup tapioca
- two cups of water
- another cup of water
- juice of one and a half large lemons
- rind of one lemon
- one cup sugar
- yolks of two eggs beaten
- whites of eggs
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Soak tapioca over night in two cups of water
2. Add another cup of water in the morning
3. Add juice of one and a half large lemons, rind of one lemon, one cup sugar, yolks of two eggs beaten
4. Cook till the tapioca is clear—about an hour
5. Whip whites of eggs with a little sugar and put on the top

title: Lemon Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound white sugar
- 6 eggs
- juice of 3 lemons
- grated rind of 2
- 3/4 pound fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put in saucepan and simmer

title: Lemon Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound white sugar
- 6 eggs (leaving out whites of two)
- juice of 3 lemons
- grated rind of 2 lemons
- ¾ pound fresh butter

## Directions

1. Put in saucepan and simmer until thick like honey

title: Lemon Trifle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lemons (juice)
- Grated rind of 1 lemon
- 1 cupful sherry
- 1 large cup of sugar
- 1 pt. cream sweetened and whipped stiff
- A little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Strain the lemon juice over the sugar and grated peel
2. Let them lie together two hours before adding the wine and nutmeg
3. Strain again
4. Whip gradually into the frothed cream
5. Serve in jelly glasses

title: Lemon Water Ice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of water
- Pared rind of two lemons
- Two and one-half cups sugar or more
- One tablespoon cornstarch
- Juice of five lemons

## Directions

1. Boil water with pared rind of two lemons (avoiding white part)
2. Add two and one-half cups sugar or more (very sweet)
3. Add one tablespoon (not heaping) of cornstarch
4. Add juice of five lemons
5. Strain and freeze
6. Should stand one hour before using

title: Lemon Water Ice, or Orange
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Four large juicy lemons
- One quart water
- One orange
- One and one-quarter pounds sugar
- White of one egg
- One tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Put sugar and water on to boil
2. Chip yellow rind from three lemons and the orange
3. Add to the syrup
4. Boil five minutes and stand to cool
5. Squeeze juice from lemons and orange
6. Add to cold syrup
7. Strain and freeze
8. Just before removing, add white of one egg beaten very stiff with one tablespoon sugar, to make white and frothy

title: Lemonade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lemons
- 4 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 1 qt. water or a bottle of Apollinaris

## Directions

1. Roll, peel, and slice four lemons
2. Put lemons (sliced) and sugar into a pitcher
3. Let them stand for an hour
4. Add water and ice
5. If you substitute Apollinaris for plain water you have a most refreshing drink

title: Lent Pies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two tablespoons of ground rice
- one pint of new milk
- five eggs
- sugar to taste
- two bay leaves
- pie crust

## Directions

1. Mix two tablespoons of ground rice with one pint of new milk.
2. Add five eggs and sweeten to taste, flavoring with two bay leaves.
3. Cover pie plates with a good crust.
4. Pour in mixture and bake.

title: Leopard Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 5 eggs
- 1/4 cup butter
- 3 cups granulated sugar
- 2 cups white flour
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- vanilla flavoring
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3/4 cup molasses
- 2 cups flour
- 1 egg
- 3/4 teaspoon soda
- spices to taste

## Directions

1. For White Part: Mix whites of 5 eggs, 1/4 cup butter, 3 cups granulated sugar, 2 cups white flour, 1 cup sweet milk, 2 teaspoons baking powder, and vanilla flavoring
2. For Dark Part: Mix 1 cup brown sugar, 3/4 cup butter, 1 cup sweet milk, 3/4 cup molasses, 2 cups flour, 1 egg, 3/4 teaspoon soda, and spices to taste
3. Put in the pan by spoonfuls

title: Lettuce Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pint cream, or rich milk
- ⅛ pint vinegar
- 1 small cup sugar
- 3 eggs well beaten
- lump of butter size of egg
- 1 teaspoon ground mustard
- 1 teaspoon pepper and salt

## Directions

1. mix all together cold and cook until thick

title: Lettuce Dressing (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small cup vinegar
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- pinch cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. place 1 small cup vinegar on stove to boil
2. when boiling add following mixture: 1 cup sugar, 2 eggs, 1 teaspoon mustard, 1 tablespoon flour, teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon butter, pinch cayenne pepper

title: Lettuce Dressing (Version 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs beaten separate
- 2 even tablespoons mustard
- 4 even tablespoons white sugar
- 4 even tablespoons butter
- ½ cup vinegar
- ¼ teaspoon salt
- dash cayenne pepper
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. mix butter, sugar, mustard, salt and pepper well
2. add beaten yolks of eggs
3. stir in vinegar, beat it well
4. cook in double boiler until it becomes soft like custard
5. when done add the white of eggs well beaten
6. when ready for use add cream or milk

title: Lettuce Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- leaf of lettuce
- buttered slices
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Place leaf of lettuce, or portion thereof, between buttered slices
2. Add a small dressing of mayonnaise
3. Keep very cold till ready to use

title: Lettuce Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Graham bread
- butter
- mayonnaise dressing
- crisp heart lettuce

## Directions

1. Cut thin slices from the end of a loaf of Graham bread, buttering before slicing.
2. Cut these into rounds with a cake-cutter.
3. Spread each slice with mayonnaise dressing and enclose between every two a leaf of crisp heart lettuce.
4. Trim off the projecting edges of the leaves.

title: Light Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups of flour
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- a handful currants
- a pinch salt
- sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together with sour milk
2. Bake in a quick oven

title: Light Biscuits (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 33.5 cups of flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1.5 cups of milk
- 2 heaping teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Light Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in water
2. Cream tartar in flour
3. Mix all ingredients together

title: Light Jelly Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 3 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 1 pint salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake in jelly tins

title: Light Sponge Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups white sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1/2 cup water
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- lemon or vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs one minute.
2. Add one and one-half cups white sugar and beat five minutes.
3. Add one cup flour and beat one minute more.
4. Add one-half cup water, one cup flour, two teaspoons baking powder; flavor to taste with lemon or vanilla.
5. Bake in a moderate oven twenty-five or thirty minutes.

title: Lightning Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter size of an egg
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Lima Bean Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 mutton chops
- 1 quart Lima beans
- 1 onion
- 2 potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- a sprig of parsley

## Directions

1. Cut up mutton chops and cut potatoes and onion in very small pieces
2. Add Lima beans, salt, pepper and a sprig of parsley
3. Cook

title: Lima Beans
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- lima beans
- salted boiling water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- hot cream or white sauce

## Directions

1. Put them into salted boiling water, and cook until tender, then drain off the water.
2. Moisten them with butter, and season with salt and pepper; and add, if convenient, a little hot cream or cover with white sauce.

title: Lima Beans
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Lima beans
- boiling water
- salt
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. After shelling, cook about half an hour in boiling water with a little salt
2. Drain dry
3. After dishing stir in a lump of butter half the size of an egg and pepper and salt to taste

title: Lima Beans (Second Method)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- lima beans
- boiling water
- salt
- butter (a lump half the size of an egg)
- pepper

## Directions

1. After shelling, cook about half an hour in boiling water with a little salt.
2. Drain dry, and after dishing stir in a lump of butter half the size of an egg and pepper and salt to taste.

title: Lima Beans (Stewed)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of beans
- hot, salted water
- four tablespoonfuls of hot milk
- a tablespoonful of butter
- a teaspoonful of flour
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Shell a quart of beans, and boil tender in hot, salted water
2. Drain, add four tablespoonfuls of hot milk, in which has been melted a tablespoonful of butter rolled in a teaspoonful of flour
3. Simmer for five minutes
4. Season with pepper and salt, and serve

title: Lime Pickle For Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lime
- 1 pint salt
- 4 gallons water

## Directions

1. Heat 1 pound of lime and 1 pint of salt to 4 gallons water together to form a solution
2. Pour the clear liquid off

title: Linseed Poultice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- four ounces of powdered linseed
- half a pint of hot water

## Directions

1. Take four ounces of powdered linseed, and gradually sprinkle it into a half pint of hot water

title: Lip Salve
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- White wax, one ounce
- Sweet oil, one ounce
- Spermaceti, one drachm
- Alkanet root for coloring
- Oil of rose for perfume

## Directions

1. Melt white wax, sweet oil, and spermaceti
2. Throw in a piece of alkanet root to color it
3. When cooling, perfume it with oil of rose
4. Pour it into small white jars or boxes

title: Little Timbales of Lamb
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cooked mutton
- butter
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- crumbs
- gravy or water
- capers

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup of cold cooked mutton, 3/4 tablespoon butter, 1 tablespoon onion minced fine, 3/4 teaspoon salt, pepper, 1/2 cup crumbs, gravy or water to moisten and 1 teaspoon capers
2. Cook onions and butter together without burning
3. Butter little moulds and place capers in moulds
4. Rub crumbs into onion and butter
5. Moisten, season and heat on stove till it begins to steam
6. Put in moulds and cook 15 or 20 minutes in moderate oven

title: Liver and Mushrooms
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of calf's liver
- 3 oz. bacon
- 1 lb. mushrooms
- 1 oz. flour
- 1/2 pint of stock
- Fried bread

## Directions

1. Fry the liver and bacon and the mushrooms separately
2. Put all into a stew-pan with half a pint of stock and simmer for one hour
3. Serve with fried bread

title: Liver Wert
body:
## Ingredients

- pig's liver
- fat pork
- onion
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a good-sized pig's liver and 1 pound of fat pork
2. Boil together until tender
3. Take out and mince fine with one large onion
4. Add pepper and salt to suit taste
5. Boil the liquor down to a small quantity and pour over the minced liver
6. Let cool
7. Can be served either warm or cold

title: Loaf Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 1/4 cups sugar
- 1 cup apple sauce
- 1 cup dates chopped
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Lobster
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster
- water

## Directions

1. Steam rather than boil it
2. Put lobster on rack with water 1 inch below
3. When water boils, put lobster in head first and quickly cover
4. Cook 20 minutes

title: Lobster A La Newberg
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups diced lobster
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons sherry
- 2/5 teaspoon salt
- paprika or cayenne
- 2 yolks
- 1 cup cream

## Directions

1. Melt butter, add lobster and heat
2. Add wine and cook 1/4 minute
3. Add cream, yolks and seasonings
4. Cook until it thickens, but does not curdle
5. Serve immediately
6. One finely chopped trifle may be added

title: Lobster a la Newburg
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 can lobster
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup sherry
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch
- 1 egg
- Salt and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Melt butter in chafing dish, add lobster
2. When hot add one small cup of milk, sherry (very slowly), salt and cayenne to taste
3. Thicken with cornstarch dissolved in a little milk
4. Just before serving stir in egg beaten very light

title: Lobster Bisque
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds lobster
- 2 cups cold water
- 4 cups milk
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 3/4 teaspoons salt
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Remove meat from lobster shell
2. Add cold water to body bones and tough end of claws
3. Cut in pieces
4. Bring slowly to the boiling point and cook 20 minutes
5. Drain and reserve liquor
6. Thicken with butter and flour
7. Scald milk with tail meat of lobster finely chopped
8. Strain and add to liquor
9. Season
10. Add tender claw meat
11. When coral is found in lobster, wash and wipe it
12. Force through strainer
13. Put in mortar with butter
14. Work until blended
15. Add flour and stir into soup

title: Lobster Chowder
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 pounds lobster
- four pounded crackers
- 1 quart boiling milk
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop lobster fine
2. Take the green part and add to it four pounded crackers
3. Stir into 1 quart boiling milk
4. Add lobster
5. Add a piece of butter 1/2 size of an egg
6. Add a little pepper and salt
7. Bring to a boil

title: Lobster Cutlet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a tin of lobster drained
- 1 ounce of butter
- 1 ounce of flour
- Half a teaspoonful of salt
- Dash of pepper, preferably cayenne
- 1 gill of lobster juice
- Milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls cream
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- Lobster, chopped fine
- Macaroni
- Egg
- Biscuit-crumb
- Butter for frying
- Fried parsley

## Directions

1. Make a panada of one ounce of butter, one ounce of flour, half a teaspoonful of salt, a dash of pepper, and one gill of lobster juice; if not enough juice add milk
2. Boil well, add two tablespoonfuls cream and one of lemon-juice
3. Stir in lobster, chopped fine
4. Beat all well together and cool
5. When set divide in small portions, form cutlets; for bone stick in a small bit of macaroni
6. Egg and biscuit-crumb the cutlets and fry in butter
7. Serve in a circle round a centre of fried parsley

title: Lobster Farce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup boiled lobster meat
- 1/2 cup milk or cream
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3/4 tablespoon flour
- 1 tablespoon bread crumbs
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1-6 teaspoon paprika
- lobster shells

## Directions

1. Put butter in pan
2. When melted stir in flour and milk or cream
3. Let boil
4. Remove from fire
5. Add lobster meat cut in pieces
6. Fill shells from which the lobster meat was taken with farce
7. Cover with buttered crumbs
8. Brown in oven
9. Arrange on bed of parsley

title: Lobster Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One can of lobster
- One head of lettuce
- 1/2 cup of dressing

## Directions

1. Cut lobster quite fine (not chop)
2. Break the leaves from a head of lettuce, let stand in a pan of ice cold water till crisp
3. Lay the leaves on a board and shave fine
4. Just before serving mix the lettuce, lobster and half a cup of dressing together lightly with a fork
5. Arrange some lettuce leaves on a dish and put the mixture in the centre

title: Love Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 3/4 teaspoon cloves
- 1 cup raisins

## Directions

1. Make base with 1 cup sugar, 3/4 cup butter, 2 eggs, 1 cup sweet milk, 3 teaspoons baking powder, and 2 1/2 cups flour
2. Take out for two layers
3. Add to third layer 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, 3/4 teaspoon cloves, and 1 cup raisins
4. Put third layer between an icing

title: Love Knots
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 4 tablespoons sweet cream
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- a pinch salt
- flour to knead very hard

## Directions

1. Mix egg, cream, sugar, and salt with flour to knead very hard
2. Roll out, cut in strips
3. Tie each one in two knots
4. Lay in hot lard
5. Sprinkle with white sugar while hot

title: Lunch Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 teaspoon saleratus dissolved in 1 cup sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Lunch Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. flour
- 2 heaping tablespoonfuls butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 cup currants
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- baking powder

## Directions

1. Into flour rub butter.
2. Add sugar, baking powder and currants.
3. Beat eggs, add to them one cupful of milk and stir into the dry mixture, adding more milk, if it is necessary, to mix to a soft dough.
4. Roll out as for biscuits; cut into three-inch squares.
5. Rub the top of each with a mixture of milk and sugar.
6. Bake in a hot oven.
7. Split, butter and serve while hot.

title: Lunch Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. flour
- 2 heaping tablespoonfuls butter
- 0.5 cup sugar
- 0.5 cup currants
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk

## Directions

1. Into flour rub butter
2. Add sugar, baking powder and currants
3. Beat eggs, add to them one cupful of milk and stir into the dry mixture, adding more milk, if it is necessary, to mix to a soft dough
4. Roll out as for biscuits
5. cut into three-inch squares
6. rub the top of each with a mixture of milk

title: Lyonnaise Potatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half tablespoonfuls of butter
- a scant tablespoonful of chopped onion
- two cupfuls of cold boiled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- a tablespoonful of chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put one and a half tablespoonfuls of butter in a frying pan.
2. When melted add a scant tablespoonful of chopped onion; let it slightly color.
3. Add two cupfuls of cold boiled potatoes cut into dice.
4. Stir until the potato has absorbed all the butter, and become slightly browned.
5. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and a tablespoonful of chopped parsley.
6. Mix well, and serve very hot.

title: Macaroni A La Italian
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound macaroni
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 3 cups cooked macaroni
- 1/8 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper
- 1 cup stock
- 1 cup tomato pulp
- 1 cup grated cheese

## Directions

1. Make a sauce of flour, butter, tomato and stock
2. Add macaroni cooked until tender
3. Add cheese

title: Macaroni and Cheese
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups macaroni (boiled)
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls grated cheese
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 1/4 teaspoonful cayenne

## Directions

1. Put butter and flour in saucepan, stir until well blended; add milk, stir until it comes to a boil, add salt, pepper and cheese; stir until cheese melts; turn boiled macaroni into the sauce; stir well, turn into a dish, sprinkle with fine bread-crumbs and brown in oven.

title: Macaroni and Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup macaroni
- 1 cup grated cheese
- ⅜ cup dry bread crumbs
- 2 cups cream sauce
- 6 cups water
- 1½ teaspoons salt

## Directions

1. Put 6 cups water and 1½ teaspoons salt on to boil
2. Break macaroni into inch pieces
3. When water boils hard, drop in macaroni
4. Keep boiling hard till macaroni is tender
5. Grate cheese and make white sauce
6. When macaroni is tender, drain through a colander and put into a baking dish
7. Put cheese and sauce with the macaroni
8. Cover with crumbs
9. Bake for about 15 minutes

title: Macaroni and Cheese (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound of macaroni broken an inch in length
- water
- salt
- grated cheese
- pepper
- butter
- 1 cup sweet cream

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan about ½ pound of macaroni broken an inch in length with water sufficient to boil tender
2. Add a little salt
3. When done, drain and put into a well-buttered dish in layers
4. Sprinkle grated cheese very liberally over each layer
5. Use pepper, salt and butter
6. When the dish is full pour over one cup sweet cream
7. Bake in a moderate oven about 25 minutes
8. Serve hot

title: Macaroni and Cheese (Variation 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ pound macaroni broken in small pieces
- 1 quart of salt water
- cheese broken in small pieces
- rolled bread or cracker crumbs
- butter
- milk

## Directions

1. Cook ¾ pound macaroni broken in small pieces about 20 minutes in a quart of salt water
2. Turn into colander and pour cold water over it
3. Let drain
4. Put layer of cheese (broken in small pieces) in bottom of baking dish
5. Put a layer of macaroni on top
6. Repeat until dish is full
7. Cover the top with rolled bread or cracker crumbs and little pieces of butter
8. Pour over the whole enough heated milk (about 2 cups) to moisten
9. Bake until brown

title: Macaroni and Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 package macaroni
- salt
- boiling water
- 1/2 can tomatoes
- salt and pepper
- butter
- 1 teacup grated cheese

## Directions

1. Put macaroni in salt boiling water
2. Boil for 3 1/2 hours
3. Drain and pour cold water over it
4. Drain again
5. Add 1/2 can tomatoes
6. Season with salt and pepper and butter
7. Boil all together
8. Remove from stove and add grated cheese

title: Macaroni Cheese Patties
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce macaroni
- 1 tablespoon cream
- 1 tablespoon grated cheese
- salt and pepper
- pie crust
- bread crumbs
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Boil macaroni in quickly boiling water until tender
2. Drain and cut into small pieces
3. Put into a pan and mix with cream, grated cheese, salt and pepper
4. Line patty pans with pie crust and fill with macaroni mixture
5. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and grated cheese
6. Bake until lightly browned

title: Macaroni Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 1 cup milk
- 1 yolk of egg
- 2 cups chopped cooked macaroni
- 2 tablespoons cheese
- pepper and salt to taste
- egg and crumbs for rolling
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare a white sauce
2. Add butter, flour, and milk together
3. Add macaroni, cheese, yolk of egg, pepper and salt
4. Mix thoroughly
5. Cool
6. Make into croquettes
7. Roll in egg and crumbs
8. Fry
9. Serve with tomato sauce

title: Macaroni Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound macaroni
- 2 cups white sauce
- 1 egg
- 1 pound dried beef
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Boil macaroni until tender
2. Drain
3. Tear beef into small pieces
4. Add egg to sauce
5. Put in layers in buttered mould
6. Cover and steam 2 hours
7. Turn out and serve with tomato sauce

title: Macaroni Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten long sticks of macaroni
- boiling water
- a quart of milk
- a cup of sugar
- a slice of butter
- three eggs
- vanilla
- oil of cloves
- oil of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Break macaroni into pieces an inch long or less.
2. Throw into a saucepan with plenty of boiling water and cook for twenty minutes.
3. Drain away the water and boil again more slowly for another twenty minutes in a quart of milk with a cup of sugar and a slice of butter.
4. Turn into a pudding dish and allow to cool.
5. Beat in three eggs, flavor with vanilla, oil of cloves or oil of cinnamon, and bake slowly.
6. Or use four eggs, keeping two whites to be beaten stiffly for the top.
7. Brown in the oven for a minute or two.

title: Macaroni Rarebit
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled macaroni
- 1 cup grated cheese
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 3 eggs
- mustard
- paprika
- 3/4 cup cream
- toast

## Directions

1. Melt butter
2. Add cheese and seasoning
3. Cook over hot water
4. When melted add macaroni, beaten egg diluted with cream
5. Cook until slightly thickened
6. Serve on toast

title: Macaroni Soup with Stock, Unthickened
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart stock
- seasonings
- 1/2 cup macaroni

## Directions

1. Cook macaroni until tender in boiling salted water
2. Cut in 1/2 inch pieces
3. Add to seasoned stock

title: Macaroni Soup with Stock, Unthickened
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart stock
- seasonings
- 1/2 cup macaroni cut in 1/2 inch pieces
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook macaroni until tender in boiling salted water
2. Add to seasoned stock

title: Macaroons
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two eggs
- one cup sugar
- two cupfuls rolled oats
- two teaspoonfuls baking powder
- ratafia flavoring

## Directions

1. Drop in a pan with spoon
2. Bake

title: Maccassar Oil for the Hair
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Alkanet root chippings, quarter of an ounce
- Sweet oil, eight ounces
- Tincture of cantharides, sixty drops
- Oil of rose, ten drops
- Oil of neroli, sixty drops
- Oil of lemon, sixty drops

## Directions

1. Tie quarter of an ounce of alkanet root chippings in coarse muslin
2. Put it in a bottle containing eight ounces of sweet oil
3. Cover to keep out dust and let stand several days
4. Add sixty drops of tincture of cantharides, ten drops of oil of rose, and sixty drops each of oil of neroli and lemon
5. Let it stand one week and it will be ready to use

title: Maccassar Oil for the Hair (Second Recipe)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Strong sage tea, one pint
- Bay rum, one pint
- Tincture of cantharides, quarter of an ounce
- Castor oil, one ounce
- Rose or other perfume, one teaspoonful

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Shake well before applying to the hair, as the oil will not mix

title: Macdonald Institute Recipe for Baking Powder Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 level cups flour
- 1 level teaspoon salt
- 2 level tablespoons shortening
- 2 1/2 level tablespoons baking powder
- about 1 1/2 cups milk

## Directions

1. Place the board, mixing knife, rolling pin, cutter, and pan ready
2. See that the oven is hot
3. Sift the salt and flour into a bowl
4. Rub the shortening into it with the fingers
5. Sift in the baking powder, and mix well
6. Mix into a soft dough with the milk, using a broad-bladed, flexible knife, in order to cut and mix it quickly
7. Flour the board lightly, turn the dough out, and roll it round to coat it with flour
8. Knead just enough to make the dough smooth
9. Roll out about an inch thick, and cut into small biscuits
10. Place them in the pan, wet the tops only with milk or water, and bake in a hot oven about 20 minutes

title: Made In Canada Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/4 cup milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons baking powder
- Butter size of a large egg
- Sugar
- Sweet cream, sweetened
- A little vanilla
- Chopped walnuts

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar and mix in the usual way
2. Bake in a large flat tin
3. When cold, cut in small squares
4. For icing: Cream butter size of a large egg
5. Add as much sugar as will cream in well
6. Add sweet cream, sweetened and a little vanilla, until proper stiffness has been obtained
7. Ice the squares and roll in chopped walnuts

title: Madeira Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 1/2 pounds flour
- 1 pound castor sugar
- grated rind of 1 lemon
- 2 ounces candied peel
- 5 or 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the butter and sugar into a clean pan
2. Add the grated rind of 1 lemon and with the hand beat it up to a very light cream
3. Add in the eggs 1 at a time, and beat well after each addition of eggs
4. When all are in and the batter is very light and creamy, add the flour
5. Stir it in lightly with the hand
6. When well mixed, divide the mixture equally into 3 or 4 round tins, papered at the bottoms and sides with buttered white paper
7. Dust sugar over from a sifter

title: Madeira Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1½ pounds flour
- 1 pound castor sugar
- grated rind of 1 lemon
- 2 ounces candied peel
- 5 or 6 eggs
- citron peel

## Directions

1. Put the butter and sugar into a clean pan, add the grated rind of 1 lemon and with the hand beat it up to a very light cream
2. Add in the eggs 1 at a time, and beat well after each addition of eggs
3. When all are in and the batter is very light and creamy, add the flour
4. Stir it in lightly with the hand, and when well mixed, divide the mixture equally into 3 or 4 round tins, papered at the bottoms and sides with buttered white paper
5. Dust sugar over from a dredger or shaker, and lay 2 very thin slices of citron peel on top
6. Bake in a moderate oven for about 1 or 1¼ hours

title: Madeira Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- Their weight in flour, butter and sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful baking-powder
- A little vanilla
- 1 tablespoonful milk

## Directions

1. Cream the butter
2. Then add the sugar
3. then the eggs one at a time
4. flavor and beat in the flour and baking powder
5. If too stiff add one tablespoonful milk
6. Bake in small moulds, half full, for fifteen or twenty minutes
7. For sauce: One and one-half tablespoonfuls butter, four tablespoonfuls sugar, one egg; beat all together until light
8. Then add boiling water just before serving, and flavor

title: Making Coffee
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee beans
- boiling water

## Directions

1. The coffee should be roasted just before use; as if kept more than one day after roasting there is a decided loss of aroma.
2. The simplest way of roasting coffee is in an enamelled frying-pan. Roast the beans over a mild, smokeless fire until the beans turn a rich brown color, not black. The beans must be constantly stirred and turned, or they will burn.
3. Take one large tablespoonful of coffee powder for each cup of coffee required.
4. Put the powder into a jug and pour boiling water over it in the proportion of half a cupful of water to each tablespoonful of coffee powder. The water must be at full boiling point.
5. Let the coffee stand in the jug for half an hour, and then strain through a linen or cotton bag (muslin is too thin) into the coffee-pot.

title: Malaga Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- nut meats
- Malaga grapes

## Directions

1. Cut grapes in halves and seed them
2. Cover with dressing

title: Manitoba Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen cucumbers
- 1 dozen onions
- weak brine
- 1 pint vinegar
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 dessertspoon tumeric
- 1 dessertspoon curry powder

## Directions

1. Pare and slice cucumbers in ¼-inch rings
2. Slice onions in equal quantity
3. Let stand for 24 hours in weak brine
4. Make dressing of 1 pint vinegar, 2 cups brown sugar, 1 tablespoon mustard, 1 dessertspoon tumeric, 1 dessertspoon curry powder
5. Dissolve in cold vinegar
6. Heat together until boiling and well blended
7. Put in pickles that have been well drained
8. Allow to come to a scald
9. Bottle hot

title: Manitoba Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen cucumbers (table size)
- 1 dozen onions or equal quantity
- weak brine
- 1 pint vinegar
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 dessertspoon turmeric
- 1 dessertspoon curry powder

## Directions

1. Pare and slice cucumbers in 1/4-inch rings
2. Let cucumbers and onions stand for 24 hours in weak brine
3. Dissolve 1 tablespoon mustard, 1 dessertspoon turmeric, and 1 dessertspoon curry powder in cold vinegar
4. Heat 1 pint vinegar and 2 cups brown sugar together until boiling and well blended
5. Add the spice mixture to the vinegar and sugar
6. Well drain the pickles and add them to the boiling dressing
7. Allow to come to a scald
8. Bottle hot

title: Maple Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- maple sugar
- 1/2 cupful of cold water

## Directions

1. Proceed as in preceding recipe, using maple sugar instead of the plain white sugar

title: Maple Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups brown sugar
- 3/4 cup milk
- butter size of walnut

## Directions

1. Boil until it forms a soft ball when put in water
2. Take it off and beat well

title: Maple Cream (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 1/2 pound almonds, blanched and cut in pieces
- 1.5 pint sweet cream

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and cream together until, when dropped into a little cold water, it forms into a firm ball
2. Add nuts
3. Beat until cold, or firm enough to spread on buttered tins

title: Maple Cream (Version 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup rich milk or cream
- piece butter size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Boil 5 minutes
2. Then add piece butter size of a walnut

title: Maple Cream 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 1 pound almonds, blanched and cut in pieces
- 3/4 pint sweet cream

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and cream together until, when dropped into a little cold water, it forms into a firm ball
2. Add nuts
3. Beat until cold, or firm enough to spread on buttered tins

title: Maple Cream 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup rich milk or cream
- piece butter size of a walnut
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 cup chopped walnut meats

## Directions

1. Boil 5 minutes
2. Add piece butter size of a walnut, 1 teaspoon vanilla, and chopped walnut meats
3. Beat mixture till thick and creamy
4. Turn in buttered tins and cool

title: Maple Cream Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three cups of brown sugar
- Half a cup of cream or milk
- A dessertspoonful of butter
- A tablespoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. When it comes to the boil add a dessertspoonful of butter
2. Let it boil for fifteen minutes, stirring just before taking off the fire
3. Put in tablespoonful of vanilla
4. Remove from the fire
5. Beat briskly for five minutes
6. Butter your plates

title: Maple Cream Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups brown sugar
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 1.5 tablespoons butter
- 3/4 teaspoon vanilla
- pinch salt

## Directions

1. Boil gently without stirring until it reaches the soft ball stage in cold water
2. When nearly done add butter
3. When done set away for 10 minutes
4. Beat until creamy
5. Just before cold pour into a buttered dish

title: Maple Cream Fudge
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1/2 cup cream or milk
- 1 cup hickorynut meats or 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Let boil without stirring for 20 minutes, or until it threads when held up with the spoon
2. Take off and beat for a few minutes, until it begins to harden
3. Add 1 cup hickorynut meats, or 1 teaspoon vanilla, to flavor
4. Pour out on a buttered pie tin or platter

title: Maple Cream Icing
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups sugar
- 6 tablespoons milk
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Boil
2. Stir until cold

title: Maple Cream Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups maple syrup
- 2 cups milk
- 2 eggs
- 3 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 cup chopped walnuts

## Directions

1. Scald milk and add to hot syrup.
2. Add cornstarch.
3. Add eggs, well whipped.
4. Remove from fire and add nuts.
5. Place in mould on ice and serve with whipped cream.

title: Maple Fudge
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup maple syrup
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- nuts

## Directions

1. Cook until a little dropped in cold water will thicken
2. Remove from fire and when nearly cold, stir in a few nuts
3. Pour into a square pan and cut in squares

title: Maple Mound
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of maple syrup
- One pint of cream
- Yolks of four eggs

## Directions

1. Boil syrup five minutes
2. Remove from stove and add beaten yolks of eggs
3. When cold stir in cream, which has been whipped
4. Pour into mould
5. Pack in ice and salt for three hours

title: Maple Mousse
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 1 cup maple syrup
- 1 pt. cream

## Directions

1. Whip the cream very stiff
2. Beat the eggs and place them in a double boiler
3. Pour in the maple syrup and stir constantly until the mixture gathers on the spoon
4. Take off the stove and beat till cold
5. Stir into the whipped cream
6. Put in a freezer that has been previously packed in ice and salt
7. Let stand four hours

title: Maple Mousse
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup maple syrup
- 1 pint whipped cream
- yolks of 4 eggs or 2 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the syrup and pour it over the well-beaten eggs
2. When cold, add to it the whipped cream
3. Pour into a covered dish and pack in ice and salt for 3 or 4 hours, using plenty salt

title: Maple Parfait
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 eggs (yolks)
- a large cupful of maple syrup
- 1 quart of cream

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks of ten eggs very light, and add a large cupful of maple syrup.
2. Put it over the fire, stir till it thickens, then take off and beat till cold.
3. Add quickly a quart of cream beaten till it is perfectly stiff.
4. Pour into a two-quart melon mould.
5. Pack in ice and salt for five or six hours.

title: Maple Sugar Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 4 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup maple syrup
- White of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Bake in layers
2. Maple syrup boiled to a soft wax and poured over the white of egg beaten to a stiff froth
3. Beat thoroughly

title: Maple Sugar Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds maple sugar
- 1 quart rich milk
- part cream

## Directions

1. Break maple sugar into small pieces
2. Put it in a saucepan with a quart of rich milk (part cream is better)
3. Let this boil until it reaches the stage where it hardens in cold water
4. Pour it into pans
5. Mark it in squares as you would taffy or caramels

title: Maple Sugar Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. maple sugar
- 1 pt. milk
- 1 tablespoonful butter

## Directions

1. Break the sugar into small pieces and put it into a double boiler with the milk
2. Put it on the stove and cook until the sugar melts
3. Set the inner vessel of the double boiler directly on the stove and boil, stirring constantly, until the syrup reaches the stage where a little dropped in cold water becomes brittle
4. Add your butter then, and when this is melted turn the syrup into greased pans
5. As it cools, mark it off in squares with a knife

title: Maple Sugar Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of maple sugar
- A quart of rich milk

## Directions

1. Break maple sugar into small pieces
2. Put it in a saucepan with a quart of rich milk — part cream is better
3. Let this boil until it reaches the stage where it hardens in cold water
4. Pour it into pans, and mark it in

title: Maple Sugar Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large cups maple sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 2 tablespoons sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- flour enough to roll soft

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and roll soft and bake.

title: Maple Sugar Cream Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup thick cream
- 2 cups maple sugar
- 1/4 cup walnut meats

## Directions

1. To cream add maple sugar cut in small pieces
2. Boil, stirring always one way until when tried in cold water the mixture adheres to spoon
3. Remove from fire and stir the opposite way until mixture gets quite thick
4. Add chopped walnuts and pour out on buttered platters
5. When cold cut in small squares

title: Maple Sugar Frosting
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound soft maple sugar
- 1/4 to 1/2 cup boiling water
- whites of eggs, beaten

## Directions

1. Add 1/4 to 1/2 cup boiling water to 1 pound soft maple sugar and stir occasionally until sugar is dissolved
2. Boil without stirring until syrup will thread when dropped from tin spoon
3. Pour gradually on beaten whites, beating mixture continually
4. Continue beating until of right consistency to spread

title: Maple Syrup Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/4 cups maple syrup
- 3 eggs
- pie crust

## Directions

1. Put crust on tin
2. Beat together syrup and eggs
3. Put in pie and bake in a moderate oven

title: Maple Syrup Pie (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup maple syrup
- 1 cup water
- 2 eggs
- 2 small tablespoons flour
- butter size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Bake the crust
2. Cook custard in double boiler

title: Maple Syrup Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¾ cups maple syrup
- 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Cook until thick
2. Fill tart shells already cooked
3. Cover with whipped cream or the meringue of whites of eggs

title: Maple Walnut
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup powdered sugar
- 1/2 cup maple syrup
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup walnuts, broken in pieces
- salt

## Directions

1. Heat sugar, maple syrup, milk, and butter and let boil until a soft ball may be made when mixture is tried in water
2. Remove from fire and beat until creamy
3. Add walnuts broken in pieces with a little salt

title: Marble Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ cup butter
- ¾ cup sugar
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- whites of 2 eggs
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- ¾ cup butter
- ½ cup brown sugar
- ¾ cup molasses
- ¾ cup milk
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 1¾ cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- cloves
- cinnamon
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Drop the white and dark in alternate spoonfuls in a loaf tin

title: Marble Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ cup butter
- 1 cup white sugar
- whites of 3 eggs
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- 1½ teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups of flour
- ¾ cup brown sugar
- ¾ cup butter
- yolks of 3 eggs
- ¾ cup molasses
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon allspice
- 1¾ teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- red sugar

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar together for light part
2. Cream brown sugar and butter together for dark part
3. By putting red sugar in ½ the light part, 3 colors may be had

title: Marble Cake 3
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 4 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar
- ¾ cup butter
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla or lemon
- 2¾ cups sifted flour
- yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ¾ cup molasses
- ¾ cup butter
- ¾ cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon mace
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1¾ cups sifted flour

## Directions

1. Put it in the cake dish alternately, first one part and then the other

title: Marble Cake 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¾ cups white sugar
- ¾ cup butter
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- ¾ teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- whites of 4 eggs
- 2¾ cups flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ¾ cup molasses
- ¾ cup butter
- ¾ cup sour milk
- ¾ teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 2¾ cups flour
- yolks of 4 eggs
- ½ tablespoon each of cloves
- ½ tablespoon allspice
- ½ tablespoon cinnamon
- ½ tablespoon ground nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat and mix light part
2. Beat and mix dark part
3. When each part is ready, drop a spoon of dark, then a spoon of light, over the bottom of the pan, in which it is to be baked
4. Proceed to fill up the pan, dropping the light upon the dark as you continue with the different layers

title: Marguerite Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ½ cup butter
- ½ cup buttermilk
- ½ cup molasses
- ½ teaspoon allspice
- ½ teaspoon cinnamon
- ½ teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- ¾ teaspoon butter
- water
- 1 cup seeded raisins

## Directions

1. Bake in 2 layers
2. For filling and icing: boil 1 cup granulated sugar, ¾ teaspoon butter, and water till it strings
3. Add 1 cup seeded raisins cut up fine
4. Partly cool, then spread some between the layers, and put the rest on the top of cake

title: Marguerites
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup water
- marshmallows
- whites of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons shredded cocoanut
- 1/4 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 cup chopped walnuts
- saltines

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water until it threads
2. Remove to back of range and drop in marshmallows cut in pieces
3. Let stand to dissolve, then gradually pour on to the whites of 2 eggs beaten until foamy
4. Add 2 tablespoons shredded cocoanut and 1/4 teaspoon vanilla
5. When partially cool add 1 cup chopped walnuts
6. Spread on saltines and brown slightly in hot oven

title: Marmalade Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls marmalade
- 3 cups breadcrumbs
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup suet
- Pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Steam and serve with sauce

title: Marmalade Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. suet
- 1 lb. breadcrumbs
- 1 lb. sugar
- 2 oz. flour or ground rice
- 1 tablespoonful milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls orange marmalade
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoonful baking-powder

## Directions

1. Finely chop the suet and put it in a bowl with the flour, sugar, breadcrumbs and baking powder
2. Mix well together
3. Beat the eggs until light, then beat into it the milk and marmalade
4. Mix all together
5. Pour into a well-greased mould
6. Twist over a sheet of paper and steam four hours

title: Marmalade Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- flour equal in weight to 2 eggs
- sugar equal in weight to 2 eggs
- butter equal in weight to 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons marmalade
- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
- 2 cups milk
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons marmalade

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar together.
2. Lightly stir in other ingredients.
3. Set in a buttered basin and steam 2 hours.
4. Turn out to serve and pour marmalade sauce round.
5. For sauce: boil milk with cornstarch, sugar and marmalade.

title: Marrowball Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece marrow, size of egg
- 1 cup grated bread crumbs
- yolk of 1 egg
- salt
- nutmeg
- cold water
- boiling stock

## Directions

1. Let piece of marrow melt slowly
2. When slightly cooled add grated bread crumbs, egg yolk, salt, and nutmeg
3. Mix well with little cold water
4. Take a teaspoonful and drop into boiling stock
5. Do not cover the kettle
6. If the ball falls apart add more bread crumbs
7. Put the mixture in by the spoonful and let boil slowly three minutes

title: Marshmallow Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 pound marshmallows
- 2 tablespoons hot water
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Put sugar and milk in saucepan and heat slowly to boiling point without stirring
2. Boil 6 minutes
3. Break marshmallows in pieces and melt in double boiler
4. Add hot water and cook until mixture is smooth
5. Add hot syrup gradually, stirring constantly
6. Beat until cool enough to spread
7. Add vanilla
8. This may be used for frosting and filling

title: Marshmallows
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 package granulated gelatine
- 12 tablespoons cold water
- 4 cups granulated sugar
- 16 tablespoons water
- 3 teaspoons powdered sugar
- 1 cornstarch

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 package granulated gelatine in 12 tablespoons cold water, letting stand while sugar boils
2. Boil 4 cups granulated sugar, moistened with 16 tablespoons water, until it threads
3. Pour hot liquid over gelatine and beat 20 to 25 minutes, having a pan sprinkled with 3 teaspoons powdered sugar and 1 cornstarch
4. Let stand for 1 day and night
5. Cut in strips 3 inches long 1 inch wide

title: Marshmallows 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons Knox's gelatine (slightly rounded)
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- speck salt
- 8 tablespoons cold water
- 3/4 cup water
- flavoring to taste

## Directions

1. Soak the gelatine in 8 tablespoons cold water
2. Heat the sugar with 3/4 cup of water until dissolved
3. Add gelatine to syrup and just bring it to the boil
4. Take off the stove and let it stand in a bowl until partially cool
5. Add salt and flavoring
6. Beat with a whip until soft, then with a large spoon, until only soft enough to settle into a sheet
7. Dust granite pans thickly with fine powdered sugar
8. Pour in the candy about 1/2 inch deep, and set to cool until it will not come off on the finger
9. Turn out on powdered paper
10. Cut in cubes and roll in sugar

title: Martha Washington Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 oz. blanched almonds
- 4 oz. powdered sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 oz. macaroons
- 2 oz. melted butter
- 1/2 gill rum
- 1/2 saltspoonful ground cinnamon
- 6 drops orange flower water

## Directions

1. Pound almonds in a mortar with two ounces of powdered sugar, adding gradually one raw egg
2. When well pounded add two ounces more of sugar, melted butter, rum, ground cinnamon, orange flower water and break in another egg
3. Pound for five minutes and add well-pounded macaroons
4. Line a pie plate with good paste
5. Pour in the preparation and bake
6. Decorate with candied fruits

title: Maryland Biscuit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two sifters of flour
- Two tablespoons of lard
- One saltspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Mix to as stiff a dough as possible.
2. Beat with sadiron or rolling pin until soft and blistering.
3. Cut out and press the knuckles into each biscuit.
4. Bake in a quick oven.

title: Maryland Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- two tender spring chickens
- salt
- pepper
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- clarified butter
- cream sauce
- thin boiled bacon
- small corn fritters

## Directions

1. Split chicken in half, detach the legs and wings
2. Lay all on a plate, and season with salt and pepper
3. Dip the pieces in beaten egg, and afterwards in bread crumbs
4. Place them in a buttered pan, pour an ounce of clarified butter over, and roast in the oven about twenty minutes
5. Pour half a pint of cream sauce on a serving dish, and arrange the fowl on it
6. Alternate with slices of thin boiled bacon, and small corn fritters

title: Mashed Chestnuts
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- a quart of large French chestnuts
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- stock
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Shell a quart of large French chestnuts.
2. Put them in hot water and boil until the skins are softened.
3. Drain off the water and remove the skins.
4. Replace the blanched chestnuts in water, and boil until soft.
5. Take out a few at a time, and press them through a colander or a potato press.
6. Season the mashed chestnuts with a tablespoonful of butter, a teaspoonful of salt, and a quarter of a teaspoonful of pepper.
7. Some cooks add a tablespoonful of chopped parsley, and moisten it with a little stock.
8. Some add also a few breadcrumbs.
9. The dressing is best seasoned only with butter, salt, and pepper.

title: Mashed Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup rich milk

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes and leave in cold water 1/2 hour
2. Put in a steamer and cook until tender
3. Have ready a granite dish and put your potatoes in it
4. Mash well and fine
5. Season with salt, 1 tablespoon butter, and 1 cup rich milk
6. Mix together well
7. If liked, put in the oven to brown

title: Mashed Turnips
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- water
- salt
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash, pare, cut in slices
2. Cook in boiling water salted until soft (30-45 minutes)
3. Drain
4. Mash
5. Season with butter, salt and pepper

title: May Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- flour equal to weight of 2 eggs
- butter equal to weight of 1 egg
- sugar equal to weight of 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons jam
- 1/2 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Beat eggs well
2. Add flour, butter and sugar to weight
3. Add 2 tablespoons jam
4. Add 1/2 teaspoon soda
5. Steam 3/4 of an hour

title: May's Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. boiling water
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 4 tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- 2 eggs, beaten separately
- 2 cups sugar

## Directions

1. Mix the cornstarch with a little cold water, add the lemonjuice.
2. Have the water boiling on the fire; put in the sugar and stir in the cornstarch.
3. When it has well thickened add the whites of the eggs beaten very stiff.
4. Stir well and pour into a mould.
5. The yolks of the eggs can be used to make a custard, with one pint of milk and one small tablespoonful of cornstarch; sugar to taste.
6. Serve cold.

title: Mayonnaise
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of three eggs
- one gill of best Lucca oil
- two tablespoons vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Stir yolks and salt in bowl with wooden or silver spoon
2. Drop in oil and vinegar alternately in small quantities
3. Always stir vigorously
4. Take half an hour to it

title: Mayonnaise
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/2 cup malt vinegar
- 1/2 teaspoonful cayenne
- 2 teaspoonfuls mixed mustard
- 1/2 cup butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 2 teaspoonfuls salt
- 2 cups cream
- 4 tablespoonfuls tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the eggs, vinegar and dry ingredients together in a double boiler till they form a thick custard.
2. When cold add two cups cream and four tablespoons tarragon vinegar added drop by drop.
3. Beat for ten minutes.
4. Will keep a fortnight or longer in a cool place.

title: Mayonnaise
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- Yolk of 1 egg
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- Dash of cayenne
- 1 cup olive oil
- three-quarters cup of olive oil
- two tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- one-half cup of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Mix well.
2. Then add drop by drop three-quarters cup of olive oil, beating constantly during mixing.
