## Directions

1. Boil the oatmeal in water for one hour
2. Add milk; set aside until cold
3. Then place in bowl, sift together flour, salt, and powder, and add
4. Mix together smoothly and deftly
5. Bake in greased tin forty-five minutes, protected with paper twenty minutes

title: Oatmeal Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rolled oats
- 1 quart hot water
- ⅜ cup molasses
- salt
- yeast cake
- 1 quart flour

## Directions

1. Put rolled oats to soak in 1 quart hot water at noon
2. Let stand until night
3. Add ⅜ cup molasses, salt, yeast cake, 1 quart flour
4. This makes 2 loaves

title: Oatmeal Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- ¼ cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 teaspoon soda in 1 cup boiling water
- 1 cup flour
- 2 cups oatmeal
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Bake in a slow oven

title: Oatmeal Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup lard
- 4 tablespoons sour milk
- ⅜ teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- 1 cup seeded and chopped raisins
- 2 cups flour
- 2 cups oatmeal

## Directions

1. Drop on buttered tins (tart tins)

title: Oatmeal Cakes Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ cups rolled oats
- 3½ cup flour
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 2 tablespoons lard
- 1 cup molasses
- ¾ cup milk
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- ½ teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon soda
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Mix all well together and drop on buttered pan
2. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Oatmeal Cookies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three cups oatmeal
- Two cups flour
- One-half cup brown sugar
- Three-quarter cup butter
- One-half teaspoonful soda dissolved in one-quarter cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Bake in oven until a light brown.

title: Oatmeal Cookies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups oatmeal
- 2 cups flour
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 teaspoonful soda dissolved in 1/4 cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Bake in oven until a light brown.

title: Oatmeal Cookies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three cups oatmeal
- three cups flour
- one cup boiling water
- one cup melted lard
- one scant teaspoon soda
- one cup sugar

## Directions

1. Roll very thin

title: Oatmeal Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups oatmeal
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup butter and lard
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 1 pound of dates cooked with 1 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Put all in pan but sour milk and soda, mix well, then add milk and soda; roll out thin.
2. Have 1 pound of dates cooked with 1 cup sugar, put a few dates in centre of cake, then place another thin rolled cake on top of dates, press down the top cake good around edges, then bake.

title: Oatmeal Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 2 cups oatmeal
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup butter and lard mixed
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Oatmeal Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup lard and butter mixed
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 2 eggs
- 7 teaspoons sour cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 3/4 teaspoon nutmeg
- 2 cups flour
- a little salt
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1/2 cup chopped nuts

## Directions

1. Do not bake too quickly.

title: Oatmeal Cookies 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 cup shortening
- 1 cup hot water
- 1/2 teaspoon soda in the water
- a good pinch salt
- oatmeal enough to make a soft dough
- flour to stiffen enough to roll very thin

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and roll very thin and bake.

title: Oatmeal Drop Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups oatmeal
- 2 cups flour
- 2 cups brown sugar
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup melted butter
- 2 eggs well beaten
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 5 teaspoons sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix oatmeal, flour, brown sugar and cinnamon together dry
2. Add 1 cup melted butter, 2 eggs well beaten, 1 teaspoon soda, 5 teaspoons sour milk
3. Drop in small pieces on a pan
4. Bake in a hot oven

title: Oatmeal Drop Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup raisins
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup cream
- 1 cup butter
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 4 cups oatmeal
- enough flour to drop on the bottom of pan

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Drop on the bottom of pan
3. Do not roll
4. Bake

title: Oatmeal Drop Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup lard
- 4 cups flour
- 4 1/2 cups oatmeal
- 2 cups raisins
- 10 tablespoons sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon soda dissolved in the milk
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix well
2. Drop a teaspoonful for each cookie, leaving plenty of room to spread
3. Bake

title: Oatmeal Fruit Drop Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon golden syrup
- 3/4 cup butter
- 4 tablespoons milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- spices to suit taste
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1 cup nut meats
- 3 small cups oatmeal (rolled oats)
- 2 good cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Drop on baking sheet
3. Bake

title: Oatmeal Gems
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup oatmeal
- 1 cup water
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 cup flour
- 2/3 tablespoonfuls soda
- 1/2 tablespoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar

## Directions

1. Soak oatmeal over night in one cupful of water
2. In the morning, add sour milk, flour, soda, salt, butter, and sugar
3. Mix, and bake in hot, well-buttered gem-pans
4. If too moist, add a little more flour

title: Oatmeal Gems
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 2 1/2 cups granulated oatmeal
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 teaspoon baking soda in 1/2 cup boiling water

## Directions

1. cut into squares
2. when done place 2 together with jelly or other filling between

title: Oatmeal Gems 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rolled oats
- 1 3/2 cups sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. soak over night

title: Oatmeal Ginger Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups oatmeal
- 2 cups flour
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 3/4 cups yellow sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 1 tablespoon water

## Directions

1. Mix molasses, sugar and butter
2. Dissolve soda in 1 tablespoon water
3. Add all together
4. Roll thin and bake slowly

title: Oatmeal Gruel
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- four tablespoonfuls of the best grits (oatmeal coarsely ground)
- one pint of boiling water
- butter
- wine
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Put four tablespoonfuls of the best grits (oatmeal coarsely ground) into a pint of boiling water
2. Let it boil gently and stir it often, till it becomes as thick as you wish it
3. Strain it, and add to it while warm, butter, wine, nutmeg, or whatever is thought proper to flavor it
4. Salt to taste
5. If you make the gruel of fine oatmeal, sift it, mix it first to a thick batter with a little cold water, and then put it into the sauce-pan of boiling water
6. Stir it all the time it is boiling, lifting the spoon gently up and down, and letting the gruel fall slowly back again into the pan

title: Oatmeal Haggis
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups granulated oatmeal
- 1 cup flour
- 1 level teaspoon of black pepper
- 1 level tablespoon salt
- 1 cup suet
- 2 medium-sized onions

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly and put in cloth and tie up
2. Leave a little room to swell
3. Boil 2 hours

title: Oatmeal Jumbles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 cups Graham flour
- 1 cup white flour
- 1 cup oatmeal
- 2 teaspoons butter
- 1 cup raisins
- a pinch salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. mix as thick as possible
2. drop in greased tins far apart the size of walnuts

title: Oatmeal Kisses
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup shortening
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup sour milk
- 3 cups oatmeal
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- honey or jam

## Directions

1. Cut in small cookies
2. Put together with honey or jam

title: Oatmeal Macaroons
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 heaping cups rolled oats
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder

## Directions

1. Grease your pan well, and drop in half teaspoonfuls of dough in the pan, leaving room to spread.
2. Bake in hot oven fifteen minutes.

title: Oatmeal Macaroons
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 3 cups rolled oats
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Put tiny drops on a greased pan in moderate oven

title: Oatmeal Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 3 eggs
- 3 1/2 cups oatmeal or rolled oats
- 4 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add eggs and beat
3. Sift in flour, salt and baking powder and beat
4. Stir in rolled oats
5. Drop from teaspoon on greased pan
6. Bake in hot oven till brown

title: Oatmeal Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups oatmeal
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup milk
- 2 eggs
- salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon vanilla
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat whites of eggs to a stiff froth
2. Drop from a teaspoon on a buttered tin
3. Bake in a quick oven

title: Oatmeal Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups sweet milk
- 2 cups oatmeal
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- small teaspoon salt
- 1 egg
- 1 cup flour
- pork drippings

## Directions

1. Soak oatmeal over night in the milk. Combine soaked oatmeal with melted butter, baking powder, salt, egg, and flour. Fry in pork drippings. These are delicious with maple syrup or honey.

title: Oatmeal Parkins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup oatmeal
- 1½ cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- ½ cup butter
- ½ cup sugar
- ⅜ cup molasses
- ¼ teaspoon each of cinnamon, allspice and nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Just before final mixing break 1 egg in the mixture and stir whole together
2. Drop in buttered tins

title: Oatmeal Parkins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup oatmeal
- 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup molasses
- 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoon allspice
- 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Just before final mixing break 1 egg in the mixture and stir whole together and drop in buttered tins

title: Oatmeal Puffs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pt. oatmeal
- 1/2 pt. graham flour
- 1/2 pt. wheat flour
- 1 pt. sweet milk
- 1/2 teaspoonful salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 3 eggs, well beaten
- 1 teaspoonful sugar

## Directions

1. Sift together oatmeal, graham flour, wheat flour, one teaspoonful sugar, one-half teaspoonful salt, two teaspoonfuls baking powder; add well-beaten eggs, one pint sweet milk.
2. Mix into a thin batter, then half fill well-greased gem pans, and bake in hot oven ten to fifteen minutes.
3. Serve hot.

title: Oatmeal Rocks
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 2 cups oatmeal
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 cup chopped raisins

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Oatmeal Wafers
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups oatmeal
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ¼ cup butter
- 1 teaspoonful ground spice
- ½ teaspoonful salt
- Scant teaspoonful soda
- cold water

## Directions

1. Mix the meal and flour and rub butter and sugar well in, add the other ingredients
2. Moisten slightly with a little cold water
3. Add more flour as required, and roll out a very small piece at a time as thin as possible
4. To get them thin the mixture must not be too soft
5. Cut with a sharp fluted tin cutter
6. Bake in a hot oven

title: Oatmeal Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 2 cups rolled oats
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 teaspoon flour

## Directions

1. Mix 1 teaspoon baking powder in 1 teaspoon of flour, drop in small quantities on greased pan

title: Oatmeal Wafers 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 3 cups oatmeal
- 1 cup brown sugar
- a pinch of salt
- 1 small teaspoon soda
- 1/4 cup butter or lard
- cold water

## Directions

1. Mix to a dough with cold water, cut in squares with a knife
2. Bake in a hot oven

title: Old Beets (Boiled)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- salt
- 3 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and cook in hot, salted water from two to three hours, according to age and size.
2. Throw at once into cold water when done, to loosen the skins.
3. Peel quickly, slice thin, dish, and pour over them a sauce made of three tablespoonfuls of scalding vinegar, a tablespoonful of butter, and a little pepper and salt.
4. Serve hot.

title: Old Country Mince Meat
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small nutmeg
- 2 lbs. apples
- 2 lemons, juice of one and rind of two
- 1 pt. brandy
- 2 lbs. raisins
- 3 lbs. currants
- 4 lbs. lean, chopped beef
- 3 lbs. beef suet, chopped
- 2 lbs. brown sugar
- 6 oz. mixed candied peel

## Directions

1. Stone the raisins and chop
2. Wash the currants and dry
3. Slice the peel thinly, grate the nutmeg, pare, core, and mince the apples, peel lemon and strain the juice
4. Get all your dry ingredients, including the beef (uncooked) and suet, well mixed
5. Add the lemon-juice and brandy last
6. Press the mixture into a jar that will exclude the air
7. Set away for a fortnight before using

title: Old-Fashioned Yeast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large potatoes
- 2 qts. water
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup salt
- 1 yeast cake
- A handful of hops

## Directions

1. Tie the hops in a piece of cheesecloth and cook in with potatoes.
2. When done put through the colander; add salt and sugar.
3. When luke warm add the dissolved yeast cake.
4. Let stand in a warm place, when it will be ready for use; after keep in a cool place.

title: Olive Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cream cheese
- olives
- buttered bread

## Directions

1. Mash cream cheese very fine
2. Chop olives also very small
3. Spread cheese on buttered bread
4. Sprinkle chopped olives over it

title: Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat eggs very light, the whites to stiff froth, the yolks to a smooth, thick batter
2. Add to yolks a small cupful of milk, pepper and salt
3. Lastly pour in whites lightly
4. Have ready in hot frying-pan a good lump of butter
5. When it sizzles pour in lightly your mixture, setting over clear fire
6. Do not stir, but as eggs set, slip broad-bladed knife under the omelet to guard against burning
7. If your oven is hot you may put your frying-pan into it as soon as the middle is set
8. Serve with brown side of omelet up

title: Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat eggs separately.
2. Add to beaten yolks the flour, baking powder, milk, salt.
3. Put the whites in last.
4. Have frying-pan hot, and put in butter.
5. When melted cook batter as quickly as possible after the whites are added.
6. Do not turn, but place in hot oven to cook top.

title: Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- two teaspoons cornstarch
- one-quarter teaspoon baking powder
- three tablespoons of milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Beat whites and yolks separately.
2. Stir in whites last with the cornstarch, baking powder, and milk, seasoning to taste.

title: Omelet of Mushroom or Potato
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- cream
- mushroom or potato

## Directions

1. Make an ordinary egg and cream omelet
2. As soon as it is set sprinkle the finely chopped scalded mushroom or hot-cooked potato cut fine
3. Fold the omelet over once
4. Dish immediately

title: One Egg Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- butter size of an egg
- 1 egg
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups flour (sifted)
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and butter well together
2. Add egg and beat together again
3. Add milk
4. Add sifted flour and baking powder
5. Bake in jelly tins

title: One Egg Cake (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup white sugar
- 1 cup cream and milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup currants (if preferred)
- flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake in 1 cake

title: One-Two-Three-Four Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 2 eggs
- 1 small cup milk
- 1/8 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup raisins
- lemon extract or grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cream 1/2 cup butter with 1 cup granulated sugar
2. Sift flour with 1 teaspoon cream tartar
3. Add 2 well-beaten eggs
4. Dissolve 1/8 teaspoon soda in milk
5. Dredge 1 cup raisins with flour
6. Combine all ingredients and flavor with lemon extract or grated nutmeg

title: Onion and Cabbage Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 onions
- 2 large head of cabbage
- salt
- 1 gallon white wine vinegar
- 3 pounds sugar
- ½ pound mustard
- 1 ounce celery seed
- 1 cup white pepper
- ½ cup cinnamon
- 3½ cup tumeric

## Directions

1. Chop onions and cabbage as fine as mincemeat
2. Put into salt water over night
3. In the morning drain off the water
4. Put the mixture on the stove with 1 gallon white wine vinegar, 3 pounds sugar, ½ pound mustard, 1 ounce celery seed, 1 cup white pepper, ½ cup cinnamon, 3½ cup tumeric
5. Bring to a boil
6. Pack in jars

title: Onion Dumpling
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup chopped suet
- 1 cup chopped onions
- cold water
- hot roast beef
- brown gravy

## Directions

1. Mix flour, salt, baking powder, suet, and onions thoroughly
2. Add enough cold water to mix stiff
3. Steam 3 hours
4. Serve with hot roast beef and brown gravy

title: Onion Fritters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- whites of two eggs
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 heaping tablespoonfuls of flour
- salt
- boiling lard

## Directions

1. Slice onion across and then pull apart.
2. Make a batter — the whites of two eggs, half cup milk; put in slowly two heaping tablespoonfuls of flour and a little salt; mix in onion thoroughly and fry in boiling lard; dry on brown paper.

title: Onion Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- onion
- parsley

## Directions

1. Make a plain omelet
2. When ready to turn spread over it a teaspoonful each of chopped onion and minced parsley
3. Fold, or if prepared, mix the minces into the eggs before cooking

title: Onion Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- boiling water
- cream
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel onions and slice them very fine
2. Cover with boiling water and let stand till cool
3. Take out the onions, squeezing out all the water possible
4. Cover with a dressing made of 2 parts of cream and 1 of vinegar
5. Use pepper and salt to taste
6. Add more vinegar if desired

title: Onion Sauce for Fowl or Game
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls flour
- 1 pt. milk
- 4 pt. cream or milk
- 1 cup chopped onions
- A pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Mix flour and salt with cream
2. Bring milk to a boil and slowly add the first mixture, stirring gently
3. When thick, stir in onions cut very fine and boiled very soft

title: Onion Souffle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sauce
- 1/4 cup breadcrumbs
- 1 cup cooked onions (chopped)
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1/4 teaspoonful white pepper
- Yolks of 3 eggs
- whites beaten dry
- 1/2 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Soak the crumbs in cold milk, add white sauce, onions, yolks, pepper, salt, and last beaten whites.
2. Turn into buttered mould and bake forty-five minutes.

title: Onion Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. milk
- 6 large onions
- Yolks of four eggs
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 cup of cream
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Put butter in frying-pan, cut onion in thin slices and drop in butter
2. Stir until they begin to cook, then cover tight and set back where they will simmer and not burn for one-half hour
3. Put milk on to boil; add the dry flour to onions, stir constantly over fire for three minutes
4. Pour mixture into milk and cook for fifteen minutes; strain
5. Season with salt and pepper and return soup to fire
6. Beat yolks thoroughly, add them to the cream and stir into soup
7. Cook three minutes, stirring constantly
8. Pour over fried croutons in tureen and serve

title: Onion Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One tablespoonful of butter
- One finely chopped onion
- One quart of soup stock
- One tablespoonful of flour
- A little cold water
- One-half a teaspoonful of salt
- One-quarter teaspoonful of pepper
- Two slices of stale bread

## Directions

1. Put one tablespoonful of butter in the frying-pan
2. When hot add one finely chopped onion; fry it until nicely browned, being careful not to burn
3. Put one quart of soup stock into a stew-pan; add the fried onion and cook for fifteen minutes
4. Strain; return to the fire, add one tablespoonful of flour wet in a little cold water, to thicken, and boil for five minutes longer
5. Season with one-half a teaspoonful of salt and one-quarter teaspoonful of pepper
6. Cut two slices of stale bread into dice; brown the dice in the oven, put them in the soup tureen, pour the soup over them and serve at once before they become soft

title: Onions Boiled
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- milk
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Select onions of uniform size
2. Remove the outer skin
3. Boil until tender in a large quantity of milk and water
4. Drain them when tender
5. Season with butter, salt and pepper

title: Onions Fried
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- lard or butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel and slice onions
2. Fry in lard or butter
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Serve hot

title: Onions with White Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 medium-sized onions
- hot water
- 2 cups milk
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 2 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Peel onions and quarter
2. Place in pot with hot water and boil 20 minutes
3. Take off and strain
4. When ready to serve, cover with white sauce made by heating 2 cups milk
5. Add 3 tablespoons flour that has been creamed with 2 tablespoons of butter
6. Stir constantly till it thickens

title: Ontario Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ounces flour
- 2 ounces sugar
- 1 ounce butter
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Steam 1 hour

title: Orange Albumen
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of half an orange
- white of an egg
- tablespoonful of water
- sugar to sweeten
- lemon

## Directions

1. Mix juice of half an orange, white of an egg, tablespoonful of water, and sugar to sweeten
2. Strain through muslin
3. Lemon can be used

title: Orange and Rhubarb Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of rhubarb
- 3 or 4 Seville oranges
- 1 1/2 pounds white sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges thinly and cut them into strips as for marmalade
2. Take off the white pith
3. Remove the pips from the fruit and cut into slices the yellow part of the peel also
4. Place oranges, rhubarb and sugar in preserving pan and boil gently until sufficiently cooked and the scum has ceased to rise
5. Put the preserves in small pots and cover while hot with paper brushed over with white of egg

title: Orange and Walnut Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Four peeled oranges
- Three or four tablespoonfuls of olive oil
- One tablespoonful of lemon-juice
- Lettuce leaves
- One cupful of sliced nut meats
- One tablespoonful of oil
- A dash of salt
- Half a teaspoonful of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Slice oranges lengthwise, dress with three or four tablespoonfuls of olive oil and one tablespoonful of lemon-juice.
2. Arrange slices in a mound upon a layer of lettuce leaves.
3. Dress one cupful of sliced nut meats with one tablespoonful of oil, a dash of salt and half a teaspoonful of lemon juice, and dispose upon the centre of mound.
4. Mix together before serving.

title: Orange Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 cup milk
- Whites of 3 eggs
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- Rind of 1 orange, grated

## Directions

1. Flavor the cake with the rind of an orange grated.
2. Bake in shallow long pan, pour icing over cake without removing from the oven; when the cake is quite done let it harden.
3. Use yolks for icing with one cup sugar and a teaspoon lemon extract.

title: Orange Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3 cups flour
- salt
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon orange extract

## Directions

1. Cream 3/4 cup butter
2. Add slowly 2 cups sugar and cream together
3. Sift together 2 teaspoons baking powder with 3 cups flour and a little salt; sift four times
4. Add 1 tablespoon of the sifted flour to the creamed butter and sugar
5. Add 4 eggs, 1 at a time unbeaten, adding a tablespoon flour before each egg
6. Continue adding the flour alternately with 1 cup milk
7. Flavor with 1 teaspoon orange extract
8. Bake in a moderate oven until cake shrinks from edge of pan and feels firm
9. When cool, frost with orange frosting

title: Orange Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 1/4 cups flour

## Directions

1. Beat 3 eggs and 1 cup sugar together to a stiff froth
2. Add 2 tablespoons cold water
3. Mix 2 teaspoons baking powder with 1 1/4 cups flour
4. Bake in 2 layers

title: Orange Cream Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 1/4 cups flour
- 1/4 cup sweet milk
- 1/4 teaspoon cream tartar
- 3/4 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon orange extract

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Orange Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- The rind and juice of 1 orange
- 1 small cup powdered sugar
- 1 small piece butter
- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup cold water
- 1 teaspoon flour

## Directions

1. Cook until of the consistency of a soft custard

title: Orange Filling For Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 pt. milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls cornstarch
- Yolks 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls sugar
- Grated yellow rind of 1 orange
- 2 tablespoonfuls orange juice

## Directions

1. Put milk in a double boiler
2. Moisten cornstarch with a little cold water
3. Add it to the scalded milk
4. Stir constantly until smooth and thick
5. Beat the yolks of eggs with sugar, until light
6. Add it to the cornstarch
7. Take from the fire
8. When cool add the grated yellow rind of one orange and two tablespoons of orange juice

title: Orange Frosting
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 yolk of egg
- 1 tablespoon water
- 1 teaspoon orange extract
- confectioner's sugar

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 yolk of egg, 1 tablespoon water and 1 teaspoon orange extract
2. Add enough confectioner's sugar to make thick enough to spread

title: Orange Ice
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups water
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups orange juice
- 3/4 cup lemon juice
- grated rind of 2 oranges

## Directions

1. Make syrup by boiling water and sugar 15 to 20 minutes
2. Add fruit juice and rind
3. Cool, strain and freeze

title: Orange Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- butter size of an egg
- 4 tablespoons milk
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 egg
- 1 orange
- little piece butter
- cornstarch

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, eggs, butter size of an egg, milk, flour and baking powder
2. Bake in layers
3. For filling: Mix 1 cup sugar, 1 cup boiling water, 1 egg, 1 orange, little piece butter and cornstarch to thicken

title: Orange Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen bitter oranges
- 5 quarts cold water
- 7 pounds granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Peel off the rind as thin as possible in long strips with a sharp knife
2. Remove the white part
3. Cut the yellow part in very thin shreds about 1 inch long
4. Cut the white part into bits the size of small beans
5. Cut up the pulp rather fine, saving the pips
6. Put pips in a bowl with 1 pint water poured over them
7. Pour the 5 quarts water on the cut-up rind and pulp
8. Let stand over night
9. Next day add the liquid from the pips, and boil 3 hours
10. Set away until next day, bring to a boil
11. Add the sugar (which has been heated)
12. Boil an hour or less if it jellies
13. Put in glasses

title: Orange Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 bitter oranges
- 3 sweet oranges
- 4 lemons
- 4 quarts water
- 8 pounds sugar
- A wineglass of whisky or brandy (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the oranges and lemons across the grain as fine as possible
2. Put all together in a jar and cover with four quarts of water
3. Let it stand for twenty-four hours
4. Boil one hour
5. Add sugar (white) and boil two hours
6. Pot for use
7. A wineglass of whisky or brandy may be added just before removing from fire to clarify the marmalade

title: Orange Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 bitter oranges
- 6 sweet oranges
- 11 qts. water
- 22 lbs. sugar
- Juice 6 lemons

## Directions

1. Cut up the oranges very thinly
2. Cover with twenty-two pints of cold water
3. Allow to stand for thirty-six hours
4. Boil quickly for two hours
5. Add sugar, and boil steadily for one hour
6. If you care to, add one wineglass of brandy as you are taking from the stove, to clear the jelly
7. Put into pots, cool, and cover

title: Orange Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 dozen bitter oranges
- sugar equal in weight to the oranges
- water

## Directions

1. Weigh the oranges, cut the skin and take it off in quarters
2. Put in a cheese-cloth bag and cook in water nearly two hours, until you can pierce easily with a straw
3. Cut in thin pieces about an inch long
4. Cut the oranges into halves and scrape out the pulp and juice, throwing away the pith
5. Put sugar equal in weight to the oranges into the water you cooked the rinds in, and boil ten minutes
6. Skim and add the rinds and pulp
7. Cook half an hour
8. Dip in tumblers, and set away to cool before sealing

title: Orange Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 bitter oranges
- 3 sweet oranges
- 2 lemons
- 8 lbs. sugar
- 1 glass whisky

## Directions

1. Slice the fruit across the grain, as thin as possible, being careful to remove all seeds
2. Place in a deep dish with four quarts of water
3. Allow it to stand for twenty-four hours
4. Add sugar and boil for one hour, or until it will jelly
5. Before taking off the fire add one glass of whisky to clarify

title: Orange Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen bitter oranges
- About 20 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. First day: Chip off the yellow rind of the oranges, using a slicer
2. Peel off the tough white skin with the fingers, and put this with skin through a mincer or chop it up very finely and mix with yellow rind
3. Measure this into pints, packing loosely, and for every pint add 2 3/4 pints cold water
4. Put into preserving kettle and let stand at least 12 hours, or until next morning
5. If there is much juice in the pulp of the oranges, strain it out and add without measuring
6. Second day: Boil 1 hour after it reaches the boiling point and set aside for another 12 hours, or until next day
7. Third day: Measure again into pints, and for every pint add pounds sugar
8. Boil for 1 hour, put in jars and wax or seal up

title: Orange Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen bitter oranges
- 1/2 dozen lemons
- 1 cup water to one cup fruit
- 1 3/4 pounds sugar to 1 pint of fruit

## Directions

1. Cut up lemons fine
2. Cook each separate until tender in 1 cup water to one cup fruit
3. Put together and add 1 3/4 pounds sugar to 1 pint of fruit
4. Boil quickly for 40 minutes

title: Orange Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- juice and grated yellow rind of 1 orange
- 1 cupful sugar
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- 1 cupful milk
- 1 saltspoonful salt
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and the sugar together.
2. Add the flour, the milk, and the grated rind and juice of the orange.
3. Place it on the fire in a double boiler, and stir until it is a little thickened.
4. Pour it into an open or tart pie, and bake thirty minutes.
5. The crust of the pie should be brushed with white of egg before adding the thickened mixture.
6. Cover the top with meringue made with the whites of the eggs and sweetened with three tablespoonfuls of sugar.
7. Pile it on irregularly, or press it through a pastry-bag into fancy shapes.
8. Place it in the oven a moment to brown.

title: Orange Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- juice and rind of 1 orange
- 1 cup sugar
- yolks of 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 cup milk
- pie paste

## Directions

1. Line pie tin with pie paste
2. Pour in mixture and bake till cooked
3. Save white of eggs for frosting

title: Orange Pie (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Juice and rind of an orange
- 1 cup sugar
- Yolk of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 teacup milk
- Paste
- Whites of 2 eggs
- 4 tablespoons pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Line the dish with paste
2. Pour in the custards
3. Bake till done
4. Beat the whites of eggs to a stiff froth
5. Add 4 tablespoons of pulverized sugar
6. Spread it on the pie and brown lightly

title: Orange Pie (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 orange
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 cup sugar
- Salt
- Yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Use whites for frosting

title: Orange Pie (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- juice and rind of 1 orange
- 1 cup sugar
- yolk of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 teacup milk
- paste
- 4 tablespoons pulverized sugar

## Directions

1. Line the dish with paste
2. Pour in the custards and bake till done
3. Beat the whites of eggs to a stiff froth
4. Add 4 tablespoons of pulverized sugar
5. Spread it on the pie and brown lightly

title: Orange Plombiere
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 oranges
- 1 cup cold water
- cups sugar
- 1 cup cream

## Directions

1. Strain juice of oranges
2. Rub rind from 1 orange into a cup cold water, add sugar, turn into freezer
3. When mixture begins to thicken, add cream
4. Freeze until very stiff
5. Remove dasher, pack firm

title: Orange Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oranges
- 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 pint milk
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- whites of 2 eggs
- a little granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and cut 3 oranges into small pieces, put into pudding dish, cover with 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar, let stand 1 hour
2. Make a custard of 1 pint milk, yolks of 2 eggs, 2 tablespoons cornstarch
3. Let milk come to a boil, stir in starch and egg, dissolved in cold water, cook a minute, pour over the oranges
4. Beat the whites of the 2 eggs, add a little granulated sugar, spread on the top, set in the oven to brown, let cool and serve

title: Orange Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup sugar
- one orange
- one cup cracker crumbs
- one pint milk
- two eggs
- one teaspoonful butter

## Directions

1. Soak the crumbs in a little of the milk, butter and sugar
2. Add grated rind and eggs beaten together
3. Add orange juice and crumbs
4. Bake half an hour in moderate oven

title: Orange Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup biscuit or bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- 1/4 teaspoon butter
- grated rind and juice of 1 orange
- 1 quart milk

## Directions

1. Bake like custard and serve when cold

title: Orange Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Several oranges
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 3 tablespoonfuls olive oil
- 1 tablespoonful lemon juice or vinegar
- crisp lettuce leaf

## Directions

1. Peel oranges, remove the inside skin, leaving the pulp as unbroken as possible
2. Make a very smooth French dressing by mixing sugar, olive oil, vinegar or lemon juice in the order given, adding oil and vinegar slowly
3. Place a little of the orange on a crisp lettuce leaf
4. Pour over a little of the dressing and serve

title: Orange Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- oranges
- pineapple
- bananas
- malmaga grapes
- chopped nuts
- 1 cup cream
- 1 teaspoon flour
- whites of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- small saltspoon salt
- a pinch cayenne pepper
- 1 teaspoon mustard

## Directions

1. Cut and dice oranges, pineapple, bananas
2. shred thin malmaga grapes and chopped nuts
3. beat the cream to boiling point, stir in flour previously wet with cold milk
4. When half cold beat in the whipped eggs, set aside to cool
5. when quite cold whip in melted butter, pepper, salt and mustard
6. When salad is ready to serve, whip in lemon juice
7. Fill in shells of oranges

title: Orange Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoons cornstarch
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 pint boiling water
- juice and rind of 2 oranges and lemons
- 2 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. let simmer 10 minutes
2. add juice and rind of oranges and lemons
3. add butter

title: Orangeade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- lemons
- oranges
- pineapple

## Directions

1. Make as you would lemonade
2. Add the juice of a lemon
3. Add a few bits of shredded orange-peel
4. Add a slice of pineapple

title: Orangeade
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds sugar
- 8 tumblers hot water
- Juice from 4 oranges
- 1 ounce citric acid
- Whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Let stand 24 hours
2. Strain and bottle
3. Use like raspberry vinegar, in water

title: Orangeade
body:
## Ingredients

- Grated rind and juice of 12 oranges
- 4 ounces citric acid
- 7 pounds granulated sugar
- 12 glasses water

## Directions

1. Let stand 24 hours
2. Strain through a cloth
3. When serving, use 2 tablespoons of liquid to a glass of water

title: Osgoode Pies
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs (whites of 2 for top)
- 1 cup raisins
- 3 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1 teaspoon mixed spice
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 cup water
- Crust

## Directions

1. Cook all together
2. Put in crust that has been previously made
3. This will make 2 pies

title: Oswego Dark Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 3/4 cups sour milk
- 1/4 cup dark molasses
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants
- 1/4 cup citron peel
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/4 teaspoon cloves
- 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon
- 3 large cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Ox-Marrow Pomade For The Hair
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One marrow bone
- Half a pint of oil
- Ten cents' worth of citronella

## Directions

1. Take the marrow out of the bone, place it in warm water, let it get almost to boiling point, then let it cool and pour the water away
2. Repeat this three times until the marrow is thoroughly fined
3. Beat the marrow to a cream with a silver fork, stir the oil in, drop by drop, beating all the time
4. When quite cold add the citronella, pour into jars and cover down

title: Ox-Tail Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ox tail
- 2 lbs. lean beef
- 4 carrots
- 3 onions
- Thyme
- 4 qts. cold water
- Butter
- Pepper
- Salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of browned flour

## Directions

1. Cut the tail into several pieces and fry brown in butter
2. Slice the onions and carrots, and when you remove the ox-tail from the frying-pan, put in these and brown also
3. When done tie them in a bag with a bunch of thyme and drop into a soup pot
4. Lay the pieces of ox-tail in the same, then the meat cut into small slices
5. Grate over them the two whole carrots, and add four quarts of cold water with pepper and salt
6. Boil four to six hours, in proportion to the size of the tail
7. Strain fifteen minutes before serving, and thicken with two tablespoonfuls of browned flour
8. Boil ten minutes longer

title: Oxford Sausage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound pork
- 1 pound lean veal
- 1 pound beef suet
- 7/8 pound bread crumbs
- 6 sage leaves
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 2 teaspoons salt
- thyme
- marjoram
- savory

## Directions

1. Chop pork, veal, and beef suet all very fine together
2. Mix with bread crumbs, 6 sage leaves, 1 teaspoon pepper and 2 of salt, thyme, marjoram and savory shred fine

title: Oxtail Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oxtail
- 5 pints of water
- 1 onion
- 1 ounce of butter
- piece of celery or pinch of celery seed
- 1 ounce of flour
- pinch herbs
- 1 carrot
- 1 teaspoon peppercorns
- piece turnip
- 2 cloves
- 1 dessertspoon catsup

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients and cook

title: Oyster A La Creme
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart oysters
- 1 pint cream
- flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Put oysters in double boiler and cook till milk juice begins to flow out
2. Drain the oysters in a colander
3. Put the cream in the double boiler
4. When it comes to a boil, thicken with flour, wet with milk to the consistency of cornstarch, ready to mould
5. Put in oysters and cook five minutes

title: Oyster Cocktail
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- cayenne
- lemon juice
- tomato sauce
- tobasco sauce

## Directions

1. Put three or four oysters in a glass (small lemonade glass)
2. Mix cayenne, lemon-juice and tomato sauce, also put a few drops of tobasco sauce (very little)
3. Let all stand in glasses, on ice, for about three hours
4. Serve glasses on small plate and watercress around the glasses

title: Oyster Cocktail
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 small oysters
- 5 tablespoonfuls of Worcestershire sauce
- 3 tablespoonfuls of fine grated horseradish
- 3 tablespoonfuls of tomato catsup
- 1 teaspoonful of tobasco sauce
- 2 teaspoonfuls of salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean and chill oysters
2. Mix with sauce and serve in sherry glasses, in grape-fruit or lemon shells, or in tomato cups
3. If fresh tomatoes are not at hand cups may be shaped from tomato jelly

title: Oyster Cocktail for Twelve Persons
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 small oysters
- 3 tablespoonfuls of fine grated horseradish
- 1 teaspoonful of tobasco sauce
- 2 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- 5 tablespoonfuls of Worcestershire sauce
- 3 tablespoonfuls of tomato catsup
- 2 teaspoonfuls of salt

## Directions

1. Clean and chill oysters
2. Mix with sauce
3. Serve in sherry glasses, in grape-fruit or lemon shells, or in tomato cups
4. If fresh tomatoes are not at hand cups may be shaped from tomato jelly

title: Oyster Dressing or Stuffing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- same ingredients as dressing or stuffing for fowls
- half a can of oysters drained

## Directions

1. Make with the same ingredients as the basic dressing or stuffing for fowls, with the exception of half a can of oysters drained and slightly chopped and added to the rest.
2. This is used mostly with boiled turkey and chicken.
3. Use the remainder of the can of oysters to make an oyster sauce to be poured over the turkey when served, and also served in a separate dish.

title: Oyster Omelet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen oysters
- eggs
- butter
- flour
- salt
- cayenne pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Parboil a dozen oysters in their own liquor
2. Skim them out and let them cool
3. Add them to the beaten eggs, either whole or minced
4. Cook the same as a plain omelet
5. Thicken the liquid with butter rolled in flour
6. Season with salt, cayenne pepper and a teaspoonful of chopped parsley
7. Chop up the oysters and add to the sauce
8. Put a few spoonfuls in the centre of the omelet before folding
9. When dished, pour the remainder of the sauce around it

title: Oyster Patties
body:
## Ingredients

- rich puff paste
- large fresh oysters
- cloves
- mace
- nutmeg
- 1 yolk of egg, boiled hard and grated
- butter
- oyster liquor

## Directions

1. Make some rich puff paste
2. Bake in very small patty pans
3. When cool, turn out upon a large dish
4. Stew some large, fresh oysters with a few cloves, a little mace and nutmeg
5. Add the yolk of one egg, boiled hard and grated
6. Add a little butter, and as much of the oyster liquor as will cover them
7. When they have stewed a little while take them out of the pan and set them to cool
8. When quite cool, lay 2 or 3 oysters in each shell of puff paste

title: Oyster Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- good puff paste
- crusts of bread
- oysters
- butter

## Directions

1. Line a shallow pudding-dish with good puff paste
2. Fill with crusts of bread
3. Cover with a top of the paste and bake
4. Butter the edges of the dish well so that the crust would not adhere to them too closely
5. When the pastry is baked lift off the top carefully
6. Remove the bread crusts
7. Put in oysters, prepared just as for oyster scallop, except that the oysters are allowed to cook in the sauce for three minutes after they go in
8. Lay the crust back on the dish and send to table

title: Oyster Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 small oysters
- 3 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful olive oil
- 1/2 teaspoonful pepper
- 1/2 teaspoonful salt
- 1 pint chopped celery
- 1 cup mayonnaise
- Olives
- Celery tips

## Directions

1. Let oysters come to a boil (no more) in their liquor. Skim and strain.
2. Season the oysters with vinegar, oil, pepper, salt, and place on ice for two hours.
3. Cut finely the tender part of celery.
4. When ready to serve mix this with the oysters and mayonnaise dressing.
5. Arrange in bowl, garnish with olives and celery tips.

title: Oyster Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 doz. or 1 pt. small oysters
- 1/4 pt. chopped celery
- 1 cup milk
- 3 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour (heaping)
- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- 2 teaspoonfuls salt
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- A pinch of cayenne
- 1/4 cup vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick over and cut oysters in half, and parboil in the liquor and add chopped celery
2. Put butter into double boiler, melt without browning, add flour, and stir till smooth, add milk
3. Beat eggs without separating, salt, mustard, sugar, vinegar and cayenne
4. Before adding vinegar, mix dry materials, add to the milk, return to fire and cook for five minutes

title: Oyster Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart or twenty-five oysters in their own liquor
- One cupful of water
- One tablespoonful of butter
- One tablespoonful of flour
- One cupful of scalded milk or cream
- Pepper, salt if necessary, and a dash of cayenne or paprica

## Directions

1. Scald a quart or twenty-five oysters in their own liquor
2. As soon as they are plump, or the gills curl, remove them
3. Add to the liquor a cupful of water
4. Make a roux of one tablespoonful each of butter and flour, dilute it with the liquor, and when it is smooth add a cupful of scalded milk or cream
5. Season with pepper, salt if necessary, and a dash of cayenne or paprica
6. Add the oysters, and as soon as they are heated serve at once
7. Examine oysters carefully, and pass the liquor through a fine sieve before being cooked, in order to remove any pieces of shell

title: Oyster Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One knuckle veal
- One small onion
- Two blades mace
- Two or three celery leaves
- Two small red peppers
- One half pint cream
- One half pint milk
- One quart oysters

## Directions

1. Boil veal with onion, mace, peppers, celery leaves and salt to taste, in four quarts of water, adding more water till the meat is boiled to shreds
2. Strain and set the liquor to cool
3. When cold skim off every particle of fat, and leave behind any grounds that may be at the bottom of the jelly, which should be firm
4. Put the jelly over the fire; when boiling add cream and milk
5. Thicken with flour previously blended to the consistency of cream, or oyster crackers powdered
6. Stir till the soup is thickened, then add the oysters, stirring constantly for three or four minutes

title: Oyster Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart oysters
- 4 cups scalded milk
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 tablespoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon pepper
- 3/4 cup cold water

## Directions

1. Clean oysters by placing in colander and pouring over them 3/4 cup cold water
2. Carefully pick over oysters and remove liquor
3. Heat liquor to boiling point and strain through double cheesecloth
4. Add oysters and cook till they are plump and edges begin to curl
5. Remove oysters with skimmer and put in tureen with butter, salt and pepper
6. Add oyster liquor and milk

title: Oyster Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 slice dry toast
- butter
- 6 oysters
- 1 half teacupful oyster liquor
- 1 half cupful milk
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a nice slice of dry toast, butter it and lay it on a hot dish
2. Put six oysters, half a teacupful of their own liquor, and half a cupful of milk, into a tin cup or basin, and boil one minute
3. Season with a little butter, pepper and salt
4. Pour over the toast and serve

title: Oysters on the Shell
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters in shell
- toast

## Directions

1. Wash the shell
2. Put them on hot coals, or upon the top of a hot stove, or bake them in a hot oven
3. Open shells with an oyster knife, taking care to lose none of the liquor
4. Serve quickly on hot plates with toast
5. Oysters may be steamed in the shells, and are excellent eaten in the same manner

title: Oysters with Anchovy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 oysters
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 teaspoonful, large, of anchovy paste
- A little cayenne
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Melt the butter and the anchovy together in the blazer, put in the oysters, cook three minutes
2. Add the cayenne and lemon-juice and serve on buttered toast or breakfast-biscuit

title: Packing Eggs For Winter Use
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- barley

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of a wooden box with barley which has been heated in the oven to thoroughly dry it, but not enough to cook or overheat it and destroy the life of the grain
2. Stand the eggs, small end down, in the barley, allowing none to touch each other
3. Cover with barley and continue thus until box is filled
4. Cover well with barley and put in a dry place
5. Do not place paper over or layers of paper between

title: Pancakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sour milk
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls soda
- 2 eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix all together and beat
2. Take out a cupful and mix your flour in
3. Mix them thicker than you want them
4. Thin the mixture with the cupful taken out
5. Make batter quite thin

title: Pancakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- nine eggs
- flour enough to make very stiff
- two tablespoonfuls of ginger
- a little salt
- milk enough to make a nice thin batter

## Directions

1. Beat eggs very light
2. Mix in flour enough to make very stiff
3. Add two tablespoonfuls of ginger and a little salt
4. Add milk enough to make a nice thin batter

title: Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart buttermilk freshly churned
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour to make a good batter

## Directions

1. Combine buttermilk, sour cream, egg, and soda. Add flour to make a good batter.

title: Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 3/8 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1/2 teaspoon melted butter

## Directions

1. mix dry
2. add liquid gradually
3. add butter
4. Do not beat too much
5. Have pan hot, rubbed with pork fat, butter burns very easily
6. Drop by spoonfuls
7. When puffed and full of bubbles, cooked on edge, turn and cook other side

title: Pancakes (Variant 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup sour milk or buttermilk
- pinch of salt
- 2 cups sifted flour
- a little brown sugar
- milk or flour to thin or thicken the batter

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in sour milk or buttermilk. Add salt and sifted flour with a little brown sugar. Add milk or flour to thin or thicken the batter.

title: Panned Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- toast
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- cayenne

## Directions

1. Clean, place on small pieces of toast in baking pan
2. Season, bake until plump
3. Serve with lemon butter, made with 3 tablespoons butter creamed, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon lemon juice, cayenne

title: Paradise Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 apples chopped fine
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 ounce bread crumbs
- grated rind and juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Put in greased mould and steam 2 hours

title: Parker House Rolls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg, well beaten
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cake yeast
- A little salt
- Flour to make light dough

## Directions

1. Beat egg and salt, add sugar, dissolve yeast in a little milk, and add to egg and salt and sugar.
2. Add flour and set to rise for an hour or an hour and one-half.
3. Turn out and knead.
4. Roll out with a rolling pin about one-half inch thick.
5. Cut with a ring, brush over with water, fold in two and brush over with egg.
6. Set to rise to twice original size and bake.
7. Use same amount of yeast for twice the quantity.

title: Parker House Rolls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 qts flour
- 1 yeast cake
- 1 pt milk
- 4 tablespoonfuls lard
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Mix at 9 o'clock with half of the flour
2. At 12 stir in rest of flour
3. At 2.30 knead well
4. At 3.30 roll about a quarter of an inch thick, cut and spread lightly with melted butter, and double over
5. Let them rise until 5.40
6. Bake twenty minutes in quick oven

title: Parker House Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups scalded milk
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 3/4 cup lukewarm water
- flour
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 White Swan Yeast Cake in 3/4 cup lukewarm water
2. Add 3 tablespoons butter, 2 tablespoons sugar and 1 teaspoon salt to milk when lukewarm
3. Add dissolved yeast cake and three cups of flour
4. Beat well, cover and let rise until light
5. Cut down and add enough flour to knead (it will take about two and a half cups)
6. Let rise again
7. Toss on floured board and knead
8. Roll out to one-third inch thickness
9. Shape with biscuit cutter first dipped in flour
10. Dip the handle of a knife in flour and with it make a crease through the middle of each piece
11. Brush over one half of each piece with melted butter
12. Fold and press edges together
13. Place in a greased pan
14. Let rise and bake in a hot oven fifteen minutes

title: Parker House Rolls, With Baking Powder
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls baking powder
- 1 qt. flour
- 1 tablespoonful cold butter
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 pt. cold milk

## Directions

1. Sift baking powder with flour; put in cold butter; add well-beaten egg, sugar, and salt; rub well together, and make into a dough, with a pint of cold milk.
2. Roll out less than half-inch thick.
3. Cut with a large biscuit-cutter.
4. Spread soft butter over the top of each, fold them together, and lay a little apart on greased tins.
5. Brush over the tops with sweet milk and set immediately in a hot oven.

title: Parliament Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 pound butter
- 5 eggs
- 2 teaspoons ground cinnamon
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 pound bread crumbs fine
- 1 pound currants
- Pie paste

## Directions

1. Dry and roll the bread crumbs fine
2. Cream together 1 pound sugar, 1 pound butter, 5 eggs, 2 teaspoons ground cinnamon, 1 pint sweet milk, and 1 teaspoon baking soda
3. Line your bake dish with pie paste
4. Pour in the mixture

title: Parsnip Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- clarified dripping

## Directions

1. Wash, boil, and scrape the parsnips tender
2. While hot mash
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Make with floured hands into small, flat cakes
5. Flour well
6. Fry in clarified dripping

title: Parsnip Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- raw parsnips
- fine bread crumbs
- eggs
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- sweet milk

## Directions

1. Grate raw parsnips to make a cupful
2. Add 1 cup fine bread crumbs
3. Add 2 beaten eggs and a piece of butter
4. Add salt and pepper
5. Moisten with sweet milk
6. Form into cakes
7. Fry brown in hot butter

title: Parsnip Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- salted water
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- flour
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Cook parsnips in boiling salted water
2. Scrape off the skins
3. Mash and season with 1 teaspoon butter, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon pepper
4. Make into oval balls on floured boards
5. Fry in butter until well browned

title: Parsnip Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- salted water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook parsnips in boiling salted water
2. Scrape off the skins
3. Mash and season with 1 teaspoon butter, teaspoon salt, teaspoon pepper
4. Make into oval balls on floured boards
5. Fry in butter until well browned

title: Parsnip Fritters (Second Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- eggs
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Mash boiled parsnips
2. Add beaten egg
3. Make into flat cakes
4. Roll in flour
5. Saute in butter

title: Parsnips Boiled and Creamed
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- salted water
- white sauce

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape parsnips
2. Cut in pieces 2 inches long and inch wide
3. Cook until soft in boiling salted water
4. Drain and add 1 cup white sauce to every 2 cups parsnips

title: Pastry for Tarts or Open Pies
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1/4 cupful butter
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- Yolks of 2 eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Sift the flour, salt, and sugar together
2. Cut in the butter as directed above
3. Mix in the beaten yolks, then enough water to make a paste which is not very stiff
4. Roll it two or three times, then wrap it in a cloth, or cover it closely, and put it in the ice-box for an hour
5. This gives enough paste for four small tarts

title: Pate de Veau
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3.5 lbs. beef or veal chopped fine
- 3 slices salt pork chopped fine
- 3 raw eggs
- 1 teaspoonful salt and pepper
- 1 nutmeg
- A piece of butter size of butternut
- 6 crackers rolled fine

## Directions

1. Mix all with flour into a deep loaf, sprinkle with bread crumbs and small pieces of butter.
2. Bake two hours in meat-pan with a little water.
3. Baste while baking.

title: Patterdale Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one-quarter pound sifted sugar
- one-quarter pound butter
- one-quarter pound flour

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream
2. Beat the eggs in one by one
3. Add the sugar and flour
4. Put into cups
5. Bake in a moderately heated oven twenty minutes

title: Pea Salad With Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- cold hard-boiled eggs
- green peas
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cut cold hard-boiled eggs in the centre
2. Carefully remove the yolks and cut into small bits
3. Mix with green peas, strained fairly dry, and heated and seasoned
4. Pour carefully into the halves of the eggs
5. Serve on lettuce leaves with mayonnaise dressing

title: Peach Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- pound for pound peaches and sugar
- cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cook peaches alone until they become soft
2. Put in the sugar, and stir 1/2 hour
3. Add the remainder of the sugar, and stir an hour and a half
4. Season with cloves and cinnamon

title: Peach Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large peaches
- 1 cup icing sugar
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Chop peaches fine
2. Add icing sugar
3. beat white of egg stiff
4. mix together and beat for 1 hour

title: Peach Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds peaches stewed and pared
- 2 1/2 pounds sugar
- juice 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Boil peaches for 3/4 hour
2. Add sugar and lemons
3. Boil 20 minutes
4. Skim while boiling

title: Peach Melba
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint milk
- yolks of five eggs
- vanilla flavoring
- peaches or pears
- raspberry syrup

## Directions

1. Make the custard with milk and eggs, vanilla essence to taste
2. make this custard into an ice
3. When ready for the melba, take the ice out of the freezing machine and place in rather a deep dish
4. put peaches or pears on the top of the ice
5. pour raspberry syrup over the whole and serve
6. To make the raspberry syrup, take a small jar of raspberry jam and pass through a fine sieve
7. take a very small bottle of raspberry syrup and well mix with the jam as the syrup alone is not thick enough

title: Peach Tapioca
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup tapioca
- syrup from a can of peaches
- water
- 1/4 cup sugar
- a little salt
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. soak tapioca 1 hour in cold water and drain
2. add enough water to the syrup poured from a can of peaches to make in all 3 cups
3. add the soaked tapioca and sugar and a little salt to this liquid
4. Cook till thoroughly clear
5. line a mould with the peaches, dust with sugar, and fill with the tapioca
6. serve with whipped cream

title: Peach, Apple, or Quince Leather
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of the fruit (apples and quince of equal quantities, or of either separately)
- One pound sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and cut up the fruit in small pieces
2. Put on to boil with as little water as possible
3. While boiling mash fine with a spoon
4. Add one pound of granulated sugar and boil about fifteen or twenty minutes longer
5. Spread the mass very thin on a flat tin or platter to dry
6. In the south it is put out of doors in the sun under netting and brought in at night
7. It can be done equally well by using a cool oven, leaving the door open, so that it may stay there at night as well
8. It must not cool, only dry
9. Several days are required in drying
10. Cut in strips, roll in sugar and roll up or leave in plain lengths

title: Peanut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1¾ cups flour
- 1½ teaspoons baking powder
- ¼ cup milk
- 1 cup of finely chopped peanuts

## Directions

1. Cream together the butter and sugar
2. Add the beaten yolk of the egg
3. Put in half the flour, then the milk, then the rest of the flour
4. The flour and baking powder should be sifted together twice
5. Lastly fold in 1 cup of finely chopped peanuts and the stiffly beaten white of the egg
6. Bake in a loaf

title: Peanut Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. peanuts
- 1 large cup sugar

## Directions

1. Shell and skin peanuts, and roll them until fine
2. Place granulated sugar in a saucepan
3. Set it over a hot fire and stir the contents quickly until it melts
4. Do this while the peanuts are being heated through in the oven, and after the pans are buttered and set on the back of the range to be kept hot
5. When the sugar has melted pour the hot peanuts into it
6. Remove from the fire and pour into the hot buttered pans
7. When cold it can be broken into pieces

title: Peanut Candy
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups granulated sugar
- peanuts, shelled and broken up

## Directions

1. Put sugar in granite dish on a hot fire and stir till all is a golden brown
2. Have peanuts shelled and broken up on a buttered platter
3. Pour the hot melted sugar over
4. Set away to cool
5. Break up into small pieces

title: Peanut Candy 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart molasses
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 level teaspoon soda
- 1 quart shelled peanuts

## Directions

1. Boil molasses, sugar, and butter rapidly until it will snap when dropped in cold water
2. Add a level teaspoon soda, and stir a moment
3. Add 1 quart shelled peanuts
4. Stir enough to mix well
5. Pour in greased tin
6. When partly cold, pull

title: Peanut Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 4 tablespoons milk
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup chopped peanuts

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together and bake.

title: Peanut Cookies 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint peanuts chopped fine
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 2 tablespoons milk
- a pinch of salt
- flour to make a soft dough

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar, add the beaten eggs, nuts and other ingredients, and enough flour to make a soft dough.
2. Roll and cut in circles.

title: Peanut Sandwich
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 8 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 cup of sweet cream
- 10 cents of peanuts
- fresh slices of bread

## Directions

1. Cook eggs, vinegar, sugar, mustard, and salt until thick. Stir in sweet cream. Shell peanuts and put through a meat chopper. Stir in dressing and spread on fresh slices of bread.

title: Peanut Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh roasted peanuts
- good oil mayonnaise
- bread

## Directions

1. Grind, or chop fine in the finest knife chopper the peanuts.
2. Mix this meat with a good oil mayonnaise.
3. Spread it between folds of bread, like butter, for sandwiches.

title: Peanut Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- shelled peanuts
- salt
- cream
- butter
- bread

## Directions

1. Crush the shelled peanuts divested of skins.
2. Season with salt and mix to a paste with cream, or omit the salt and add to creamed butter.

title: Peanut Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of rich milk
- One large cupful of peanuts, shelled and skinned
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Red pepper
- One cupful of cream

## Directions

1. Put milk on to cook in a double boiler; add salt to taste, and season highly with black and red pepper
2. Add the peanuts, which have been put through a meat chopper two or three times until they are ground fine
3. Cook twenty or thirty minutes
4. Just before taking from the fire add a cupful of cream
5. Strain

title: Pear and Quince Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 pears
- 10 ripe quinces
- Juice 3 lemons
- 1 1/2 lbs. sugar to each 1 lb. fruit
- 1 pt. water to each lb. sugar

## Directions

1. Weigh fruit after it is ready to cook
2. Pare and core the fruit and throw into cold water
3. Stew parings and cores in a little cold water to make syrup
4. When the parings are well boiled strain off liquid
5. When cold put in fruit and bring quickly to a boil, boiling until smooth
6. Add the sugar and lemon juice, and cook steadily for an hour
7. Work with a spoon to a rich jelly

title: Pear Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large baking pears
- ½ lb. sugar
- ½ pt. wine
- 8 whole cloves
- ½ lemon
- ½ oz. gelatine

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and cut them into quarters
2. Put them into a shallow dish with sugar, cloves, and water enough to cover them
3. Stew until tender, not broken
4. Take the pears from the liquor and put them in a mould
5. To half a pint of liquor add the gelatine juice, grated rind of lemon and the wine
6. Let these ingredients boil quickly five minutes
7. Strain the warm liquid over the pears and set in a cool place

title: Pear Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pounds pears
- 12 pounds granulated sugar
- 1 pound sultana raisins
- 3 oranges
- 3 lemons
- 3 1/2 pound shelled walnuts

## Directions

1. Cut and pare pears
2. Add sugar, raisins, oranges, lemons, and walnuts
3. Put all through a mincer
4. Boil 3 or 4 hours

title: Pear Marmalade 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds pears
- 7 pounds sugar
- 3/4 pound preserved ginger cut fine
- juice of 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Peel and cut fine pears
2. Add sugar and let stand 24 hours
3. Add preserved ginger cut fine
4. Boil 3 hours
5. Add juice of 3 lemons and boil a few minutes longer

title: Pear Marmalade 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds pears
- 8 pounds sugar
- 4 lemons
- 1/2 pound crystallized ginger
- water

## Directions

1. Boil lemons whole in water until soft
2. Remove and chop fine, taking out seeds
3. Peel fruit and slice thinly
4. Put all in kettle, using water lemons were boiled in
5. Simmer gently until cooked, stirring almost constantly

title: Pear Panape
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds pears
- 6 pounds sugar
- 2 oranges
- 2 lemons
- 2 ounces preserved ginger

## Directions

1. Cut pears in 8 pieces
2. Cut rinds of orange and lemon fine
3. Squeeze pulp
4. Put altogether over slow fire, using no water
5. Cook until well preserved

title: Pear Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- white wine vinegar
- white sugar
- whole spice
- cloves

## Directions

1. Peel pears and stick a clove in blossom end
2. Have white wine vinegar scalding with enough white sugar to make pretty sweet
3. Have mixed whole spice in a bag
4. Put in pears and cook till tender
5. Take out pears in bottles and pour the boiling vinegar over and seal

title: Peel Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 lb. sugar
- 1/4 lb. lemon peel
- 1 lb. flour
- 1/4 lb. citron peel
- 1/2 lb. almonds
- 1/4 lb. orange peel
- 1 gill brandy
- 1 lb. butter

## Directions

1. Beat yolks and whites separately; lemon, orange, and citron peel cut into small pieces, butter beaten to a cream, add sugar, almonds chopped very fine, flour, yolks of eggs and brandy.
2. Add whites of eggs last.
3. This cake will keep for months in a cool dry place.

title: Penoche
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup light brown sugar
- 1 cup cream
- 1 cup walnuts, chopped fine
- butter size of a walnut
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar and cream in water till it makes a ball in water
2. Put in the butter, vanilla and nuts
3. Beat creamy
4. Spread on a platter

title: Pepper Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup syrup
- 3/4 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- 3/4 teaspoon black pepper
- 3/4 nutmeg
- 1 cup raisins
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together

title: Pepper Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 dozen peppers
- 1/2 dozen onions
- 1/2 cup salt
- 1 head cabbage
- vinegar
- 1/2 cup white mustard seed
- 1 tablespoon celery seed
- sugar to suit the taste

## Directions

1. Chop peppers and onions fine
2. Add salt and let drain over night
3. Chop cabbage fine
4. Mix the whole together and cover with vinegar
5. Add mustard seed and celery seed
6. Add sugar to suit the taste
7. Let come to a boil

title: Perdrix aux Choux
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- turkey dressing
- pork fat
- lard
- pepper
- salt
- four onions
- cloves
- one small cabbage
- four slices of pork and bacon

## Directions

1. Stuff partridge with turkey dressing
2. Stick on breast pieces of pork fat
3. Put lard down in pot and brown the partridge alone with pepper and salt
4. Roast well for twenty minutes in a covered pot
5. Take out the partridge and put in the pot four onions stuck with cloves, one small cabbage cut in four, four slices of pork and bacon
6. Fry brown

title: Perdrix Aux Choux
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- turkey dressing
- pork fat
- lard
- pepper
- salt
- four onions
- cloves
- one small cabbage
- four slices of pork and bacon
- hot water
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Stuff partridge with turkey dressing
2. Stick on breast pieces of pork fat
3. Put lard down in pot and brown the partridge alone with pepper and salt
4. Roast well for twenty minutes in a covered pot
5. Take out the partridge and put in the pot four onions stuck with cloves, one small cabbage cut in four, four slices of pork and bacon; fry brown
6. Put back the partridge and cover with hot water
7. Simmer gently for an hour
8. Before taking off put in a tablespoonful of browned flour

title: Perfection White Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- whites 4 eggs beaten very stiff

## Directions

1. Bake in 2 layers and put together with boiled icing

title: Perpetual Yeast
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups potato water
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 3 cups warm water
- 3 cups of flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon salt

## Directions

1. Add 3 cups potato water, 1 cup granulated sugar and 1 White Swan Yeast Cake together
2. Let stand 48 hours where temperature is moderate, then seal in 2 quart can and set in cool place
3. At noon, save 3 cups potato water and add 3/4 cup granulated sugar
4. Pour in the yeast
5. At night take 3 cups of this liquid and to it add 3 cups warm water (always cup for cup)
6. Allow 3 cups of flour to 1 of liquid
7. Add 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon salt
8. Knead well and put in pan to rise
9. In morning make into loaves
10. If yeast is put with potato water at night you may knead stiff in morning
11. Grease loaves with melted butter just before placing in oven

title: Philadelphia Ice Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of cream
- one small cup of sugar
- one tablespoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and flavoring with cream.
2. When the sugar is dissolved strain into the freezer.

title: Picalli
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 gallons cabbage chopped fine
- 1 gallon green tomatoes chopped fine and drained
- 12 large onions chopped fine
- 1.5 pounds brown sugar
- 1/2 pound mustard seed
- 1 ounce celery seed
- 1 ounce cloves
- 1 ounce allspice
- 1 ounce pepper
- 1 ounce tumeric
- 1 gill salt
- 1 gallon good vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil 30 minutes slowly

title: Picalli 2
body:
## Ingredients

- Green tomatoes cut fine
- cabbage
- onions
- celery
- vinegar
- sugar
- spices
- pepper
- 1 cup of horseradish

## Directions

1. Put on stove with vinegar, sugar, spices, pepper, and horseradish
2. Boil until cooked

title: Pickle for Tongue
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls salt
- 1 tablespoon saltpetre
- 1/8 teaspoon allspice
- 1/8 teaspoon cloves
- tongue

## Directions

1. Rub the tongue well, and leave in pickle for 10 days.
2. Boil gently until tender, press and jelly.

title: Pickled Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck sweet apples
- 3 pounds sugar
- 2 quarts vinegar
- 1/2 ounce cinnamon
- 1/2 ounce cloves

## Directions

1. Pare the apples, leaving them whole
2. Boil them in part of the vinegar and sugar until you can put a fork through them
3. Take them out
4. Heat the remainder of the vinegar and sugar
5. Pour over them
6. Be careful not to boil them too long or they will break

title: Pickled Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pints butter beans
- salted water
- 3 pints vinegar
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1 dessertspoon celery seed
- 1 dessertspoon turmeric powder
- 3/4 cup mustard
- 1 cup flour
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil 4 pints butter beans in salted water
2. drain
3. prepare mixture of 3 pints vinegar, 1 pound brown sugar, boil together
4. add 1 dessertspoon celery seed, 1 dessertspoon turmeric powder, 3/4 cup mustard, 1 cup flour
5. mix together with cold vinegar
6. add to boiling vinegar and sugar
7. boil all for 5 minutes, stirring constantly

title: Pickled Beans (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck butter beans
- weak salt water
- 3 pints cider vinegar
- 3 pounds white sugar
- 1 large cup flour
- 1 cup mustard
- 2 tablespoons celery seed
- 2 teaspoons turmeric powder
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut beans small, wash and put on to boil in weak salt water
2. boil 3 pints cider vinegar and 3 pounds white sugar together
3. prepare 1 large cup of flour, 1 cup mustard, 2 tablespoons celery seed, 2 teaspoons turmeric powder
4. mix to a paste with cold vinegar
5. stir into your boiling vinegar and sugar
6. pour over cooked beans
7. bottle and seal

title: Pickled Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- young beets
- vinegar
- sugar
- spices as liked

## Directions

1. Cook beets till tender
2. Peel beets
3. Place in bottles whole
4. Pour hot vinegar over beets
5. Seal bottles
6. Will keep 2 years
7. If too large, may be sliced or quartered

title: Pickled Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled beets
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice cold boiled beets
2. cover with vinegar and let stand for a few hours before serving

title: Pickled Beets for Winter Use
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups vinegar
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup water
- beets

## Directions

1. Boil vinegar, sugar, and water together
2. Boil beets
3. Slice beets into sealers
4. Pour liquor over while all is hot

title: Pickled Cherries
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries (5 pounds)
- vinegar (1 quart)
- sugar (2 pounds)
- cinnamon (0.75 ounce)
- cloves (0.5 ounce)
- mace (1 ounce)

## Directions

1. Grind spices and tie in a muslin bag
2. Boil sugar, vinegar and spices together
3. Pour hot over the cherries

title: Pickled Lemons
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 doz. lemons
- 1 oz. whole mace
- 1 oz. nutmeg, sliced
- 1/2 oz. cloves
- 2 oz. ginger, bruised
- 1/2 oz. peppercorns, white
- 1/2 oz. Jamaica long pepper and cayenne pods
- 1/2 oz. shallots, pared
- a piece of horseradish
- 3 qts. vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Take lemons, grate off the rind, prick them, and put them in a dish so as not to touch each other.
2. Cover them with salt, and turn them every day for a month.
3. Wipe them dry and let them remain within the air of the fire three or four days to dry.
4. Take mace, nutmeg, cloves, ginger, white peppercorns, Jamaica long pepper and cayenne pods and tie them in two or three separate muslin bags.
5. Put the lemons and seasoning in well glazed jars.
6. Boil vinegar and pour it on the lemons while hot.
7. Let the jars remain near the fire a few days, shaking them gently each day.
8. Look at them in a few weeks to see if they require more vinegar.

title: Pickled Mushrooms
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- small button mushrooms
- flannel
- vinegar
- salt
- mace
- cloves

## Directions

1. Take the small buttons and rub them very clean with a flannel and some vinegar, then put them in a dish with a little salt over them to draw the liquor from them.
2. When this is done put them into a kettle with their own liquor and sufficient good vinegar to cover them, also some mace, cloves

title: Pickled Mushrooms
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- small button mushrooms
- vinegar
- salt
- mace
- cloves
- black pepper

## Directions

1. Take the small buttons and rub them very clean with a flannel and some vinegar
2. Put them in a dish with a little salt over them to draw the liquor from them
3. When this is done put them into a kettle with their own liquor and sufficient good vinegar to cover them, also some mace, cloves and black pepper to suit taste
4. Boil gently for a few minutes
5. Bottle and cork tightly

title: Pickled Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- salt
- water
- whole peppers
- mace
- cloves
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash them from their liquor and put them into a porcelain-lined kettle, with strong salt and water to cover them
2. Let them come to a boil, and then skim them into cold water
3. Scald whole peppers, mace and cloves in a little vinegar, the quantity of these to be determined by the number of oysters
4. When oysters are cold put into a stone jar with layers of spice between them
