7. Pour the hot sugar on the froth
8. Flavor to suit the taste and beat till cool enough to stay on the cake
9. Put layers together with boiled icing

title: Prince of Wales Dessert
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sponge cakes, stale preferred
- sherry or syrup
- three-quarters pound cornflour
- one ounce arrowroot
- one pint boiling milk
- one teaspoon vanilla
- red currant jelly
- chopped pistachio nuts
- whipped cream
- cherries
- chopped citron peel

## Directions

1. Take sponge cakes, stale preferred; dip in sherry or syrup and line a mould with them.
2. Take three-quarters pound cornflour, one ounce arrowroot, and mix together.
3. Add one pint boiling milk and cook a few minutes.
4. Add one teaspoon vanilla and pour this into the lined basin.
5. When cold turn out.
6. Put a large spoonful of red currant jelly on the top, and sprinkle well with chopped pistachio nuts.
7. Alternatively, put the cakes soaked in raspberry juice or wine in a glass dish; fill as above over the top of this.
8. When cold spread whipped cream; decorate with cherries and chopped citron peel.
9. Serve very cold and in the glass dish in which it was made.

title: Priscilla Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts onions
- 2 quarts green tomatoes
- 2 quarts green cucumbers
- 4 bunches celery
- 2 small or 1 large cabbage cut fine
- salt
- 1 gallon vinegar
- 3/4 pound mustard
- 1 cup flour
- 1 ounce turmeric
- 2 3/4 cups brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon celery seed

## Directions

1. Salt vegetables over night and drain well in morning
2. Put in kettle with 1 gallon vinegar
3. Cook 15 minutes
4. Mix mustard, turmeric and flour with cold vinegar and add to mixture
5. Cook 15 minutes longer

title: Prize Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups raisins
- 3 cups sugar
- 4 cups currants
- 1/2 pound lemon peel
- 1 cup molasses
- 2 cups buttermilk
- 1 pound butter
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 teaspoons cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- a saltspoon salt
- butter size of an egg
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- whites of 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Combine 4 cups raisins, 3 cups sugar, 4 cups currants, 1/2 pound lemon peel, 1 cup molasses, 2 cups buttermilk, 1 pound butter, 1 teaspoon soda, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 2 teaspoons cinnamon, 1 teaspoon cloves and 1 teaspoon nutmeg.
2. Bake with slow fire.
3. For White Part: beat 1 cup granulated sugar, a saltspoon salt, butter size of an egg, and 1/2 cup milk together.
4. Fold in 1 1/2 cups flour and the whites of 3 eggs beaten light with 1 teaspoon baking powder.
5. Bake in layers.
6. Put together with fruit part and ice with caramel frosting.

title: Prune Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of prunes
- Half a box of gelatine
- Sugar to taste
- A pinch of salt
- Essence of vanilla or juice and rind of a lemon
- Juice of two oranges

## Directions

1. Wash the prunes
2. Boil slowly till soft in sufficient water to cover
3. Take out the stones
4. Sweeten to taste
5. Add essence of vanilla, or if preferred the juice and rind of a lemon, and the juice of two oranges
6. Soak the gelatine till soft
7. Add the above, stir all well together
8. Pour into a mould and let stand till it is very cold

title: Prune Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of prunes, well washed
- Water
- Sugar
- Gelatine soaked in one cup of water
- Juice of lemon
- Juice of orange

## Directions

1. Cover prunes with water and allow to soak for six hours
2. Put on to boil in same water until tender
3. Add sugar, and boil ten minutes
4. Strain and remove stones
5. Put on stove juice from prunes equal to two and a half cupfuls
6. Add juice of lemon and orange
7. When this commences to boil add gelatine and prunes
8. Turn into mould and serve when cold with whipped cream

title: Prune Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of stewing prunes
- Half a package of gelatine
- About half a cupful of sugar
- Water
- Whipped cream

## Directions

1. Wash prunes well and leave them to soak about an hour
2. Put half a package of gelatine to soak
3. Put the prunes in a saucepan on the fire, well covered with water and about half a cupful of sugar
4. Boil for about an hour
5. Strain the juice from the prunes
6. Add the gelatine to the juice and put on the fire to boil up
7. Cover the prunes with the juice and gelatine mixed
8. Put in hot in a mould and leave till cold
9. Serve with whipped cream

title: Prune Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound prunes
- 1 quart cold water
- 3/2 cup gelatine
- cold water
- 1 lemon sliced thin, rind and all
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Soak prunes over night in 1 quart cold water
2. Boil in the morning in the same water till soft
3. Remove from stove and take out stones
4. Have ready 3/2 cup gelatine which has been dissolved in cold water
5. Add 1 lemon sliced thin, rind and all, to the prunes
6. Boil a few minutes longer and turn into mould
7. Serve with whipped cream

title: Prune Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound prunes
- 1 quart cold water
- 3 1/2 cup gelatine dissolved in cold water
- 1 lemon sliced thin, rind and all
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Soak prunes over night in 1 quart cold water
2. Boil in the morning in the same water till soft
3. Remove from stove, take out stones
4. Have ready 3 1/2 cup gelatine which has been dissolved in cold water
5. Add 1 lemon sliced thin, rind and all, to the prunes
6. Boil a few minutes longer and turn into mould
7. Serve with whipped cream

title: Prune Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound prunes
- Whites of four eggs beaten very stiff
- Three tablespoons white sugar
- Cream

## Directions

1. Stew prunes and remove the pits.
2. Put sugar and whites of eggs together.
3. Add prunes.
4. Bake one-half hour.
5. Serve with cream.

title: Prune Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound prunes
- 1 teacupful sugar
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Stew one pound prunes and sweeten with one teacupful of sugar
2. Beat to a very stiff froth the whites of four eggs and stir lightly into the prunes when prunes are quite cold
3. Bake twenty minutes
4. Serve cold with cream

title: Prune Shape
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half pounds of best prunes (or figs)
- sugar
- one six-ounce packet of gelatine
- one pint of cold water
- one-half pint of boiling water
- juice of prunes
- one cup sugar
- a few drops of ratafia
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Stew prunes with a little sugar till quite tender
2. Strain the liquid from them and take out the stones
3. Dissolve gelatine in one pint of cold water
4. Add a small half pint of boiling water mixed with juice of prunes
5. add one cup sugar and a few drops of ratafia
6. Place the prunes round the mould and pour liquid over
7. Let it stand till quite cold
8. Blanched almonds are an improvement

title: Prune Whip
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound prunes
- beaten whites of 5 eggs
- 1 cup white sugar

## Directions

1. Boil prunes until tender and dry
2. remove the pits and crack, then chopping the nut with the prune pulp
3. add the beaten whites of eggs, and white sugar
4. bake in a moderate oven for 20 minutes

title: Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk
- 4 beaten eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. make a batter of milk, beaten eggs, flour, and salt
2. Be careful not to get batter too stiff
3. Pour batter into pan under the meat
4. let the juice from the meat drip on it while baking
5. It should be yellow-brown when done

title: Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup grated potatoes
- 1 cup grated carrots
- 1 cup suet
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup yellow sugar
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all the ingredients
2. Place in a mould
3. Steam 4 hours

title: Pudding a la Parisienne
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce butter
- one ounce sugar
- one ounce flour
- one gill milk
- vanilla
- three eggs
- four ounces mixed fruits: pears, peaches, glace cherries, angelica, apricots, pineapple
- wine sauce

## Directions

1. Dissolve butter in a sauce-pan, mix in sugar, flour, milk
2. Stir all over the fire until boiling
3. Cover it with a lid, and let it stand on a warm part of the stove for five minutes
4. Remove it to the table and stir in the yolks of the eggs
5. Whip the whites to a stiff froth and stir them in lightly and thoroughly
6. Add a few drops of vanilla essence
7. Add about four ounces of different sorts of fruit, cut into small pieces, such as pears, peaches, glace cherries, angelica, apricots, pineapple, etc.
8. Put the pudding into a plain round mould, which must be previously buttered, covered with a greased paper
9. Steam for three-quarters of an hour
10. Turn on to a dish and pour wine sauce round

title: Pudding Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup water
- two teaspoonfuls of flour or cornstarch
- butter the size of an egg
- pinch of salt
- nutmeg and sugar to taste
- teaspoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients

title: Pudding Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Combine ingredients

title: Puff Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups white sugar
- 3 eggs
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix white sugar, eggs, butter, and milk
2. Add flour and baking powder
3. Bake quickly in loaf

title: Puff Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 3/4 cup white sugar
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 tablespoon lard
- 1 cup sour cream
- flour
- currants

## Directions

1. Beat whites and yolks of 3 eggs separately
2. Mix sugar, cream tartar, and baking soda
3. Add lard and sour cream
4. Beat to a stiff batter with flour
5. Put into well-greased tart tins
6. Shake a few currants on top of each

title: Puff Paste
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 2 cups flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 to 3/4 cup ice water

## Directions

1. Wash butter in ice water until soft and waxy
2. Place in napkin and knead it a little to free from moisture
3. Reserve 2 tablespoons butter and shape remainder into a circular piece 1/4 inch thick and keep on ice
4. Work 2 pounds butter into flour and moisten to a dough with ice water
5. Turn on a slightly floured board and knead 1 minute
6. Roll on a napkin and place on ice for 15 minutes
7. Pat and roll into a piece 1/8 inch thick
8. Place butter in centre of lower half and fold upper half of paste over it
9. Fold right side of paste over enclosed butter and left side under
10. Turn half way round, cover and let stand 5 minutes
11. Pat and roll 1/8 inch thick and fold from ends towards centre making 3 layers
12. Cover and let stand 5 minutes
13. Repeat twice, turning paste half-way round each time before rolling
14. Fold from end to centre and double making four layers
15. Put in cold place to chill or fold in a towel
16. Put in dripping pan and place between pans of crushed ice
17. After shaping, chill thoroughly before baking
18. Bake in very hot oven with greatest heat coming from the bottom
19. While rising, decrease the heat as needed by opening check to stove or placing basin of ice water in oven
20. Turn the paste frequently that it may rise evenly
21. Do not open door for first five minutes
22. When risen to full height, slip a pan under sheet on which paste is baking
23. Bake on a tin sheet covered with double thickness of brown paper or in a dripping pan

title: Puff Pastry
body:
## Ingredients

- ¾ pound flour
- ¾ pound butter
- Ice-cold water

## Directions

1. Wash the butter, form it into a flat, round cake, and put it away to chill
2. Make the flour into a stiff dough with ice water, and knead until perfectly smooth
3. Roll into two pieces a trifle larger than the butter pat; place the butter on one, moisten the edge, place the other on top, and press the edges together
4. Put aside to chill
5. Roll it out in one direction only, until about three times as long as broad, fold into three or four layers, turn around and roll again in the other direction, then set aside to chill
6. Roll and chill twice more in the same way, but fold it in half the last time
7. Roll out and cut into shape for baking
8. Bake in a rather hot oven at first, then cooler

title: Puff Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint flour
- pinch salt
- heaping teaspoon baking powder
- sweet milk
- whites of 2 eggs beaten stiff
- preserved fruit

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pint flour, pinch salt and heaping teaspoon baking powder into a soft batter with sweet milk, add whites of 2 eggs beaten stiff
2. Have ready well-greased cups, this quantity requires 6 or 7
3. Into each put a couple teaspoons batter, then a little preserved fruit, then more batter, leaving plenty room to rise
4. Steam 20 or 30 minutes
5. Serve hot with sauce

title: Puff Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint flour
- pinch salt
- heaping teaspoon baking powder
- sweet milk
- whites of 2 eggs
- preserved fruit
- 2 yolks
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- flavoring to suit

## Directions

1. Mix 1 pint flour, pinch salt and heaping teaspoon baking powder into a soft batter with sweet milk
2. Add whites of 2 eggs beaten stiff
3. Have ready well-greased cups, this quantity requires 6 or 7
4. Into each put a couple teaspoons batter, then a little preserved fruit, then more batter, leaving plenty room to rise
5. Steam 20 or 30 minutes
6. Serve hot with sauce made of the 2 yolks, a cup milk, 2 tablespoons sugar, flavoring to suit

title: Puffed or Souffle Potatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- cold water
- fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and cut the sides square, and trim off the corners, so as to give an oval shape.
2. With one even cut slice them one-eighth of an inch thick the length of the potato; they must be all the same size and shape.
3. Soak them in cold water for half an hour and dry them on a napkin.
4. Fry them in fat which is only moderately hot until they are soft, but not colored.
5. Remove and place them on a sieve to drain and cool.
6. Immerse them in hot fat, when they will puff into balls.
7. Toss the basket, and remove any that do not puff.
8. Sprinkle with salt, and serve them on a napkin, or as a garnish.

title: Pufferts or Potato Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large potatoes (grated or ground in a food chopper)
- 1 egg well beaten
- a little salt
- grease

## Directions

1. Grate or grind potatoes in a food chopper, and add 1 egg well beaten, and a little salt
2. Fry like griddle cakes, only use a little more grease and have it very hot
3. Use 1 large spoonful for 1 pancake

title: Pumpkin Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds pumpkins cut very fine
- 7 pounds sugar
- 3 lemons
- 6 oranges with rind

## Directions

1. Put the sugar on the pumpkins over night
2. Put lemons and oranges in and boil 2 3/4 hours
3. Stir while boiling

title: Pumpkin Marmalade 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 pounds pumpkins cut up small
- 5 pounds granulated sugar
- 2 ounces root ginger
- 3 lemons
- 3 oranges

## Directions

1. Let all stand together over night
2. Cook till it thickens

title: Pumpkin Marmalade 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quarts pumpkin
- 3 quarts sugar
- juice of 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Cut the pumpkin in dice
2. Let stand over night
3. Cook in morning

title: Pumpkin Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 cups pumpkin, cooked
- 2 cupfuls milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful molasses
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 teaspoonful butter
- 1 teaspoonful cinnamon
- 1 teaspoonful ginger
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. To pulp add milk, salt, butter, cinnamon, and ginger, molasses, eggs, and sugar.
2. Add the beaten eggs last and after the mixture is cold.
3. Pour it into an open crust and bake slowly forty to fifty minutes.

title: Pumpkin Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cups cooked pumpkin
- 3 eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 2 finely rolled crackers
- 1 teaspoonful ginger
- 1/4 nutmeg
- Cinnamon and salt to taste
- rich milk

## Directions

1. Put pumpkin in a pan with eggs, sugar, molasses (maple preferred), crackers, ginger, nutmeg, cinnamon and salt.
2. Stir well, add enough rich milk to thin.
3. Bake in pastry shape.

title: Pumpkin Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three-quarters cupful of pumpkin (well mashed)
- one and a half cupfuls of milk
- half cupful of sugar
- one egg
- half a teaspoonful of ginger
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Mix together three-quarters cupful of pumpkin (well mashed), one and a half cupfuls of milk, half cupful of sugar, one egg, half a teaspoonful of ginger, nutmeg, cinnamon and cloves.

title: Pumpkin Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts stewed mash pumpkins
- 1 quart rich milk
- 6 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups light brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon allspice
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon ginger
- Nutmeg
- Juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. To make 4 large pies, combine all ingredients and bake

title: Pumpkin Pie (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups mashed pumpkin
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 2 cups milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 dessertspoon butter
- 1 small teaspoon salt
- 1 good teaspoon ginger
- 1 small teaspoon cinnamon

## Directions

1. Put all in but the eggs while the pumpkin is warm
2. The above quantities make filling for 2 pies

title: Pumpkin Pie Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups mashed pumpkin
- 1 pint sweet milk
- 2 soda biscuits rolled fine
- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 teaspoon ginger
- A little grated nutmeg
- 3/4 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. This makes enough for 2 pies

title: Pumpkin Pie Without Eggs (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint cooked pumpkin
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1 quart good hot milk
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- A little salt
- 4 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients

title: Pumpkin Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup sweet milk
- lump butter size of an egg
- nutmeg
- pumpkin

## Directions

1. Cut a fair-sized pumpkin in halves
2. Remove seeds and waste matter
3. Cut out the meat from one half, leaving only the rind whole
4. Place in other half and cover with hollow half
5. Set in kettle of boiling water to cook
6. When done, remove from water and add the eggs beaten well
7. Add sugar, milk, butter and flavor with nutmeg to taste
8. Stir together thoroughly until the whole assumes the form of a custard
9. Replace the pumpkin covering and bake in a moderate oven

title: Pumpkin Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup of cooked pumpkin
- 1 quart milk
- salt
- 1 tablespoon hot melted butter
- croutons
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut pumpkin in small pieces, sprinkling lightly with salt
2. Cook in boiling water for five minutes
3. Drain off the water
4. Mash thoroughly
5. Stir in hot melted butter
6. Pour boiling milk over croutons arranged in soup dish and lightly sugared
7. Add the pumpkin
8. Stir the whole well together and serve

title: Puree of Green Peas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- half a peck of peas
- hot, salted water
- mint (a sprig)
- flour (1 tablespoonful)
- butter (2 tablespoonfuls)
- pepper
- salt
- cream (3 tablespoonfuls)
- soda (a tiny bit)
- fried bread

## Directions

1. Shell half a peck of peas and set them in a cold place while you boil the pods for twenty minutes in just enough hot, salted water to cover them.
2. Strain them; return the water to the fire with the peas and a sprig of mint, and boil until they are soft enough to rub through a colander.
3. When you have pressed all through that will go, stir into them a cupful of the water in which they were cooked, season with pepper and salt and put back into the colander.
4. As they begin to simmer stir in a roux of one tablespoonful of flour, cooked for three minutes in two tablespoonfuls of butter, cook one minute, take from the fire and add three tablespoonfuls of cream, that have been heated with a tiny bit of soda.
5. Pour upon squares of fried bread laid on a hot platter.

title: Putting Up Butter to Keep
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pure common salt
- white sugar
- saltpetre
- butter
- brine
- egg

## Directions

1. Take of the best pure, common salt two quarts, one ounce of white sugar and one of saltpetre
2. Pulverize them together completely
3. Work the butter well, then thoroughly work in an ounce of this mixture to every pound of butter
4. Make the butter into half-pound rolls
5. Put into a brine made of three gallons of brine strong enough to bear an egg, with a quarter of a pound of white sugar added

title: Quail on Toast
body:
## Ingredients

- salt
- pepper
- flour
- water
- butter
- baker's bread
- browned flour

## Directions

1. After the birds are nicely cleaned cut them open down the back
2. Salt and pepper them, and dredge with flour
3. Break down the breast and back bones so they will lie flat
4. Place them in a pan with very little water and butter in a hot oven, covering them up tightly until nearly done
5. Place in a spider in hot butter, and fry a moment till a nice brown
6. Have ready slices of baker's bread toasted, and slightly buttered upon a platter
7. The toast should be broken down with a carving knife so that it will be tender
8. On this place the quail
9. Make a sauce of the gravy in the pan, thicken lightly with browned flour and pour over each quail and the toast

title: Quaker Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup oats (Quaker)
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 3 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Mix and bake as muffins

title: Queen Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup boiling water
- 1 cup flour
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. mix as cream puffs
2. drop by spoonfuls and fry in deep fat
3. Drain
4. fill with preserves or cream filling
5. sprinkle with powdered sugar

title: Queen of Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 cups flour
- 3/4 teaspoon baking powder
- 3/4 cup milk

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add beaten egg
3. Add milk and flour and baking powder alternately
4. Bake in buttered gem pans

title: Queen of Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts white onions
- 2 quarts green tomatoes
- 2 quarts green cucumbers
- 2 large cauliflowers
- 1 bunch celery
- 1 dozen green peppers
- cayenne
- salt
- vinegar
- 4 cups brown sugar
- 1/2 cup flour
- 3.5 cup mustard
- 1 ounce tumeric
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Cut up vegetables and sprinkle salt on over night
2. Drain off brine and cook all the above in vinegar until tender, not soft
3. Strain off vinegar and add to it 4 cups brown sugar, 1/2 cup flour, 3.5 cup mustard, 1 ounce tumeric, 1 egg, 1 tablespoon butter
4. Boil egg, vinegar, butter, mustard and flour together, being careful not to burn
5. Turn on vegetable and bottle

title: Queen of Puddings
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint bread crumbs
- 1 quart milk
- 5 tablespoons sugar
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoon lemon extract
- jelly
- 1 tablespoon brown sugar

## Directions

1. Beat eggs and sugar together, leaving out whites of 2 for meringue
2. Stir in 1/2 pint milk
3. Put bread crumbs into a buttered pudding dish
4. Pour the other pint milk over them, then the eggs
5. Cook in a moderate oven 1 hour
6. Spread over top jelly and whites of eggs sweetened with 1 tablespoon brown sugar

title: Queen of Puddings 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups bread crumbs
- 4 cups milk
- 4 eggs yolks
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 lemon grated rind only
- 1 cup sugar
- jelly
- 4 eggs whites
- 1 lemon juice
- 3/4 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Soak bread crumbs in milk until soft
2. Beat the eggs, sugar and a little salt together
3. Add to the bread and milk with the butter and lemon
4. Mix thoroughly
5. Bake in a quick oven, but do not let it get watery
6. After it is cooked spread with jelly
7. Frost with whites of eggs, lemon juice and sugar mixed together and whipped until light

title: Queen Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes chopped fine
- 3 red peppers
- 6 onions
- 1 cup salt
- ground cloves
- allspice
- cinnamon
- 7/8 cup grated horseradish
- 1 cup sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop green tomatoes, red peppers, and onions fine
2. Add 1 cup salt and mix all together
3. Let drain over night
4. In morning add 1 teaspoon each of ground cloves, allspice, and cinnamon
5. Add 7/8 cup grated horseradish and 1 cup sugar
6. Cover with vinegar and boil till tender
7. Bottle

title: Queen's Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes
- 2 heads cabbage
- 3 or 4 green cucumbers
- 8 or 10 large onions
- 5 or 6 green peppers
- salt
- vinegar
- 2 pounds sugar
- 2 tablespoons mustard
- 2 tablespoons curry powder
- 2 tablespoons tumeric
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- celery seed
- pickling spice

## Directions

1. Sprinkle with salt and let stand over night
2. Next morning drain
3. Chop all fine and let stand over night
4. Drain next morning, cover with vinegar
5. Add sugar, mustard, curry powder, tumeric, cinnamon, and cloves
6. Use celery seed and pickling spice to suit taste
7. Tie in a cloth and cook tender

title: Quenelles For Clear Or White Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce of butter
- 1 dessertspoonful of flour
- 1 tablespoon of cream
- 2 ounces of boiled macaroni
- 2 ounces of Parmesan cheese grated
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Melt an ounce of butter in a stew-pan over a gentle fire
2. Beat it up with a dessertspoonful of flour and a tablespoon of cream, so as to make a thick paste
3. Add two ounces of boiled macaroni, two ounces of Parmesan cheese grated, a little salt, pepper, and a grate of nutmeg
4. Beat the mixture over the fire till smooth and firm and leaves the sides of the saucepan with the spoon
5. Mould it into quenelles with a teaspoon dipped in hot water
6. Poach them in boiling gravy till they are done thoroughly
7. Lift them out with a skimmer, and put them into the tureen with the soup

title: Quick Aspic
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/4 cups cold water
- 1 tablespoon chopped celery
- 1 tablespoon chopped carrot
- 1 teaspoon beef extract
- 1 cup hot water
- 1 slice onion
- 1 bay leaf
- 3 cloves
- 1 sprig parsley
- 1 ounce gelatine
- 3/4 cup water for soaking
- 1 tablespoon sherry or tarragon vinegar
- kitchen bouquet or caramel

## Directions

1. Put 1 3/4 cups cold water, celery, carrot, beef extract dissolved in 1 cup hot water, onion, bay leaf, cloves, and parsley in a saucepan
2. Cover and let simmer 3/4 hour
3. Soak 1 ounce gelatine in 3/4 cup water for an hour
4. Add gelatine to saucepan and stir until dissolved
5. Season with tablespoon sherry or tarragon vinegar
6. If deeper color is desired, add a few drops kitchen bouquet or caramel
7. Clear if necessary and strain through double cloth
8. If for moulding use at once; if for garnishing, turn into a shallow pan to set
9. Can be stamped or cut into fancy shapes more easily if cooked in layers of right thickness
10. Observe always the proportion of 2 ounces gelatine to 6 cups liquid

title: Quick Coffee Cake for Breakfast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- ¾ cup sugar
- 3 level teaspoons baking powder
- ¾ teaspoon salt
- ¼ teaspoon cinnamon
- 4 tablespoons melted butter
- 1 well-beaten egg
- ½ cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Sift together twice flour, sugar, baking powder, salt and cinnamon
2. Mix in a soft dough with melted butter, egg and sweet milk
3. Spread in a shallow pan and sprinkle thickly with sugar and cinnamon
4. Bake in a quick oven and serve fresh and hot for breakfast

title: Quick Graham Bread
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint sour milk
- One teaspoonful of soda
- Half a cup of molasses
- Half teaspoon salt
- Graham flour to make a stiff dough
- A little shortening

## Directions

1. Stir in graham flour to make a stiff dough
2. Bake in a quick oven

title: Quick Method Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 3/4 cup water
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 teaspoon lard or butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Put the sugar and yeast into a cup, add 3/4 cup of the water warmed to 90° F., mix until the whole is smooth and the sugar dissolved, and stand in a temperature of 80° F. for 15 or 20 minutes to begin fermenting.
2. Put the milk on to scald.
3. Measure the salt and shortening into the kneading-pan, pour in the scalded milk, add the rest of the water, and bring the temperature to about 90° F.
4. Add strong flour enough at the same temperature to make a batter that will beat without spattering; add the yeast, now fermenting vigorously, and beat with a wooden spoon until it looks smooth and elastic.
5. Mix in at once enough warm flour to make a dough that will not stick to the fingers, turn out on the moulding board and knead as usual.
6. Return to the warm greased kneading-pan, cover closely, and stand in a temperature of 70° to 80° F. until risen to rather more than double in volume. This will take 2 to 3 hours.
7. Knead lightly in the pan for a minute to get rid of the larger bubbles, and stand back in the warm place to rise again until double in volume. This will take 1 to 2 hours.
8. Shape into loaves that will half fill the bread tins, kneading only to get rid of large bubbles, and smooth the surface.
9. Let rise until doubled in volume, then bake and cool as usual.

title: Quick Suet Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup chopped suet
- cold water
- raspberries or any fruit preferred

## Directions

1. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt
2. Add chopped suet
3. Make in a batter with cold water
4. Put raspberries or any fruit preferred in the bottom of pudding dish
5. Pour batter on
6. Steam 1 hour

title: Quince Honey
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large quinces
- 2 large Spitzenburg apples grated
- 4 pounds white sugar
- 2 quarts water

## Directions

1. Grate the quinces and apples and put on to boil with the water for 15 minutes
2. Have your sugar warm and add
3. Let boil till thick

title: Quince Honey
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large quinces
- 2 large Spitzenburg apples
- 4 pounds white sugar
- 2 quarts water

## Directions

1. Grate the quinces and apples and put on to boil with the water for 15 minutes
2. Have your sugar warm and add
3. Let boil till thick

title: Quince Honey
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 quinces
- 2 apples
- 1 quart of water
- 5 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Grate them or put through your finest meat chopper
2. Have on the stove 1 quart of water and 5 pounds sugar melted
3. Add quinces and apples and boil 30 minutes, stirring that it does not burn

title: Quince Honey 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 quinces
- 2 apples
- 1 quart of water
- 5 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Grate quinces and apples or put through your finest meat chopper
2. Have on the stove 1 quart of water and 5 pounds sugar melted
3. Add quinces and apples

title: Quince Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- water that quinces were boiled in
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take water that quinces were boiled in, add 3/4 pound sugar to a pint of juice and boil till it jells

title: Quince Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large quinces
- 3 pounds granulated sugar
- 1 pint water

## Directions

1. Grate the quinces
2. Add 1 pint water
3. Boil slowly for 2 hours

title: Rabbit or Old Hare Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One rabbit cut up
- One-half pound salt pork minced fine
- One large onion, chopped
- One stalk celery, chopped
- One sprig parsley, chopped
- One gallon of water
- One heaping tablespoonful brown roux
- One teaspoonful of Worcestershire sauce
- One tablespoonful tomato catsup
- One glassful brown sherry
- Juice of four lemons
- Two tablespoonfuls good drippings
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Fry the onion in the dripping, and when lightly browned, add the pieces of rabbit
2. Cover with cold water and cook very slowly for four hours, or until the meat is in rags
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Let all get cold together
5. Skim off the fat; strain through a coarse cloth, return to the fire and when it boils thicken with the roux
6. Put in the catsup, wine, lemon-juice, and, if you fancy, a pinch of ground allspice
7. If not brown enough, color with a little caramel

title: Radish Slaw
body:
## Ingredients

- radish
- cream
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash and peel radish
2. Grate
3. Let stand a few minutes
4. Drain off any liquid that has gathered
5. Add cream, salt and vinegar

title: Ragout of Duck and Green Peas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- remnants of a pair of roast ducks
- a quart of green peas

## Directions

1. Cook the remnants of a pair of roast ducks as directed in recipe for Salmi of Duck
2. When done pile the meat in the centre of the dish
3. Put a quart of green peas, well boiled and drained, about them like a green fence
4. Pour the gravy over all

title: Railroad Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- butter size of an egg
- 3 tablespoons milk
- a little salt
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Raised Muffins
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1.5 pts milk
- 0.25 teacup yeast
- 1 teaspoonful lard
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Sift the flour into a pan with a pinch of salt
2. Warm the milk and add lard, and stir into the flour
3. Beat the eggs light, add to the mixture
4. When thoroughly mixed add yeast
5. Set to rise about three hours before using
6. When very light bake in muffin rings in a quick oven
7. Serve the instant they come from the oven
8. Tear the muffins open when done, put a bit of butter in each and keep warm until served
9. Never cut them

title: Raisin and Nut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- ¾ cup sweet milk
- 2½ cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1½ cream tartar
- ⅜ cup raisins
- ½ cup nuts chopped fine

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Ice and put nuts on top

title: Raisin Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ½ cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 small teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 small nutmeg
- ¾ cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour to thicken

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in buttermilk
2. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Raisin Cake (Second Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup sour milk
- 3 cups flour
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- little spice if wished

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together and bake

title: Raisin Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1½ cups sugar
- 1 cup shortening
- ½ cup sweet cream
- 1 cup raisins seeded and chopped
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- a little nutmeg
- flour enough to make a soft dough

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Raisin Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 cup chopped and seeded raisins

## Directions

1. Let 1 cup white sugar, 1/2 cup sweet milk, and 1 teaspoon butter boil 5 minutes
2. Add 1 cup chopped and seeded raisins
3. Boil a few minutes longer
4. Stir until cool

title: Raisin Layer Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1/4 cup sour milk
- 1/4 teaspoonful soda
- 1/4 teaspoonful each cinnamon, nutmeg and allspice

## Directions

1. Bake in layers in a moderate oven.

title: Raisin Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/4 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 cups chopped raisins
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1/4 teaspoon cloves
- 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, sugar, butter, and sour milk
2. Add soda
3. Add chopped raisins
4. Add flour, cloves and cinnamon
5. Bake in layers

title: Raisin Layer Cake (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups chopped raisins
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1/4 cup sour milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- spice to taste

## Directions

1. Mix raisins, sugar, butter, sour milk and eggs
2. Add flour and soda
3. Add spice to taste
4. Bake in layers

title: Raisin Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound raisins chopped
- 2 eggs
- 1 lemon
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Butter half the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Combine ingredients

title: Raisin Pie (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups sweet cream
- Pinch of salt
- 1 cup seeded raisins chopped fine
- Flour
- Vanilla flavoring
- Baked crust
- Whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook till it thickens
2. Flavor with vanilla
3. If not quite thick enough add a little flour
4. Put into a baked crust
5. Beat the whites of the 2 eggs
6. Add a little sugar and vanilla and spread over the top
7. Set in the oven to harden a little

title: Raisin Puff
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup raisins, chopped
- 3 teaspoonfuls baking powder sifted in 2 cups flour
- 1 teacupful milk

## Directions

1. Steam one hour in an ordinary brown bread steamer.
2. Serve with whipped cream or cold sauce.

title: Raspberry Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- ⅜ cup butter
- ⅜ cup sugar
- 1 cup canned raspberries
- 2 small cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 tablespoon sour cream

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in 2 layers, or in loaf
3. For icing: Make boiled icing with 1 cup granulated sugar boiled with ½ cup water until it hairs, beaten white of 1 egg, and chopped raisins

title: Raspberry Cake (Second Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ½ cup butter
- 2 tablespoons sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 cup raspberries
- 2 cups flour
- cloves, cinnamon and nutmeg for seasoning

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Bake in 2 layers

title: Raspberry Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound white sugar
- 1/4 pound raspberry jam
- whites of 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat the whites of four eggs together with one-quarter pound white sugar and one-quarter pound raspberry jam for an hour

title: Raspberry Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound white sugar
- one-quarter pound raspberry jam
- whites of four eggs

## Directions

1. Beat together for an hour

title: Raspberry Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 or 6 pounds fine red raspberries (not too ripe)
- equal quantity of the finest quality white sugar
- 1 quart currant juice

## Directions

1. Mash the whole well in a preserving kettle
2. Add 1 quart currant juice and boil gently until it jellies upon a cold plate
3. Put into small jars, cover with brandied paper, and tie a thick white paper over them
4. Keep in a dark, dry and cool place

title: Raspberry or Blackberry Vinegar
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon berries
- cider-vinegar
- water
- sugar
- beeswax
- resin

## Directions

1. Put a gallon of berries into a great crock and crush them well with a potato-beetle or wooden mallet
2. Cover an inch deep in cider-vinegar
3. Set in the hot sunshine for a day and leave all night in the cellar
4. Stir six times during the day of sunning
5. Strain and squeeze the berries dry and throw them away
6. Put another gallon of mashed berries into the strained vinegar
7. Leave again in the sun all day and another night in the cellar
8. Strain and squeeze the berries and measure the liquid thus gained
9. For each quart allow a pint of water, and for every pint of the water thus added, five pounds of sugar
10. Turn into a porcelain-lined or agate-iron kettle and set over the fire, stirring until the sugar melts
11. Heat to boiling, and boil hard one minute to throw up the scum
12. Skim well, take from the fire, strain and, while still warm, bottle
13. Seal the corks with a mixture of beeswax and resin

title: Raspberry Tapioca
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup tapioca
- 4 cups boiling water
- 1 pint fresh berries
- sugar to taste
- cream

## Directions

1. Put 3/4 cup tapioca into a kettle
2. Cover with 4 cups boiling water
3. Cook until transparent
4. Stir into it 1 pint of fresh berries
5. Add sugar to taste
6. Pour into a mould
7. Serve cold with cream

title: Raspberry Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 quarts raspberries
- good strong vinegar
- 1 pint sugar to each pint of juice

## Directions

1. Take 9 quarts raspberries and cover with good strong vinegar
2. Let stand for 24 hours
3. Put all into thin muslin bag and let juice drain out
4. To each pint of juice add 1 pint sugar
5. Boil for 15 minutes

title: Raw Beef Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- finely minced beef
- salt
- pepper
- thick slices of buttered bread
- ale
- celery

## Directions

1. Season finely minced beef well
2. Spread between rather thick slices of buttered bread
3. Serve with ale and celery

title: Raw Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 1 cup salt
- 1 tablespoon pepper
- cayenne pepper
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 quart good vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and slice tomatoes
2. Strain in salt in alternate layers of tomatoes
3. Let stand over night
4. Drain off all juice
5. Chop fine
6. Add pepper, cayenne pepper, sugar, and vinegar
7. Bottle for use
8. Strain if desired

title: Raw Tomato Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 1 cup salt
- 1 tablespoon pepper
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1 quart good vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and slice tomatoes
2. Sprinkle with salt alternately
3. Let stand over night
4. Drain
5. Put through colander
6. Add pepper, brown sugar, and vinegar
7. Bottle for use

title: Recipe for Using Cold Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled or roasted beef
- 3 carrots
- 1 large onion
- dripping or butter
- 1 cup hot water
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Take cold boiled or roasted beef and chop with food chopper or a knife into small bits
2. Take 3 carrots and 1 large onion and cut up fine
3. Have the frying pan hot with good dripping or butter, and 1 cup of hot water
4. Drop in the vegetables and boil until tender, without the addition of more water
5. Add the meat and pepper and salt to taste
6. If any gravy left over, add when the meat is put in
7. If no gravy is on hand, water may be added, just in proportion as required, either for hash or a soup

title: Red Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 1/2 cup cooking sugar
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- salt to taste
- 1/2 teaspoon ginger
- 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper
- 3 tablespoons mixed spice

## Directions

1. Extract juice from tomatoes
2. Use 2 quarts juice
3. Add sugar, vinegar, salt, ginger, and cayenne pepper
4. Tie mixed spice in a bag and add
5. Boil 3 hours

title: Red Currant Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- currants (2 quarts)
- sugar (2 quarts)
- vinegar
- cinnamon (1 tablespoon)
- cloves (0.75 tablespoon)
- allspice (0.75 tablespoon)
- cayenne (0.75 tablespoon)

## Directions

1. Cover currants and sugar with vinegar
2. Add cinnamon, cloves, allspice, and cayenne
3. Boil slowly for 40 minutes

title: Red Currant Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. currants
- 1/4 lb. raisins
- 6 lbs. sugar
- 6 oranges
- 1/8 teaspoonful mace
- 1/8 teaspoonful cinnamon

## Directions

1. Boil the currants and press through a sieve.
2. Boil the yellow rind of the oranges in a little water and chop finely.
3. Chop raisins and pulp of oranges.
4. Boil all together till thick, half or three-quarters of an hour.
5. Half yellow rind after boil is sufficient.

title: Red Currant Snow
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiling water
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 1 cup sugar
- juice of 2 cups red currants
- whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook boiling water, cornstarch, and sugar until thick
2. Remove from fire
3. Add juice of red currants crushed and pressed through colander
4. Beat the egg whites
5. Add a little sugar to egg whites
6. Pour over all

title: Red Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart raw cabbage chopped fine
- 1 quart boiled beets chopped fine
- 1 cup grated horseradish
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 tablespoon black pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon red pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Cover with vinegar

title: Red Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck tomatoes
- 12 apples
- 6 onions
- 4 peppers chopped fine
- 1 pint vinegar
- 2 cups sugar

## Directions

1. Chop tomatoes, apples, onions, and peppers fine
2. Add 1 pint vinegar and 2 cups sugar
3. Boil until thick

title: Redhead or Canvasback Duck (Broiled)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- salad oil
- lemon-juice
- currant or grape jelly
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean and wipe with a soft, damp cloth within and without
2. Split down the back and flatten the protuberant breast-bone with the broadside of a hatchet
3. Leave them in a marinade of salad oil and lemon-juice for one hour, setting them in cold place
4. Without wiping them, broil over red, clear coals for twenty minutes, if they are plump and large; less time will do for small birds
5. Turn them twice
6. Serve with currant or grape jelly
7. When dishing put upon each breast a teaspoonful of butter beaten to a cream with lemon-juice and finely chopped parsley

title: Redhead or Canvasback Duck (Roasted)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- ducks
- salad oil
- lemon-juice
- currant jelly or three or four cranberries
- boiling water
- butter

## Directions

1. Singe and draw, but do not wash the ducks
2. Wipe them, inside and out, with a soft, damp cloth
3. Cut off the pinions and tie what is left of the wings to the bodies
4. Pepper and salt the cavity of the body
5. Wash out with salad oil and lemon-juice and put a teaspoonful of currant jelly, or three or four cranberries, in each
6. Put into your covered roaster
7. Pour half a cupful of boiling water into the dripping-pan beneath
8. Cover closely and cook half an hour, basting three times
9. Uncover, wash all over with a mixture of butter and lemon-juice, and brown
10. Serve with currant jelly

title: Remedy for Cough
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One ounce of Balm of Gilead buds
- One quart of water
- One pint of Bourbon whisky
- One pound of loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Put buds in a quart of water and boil down to a pint
2. Add one pint of Bourbon whisky and one pound of loaf sugar

title: Remedy for Dandruff
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Glycerine, four ounces
- Tincture of cantharides, five ounces
- Bay rum, four ounces
- Water, two ounces

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Apply once a day and rub well down the scalp

title: Rhubarb and Cornstarch Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb
- water
- sugar
- cornstarch
- sweetened cream

## Directions

1. Prepare rhubarb as for sauce
2. Boil with plenty water
3. Add sugar to make quite sweet
4. When well cooked add enough cornstarch previously dissolved in cold water to make quite thick
5. Stir briskly to keep smooth
6. Serve when quite cold with sweetened cream

title: Rhubarb and Orange Compote
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint bottle rhubarb
- 3 oranges
- 1 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the oranges, removing as much as possible of the white pith
2. divide into sections
3. put all the ingredients together into a preserving kettle and simmer gently for about an hour

title: Rhubarb and Pineapple Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart pineapple (peeled, sliced and cut in small cubes)
- 1 quart rhubarb
- 1 quart sugar
- a little water

## Directions

1. Cook well

title: Rhubarb and Pineapple Preserve 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds rhubarb
- 1 pineapple sliced and cut in small pieces
- 5 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Wash rhubarb but do not peel
2. Cut in pieces about 1 inch long and dry it
3. Slice and cut in small pieces 1 pineapple and boil it separate 1/2 hour
4. Mix with rhubarb
5. Add 5 pounds sugar and let simmer till cooked

title: Rhubarb and Raisin Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup raisins
- 1 1/2 cups rhubarb
- 1 cup sugar
- Lemon (grated rind only)
- 2 yolks

## Directions

1. Stone raisins
2. Chop fine with rhubarb
3. Add sugar and rind
4. Cook
5. As removed from fire add yolks
6. Stir vigorously

title: Rhubarb Custard Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups rhubarb
- 1 3/4 cups sugar
- 2 yolks
- About 1 tablespoon water
- Meringue

## Directions

1. Cook rhubarb with about 1 tablespoon water
2. Add sugar just as remove from fire
3. Add beaten yolks of eggs and stir vigorously
4. Put in shell
5. Cover with meringue

title: Rhubarb Jam
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds rhubarb
- 5 pounds sugar
- 1 pound figs
- 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Cut the rhubarb into small pieces
2. Peel the lemons very finely and cut into small pieces, also the figs
3. Put all together into an earthen vessel
4. Put half the sugar on and let it remain all night
5. Next day add the other sugar and boil it well for 3 hours

title: Rhubarb Jam (Green)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 lbs. green rhubarb
- 1 lb. sugar
- 1/2 oz. bitter almonds
- Rind of 1/2 lemon
- A little ginger

## Directions

1. Wipe the rhubarb quite dry, cut it into pieces about two inches long
2. Put it into a preserving-pan with the sugar, the rind of the lemon cut very fine, and the almonds blanched and divided
3. Boil the whole well together, taking care to stir and skim frequently
4. When it is nearly done stir in the ginger
5. Young rhubarb will take about three-quarters of an hour, but if old it must be boiled for an hour and a half

title: Rhubarb Jam (Red)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of rhubarb (the red kind)
- 4 pounds of loaf sugar
- 5 ounces whole ginger

## Directions

1. Peel and cut up the rhubarb into small pieces
2. Add the sugar and ginger
3. Boil until clear
4. Pot and tie down as for other preserves

title: Rhubarb Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cups rhubarb chopped fine
- 3 large oranges
- 5 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1 3/4 cups sugar to each cup rhubarb
- 1 cup chopped English walnuts

## Directions

1. Boil until thick

title: Rhubarb Marmalade 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds rhubarb
- 4 lemons
- 4 pounds sugar
- 3/4 pound broken walnuts

## Directions

1. Cut rhubarb and lemons small
2. Cover with water
3. Boil 1 1/2 hours
4. Add 4 pounds sugar and boil 15 minutes
5. Just before removing from the fire, add 3/4 pound broken walnuts

title: Rhubarb Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart rhubarb
- 1 pint onions
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups vinegar
- a pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoon mixed spice (ground)
- 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Boil well

title: Rhubarb Pickle 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint vinegar
- 7 pounds cut rhubarb (not peeled)
- 1 ounce whole cloves
- 1 ounce stick cinnamon
- 3 pounds brown sugar

## Directions

1. Boil until a little soft

title: Rhubarb Preserve
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb
- sugar
- lemon rind
- almonds blanched
- ginger

## Directions

1. Wipe the rhubarb quite dry and cut it into pieces about two inches long
2. Put it into a preserving-pan with the sugar, the rind of the lemon cut very fine, and the almonds blanched and divided
3. Boil the whole well together, taking care to stir and skim frequently
4. When it is nearly done stir in the ginger
5. Young rhubarb will take about three-quarters of an hour, but if old it must be boiled for an hour and a half

title: Rhubarb Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup white sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 2 cups rhubarb cut in dices
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix together sugar, eggs, cream, rhubarb, flour and baking powder
2. Bake in oven

title: Rhubarb Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb cut in pieces
- boiling water
- 4 pounds sugar to every gallon of wine
- 1 ounce whole ginger
- juice of 1 lemon
- 7 cayenne pods
- 1/2 ounce isinglass dissolved in a little hot water
- 1 gill of rum to every gallon of wine

## Directions

1. To every pint of rhubarb cut in pieces add 1 pint of boiling water
2. Let it stand a week, stirring it once every day
3. Strain and add 4 pounds sugar to every gallon of wine, 1 ounce whole ginger, the juice of 1 lemon, 7 cayenne pods, 1/2 ounce isinglass dissolved in a little hot water
4. Let it stand 2 weeks to ferment
5. Strain again and add 1 gill of rum to every gallon of wine
6. Bottle

title: Rhubarb, or Pie-Plant Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb
- sugar
- 1 cupful of sour milk
- 2 eggs
- a piece of butter the size of an egg
- 1/2 teaspoonful of soda
- flour

## Directions

1. Chop rhubarb pretty fine, put in a pudding-dish, and sprinkle sugar over it
2. Make a batter of one cupful of sour milk, two eggs, a piece of butter the size of an egg, half a teaspoonful of soda, and enough flour to make batter about as thick as for cake
3. Spread it over the rhubarb, and bake till done
4. Turn out on a platter upside down, so that the rhubarb will be on top
5. Serve with sugar and cream

title: Ribbon Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 3 eggs
- ½ cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- A pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoonful saleratus or 3 of baking powder
- Flavor with lemon or almond
- one tablespoon of molasses
- one large cup of raisins stoned and chopped
- one teaspoon of cinnamon
- half a teaspoon each of cloves and allspice
- nutmeg
- one spoonful of flour
- jelly or raspberry jam

## Directions

1. Put half the above in two oblong pans
2. To the remainder add one tablespoon of molasses, one large cup of raisins stoned and chopped, one teaspoon of cinnamon, half a teaspoon each of cloves and allspice, grate in a little nutmeg, then add one spoonful of flour
3. Put into two pans of the same size and shape as those above
4. Put the sheets together while warm, alternately, with a little jelly or raspberry jam between
5. Cut in thin slices for the table
6. It will cut most easily the day after it is baked
7. It may be baked in one large pan without the fruit, pouring in the dark and light in alternate layers
8. When baked thus it is a handsome marble cake

title: Ribbon Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 4 cups flour (scant)
- 4 eggs
- ⅜ teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 4 teaspoons cinnamon
- 1 cup currants
- ½ pound citron cut fine
- jelly for spreading

## Directions

1. Beat the butter to a cream, add the sugar gradually, beating all the while, then the flavoring
2. Beat the eggs very light, add them and the milk
3. Sift the flour, soda and cream tartar with it, beat quickly
4. Take 3 sheet pans of the same size, and in each of 2 put ⅓ of the mixture and bake
5. To the other ⅓ add 4 teaspoons cinnamon, 1 cup currants, ½ pound citron cut fine, and bake in the remaining pan
6. When done, spread the light cake with a thin layer of jelly while warm
7. Place on this the dark cake and spread with jelly, then other sheet of light cake
8. Press all together by putting a weight on top

title: Ribbon Cake (Second Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sweet milk
- ½ cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- ½ teaspoon soda
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon molasses
- ½ teaspoon each cinnamon, cloves, allspice and nutmeg
- ½ cup each raisins and currants
- jelly for putting together

## Directions

1. Dissolve the soda in the milk
2. Mix the cream tartar in the flour
3. Beat the eggs, sugar and butter well together
4. Add the milk and flour
5. Take 1 cup of this mixture, 1 teaspoon molasses, ½ teaspoon each cinnamon, cloves, allspice and nutmeg, ½ cup each raisins and currants
6. Dredge the raisins and currants with flour, mix with the molasses and spices into the cup batter taken from the first
7. Bake in oblong tins in 3 layers, put together with jelly, the dark part between the light parts

title: Ribbon Cake (Third Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 3 eggs beaten
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 1½ teaspoons baking powder
- melted chocolate
- pink cake coloring

## Directions

1. Beat together butter and sugar
2. Add 3 beaten eggs and beat again
3. Add 1 cup milk, beating well after each addition
4. Beat in 3 cups flour in which 1½ teaspoons baking powder have been sifted
5. Divide the batter into four parts, coloring 1 with melted chocolate and another with pink cake coloring
6. Bake in jelly tins, and put together with a filling

title: Rice and Meat Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup boiled rice
- 1 cup finely chopped left-over meat (beef)
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. put milk on to boil
2. add meat, rice and seasoning
3. when boiling add beaten egg

title: Rice Apple Dumpling
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of rice
- butter
- salt
- two well-beaten eggs
- squares of white muslin
- flour
- apples, peeled and cored
- currant jelly
- sugar
- spice
- cream or vanilla sauce

## Directions

1. Boil rice and season it with butter and salt.
2. After removing it from the fire stir in two well-beaten eggs.
3. Have ready squares of white muslin. Dip them one by one in hot water, sprinkle with flour and put in each two tablespoonfuls of the rice.
4. Spread the rice and in the centre place a peeled and cored apple.
5. In the cavity left by the core, put some currant jelly and sugar or spice and sugar.
6. Draw the corners of the cloth together and fasten closely at the top with pins.
7. Boil or steam for a half hour.
8. Serve with cream or vanilla sauce.

title: Rice Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teacup soft boiled rice
- yolk of 1 egg
- pinch salt
- 2 tablespoons sifted flour
- sweet milk
- beaten white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Beat together rice, yolk of egg, salt, and flour
2. Add sweet milk until about the consistency of sponge cake or thick cream
3. Just before baking stir in lightly the beaten white of an egg

title: Rice Croquettes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup of milk
- 1 generous tablespoonful of butter
- 1 heaping teaspoonful of sugar
- 1 1/2 cups of boiled rice
- 1 saltspoonful of powdered cinnamon or nutmeg
- salt
- 1 beaten egg
- hot butter

## Directions

1. Put three-quarters of a cup of milk in a sauce-pan over the fire with a generous tablespoonful of butter, a heaping teaspoonful of sugar, and, when it comes to a boil, add a cup and a half of boiled rice, a saltspoonful of powdered cinnamon or nutmeg, and salt to taste.
2. Mix well, let it come to a boil.
3. Add a beaten egg, remove from the fire, turn into a plate to get cold, form into cylinders and fry in hot butter.

title: Rice Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons cooked rice
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper
- 1/2 teaspoon minced parsley
- few grains of cayenne
- beaten egg
- cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Form rice mixture into balls
2. Roll in beaten egg and cracker crumbs
3. Fry in deep fat

title: Rice Croquettes II
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup rice
- 1/4 cup scalded milk
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- grated rind of 1 1/2 lemons
- 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
- eggs and crumbs for rolling
- jelly cubes

## Directions

1. Wash rice and cook until tender
2. Drain
3. Add water or milk, yolks of eggs and butter
4. Cool
5. Shape
6. Roll in eggs and crumbs
7. Fry
8. Put cubes of jelly on each

title: Rice Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled rice
- a little flour
- 1 beaten egg

## Directions

1. make into thin cakes or drop from a spoon
2. fry in butter to a golden brown
3. Lovely with maple syrup

title: Rice Griddle Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of rice
- three eggs
- three cups of milk
- half a cup of flour
- a pinch of salt
- baking powder

## Directions

1. Boil one cup of rice
2. Beat together three eggs, adding three cups of milk with the rice
3. Sift into this half a cup of flour to which a pinch of salt and baking powder has been added
4. Fry and serve immediately

title: Rice Muffins
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 scant cup boiled rice
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- A little salt
- 1 1/2 cups milk
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- 1 tablespoonful melted butter

## Directions

1. Combine boiled rice, egg, sugar, salt, and milk
2. Mix in flour and baking powder
3. Stir in melted butter
4. Bake in muffin tins

title: Rice Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart milk (boiled)
- 1 small teacup rice flour
- A little cold milk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- Five eggs well beaten
- Sugar to taste
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Mix 1 small teacup rice flour in a little cold milk
2. Add to the boiling milk
3. Add 2 tablespoons butter
4. When cold add five eggs well beaten
5. Sweeten to taste
6. Flavor with vanilla and bake

title: Rice Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teacupful rice
- 3 tablespoonfuls butter
- 5 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 1 qt. cream
- 1 glass wine
- 5 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of ground nutmeg
- salt
- 1 lb. raisins, cut in halves
- 1 lb. Zante currants
- 1 lb. citron cut in strips

## Directions

1. Wash rice, and boil it in two teacupfuls of water
2. Then add, while the rice is hot, butter, sugar, eggs well beaten, nutmeg, a little salt, one glass of wine, raisins, currants, citron, and cream
3. Mix well, pour into buttered dish and bake an hour in a moderate oven

title: Rice Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup boiled rice
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 pint cream
- 3/4 pound raisins
- 1/4 pound peel
- sugar to suit taste

## Directions

1. Combine boiled rice, eggs, nutmeg, cream, raisins and peel
2. Add sugar to taste
3. Bake until done

title: Rice Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/3 cup rice
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 quart milk
- 1 cup currants

## Directions

1. Put rice, sugar, milk and currants together
2. Put in oven 2 hours
3. Do not stir when in oven

title: Rice Pudding Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup rice
- 1 1/2 quarts milk
- grated nutmeg
- brown sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Wash 3/4 cup rice
2. Add 1 1/2 quarts milk
3. Add grated nutmeg
4. Sweeten to taste with brown sugar
5. Bake very slowly and stir well once in a while

title: Rice Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cup rice
- 1 quart boiling water
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup cold meat, any kind
- pepper and salt
- 2 tablespoons catsup
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Wash rice in cold water
2. Add 1 quart boiling water and 1 teaspoon salt
3. Boil quickly until tender and water is all boiled away
4. Mince 1 cup cold meat of any kind
5. Season with pepper and salt
6. Place in granite dish
7. Spread over the boiled rice 2 tablespoons catsup
8. On top place a few lumps of butter and some bread crumbs
9. Bake 20 or 30 minutes

title: Rice with Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- grated cheese
- milk
- celery salt or table salt

## Directions

1. Boil the quantity of rice desired
2. Put into a baking dish a layer of rice and a layer of grated cheese alternately
3. Add milk as preparing for macaroni or spaghetti
4. Season with celery salt (table salt may be used)

title: Rice With Dates
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup rice
- salted water
- 1/2 cup pitted dates
- cream for serving

## Directions

1. Wash 3/4 cup rice
2. Cook in large quantity of salted water until nearly tender
3. Drain thoroughly
4. Put into double boiler with 1/2 cup pitted dates
5. Cook until tender
6. Serve with cream

title: Rice with Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- salted water
- cold tender meat
- salt
- pepper
- 1 beaten egg
- 2 tablespoons cracker crumbs
- hot water or stock
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Boil 1 cup rice until tender in salted water
2. Line a mould half an inch deep
3. Chop fine any kind of cold tender meat
4. Season to taste
5. Add 1 beaten egg
6. Add 2 tablespoons cracker crumbs
7. Moisten with hot water or stock
8. Pack in the mould or cover with rice
9. Steam 45 minutes
10. Turn out on platter
11. Pour tomato sauce over

title: Rice with Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- 3/4 cup stock
- 1/2 can tomatoes
- 1 sprig parsley
- 1 sprig thyme
- 2 cloves
- 3/4 teaspoon pepper-corn
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 egg
- 1/4 cup grated cheese
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Wash rice
2. Steam with stock until stock is boiled away
3. Add tomatoes which have been immersed 20 minutes, with seasoning and sugar, then rubbed through a sieve
4. Add other materials
5. Cool and shape

title: Rich Chocolate Torte (German)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound chocolate
- one pound pounded almonds
- one-quarter pound sugar
- yolks of seven eggs
- bread or cracker crumbs
- unsalted butter

## Directions

1. Beat yolks very light with sugar, add chocolate, melted, almonds pounded, and last the whites of eggs beaten very stiff.
2. Butter a flat shallow pan with unsalted butter, and sift in finely rolled cracker or bread crumbs.
3. Pour in the mixture and cook half an hour in a moderate oven.
4. Must be cut while hot into cubes.

title: Rich Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart sugar
- 1 pound butter
- 1 dozen eggs
- 1 3/4 pounds flour
- 1 3/4 pounds mixed peel
- 1 pound blanched almonds
- 3 pounds currants
- 2 pounds raisins
- 2 pounds sultanas
- 1 ounce rose water
- 1/20 ounce mixed spice
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 3/4 pint sherry
- 3/4 pint brandy
- juice of 2 oranges
- juice of 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Bake very, very slow for 6 hours

title: Rich Plum Cake, Or Wedding Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. flour
- 3.5 lbs. butter
- 3.5 lbs. sugar
- 36 eggs, leaving out twelve whites
- 2 lbs. citron
- 2 lbs. orange
- 2 lbs. lemon
- 4 lbs. almonds mixed with as much sugar
- 4 lbs. raisins
- 3 lbs. currants
- 3 oz. mixed spice
- 1 pt. each brandy, white wine and rose water

## Directions

1. Pound almonds fine, and mix with as much sugar
2. Put in an iron pot and stir constantly over the fire until quite dry
3. Stone raisins, and mince half of them fine
4. Mix batter and add fruits and spices
5. Add wine and egg whites last
6. Bake four hours in a well-buttered and papered pan
7. Sift plenty of flour on top before putting the cake in the oven

title: Richelieu Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 teaspoon arrowroot
- 1 tablespoon kirsch
- 2 tablespoons shredded almonds
- candied cherries

## Directions

1. Put sugar into saucepan with boiling water
2. let it boil 5 minutes
3. add arrowroot moistened with a little water and cook till clear
4. Remove from fire
5. flavor with kirsch
6. add shredded almonds and candied cherries. Cut into small pieces

title: Ripe Cucumber Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart ripe tomatoes, chopped
- 1 quart chopped ripe cucumbers
- 1 pint onions
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 pint vinegar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1/2 tablespoon tumeric powder
- 2 tablespoons mustard

## Directions

1. Combine tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, sugar, and vinegar
2. Boil 1 hour
3. Mix flour, salt, tumeric powder, and mustard in a little vinegar
4. Add to the bulk
5. Boil for a few minutes

title: Ripe Cucumber Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 large cucumbers
- 8 large onions
- 1 tablespoon pepper
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon tumeric
- 2 cups brown sugar
- vinegar to cover

## Directions

1. The night before, peel cucumbers and onions
2. Take out all seeds from cucumbers
3. Chop fine and let stand in an earthen or granite vessel in weak vinegar and water and salt over night
4. Simmer for 30 minutes

title: Ripe Cucumber Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 large cucumbers, firm part only
- 15 onions
- 2 large heads of cabbage
- salt water
- 1 gallon white wine vinegar
- 3 pounds sugar
- 1/2 pound mustard
- 1 ounce celery seed
- 1/4 cup white pepper
- 1/4 cup cinnamon
- 3/4 cup tumeric

## Directions

1. Chop cucumbers, onions, and cabbage as fine as mincemeat
2. Put into salt water over night
3. In the morning drain off the water
4. Put the mixture on the stove with vinegar, sugar, mustard, celery seed, white pepper, cinnamon, and tumeric
5. Bring to a boil
6. Pack in jars

title: Ripe Gooseberry Jam
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fruit
- 3/4 pound sugar per pound of fruit
- 1/2 pint red-currant juice per 6 pounds of fruit

## Directions

1. Select red, hairy gooseberries, gathered in dry weather, when quite ripe
2. Weigh them, cut off the tops and tails
3. To every six pounds of fruit add half a pint of red-currant juice, drawn as for jelly
4. Put the fruit and juice into a preserving-pan, and let them boil rather quickly, keeping them well stirred
5. When they begin to break, which will be in about an hour, add the sugar
6. Keep simmering until it becomes firm, stirring and skimming all the time
7. Put it into pots and when cold cover with oiled and egged paper

title: Ripe Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck tomatoes
- 3/4 cup salt
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 6 onions
- 3 green peppers
- 5 cups vinegar
- 1 teaspoon cassia
- cloves
- allspice

## Directions

1. Chop all ingredients fine
2. Cook well

title: Ripe Tomato Soy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pk. tomatoes, peeled and sliced
- 8 large onions, sliced
- 1 cup salt
- 2 qts. vinegar
- 2 lbs. sugar
- 1/2 lb. mustard seed
- 1 tablespoonful ground cloves
- 1 tablespoonful ground mustard
- 1 tablespoonful ground allspice
- 1 tablespoonful ground ginger
- 1 teaspoonful cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Put tomatoes and onions in alternate layers, cover with salt and let stand twenty-four hours.
2. Drain off all the liquor.
3. Add vinegar, ground mustard, ground ginger, ground cloves, ground allspice, cayenne pepper.
4. Stew slowly two or three hours.
5. When nearly done add brown sugar and mustard seed.

title: Ripe Watermelon Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- watermelon
- powdered alum
- water
- 2 pounds white sugar
- 2 pints white wine vinegar
- whole cloves
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut in 4-inch pieces
2. Cover with alum water, allowing 2 teaspoons powdered alum to each quart water
3. Heat to boiling point
4. Let stand on back of stove for 2 hours
5. Remove and chill in water
6. Make syrup of 2 pounds white sugar, 2 pints white wine vinegar, 2 tablespoons whole cloves and cinnamon (1 of each), tied in bag
7. Add your pickles
8. Cook 10 minutes, put in jar
9. Heat syrup 3 mornings, pour over
10. The last, pour fruit and all, scald up
11. Put in jars and seal

title: Ripe Watermelon Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds watermelon
- 4 cups brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons whole cloves
- allspice
- cinnamon
- 4 cups vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil watermelon until it can be pierced with a fork
2. Strain, put in sealers
3. Boil together the other ingredients a few minutes
4. Pour over fruit while hot, and set away in a cool, dark place

title: Ris de Veau a la Casserole Supreme
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads
- Onion
- Celery
- Stock or white sauce
- Fried bread
- Truffles and mushrooms or peas

## Directions

1. Steep the sweetbreads in water for an hour
2. Blanch them and press between two dishes
3. When cold, cut away any sinews or fat
4. Place them in a stew-pan with a little onion, celery, and stock of white sauce
5. Braise for twenty or thirty minutes
6. Take out of saucepan and put into the oven to brown, basting with its own liquor
7. Serve on fried bread with sauce around with truffles and mushrooms or peas

title: Roast Beef
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- a rib piece or loin roast of seven or eight pounds
- butter or suet fat
- salt
- pepper
- sifted flour

## Directions

1. Wipe the roast thoroughly all over with a clean wet towel
2. Lay it in a dripping-pan, and baste it well with butter or suet fat
3. Set it in the oven
4. Baste it frequently with its own drippings, which will make it brown and tender
5. When partly done season with salt and pepper
6. Dredge with sifted flour to give it a frothy appearance
7. Roast for about two hours' time to be properly done, leaving the inside a little rare or red — half an hour less would make the inside quite rare
8. Remove the beef to a heated dish, set where it will keep hot

title: Roast Beef and Yorkshire Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- roast
- flour
- salt
- hot water

## Directions

1. Place grate in roasting pan, put roast in this
2. Dredge meat with flour and salt
3. When the flour is browned put in 1/2 pint hot water
4. Watch carefully that the water does not boil away and the gravy become burned
5. Add little water at a time and baste often

title: Roast Duck
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- bread-crumbs
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of onion-juice
- 1 pinch of powdered sage
- flour
- 1 cupful of boiling water
- green peas

## Directions

1. Clean with care, and, after washing well, rinse out with soda and water
2. Lay in cold water for half an hour
3. Wipe dry and stuff with bread-crumbs, seasoned with butter, pepper, salt, a half teaspoonful of onion-juice, and just a pinch of powdered sage
4. Dredge with salt, pepper, and flour
5. Dash a cupful of boiling water over them and roast, covered, twelve minutes to the pound, if you like them rather rare; fifteen, if you would have them well done
6. Baste four times, the last time with butter, after which dredge with flour and brown
7. Chop the giblets for the gravy, and thicken with browned flour
8. Green peas should accompany ducks

title: Roast Duckling
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoonfuls of mashed potatoes
- butter
- 1 tablespoonful of cream
- celery salt
- white pepper
- 3 tablespoonfuls of almonds, blanched and chopped very fine
- 2 young ducks
- cotton-twine
- 1 split onion
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- 1 cupful of boiling water
- gravy
- 1 tablespoonful of browned flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of currant jelly

## Directions

1. Whip three tablespoonfuls of mashed potatoes to a white cream with butter and a tablespoonful of cream
2. Season with celery salt and white pepper
3. Add three tablespoonfuls of almonds, blanched and chopped very fine
4. Stuff your young ducks when you have cleaned and washed them with this mixture
5. Do not distend the bodies, but fill without packing
6. Truss and bind legs and wings into position with cotton-twine
7. Lay the plump creatures upon the grating of your roaster
8. Rub the breast with a split onion
9. Dust with pepper, salt and flour
10. Put a cupful of boiling water into the pan and cover
11. Set in a very quick oven for the first fifteen minutes
12. Change, then, to a more moderate, and cook, still covered, ten minutes to the pound
13. Uncover, baste well with gravy, then with butter
14. Dredge with flour, and brown
15. Skim the fat from the gravy
16. Thicken with a tablespoonful of browned flour, rubbed up with two tablespoonfuls of currant jelly, and send to table in a boat

title: Roast Goose
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- three pints of bread crumbs
- six ounces of butter, or part butter and part salt pork
- one teaspoonful sage
- one teaspoonful black pepper
- one teaspoonful salt
- one chopped onion
- water
- vinegar
- giblets
- flour

## Directions

1. Stuff the goose with the following mixture: Three pints of bread crumbs, six ounces of butter, or part butter and part salt pork, one teaspoonful each of sage, black pepper and salt, one chopped onion
2. Do not stuff very full, and stitch openings firmly together to keep flavor in and fat out
3. Place in a baking pan with a little water
4. Baste frequently with salt and water (some add vinegar)
5. Turn often so that the sides and back may be nicely browned
6. Bake two hours or more
7. When done take from the pan, pour off the fat
8. To the brown gravy left, add the chopped giblets which have previously been stewed until tender, together with the water they were boiled in
9. Thicken with a little flour and butter rubbed together
10. Bring to a boil and serve

title: Roast Goose
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Stuff the roast in same manner as ducks
2. Many cooks cover poultry with a paste of flour and water while baking, removing it before being served

title: Roast Goose
body:
## Ingredients

- goose
- dressing as for ducks
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Stuff the roast in same manner as ducks
2. Many cooks cover poultry with a paste of flour and water while baking, removing it before being served

title: Roast Hare or Rabbit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- hare or rabbit
- livers
- 1 slice fat pork
- bread-crumbs
- champignons or mushrooms
- pepper
- salt
- onion-juice
- salad oil
- lemon-juice
- 1 cupful weak stock, consomme, or butter and water
- bacon
- butter
- browned flour
- 1 teaspoonful catsup
- 1/2 glass claret

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the hare or rabbit thoroughly.
2. Chop the livers fine, also a slice of fat pork, and mix with bread-crumbs.
3. Add a few champignons or mushrooms if you like.
4. Season with pepper, salt, and onion-juice.
5. Stuff the rabbits with this, sew them up, and anoint well with salad oil and lemon-juice, leaving them in this marinade for an hour.
6. Put into the roaster, pour a cupful of weak stock, or consomme, or butter and water under them; cover and cook for an hour.
7. Take off the bacon, wash over with butter, and brown.
8. Dish the hares, and keep hot, while you thicken the gravy with browned flour, boil up, add a teaspoonful of catsup and half a glass of claret, pour a few spoonfuls over the rabbits, the rest into a boat.

title: Roast Haunch of Venison
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- haunch of venison
- tepid water
- buttered paper
- paste of flour and water
- stout paper
- twine
- water
- butter or clarified drippings
- flour
- unflavored gravy
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Wash the haunch of venison slightly in tepid water, and dry it thoroughly by rubbing it with a clean, soft cloth.
2. Lay over the fat side a large sheet of thickly buttered paper, and next a paste of flour and water about three-quarters of an inch thick.
3. Cover this again with two or three sheets of stout paper, secure the whole well with twine, and put down to roast, with a little water, in the dripping-pan.
4. Let the fire be clear and strong; baste the paper immediately with butter or clarified drippings, and roast the joint from three to four hours, according to its weight and quality.
5. Doe venison will require half an hour less time than buck venison.
6. About twenty minutes before the joint is done remove the paste and paper, baste the meat in every part with butter, and dredge it very lightly with flour.
7. Let it take a pale brown color, and serve hot with unflavored gravy made with a thickening, in a tureen and good currant jelly.

title: Roast Leg of Lamb
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of lamb
- cupful of boiling water
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- mint sauce

## Directions

1. Put into the covered roaster, dash a cupful of boiling water over it, cover and cook about fifteen minutes to the pound.
2. Twenty minutes before taking it up, take off the cover, rub all over with butter, dredge with pepper, salt, and flour, and brown.
3. Serve with mint sauce.

title: Roast Partridge
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- fat salt pork or bacon
- boiling water
- pepper
- salt
- melted butter
- bread sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and truss as you would chickens
