2. Bind thin slices of fat salt pork or bacon over the breasts and put into your roaster with half a cupful of boiling water
3. Pepper and salt the birds and wash over with melted butter, letting it drip into the pan below
4. Cook, covered, forty-five minutes, basting four times with butter and water
5. Serve with a good bread sauce, but after dishing pour over the birds several spoonfuls of their own gravy from the pan

title: Roast Pigeon (Wild)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon
- salt
- pepper
- cotton string
- fat bacon
- hot water or weak stock
- butter
- lemon-juice
- currant jelly
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Draw and wash them thoroughly
2. Wipe dry, salt and pepper the insides
3. Truss and bind them into shape with cotton string
4. Cover the breasts with thin slices of fat bacon tied in place
5. Lay them, breasts upward, in your roaster, and pour in half a cupful of hot water or weak stock
6. Cook, covered, fifteen minutes
7. Remove the pork, rub all over with butter and lemon-juice, and brown
8. Keep the pigeons hot while you stir into the gravy a tablespoonful of butter cut up in one of browned flour and another of currant jelly
9. Boil up once and pour over the pigeons

title: Roast Pork
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pork leg, loin, shoulder, or chine
- boiling water
- force-meat
- bread crumbs
- sage
- onions
- pepper
- salt
- grated lemon-peel
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. The skin is scored in squares, or in parallel lines, the knife just cutting through to the flesh.
2. Put into the roaster, dash a cup of boiling water over it; heat gradually until the fat begins to run, when quicken the fire.
3. Baste often and abundantly, that the skin may be tender, even when crisp.
4. Allow at least twenty minutes to the pound.
5. Rub well into the deep lines made by the knife in the rind a force-meat of crumbs, sage and onions, seasoned with pepper, salt, a little grated lemon-peel, and the juice of a lemon.
6. This was done before the meat went into the oven and the cracks were well filled.
7. Serve apple sauce with roast pork, or Chili sauce, or catsup, or a good bread sauce.

title: Roast Prairie Chicken or Grouse
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- prairie chicken or grouse
- butter
- lemon-juice
- salt
- pepper
- fat salt pork
- currant jelly
- bread sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and wipe the birds
2. Test them, and if they are tough, put them—trussed as for roasting—into a steamer and set over hard-boiling water for half an hour
3. While still hot rub them well with butter and lemon-juice, salt and pepper, inside and out
4. Put a small bit of fat salt pork in each
5. Roast, covered, in a quick oven for half an hour
6. Baste three times with butter and hot water, and, just before taking them up, with butter alone
7. Serve with currant jelly and bread sauce

title: Roast Quail
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- quail
- raw oyster
- fat bacon
- hot water
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- buttered toast
- gravy

## Directions

1. Draw and wipe carefully within and without with a soft, damp cloth
2. Put a whole raw oyster in the body of each, and truss as you would a chicken
3. Bind thin slices of fat bacon over the breast
4. Lay upon the grating of your roaster, put a very little hot water under them and cook, covered, in a lively oven, for twenty minutes, basting three times with butter and water
5. Wash well with butter, pepper, and salt
6. Serve upon squares of buttered toast, wet with gravy from the roaster

title: Roast Shoulder of Lamb
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 shoulder of lamb
- water
- gravy
- butter
- lemon-juice
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- breadcrumbs
- dressing

## Directions

1. Cook with more water in the pan and more slowly than the leg.
2. When nearly done, baste plentifully with the gravy, and, five minutes later, with butter into which a little lemon-juice has been beaten.
3. Brown lightly, after dredging with salt, pepper, and flour.
4. Your object should be to make every part of the shoulder eatable, the muscles soft, and the skin gelatinous.
5. You can vary the dish by having the bone of the shoulder taken out, filling the cavity with a dressing of breadcrumbs and butter, seasoned with pepper and salt.

title: Roast Sturgeon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sturgeon
- force-meat
- bread
- butter
- brown gravy
- anchovy
- Seville orange
- lemon

## Directions

1. Put a good-sized piece in a large cradle-spit (five or six pounds will make a handsome dish for the head of the table).
2. Stuff it with force-meat.
3. Keep it at the fire for two or three hours, but remove the skin.
4. Cover it with crumbs of bread, and brown it with the salamander.
5. Baste it constantly with butter, and serve with a good brown gravy, an anchovy, a squeeze of Seville orange or lemon, and a glass of wine.

title: Roast Sturgeon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six pounds sturgeon
- force-meat
- crumbs of bread
- butter
- brown gravy
- anchovy
- Seville orange or lemon
- sherry

## Directions

1. Put a good-sized piece in a large cradle-spit
2. stuff it with force-meat
3. keep it at the fire for two or three hours, but remove the skin
4. cover it with crumbs of bread, and brown it with the salamander
5. baste it constantly with butter
6. serve with a good brown gravy, an anchovy, a squeeze of Seville orange or lemon, and a glass of sherry boiled up, and poured into the dish

title: Roast Turkey
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young turkey
- soft butter
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- boiling water
- cranberry sauce

## Directions

1. Select a young turkey; remove all the feathers carefully, singe it over a burning newspaper on the top of the stove
2. Draw it nicely, being very careful not to break any of the internal organs; remove the crop carefully; cut off the neck close to the body
3. Rinse the inside of the turkey out with several waters, and in the next to the last, mix a teaspoonful of baking soda
4. Wipe the turkey dry, inside and out, with a clean cloth
5. Rub the inside with some salt, then stuff the breast and body with Dressing for Fowls
6. Sew up the turkey with a strong thread, tie the legs and wings to the body
7. Rub it over with a little soft butter, sprinkle over some salt and pepper, dredge with a little flour
8. Place it in a dripping pan, pour in a cup of boiling water, and set it in the oven
9. Baste the turkey often, turning it round occasionally so that every part will be uniformly baked
10. When pierced with a fork and the liquid runs out perfectly clear, the bird is done
11. If any part is likely to scorch, pin over it a piece of buttered white paper
12. A fifteen-pound turkey requires between three and four hours to bake
13. Serve with cranberry sauce

title: Roast Turkey or Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- pepper
- salt
- bread and cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Having picked and drawn the fowl, wash them well in 2 or 3 waters
2. Wipe them dry
3. Dredge them with a little flour inside and out
4. Sprinkle on a little pepper and salt
5. Prepare a dressing of bread and cracker crumbs
6. Fill the bodies and crops of the fowls
7. Bake them from 2 to 3 hours
8. Baste them frequently while roasting
9. Stew the giblets in a saucepan
10. Just before serving chop the giblets fine, after taking up the chicken and the water in which the giblets are boiled, and the chopped giblets to a gravy of the roast fowl
11. Thicken with a little flour which has been previously wet with the water
12. Boil up and serve in a gravy dish
13. Roast chicken and turkey should be accompanied with celery and jellies

title: Roast Turkey or Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey or chicken
- flour
- pepper
- salt
- bread and cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Pick and draw the fowl
2. Wash them well in 2 or 3 waters
3. Wipe them dry
4. Dredge them with a little flour inside and out
5. Sprinkle on a little pepper and salt
6. Prepare a dressing of bread and cracker crumbs
7. Fill the bodies and crops of the fowls
8. Bake them from 2 to 3 hours
9. Baste them frequently while roasting

title: Roasted Boned Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- boned chicken
- force-meat
- salt
- pepper
- slices of salt pork
- flour
- giblet or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Spread the boned chicken on a board, the skin side down.
2. Turn the flesh of the legs and wings right side out, and stuff them with force-meat into shape.
3. Equalize the meat as well as possible, placing the mignon fillets, or little strips of white meat, next the bone, over the dark meat.
4. Dredge with salt and pepper.
5. Make a roll of the stuffing or force-meat and lay it in the chicken.
6. Draw the skin up, and sew it together securely.
7. Turn it over, place the legs and wings into the position of a trussed fowl, press the body into natural shape, and tie it securely.
8. Cover with slices of salt pork and roast in oven, allowing twenty minutes to the pound.
9. Baste frequently.
10. Remove the pork the last fifteen minutes, dredge with flour and let it brown.
11. Serve with giblet or tomato sauce.

title: Roasted Boned Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- force meat
- salt
- pepper
- salt pork
- flour
- giblet or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Spread chicken on board, skin side down
2. Turn the flesh of legs and wings right side out and stuff with force meat into shape
3. Equalize the meat as well as possible, placing against the little strip of white meat only the dark meat, dredge with salt and pepper
4. Make a roll of the force meat and lay it in the chicken
5. Draw the skins up and sew it together securely
6. Turn it over, place legs and wings into the position of a trussed fowl
7. Press the body into natural shape and tie securely
8. Cover with slices of salt pork and roast, allowing 20 minutes to the pound, baste frequently
9. Remove pork last 15 minutes, dredge with flour, let brown, serve with giblet or tomato sauce

title: Roasted Chicken
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- stuffing
- salt
- pepper
- salt pork
- water
- butter
- flour
- parsley
- giblet sauce

## Directions

1. Truss the chicken as directed, or use skewers, doubling the cord across the back and around the ends of the skewers to hold them in place.
2. Dredge the chicken with salt and pepper, and place it on slices of salt pork in a baking-pan.
3. Add a very little water.
4. Bake in hot oven, allowing fifteen minutes to the pound.
5. Baste frequently.
6. Fifteen minutes before it is done, rub it over the top and sides with butter, dredge it with flour, and replace it in the oven until it becomes a golden brown and looks crisp.
7. Draw out the trussing cords, and garnish with parsley.
8. Serve with it a giblet sauce.

title: Roasted Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- butter
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- crumbs
- bread

## Directions

1. Peel the tomatoes and cut a piece off the top, and remove a little of the pulp.
2. Put a piece of butter or a few drops of oil in each one; dust with salt and pepper, replace the top, sprinkle it with crumbs, pepper, and salt.
3. Put a small piece of butter or a little oil on each one, and place on a slice of bread.
4. Bake in oven fifteen to twenty minutes.

title: Rock Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup sugar
- two tablespoons butter
- two eggs
- two cups flour
- two teaspoons baking powder
- two cups currants

## Directions

1. Drop from a spoon in small cakes

title: Rock Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 lb. butter
- 0.5 lb. sugar
- 1 lb. flour
- 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful cream tartar
- 0.5 teaspoonful soda
- 1 cup currants
- Flavor to taste

## Directions

1. Drop on buttered tins
2. Bake in moderate oven

title: Rocks
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- ⅜ cup butter
- 2 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 pound each chopped English walnuts and raisins
- 1 teaspoon each cinnamon, cloves and soda

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in a little hot water
2. Drop by spoonfuls on buttered tins
3. Bake

title: Rocks (Second Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1½ cups brown sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup dates
- 1 cups chopped walnut meats
- 3 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Drop in small teaspoons on buttered tins and bake

title: Rocks (Third Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1 cup nuts
- 1 cup rolled oats
- 2 cups flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3½ cup hot water

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Drop on greased pan and bake

title: Roger Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- ⅜ cup syrup
- ⅜ cup brown sugar
- ⅜ cup sour milk
- yolk of 1 egg
- butter size of egg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- pinch salt
- flour to thicken (not too thick)
- 1 cup white sugar
- 5 teaspoons water
- 1 cup raisins stoned and chopped fine
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix syrup, brown sugar, sour milk, egg yolk, butter, soda, salt and flour
2. For icing: boil water and sugar until it strings
3. Add 1 cup raisins stoned and chopped fine, and cook a few minutes
4. Cool a little and add the stiff white of the egg
5. Put between layers and on top

title: Roll Jelly Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs well beaten
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup flour
- lemon flavoring
- jelly

## Directions

1. Beat eggs with sugar
2. Add sweet milk
3. Sift baking powder with flour and add to mixture
4. Flavor with lemon
5. Bake at once in a moderately hot oven
6. Turn out on a cloth wet with cold water
7. Spread with jelly and roll quickly

title: Roll Jelly Cake No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs beaten to a froth
- 1 cup white sugar
- 3 small tablespoons cold water
- 1 cup sifted flour
- 1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
- jelly

## Directions

1. Beat eggs to a froth
2. Add white sugar and beat again
3. Add cold water, sifted flour and baking powder
4. Spread with jelly and roll

title: Roll Jelly Cake No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs well beaten
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- pinch of salt
- jelly

## Directions

1. Beat eggs well
2. Add sugar and flour
3. Add baking powder and salt
4. Bake in long pan
5. Spread jelly on while hot

title: Roll Jelly Cake No. 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs beaten separately
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 tablespoons cream or 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup pastry flour
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- jelly

## Directions

1. Beat eggs separately very light, then beat them together
2. Add sugar, cream or butter
3. Add pastry flour, soda and cream tartar
4. Bake in moderate oven in long shallow tins in very thin layer
5. While warm, spread with jelly
6. Trim edges and roll

title: Rolled Bread and Butter
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- loaf of bread
- butter
- parsley or cress

## Directions

1. Butter the loaf — not a fresh one — having first decrusted it with a very sharp knife
2. Cut a slice as thin as possible
3. Roll each slice with flat of hand
4. Pile the rolls log-fashion, or in a pyramid, on a doyley-covered bread plate
5. Garnish daintily with parsley or cress

title: Rolled Bread and Butter
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- bread loaf
- butter

## Directions

1. Butter the loaf, not a fresh one, having first decrusted it with a very sharp knife
2. Cut a slice as thin as possible
3. Roll each slice with flat of hand
4. Pile the rolls log-fashion, or in a pyramid, on a doyley-covered bread plate
5. Garnish daintily with parsley or cress

title: Rolled Bread Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart bread crumbs
- 1 cup hot milk
- 1 cupful molasses
- 1 cup fruit or chopped raisins
- 1 teaspoon each of spices
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup sifted flour

## Directions

1. Soak bread crumbs soft in hot milk
2. Add molasses, fruit or chopped raisins, spices, butter, salt, soda, and flour
3. Boil or steam 3 hours
4. Serve with sweet sauce

title: Rolled Steak
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 round steak, pounded
- pepper
- salt
- bread crumbs
- cream
- sage

## Directions

1. Make a dressing by crumbling bread crumbs with pepper and salt
2. Wet dressing with a little cream and any other seasoning such as sage
3. Spread over top of steak
4. Roll and tie with string
5. Put in pan and roast 40 minutes

title: Rolled Tea Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon baking soda

## Directions

1. Mix sugar with flour
2. Add butter, eggs, cream tartar, and baking soda
3. Mix well
4. Roll thin
5. Bake

title: Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- salt
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- flour

## Directions

1. Bring milk, sugar, and butter to a boil
2. Allow the mixture to cool to lukewarm
3. Add 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
4. Stir in flour enough to make a thin batter
5. Cover warm and let stand over night
6. In the morning mix the same as bread (not very stiff)
7. Let rise
8. Roll out like biscuits
9. Spread with melted butter
10. Cut like biscuits and fold one side over on other and press down once
11. Put in tin
12. Let rise
13. Bake in moderate oven

title: Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups milk
- 3/4 cup water
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 2 level teaspoons salt
- 2 level tablespoons sugar
- 2 level tablespoons butter
- 1 level tablespoon lard
- Flour

## Directions

1. Put the yeast cake and one tablespoon of the sugar in a cup, add 3/4 cup of the water warmed to 90° F., mix until smooth and the sugar is dissolved, and stand in a temperature of 80° F. for 20 minutes.
2. Put the milk on to scald.
3. Measure the butter, lard, salt, and the rest of the sugar into a kneading-pan, pour in the scalding milk, add the remaining 3/4 cup of cold water, and allow it to come to 90° F.
4. Add enough flour to make a batter that will not spatter when beaten, stir in the fermenting yeast, and beat until it looks smooth and elastic.
5. Mix in enough more flour to make a dough that will not stick to the hands.
6. Turn out on the board and knead until smooth, velvety and even textured.
7. Return to the warm, greased bread-tin, cover closely, and keep at 80° F. until nearly tripled in volume.
8. Knead down in the pan, and let rise again at 80° F., until double in volume.
9. Shape into small rolls, remembering that they will increase greatly in size, put them into greased pans, and stand in a temperature of 80° F. until doubled in volume.
10. Take care that the surface does not dry. If it is not possible to keep them covered closely, it will be necessary to moisten the top occasionally with warm milk or water.

title: Rolls 2
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of bread sponge
- beaten whites of 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup butter

## Directions

1. Add beaten whites of 2 eggs, 1/2 cup sugar, and 1/4 cup butter to one pint of bread sponge
2. Mix well and set in a warm place to rise
3. Roll out to a thickness of 1/4 inch
4. Cut out with cake cutter
5. Butter top of one and place another on top
6. Set aside to rise
7. Bake for one-half hour
8. Currants may be added if desired

title: Rose Water
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Attar of rose, twelve drops
- White sugar, half an ounce
- Carbonate magnesia, two drachms
- Water, one quart
- Proof spirit, two ounces

## Directions

1. Rub attar of rose with half an ounce of white sugar and two drachms carbonate magnesia
2. Add gradually one quart of water and two ounces of proof spirit
3. Filter through paper

title: Rough and Readys
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups molasses
- 1 cup shortening
- 2 teaspoons soda dissolved in 1 cup boiling water
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- flour

## Directions

1. Add enough flour to roll
2. Brush tops in milk and dip in white sugar

title: Roulades of Beef
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of undercooked roast beef
- slices of boiled ham
- one egg
- pepper and mustard
- a little thick gravy
- fine crumbs
- butter or dripping for frying

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into thin, even, oblong slices, the ham rather thinner and smaller
2. spread one side of the beef with mustard, and pepper the ham
3. Lay the ham upon the beef and roll up together as lightly as possible
4. brush over with the egg, roll each in the crumbs, and pierce through with a slender skewer, in such a manner as to keep the roll pinned together
5. Put several on each skewer, but do not let them touch one another
6. fry brown, lay on a hot dish, and gently withdraw the skewers, then pour the gravy boiling hot over them, and serve

title: Royal Egg Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 large tablespoon butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3 eggs
- 1 1/4 pints milk

## Directions

1. Sift together flour, sugar, salt and powder
2. Rub in the butter
3. Add beaten eggs and milk
4. Mix into smooth batter
5. Fill greased muffin pans about
6. Bake in hot oven 15 minutes

title: Royal Marmalade
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve Seville oranges
- three pints of cold water per pound of fruit
- one and a half pounds of sugar per pound of boiled fruit
- rind and juice of two lemons

## Directions

1. Cut each orange into eight quarters and slice them very fine.
2. Take out the seeds and put them into a basin and cover with water.
3. To every pound of fruit add three pints of cold water.
4. Let it stand for twenty-four hours, then boil until tender.
5. Put the juice from the seeds in also.
6. Let it stand until the following day.
7. To every pound of boiled fruit add one and a half pounds of sugar and the rind and juice of two lemons.
8. Boil, stirring constantly until the syrup jellies.

title: Rules for Pie Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints chopped meat
- 6 pints chopped apple
- 1 pint boiled cider
- 1 pint molasses
- 2 pints brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons cloves
- 3 tablespoons cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon nutmeg
- 3 tablespoons salt
- currants
- raisins
- citron
- wine or brandy

## Directions

1. Cook slowly

title: Russian Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box gelatine
- 1 qt. milk
- 3/4 cups sugar
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Soak gelatine in cup of milk
2. Add to rest of milk, heated
3. Add yolks of eggs and sugar
4. Cook until smooth
5. Remove from stove and add whites of eggs
6. Pour in mould to cool

title: Russian Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked vegetables
- capers
- pickled gherkins
- olives
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut all sorts of cooked vegetables neatly in small dice, balls, cubes, in equal proportion
2. Add some capers and pickled gherkins cut into shreds
3. Use olives for garnish
4. Mix three parts of oil to one of vinegar, salt and pepper as desired
5. Toss vegetables in this dressing
6. Pile on a china stand and garnish with shreds of any cold game, fowl, smoked salmon, lobster coral, anchovy, olives, hard-boiled eggs, parsley, lettuce, or celery tops, with fancy stars of beetroot

title: Russian Tea
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of boiling water
- two tablespoonfuls of black tea
- large cup or three parts of sugar
- juice of four lemons
- large piece of ice

## Directions

1. One quart of boiling water poured on two tablespoonfuls of black tea; add large cup or three parts of sugar and the juice of four lemons.
2. Let stand two hours, then strain through a cloth; add large piece of ice. A delicious drink for hot weather.

title: Rye Bread
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup yeast
- 1 pt. warm water
- 2 pts. rye flour
- 1 pt. wheat flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls lard
- 2 tablespoonfuls brown sugar
- 1 qt. warm milk
- 1 cup Indian meal
- Enough rye flour to make into dough

## Directions

1. Mix together yeast, warm water, rye flour, flour, lard and brown sugar
2. Beat together, and let rise over night
3. In the morning mix with this: One quart of warm milk, one cup of Indian meal, enough rye flour to make into dough
4. Knead; cover, set in a warm place to rise two or three hours
5. Knead again, and make into loaves
6. If there is the least tendency to sourness, add a teaspoonful of soda, dissolved in warm water
7. It is best to always add this in warm weather
8. Rub soda smooth with a knife blade before measuring

title: Rye Gems
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of warm milk
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- A pinch of salt
- 2 eggs, well beaten
- Rye flour enough to make a thin batter

## Directions

1. Dissolve soda in warm milk
2. Add a pinch of salt and two eggs, well beaten
3. Add rye flour enough to make a thin batter
4. Bake in gem-pans

title: S's
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. flour
- 1/4 lb. butter
- 1/4 lb. sugar
- Yolks 5 eggs
- Almonds crushed

## Directions

1. Mix well together and put on ice to cool; then form into S's; put on ice again till quite hard, then dip into egg and sugar, or almonds.
2. Bake till brown.

title: Sago and Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup sago
- water
- 1/8 teaspoon salt
- small piece butter
- heaping tablespoon sugar
- apples

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 cup sago in about 5 times its bulk of water for hour
2. Add 1/8 teaspoon salt, a small piece of butter, and a heaping tablespoon sugar
3. Pare and core as many apples as will cover the bottom of the baking dish
4. Pour sago over them
5. Cook in a moderate oven until the apples are done
6. If the apples are large they can be quartered, and the baking dish can be about filled with them, and the sago put over them the same way

title: Salad Cream Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cream
- 1 spoon flour
- whites of 2 eggs
- 3 spoons vinegar
- 2 spoons salad oil
- 2 spoons powdered sugar
- 1 spoon salt
- 1/2 spoon pepper
- 1 spoon made mustard
- cold milk

## Directions

1. Heat cream almost to boiling
2. Stir in flour previously wet with cold milk
3. Boil 2 minutes, stirring constantly
4. Add sugar and remove from fire
5. When half cold beat in whipped whites of eggs
6. Set aside to cool
7. When quite cold, whip in the oil, pepper, mustard and salt
8. If the salad is ready, add vinegar and pour at once over it

title: Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 1-1/2 tablespoonful mixed mustard
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful pepper
- 1 cup cold water
- 1 cup vinegar
- butter size of egg

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs together
2. Add sugar, mixed mustard, water, vinegar, butter melted, salt and pepper
3. Cook over steam until it thickens

title: Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful brown sugar
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- 1 teaspoonful butter
- A little salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Beat egg, add dry ingredients and melted butter.
2. Mix in gradually half a cup of vinegar and three-quarters of a cup of milk, then boil till thick.
3. Have quite cold before using.

title: Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 3 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful olive oil
- 1 teaspoonful dry mustard
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 3 tablespoonfuls cream

## Directions

1. Beat all together
2. Let come to a boil until of the consistency of thick cream
3. Serve when cold

title: Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- Pepper to taste
- 2 cups milk or cream
- Vinegar to thicken

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard and take the yolks
2. Rub smooth with melted butter, salt, mustard, sugar, and pepper to taste
3. Add to this mixture milk or cream, stirring it in gradually
4. Add enough vinegar to thicken it
5. Boil all together, stirring briskly
6. Do not mind if it curdles, go on stirring till it gets quite smooth
7. If no cream is used a tablespoonful of cornstarch dissolved in the milk will help to thicken it to the consistency of batter
8. Use whites of eggs to garnish salad

title: Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 1/4 cup vinegar
- 3 tablespoonfuls sugar
- Dash of cayenne
- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- Salt to taste
- 1/4 cup rich cream

## Directions

1. Mix the vinegar, sugar, mustard, pepper, and stir in the egg well beaten; will take nearly five minutes
2. Cook all in a double boiler, stirring constantly
3. When cold add the cream and salt
4. This makes a pint and will keep for weeks

title: Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks 4 eggs
- 1/4 cup sugar
- 1/4 teaspoonful each salt, pepper and mustard
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1/4 cup cream
- 1/4 teaspoonful cayenne
- 1/2 pt. of vinegar

## Directions

1. To the well-beaten yolks add sugar, salt, mustard and black pepper, cream and cayenne pepper
2. Beat all these thoroughly
3. Take vinegar and heat; when hot add butter
4. Pour this over the mixture, boil up and when cold put into large-mouthed bottles
5. This will keep many weeks in a cool place

title: Salad Dressing
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoonfuls sugar
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful mustard
- 1/4 cup vinegar
- 2 eggs
- Butter size of egg
- 1/4 cup milk

## Directions

1. Take all ingredients and mix together (without vinegar)
2. Put in double boiler and let it come to boil, stirring constantly
3. Then add vinegar and come to boil again
4. If too thick when cold add cream to thin it
5. Will keep for a long time

title: Salad Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 4 tablespoons brown sugar
- 2 teaspoons mustard
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- a dash of cayenne pepper
- butter size of a walnut
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 1 cup vinegar and water mixed (more water than vinegar)

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Cook in double boiler till thick

title: Salad Dressing 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons white sugar
- 2 teaspoons mustard
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- butter the size of an egg
- yolk of 1 egg
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 8 tablespoons milk
- 1 tablespoon heated vinegar
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, mustard, salt, egg yolk, flour and milk
2. Cook until thick
3. Add 1 tablespoon heated vinegar
4. When cold fold in the beaten white of egg

title: Salad Dressing 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup vinegar
- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/6 teaspoon pepper
- 1/2 cup cream

## Directions

1. Heat 1 cup vinegar
2. Beat 2 eggs well
3. Pour vinegar over eggs
4. Add sugar, salt, pepper and cream

title: Salad Dressing 4
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 3/4 teaspoon paprika or red pepper
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup vinegar

## Directions

1. Melt butter
2. Stir in flour
3. Add 1 cup milk and boil
4. Mix sugar, salt, mustard, paprika or red pepper and egg
5. Add to first mixture and cook until thick
6. Add 1/2 cup vinegar slowly

title: Salad Dressing 5
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- butter size of a nut
- 3/4 cup sugar
- pepper to taste
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. Beat eggs
2. Add mustard, salt, butter, sugar and pepper
3. Cook until thick
4. Thin with milk or cream for chicken

title: Salad Dressing 6
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 tablespoons mustard
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon cornstarch
- 4 eggs
- 1 pint cream
- 1 scant cup vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix butter, sugar, mustard, salt and cornstarch to a cream
2. Add beaten eggs
3. Add cream
4. Add vinegar a little at a time
5. Cook in a double boiler until thick like cream

title: Salad Dressing for Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup vinegar
- a pinch of salt
- 1 dessertspoon sugar
- 1 dessertspoon mustard
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Boil all together
2. Have beet cooked and sliced
3. Let cool

title: Salad Dressing for Lettuce or Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup vinegar
- 3/4 cup cold water
- 1 cup white sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon black pepper
- a little cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Put on the stove in a granite pan just to simmer 1/2 cup vinegar, 3/4 cup cold water, 1 cup white sugar, and 1 teaspoon butter
2. Beat up 2 eggs; take in another dish, 1 teaspoon mustard, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 3/4 teaspoon black pepper, mix with a little cold vinegar, add this to the eggs and stir into the pan
3. Stand over it and stir till it boils up, then let get cold and pour over your cabbage, having it chopped up fine

title: Salad Dressing Without Eggs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter size of walnut
- 2 teaspoonfuls sugar
- 1 saltspoonful salt and pepper
- 1 teaspoonful mixed mustard
- 1/2 cup cream or milk
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- Cornstarch

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan, butter, sugar, salt and pepper, mustard, cream or milk, and vinegar, thicken with a little cornstarch.

title: Salad Dressing Without Oil
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Yolks of 11 eggs
- 3 teaspoonfuls salt
- 3 teaspoonfuls mustard
- 1 1/2 cups vinegar
- 9 teaspoonfuls sugar

## Directions

1. In a double boiler put the yolks of eggs, salt, mustard, and sugar; stir well
2. Add vinegar (not too strong)
3. Cook till quite thick, stirring all the time
4. When sufficiently cooked take from the fire and beat with an egg-beater until cool
5. This will keep for weeks
6. When required to use add cream to thin

title: Sally Lunns
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt flour
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- butter size of egg
- little salt
- 3 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 2 cups milk
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Stir well together
2. Add two eggs not beaten, two cups milk
3. Mix all
4. Bake in muffin rings

title: Salmi of Duck
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 roasted or braised duck
- salad oil
- lemon-juice
- cold water
- parsley
- pepper
- salt
- browned flour
- butter
- 1/2 onion
- 1/2 can of champignons or fresh mushrooms
- sippets of fried bread

## Directions

1. Cut up the meat of a roasted or braised duck into neat dice
2. Cut the bones, stuffing, and skin into small pieces
3. Cover the meat-dice with a marinade of salad oil and lemon-juice, and leave in a cold place
4. Cover the bones, etc., well with cold water
5. Add parsley, pepper, and salt, and simmer, after this reaches the boil, for two hours
6. Strain, thicken the gravy with browned flour rubbed up with a spoonful of butter
7. Add the juice of half an onion, boil up and put in the meat
8. Draw to the side of the range and let it almost, but not quite, boil
9. Take out the meat and arrange neatly upon a flat dish
10. Add to the gravy half a can of champignons (or, if you can get them, fresh mushrooms are far better)
11. Simmer three minutes and pour over the meat
12. Garnish with sippets of fried bread

title: Salmi of Grouse
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of underdone grouse
- cupful of good stock or consomme
- salt
- minced onion
- chopped carrot
- stalk of celery
- minced parsley
- brown roux
- mushrooms
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut neatly into joints a pair of underdone grouse and divide the breasts into two pieces each
2. Put a cupful of good stock or consomme in a saucepan, season well
3. Add a minced onion, a chopped carrot, and a stalk of celery, with a little minced parsley
4. Cook slowly one hour
5. Rub through a colander
6. Stir in a tablespoonful of brown roux
7. Bring to a boil and put in the grouse
8. After this it must not boil, but set it in a saucepan of boiling water just where it will keep at the scalding-point for half an hour
9. At the last put in half a cupful of mushrooms, heated in their own liquor
10. Serve

title: Salmon Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large potatoes
- 4 biscuits
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup sweet milk or cream
- pepper
- salt
- 1 can salmon
- butter

## Directions

1. Mash 3 large potatoes very fine
2. Roll 4 biscuits smooth
3. Mix together potatoes, biscuits, 1 egg, 3/4 cup sweet milk or cream, little pepper, salt, and 1 can salmon
4. Fry in buttered pan

title: Salmon Balls 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 12 soda biscuits
- 2 eggs
- milk
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Roll 12 soda biscuits fine
2. Mix 1 can salmon, rolled biscuits, 2 eggs, and enough milk to moisten
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Roll in balls
5. Fry in butter

title: Salmon Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 3 eggs
- 8 soda biscuits rolled fine
- 3 tablespoons sweet milk
- salt and pepper
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Break eggs on salmon
2. Stir thoroughly
3. Fry in butter

title: Salmon Cakes (Another Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box salmon
- 4 biscuits rolled
- 3 eggs
- salt
- butter for frying
- stale bread crumbs (optional, may be used instead of biscuits)

## Directions

1. Make in small flat balls
2. Salt and fry in butter till light brown
3. Note: any fish left over from roasting or frying may be used the same way

title: Salmon Cheese
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can good salmon
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup cream
- 6 soda biscuits
- pepper
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Roll 6 soda biscuits fine
2. Mix 1 can good salmon, 4 eggs, 1 cup cream, rolled biscuits, pepper and salt and a little vinegar to taste together
3. Steam 2 hours
4. When cold slice down and serve

title: Salmon Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon well blended
- Yolks of two eggs
- 2 pinches of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of cayenne
- 1 teaspoonful mace
- Small piece of onion
- 8 tablespoonfuls milk or cream
- Small piece of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Mix well
2. Put in mould and steam
3. Serve with butter sauce

title: Salmon Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup salmon
- 8 biscuits
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup milk
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Roll 8 biscuits
2. Mix 1 cup salmon, rolled biscuits, 1 egg, 1/2 cup milk, pepper and salt to taste together
3. Fry in butter till a rich brown

title: Salmon Croquettes 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 3 eggs
- 8 soda biscuits
- 3 tablespoons sweet milk
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Roll 8 soda biscuits fine
2. Break 3 eggs on salmon
3. Stir thoroughly with rolled biscuits, 3 tablespoons sweet milk, a little salt and pepper
4. Fry in butter

title: Salmon Croquettes 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box salmon
- 4 biscuits
- 3 eggs
- salt
- butter
- stale bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Roll 4 biscuits
2. Make in small flat balls with 1 box salmon, rolled biscuits, 3 eggs, and salt
3. Fry in butter till light brown
4. Stale bread crumbs may be used instead of biscuits
5. Any fish left over from roasting or frying may be used the same way

title: Salmon Custard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- small lump of butter
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Bake for 20 minutes or half an hour till brown

title: Salmon Custard (Another Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- salt and pepper to taste
- rolled soda biscuit crumbs
- 1 well-beaten egg
- 1 3/2 cups milk
- bits of butter

## Directions

1. Take a deep bake dish
2. Put alternate layers of salmon and rolled soda biscuit crumbs
3. Pour milk and egg mixture over salmon
4. Put bits of butter on top of the dish
5. Bake until custard is set
6. Let get thoroughly cold and slice

title: Salmon Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1/2 cup vinegar
- 1/2 cup sour cream

## Directions

1. Let come to a boil
2. When cold add sour cream
3. For 2 cans salmon

title: Salmon Dressing (Another Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon flour
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 3/4 teaspoon mustard
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 egg
- vinegar to taste

## Directions

1. Cook so that it will not taste raw
2. Remove from stove and add egg and vinegar to taste

title: Salmon Flitters
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 can salmon
- butter size of walnut
- pepper and salt to taste
- 2 cups of rolled biscuits

## Directions

1. Make in little pats
2. Fry in butter over quick fire

title: Salmon Fluff
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 6 soda biscuits
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Roll biscuits
2. Beat eggs and work all together
3. Form into pats
4. Fry a few minutes

title: Salmon For Sandwich
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- juice of lemon
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 2 tablespoons pickles
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients

title: Salmon Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 can salmon
- 1 cup mashed potatoes
- 1 slice of bread squeezed out of cold water
- 1 egg
- salt and pepper to taste
- flour for rolling
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Mould into round flat balls
2. Roll in flour
3. Fry until brown

title: Salmon Headcheese
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 3 eggs
- 6 rolled soda biscuits
- 1 pint sweet milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mix 1 can salmon, 3 eggs, 6 rolled soda biscuits, 1 pint sweet milk, salt and pepper to taste together
2. Bake

title: Salmon in a Mould
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One can of salmon
- Four tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- Salt, pepper and minced parsley
- A little celery, if liked
- Four eggs
- Half a cupful of cracker-crumbs
- One cupful of milk
- One tablespoonful of cornstarch
- One tablespoonful of butter
- One egg
- One teaspoonful of catsup

## Directions

1. Drain the liquor from one can of salmon, and remove the bones and skin
2. Chop fine and rub into it until smooth, four tablespoonfuls of melted butter, season with salt, pepper and minced parsley, also a little celery, if liked
3. Beat four eggs well, add half a cupful of cracker-crumbs, mix all well and thoroughly
4. Put into a buttered mould and steam one hour
5. For the sauce: Boil one cupful of milk and thicken with one tablespoonful of cornstarch
6. Add to the liquor from the salmon, one tablespoonful of butter, one egg, and one teaspoonful of catsup
7. Put the egg in last and very carefully
8. Boil one minute
9. Turn the salmon out of the mould and pour the sauce around

title: Salmon Loaf
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One can of salmon minced
- Four eggs
- Four tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- One-half cup of breadcrumb
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Pick fish fine; rub butter smooth
2. Beat the crumbs into the eggs, and season before mixing
3. Steam one hour in a buttered mold
4. For the sauce: One cup of milk thickened with one tablespoonful of cornstarch, one tablespoonful butter
5. Add the liquor off the fish and one raw egg, then pour over the loaf

title: Salmon Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 4 eggs well beaten
- 4 tablespoons soft butter
- 1/4 cup bread crumbs or crackers
- pepper and salt to taste
- 1 tablespoon minced parsley

## Directions

1. Pick carefully the skin and bones of salmon
2. Add eggs, butter, bread crumbs or crackers, pepper, salt, and parsley
3. Mix well
4. Pack in buttered mould
5. Steam 1 hour

title: Salmon Loaf (Another Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup salmon
- 3/2 cup bread crumbs
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 egg
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 3/4 teaspoon mustard
- 3/4 teaspoon parsley
- 3/4 teaspoon lemon juice
- pepper and onion juice

## Directions

1. Mix dry ingredients first
2. Beat egg
3. Add milk
4. Add to dry mixture
5. Add lemon and onion juice last
6. Bake in oven till done
7. Serve with white sauce

title: Salmon Loaf (Fifth Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 2 eggs
- 2 soda biscuits rolled finely
- pepper and salt
- little lemon juice
- butter
- hot water

## Directions

1. Wet this mixture with hot water
2. Press in a dish
3. Steam for 3/4 hour
4. Slice and eat cold

title: Salmon Loaf (Fourth Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 3 eggs beaten
- melted butter the size of a good big egg
- salt and pepper to taste
- 2 cups of bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Take out the bones and skin
2. Drain off oil
3. Add beaten eggs, melted butter, salt, pepper, and bread crumbs
4. Mix all well
5. Put in a buttered dish
6. Steam an hour

title: Salmon Loaf (Sixth Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- 2 eggs beaten
- 1 cup milk
- six rolled biscuits
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Open salmon
2. Pick out skins and bones
3. Beat eggs and add milk, rolled biscuits, salt and pepper
4. Mix all together
5. Put in a buttered dish
6. Cook 3/4 hour in oven
7. Nice for cold meat for supper or Sunday

title: Salmon Loaf (Third Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of salmon
- 4 soda biscuits rolled very fine
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup milk
- butter
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Steam an hour and a half
2. Serve cold and garnished with a little parsley

title: Salmon Patties
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 1 1/2 dozen soda biscuits rolled fine
- 1 cup sweet milk
- pepper and salt to taste
- butter for cooking

## Directions

1. Work all together
2. Form in lumps the size of eggs or larger
3. Cook in a hot buttered pan till a nice brown
4. Turn once

title: Salmon Patties
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 1½ dozen soda biscuits
- 1 cup sweet milk
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Roll soda biscuits fine
2. Work salmon, biscuits, milk, pepper and salt all together
3. Form in lumps the size of eggs or larger
4. Cook in a hot buttered pan till a nice brown
5. Turn once

title: Salmon Patties 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- ½ can salmon
- 1 egg
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook rice till tender
2. Add salmon, egg, season with pepper and salt
3. Form into oblong shape
4. Fry brown in butter

title: Salmon Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- salt
- pepper
- celery salt
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Beat salmon lightly
2. Season with salt, pepper and celery salt
3. Mix in bread crumbs and melted butter
4. Beat eggs lightly and add
5. Put in a buttered mould
6. Cook over boiling water until done
7. Turn out and serve with cream sauce

title: Salmon Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon
- chopped celery
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- salt
- 1 cup vinegar

## Directions

1. Pick the salmon to pieces
2. Add twice as much chopped celery as fish
3. Mix well
4. For dressing: mix eggs, sugar, mustard, salt and vinegar
5. Boil until it thickens
6. Combine with salmon and celery

title: Salmon Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- canned salmon
- mayonnaise
- cayenne
- thin buttered bread

## Directions

1. Pound canned salmon
2. Mix with a little mayonnaise
3. Season with a dash of cayenne
4. Spread on thin buttered bread

title: Salmon Scallop
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Drain salmon and remove bones and skin
2. Heat milk and butter together
3. Thicken with flour smoothed in part of the cold milk
4. Mix this with the salmon and part of the bread crumbs
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste
6. Put remainder of bread crumbs on top
7. Dot with butter
8. Bake 20 minutes

title: Salmon Souffle
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint milk
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- pepper
- onion juice
- 1 cup stale bread crumbs
- 1 can salmon
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a white sauce of milk, flour and butter with salt, pepper and onion juice
2. Cook the sauce
3. Add stale bread crumbs
4. Add salmon picked free from bones and rubbed smooth with a silver fork
5. Add beaten yolks of eggs and beat all
6. Fold in whites beaten very stiff
7. Turn into buttered dish
8. Bake in pan standing in water in oven for 25 minutes

title: Salmon Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cooked salmon
- 1 pt. milk
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1 tablespoonful flour
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 sprig parsley
- 1 slice onion

## Directions

1. Put milk in double boiler, and bay leaf, onion and parsley
2. Let it come to scalding point
3. Rub butter and flour together, put into milk, stir till it thickens
4. Remove flavorings, add salmon, which has been rubbed through a colander
5. Stir until it becomes smooth, add salt and cayenne
6. Serve

title: Salmon Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart sweet milk
- 1/2 a can of salmon
- three or four rolled soda biscuits
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Put sweet milk in a granite kettle and set it on the stove until the milk comes to a boil
2. Add salmon and rolled soda biscuits
3. Let it all boil for three minutes
4. Add pepper and salt
5. Serve

title: Salmon Timbale
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 1 cup crumbs
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- ¾ teaspoon salt
- 1/16 teaspoon pepper
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- ½ cup milk

## Directions

1. Mix salmon, crumbs, parsley, salt, pepper, eggs and lemon juice thoroughly
2. Add milk enough to moisten
3. Put into buttered mould
4. Place in the oven and poach
5. Turn and serve with parsley sauce

title: Salmon Turbot
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can salmon
- 1 cup milk
- 4 soda biscuits
- butter half size of an egg
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Roll soda biscuits
2. Beat all ingredients well together
3. Steam 3 hours in a well-buttered dish
4. Turn out and cover with sauce made of 1 cup milk thickened with a tablespoon cornstarch, 1 egg, pepper and salt
5. Pour sauce over salmon

title: Salmon With Poached Egg
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- bread rounds, one inch thick
- melted butter
- canned salmon
- cream sauce
- poached egg
- parsley
- sliced lemon

## Directions

1. Stamp out the required number of rounds of bread an inch thick
2. Cut the centres out of these, leaving a case with a narrow rim
3. Brush over the outsides of cases with melted butter
4. Brown in oven
5. Fill the space in the centre with canned salmon flaked and heated in a cup of cream sauce
6. Lay a poached egg above the salmon
7. Serve garnished with parsley and sliced lemon

title: Salsify Frittées
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one bunch salsify
- two eggs
- one-quarter cup milk
- flour
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Scrape and grate the roots, and stir into a batter made of the beaten eggs, the milk, and flour
2. Grate the salsify directly into this, that it may not blacken by exposure to the air
3. Salt, and drop a spoonful into the hot fat to see if it is of the right consistency
4. As fast as you fry the fritters, throw into a hot colander to drain
5. One great spoonful of batter should make a fritter

title: Salt Cod With Egg Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. salt cod soaked, boiled and picked up very fine
- 1 small cup milk or cream
- 1 teaspoonful flour
- 2 eggs beaten light
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- Little chopped parsley
- Half as much mashed potato as fish
- Pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Heat the milk
2. Thicken with the flour, then the potato rubbed very fine
3. Add the butter, the eggs, and parsley
4. Last, add the fish
5. Stir and toss until smoking hot all through
6. Pour into a deep dish
7. Alternatively: make a sauce of all the ingredients except the fish and potato
8. Mix the fish and potato well together with a little melted butter
9. Heat in a saucepan, stirring all the while
10. Heap in the centre of a dish and pour the sauce over all

title: Salt Codfish Balls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- six pared or quartered potatoes
- boiling water
- one cupful of picked codfish
- a beaten egg
- beef fat

## Directions

1. Cover six pared or quartered potatoes with boiling water
2. Put a cupful of picked codfish above the potatoes and cook until tender
3. Drain, mash and season to taste
4. Add a beaten egg and beat the mixture until light
5. Shape into smooth, light balls
6. Fry in beef fat, smoking hot
7. Drain carefully and serve at once

title: Salt Corn for Winter Use
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 cups corn
- 2 cups water
- 1 cup sugar
- 3½ cup salt

## Directions

1. Boil 20 minutes
2. seal tight

title: Salt Mackerel With Tomato Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt mackerel
- Tomatoes, stewed and strained
- Onion-juice
- Pepper
- Salt
- Sugar
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Proceed as with boiled mackerel
2. When dished, pour over it a tomato sauce made of stewed, strained tomatoes seasoned with onion-juice, pepper, salt, and sugar, and thickened with a brown roux of butter and flour
3. Let the fish lie in this for ten minutes and serve

title: Salt-Rising Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pint water
- 2 tablespoons cornmeal
- salt
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 pint fresh sweet milk or warm water
- 2 quarts fresh morning's milk
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Pour half a pint of water on 2 tablespoons of cornmeal and a generous pinch of salt.
2. Let it stand for 10 minutes, then stir in 2 tablespoons of flour and set it in a warm place to rise over night.
3. In the morning add half a pint of fresh, sweet milk, or warm water — either must be lukewarm — and just flour enough to make the yeast smooth.
4. Put the vessel containing it in a pan or kettle of hot water, hot enough to bear your hand in and be very careful to keep it the same temperature.
5. When this rises pour it into a batter made of 2 quarts of fresh morning's milk, scalded, then cooled to lukewarm, and mixed with flour enough to make a batter.
6. Beat this well, using a long spoon, then set the whole to rise again in a warm place.
7. This will be about twenty-five or thirty minutes if it has been well managed.
8. Now stir in enough flour to make a batter so stiff it cannot be stirred with a spoon.
9. Turn it out on a moulding board well dredged with flour, and before kneading it add a piece of lard the size of a large apple.
10. Mix this well into the dough.

title: Salted Almonds
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds almonds
- three tablespoonfuls best olive oil
- one tablespoonful fine salt

## Directions

1. Blanch almonds
2. pour oil over them, adding salt
3. Let them stand for an hour or two, stirring frequently
4. Place in a pan large enough for the almonds to rest on the surface, not being crowded one on another
5. Place in an oven sufficiently slow to allow twenty minutes for the nuts to brown nicely
6. shake the pan frequently that they may color evenly

title: Salted Almonds
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Almonds
- Butter
- Fine salt
- Pinch of cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch almonds by pouring boiling water over and allowing them to stand till the skins slip off easily.
2. Lay on tins with small lumps of butter and place in a hot oven, stirring occasionally.
3. When almonds are a golden brown take from oven, sprinkle with fine salt and a pinch of cayenne pepper, cover with another tin and shake thoroughly.

title: Sand Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup milk
- 1 egg
- Sugar to suit taste
- Crust for custard pie

## Directions

1. Make crust as for custard pie
2. Pour in mixture and bake

title: Sand Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound butter
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1 pound flour
- 3 eggs
- granulated sugar
- cinnamon
- raisin or almond

## Directions

1. Roll out, cut like cookies
2. Brush with white of egg
3. Sprinkle with granulated sugar and cinnamon melted
4. Put raisin or almond in centre of each
5. Bake in quick oven

title: Sandwich Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup citron shaved thin
- 1 cup nuts
- 1 cup raisins chopped
- 1/4 cup flour
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 3/8 cup flour
- butter size of an egg
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1/4 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Combine citron, nuts and chopped raisins
2. Sift over fruit 1/4 cup flour, 1/2 cup sugar and 3/8 cup flour
3. Add butter size of an egg, yolks of 3 eggs and 1/4 cup milk
4. Add 1/4 teaspoon baking powder
5. Add this to the fruit
6. Bake in layers

title: Saratoga Chips
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash, pare, slice thinly into cold water
2. Let stand 2 hours
3. Drain dry between towels
4. Fry, drain, sprinkle with salt

title: Saratoga Potatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- ice
- smoking-hot fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the potatoes with a plane into slices as thin as paper if possible.
2. Let them soak in cold water for a little time to wash out the starch.
3. Put them into fresh water with a piece of ice to thoroughly chill them.
4. Drain a few of the slices at a time, dry them on a napkin.
5. Put them in a frying-basket and immerse them in smoking-hot fat.
6. Keep them separated, and remove as soon as slightly colored.
7. Turn them into a colander to drain, and sprinkle them with salt.

title: Saratoga Potatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- cold water
- ice
- fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the potatoes with a plane into slices as thin as paper if possible.
2. Let them soak in cold water for a little time to wash out the starch.
3. Put them into fresh water with a piece of ice to thoroughly chill them.
4. Drain a few of the slices at a time and dry them on a napkin.
5. Put them in a frying-basket and immerse them in smoking-hot fat.
6. Keep them separated, and remove as soon as slightly colored.
7. Turn them into a colander to drain, and sprinkle them with salt.
8. When the second lot are fried turn those in the colander on to a paper in the open oven, and so on until all are done.
9. Saratoga potatoes should be perfectly dry and crisp.
10. If wanted hot, place them in the oven a moment before serving.

title: Sardine Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines
- mashed yolks of hard-boiled eggs
- lettuce leaves
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Mix sardines with equal quantity of mashed yolks of hard-boiled eggs
2. Arrange in nests of lettuce leaves
3. Serve with mayonnaise

title: Sardine Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 boxes sardines
- 1 head lettuce
- Salt and pepper
- A little lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take sardines and throw into hot water, having first drained away all the oil
2. A few minutes will free the sardines from grease
3. Pour away the water and dry the fish in a cloth
4. Scrape away the skins and pound the sardines in a mortar till reduced to paste
5. Add pepper, salt, and some tiny pieces of lettuce
6. Spread on the sandwiches, which have been previously cut
7. Alternatively, chop the sardines up fine and squeeze a few drops of lemon-juice into them and spread between buttered bread or cold biscuits

title: Sardine Savoy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three slices of bread
- sardines
- two eggs
- one quarter ounce of butter
- one teaspoon Tarragon vinegar
- one quarter teaspoon made mustard
- salt
- Worcester sauce

## Directions

1. Cut three slices of bread a little larger than the size of a sardine
2. Fry a delicate brown on both sides
3. Place a sardine on each and make them hot in the oven
4. Beat up two eggs and mix with a quarter ounce of butter, one teaspoon Tarragon vinegar, quarter teaspoon made mustard, salt to taste, and a little Worcester sauce
5. Put these in a small saucepan and stir over the fire until it thickens (not boils)
6. Pour over the bread and sardines

title: Sardines Au Gratin
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Sardines in oil
- lemon-juice
- peppered cracker-dust
- butter
- crackers
- sliced lemon

## Directions

1. Lift each fish carefully from the oil in which it was put up
2. Hold suspended for a moment to let most of the oil drip from it
3. Squeeze a few drops of lemon-juice upon it
4. Roll in very fine, peppered cracker-dust
5. Lay upon a buttered tin, or stoneware plate
6. Brown lightly upon the upper grating of a quick oven
7. Pass crackers, heated and buttered, and sliced lemon with them

title: Sardines Au Gratin
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines in oil
- lemon juice
- cracker dust
- pepper
- butter
- crackers

## Directions

1. Lift each fish carefully from the oil in which it was put up
2. Hold suspended for a moment to let most of the oil drip from it
3. Squeeze a few drops of lemon-juice upon it and roll in very fine, peppered cracker-dust
4. Lay upon a buttered tin, or stoneware plate
5. Brown lightly upon the upper grating of a quick oven
6. Pass crackers, heated and buttered, and sliced lemon with them

title: Sardines On Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Sardines
- cayenne
- lemon-juice
- hot-buttered toast
- anchovy paste

## Directions

1. Scrape and bone the sardines
2. Lay them on a plate
3. Sprinkle them with lemon-juice and a little cayenne pepper
4. Stand them in the oven until thoroughly hot
5. Have ready some neat slices of hot-buttered toast
6. Lay the sardines on these and serve at once
7. This dish may be varied by spreading the toast with anchovy paste before laying on the sardines

title: Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three eggs
- Three ounces sugar
- Half pint of whipped cream
- Half glass of chartreuse

## Directions

1. Whip eggs and sugar over boiling water for ten minutes
2. Remove and whip until cold
3. Add cream and chartreuse

title: Sauce for Cottage Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup boiling water
- two tablespoonfuls of brown sugar
- lump of butter size of an egg
- one egg

## Directions

1. Add brown sugar, butter, and beaten egg to boiling water
2. Let all come to a boil
3. Take care not to scorch

title: Sauce for Cracker Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup sugar
- one egg
- one cup hot milk
- little salt
- wine or brandy

## Directions

1. Beat egg to a froth
2. Pour one cup hot milk over it with little salt
3. Flavor with wine or brandy

title: Sauce for Moulded Salmon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of milk
- 1 tablespoonful of cornflour
- 1 large spoonful of butter
- 1 raw egg
- 1 teaspoonful of anchovy, mushroom or tomato catsup
- 1 small pinch of mace
- 1 pinch of cayenne

## Directions

1. Heat the milk to a boil
2. Thicken with a tablespoonful of cornflour
3. Add one large spoonful of butter
4. Put the egg in last
5. Very carefully boil one minute to cook it
6. When the pudding is turned from the mould, pour over it and serve

title: Sauce for Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup sugar
- one egg
- one cup hot milk
- little salt
- wine or brandy

## Directions

1. Beat egg to a froth
2. Pour one cup hot milk over it with little salt
3. Flavor with wine or brandy

title: Sauce for Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs (yolks only)
- two tablespoons castor sugar
- one cup sherry

## Directions

1. Whip egg yolks and sugar to cream
2. Cook in double saucepan one minute
3. Serve immediately

title: Sauce for Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- a little cold water
- 1 cup maple syrup
- 1 3/4 cups boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and cornstarch with a little cold water
2. Add maple syrup and boiling water
3. Cook about 5 minutes

title: Sauce for Salmon Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch or flour
- liquid from the salmon
- 1 large spoon butter
- 1 teaspoon tomato catsup
- red pepper and salt to taste
- 1 egg beaten

## Directions

1. Thicken milk with cornstarch or flour
2. Add the liquid from the salmon
3. Add butter and tomato catsup
4. Add red pepper and salt to taste
5. Beat in egg very carefully
6. Can be eaten hot or cold

title: Sausage Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh pork
- fat
- salt
- sage
- pepper

## Directions

1. Use 1/2 fat to 3/4 lean fresh pork
2. To each pound of meat add 1 even tablespoon salt, 1 teaspoon sifted sage, a scant 3/4 teaspoon pepper
3. Cut in thin strips and put through sausage machine or meat chopper
4. Add the seasoning and mix thoroughly

title: Sausage Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- baking powder
- salt
- sausage or sausage meat

## Directions

1. Make a dough as for biscuits
2. Roll out quite thin and cut in large rounds
3. Lay sausage or sausage meat on half of each piece
4. Turn other half over and pinch together
5. Put in baking tin and bake 1/2 hour
6. These are good either hot or cold

title: Sauted Egg Plant
body:
## Ingredients

- egg plant
- salt
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Pare, cut in slices
2. sprinkle slices with salt
3. pile on plate and apply pressure for 1 hour
4. dredge with flour
5. saute in butter

title: Sauted Parsnips
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnips
- cold water
- salted boiling water
- butter
- sweet dripping
- salt
- pepper
- cream
- eggs

## Directions

1. Soak the parsnips in cold water
2. Scrape them and cut in sections or thick slices
3. Boil in salted boiling water until tender
4. Drain them
5. Put a little butter or sweet dripping in a hot frying pan
6. Saute the parsnips until nicely browned
7. Alternatively, mash the boiled parsnips until very smooth
8. Season with salt, pepper and butter, or a little cream
9. Form into cakes and saute them

title: Sauteed Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- crumbs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean oysters and dry
2. Season
3. Dip in crumbs
4. Saute

title: Savory Celery
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- celery stalks
- stock
- onion-juice
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- roux

## Directions

1. Select the whitest and tenderest stalks and lay aside in ice-water
2. Cut the outer, coarser stalks into three-inch lengths
3. Stew in a cupful of stock, seasoned with half teaspoonful of onion-juice, salt, pepper, and parsley
4. Cook, covered, for an hour, slowly
5. Drain and press in a colander
6. Return the stock to the fire
7. When it boils put the reserved stalks, also cut into short lengths, into it
8. Cook gently until tender
9. Thicken with a good spoonful of roux
10. Boil up and serve

title: Savory Cream (Cold Entree)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- A small lobster or 1 can passed through a sieve
- 1/2 pt. of cream
- 1 gill tomato juice
- 1 gill aspic jelly
- 1 tablespoonful of mayonnaise
- 1/2 oz. or little more of gelatine
- Small pieces of lobster
- Small leaves of parsley

## Directions

1. Put a little aspic jelly in the bottom of a border mould, decorate with small pieces of lobster and small leaves of parsley
2. Beat gelatine in tomato juice, whip the cream; also whip the aspic
3. Mix these together
4. Stir in the mayonnaise and the melted gelatine and tomato, also your lobster
5. Fill the mould carefully and set
6. When cold turn out and fill the centre with small salad

title: Savory Duck
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- 1 pound of shin of beef
- 1 onion
- 2 cloves
- 1 bay-leaf
- thyme
- parsley
- 1 carrot
- 1 small piece of turnip
- pepper
- salt
- 3 pints of stock or water
- 1/2 pound of veal
- 1 little grated nutmeg
- 1/2 pound of green peas
- 1 ounce of gelatine
- whites of 2 eggs
- cooked peas
- cooked carrots
- cooked turnip

## Directions

1. Bone the duck by cutting it open down the back
2. Take out the back bone, the breast and then the leg bones
3. Put the bones, one pound of shin of beef, one onion, two cloves, one bay-leaf, some thyme and parsley, one carrot, a small piece of turnip, pepper and salt into a saucepan with three pints of stock or water
4. Chop one-half pound of veal with an onion, a little grated nutmeg, pepper, salt and one-half pound of green peas
5. Fill the duck with this and sew it up
6. Stew it with the bones for two hours
7. Take out the duck and strain the gravy
8. Put it in another pan and mix in one ounce of gelatine and the whites of two eggs
9. Beat it over the fire until it boils, then let it boil for ten minutes without stirring, with the lid on
10. It will then be clear when again skimmed
11. Cover the bottom of a dish (large enough to hold the duck) with cooked peas, carrots and turnip
12. Put a small cupful of the gravy, and let it get cool
13. Put in the duck (breast downwards)
14. Let the gravy be nearly cold, then pour it over the duck
15. The duck will then set in a firm jelly, and can be turned out on to another dish

title: Savory Eggs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- chopped ham
- parsley
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- cream
- bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil some eggs quite hard
2. Shell them and cut in halves
3. Take out the yolks
4. Pass through a sieve
5. Mix with chopped ham, parsley, a little bit of onion, pepper and salt and a little cream
6. Put back in the whites
7. Place on little rounds of bread and butter
8. Serve cold

title: Savory Grill
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat or fowl
- flour
- 1 tablespoonful of Worcestershire sauce
- 1 dessertspoonful of chutney sauce
- 1 dessertspoonful of Yorkshire relish
- 1 teaspoonful of anchovy sauce
- butter the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Cut up any cold meat or fowl, and sprinkle with flour on both sides
2. Place the mixture in any dish that will stand the heat of the top of the stove
3. Mix well
4. Allow it to heat slowly and thoroughly

title: Savory Rabbit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- 2 ounces butter
- seasoning
- 1/2 onion
- parsley
- 2 ounces flour
- 1 pint of broth
- grated nutmeg
- mixed herbs
- bacon

## Directions

1. Cut rabbit into small joints
2. Place them in a saucepan with 2 ounces butter, some seasoning, 1/2 onion, and some parsley chopped very fine
3. Fry very slowly until cooked golden brown
4. Dust over 2 ounces of flour
5. Add 1 pint of broth, a little grated nutmeg and mixed herbs
6. Let simmer 3/4 hour
7. Pile the rabbit in a heap on hot platter and pour sauce over
8. Garnish with small pieces of bacon

title: Savory Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled yolk of eggs
- salt
- pepper
- French mustard
- melted butter
- thin crackers
- celery
- ale or beer

## Directions

1. Pound hard boiled yolk of eggs, and if necessary pass through sieve
2. Add a seasoning of salt, pepper, French mustard and a little melted butter
3. Spread on thin crackers
4. Serve with celery and ale or beer

title: Savory Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chopped chicken
- 1 slice boiled ham, minced
- 3 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1/4 teaspoonful mace
- A few drops onion juice
- A few spoonfuls oyster juice
- Graham bread slices

## Directions

1. Mix chicken, boiled ham (minced), butter, mace, and onion-juice into a soft paste with a few spoonfuls of oyster-liquor
2. Set in a saucepan of boiling water and stir until smoking hot
3. Set aside to get cold
4. Spread between thin slices of Graham bread

title: Scalloped Apples
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups stale bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 cups sliced apples
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- grated rind and juice of 1/2 a lemon

## Directions

1. Butter pudding dish
2. Cover with bread crumbs
3. Put in a layer of apples
4. Sprinkle with sugar, lemon rind and juice
5. Dot with butter
6. Repeat till dish is full, finishing with bread crumbs
7. Cover when first put in the oven to prevent crumbs browning too rapidly

title: Scalloped Asparagus
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- butter
- four hard-boiled eggs
- pepper
- one large tablespoonful butter
- one tablespoonful flour
- one cupful hot milk
- paprica
- fine crumbs
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Wash the asparagus and cut off the hard, woody part of the stalks
2. Cut the tender part into inch lengths and parboil for ten minutes in hot, salted water
3. Drain and put a layer of them in a buttered bake-dish
4. Scatter over this minced, hard-boiled eggs, season with salt, pepper, and butter-bits, and go on thus until the ingredients are used up
5. Make a roux of a large tablespoonful of butter and one of flour, and thin with a cupful of hot milk
6. Cook for a minute, season with paprica, and pour over the asparagus, a layer of which should be uppermost in the scallop
7. Sift fine crumbs over all with bits of butter stuck in it and grated cheese upon this
8. Bake twenty minutes, covered, then brown slightly

title: Scalloped Beef and Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast or boiled beef
- 1 can tomatoes
- 1 cup gravy
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Drain the juice from tomatoes and set on the fire with 1 tablespoon of sugar
2. Cut the beef into slices and place a layer in a greased baking dish
3. Over this put some tomato, moisten thoroughly with the juice and gravy
4. Season highly, sprinkle with bread crumbs, dot with bits of butter
5. Add a little pepper and salt
6. Bake 3 1/2 hour, uncover, brown and serve

title: Scalloped Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 firm cabbage
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 eggs well beaten
- 3 tablespoons cream or milk
- pepper and salt
- fine crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil a firm cabbage in two waters, drain and press and let it get perfectly cold, then mince fine
2. Add 2 tablespoons melted butter, 2 eggs well beaten, 3 tablespoons cream or milk, pepper and salt to your taste
3. Put into a buttered bake dish, sift fine crumbs on top, and bake, covered, half an hour, then brown

title: Scalloped Cheese
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 slices bread
- 1/4 lb. cheese
- 4 eggs
- 3 cups milk
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Take three slices of bread, well buttered, first cutting off the brown outside crust.
2. Grate fine any kind of good cheese; lay the bread in layers in a buttered baking-dish, sprinkle over each the grated cheese, some salt and pepper to taste.
3. Mix well-beaten eggs with milk; pour it over the bread and cheese.
4. Bake it in a hot oven as you would cook a bread pudding. This makes an ample dish for four people.

title: Scalloped Cheese
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 2 cups milk
- 3 eggs
- 1/4 lb. grated cheese
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Roll bread crumbs, soak until soft in milk; mix with eggs beaten light, add grated cheese and butter in small pieces.
2. Salt and pepper to taste.
3. Put all in baking dish and cover the top with sifted bread crumbs which have been buttered, peppered and salted.
4. Bake fifteen minutes.

title: Scalloped Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flaked fish
- 1 cup stale crumbs
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1-16 teaspoon pepper
- 1 1/16 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Mix
2. Put in buttered dish
3. Cover with buttered crumbs
4. Bake in a moderate oven 30 to 40 minutes

title: Scalloped Lobster
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Lobster
- Cracker-crumbs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Milk

## Directions

1. Butter the dish, chop the lobster quite fine
2. Layer of lobster and cracker-crumbs alternately
3. Add salt, pepper, and butter
4. Moisten with milk
5. Bake for twenty minutes

title: Scalloped Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups meat
- 3 teaspoons fat
- 3 teaspoons flour
- 1 1/2 cups hot milk or stock
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper
- onion juice or parsley
- 2 cups buttered crumbs

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 cup buttered crumbs in a baking dish
2. Mix the meat in the sauce
3. Pour this into the dish
4. Cover the top with the remainder of the crumbs
5. Brown in a hot oven 20 minutes

title: Scalloped Meat (Alternative)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cold meat
- 1/2 cup mashed potatoes
- 1/2 teaspoon made mustard
- gravy or stock
- 1 cup mashed potatoes
- 2 teaspoons butter
- 1 cup milk
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Chop meat fine
2. Mix 1 cup cold meat chopped fine, 1/2 cup mashed potatoes, 1/2 teaspoon made mustard, and enough gravy or stock to moisten
3. Put in buttered baking dish
4. Mix 1 cup mashed potatoes, 2 teaspoons butter, 1 cup milk, and one egg well beaten
5. Cover this over the top of the meat
6. Bake in the oven until a nice brown

title: Scalloped Mutton
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast mutton
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- oysters
- bread crumbs
- tomato juice or brown gravy
- cold boiled macaroni

## Directions

1. Remove fat and skin from cold roast mutton, cut in small, thin slices, season with pepper and salt.
2. Butter a small low dish, put in a layer of meat, then a layer of oysters, strained and seasoned, then a layer of bread crumbs, then a second layer of meat, oysters and crumbs for the top.
3. Strew a spoonful of butter cut in small pieces over the top, and moisten the whole with tomato juice or brown gravy.
4. Cold boiled macaroni, cut in inch pieces, may be used instead of oysters.

title: Scalloped Oysters
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- fine cracker-crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- oyster liquor
- cream
- sliced lemon

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of a greased bake-dish with oysters
2. Cover the oysters with fine cracker-crumbs
3. Sprinkle these with pepper, salt, and bits of butter
4. Lay in more oysters and go on in this order until all are in
5. The top layer should be of crumbs and well buttered
6. Pour over each layer of oysters as it goes in, a few spoonfuls of oyster liquor
7. Upon the crumbs pour the same quantity of cream
8. Bake, covered, in a quick oven until hot all through
9. Uncover and brown lightly
10. Serve with sliced lemon

title: Scalloped Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- crushed cracker-crumbs
- oyster liquor
- milk
- oysters
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Butter a pudding-dish
2. Cover the bottom with a layer of crushed cracker-crumbs, moistening them with oyster liquor and a little milk
3. Put a layer of oysters on this, dotting it with bits of butter and seasoning with salt and pepper
4. Cover with another layer of crumbs
5. Repeat the layer of oysters
6. Continue in this order until the dish is full, making the top layer of crumbs
7. Strew thickly with bits of butter
8. Bake covered for 20 minutes and brown

title: Scalloped Oysters (Second Recipe)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of oysters
- oyster liquor
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
- one cup of hot milk
- one cup of oyster liquor
- two teaspoons of butter
- six tablespoons of bread crumbs
- three tablespoons of chopped celery
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Scald a quart of oysters in their own liquor till plump
2. Drain and place to keep warm
3. Make sauce of one tablespoon each of butter and flour
4. Heat in a pan two teaspoons of butter, and brown six tablespoons of bread crumbs
5. Put three tablespoons of chopped celery in the bottom of a bake dish
6. Add the white sauce flavored with salt and pepper to taste
7. Top with the browned crumbs
8. Place in oven and heat thoroughly
9. Serve very hot

title: Scalloped Oysters (Third Recipe)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- one egg
- one tablespoonful of warm water
- fine bread-crumbs
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- onion juice
- one tablespoonful of chopped celery
- one-half cupful of thin cream

## Directions

1. Pick over, rinse, drain, and dry the oysters, which should be of fair size
2. Break an egg into a saucer and add a tablespoonful of warm water
3. Beat just enough to mix
4. Have ready in a bowl a quantity of fine bread-crumbs
5. Drop each oyster in the beaten egg, then into the crumbs, and lay in a buttered dish
