2. Mix in currants, raisins, molasses, vanilla, and mixed spice
3. Dissolve baking soda in vinegar, fill cup with buttermilk and add to cake
4. Mix stiff with flour
5. Put in a dish and steam 2 hours
6. Then bake slowly an hour

title: Steamed Coffee
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ounces coffee
- 1 pint water
- 1 raw egg
- 4 tablespoons cold water

## Directions

1. Beat dry coffee into 1 raw egg mixed with 4 tablespoons of cold water
2. Add boiling water
3. Set coffee-pot on trivet in kettle of boiling water
4. Steam 20 minutes

title: Steamed Rice
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup rice
- salt
- 3/4 cup stock
- 1/2 can tomatoes
- 1 spoonful thyme

## Directions

1. Combine rice, salt, stock, tomatoes, and thyme
2. Steam until cooked

title: Steamed Roly Poly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup suet, chopped very fine
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup water
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking-powder
- A pinch of salt
- Plum jam
- Two eggs
- one cup of sugar
- half cup of butter
- one cup of boiling wine

## Directions

1. Roll on a board and spread thickly with plum jam
2. Place in a tin and steam one and a half or two hours
3. For sauce: Mix two eggs, one cup of sugar, and half cup of butter well together
4. Pour into the mixture one cup of boiling wine (sherry is best)

title: Steamed Sago Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. milk
- 3 oz. sago
- 2 oz. fresh butter
- 3 oz. sugar
- Grated rind of 1 lemon
- three eggs
- one-half tablespoon flour
- one and one-half teaspoonfuls sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan milk, sago, fresh butter, sugar, and the grated rind of one lemon
2. Boil all together for thirteen and a half minutes
3. Work in three eggs
4. Mix together one-half tablespoon flour and one and one-half teaspoonfuls sugar
5. Butter mould and dust flour and sugar into it well
6. Pour in the mixture and put buttered paper on top
7. Steam one hour

title: Steamed Salmon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of salmon
- 1 cup of fine breadcrumbs
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful melted butter
- A little milk
- Anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Steam in a mould one hour
2. Make a white sauce
3. Season with teaspoonful anchovy sauce
4. Pour over the salmon when served

title: Stewed Breast of Lamb
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One breast of lamb
- pepper and salt to taste
- sufficient stock to cover it
- thickening of butter and flour
- a pint and a half of green peas
- A few stewed mushrooms

## Directions

1. Skin the lamb and cut into pieces, and season them with pepper and salt
2. lay these in a stew-pan with sufficient stock of gravy to cover them, and stew gently for an hour and a half
3. Just before serving, thicken the gravy with a little butter and flour, give one boil, and pour it over the meat
4. Have ready a pint and a half of green peas and lay them over and around the meat
5. A few stewed mushrooms will be found an improvement if they can be obtained, but they are not necessary for this dish

title: Stewed Carrots
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- weak stock
- two tablespoonfuls of milk
- one tablespoonful of butter
- one tablespoonful of flour
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Scrape and boil whole three-quarters of an hour
2. Drain
3. Cut into cubes half an inch square
4. Have ready in a saucepan enough weak stock to cover the carrot-dice
5. Put them on in it and cook twenty minutes, or until tender
6. Add two tablespoonfuls of milk, a tablespoonful of butter cut up in one of flour, salt and pepper to taste
7. Simmer five minutes
8. Serve

title: Stewed Cauliflower a la Hollandaise
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- weak stock or bouillon
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- drawn-butter
- yolks of two raw eggs

## Directions

1. Cut into large clusters of uniform size and stew tender in weak stock or bouillon
2. Drain, butter, salt, and pepper, and pass with it drawn-butter, into which have been whipped the yolks of two raw eggs

title: Stewed Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- bacon
- chicken
- flour
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Place a layer of bacon in bottom of stew kettle, then a layer of chicken, season and sprinkle a little flour, and so on until you have sufficient
2. Fill kettle with water until within 1 inch of top of meat
3. Cover tightly and cook about 2 hours
4. Do not stir, only turn in round in the pot
5. Skim off grease
6. Make gravy with butter and a little flour

title: Stewed Corn
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- cold water
- hot water

## Directions

1. Husk and clean the corn, and leave it in cold water for fifteen minutes
2. With a sharp knife split each row of grains all the way down from stem to tip of the ear
3. Shave, rather than cut, them off down to the cob
4. Cover with hot water in a sauce-pan, and stew slowly for twenty minutes

title: Stewed Corn
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- hot water
- butter (1 tablespoonful per pint of corn)
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Husk and clean the corn, and leave it in cold water for fifteen minutes.
2. With a sharp knife split each row of grains all the way down from stem to tip of the ear; then shave, rather than cut, them off down to the cob.
3. Cover with hot water in a sauce-pan, and stew slowly for twenty minutes.
4. Stir in a tablespoonful of butter for a pint of corn; pepper and salt and serve.

title: Stewed Corn and Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- corn
- hot water
- tomatoes (1 cupful, chopped and peeled)
- butter
- flour
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook as in last recipe, and when the corn has simmered five minutes add a cupful of chopped tomatoes (peeled).
2. Cook twenty minutes longer after the boil recommences, season and serve.
3. If there is much liquid in the stew, roll the butter in flour before adding it, and boil a minute more than if the flour were not used.

title: Stewed Cranberries
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cranberries
- 1 quart water
- 1 pint sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 1 quart cranberries in 1 quart water for 10 minutes
2. Add 1 pint sugar
3. Cook 10 minutes
4. Remove from stove

title: Stewed Duck
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- good dripping
- 1 sliced onion
- 1 cupful of stock or consomme
- 1 tablespoonful of tomato catsup
- 1 dash of lemon-juice
- 1 tablespoonful of brown roux
- 1 glass of sherry

## Directions

1. Joint as for fricassee
2. Pepper, salt, and flour the duck
3. Heat good dripping in a frying-pan and fry a sliced onion to a light brown
4. Take out the onion, put in the duck, and cook ten minutes, turning two or three times
5. Put into a sauce-pan a cupful of stock or consomme, and while it is still cold lay in the jointed duck
6. Cover and stew slowly until tender
7. Season with pepper and salt, a tablespoonful of tomato catsup, and a dash of lemon-juice
8. Simmer three minutes
9. Stir in a tablespoonful of brown roux
10. Cook a minute to thicken it
11. Add a glass of sherry, and serve

title: Stewed Eggs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Spanish onions
- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 1/2 pt. milk or cream
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fry onions, sliced in butter in a covered pan till very tender, but do not brown
2. Add flour and mix well
3. Add milk and stir until thickened
4. Season
5. Add eggs cut in quarters
6. Simmer ten minutes
7. Serve with garnish of fried strips of bread

title: Stewed Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- fish
- 6 onions
- salt
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Put 2 tablespoons butter in the frying pan
2. When hot lay the fish in with the outside down
3. Slice 6 onions and put on top of fish
4. Sprinkle well with salt and pepper
5. Cover tight and allow to cook 20 minutes or 1/4 hour
6. If it should cook dry, add a very little water
7. Serve hot

title: Stewed Frogs' Legs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Frogs' legs
- Milk
- Flour
- Salt
- Pepper
- Hot butter
- Hot water
- Cream
- Butter
- Flour
- Chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Skin, lay in milk for fifteen minutes
2. Roll in peppered and salted flour, and saute in hot butter for three minutes
3. Cover (barely) with hot water, and stew tender
4. Twenty minutes should suffice
5. Heat half a cupful of cream to boiling, stir in a tablespoonful of butter rolled in flour, boil up
6. Turn into the saucepan where the frogs' legs are simmering
7. Season with pepper, salt, and a little chopped parsley
8. Cook gently for three minutes and serve

title: Stewed Hare or Rabbit
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- cold water
- 1 small onion
- boiling water
- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt
- pepper
- 3/4 teaspoon allspice

## Directions

1. Skin and draw the hare, retaining only the heart
2. Wash in cold water
3. Separate the legs from the body, cut the body into sections and put the whole into cold water to cover, over a slow fire
4. Bring to a boil, skim off any scum that may gather and let boil slowly for 1 1/2 hours, adding boiling water if necessary from time to time
5. Then add 1 small onion, finely minced, and boil 3/4 hour longer
6. At the end of the 2 hours, lift the meat on to the platter
7. Add boiling water to the liquor to make 1 1/2 pints gravy approximately
8. Stir in thickening made with 1 tablespoon flour mixed with cold water to a thin paste
9. Add salt and pepper to taste, and 3/4 teaspoon allspice
10. Let boil up well, pour over the meat and serve at once

title: Stewed Kidneys
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- flour
- water
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil kidneys the night before until very tender
2. Turn meat and gravy into a dish and cover over
3. Next day boil a few minutes
4. Thicken with flour and water
5. Add part of an onion chopped fine, pepper, salt and a lump of butter

title: Stewed Kidneys, With Wine
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Kidneys
- Cold water
- Flour
- Butter
- 1 slice of onion
- 1 cupful of boiling water or heated stock
- 1 tablespoonful of catsup
- A dash of paprica or cayenne
- Salt
- 1 small tablespoonful of butter
- Browned flour
- A generous glass of sherry or claret

## Directions

1. Slice the kidneys, after they have been soaked in cold water
2. Wipe dry and roll in flour
3. Have ready in a saucepan a little butter in which has been fried a slice of onion
4. Lay in the kidneys and roll them over and over, coating them with the butter, for two minutes
5. Pour in a cupful of boiling water or heated stock
6. Simmer not longer than ten or twelve minutes
7. Take them up and lay upon a hot dish
8. Add to the gravy a tablespoonful of catsup, a dash of paprica or cayenne, and salt, a small tablespoonful of butter that has been rolled in browned flour
9. When it has boiled up, add a generous glass of sherry or claret
10. Pour over the kidneys and serve

title: Stewed Lamb and Green Peas
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds of coarser parts of the lamb
- flour
- two tablespoonfuls of good dripping
- half a sliced onion
- cupful of boiling water or weak stock
- generous cupful of green peas
- tablespoonful of brown roux
- slices of toast
- hot tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Buy three pounds of the coarser parts of the lamb; cut into inch lengths and dredge with flour.
2. Have ready in a saucepan two tablespoonfuls of good dripping, and when it hisses put in half a sliced onion, and fry to a light brown.
3. Skim out the onion and put in the meat, cooking for five minutes and turning often to keep it from sticking to the bottom of the pan.
4. Then add a cupful of boiling water, or weak stock, cover closely and cook gently for one hour.
5. Add then a generous cupful of green peas. Canned will do, but the fresh are better.
6. Stew for twenty minutes longer, or until the peas are tender, add a tablespoonful of brown roux, boil up once, and pour upon slices of toast that have been soaked in hot tomato sauce.

title: Stewed Lamb Breast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One breast of lamb
- pepper and salt to taste
- sufficient stock to cover it
- thickening of butter and flour
- 1 pint and a half of green peas
- stewed mushrooms

## Directions

1. Skin the lamb and cut into pieces, and season them with pepper and salt
2. Lay these in a stew-pan with sufficient stock of gravy to cover them
3. Stew gently for an hour and a half
4. Just before serving, thicken the gravy with a little butter and flour
5. Give one boil, and pour it over the meat
6. Have ready a pint and a half of green peas and lay them over and around the meat
7. Add a few stewed mushrooms

title: Stewed Ox-Tails
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ox-tails
- 1 onion
- 3 cloves
- 1 blade mace
- 1/2 teaspoonful whole black pepper
- 1/2 teaspoonful allspice
- 1/2 teaspoonful salt
- Small bunch of savory herbs
- 1 tablespoonful lemon juice
- 1 tablespoonful mushroom catsup
- Thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Divide the tails at the joints, wash, and put them into a stew-pan with sufficient water to cover, and set them on the fire.
2. When the water boils remove the scum, and add the onions cut into rings, the spice, seasoning and herbs.
3. Cover the stew-pan closely, and simmer gently until tender, which will be in about two and one-half hours.
4. Take the tails out, make a thickening of butter and flour; add it to the gravy, and let it boil for one-quarter of an hour.
5. Strain it through a sieve into a saucepan; put back the tails, add the lemon juice and catsup; let the whole just boil up, and serve.
6. Serve with croutons or sippets of toasted bread.

title: Stewed Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts oysters
- 1 pint hot water
- salt
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 large cup boiling milk

## Directions

1. Add a pint of hot water to the liquor drained from 2 quarts of oysters
2. Put a little salt and pepper with it
3. Set over the fire and let it boil up once
4. Turn in the oysters and let them cook for not more than five minutes, or until the gills begin to crimp
5. Watch carefully for this stage, as every second beyond it means injury to the flavor of the oyster
6. Add 2 tablespoons of butter and a large cup of boiling milk
7. Take from the fire immediately and serve as soon as possible
8. Never let them stand at the back of the stove and keep warm

title: Stewed Rabbit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- 2 large onions
- 6 cloves
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped lemon peel
- A few forcemeat balls
- 1 large tablespoonful mushroom catsup
- Thickening of butter and flour

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into small joints, put them into a stewpan, add the onions sliced, and the cloves and minced lemon peel.
2. Pour in sufficient water to cover the meat and when the rabbit is nearly done drop in a few forcemeat balls, to which has been added the liver finely chopped.
3. Thicken the gravy with flour and butter, put in the catsup, give one boil and serve.
4. Time, rather more than one-half hour.

title: Stewed Salsify
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one bunch of salsify
- one cup milk
- one tablespoonful butter
- three tablespoonfuls cracker crumbs
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Scrape a bunch of salsify and drop into cold water as you cut into inch lengths
2. Boil in hot, salted water until tender
3. Drain and pour into the saucepan with the salsify a cupful of hot milk
4. Simmer five minutes, and stir in cracker-dust, with pepper and salt
5. Stew gently for three minutes

title: Stewed Steak and Macaroni
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs. of steak or other lean beef
- ¼ lb. of macaroni
- Butter for frying
- 1 tablespoonful of catsup
- A little flour
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the beef in small pieces, roll it in flour, and fry slightly in a little butter
2. put it into a stew-pan, cover with hot water, and allow it to simmer slowly for an hour and a half
3. then add the macaroni and simmer again for three-quarters of an hour
4. season with pepper, salt, and catsup, and stew for ten minutes after the seasoning is added
5. Serve on a hot dish, the beef in the centre, and the macaroni round

title: Stewed Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of butter per quart
- corn-starch
- fine cracker-dust
- 1 teaspoonful of salt per quart
- 1 teaspoonful of pepper per quart
- 1/2 teaspoonful of grated onion per quart

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water upon tomatoes to loosen their skins, and peel them.
2. Slice, or cut into dice, and cook in a porcelain or agate-iron saucepan for twenty minutes.
3. Drain off the superfluous liquid, pepper and salt it, and keep for sauces, stews, and soups.
4. Stir into the hot tomatoes, for each quart, a tablespoonful of butter rolled in corn-starch or in fine cracker-dust, a teaspoonful each of salt and pepper, and half a teaspoonful of grated onion.
5. Cook three minutes longer and serve.

title: Stewed Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh tomatoes
- boiling water
- salt
- sugar
- pepper
- butter
- cornstarch
- cold water
- cracker or breadcrumbs
- chopped onions

## Directions

1. If fresh tomatoes are used remove the skins by placing them in boiling water a few minutes; they will then peel off easily.
2. Cut them in pieces, and stew in a granite-ware saucepan until tender.
3. To one quart of tomatoes add one teaspoonful each of salt and sugar, one-quarter teaspoonful of pepper, and a tablespoonful of butter.
4. Thicken with a teaspoonful of cornstarch wet in cold water, or with one-half cupful of cracker or breadcrumbs.
5. Chopped onions are relished in this dish and should be well cooked.

title: Stewed Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe tomatoes
- hot water
- butter
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Put ripe tomatoes into hot water and skin them
2. Throw them into an earthen stewpan, cut up
3. Let tomatoes cook gently for a few minutes
4. Season with butter, pepper and salt
5. Serve

title: Stiff Cranberry Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- berries
- water
- sugar (granulated)

## Directions

1. To each pound of berries add one pint of water, and boil half an hour, stirring gently and skimming
2. Strain well
3. to each pint of juice add one pound of sugar (granulated)
4. Boil again until it jellies

title: Stock
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lbs. of shin of beef, or 6 lbs. of knuckle of veal
- Bones, trimmings of poultry, or fresh meat
- 1 lb. of lean bacon or ham
- 2 oz. of butter
- 2 large onions, each stuck with cloves
- 1 turnip
- 3 carrots
- 1 head of celery
- 2 oz. of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of whole pepper
- 1 large blade of mace
- 1 bunch of savory herbs except sage
- 4 quarts and 1/2 pt. of cold water

## Directions

1. Cut up the meat and bacon, or ham, into pieces of about three inches square
2. Break the bones into small pieces
3. Rub the butter on the bottom of the stewpan
4. Put in one-half a pint of water, the broken bones, then meat and all other ingredients
5. Cover the stew-pan, and place it on a sharp fire, occasionally stirring its contents
6. When the bottom of the pan becomes covered with a pale, jelly-like substance, add the four quarts of cold water, and simmer very gently for five or six hours
7. Do not let it boil quickly
8. When nearly cooked, throw in a tablespoonful of salt to assist the scum to rise
9. Remove every particle of scum whilst it is doing, and strain it through a fine hair sieve
10. When cool remove all grease

title: Stone Cream
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Preserve, strawberry, raspberry or peach
- Three large tablespoonfuls of lemon juice
- One pint of cream
- Three-quarters of an ounce of isinglass
- Very little warm milk
- Wine
- Loaf sugar
- Blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Put some preserve into a glass dish with three large tablespoonfuls of lemon juice on the top
2. Boil a pint of cream with three-quarters of an ounce of isinglass dissolved in a very little warm milk
3. Add wine and loaf sugar
4. When new milk warm, keep moving it round on the sweetmeats on the dish
5. When quite cold cut some blanched almonds lengthwise and stick them all over it

title: Story Cake or Large Christmas Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 eggs
- 5 cups brown sugar
- 2 cups blackstrap
- 1 1/2 pounds butter
- 2 1/2 cups buttermilk
- 3 cups sour cream
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 1 1/2 teaspoons ground cloves
- 1 1/2 teaspoons ground cinnamon
- 1 1/2 grated nutmegs
- 3 lemon peels chopped fine
- 7 cups washed currants
- 7 cups stoned raisins
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients with flour to make a very stiff batter
2. Bake in slow oven
3. Can be iced with the beaten yolks of the eggs and white sugar, or with icing sugar and decorated with candies and other ornaments

title: Stove Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- scraps of meat or bones
- vegetables
- potatoes
- pepper
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Put meat or bones in a stewpan with as many different kinds of vegetables as can be got and cut into convenient size for cooking, along with as many potatoes wished
2. Gently cook in just enough water to keep from scorching till done
3. Season with pepper and salt at the start

title: Strawberry Mould
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon jelly
- strawberries
- half a pint of cream
- one-quarter of an ounce of best leaf gelatine
- water or milk
- strawberry pulp

## Directions

1. Line a mould with nicely flavored lemon jelly
2. Put in a layer of strawberries, freed from their stalks and cut in halves
3. Set this layer with a little more jelly, have a smaller mould the same shape as the first
4. stand it in the centre of the larger mould
5. fill up the outer circumference with alternate layers of the strawberries and lemon jelly
6. Place the mould on ice to set
7. whip half a pint of cream to a stiff froth, adding about one-quarter of an ounce of best leaf gelatine, dissolved in a very little water or milk
8. very gradually add to this a cupful of strawberry pulp, sweetened to taste
9. When the jelly is set, remove the inner mould by pouring a little warm water into it and lifting out as quickly as possible
10. fill up the space thus left with the whipped cream and strawberry pulp
11. put the mould back on the ice for an hour or two
12. turn out and serve with cream

title: Strawberry Sherbet
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of berries
- 1 pint of granulated sugar
- 1 pint of water
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1 tablespoonful of gelatine

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatine in the water.
2. Add berries and sugar, and last of all the lemon juice.
3. Stir lightly and freeze.

title: Strawberry Shortcake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- ½ cup butter
- milk
- 1 quart strawberries
- 1 pint sugar

## Directions

1. Sift cream tartar and soda into flour
2. Rub in butter
3. Add milk enough to make it roll out about 1 inch thick
4. Bake in oven
5. When baked, cut in square pieces
6. Split the squares and spread with butter
7. Make a filling with strawberries and sugar mashed together
8. Spread the squares with the filling and put together again

title: Strawberry Whip
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup strawberries
- 1 cup fine granulated sugar
- white of 1 egg

## Directions

1. Crush strawberries in fine granulated sugar
2. Beat the white of egg stiff
3. mix together and beat 20 minutes
4. Sprinkle with strawberries

title: Stuffed Beefsteak
body:
## Ingredients

- flank or round steak
- pepper
- salt
- dressing
- twine
- water

## Directions

1. Pound steak well
2. Season with pepper and salt
3. Spread with a nice dressing
4. Pull out and tie closely with twine
5. Put in a kettle a quart of boiling water
6. Boil slowly 1 hour
7. Take out and place in a dripping pan, adding water in which it was boiled
8. Baste until a nice brown
9. Make gravy of the drippings
10. Slice and serve

title: Stuffed Chicken or Turkey Legs
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken or veal, chopped very fine
- liver
- larding pork
- salt
- pepper
- chopped parsley
- egg
- beaten egg
- breadcrumbs
- hot fat
- butter
- mustard
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Carefully remove the tendons and bone from the drumsticks, all but about an inch and a half at the small end.
2. Stuff the leg with a force-meat made of chicken or veal chopped very fine, and use with it the liver and a little strip of larding pork; season it with salt, pepper, and chopped parsley, and moisten it with one egg.
3. Draw the skin over the end and sew it closely together, keeping the shape as natural as possible.
4. Lay the stuffed legs in a baking-pan; cover with boiling water, and simmer an hour, or until tender.
5. Remove them from the water, press them into shape, and let cool.
6. When cold, take out the stitches, dredge with salt and pepper, roll in beaten egg and breadcrumbs, and fry in hot fat until browned; or broil them on both sides four minutes, if chicken; six minutes, if turkey legs; or they may be sauted in butter.
7. They may be deviled by rubbing them with mustard and a little red pepper before coating with the eggs and crumbs.
8. Serve them arranged like chops, the bones masked with paper frills.

title: Stuffed Dates
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- dates
- fondant or peanut or blanched almond
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stone
2. Put in its place a bit of fondant, or, better still, a peanut or a blanched almond
3. Dust with fine sugar

title: Stuffed Dates
body:
## Ingredients

- shelled walnuts
- dates
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Stone the dates
2. Place walnut in centre of each date
3. Roll date in granulated sugar

title: Stuffed Green Peppers
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet green peppers
- two peppers
- one small onion
- one large tomato
- stale breadcrumbs
- one teaspoonful salt
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut the tops off the sweet green peppers and carefully remove the seeds
2. Chop together very fine two peppers, one small onion and one large tomato (peeled)
3. Add an equal amount of stale breadcrumbs, one teaspoonful of salt and sufficient melted butter to moisten the mixture
4. Fill the peppers with the mixture, replace the tops and bake for half an hour in a moderate oven

title: Stuffed Ham
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- breadcrumbs
- pepper
- butter
- onion
- Worcestershire sauce
- mosquito-netting
- cold water
- vinegar
- black pepper or Hungarian sweet red pepper (paprica)
- asparagus tops
- nasturtium flowers or parsley and marigolds

## Directions

1. Soak the ham over night and scrub well in the morning.
2. Run a narrow sharp knife along the bone, loosening the meat for the whole length; shake and pull the bone while doing this until you can withdraw it.
3. Dig out the flat bone from the butt-end of the ham.
4. Fill the cavity left by the bones with a stuffing of breadcrumbs, seasoned with pepper, butter, onion, and Worcestershire sauce.
5. Pack it in well and sew the ham tightly into shape in mosquito-netting.
6. Cover with cold water in which have been stirred two tablespoonfuls of vinegar, and cook twenty minutes to the pound after the boil begins.
7. Leave the ham in the water until it is lukewarm, take it out and put to press under an inverted dish with a heavy weight on top.
8. Leave it thus for eight or ten hours; take off the cloth, and then the skin.
9. Dot the top with black pepper, or Hungarian sweet red pepper (paprica), using the tip of the middle finger to make the impressions.
10. Send to table surrounded by a garland of asparagus tops and nasturtium flowers, or parsley and marigolds.

title: Stuffed Kidneys
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- melted butter
- bread crumbs
- cooked salt pork
- parsley
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- onion-juice
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Split the kidneys lengthwise, leaving enough meat and skin on one side to serve as a hinge.
2. Rub well inside with melted butter, and broil them, back downward, over a bright fire for eight minutes.
3. Have ready a stuffing of bread crumbs, cooked salt pork, parsley and butter, seasoned with pepper, salt, and onion-juice.
4. Heat in a double boiler, stir in the juice of a half a lemon.
5. Fill the kidneys with the mixture, run a toothpick through the outer edges or lips to keep in the stuffing.
6. Pepper them and serve with sauce piquante.

title: Stuffed Leg of Mutton
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton with bone removed
- cupful of breadcrumbs
- butter
- two tablespoonfuls of finely minced almonds
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- onion-juice
- carrot
- sliced tomato
- small onion
- stalk of celery
- two cupfuls of boiling water
- browned flour

## Directions

1. Have the bone removed, tearing as little as possible.
2. Fill the cavity with a dressing of a cupful of breadcrumbs worked up with butter, two tablespoonfuls of finely minced almonds, pepper, salt, parsley, and a little onion-juice.
3. Sew or tie up the gash, that the stuffing may not escape.
4. Have ready in your roaster a carrot cut into dice, a sliced tomato, a small onion, minced, a stalk of celery, and a little parsley.
5. Lay the mutton upon them, pour over it two cupfuls of boiling water, cover closely and cook two hours, basting four times.
6. Remove the cover, brown, after basting once with butter and sprinkling with pepper, salt, and flour.
7. Rub the gravy through the colander, thicken with browned flour and send to table in a boat.

title: Stuffed Mutton Chops
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- tablespoonful of butter
- tablespoonful of flour
- scant half-cupful of stock
- half a cupful of chopped almonds or mushrooms

## Directions

1. Make a white roux of a tablespoonful of butter and the same of flour.
2. When it has thickened well, stir in a scant half-cupful of stock; mix thoroughly until it bubbles; add half a cupful of chopped almonds, or, if you prefer, mushrooms, and season to taste.
3. Boil up once and let it get cold and stiff.
4. The chops should be tender, juicy, and cut twice as thick as for ordinary uses.
5. Split each horizontally clear to the bone, leaving that to hold it together, and fill the slit with the cold paste.
6. Close the sides upon it and quilt a wooden toothpick through the edges to hold them together, and broil slowly over clear coals, turning often for ten minutes.
7. Withdraw the skewers, and dish upon a bed of green peas.

title: Stuffed Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- chopped ham
- butter
- salt
- parsley
- milk

## Directions

1. Select 6 large potatoes
2. Wash and put in hot oven and bake until soft
3. Take out, remove the one end and with a teaspoon take out all the inside, being careful not to break the jacket
4. Mash thoroughly
5. Add 3/4 cup chopped ham (any other meat will do), a small piece of butter, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon chopped parsley and sufficient milk so you can cream it
6. Fill up jackets again, replace the end and put in oven for 10 or 15 minutes
7. Serve hot

title: Stuffed Potatoes (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- left-over mashed potatoes
- egg
- bread crumbs
- cream
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- sage
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Take left-over mashed potatoes, 1 egg, 1 cup bread crumbs, 1/2 cup cream, 1 onion chopped and season with pepper, salt and sage
2. Shape into a loaf and put where cool
3. Cut in slices, dip in flour and fry in a little butter

title: Stuffed Roast Ribs
body:
## Ingredients

- whole side of spare ribs
- stale bread
- salt
- pepper
- sage
- onion
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Take a whole side of spare ribs, hack down the centre, but do not separate the pieces, wash and thoroughly dry
2. Prepare a stuffing as you would for turkey, using about half a loaf of rather stale bread, slice and rub until finely crumbled
3. Season with salt, pepper and sage, also an onion cut fine
4. Rub on a tablespoon of butter and moisten the whole with a little cold water
5. Lay the stuffing in the hollow ribs next to the backbone, pack firmly, then turn over the other half
6. Take a cord, and beginning at one end wind smoothly around, covering all the stuffing
7. Tie firmly, put in pan with about cup of hot water
8. Roast in a rather hot oven for an hour and a half
9. When done take out of the pan, lay on a platter, remove the cord
10. For the gravy pour off some of the fat in the pan, add a tablespoon flour, rub smooth
11. Pour in boiling water till of the right consistency, stirring all the time
12. Let boil for a minute, season with salt and pepper, and serve with the roast

title: Stuffed Sweet Peppers
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet peppers
- force-meat of tongue, chicken, ham, or veal
- boiled rice
- salt
- onion-juice
- butter
- weak stock
- brown roux

## Directions

1. Make an incision in one side, and extract the seeds through this with a bit of stick
2. Stuff with a force-meat of tongue, chicken, ham, or veal, mixed up with boiled rice, and seasoned with salt, a dash of onion-juice, and a little butter
3. Sew up the peppers with a few stitches
4. Pack them into a bake-dish
5. Pour in enough weak stock to keep them from burning
6. Cover and bake in a moderate oven for an hour
7. Dish, withdrawing the strings
8. Keep hot while you add to the gravy in the dish a tablespoonful of brown roux
9. Boil up once and pour over the peppers
10. Should the gravy have boiled away too much, put in a little boiling water to thin the roux

title: Stuffed Sweet Peppers
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet peppers
- force-meat of tongue, chicken, ham, or veal
- boiled rice

## Directions

1. Make an incision in one side
2. Extract the seeds through this with a bit of stick
3. Stuff with a force-meat of tongue, chicken, ham, or veal, mixed up with boiled rice

title: Stuffed Tomatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- large firm tomatoes
- butter
- onion
- chopped meat
- parsley
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- bread
- water

## Directions

1. Select large, firm tomatoes; do not remove the skins; cut a small slice off the stem end, and scoop out the inside.
2. Put butter in a saucepan; when hot add onion chopped fine.
3. Let it color slightly; then add meat, chicken, or livers, parsley, breadcrumbs, the pulp taken from the tomatoes, salt, pepper, egg.
4. Stir it over the fire until it is consistent.
5. Dust the inside of the tomatoes with salt and pepper, and fill them, letting the stuffing rise half an inch above the tomato, and place a piece of butter on it.
6. Cut slices of bread one-half inch thick into circles the size of the tomato.
7. Dip them quickly in water, and place in a baking-pan.
8. Place a tomato on each piece of bread, and bake in oven about fifteen minutes, or until the stuffing is browned.
9. A brown sauce may be served with this dish.
10. The meat may be omitted from the stuffing if desired.
11. If convenient it is better to use oil instead of butter with tomatoes.

title: Stuffing for Fowl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups bread crumbs
- 1 cup onions finely chopped
- 1 tablespoon summer savory
- 1 tablespoon sage
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup butter
- 3 1/2 cup hot water

## Directions

1. Put butter in the frying pan and heat
2. Add the ingredients
3. When real hot add 3 1/2 cup hot water
4. Cover and let steam a few minutes
5. When it is ready to mash nicely, then it is ready for use

title: Stuffing For Fowls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cupful of breadcrumbs
- one tablespoonful of melted butter
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- chopped parsley
- onion-juice
- one minced onion
- one cupful of soaked bread
- one-half cupful of stock
- one teaspoonful of salt
- one-half teaspoonful of pepper
- one-half teaspoonful of thyme
- one-half cupful of celery

## Directions

1. Moisten a cupful of breadcrumbs with a tablespoonful of melted butter.
2. Season highly with salt, pepper, thyme, chopped parsley, and onion-juice.
3. Alternatively, put in a saucepan a tablespoonful of butter and fry in it one minced onion.
4. Add one cupful of soaked bread, the water being pressed out, one-half cupful of stock, one teaspoonful of salt, one-half teaspoonful each of pepper and thyme, and one-half cupful of celery cut into small pieces.
5. Stir it until it leaves the sides of the pan.

title: Sturgeon Cutlets
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sturgeon
- flour
- egg
- crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- parsley
- thyme
- Indian pickle
- tomato sauce or piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Cut in slices quarter of an inch thick
2. dry, flour, and egg them
3. dip in crumbs, seasoned with pepper, salt, parsley, and thyme
4. fry them
5. serve with Indian pickle, tomato, or piquant sauce

title: Succotash
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 ears of corn
- 1 pint string beans cut in pieces
- hot water, salted
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cut the corn from the cob, bruising as little as possible.
2. Put over the fire with the beans in enough hot water, salted, to cover them, and stew gently half an hour.
3. Turn off nearly all the water and add a cupful of milk.
4. Simmer in this, stirring to prevent burning, twenty minutes; add the floured butter, the pepper and salt, and stew ten minutes.
5. Serve in a deep dish.

title: Succotash
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 ears corn cut off the cob
- 3 cups Lima beans
- 1 pound chopped meat
- 1 onion cut up fine
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cook until done

title: Succotash (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- hot boiled corn from cob
- equal quantity of hot boiled shelled beans
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut hot boiled corn from cob
2. add an equal quantity of hot boiled shelled beans
3. season with butter and salt
4. re-heat before serving

title: Succotash (Version 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of beans
- 1 quart of green corn
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 large tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and boil beans for nearly an hour
2. add a quart of green corn, salt and pepper to taste, a large tablespoon butter, a tablespoon sugar
3. cook until done
4. Dried corn may be used by soaking for a while

title: Suet Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. chopped suet (fine)
- 1/2 cup seeded raisins (chopped)
- 3 tablespoonfuls moist sugar
- 1 teaspoonful baking powder
- 3 eggs (yolks of 3 and whites of 2 well beaten)
- 3 tablespoonfuls flour
- A little nutmeg
- A little salt

## Directions

1. Boil for four hours or steam in basin.

title: Suet Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup grated potato
- 1 cup grated carrot
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup suet
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon soda
- a little salt
- 2 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix grated potato, grated carrot, sugar, suet, raisins, currants, soda, salt, and flour
2. Steam for 3 hours

title: Suet Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup finely chopped suet
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 cup sour milk
- 2 teaspoons soda
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 3/4 teaspoon each cloves, cinnamon and nutmeg

## Directions

1. Mix finely chopped suet, molasses, sour milk, soda, chopped raisins, flour, cloves, cinnamon and nutmeg
2. Steam 2 hours

title: Suet Pudding 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chopped suet
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons molasses
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3/4 teaspoon each of cloves, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt
- 3/4 cup currants
- 3/4 cup seeded raisins
- flour to make middling stiff

## Directions

1. Mix chopped suet, sweet milk, sugar, molasses, soda, cloves, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, currants, and seeded raisins
2. Add flour to make middling stiff
3. Steam 3 hours or longer. Extra cooking does not injure

title: Suet Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup suet
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup sugar
- grated nutmeg to taste
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Bake in tart dishes in good rich crust

title: Sugar Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- ½ cup lard or butter
- ¾ cup sour milk
- ½ teaspoon soda
- just enough flour to roll
- any flavoring desired

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out thin
3. Bake quickly

title: Sugar Cookies No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- ¾ cup butter
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 6 tablespoons milk
- flour enough to roll easy
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out
3. Bake

title: Sugar Nut Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. sugar
- 1/4 lb. butter
- 1 pt. water
- 1 lb. hickory-nut meats
- 1/4 pt. vinegar

## Directions

1. Put the sugar, butter, vinegar and water together into a thick saucepan
2. When it begins to thicken, add the nuts
3. To test it, take up a very small quantity as quickly as possible directly from the centre, taking care not to disturb it any more than is necessary
4. Drop it into cold water, and remove from the fire the moment the little particles are brittle
5. Pour into buttered plates
6. Use any nuts with this recipe

title: Sugar Snaps
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 2 heaping cups brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients with flour to make quite stiff dough

title: Sugar Toffee
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds best brown sugar
- 1 pound butter
- water

## Directions

1. Add enough water to moisten sugar
2. Boil until crisp when dropped into cold water
3. Usually requires 0.75 hour boiling fast without stirring
4. Pour into pans or upon platters as thin as possible

title: Sugared Beets
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 hot boiled beets
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1½ teaspoons salt

## Directions

1. Cut into slices
2. Add sugar and salt
3. Reheat

title: Sultana Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound flour
- 6 ounces butter
- 1 cup sultanas
- small piece lemon peel
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sweet milk
- 6 ounces white sugar

## Directions

1. Beat butter and sugar together
2. Add eggs well beaten
3. Add the other ingredients
4. Bake in slow oven 1 1/2 or 2 hours

title: Sultana Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 3 eggs beaten separately
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 1/2 cups sifted flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups raisins
- 1 ounce peel

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake in slow oven

title: Sultana Cream Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sultanas
- 1 cup thick sour cream
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoon allspice
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons cracker crumbs

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Heat to thicken
3. Cool and put in shell
4. Make meringue

title: Summer Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh fruit (berries preferred)
- a little sugar
- slices of bread

## Directions

1. Boil some fresh fruit and a little sugar for 10 minutes
2. Line a wet bowl with slices of bread
3. Pour fruit into bowl
4. Cover with slices of bread
5. Place a weighted cover on bowl
6. Put to cool in a pan or basin of cold water or pack round with ice
7. Best eaten next day

title: Summer Squashes
body:
## Ingredients

- summer squashes
- butter
- pepper
- salt
- 1 tablespoon cream

## Directions

1. Cook squashes whole
2. When tender, if large, skin and remove the seeds; if small, this will not be necessary
3. Drain and press the water out with a plate
4. Put in a stewpan and season well with butter, pepper and salt
5. Add a tablespoon of cream

title: Superior Gingerbread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 cup molasses
- 2 cups sifted flour
- 3/4 tablespoon ginger
- 3/4 teaspoon soda
- water
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups raisins
- 1 cup chopped walnuts
- spice to taste

## Directions

1. Combine brown sugar and butter
2. Add sour milk and molasses
3. Add sifted flour
4. Add ginger
5. Dissolve soda in a little water
6. Add eggs
7. Add raisins and walnuts
8. Put soda in last, when well mixed

title: Surprise Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- cold mashed potatoes
- cold lean meat
- pepper
- salt
- gravy
- melted butter or milk

## Directions

1. From cold mashed potatoes make balls
2. With a teaspoon press a hollow in the top
3. Chop fine some cold lean meat and season with pepper and salt
4. Moisten meat with a little gravy
5. Put a spoon of meat in the hollow of potatoes with a little melted butter or milk
6. Place in the oven and brown

title: Surprise Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- vanilla extract
- lemon extract

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake

title: Surprise Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake

title: Surprise Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups white sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 3/4 cup water
- 1 cup flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat 3 eggs 1 minute
2. Add 1 1/2 cups white sugar and beat 5 minutes
3. Add 1 cup flour and beat 1 minute
4. Add 3/4 cup water and flavor to taste
5. Mix 1 cup flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder together and add
6. Bake in a cake pan
7. Ice with icing sugar moistened with milk and flavored to suit
8. Lay on shelled walnuts so you can cut in square pieces

title: Swedish Macaroons
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ounces shredded almonds
- 4 ounces ground almonds
- 4 ounces cornstarch
- 1 pound sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- grated rind of 2 oranges

## Directions

1. Pass sugar and cornstarch through sieve twice
2. Add almonds, rind and eggs beaten light, without separating
3. With buttered hands shape mixture into balls the size of walnuts
4. Bake in slow oven on tins covered with waxed or oiled paper

title: Sweet Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups sweet cream
- 1 cup milk
- a little salt
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup currants
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour

## Directions

1. Stir 4 cups sweet cream, 1 cup milk, a little salt, 1 cup sugar, 1 cup currants, 2 teaspoons baking powder, and 1 teaspoon soda with a spoon
2. Add flour enough to make a stiff dough
3. Roll out on board
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Sweet Chili Sauce With Celery
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pk. ripe tomatoes
- 4 bunches celery
- 3 cups vinegar
- 3 cups brown sugar
- ½ cup salt (small)
- ½ lb. whole mixed spice
- 1 teaspoonful pepper
- 1 teaspoonful mustard

## Directions

1. Peel tomatoes and slice
2. Chop celery fine
3. Add vinegar, brown sugar, salt, mixed spices tied in a bag, pepper and mustard
4. Boil all together one hour or longer

title: Sweet Corn Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ears of corn
- 1 small head cabbage
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 teaspoon tumeric
- 4 onions
- 2 tablespoons salt
- 2 red peppers
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 quart vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut grain from cob of corn
2. Add cabbage, sugar, tumeric, onions, salt, red peppers, mustard, flour, and vinegar
3. Chop all fine and cook 1 hour
4. Seal while hot

title: Sweet Cream Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sweet cream
- 2 cups flour
- 1 heaping teaspoon baking powder
- a pinch of salt
- flavor to taste
- 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup rich sweet cream

## Directions

1. Beat 2 eggs in cup and fill cup with sweet cream
2. Sift flour 3 times with baking powder, salt, and flavor
3. Mix together
4. For nut cake, add a cup of chopped nuts, or for loaf cake use 1 cup chopped raisins
5. For icing: boil 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar with 1/2 cup rich sweet cream till it hairs
6. Beat till cool, then add flavoring and spread between layers and on top

title: Sweet Cream Cake (Variation 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour for a thin batter

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake in a shallow dish in a moderate oven
3. Ice this cake with whipped cream, sugar and vanilla

title: Sweet Cream Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- sweet cream
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flavoring
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Break 2 eggs in cup, fill the cup with sweet cream and beat till light
2. Mix in 1 cup sugar
3. Add 1 3/4 cups flour
4. Add 2 teaspoons baking powder, flavoring and a pinch of salt

title: Sweet Dressing
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup water
- 3 eggs
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. boil sugar and water 5 minutes
2. beat whites until stiff
3. add yolks to whites, beat until mixed
4. add syrup gradually
5. add lemon juice
6. chill

title: Sweet Pickled Cucumbers
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds ripe cucumbers, peeled and seeded
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon allspice
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon black pepper
- 2 tablespoons salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and seed cucumbers
2. Combine with sugar, cloves, allspice, cinnamon, and black pepper
3. Add salt
4. Cover with vinegar
5. Boil together till tender

title: Sweet Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups vinegar
- 4 cups brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons whole cloves
- allspice and cinnamon
- 7 pounds fruit (melon, apples, large-sized cucumbers)

## Directions

1. Peel and seed cucumbers
2. Boil fruit until it can be pierced with a straw
3. Put in glass sealers
4. Boil syrup a few minutes
5. Pour over fruit while hot
6. Set away to cool
7. Seal tight

title: Sweet Potato Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups mashed sweet potatoes
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon cream
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Cool
3. Shape
4. Fry
5. Drain
6. Serve with sauce

title: Sweet Potato Custard Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. mashed sweet potato
- 1 teacup sweet milk
- Yolks of 4 eggs
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- A little nutmeg or cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cream sugar and butter together
2. Mix with potatoes, flavor with nutmeg or cinnamon
3. Add beaten yolks and milk
4. Beat the whites to a stiff froth and stir in
5. Pour in pie-pan lined with crust and bake quickly

title: Sweet Potato Puree
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- hot milk
- egg
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Mash thoroughly the boiled potatoes, and season them well with salt, pepper, and butter.
2. Add enough hot milk to moisten them.
3. Serve it the same as mashed white potato; or put it in a pudding-dish, brush the top with egg, and brown it in the oven.
4. Serve with it a tomato sauce, and use as a luncheon dish.
5. Either boiled or baked potatoes may be used.

title: Sweet Potato Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Three large sweet potatoes
- Two stalks celery
- French dressing
- Olives
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes and cut into squares; add the celery cut small.
2. Mix, and pour over French dressing.
3. Garnish with olives and parsley.

title: Sweet Potatoes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Wash and scrub the potatoes and put them in boiling water.
2. Cook until they can be pierced with a fork, then pour off the water.
3. Cover the pot with a cloth, and draw it to the side of the range to let the potatoes steam for ten minutes.
4. Peel them before serving.

title: Sweet Potatoes Au Gratin
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- 3 tablespoons brown sugar
- butter
- buttered crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut boiled potatoes in 1/4-inch slices
2. Put layer in baking dish
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and brown sugar
4. Dot with butter
5. Repeat layers
6. Cover with buttered crumbs
7. Bake until browned

title: Sweet Potatoes Mashed
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups hot riced potatoes
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- hot milk

## Directions

1. Beat potatoes, butter, and salt together
2. Add enough hot milk to make thin
3. Beat until light

title: Sweet Potatoes Pared and Baked
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- melted butter
- brown sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and pare potatoes
2. Parboil and drain
3. Brush with melted butter
4. Sprinkle with brown sugar
5. Bake in hot oven until browned

title: Sweet Tomato Pickles
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4.5 lbs. green tomatoes
- 4.5 lbs. apples, quartered
- 3 pts. vinegar
- 4 lbs. sugar
- 1 oz. allspice
- 1 oz. peppers
- stick cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Quarter apples and stick a few cloves (two) in each quarter.
2. Tie all the spices in muslin bags.
3. Let vinegar, sugar and spices come to a boil.
4. Add tomatoes and apples, and boil till tender.

title: Sweet Tomato Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pounds green tomatoes
- 4 pounds sugar
- 3 pints vinegar
- 1 ounce cloves
- 1 ounce cinnamon
- 1 ounce ginger
- salt

## Directions

1. Slice tomatoes and sprinkle a little salt over them, let stand over night
2. Put all ingredients together except tomatoes, let come to a boil
3. Add tomatoes and boil until tender

title: Sweet Tomato Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes
- 6 large onions
- salt
- 3 1/2 gallon vinegar
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon cloves
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 3 pounds sugar

## Directions

1. Slice green tomatoes and onions, sprinkle with salt and let stand over night
2. Drain
3. Cook in 3 1/2 gallon vinegar, 2 tablespoons cinnamon, 1 tablespoon mustard, 1 tablespoon cloves, 1 tablespoon ginger, 3 pounds sugar

title: Sweetbread Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- sweetbreads
- celery or cucumber
- salt and pepper
- oil
- lettuce leaves or cucumber shells

## Directions

1. Parboil sweetbreads twenty minutes
2. Drain and cool
3. Cut in one-half inch cubes
4. Mix with celery or cucumber cut in small pieces
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Moisten with oil
7. Arrange on lettuce leaves or serve in cucumber shells

title: Sweetbreads Breaded with Tomato Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweetbreads
- Eggs
- Flour
- Pepper and salt
- Cracker dust or dry breadcrumbs
- Hot boiling lard
- Tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare a batter of eggs, flour, and pepper and salt, beat well together
2. Turn the sweetbreads in this batter until they are all covered with it
3. Turn them afterwards in cracker dust or dry breadcrumbs
4. Fry in hot boiling lard to a fine light brown color
5. Serve with tomato sauce

title: Sweetbreads for Timbales
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pairs sweetbreads
- 1 can mushrooms
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1.5 tablespoonfuls flour
- 1 small onion
- 1 blade mace
- Salt and cayenne

## Directions

1. Boil sweetbreads twenty minutes in salted water, throw in cold water to harden, free from skin and cut in dice, cut mushrooms in dice.
2. Put onion and mace in milk till flavored, then take out, melt butter, add flour, then the milk, let boil a minute.
3. Add sweetbreads and mushrooms.

title: Swiss Cheese Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- porous, leathery Swiss cheese
- French mustard
- thin buttered bread

## Directions

1. Procure porous, leathery Swiss cheese and shave into thinnest slices
2. Spread with a little French mustard
3. Place between thin buttered bread
4. Cut a much larger size than others

title: Syrup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts potatoes
- 1 quart boiling water
- 3 pounds white sugar
- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 1/2 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Boil potatoes with skins on and drain
2. Add 1 quart boiling water, 3 pounds white sugar, 2 pounds brown sugar, 3/4 cup sweet milk, 1/2 teaspoon soda
3. Boil 1 hour

title: Syrup for Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup water
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice

## Directions

1. Let water and sugar come to boil and add lemon.

title: Syrup for Pancakes (Variant)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 cup nice yellow sugar
- 1 1/2 cups water

## Directions

1. Boil 5 minutes after dissolved.

title: Syrup For Pancakes 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 cup nice yellow sugar
- 1 1/2 cups water

## Directions

1. boil 5 minutes after dissolved

title: Syrup from Orange Peel
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- four sweet oranges
- three pints of cold water
- half a pound of loaf sugar

## Directions

1. Peel oranges, being careful not to get any of the white skin in
2. Put the yellow skin in cold water and loaf sugar
3. Cook together into a syrup

title: Syrup Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- a little water
- 1 egg
- a little butter
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Let sugar melt
2. Add 1 egg, a little butter and vanilla

title: Tait Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 tablespoons apple butter
- 1 small teaspoon allspice
- Butter size of an egg
- Bottom crust

## Directions

1. Bake with bottom crust
2. This makes 2 pies

title: Tapioca Cream Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup tapioca
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 eggs
- 3 pints milk
- vanilla

## Directions

1. At night wash 1 cup tapioca and put it into a small tin pail, with 3 pints good milk
2. Stir and set away in cold place until morning
3. Then place pail with mixture in a pot of boiling water
4. Boil until tapioca is clear, stirring several times
5. Add sugar and last of all the eggs, which have been well beaten
6. Stir the eggs and flavoring in well, but do not boil after adding them
7. Pour into serving dish
8. Serve cold with cream and sugar

title: Tapioca Cream Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons tapioca
- 1 cup water
- 1 pint sweet milk
- yolks of 2 eggs
- whites of 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar
- pinch salt
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Soak 4 tablespoons tapioca in 1 cup water over night
2. Add 1 pint sweet milk, yolks of 2 eggs, 3/4 cup sugar, pinch salt, and flavor with vanilla
3. Boil till thick
4. Heap beaten whites on top

title: Tapioca Fruit Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup tapioca
- one quart of water
- one cup of sugar
- rind and juice of one lemon
- box and a half of fresh fruit (red currants delicious)

## Directions

1. Steep tapioca over night in one quart of water
2. Drain off water in the morning and add one cup of sugar and the rind and juice of one lemon to tapioca
3. Put fruit in pudding-dish and add tapioca and mix
4. Bake in a slow oven for one hour
5. Serve cold

title: Tapioca Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart hot milk
- 2 heaping tablespoons tapioca
- a little salt
- yolks of 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- whites of 2 eggs
- any flavoring

## Directions

1. Cook in double boiler for 15 minutes, hot milk, tapioca, and salt, stirring often
2. Beat yolks of eggs and sugar
3. At the end of 15 minutes stir into milk and tapioca
4. Let cook till it thickens like custard
5. Pour into dish
6. Whip in the whites, beaten till no whites are seen
7. Add any flavoring
8. This is delicious if poured over, when cold, any fresh fruit such as strawberries, raspberries, peaches or oranges

title: Tapioca Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup tapioca
- a pint of water
- nearly a quart of milk
- beaten yolk of 4 eggs
- 1 small cup sugar
- salt
- vanilla
- whites of 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Soak tapioca over night in a pint of water
2. In the morning add nearly a quart of milk
3. Boil in double boiler until clear
4. Add the beaten yolk of 4 eggs, sugar, salt and vanilla
5. Pour into a dish
6. Beat the whites of 4 eggs
7. Season with sugar and vanilla
8. Spread on top and brown in the oven

title: Tart Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup currants
- 1 tablespoon butter
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Mix altogether
2. Put in tart shell
3. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Tart Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg beaten light
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook in the tart shells

title: Tart Filling
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup currants

## Directions

1. Fill tarts
2. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Tart Shells
body:
## Ingredients

- white of 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons granulated sugar
- 1 cup lard
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- 2 small teaspoons baking powder
- flour

## Directions

1. Put lard in dish and add sugar and mix together
2. Add the beaten white of egg and water
3. Sift baking powder with flour and mix stiff enough to roll
4. Cut with large biscuit cutter
5. Take 2 off sides and turn towards centre which has been dampened with a little water
6. Turn other 2 sides and press down in centre with finger
7. Bake in very hot oven
8. Fill with good jelly or jam when needed

title: Tarts
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- paste
- rice, dried peas, beans, cornmeal, or any dry material
- egg
- apricots or peaches
- blanched almond
- fruit juice or water
- arrowroot
- plums or cherries
- apples
- lemon juice
- sugar
- nutmeg
- cinnamon
- berries

## Directions

1. Roll the paste one-eighth of an inch thick, lay it on a deep pie-dish; let it shrink all it will, and use as little pressure as possible in fitting it to the tin.
2. Cut the paste an inch larger than the dish, and fold it under, giving a high twisted edge.
3. Prick the paste on the bottom in several places with a fork.
4. Lay over it a thin paper, and fill the tart with rice, dried peas, beans, cornmeal, or any dry material convenient.
5. Brush the edge with egg, and bake it in a moderate oven.
6. When done remove the rice, or other filling, and the paper.
7. Brush the bottom with white of egg.
8. If apricots or peaches are to be used, peel and cut them in halves, lay them evenly over the tart with the centre side up.
9. Place the half of a blanched almond in each one to represent the pit.
10. Put the juice of the fruit into a saucepan on the fire; if there is no juice use a cupful of water.
11. Sweeten to taste, and when it boils add to each cupful of juice one teaspoonful of arrowroot dissolved in a little cold water, and let it cook until clear.
12. Pour it around the fruit, but not over it, as the fruit should lie on top and show its form.
13. Place in the oven only long enough to cook the fruit tender.
14. If canned fruit is used, cook the juice and arrowroot until a little thickened and clear; then pour it around the fruit, and let cool.
15. When plums or cherries are used, remove the pits carefully, and place the fruit close together, with the whole side up.
16. For apple tarts, cut the apples in even quarters or eighths; stew them in sweetened water, with a little lemon juice added, until tender.
17. Lay them overlapping in even rows or circles in the tart.
18. To a cupful of water in which the apples were stewed add a teaspoonful of arrowroot, and cook until clear; pour it over the apples, sprinkle with sugar, nutmeg, and cinnamon.
19. With berries, the fruit may be stewed or not before being placed in the tart; then strips of paste are laid across it, like lattice-work, and the paste brushed with egg.
20. Bake long enough to cook the fruit and the strips of paste.
21. When cold place a fresh berry on each piece of crust where it crosses; or place a drop of meringue on the crusts, and the berries in the openings.

title: Tea
body:
## Ingredients

- tea
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Allow 1 teaspoon of tea to each person
2. Have the teapot hot and then put in the tea
3. Pour over this boiling water until steeper is a little more than half full
4. Cover tightly and let it stand where it will keep

title: Tea a la Russe
body:
## Ingredients

- tea
- boiling water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Make tea in the same way as regular tea
2. Serve with a slice of lemon either in cup and tea poured over it, or placed on saucer beside cup
3. Rind is always left on
4. Do not use cream in this tea

title: Tea Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 4 cups flour
- 1 tablespoon shortening
- 2 cups sweet milk

## Directions

1. Sift 1 teaspoon soda, 2 teaspoons cream tartar, with 4 cups flour
2. Rub in 1 tablespoon shortening
3. Add 2 cups sweet milk
4. Knead as little as possible
5. Bake in a hot oven

title: Tea Biscuits (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup cream
- sour milk

## Directions

1. Add 1 teaspoon soda, 2 teaspoons cream tartar, 1 teaspoon salt to 5 cups flour
2. Mix together well
3. Add 1 cup cream and enough sour milk to make a nice soft dough
4. Bake in a good hot oven

title: Tea Biscuits (3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups sifted flour
- 1.5 teaspoons baking powder
- a piece of butter the size of an egg
- 1.5 cups brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- a handful currants
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Sift 3 cups sifted flour with 1.5 teaspoons baking powder
2. Melt a piece of butter the size of an egg
3. Mix 1.5 cups brown sugar, 1 egg (not beaten), 1 cup sweet milk, a handful currants, a little grated nutmeg together
4. Stir into flour

title: Tea Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint flour
- two teaspoons baking powder
- one cup milk
- one egg
- three tablespoons sugar
- one large tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Bake in flat tins in quick oven.

title: Tea Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 cup lard
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake

title: Tea Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pt. flour
- 0.5 teaspoonful salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 2 tablespoonfuls sugar
- 1 generous tablespoonful shortening
- 1 large tablespoonful mashed potato
- 1 small cup raisins or currants

## Directions

1. Sift flour, baking powder, sugar and salt twice
2. cut in shortening
3. moisten with milk, adding potato and currants
4. Roll gently to thickness of one inch
5. cut in round cakes the size of small tea-plate
6. Bake twenty minutes in hot oven
7. cut open, butter and replace, cutting each cake in four
8. Serve hot

title: Tea Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1 teaspoon soda sifted
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup currants
- 1/2 orange peel cut fine
- sour milk

## Directions

1. Rub butter into flour with soda sifted in
2. Mix in sugar, currants, and orange peel
3. Add enough sour milk to make a soft dough
4. Roll to 1/2 inch thick
5. Cut any shape desired
6. Bake in a moderate oven for 20 minutes
7. Eat warm with maple syrup

title: Tea Cakes 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 1 tablespoon molasses if sugar is light
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1 small teaspoon soda
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients with flour enough to roll
2. Bake

title: Tea Cakes 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1/2 cup sugar
- a pinch of salt
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake in small tins

title: Tea Cakes or Cookies Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup thick sour cream
- 1 cup sugar
- teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon soda sifted into flour 3 times
- a little grated nutmeg or seasoning to taste
- cocoanut optional for sprinkling on top
- flour to handle nicely without sticking

## Directions

1. Mix sour cream and sugar together
2. Add salt and soda sifted with flour
3. Add nutmeg or other seasoning to taste
4. Add flour to handle nicely without sticking
5. Optional: sprinkle cocoanut on top and roll in just before cutting
6. Bake in a quick oven

title: Tea Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sour cream
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 quart flour

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake in a hot oven

title: Tea Cookies No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1¼ cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 teaspoon soda sifted with flour 3 times
- 4 eggs
- a little grated nutmeg
- flour enough to handle nicely without sticking

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake in quick oven

title: Tea Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts flour
- 1 large tablespoon lard
- 1 pint cold boiled milk
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1/2 White Swan Yeast Cake
- butter

## Directions

1. Into 2 quarts flour rub 1 large tablespoon lard
2. Make a hole in flour, pour in 1 pint cold boiled milk, 3/4 cup sugar, 1/2 White Swan Yeast Cake
3. Let rise over night
4. Knead in morning and let rise until noon
5. Knead and roll out
6. Cut with round cake cutter
7. Butter one half
8. Turn other half over on to it
9. Let rise until tea time
10. Bake 25 minutes in a quick oven

title: Tea Scones
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 egg
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- butter size of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar (optional)
- handful of currants (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix flour, soda, cream tartar, salt, and butter
2. Add milk and egg
3. Add sugar and currants if wished sweet
4. Bake

title: Tea Scones
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 egg
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- butter size of 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar (if wished sweet)
- handful of currants (if wished sweet)

## Directions

1. Mix flour, soda, and cream tartar
2. Add milk, egg, salt, and butter
3. Add sugar and currants if desired
4. Bake as scones

title: Tenderloins
body:
## Ingredients

- tenderloins
- butter
- water
- salt
- flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take tenderloins and put in a saucepan with 2 tablespoons butter
2. Keep turning till all sides are browned nicely
3. Cover with water and a pinch of salt and let boil till tender
4. Let the water boil down
5. Thicken with 2 teaspoons flour and a little water
6. Season with pepper to taste

title: The Begum's Curry
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- meat or chicken
- 1 tablespoonful curry powder
- 1 tablespoonful vinegar
- 0.5 tablespoonful Harvey sauce
- salt
- cocoanut juice
- ground rice
- butter
- onions
- 0.5 cup gravy

## Directions

1. Cut some meat into small dice, and put butter in frying-pan.
2. Fry the meat a nice brown.
3. While cooking, add all ingredients, which must be previously all mixed together: one tablespoon curry powder, one of vinegar, one-half of Harvey sauce, a little salt, the juice of a cocoanut, ground rice and butter.
4. Add half a cup of gravy.
5. Stir all together.
6. Let it cook a little while, and then turn the curry into a brown earthenware jar.
7. Fry some onions in butter, and add to the curry.
8. Let the jar stand on the hob and simmer until required.

title: Three-Decker Pies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 pint table syrup
- 1/2 pound currants
- 2 eggs
- 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Cut rind in very small pieces
2. Use good rich pastry
3. Divide the mixture in half
4. Line 3 plates with pastry
5. Put 1/2 of mixture in them
6. Cover with thin pastry without markings
7. Add remaining 1/2 of mixture and cover
8. Bake well in a moderate oven

title: Three-Hour Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 cup flour
- 3/4 cup white sugar
- 3/4 cup salt
- potato water
- 1 quart of mashed potatoes
- 3 quarts of boiling water
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake

## Directions

1. Scald flour, white sugar and salt with the water of the potatoes, add 1 quart of mashed potatoes and 3 quarts of boiling water and when cool enough add 1 White Swan Yeast Cake.
2. Set in a warm place to rise over night.
3. In the morning put this yeast into your flour (have the flour warm), mix stiff, let rise 1 hour, mix down, let rise 1 hour, then put in pans.
4. Let rise and bake.

title: Tiger Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ripe tomatoes
- 13 large sour apples
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 2 large cups onions chopped fine
- 1 small cup salt
- 1 teaspoon cayenne
- 1 1/4 teaspoons turmeric
- 4 tablespoons mustard
- 1 pint vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook tomatoes and apples separately
2. Put through colander
3. Add 1 pound brown sugar, 2 large cups onions chopped fine, 1 small cup salt, and 1 teaspoon cayenne
4. Add 1 1/4 teaspoons turmeric
5. Mix 4 tablespoons mustard with water and add
6. Add 1 pint vinegar

title: Tiger Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound raisins chopped fine
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1/2 pound salt
- 1 tablespoon ground red pepper
- 1 1/4 ounces ground mustard
- 7/8 ounce turmeric
- 2 cups onions chopped
- 13 large sour apples
- 12 large ripe tomatoes
- 3 pints white wine vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel apples and boil in 1 pint vinegar
2. Peel and boil tomatoes in another pint of vinegar
3. Put apples and tomatoes through a colander
4. When cool mix with 3/4 pound raisins chopped fine, 1 pound brown sugar, 1/2 pound salt, and 2 cups onions chopped
5. Mix mustard, turmeric and pepper with a little vinegar and add
6. Seal in jars

title: Tilden Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 3 cups flour
- 1/2 cup cornstarch
- 4 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 teaspoons lemon extract

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Bake

title: Timbale Batter
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour, measured after sifting
- 1/2 cup of milk
- 1 egg
- 1/2 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Add salt to flour, stir in milk by degrees, and egg beaten light, yolk and white together; strain and fry on timbale iron.
2. Fill with creamed lobsters, sweet breads, chicken, salmon, or other mixture.

title: Timbale de Volaille
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cooked chicken
- mushroom
- ham
- truffles
- white sauce
- macaroni, cooked

## Directions

1. Cut a cooked chicken into small pieces
2. Chop up mushroom, ham, and truffles, and stir into white sauce
3. Line a mould with pieces of macaroni, cooked, cut in even lengths
4. Fill in with the volaille
5. Steam one hour
6. Turn out very carefully
7. Serve with white or brown sauce

title: Timbales
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup flour
- 1 egg, well beaten
- lard for frying
- creamed chicken
- parsley or cress

## Directions

1. Beat all together; let stand one hour.
2. Have ready a saucepan of boiling lard.
3. Put timbale-iron first in lard, then in batter and then in lard again for one-half minute, when the shells will fall off the iron readily.
4. Fill with creamed chicken and garnish with parsley or cress after heating a few moments in the oven.
5. The shells can be made the day before if desired and heated when required.

title: To Boil a Turkey
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter
- cream
- oysters
- eggs

## Directions

1. Make a stuffing for the craw of chopped bread and butter, cream, oysters and the yolks of eggs
2. Sew it in and dredge flour over the turkey
3. Put it to boil in cold water, with a spoonful of salt and enough water to cover them well
4. Let it simmer for 2 hours, or, if small, less time
5. Skim it while boiling
6. It looks nicer if wrapped in a cloth dredged with flour
7. Serve it with drawn butter, in which put some oysters

title: To Boil Rice for Curry
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- rice, a cupful
- cold water
- water, two quarts
- salt, a teaspoonful

## Directions

1. Pick over the rice, a cupful.
2. Wash it thoroughly in two or three cold waters; then leave it about twenty minutes in cold water.
3. Put into a stewpan two quarts of water with a teaspoonful of salt in it, and when it boils, sprinkle in the rice.
4. Boil it briskly for twenty minutes, keeping the pan covered.
5. Take it from the fire, and drain off the water.
6. Afterwards set the saucepan on the back of the stove, with the lid off, to allow the rice to dry and the grains to separate.
7. Serve it hot in a separate dish or laid round the chicken curry.

title: To Can Berries
body:
## Ingredients

- berries (raspberries or blackberries)
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Fill jars with berries
2. Make a thin syrup and pour boiling over berries
3. Seal jars and place in the reservoir of hot water for 20 minutes or less
4. If water is boiling, take out and turn upside down to be sure that they are airtight

title: To Cook a Ham
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- water
- vinegar
- whole allspice
- cloves
- mustard
- bread crumbs
- sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the ham in cold water for 12 hours
2. Put it on to boil, allow 15 minutes for each pound
3. Pour the water off, fill the pot with fresh water (boiling)
4. Put in a pint of vinegar, a tablespoon of whole allspice, teaspoon cloves
5. Allow the ham to cook in this, 15 minutes for each pound
6. Let it remain in this liquor till cold, skim it
7. Make a paste of vinegar and mustard and cover the top with it
8. Put bread crumbs and a little sugar on, then return to oven for a short time

title: To Cook Liver
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- pork

## Directions

1. Put liver through meat chopper
2. Add pepper and salt
3. Chop a little pork and mix with liver
4. Flour so you can lift large spoonful at a time
5. Fry

title: To Cook Salt Pork
body:
## Ingredients

- salt pork
- buttermilk
- water
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Parboil in buttermilk and water
2. Dip in sweet cream and fry
3. This makes it like fresh meat

title: To Corn Beef
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound salt
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 1 ounce saltpetre
- water
- coarse salt
- beef

## Directions

1. Boil salt, brown sugar, and saltpetre with water
2. Skim and let cool
3. First sprinkle and rub beef with coarse salt
4. Let stand 24 hours
5. Wash off salt
6. Pack in tub or barrel
7. Pour pickle over meat
8. Will be ready to use in a week
9. When ready to cook, simply wash off and put on to cook

title: To Glaze a Ham or Tongue
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One ounce of glaze
- One tablespoonful of boiling water
- One quarter of a pound of butter

## Directions

1. Put one ounce of glaze and one tablespoonful of boiling water into saucepan over fire till melted
2. Brush it over ham or tongue; two coats if not dark enough
3. Beat one quarter of a pound of butter to a cream
4. Put into icing tubes and ornament with it

title: To Make Butter
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- thick cream
- ice or spring water
- cold water
- warm water
- salt

## Directions

1. Thoroughly scald the churn, then cool well with ice or spring water
2. Pour in the thick cream
3. Churn fast at first, then, as the butter forms, more slowly, always with perfect regularity
4. In warm weather, pour a little cold water into the churn, should the butter form slowly
5. In winter, if the cream is too cold, add a little warm water to bring to the proper temperature
6. When the butter has "come," rinse the sides of the churn down with cold water
7. Take the butter up with the perforated dasher or a wooden ladle, turning it dexterously just below the surface of the buttermilk to catch every stray bit
8. Plunge the dasher into very cold water in a deep wooden tray; the butter will float off, leaving the dasher free
9. Gather the butter behind a wooden butter-ladle, and drain off the water, squeezing and pressing the butter with the ladle
10. Pour on more cold water, and work the butter with the ladle to get the milk out
11. Drain off the water
12. Sprinkle salt over the butter—a tablespoonful to a pound
13. Work it in a little, and set in a cool place for an hour to harden
