14. Work and knead it until not another drop of water exudes, and the butter is perfectly smooth and close in texture and polish
15. With the ladle make up into rolls, little balls, stamped pats, etc.

title: To Make Butter Quickly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- cream
- wooden spoon
- cold water
- fine salt
- fine white sugar

## Directions

1. Immediately after the cow is milked, strain milk into clean pans, and set it over a moderate fire until it is scalding hot; do not let it boil
2. Set it aside; when it is cold, skim off the cream; the milk will still be fit for any ordinary use
3. When you have enough cream, put it into a clean earthen basin
4. Beat it with a wooden spoon until the butter is made, which will not be long
5. Take it from the milk and work it with a little cold water, until it is free from milk
6. Drain off the water, put a small tablespoonful of fine salt to each pound of butter, and work it in
7. Work in a small teaspoonful of fine white sugar with the salt
8. Make the butter in a roll; cover it with a bit of muslin, and keep it in a cool place

title: To Make Crabapple Jelly in Quantities
body:
## Ingredients

- crabapples
- cold water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Fill preserving kettle with crabs picked fresh from the tree
2. Pour in enough cold water to just come to the top of the apples
3. Set on the stove to boil until the apples are quite soft
4. Take a cup and dip the apples into the bag and then tie very tightly with a strong string
5. Carry the bag to the pantry or cellar where there is no dust or flies
6. Hang up the bag on a strong nail and leave to drip into the bowl all night
7. Next day boil the juice for about 20 minutes
8. Add the sugar, allowing one pound sugar to one pint juice
9. Boil until it will jelly when a little is cooled in a saucer
10. Pour into large-mouthed quart glass jars which have been heating in the oven

title: To Pickle Red Cabbage
body:
## Ingredients

- red cabbage
- salt
- sliced beet root
- vinegar
- mace
- bruised ginger
- whole pepper
- cloves

## Directions

1. Slice cabbage into a sieve
2. Sprinkle each layer with salt
3. Let the whole drain 3 days
4. Add some sliced beet root
5. Place the whole in a jar
6. Pour boiling vinegar over
7. Mace, bruised ginger, whole pepper and cloves may be boiled in the vinegar for improvement

title: To Roast Venison
body:
## Ingredients

- a saddle of venison
- vinegar
- red pepper
- salt
- strips of salt pork
- seasoned bread crumbs
- sweet marjoram
- sweet basil
- currant jelly
- 1 bottle of claret wine
- flour
- water
- butter
- 1 pound and a half of scraps of venison
- 3 pints of water
- cayenne pepper
- 1 pint currant jelly
- 1 pint claret
- 3/4 pound butter

## Directions

1. Wash a saddle of venison thoroughly in several waters
2. Rub it over with vinegar, red pepper and a little salt
3. Lard with strips of salt pork rolled in seasoned bread crumbs
4. Season if you like with sweet marjoram and sweet basil, 1 teaspoon each, also pepper
5. Rub the whole with currant jelly and pour over it 1 bottle of claret wine
6. Let it stand all night
7. Next morning cover venison with a paste made of flour and water 1 inch thick
8. Cover with a soft paper and secure well with strings
9. Place in dripping pan with claret, butter and water, and baste very often
10. Half an hour before taking it up remove paste and paper
11. Baste it with butter and dredge with flour to make it brown
12. For Sauce: Take a pound and a half of scraps of venison, with 3 pints of water, and salt and cayenne pepper to taste
13. Boil down to a pint and skim off the fat and strain
14. Add 1 pint of currant jelly, 1 pint claret, and 3/4 pound butter, divided into bits and rolled in flour

title: To Roast Wild Fowl
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- salt
- hot water
- salt pork
- butter
- flour
- bread
- lemon
- parsley
- currant jelly

## Directions

1. Put an onion, salt and hot water into a pan, and baste for 10 or 15 minutes
2. Change the pan, put in a slice of salt pork and baste with butter, and pork drippings very often
3. Just before serving dredge lightly with flour and baste
4. Ducks take from 25 to 35 minutes
5. Do not draw or take off the heads of either, garnish with fried or toasted bread, lemon, parsley and currant jelly

title: To Stew Birds
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crumbs
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- chopped salt pork
- bacon
- water
- sliced onions
- sweet herbs
- mace

## Directions

1. Wash and stuff them with bread crumbs seasoned with pepper, salt, butter or chopped salt pork, and fasten them tight
2. Line a stewpan with slices of bacon
3. Add a quart of water and a bit of butter the size of a goose egg, or else four slices of salt pork
4. Add, if you like, sliced onions, sweet herbs and mace
5. Stew till tender
6. Take them up and strain the gravy over them
7. Add boiling water if the liquor is too much reduced

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat scraps
- butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- pepper
- salt
- 1 small onion

## Directions

1. Cut all meat off the bones and chop fine
2. If fat put in frying-pan and let fry till brown; if not fat, add a little lump of butter
3. When brown add a tablespoon flour, pepper and salt to taste
4. Last, add 1 small onion
5. Cook for a few minutes

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of meat
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take pieces of meat and put through grinder
2. Cook for a few minutes with just enough water to wet nicely
3. Season well with salt and pepper
4. Press in bowl to slice when cold

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- roast leftover
- water
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- bread
- flour

## Directions

1. Take what is left from a roast and cut in small pieces
2. Put in a stewpan and cover with plenty of water
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Stew from 20 to 30 minutes
5. Flavor with onion cut in small pieces if desired
6. Toast a couple of slices of bread and cut in small squares and put on platter
7. Thicken the stew with flour and pour over toast
8. Serve hot

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 4)
body:
## Ingredients

- rich pie dough
- meat
- raw potatoes
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- water or gravy

## Directions

1. Line sides of pudding dish with rich pie dough
2. Cut meat in small pieces on bottom of dish
3. Add a layer of thinly sliced raw potatoes, a little onion, salt and pepper
4. Continue with a layer of meat and potatoes
5. End with meat and cover with a rich pie dough
6. Put a little water or gravy on so it will not burn
7. Bake 3/4 hour

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 5)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chopped meat
- 1 cup mashed potatoes
- 1 egg
- seasoning

## Directions

1. Take 1 cup chopped meat, 1 cup mashed potatoes, 1 egg well beaten, and a little seasoning
2. Make into patty cakes and fry

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 6)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onion
- meat cooked
- gravy or stewed tomatoes
- seasoning

## Directions

1. Put a small piece of butter in a frying pan and slice an onion in
2. When cooked add slices of meat cooked
3. Cover closely a few minutes until meat is thoroughly heated
4. If you have left over gravy, add it and season to taste; if not add stewed tomatoes
5. Let come to boil and serve hot

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 7)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold meat
- cold potatoes
- onion
- bread
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- biscuit crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop cold meat, a few cold potatoes, an onion and a little bread
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Roll in balls
4. Dip in well-beaten egg and biscuit crumbs
5. Fry in butter

title: To Use Up Odd Left-Overs (Method 8)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cold meat minced
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 1/2 tablespoon chopped parsley or sage
- 1 very small onion chopped fine
- pepper
- salt
- beaten eggs
- brown gravy or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 cup cold meat minced, 1 cup bread crumbs, 1 tablespoon butter, 1 1/2 tablespoon chopped parsley or sage, 1 very small onion chopped fine, pepper and salt to taste
2. Moisten with beaten eggs
3. Bake in hot oven 15 or 20 minutes
4. Serve with brown gravy or tomato sauce

title: To Warm Cold Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 egg
- salt and pepper to taste
- 2 cups finely chopped potatoes
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Stir milk, egg, salt, pepper, and potatoes together
2. Heat deep baking dish hot and butter well
3. Pour in the mixture
4. Sprinkle with flour and bits of butter
5. Bake in a hot oven until nicely browned

title: To Warm Cold Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- egg
- salt
- pepper
- potatoes
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix 3/4 cup milk, 1 egg well beaten, salt and pepper to taste, 2 cups fine chopped potatoes all together
2. Heat deep baking dish hot, and butter well
3. Pour in the mixture
4. Sprinkle with flour and bits of butter
5. Bake in a hot oven until nicely browned

title: Toad in the Hole
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound beef, mutton or sausage
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- 1 egg
- 1/4 pound flour
- 1/2 pint milk

## Directions

1. If beef or mutton is used, cut it into pieces about an inch square
2. If sausage is used, prick them and boil them 2 or 3 minutes and then remove the skin
3. Grease a pie dish
4. Put the meat or sausage at the bottom
5. Sprinkle seasoning over it
6. Make the batter as for pancakes
7. Pour it over the meat
8. Bake in hot oven for 3/4 hour

title: Toast and Tomatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slices of bread
- butter
- 1 can tomatoes
- 1 pint milk
- a lump of soda
- 0.5 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Toast and butter some thin slices of bread
2. Place in a deep dish
3. Take 1 can tomatoes, put in a porcelain dish and heat
4. Add 1 pint milk, a lump of soda, 0.5 teaspoon salt
5. When hot pour over toast
6. Serve at once

title: Toast Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- thin stale bread
- cream
- thin slices of buttered bread
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Toast very carefully thin stale bread
2. Spread with cream while hot
3. Place between thin slices of buttered bread
4. Some add a dash of nutmeg to the cream and sweeten it a very little
5. Cut in oblong fingers

title: Toast Water, or Crust Coffee
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- stale pieces of crust of bread
- the end pieces of the loaf
- boiling water
- ice
- cream and sugar

## Directions

1. Take stale pieces of crust of bread, the end pieces of the loaf
2. Toast them a nice, dark brown, care to be taken that they do not burn in the least, as that affects the flavor
3. Put the browned crusts into a large milk pitcher, and pour enough boiling water over to cover them
4. Cover the pitcher closely, and let steep until cold
5. Strain, and sweeten to taste
6. Put a piece of ice in each glass
7. This is also good, drank warm with cream and sugar, similar to coffee

title: Toasted Kidneys
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kidneys
- Thin slices of fat bacon
- Toast
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut each one of three kidneys into three pieces, and lay upon a very hot tin plate in front of a hot fire, where a clear glow will fall upon them
2. Have ready thin slices of fat bacon, hold each slice upon a fork close to the red grate so that the gravy will drip upon a slice of kidney below
3. Having toasted all the bacon, lay it upon a second hot plate, taking up the first and draining off every drop of gravy over the bacon
4. Now toast the kidneys over the bacon
5. When no more juice drips from each kidney it is done
6. Lay each in turn upon a slice of toast, in a hot dish, garnish with the pork, sprinkle with pepper and pour the gravy over the kidneys
7. Serve hot

title: Tomato Bisque Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. tomatoes
- 3 pts. milk
- 1 large tablespoonful of flour
- Butter size of an egg
- 1 scant teaspoonful of soda
- Pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Put tomatoes on to stew and the milk in a double boiler, reserving half a cup to mix the flour
2. Mix flour smoothly and stir in boiling milk and cook ten minutes
3. Add soda to tomato, stir well and strain, add butter, salt and pepper to milk and then the tomato

title: Tomato Bouillon
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can tomatoes
- 2 qts. stock
- 1 tablespoonful chopped onion
- 2 bay leaves
- 4 whole cloves
- 1 level teaspoonful celery seed
- Whites of 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Put tomatoes and stock over fire, add onion, bay leaves, cloves, celery and pepper
2. Cover and cook twenty minutes
3. Strain through a sieve
4. Beat whites of eggs till partly light, add these to tomato, bring to boil and boil rapidly five minutes
5. Strain through two thicknesses of cheesecloth
6. Re-heat, season with salt and serve with tiny cubes of toasted bread

title: Tomato Butter
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 lbs. tomatoes
- 1 qt. vinegar
- 3 lbs. brown sugar
- 4 large apples
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 1 teaspoonful cayenne
- 1 tablespoonful whole cloves
- 1 tablespoonful whole allspice
- 1 tablespoonful whole cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel and cut tomatoes, tie spices in muslin bag and boil three hours.

title: Tomato Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 pounds green tomatoes
- 3 pounds green apples
- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 1 quart vinegar
- 2 large onions
- 1 3/4 tablespoons salt
- 1 teaspoon red pepper
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon

## Directions

1. Chop fine and put all together and boil well

title: Tomato Butter
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 pounds ripe tomatoes peeled
- 3 pounds sugar
- 1 pint vinegar
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- 1 teaspoon allspice
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Boil all together for 3 hours

title: Tomato Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 gallon tomatoes
- 1 quart vinegar
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 4 tablespoons salt
- 3 tablespoons red peppers
- 3 tablespoons mustard
- 1 ounce whole cloves
- 1 ounce whole allspice
- 2 ounces ginger

## Directions

1. Boil 2 hours
2. Strain

title: Tomato Catsup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bu. tomatoes
- 6 onions
- 1 qt. vinegar
- 1 teacup salt
- 2 lbs. sugar
- 1 lb. whole black peppers
- 1 lb. whole allspice
- 1 oz. whole cloves
- 1 oz. whole cinnamon
- 1 teaspoonful cayenne

## Directions

1. Cut up tomatoes and onions and boil soft, strain, tie all spices in a bag and boil together three hours.
2. Bottle and cork while hot.

title: Tomato Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 bushel skinned tomatoes
- 1 quart good vinegar
- 3/4 pound salt
- 3/4 pound black pepper
- 1 ounce African cayenne
- 3/4 pound allspice
- 1 ounce cloves
- 3 boxes mustard
- 20 cloves garlic
- 6 good onions
- 2 pounds brown sugar
- 1 handful peach leaves

## Directions

1. Boil all ingredients for 3 hours
2. Constantly stir to keep from burning
3. When cool, strain through fine sieve
4. Bottle for future use
5. Will improve by age

title: Tomato Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 6 onions
- 3.5 ounce allspice
- 0.75 ounce cloves
- 0.75 ounce ginger
- 4 ounces salt
- 4 ounces mustard
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 2 or 3 red peppers
- 1 quart cider vinegar
- 1 ounce black pepper

## Directions

1. Boil tomatoes and onions together
2. Put through colander
3. Add allspice, cloves, ginger, salt, mustard, brown sugar, peppers, vinegar, and black pepper
4. Boil 1.5 hours

title: Tomato Chutney
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. ripe tomatoes
- 4 lbs. sour apples
- 2 lbs. raisins
- 2 lbs. brown sugar
- 4 small onions
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 8 oz. salt
- 3 oz. ginger
- 2 oz. allspice
- 1 oz. cayenne
- 1 nutmeg grated
- 2 qts. vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop tomatoes, apples, onions and raisins very fine; add everything else and boil one hour slowly; bottle when cold.

title: Tomato Chutney
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 pounds ripe tomatoes
- 3 pounds ripe apples
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1 pound raisins
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 ounce ginger
- 0.25 ounce cayenne pepper
- 1 ounce allspice
- 3 lemons
- quarts vinegar
- 1 large onion

## Directions

1. Boil tomatoes, apples, raisins and lemons together
2. Strain
3. Add vinegar and spice
4. Tie spice in muslin bags

title: Tomato Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 ripe tomatoes
- 12 sour apples
- 12 large onions
- 1 quart good vinegar
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- salt
- 1 ten cent can of mustard

## Directions

1. Bring tomatoes to a boil and then put through a sieve to take out seeds
2. Boil apples and onions till soft and put through sieve
3. Add all together
4. Add 1 quart good vinegar, 2 cups sugar, 1 teaspoon pepper, salt to taste, and 1 ten cent can of mustard
5. Dissolve mustard in a little cold vinegar before adding to the mixture
6. Boil till real thick, then bottle

title: Tomato Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 even-sized tomatoes
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon milk
- 1 ounce butter
- a little chopped parsley
- 6 rounds of buttered toast

## Directions

1. Cut tomatoes in halves through the centre, not from stem to top
2. Take out some of the pulp into a saucepan with 1 ounce butter, and cook lightly
3. Beat eggs and add to milk and put in saucepan with pulp and allow all to cook, but not set
4. Place the tomato shells in the oven and let them cook until tender
5. Arrange them on toast and place mixture in them
6. Sprinkle parsley over them and decorate the dish with parsley leaves

title: Tomato Hash
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sliced tomatoes
- cold meat
- bread and butter
- pepper
- salt
- beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Butter the dish well
2. Put in a layer of sliced tomatoes, a layer of cold meat sliced thin, a layer of bread and butter, and so on until the dish is full
3. Season well with pepper and salt
4. Pour beaten eggs over the top
5. Bake brown

title: Tomato Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can tomatoes
- 1 teaspoonful cinnamon
- 1 onion
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- Salt and pepper
- 1 tablespoonful gelatine

## Directions

1. Cook till tomatoes are soft, then strain
2. Dissolve one tablespoonful gelatine in half a cup of cold water
3. Pour the tomatoes over the gelatine while hot
4. Pour into moulds and cool
5. For sauce: One tablespoonful grated horseradish, four tablespoonfuls whipped cream, two tablespoonfuls of vinegar
6. Salt and cayenne to taste

title: Tomato Jelly
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can tomatoes
- 3 tablespoons of gelatine
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- 1 cup of boiling water
- pepper
- salt
- sugar
- 1 large cup of shelled walnuts

## Directions

1. Rub tomatoes through sieve
2. Soften 3 tablespoons of gelatine in 2 tablespoons cold water
3. Add 1 cup of boiling water and stir
4. When gelatine is dissolved, strain and mix with tomatoes
5. Season with pepper, salt and sugar to taste
6. Have ready 1 large cup of shelled walnuts
7. Mix into the mixture when it begins to thicken
8. Pour into mould

title: Tomato Marmalade
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds tomatoes
- 5 pounds granulated sugar
- 1 good-sized lemon
- 2 good-sized oranges

## Directions

1. Cut lemon and oranges very small
2. Boil tomatoes and fruits together until a little thick, and fruit is tender
3. Add sugar and just let boil up
4. Can

title: Tomato Mincemeat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes
- 4 pounds sugar
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 2 tablespoons cloves
- 2 tablespoons allspice
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 3/4 cups vinegar
- 2 pounds raisins
- 3 lemons

## Directions

1. Cut green tomatoes fine
2. Sprinkle tomatoes with salt, let stand over night, then drain
3. Cook all together
4. Add 2 pounds of raisins and 3 lemons
5. Use for pie or as a sauce with cold meats

title: Tomato Mustard
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pk. tomatoes
- 3 red peppers
- 5 onions
- 1 lb. salt
- 1 pt. vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful black pepper
- 1 oz. ginger
- 1 oz. cloves
- 1 oz. mace
- 1 lb. mustard

## Directions

1. Boil tomatoes with peppers and onions for an hour.
2. Then strain through a colander and add salt, black pepper, ginger, cloves, mace (ground).
3. Boil for one hour; when cold add mustard and vinegar.

title: Tomato Mustard
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pk. ripe tomatoes
- 6 red peppers
- 1/2 lb. salt
- 3 tablespoonfuls black pepper
- 1/2 pt. vinegar
- 1/2 lb. mustard
- 1 oz. ginger
- 1 oz. allspice
- 1 oz. mace
- 1 oz. cloves
- 2 onions
- a few cloves of garlic
- 1 tablespoonful red pepper

## Directions

1. Take out stalks of tomatoes and boil for one hour with red peppers.
2. Strain through a colander and add salt, black pepper, ginger, allspice, mace, cloves, and garlic, two onions.
3. Boil for one hour.
4. When cold add vinegar, red pepper, and mustard.

title: Tomato Mustard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 1/4 pound salt
- 1 pound cooking sugar
- 3.5 pound mustard
- 1 ounce whole black pepper
- 1 ounce allspice (whole)
- 1 ounce cloves (whole)
- 1 ounce ginger root pounded
- 1 onion chopped fine
- 1 teaspoon curry
- 1 quart vinegar

## Directions

1. Strain the tomatoes after boiling
2. Tie all the spices and the onion in a muslin cloth
3. Boil these together 1.5 hours
4. Rub mustard perfectly smooth in the vinegar
5. Add the vinegar with the mustard to the boiling mixture
6. Boil 20 minutes longer

title: Tomato Mustard (Alternative)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 apples
- 12 onions
- 12 tomatoes
- 1 pint vinegar
- 1 tablespoon mustard
- 3/4 cup salt
- 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoon cloves
- 2 cups sugar

## Directions

1. Boil apples, onions, and tomatoes together till soft
2. Put through a sieve
3. Add 1 pint vinegar, 1 tablespoon mustard, 3/4 cup salt, 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper, 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 1/4 teaspoon cloves, and 2 cups sugar
4. Set on stove
5. Heat up and bottle

title: Tomato Mustard Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quarts green tomatoes
- 2 quarts green cucumbers
- 1 quart onions
- 1 quart cauliflower
- 2 bunches celery
- 3 green peppers
- salt
- 1/2 gallon vinegar
- 4 tablespoons mustard
- 1 tablespoon tumeric
- 1 cup flour
- 4 cups white sugar

## Directions

1. Slice all very fine, sprinkle with salt; let stand over night
2. In the morning scald in the same water and then drain well
3. Boil 1/2 gallon vinegar, 4 tablespoons mustard, 1 tablespoon tumeric, 1 cup flour, 4 cups white sugar
4. Add the pickles; let all come to a boil, then seal airtight

title: Tomato Pilaff
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup stewed and strained tomato
- 1 minced onion
- 1 cup stock or gravy
- salt
- pepper
- 1 cupful of rice
- 1/2 cupful of butter

## Directions

1. Cook together one cup of stewed and strained tomato, one minced onion, one cup stock or gravy, with seasoning of salt and pepper.
2. When boiling add one cupful of rice and toss lightly until the liquor is absorbed.
3. Melt one-half cupful of butter, pour over the rice and set on back of stove to steam.
4. After about twenty minutes remove the cover, shake well, that the kernels may be distinct, and cover with a towel until ready to serve.

title: Tomato Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck ripe tomatoes
- 1/2 peck ripe apples
- 3 1/2 dozen lemons
- 2 ounces whole ginger
- 2 ounces cinnamon
- 1 pint good vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and stew the tomatoes with a pint of good vinegar, until the water is pretty well boiled out of them
2. Add a pound of sugar to each pint of the mixture and boil until thick

title: Tomato Puree
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. canned tomatoes
- 1 pt. stock or water
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 sprig parsley
- 1 stick celery
- 6 peppercorns
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 sliced onion
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- Salt
- Small croutons

## Directions

1. Simmer tomatoes, stock or water, bay leaf, parsley, celery, peppercorns and sugar together until the tomato is thoroughly soft
2. In another saucepan put a tablespoonful of butter; when it is hot add a sliced onion, and fry, but not brown it
3. Then add a tablespoonful of flour, and cook, but not brown the flour
4. To this roux add enough of the tomato to dilute it, and then mix it well with the rest of the tomato, and season with salt
5. Pass the whole through a fine sieve or strainer
6. Heat it again before serving, and sprinkle over the top small croutons

title: Tomato Relish
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 dozen large ripe tomatoes
- 3 large onions cut up fine
- salt to taste
- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- 1 tablespoon cinnamon
- 1 cup vinegar

## Directions

1. Boil 2 hours

title: Tomato Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. can tomatoes
- 2 bay leaves
- Juice 1 small onion
- A pinch of cayenne
- 1 teaspoonful celery salt or celery tops and salt
- 1 tablespoonful lemon juice
- 1 box gelatine

## Directions

1. Put tomatoes, bay leaves, salt, celery, onion and pepper in pan on stove
2. Stew gently for three hours
3. Soak gelatine in one cup of cold water for half an hour
4. Add it and one tablespoonful lemon juice to tomatoes, and strain into moulds
5. If tomatoes are very thick when opened, add a little water to them

title: Tomato Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup strained tomatoes
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- a little pepper

## Directions

1. Make the same as cream sauce, using 1 cup strained tomatoes instead of the milk

title: Tomato Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty pounds tomatoes (peeled)
- one quart vinegar
- three-quarters of a pound of salt
- one ounce cloves
- two ounces allspice (whole)
- one-half ounce cayenne pepper
- one pound sugar
- two ounces garlic (peeled)
- one ounce black pepper (whole)
- four or five pounds apples

## Directions

1. Boil five hours.

title: Tomato Sauce
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 lbs. ripe tomatoes
- 1 lb. onions
- 1 lb. apples
- 1 lb. raisins
- 2 lemons
- 1 lb. brown sugar
- 1 cup suet
- 1/2 oz. red pepper
- 1 qt. vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop all fine together.
2. Add sugar, suet, pepper and vinegar.
3. Boil two hours.

title: Tomato Sauce 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 can tomatoes
- 2 teaspoons sugar
- bit of bay-leaf
- 8 peppercorns
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 4 teaspoons flour
- 4 teaspoons butter
- 1 cup stock

## Directions

1. Cook first 5 ingredients together until tomatoes are soft
2. Strain and add stock
3. Melt butter, add flour, and when browned add liquid gradually

title: Tomato Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can tomatoes
- 1 qt. stock
- 1 gill cream or milk
- 1 oz. butter
- 1 oz. flour
- Pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Boil together the stock and tomatoes for fifteen minutes, then rub them through a sieve
2. Melt the butter in a saucepan, stir in flour and strained stock, boil two minutes
3. Allow the boil to go off, then add cream, and do not allow it to boil again or the cream will curdle

title: Tomato Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of peeled tomatoes
- A pinch of soda
- 1 quart of hot boiled milk
- Salt
- Pepper
- A small piece of butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls of rolled crackers

## Directions

1. Add a pinch of soda to one quart of peeled tomatoes, boil for one hour, or longer
2. Strain and return to the fire and add one quart of hot boiled milk
3. Season with salt, pepper, and a small piece of butter
4. Add three tablespoonfuls of rolled crackers and serve hot

title: Tomato Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of tomatoes
- 1 tablespoon of butter
- small onion
- pepper
- salt
- teaspoonful of sugar
- 3 teaspoons soft butter
- 3 teaspoons flour
- 1.5 pints of boiling milk

## Directions

1. Turn tomatoes into a saucepan
2. Add butter and chopped onion
3. Let boil 3/4 hour
4. Strain through a coarse sieve
5. Return to the saucepan
6. Season with pepper, salt and sugar
7. Rub soft butter with flour and add to the puree
8. Let cook about 3 minutes
9. Pour into the soup tureen
10. Add boiling milk
11. Stir well and serve hot

title: Tomato Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart stock
- 1 can tomatoes
- 1/2 teaspoon peppercorns
- 1 small bay leaf
- 3 cloves
- 1/2 each onion, celery, carrot and raw ham cut in dice
- Salt and pepper
- 4 tablespoons flour
- 4 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Cook vegetables and ham in butter 5 minutes
2. Add flour and seasonings, cook 3 minutes
3. Add tomatoes, cover and simmer 1/2 hour
4. Rub through strainer, add hot stock

title: Tomato Soup (Version 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 medium-sized ripe tomatoes
- butter the size of an egg
- pinch of soda
- 1 quart sweet milk
- 1/2 cup sweet cream
- pepper
- salt
- broken biscuits

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter tomatoes
2. Put butter in a soup kettle and let it get hot
3. Add tomatoes and let boil till tender
4. When tender add a pinch of soda to prevent curdling
5. Add sweet milk and sweet cream
6. Add pepper and salt to taste
7. Add enough broken biscuits to thicken nicely
8. Let boil 3 minutes and serve hot

title: Tomato Soup (Version 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of tomatoes
- 1 quart water
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 quart milk
- small piece of butter
- few rolled biscuits
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Bring tomatoes and water to a boil
2. Add soda
3. Add milk and butter
4. Add rolled biscuits
5. Add pepper and salt to taste

title: Tomatoes and Cheese
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tin of tomatoes
- 1 cup of grated cheese
- dash of cayenne pepper
- salt
- lump of butter
- 4 or 5 soda biscuits

## Directions

1. Crumb four or five soda biscuits.
2. Mix one tin of tomatoes, one cup of grated cheese, dash of cayenne pepper and salt in cheese, lump of butter on top, and crumbed soda biscuits.
3. Bake in a pudding-dish in oven.

title: Tomatoes au Gratin
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. tomatoes
- 1 cup breadcrumbs
- 1 small onion chopped
- 1 teaspoonful sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls melted butter
- cayenne
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut a piece from the top of each tomato.
2. Scoop out the inside, leaving a hollow shell.
3. Chop the pulp fine, mix with the crumbs, butter, sugar, pepper, salt, and onion.
4. Fill the cavities of the tomatoes with this stuffing, heaping and rounding each.
5. Scatter fine crumbs on the top, and arrange in a bake-dish.
6. Set the dish, covered, in an oven, and bake half an hour before uncovering.
7. After which brown lightly, and send to table on a hot platter.

title: Tomatoes For Breakfast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can tomatoes
- pepper
- salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 cup sweet cream

## Directions

1. Put tomatoes through colander
2. Bring to a boil on the stove
3. Season with pepper, salt, and sugar
4. Mix flour with half a cup of sweet cream
5. Stir it in with the tomatoes while boiling
6. Serve hot

title: Tomatoes on Toast
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- firm tomatoes
- bread
- pepper
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Wash and slice in half-inch rounds good firm tomatoes.
2. Place a layer of tomatoes on slices of bread half-inch thick.
3. Sprinkle with pepper and salt, and on each round of tomato put a small piece of butter.
4. Bake in shallow meat-pan in rather quick oven until bread turns golden brown.
5. Serve at once.

title: Tongue Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup finely chopped tongue
- 1/4 cup chopped boiled ham
- 3 tablespoonfuls melted butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls salad oil
- 1/4 teaspoonful made mustard
- 1/4 teaspoonful paprica
- Yolk of 1 egg
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Mix tongue with ham
2. Stir in melted butter beaten light with salad oil, mustard, and paprica
3. When the mixture is smooth and light set in a saucepan of boiling water over the fire and cook until it is thoroughly heated
4. Beat in the yolk of a whipped egg
5. Take from the fire and set by until perfectly cold
6. Spread between thin slices of bread

title: Tonic
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of rye whisky
- one ounce fluid extract of celery
- two ounces fluid extract of hops
- half ounce fluid extract of rhubarb
- four quarts of cold water

## Directions

1. Mix well, and bottle for use
2. One tablespoonful before each meal and at bedtime

title: Toothsome Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 12 tablespoons milk
- 3 tablespoons baking powder
- 2 quarts flour

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll thin
3. Bake quick to a light brown

title: Toronto Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups white sugar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 cup sour milk or buttermilk
- 1/2 cup baking syrup
- 1 teaspoon ground cloves
- a little nutmeg and allspice
- flour

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add eggs
3. Mix soda with sour milk or buttermilk
4. Add soda mixture and syrup to batter
5. Add cloves, nutmeg, and allspice
6. Add flour to reach desired consistency, not too stiff
7. Bake in layers in moderate oven
8. Fill with date filling

title: Toronto Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 2 eggs (save white of one)
- 1/2 cup syrup
- 1/2 cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon spice

## Directions

1. Mix butter and brown sugar
2. Add eggs and syrup
3. Add buttermilk with soda dissolved in it
4. Add flour and spice
5. Bake in jelly tins
6. Cool and frost with icing

title: Toronto Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 4 apples
- 1/4 pound bread crumbs
- 4 ounces sugar
- 3 ounces currants
- salt and grated nutmeg to taste
- rind of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Pare, core and mince the apples into small pieces
2. Mix them with the other dry ingredients
3. Beat up the eggs
4. Moisten the mixture with these
5. Beat it well
6. Put the pudding into a buttered mould
7. Tie it down with a cloth
8. Boil for 1 hour and a half
9. Serve with sweet sauce

title: Traveller's Sandwiches
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines
- ham
- pickles
- mustard
- pepper
- catsup
- Worcestershire sauce
- salt
- vinegar
- buttered bread

## Directions

1. Chop sardines, ham and a few pickles finely together
2. Season with mustard, pepper, catsup, Worcestershire sauce, salt, vinegar, etc., to taste
3. Spread between nicely buttered bread

title: Treacle Sponge
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Six ounces bread crumbs
- four ounces suet (a little salt)
- one egg
- one tablespoonful baking powder
- two tablespoonfuls golden syrup

## Directions

1. Steam
2. when steamed, pour syrup over as sauce

title: Trifle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls sherry wine
- 4 tablespoonfuls brandy
- strawberry or raspberry jam
- 1 qt. cream
- 1/2 lb. macaroons
- 1 doz. lady fingers
- 1 teaspoonful vanilla
- 1/2 teacup seeded raisins
- 1/2 cupful chopped, blanched almonds
- any kind of stale cake, sponge or pound cake preferred

## Directions

1. In a dish put first a layer of cake, then a little brandy, then jam, then cake, wine, raisins and nuts, also a little cream whipped.
2. Begin again as before, then add a few macaroons and make a wall of lady fingers.
3. After all the cake, wine and raisins are used, over the top put plenty of whipped cream (stiff).

title: Trifle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Sponge cake
- Sherry wine
- Chopped figs
- One pint of almond custard
- Large cup of strawberry jam
- One pint of cream, whipped

## Directions

1. Soak sponge cake in sherry wine
2. Layer with chopped figs and almond custard
3. Top with strawberry jam
4. Top with one pint of cream, whipped

title: Trilbys
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 2 cups flour
- 2 cups oatmeal
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar

## Directions

1. Mix butter and sugar
2. Add sour milk
3. Mix flour, oatmeal, soda, and cream tartar
4. Add flour enough to roll out
5. Bake as cookies
6. When cool spread date filling

title: Trilbys 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups yellow sugar
- 1 cup butter and lard mixed
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 2 cups oatmeal
- 2 cups flour
- 2 eggs (if you have plenty)
- 1 teaspoon soda
- little salt

## Directions

1. Mix butter and lard with sugar
2. Add buttermilk
3. Mix oatmeal, flour, soda, and salt
4. Add eggs if desired
5. Combine all ingredients
6. Bake as cookies
7. Spread filling when cool

title: Tripe Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Tripe
- Water
- A little vinegar
- 10 eggs
- Salt
- Lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Cleanse the tripe thoroughly, slice in small pieces and plunge in boiling water
2. Remove carefully, wash again in hot water, and if there are any spots black or red left, scrape with a knife
3. Put in a sauce-pan with plenty of water and a little vinegar and boil until tender
4. Break ten eggs in another dish, add salt and lemon-juice, beat thoroughly and stir into it a little of the boiling broth until danger of curdling is past
5. Then add to the tripe and water, cook a moment longer and serve

title: Tumeric Pickles
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quarts large green cucumbers peeled and sliced
- brine
- vinegar
- water
- 4 large onions sliced
- 2 tablespoons mustard
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 teaspoon tumeric
- 1 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Slice cucumbers about 1 inch thick
2. Soak in brine over night
3. Drain and scald well in about equal parts of vinegar and water until they look a little cooked, then drain
4. Put on clear vinegar, add onions sliced, cook until tender
5. Stir up mustard, flour, tumeric, and sugar in a little water
6. Pour in the boiling pickles, stirring constantly until it thickens
7. Seal while hot

title: Turbot
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one large whitefish
- salt
- pepper
- one pint milk
- two tablespoonfuls flour
- two eggs
- one-fourth pound butter
- onion-juice
- bread-crumbs

## Directions

1. Steam till tender one large whitefish
2. remove bones and sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. heat one pint of milk thickened with two tablespoonfuls of flour
4. when cold add two eggs and one-fourth of a pound of butter
5. put into a baking-dish a layer of the fish and a layer of dressing
6. season with one-half teaspoonful of onion-juice
7. cover top with bread-crumbs, and bake one-half hour

title: Turkey Hashed
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey remnants
- turkey bones
- water
- turkey gravy
- white stock
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut the remnants of turkey from a previous dinner into pieces of equal size
2. Boil the bones in a quart of water, until the quart is reduced to a pint
3. Take out the bones, and to the liquor in which they were boiled add turkey gravy, or white stock, or a small piece of butter with salt and pepper
4. Let the liquor thus prepared boil up once
5. Put in the pieces of turkey, dredge in a little flour, give it one

title: Turkey Hashed
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- remnants of turkey from a previous dinner
- bones
- a quart of water
- turkey gravy or white stock or a small piece of butter
- salt
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut the remnants of turkey from a previous dinner into pieces of equal size.
2. Boil the bones in a quart of water, until the quart is reduced to a pint.
3. Take out the bones, and to the liquor in which they were boiled add turkey gravy, or white stock, or a small piece of butter with salt and pepper.
4. Let the liquor thus prepared boil up once.
5. Put in the pieces of turkey, dredge in a little flour, give it one boil-up, and serve in a hot dish.

title: Turkey Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey bones
- Water enough to cover them
- A little dressing
- 1 beaten egg
- A little butter
- Pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cook the turkey bones for 1 hour in water enough to cover them
2. Stir in a little dressing and a beaten egg
3. Take from the fire, and when the water has ceased boiling add a little butter with pepper and salt

title: Turkey Warmed Over
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of cold turkey or chicken
- butter
- small thick slices of bread or biscuit
- hot salted water
- a large spoonful of butter
- one or two cupfuls of milk
- any gravy that may be left over
- flour
- cold milk or water
- salt
- pepper
- dark meat

## Directions

1. Warm up pieces of cold turkey or chicken with a little butter in a frying-pan.
2. Place it on a warm platter and surround it with small thick slices of bread or biscuit halved, first dipping them in hot salted water.
3. Place the platter in a warm oven with the door open.
4. Into the frying-pan put a large spoonful of butter, one or two cupfuls of milk, and any gravy that may be left over.
5. Bring it to a boil.
6. Add sufficient flour, wet in a little cold milk or water, to make it the consistency of cream.
7. Season with salt, pepper and add a little of the dark meat chopped very fine.
8. Let the sauce cook a few moments.
9. Pour over the biscuit and fowl.

title: Turkish Delight
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 oz. gelatine
- 1 lb. granulated sugar
- 1 cup cold water
- juice of 1 lemon
- juice of 1 orange
- 1 tablespoonful of rum

## Directions

1. Soak 1 oz. gelatine in 1/2 cup of cold water for two hours
2. Put 1 pound of granulated sugar in a pan with 1/2 cup of cold water
3. Stand the pan over the fire until the sugar is melted and comes to the boil
4. Add soaked gelatine and boil steadily for twenty minutes
5. Flavor with the juice of one lemon and one orange and a tablespoonful of rum
6. Wet a tin in cold water and turn the mixture in, having it about one inch thick
7. When it is hard or jellied, spread icing sugar over the top and cut into inch square pieces
8. Roll in the icing sugar
9. Let the mixture stand in the pan over night before cutting, as it is very sticky

title: Turkish Pilaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup rice
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 1/2 cups stewed tomatoes
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and drain rice
2. Cook in butter until brown
3. Add water and steam until absorbed
4. Add tomatoes
5. Cook until soft
6. Season

title: Turkish Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 qt. white stock
- 1 gill cream
- Yolks of two eggs
- 1/4 teaspoonful pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls sago or round tapioca

## Directions

1. When stock is boiling put in the sago (soaked for half an hour)
2. Beat yolks with cream
3. When sago boils for ten minutes, add a cup of soup gradually to cream and eggs, stirring all the time
4. Put back on the stove and cook a few minutes, stirring all the time

title: Turkish Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cups stock
- 3/4 cup rice
- 1 1/2 cups strained tomatoes
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 slices onion
- 1 ounce peppercorns
- 3/4 teaspoon celery salt
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Add seasonings to tomatoes, cover, simmer 30 minutes
2. Rub through sieve into hot stock
3. Brush with butter and flour
4. Add cooked rice

title: Turnip and Meat Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint left-over meat (pork preferred)
- 1 quart small, thinly cut slices of turnip
- water
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- a pinch of salt
- flour
- fat pork (uncooked)

## Directions

1. Cut meat fine
2. Put meat and turnip slices in a large-size pudding dish or baking tin
3. Pour over sufficient water to nicely cook it, about 20 minutes
4. Season to taste
5. Make a batter consisting of 1 cup buttermilk, 1 teaspoon soda, a pinch of salt and enough flour for batter
6. Spread over turnip and meat
7. Sprinkle over batter real small pieces of fat pork, uncooked
8. Bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Turnip Fritters
body:
## Ingredients

- beaten egg
- mashed turnips
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Add beaten egg to mashed turnips
2. Shape into flat cakes
3. Roll in flour
4. Saute in butter

title: Turnips a la Creme
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young turnips
- cream
- capers
- toast or fried bread

## Directions

1. Cook some young turnips in the usual manner and mash them with plenty of cream.
2. Serve in small portions on slices of toast or fried bread with a few capers spread over them.

title: Tutti Frutti Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- whites of 5 eggs
- 4 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup nuts
- 1/2 cup chopped raisins
- 1/2 cup grated cocoanut
- grated rind of a lemon

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Beat egg whites to a stiff froth
3. Divide batter into four parts
4. Leave one part plain
5. Add 1 cup nuts to one part
6. Add 1/2 cup chopped raisins to another part
7. Add 1/2 cup grated cocoanut and grated rind of a lemon to the fourth part
8. Bake all parts
9. Put together with white icing, placing the layers in any order you prefer

title: Twin Mountain Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cup butter
- 3/4 cup granulated sugar
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1 egg well beaten
- 3/4 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 4 level teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Bake in buttered tins in quick oven

title: United States Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoon cloves
- 1/2 nutmeg
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Bake in long tin
2. when done ice with chocolate and sprinkle with chopped walnuts cut in squares

title: Vanilla Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 tablespoons sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- flour to roll

## Directions

1. Bake in quick oven

title: Vanilla Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients

title: Vanilla Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 2 cups flour
- teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Bake

title: Vanilla Wafers
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 egg
- 4 tablespoonfuls milk
- 4 tablespoonfuls vanilla
- 1.5 teaspoonful cream tartar
- 0.5 teaspoonful soda
- Flour to make stiff enough to roll

## Directions

1. Cut in pieces three inches by one inch.

title: Vanilla Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 egg
- 4 tablespoons sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon vanilla
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar
2. add the egg well beaten, then the milk and vanilla
3. add the flour
4. Bake in quick oven

title: Variety Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts green tomatoes
- 4 quarts cabbage
- 1 quart onions
- 4 green peppers
- 1 ounce celery seed
- 1 ounce tumeric powder
- 2 tablespoons cinnamon
- 2 tablespoons cloves
- 2 tablespoons allspice
- 2 tablespoons salt
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1 gallon vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop green tomatoes, cabbage, onions, and green peppers fine
2. Add celery seed, tumeric powder, cinnamon, cloves, allspice, salt, sugar, and vinegar
3. Cook until tender

title: Veal Loaf
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds raw veal
- 0.25 pound raw or cooked bacon or ham
- pepper
- salt
- nutmeg
- savory or other herbs
- 0.5 cup of breadcrumbs
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons good stock
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Mince veal and bacon or ham thoroughly
2. Season with pepper and very little salt, as the bacon or ham salts it
3. Add a little nutmeg, savory or other herbs
4. Add 0.5 cup of breadcrumbs and two eggs well beaten, keeping out enough to brush over the top
5. Add 2 tablespoons good stock
6. Mix thoroughly and press into a square pan to shape it
7. Turn out and brush over with the beaten egg
8. Bake 1.5 hours in a slow oven, basting occasionally with a teaspoonful of butter melted in 0.5 cup of water
9. Serve with brown gravy thickened slightly

title: Veal Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds veal fat and lean
- 1 slice salt pork
- 3 common crackers
- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup butter
- 2 small teaspoons pepper
- pinch of cloves
- herbs
- water
- flour

## Directions

1. Chop the veal and salt pork together raw.
2. Pound the crackers fine and mix with egg, butter, pepper, cloves, and any herb to suit taste.
3. Mix it well together and make into a loaf.
4. Put in a shallow baking pan with a little water, cover with bits of butter, and sift flour over it.
5. Bake slowly for 1.5 hours.

title: Veal or Chicken Chevreux
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- carrot
- peas
- beet
- meat
- warm aspic jelly

## Directions

1. Garnish small moulds with carrot, peas and beet, cooked and chopped with fancy cutter
2. Cut meat in small squares
3. Pack in moulds and fill with warm aspic jelly

title: Veal Or Mutton Broth
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- scrag-end of mutton (two pounds)
- two quarts of cold water
- an ounce of pearl barley or rice
- half a teaspoonful of salt
- one turnip
- one carrot
- one onion

## Directions

1. Put scrag-end of mutton in a saucepan, with two quarts of cold water, and an ounce of pearl barley or rice
2. When it is coming to a boil, skim it well
3. Then add half a teaspoonful of salt
4. Let it boil until half reduced, then strain it, and take off all the fat
5. If vegetables are liked in this broth, cut turnip, carrot, and onion in shreds, and boil them in the broth half an hour
6. The barley may be served with the vegetables in broth

title: Veal Patty
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of veal or beef chopped fine
- 5 tablespoonfuls of cracker-crumbs
- 1 tablespoonful of melted butter
- 1 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1/4 cup milk or cream
- 2 eggs, beaten

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Form into a loaf and cover with cracker-crumbs
3. Put in the oven with a little water in the pan for two hours
4. Baste occasionally

title: Veal Patty (or Beef)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. raw leg of veal
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 8 tablespoonfuls of rolled crackers
- 3 tablespoonfuls cream
- butter size of egg
- pepper
- water
- cracker crumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. Chop veal very fine
2. Mould into a loaf
3. Put into pan with a little water
4. Sprinkle with cracker crumbs and small bits of butter on the top
5. An egg may be added
6. Bake two hours and eat cold

title: Veal Shape
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ lbs. veal
- Pepper and salt
- 1 lemon
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 slice of ham

## Directions

1. Stew the meat, with the thin rind of the lemon, in a very little water till quite tender
2. When done, cut up both veal and ham into small pieces, mince the lemon rind finely, and set these aside to cool
3. Strain the stock, add the lemon-juice and seasonings, and let this also cool
4. Cut the eggs into slices, and arrange them in a plain mould or dish, pour in the cool stock and meat, and set aside till quite cold, when it should turn out whole

title: Veal Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- A knuckle of veal
- Three quarts of water
- A little salt
- One tablespoonful of rice
- Yolk of one egg
- One cup of cream
- A piece of butter the size of a hickory nut

## Directions

1. Put a knuckle of veal into three quarts of water, a little salt and one tablespoonful of rice
2. Boil slowly, hardly above simmering, until liquor is reduced one-half
3. Remove from the fire
4. Into a dish put the yolk of one egg; stir well into it a cup of cream; add a piece of butter the size of a hickory nut
5. Into this strain the soup boiling hot, stirring all the time
6. Just at last beat well for one minute

title: Vegetable Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup apples
- 1 cup potatoes grated
- 1 cup carrots grated
- 1 cup suet
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 1 cup flour
- 2 cups raisins
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Steam 3 hours

title: Vegetable Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup grated carrots
- 1 cup grated potatoes
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup suet
- a little salt
- 1 dessertspoon soda

## Directions

1. Steam 1 1/2 hours

title: Vegetable Pudding 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup grated carrots
- 1 cup grated potatoes
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup currants
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup suet
- a little salt
- 1 dessertspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix grated carrots, grated potatoes, sugar, flour, currants, raisins, suet, salt, and soda
2. Steam 1 1/2 hours

title: Vegetable Puree
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- carrots
- potatoes (boiled first)
- beans of any kind
- parsnips
- celery
- peas
- leek
- turnips
- cauliflower
- 1 teacup dripping or stock-fat
- 1 large spoonful flour
- 1 pint hot water
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut up a large plateful of any and all kinds of vegetables, having potatoes or beans for thickening
2. Put teacup of dripping or stock-fat into saucepan and when very hot add sliced onions
3. Stir well to prevent burning, and when onions are red stir in large spoonful of flour till it is the same color
4. Stir in pint of hot water and some pepper and salt
5. Add the rest of the vegetables and let them simmer, adding more hot water as necessary, for two hours
6. Press them through a colander
7. Return them to the range and let them simmer till the moment of serving

title: Vegetable Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 boiled beets
- 1 cup celery
- 1 parsnip
- 1 carrot
- salt
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Chop the beets fine
2. Chop the celery fine
3. Boil and chop fine the parsnip and carrot
4. Boil another parsnip and carrot and cut oblong
5. Lay the oblong pieces alternately for garnishing
6. Season with salt and whipped cream for dressing
7. Cool and serve at once

title: Vegetable Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup vegetable water
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- sprinkle pepper

## Directions

1. Melt butter, add flour and stir over the fire until frothy
2. Add vegetable water and stir constantly until it thickens and boils a minute
3. Pour over the vegetable, allowing 1 cup of sauce to 2 cups of vegetable pulp

title: Vegetable Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart common stock
- 1 pint parboiled mixed vegetables cut into small dice
- salt
- pepper
- 1 teaspoonful sugar

## Directions

1. Add parboiled mixed vegetables to stock
2. Simmer until vegetables are tender but not pasty
3. Season with salt, pepper, and sugar
4. Serve without straining

title: Vegetable Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two potatoes
- Hot water
- Two tomatoes
- Two ears of corn
- One slice of onion
- Two quarts of cold water
- A level tablespoonful butter
- One teaspoon salt
- One-half teaspoon pepper
- One of minced parsley
- One teaspoonful of flour

## Directions

1. Peel and slice two potatoes, parboil them in enough hot water to cover them
2. While they are cooking, chop two tomatoes, slice the corn off two ears of corn, and add one slice of onion
3. Drain the potatoes, and put all on to cook in two quarts of cold water
4. When done, rub all through a colander, return the soup to the pot
5. Add a level tablespoonful butter, one teaspoon salt, one-half teaspoon pepper and one of minced parsley
6. If not thick enough, moisten a teaspoonful of flour with cold water, thin with the soup and stir in; let boil up once, and it is ready for the table

title: Vegetable Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart of common stock
- One pint of parboiled mixed vegetables
- Salt
- Pepper
- One teaspoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. To one quart of common stock add one pint of parboiled mixed vegetables cut into small dice
2. Simmer until the vegetables are tender but not pasty
3. Season with salt, pepper, and one teaspoonful of sugar
4. Serve without straining

title: Vegetable Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups vegetable water
- 2 cups milk
- 1 cup cooked vegetable
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 4 tablespoons flour
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with 1/4 cup of the cold vegetable water or milk
2. Add this to the heated vegetable water and milk, stirring constantly until it thickens
3. Add the butter and vegetable finely divided
4. Season and simmer ten minutes

title: Vegetable Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds soup meat
- 1 cup barley
- Salt and pepper
- 1 onion
- 2 carrots
- 2 turnips
- 3 potatoes
- 1/4 cabbage cut up fine
- 2 cups lima beans
- 2 cups corn
- 3 or 4 tomatoes
- A little parsley

## Directions

1. Chop vegetables up fine, all but potatoes and tomatoes
2. Cut potatoes and tomatoes up and add when near done
3. Cook until done

title: Vegetable Soup with Rolled Oats
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large potatoes
- 3 carrots
- 1 good-sized turnip or swede
- 6 onions
- Water
- Pepper and salt
- 1 cup rolled oats
- A lump of butter
- 1 cup cream
- A little flour

## Directions

1. Slice all vegetables, cover with water, with pepper and salt
2. Put on to boil
3. When nearly cooked add a cup of rolled oats, with a lump of butter
4. Add a cup of cream
5. Thicken with a little flour

title: Vegetable Soup Without Meat
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups turnips
- 1 1/2 cups carrots
- 1 3/4 quarts water
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 4 nice-sized parsnips
- 3 large onions
- 1 teaspoon summer savory
- A little pepper
- Butter
- 1 heaping tablespoon sifted flour

## Directions

1. Boil turnips and carrots in water with salt for 1 1/2 hours
2. Add parsnips, onions, summer savory, pepper and butter and boil 1/2 hour longer
3. Add sifted flour to thicken

title: Velvet Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup cornstarch
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- whites of 4 eggs
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Flavor with vanilla and bake

title: Velvet Cream Candy
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cups of sugar
- One cup of milk

## Directions

1. Cook until it will roll in fingers
2. Let it cool, and pull and pat until it becomes creamy
3. Pat out flat in platter, and cover with a damp napkin until ready to use

title: Velvet Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs beaten light
- 1 cup granulated or powdered sugar
- 3/4 cup sifted flour
- 1 cup flour sifted with 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 3/4 (scant) cup boiling water
- butter for tin

## Directions

1. Beat eggs light
2. Beat in sugar
3. Add 3/4 cup sifted flour
4. Add next cup flour sifted with baking powder
5. Add boiling water very gradually
6. Butter the tin and fill
7. Bake immediately in a well-heated oven

title: Velvet Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup granulated or powdered sugar
- ¾ cup sifted flour
- 1 cup flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- ⅜ (scant) cup boiling water

## Directions

1. Beat eggs light
2. Beat in sugar
3. Add ¾ cup sifted flour
4. Add next cup flour sifted with baking powder
5. Add boiling water very gradually
6. Have the tin buttered, fill

title: Venison Pie or Pastry
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- neck, breast and shoulder of venison
- fat and lean meat together
- bones and trimmings
- pepper and salt
- water or veal broth
- good rich paste
- nutmeg and mace
- a glass of port wine
- bits of butter rolled in flour
- beaten egg

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into pieces (fat and lean together) and put the bones and trimmings into the stew-pan with pepper and salt, and water or veal broth enough to cover it.
2. Simmer it till you have drawn out a good gravy.
3. Strain it.
4. Make a good rich paste, and roll it rather thick.
5. Cover the bottom and sides of a deep dish with one sheet of it, and put in your meat, having seasoned it with pepper, salt, nutmeg and mace.
6. Pour in the gravy which you have prepared from the trimmings, and a glass of port wine.
7. Lay on the top some bits of butter rolled in flour.
8. Cover the pie with a thick lid of paste and ornament it handsomely with leaves and flowers formed with a tin cutter.
9. Bake two or more hours according to the size.
10. Just before it is done, pull it forward in the oven, and brush it over with beaten egg; push it back and let it slightly brown.

title: Vermicelli or Spaghetti Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Vermicelli or spaghetti
- Salt
- 1 quart cleared boiling stock

## Directions

1. Break the vermicelli or spaghetti into inch lengths, and cook tender and clear in boiling salted water.
2. Drain this off; spread the vermicelli upon a dish and allow it to get almost cold, when drop into a quart of cleared boiling stock.
3. Let it just boil again, and serve.
4. The pipe macaroni may be used in like manner, cut into quarter-inch lengths after it is cooked.

title: Vermicelli Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- A knuckle of lamb
- A small piece of veal
- Water to cover well
- Salt and pepper
- Herbs to taste
- A small onion
- About 1 tablespoon Halford or Worcestershire sauce
- 1/2 pound vermicelli

## Directions

1. Cook lamb and veal in water until well cooked
2. Season with salt and pepper, herbs to taste, onion and Halford or Worcestershire sauce
3. Boil vermicelli until tender
4. Strain soup from the meat, add the vermicelli
5. Let it boil well and serve

title: Vienna Bread
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk, scalded
- 1 cup hot water
- 1 yeast cake
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- Flour to knead (about 4 cups)

## Directions

1. In three tablespoons milk and water dissolve the yeast thoroughly and add to remaining milk and water.
2. Add salt and sufficient flour (warmed) to make a light dough (about four cups).
3. Turn out and knead until thoroughly smooth and does not stick to the hands.
4. Put in a greased basin, cover, and stand in a warm place to rise, for three hours.
5. When risen, turn out and cut into small rolls, knead each roll a little and put in a greased tin.
6. Grease over tops with melted butter, cover and stand in a warm place till twice their original size.
7. Grease again and bake in a hot oven.

title: Vienna Rolls
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. sifted flour
- 1 pt. milk
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 1 compressed yeast cake

## Directions

1. Bank flour around pan, one-half pint of milk, one-half pint of water; mix to a thin batter, quickly add one-half pint of milk in which has been dissolved one teaspoonful of salt and one compressed yeast cake; leave remainder of flour against side of pan; cover and keep free from air fifty minutes; then mix in rest of flour until dough leaves side and bottom of pan; let stand for two and a half hours.
2. Divide into one-pound pieces; sub-divide into twelve pieces.
3. Flatten these small pieces of dough in squares three-quarters of an inch thick, fold their corners to the centre, pinch them down to hold them, and turn the little rolls thus made over on a board covered with cloth; let them stand for about ten minutes.
4. Turn them up again on a baking-pan and put into a hot oven to bake quickly, for about fifteen minutes; when half done brush them with milk, return them to the oven and finish baking.

title: Vinegar
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 pounds brown sugar
- 1 gallon clean soft water
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake

## Directions

1. Let brown sugar and water boil
2. Put in a crock and when cool, not cold, add 1 White Swan Yeast Cake (1 yeast cake is enough for 3 gallons)
3. Stir every day, and when it has stopped working, let it stand to get sour
4. Keep in a warm place all the time

title: Vinegar Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 heaping tablespoon flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon sharp vinegar
- 1 cup cold water
- nutmeg
- 2 pie crusts

## Directions

1. Beat egg, flour, and sugar well together
2. Add vinegar and cold water
3. Flavor with nutmeg
4. Bake with 2 crusts

title: Vinegar Tarts
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- ½ teaspoon essence lemon

## Directions

1. Mix all together. This will make 12 tarts

title: Virginia Creamed Oysters
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 or 4 oysters per nappy
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 cupful oyster liquor
- 1 cup cream
- baking soda
- salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Cook butter and flour together until they bubble
2. Pour on them a cupful of oyster liquor and 1 of cream
3. Add a tiny pinch of baking soda to the cream
4. Stir over the fire in a double boiler until you have a thick, smooth sauce
5. Season to taste with salt and white pepper
6. Beat up hard once and pour over the oysters in the shells or the nappies
7. Set in the oven and after the shells or dishes are hot, bake for about 5 minutes

title: Waffles
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- small cup and a half of milk
- one cup of flour
- one egg
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- one-half teaspoon of salt

## Directions

1. Mix flour, baking powder, and salt
2. Grease iron (special iron for waffles) well and have hot
3. Cook until done

title: Waffles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 1 teaspoon lard or butter melted
- 2 cups milk scalded and cooled
- 2 eggs
- 2 1/2 cups sifted flour
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve yeast and sugar in lukewarm liquid. Add lard or butter, flour, salt and eggs well beaten. Beat thoroughly until batter is smooth. Cover and set aside to rise in a warm place free from draught for about one hour. When light, stir well. Have waffle irons hot and well greased. Fill the cool side. Brown on one side, turn the iron and brown on the other. If batter is too thick the waffles will be tough. If wanted for over night use, 3/4 cake of yeast and an extra teaspoon salt. Cover and keep in a cool place.

title: Waldorf Pumpkin Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pts. pumpkin pulp
- 2 tablespoonfuls flour
- 4 eggs
- 1 lb. sugar
- 1 tablespoonful ginger
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 2 qts. milk
- pie crust

## Directions

1. Make crust and line pie dish.
2. Cook pumpkin pulp, flour, eggs, sugar, ginger, salt and milk all together until well thickened.
3. Bake in a moderate oven one hour.

title: Waldorf Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- One quart apples cut in dice
- One quart celery same size
- Rich creamy mayonnaise
- Lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Dress with rich creamy mayonnaise and serve very cold, on lettuce leaves.

title: Waldorf Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 dozen rather tart apples
- 1 cup or more diced celery
- 1 cup chopped English walnuts

## Directions

1. Select 1/2 dozen rather tart apples, pare and slice evenly in salad dish
2. Mix with 1 cup or more diced celery and 1 cup chopped English walnuts
3. May also be served in cups made of hollowed apples

title: Walnut Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites of 4 eggs
- 2 cups sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 1 cup chopped nuts
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- 2 teaspoonfuls cream tartar
- Vanilla flavoring

## Directions

1. Beat butter in sugar, add milk, flour, nuts, eggs last
2. then bake slowly
3. Frost with chocolate frosting, with half walnuts on top

title: Walnut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- lemon or vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Add eggs
3. Add milk
4. Mix flour, cream tartar, and soda
5. Add flour mixture to batter
6. Season with lemon or vanilla
7. Bake in 2 sheets
8. Fill and frost

title: Walnut Cake 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs beaten separately
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup butter creamed
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
- 10 cents' worth shelled walnuts

## Directions

1. Cream butter with sugar
2. Add eggs beaten separately
3. Sift cream tartar and soda with flour
4. Mix in flour
5. Chop half the walnuts
6. Put into well-buttered pan
7. Spread thin layer of mixture
8. Add layer of chopped nuts
9. Cover with rest of batter
10. Ice and put rest of nuts on top

title: Walnut Cake 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups white sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 3 cups sifted flour
- 1 heaping teaspoon baking powder
- 1 tablespoon powdered mace
- a coffee cup walnuts chopped small
- raisins

## Directions

1. Cream sugar with butter
2. Add eggs
3. Mix baking powder with flour and sift
4. Add flour mixture and milk alternately
5. Add powdered mace
6. Add walnuts
7. Fill cake pans with layer of cake
8. Add layer of raisins
9. Add layer of nuts
10. Repeat until pan is two-thirds full
11. Line tins with well-buttered paper
12. Bake in steady, but not quick, oven

title: Walnut Cake 4
body:
## Ingredients

- whites of 5 eggs
- 1 scant cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2 cups white sugar
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar
2. Beat egg whites
3. Mix cream tartar and soda with flour
4. Add flour mixture and milk alternately to batter
5. Bake
6. Fill and ice

title: Walnut Cake 5
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup walnuts
- 3/4 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and add the salt, sugar and nuts
2. Stir well together
3. Add the milk and flour
4. Butter tins and set in warm place for 20 minutes
5. Bake in a moderate oven for 20 or 30 minutes

title: Walnut Cream Filling for Layer Cakes
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Quarter pound of butter
- quarter pound icing sugar
- half pound walnuts
- vanilla
- a little sherry

## Directions

1. Beat butter with sugar to a cream
2. chop the walnuts fine and beat them well into the sugar and butter
3. flavor with vanilla and a little sherry

title: Walnut Filling for Walnut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup water
- white of 1 egg
- 1/2 cup chopped raisins
- 1/2 cup chopped walnuts
- 2 tablespoons cocoanut
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water till stringy
2. Beat the white of 1 egg
3. Mix egg with the sugar
4. Add chopped raisins, walnuts, and cocoanut
5. Flavor with vanilla
6. Spread between the layers
7. Frost the cake with white frosting

title: Walnut Loaf
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups white flour
- 1 egg
- 1 cup chopped nuts
- 4 tablespoons brown sugar
- 1 1/2 cups milk
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- pinch salt

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients
2. Pour in baking pan
3. Let stand 1/4 hour
4. Bake

title: Walnut Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- walnut meats
- mayonnaise
- chopped peanuts or pistachio
- close-grained, home-made bread

## Directions

1. Mix walnut meats with mayonnaise
2. Spread between close-grained, home-made bread
3. Chopped peanuts or pistachio may be used instead

title: Walnut Wafers
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup chopped walnuts
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon butter
- about 8 or 10 tablespoons flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat sugar and eggs together
2. Add nuts and butter
3. Add flour last
4. Spread on pan very thin
5. When baked, cut in squares while warm

title: Walnut Waffers
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup walnut meats
- 1 well-beaten egg
- 6 teaspoons flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Drop small drops in well-buttered pans and bake in a moderate oven.

title: Warm Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon soda
- buttermilk

## Directions

1. Sift baking powder and soda into flour
2. Mix butter into flour
3. Add buttermilk enough to make a soft dough
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Warm Biscuits
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon soda
- buttermilk enough to make a soft dough

## Directions

1. Sift baking powder and soda into flour
2. Mix butter into flour
3. Add the buttermilk
4. Bake in a quick oven

title: Wash-Day Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup rice
- 1 quart sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- a little salt
- 1/2 cup raisins
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Bake in slow oven 4 hours

title: Washington Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup chopped raisins
- 1 lemon peel
- 3/4 cup buttermilk
- 2 teaspoons nutmeg
- 2 teaspoons cassia
- 3 teaspoons syrup
- 1 teaspoon soda in 2 teaspoons warm water
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Bake in 2 layers and put together with lemon filling

title: Washington Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/4 cups flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 2 eggs
- butter the size of an egg
- milk
- jam or jelly

## Directions

1. Break 2 eggs into a cup
2. Add a piece of melted butter the size of an egg to the eggs
3. Fill the cup with milk
4. Mix with flour, sugar, cream tartar, and soda
5. Bake in 2 large layers
6. Place jam or jelly between layers

title: Washington Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 2 eggs
- A piece of melted butter the size of an egg
- Milk
- Jam or jelly

## Directions

1. Break into a cup 2 eggs
2. Add to them a piece of melted butter the size of an egg
3. Fill the cup with milk
4. Bake in 2 large layers
5. Place jam or jelly between

title: Washington Pie (2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup milk
- Butter the size of an egg
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 cup flour
- Strawberry or raspberry jam
- Whites of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a stiff batter as for cake, using 1 teaspoon baking powder to 1 cup flour
2. Bake in jelly pan
3. Slip it off and spread with strawberry or raspberry jam
4. Over this spread the whites of 2 eggs beaten to a stiff

title: Washington Pie 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup milk
- butter the size of an egg
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 cup flour
- strawberry or raspberry jam
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Make a stiff batter using sugar, egg, milk, butter, baking powder, and flour as for cake
2. Bake in jelly pan
3. Slip it off and spread with strawberry or raspberry jam
4. Spread the whites of 2 eggs beaten to a stiff froth with a little sugar over the jam
5. Put in oven till set
6. Eat with or without cream while hot
7. Can also be cut when cold and used for a cake

title: Washington Pie 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1/2 cup sugar
- butter size of walnut
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- a few drops vanilla
- 1 pint milk
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch

## Directions

1. Bake cake in a round tin using egg, milk, sugar, butter, flour, baking powder, and vanilla
2. When baked, cut in two
3. For filling: put milk on the stove and when it boils add the cornstarch dissolved with a little of the milk
4. Add the beaten eggs, sugar and vanilla to the filling
5. Fill the cake with this filling

title: Water Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup slightly warm water
- 2 cups sifted flour
- 3/4 cup butter
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. This makes one large loaf

title: Water Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup slightly warm water
- 2 cups sifted flour
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3 teaspoons

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients
2. Bake

title: Watercress Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- watercress
- hard-boiled eggs
- salt and pepper
- stale loaf
- fresh butter
- lemon-juice

## Directions

1. Wash well some watercress and dry them in a cloth, pressing out every atom of moisture
2. Mix with the cresses hard-boiled eggs chopped fine, and seasoned with salt and pepper
3. Have a stale loaf and some fresh butter
4. Cut as many thin slices as will be required for two dozen sandwiches with a sharp knife
5. Cut the cress into small pieces, removing the stems
6. Place it between each slice of bread and butter, with a slight sprinkling of lemon-juice
7. Press down the slices hard and cut them sharply on a board into small squares, leaving no crust

title: Watermelon Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups fine white sugar
- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup milk
- 3 3/4 cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- the whites of 8 eggs beaten very light
- flavor
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup red sugar
- 3/4 cup milk
- 1 cup seedless raisins
- 2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flavor

## Directions

1. Put the red part in the centre of pan and white around the outside

title: Watermelon Preserve
body:
## Ingredients

- allspice
- cinnamon
- 4 cups vinegar
- 7 pounds watermelon

## Directions

1. Boil watermelon until it can be pierced with a fork
2. Strain
3. Put in sealers
4. Boil together the other ingredients a few minutes
5. Pour over fruit while hot
6. Set away in a cool, dark place

title: Wedding Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds brown sugar
- 1 pound butter
- dash of lard
- 3 pounds currants
- 4 pounds seeded raisins
- 1/2 pound lemon peel
- 1/2 pound citron peel
- 1/2 pound orange peel
- 1 pound shelled almond nuts
- bottle cinnamon
- 10c. rose water
- 2 nutmegs
- 1/2 cup dark molasses
- 5c. mace
- 9 eggs
- 1 cup flour
- water and cream
- 4 teaspoons baking soda
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients as indicated

title: Welsh Barebit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter size of egg
- 2 or 3 cups grated cheese
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 pt. beer
- A little onion juice
- Salt and cayenne to taste

## Directions

1. Melt butter, add cheese and seasoning, then beer and beaten eggs

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oz. cheese
- Yolks 2 eggs
- 4 oz. grated bread
- 1 dessertspoonful mustard
- 3 oz. butter
- A little salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Grate cheese, and mix it with the yolks of eggs, bread, and butter
2. Beat the whole together in a mortar with made mustard, a little salt and some pepper
3. Toast some slices of bread, cut off the outside crust, cut it in shapes and spread the paste thick upon them
4. Put them in the oven, let them become hot and slightly browned
5. Serve hot as possible

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. cheese
- 2 oz. butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls ale or Yolks 2 eggs beaten in 1/2 cup milk
- 1 saltspoonful salt
- 1 saltspoonful dry mustard
- 1/2 saltspoonful pepper
- A dash of cayenne

## Directions

1. Stir in a saucepan over the fire until melted smoothly together
2. Pour on a couple of slices of toast laid on a hot dish

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. cheese
- 1/4 cup cream or milk
- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- 1/2 teaspoonful salt
- 1 egg
- 1 teaspoonful butter
- Salt and cayenne
- 4 slices of toast

## Directions

1. Break the cheese in small pieces, or if hard grate it
2. Put it with the milk in a double boiler
3. Toast the bread and keep it hot
4. Mix the mustard, salt and pepper; add the egg, and beat well
5. When the cheese is melted, stir in the egg and butter, and cook two minutes, or until it thickens a little, but do not let it curdle
6. Pour it over the toast

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 glass old ale
- 1/2 lb. old cheese
- Pinch red pepper
- 1/2 teaspoonful salt
- Dash of Worcestershire sauce

## Directions

1. Grate cheese fine, place in a chafing-dish or small saucepan on fire, rub well with back of spoon until thoroughly dissolved
2. Mix pepper, mustard and Worcester sauce thoroughly with ale, and pour into cheese
3. Thoroughly mix until smooth
4. Serve on buttered toast, cut diamond shape on red-hot dish

title: Welsh Rarebit
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
