## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 lb. soft cheese
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 1/4 teaspoonful salt
- 1 teaspoonful mustard
- Saltspoonful cayenne
- 1/2 cup milk or cream

## Directions

1. Break eggs into the upper pan of the chafing-dish, beat them well, then add cheese, broken into small bits; butter, salt, mustard, cayenne and cream or milk
2. stir this mixture well, until cheese is melted
3. Serve on crisp toast or on toasted thin water-crackers

title: Welsh Rarebit
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups German chopped cheese
- 1/4 cup milk (sweet)
- yolks of 2 eggs
- salt and pepper
- cayenne to taste
- square slices of bread with crust removed
- butter

## Directions

1. Toast carefully square slices of bread with crust removed
2. While hot, butter them
3. Plunge into bowl of hot water
4. Place in a heated dish and stand in the oven to keep warm
5. Put the milk into a granite saucepan
6. Stand it over a moderate fire
7. When boiling hot add the cheese
8. Stir constantly until the cheese is melted
9. Add salt and pepper and yolks of eggs
10. Pour over the toasted bread

title: Welsh Rarebit No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon cornstarch
- 6 tablespoons grated cheese
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 cup thin cream
- 1/4 teaspoon mustard
- a few grains cayenne
- bread

## Directions

1. Make a cream sauce with the starch, butter and cream
2. Mix in with the starch the mustard and cayenne before adding these to the butter
3. When the sauce is made, stir in the cheese
4. Do not cook any longer unless necessary to melt cheese
5. Pour over the untoasted side of bread

title: Welsh Rarebit No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- stale bread
- butter
- thin layers of cheese

## Directions

1. Take stale bread, cut in slices, butter it
2. Put in a baking pan butter side down
3. Cut thin layers of cheese and lay on your slices of bread
4. Put into the oven and bake until the cheese begins to spread
5. Have your oven hot and serve at once when removed from the oven

title: Wet Yeast
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large handful of hops
- 1 gallon of water
- 5 medium-sized potatoes
- a little salt
- sugar
- ginger
- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake

## Directions

1. Boil 1 large handful of hops in a gallon of water for half an hour
2. Grate 5 medium-sized potatoes and strain the boiling liquid over it, stirring well
3. Add a little salt, sugar and ginger
4. Let cool, then add 1 White Swan Yeast Cake and put in a warm place to rise
5. Stir frequently
6. It is best made in a large dish and afterwards put into a jar and corked tight

title: Wheat Gems
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart flour
- 1 quart milk
- 4 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls (small) butter

## Directions

1. Sift the flour with the salt
2. Stir the milk in smoothly
3. Beat the yolks and whites well and separately
4. Stir first the yolks in the milk and flour, then the whites, then the melted butter
5. Half fill the gem-pans and bake in a deep pan or on a baking-sheet in a moderate oven for twenty-five minutes; if baked in earthen cups, forty-five minutes
6. Let them be thoroughly baked, or they will fall on being taken from the oven

title: Wheatlet Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups wheatlets
- 7½ cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup brown sugar
- ¾ cup lard
- ½ cup butter
- ¼ cup sweet milk
- dates optional

## Directions

1. Rub wheatlets, flour, baking powder, brown sugar, lard and butter all together
2. Add sweet milk
3. Mix into dough and roll out thin
4. Optional: place dates between cookies
5. Bake

title: Wheatmeal Pancakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 cup buttermilk
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 1 egg (yolk and white beaten separately)
- 1 cup wheatmeal
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Combine sour cream, buttermilk, sweet cream, melted butter, and egg yolks and whites beaten separately. Add wheatmeal, flour, soda, and baking powder.

title: Whipped Cream Without Cream
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup bananas
- white of 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons white sugar

## Directions

1. beat with fork for 10 minutes

title: White Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup butter
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup milk
- lemon flavoring
- flour enough to drop off spoon nicely

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients

title: White Cake No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- whites of 5 eggs beaten light
- 2 cups sifted flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon almond extract

## Directions

1. Cream the butter and sugar
2. add the whites, then the milk, lastly the flour sifted with the baking powder
3. Beat until very light
4. Bake in moderate oven

title: White Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1½ cups sugar
- 1 heaping cup shortening
- ¼ cup sweet milk
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- seasoning to taste
- currants optional

## Directions

1. Mix eggs, sugar, shortening, sweet milk, and vinegar together
2. Add soda and baking powder
3. Season to taste
4. Stir all the flour you can in with a spoon before taking on the board
5. Have them stiff enough not to stick to board
6. Optional: add currants to part of the dough
7. Bake

title: White Cookies No. 2
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lard
- 2 cups buttermilk
- 2 eggs
- ¾ teaspoon soda
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 3 cups sugar
- flour to roll

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out
3. Bake

title: White Cookies No. 3
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 teaspoon cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 tablespoon butter
- salt and nutmeg
- flour to mix and roll

## Directions

1. Mix ingredients together
2. Roll out
3. Bake

title: White Fruit Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups sugar (1 cup white and 1/2 cup brown)
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 cup orange juice
- 3/4 cup milk
- small teaspoon vanilla
- 1/2 nutmeg
- grated rind of orange
- 1/2 pound blanched almonds cut fine
- 1/2 pound figs cut fine
- 1/2 pound citron peel cut fine
- 1 pound sultana raisins
- 1 large cup currants
- 1 pound cocoanut
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Cream together 1 cup butter and 1 1/2 cups sugar.
2. Add 4 eggs, 1/2 cup orange juice, 3/4 cup milk, and small teaspoon vanilla.
3. Add grated rind of orange and 1/2 nutmeg.
4. Stir in 1/2 pound blanched almonds cut fine, 1/2 pound figs cut fine, and 1/2 pound citron peel cut fine.
5. Add 1 pound sultana raisins and 1 large cup currants.
6. Sift together 2 1/2 cups flour and 2 teaspoons baking powder and stir in.
7. Put in 1 pound cocoanut at the last.
8. Bake in tin lined with paper for 2 hours.

title: White Layer Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup milk
- 2 1/4 cups flour
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 2 1/4 teaspoons baking powder
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix flour, salt and baking powder
2. Cream butter and add 1/2 of sugar and beat
3. Add beaten egg and remainder of sugar and beat 4 minutes
4. Add flour and milk alternately, beating well
5. Add vanilla
6. Bake in quick oven

title: White Layer Cake Without Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
- 1/2 cup shortening
- 1 3/4 cups sweet milk
- 3 cups flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- juice and grated rind of 1 lemon
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 1 egg
- yolk of another egg
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, shortening, milk, flour and baking powder
2. Bake in 3 layers
3. For Lemon Honey Filling: Mix lemon juice, grated rind, 1 cup granulated sugar, 1 egg and yolk of another egg with 1 tablespoon butter
4. Beat well and cook in double boiler until transparent
5. When cool spread between layers of white cake

title: White Patty Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- 1/4 cup butter
- 3/4 cup white sugar
- 2 teaspoons cream tartar
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- raisins or dates

## Directions

1. Put raisins or dates on top

title: White Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large cucumbers peeled and sliced
- 12 onions sliced
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel and slice cucumbers and onions
2. Sprinkle with salt, and let stand

title: White Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 large cucumbers
- 12 onions
- salt
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 2 teaspoons tumeric
- 2 teaspoons mustard
- white pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel and slice cucumbers and onions
2. Sprinkle with salt and let stand over night
3. In the morning drain
4. Cover with vinegar
5. Add sugar, tumeric, mustard, and white pepper
6. Cook 1/2 hour

title: White Rock Cake
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Whites 3 eggs
- 0.5 cup melted butter
- 1 cup granulated sugar
- 2.5 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- 0.75 cup flour
- 0.75 cup milk
- 1 cup cornstarch

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs to a stiff froth
2. Sugar and butter to a cream
3. Mix flour and cornstarch with two and one-half teaspoonfuls baking powder
4. Mix all together

title: White Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup milk

## Directions

1. Boil all together, mixing the flour with a little of the cold milk

title: White Soup
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 good-sized knuckle of veal
- water
- 1 quart of milk
- 1 piece of fresh butter the size of an egg
- 1/2 teaspoonful of ground mace
- ground red or white pepper
- salt
- 2 and 1/2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- cold water
- yolks of 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Put the knuckle of veal on to boil well covered with water, removing the scum as it comes to a boil
2. Boil till the meat comes freely from the bones, which remove, returning the bones into the pot and boil for another three-quarters of an hour
3. Renew the water when you see fit, so that it may not boil dry
4. Strain all through a colander
5. Set it away in a cool place, and the following day put it on to boil with a quart of milk and a piece of fresh butter the size of an egg
6. Put one-half teaspoonful of ground mace, ground red or white pepper, and salt to taste
7. When all comes to a boil have two and one-half tablespoonfuls of flour well blended in cold water, pour this into the boiling soup
8. Let it boil for three or four minutes
9. Remove the pot from the stove
10. Take the yolks of six eggs well beaten, put a little of the soup with the eggs and mix all together
11. Pour slowly into the soup, stirring it quietly all the while
12. The eggs must not be added to the soup while boiling, as they will curdle

title: White Stock
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds of knuckle of veal, cut up in small pieces
- poultry trimmings
- 4 slices of lean ham

## Directions

1. Proceed according to directions given in Stock, above

title: White Swan Yeast Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sugar
- 2 cups molasses
- 1 cup butter
- 1 tablespoon soda dissolved in 2 tablespoons sour milk
- 1 tablespoon ginger
- flour to make stiff dough

## Directions

1. Mix brown sugar, molasses, and butter
2. Dissolve soda in sour milk and add to mixture
3. Add ginger
4. Mix with flour to make stiff dough

title: White Swan Yeast Cakes - Ferment, Sponge and Dough Method
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 2 quarts liquid
- 2 level tablespoons salt
- 2 level tablespoons sugar
- 2 level tablespoons lard
- Flour
- strong flour

## Directions

1. Preparation of Ferment: Put a pint of water at a temperature of about 90° F. into a bowl, drop the dry yeast cake into it and soak for half an hour, then stir in enough flour to make a thin batter, add one tablespoon sugar, and beat with a dover beater until well mixed and full of bubbles. Stand in a temperature of 70° to 80° F. until light, which will take from four to five hours.
2. Preparation of Sponge: When the ferment is ready, put the rest of the sugar, salt and lard into a kneading-pan, bring the rest of the liquid to 90° F. and add it to the ingredients in the pan. Add enough strong flour to make a batter that will beat without spattering; add the ferment and beat until it looks smooth and elastic. This will probably take 15 or 20 minutes. Cover closely, and keep at a temperature of 70° F., until light and spongy. This will take from 9 to 10 hours.
3. Preparation of Dough: When the sponge is ready stir in strong flour until too stiff to use the spoon, then mix in more with a stiff-bladed knife or the hand, until the dough no longer sticks to the fingers. Turn the dough out on the moulding-board to knead, leaving the pan quite clean. The dough should knead without flour being put on the board or hands; if it proves sticky return it to the pan and mix in more flour. Knead lightly until the mass is elastic and velvety, the surface covered with a film of tiny bubbles, and a cut with a sharp knife shows the inside full of fine even bubbles, and free from lumps or unmixed portions. Grease the kneading-pan lightly with lard, warm both pan and cover if they are cold, put in the dough, cover closely, and keep at a temperature of 80° F. until rather more than doubled in volume, or until a gentle slap with the tips of the fingers causes it to fall in. This will take from 2 to 3 hours.
4. Knead lightly in the pan for a minute to get rid of the larger bubbles and return to rise a second time until double in volume. This will take from 1 to 2 hours.
5. Divide into loaves that will half fill the bread tins. Knead each piece only enough to get rid of large bubbles and smooth the surface, and put it into a greased tin. Keep at a temperature of 70° to 80° F. until doubled in volume, when they should have a bold, nicely-rounded appearance.
6. Bake an hour in an oven about 350° F. When done, the loaves should give a hollow sound when tapped on the bottom.
7. When baked remove at once from the pan, and stand on edge or across the top of the pans, that the air may get to all parts and cool it quickly.

title: White Swan Yeast Cakes - Sponge and Dough Method
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 White Swan Yeast Cake
- 2 quarts liquid at 90° F.
- 2 level tablespoons salt
- 2 level tablespoons sugar
- 2 level tablespoons lard or butter
- Flour
- strong flour

## Directions

1. Mix the yeast and half of the sugar in 1/4 cup of warm water, and let it stand at a temperature of 80° F. for 15 or 20 minutes to start fermentation.
2. Measure the shortening, salt, and rest of sugar, into kneading-pan, stir in the rest of the liquid, and mix in flour enough to beat without spattering. Then add the well-fermented yeast and beat with a wooden spoon until it looks smooth and elastic.
3. From this point treat just the same as in the ferment, sponge and dough method: Cover closely, and keep at a temperature of 70° F., until light and spongy. This will take from 9 to 10 hours.
4. When the sponge is ready stir in strong flour until too stiff to use the spoon, then mix in more with a stiff-bladed knife or the hand, until the dough no longer sticks to the fingers. Turn the dough out on the moulding-board to knead, leaving the pan quite clean. Knead lightly until the mass is elastic and velvety, the surface covered with a film of tiny bubbles, and a cut with a sharp knife shows the inside full of fine even bubbles, and free from lumps or unmixed portions. Grease the kneading-pan lightly with lard, warm both pan and cover if they are cold, put in the dough, cover closely, and keep at a temperature of 80° F. until rather more than doubled in volume, or until a gentle slap with the tips of the fingers causes it to fall in. This will take from 2 to 3 hours.
5. Knead lightly in the pan for a minute to get rid of the larger bubbles and return to rise a second time until double in volume. This will take from 1 to 2 hours.
6. Divide into loaves that will half fill the bread tins. Knead each piece only enough to get rid of large bubbles and smooth the surface, and put it into a greased tin. Keep at a temperature of 70° to 80° F. until doubled in volume, when they should have a bold, nicely-rounded appearance.
7. Bake an hour in an oven about 350° F. When done, the loaves should give a hollow sound when tapped on the bottom.
8. When baked remove at once from the pan, and stand on edge or across the top of the pans, that the air may get to all parts and cool it quickly.

title: White Wedding Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 2 cups granulated sugar
- 1 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- whites 8 eggs
- 1 pound citron sliced fine
- 1 pound almonds blanched and chopped fine
- 1 small cocoanut grated

## Directions

1. Cream butter and sugar thoroughly together
2. add the milk and flour and baking powder sifted 3 times
3. stir all well together
4. add the citron and nuts, stirring only enough to mix thoroughly
5. Last of all add the whites of eggs beaten to a stiff froth
6. Fold in lightly and bake in a moderate oven
7. When cold, ice with confectioner's sugar mixed with white of egg

title: Whole Wheat Flour Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half cups of grated whole wheat bread-crumbs
- half a cupful of butter
- half a cupful of sugar
- one cupful of chopped figs
- one egg well beaten
- a good half cup of milk

## Directions

1. Rub butter into bread-crumbs.
2. Add sugar, figs, egg and milk.
3. Steam three hours.

title: Whole Wheat Ginger Snaps
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup Orleans molasses
- 1/2 cup shortening
- 1/2 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- whole wheat flour

## Directions

1. Place on stove and let come to a boil, then take off immediately and add a half teaspoon soda and a teaspoon ginger.
2. Add whole wheat flour until thick enough to roll.

title: Wine Jelly
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 box gelatine
- 1 pt. cold water
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 lemons
- 2 eggs
- 1 pt. hot water
- 0.5 cup wine
- Wine glass whisky
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak gelatine for several hours in pint of cold water
2. Grate the rinds of lemons
3. Add with the juice of the lemons the whites and shells of two eggs, hot water (not boiling), wine, whisky, some cinnamon and sugar
4. Let all boil
5. Skim and strain through flannel bag
6. Add large cup sugar and remainder of wine
7. Put in mould to cool

title: Winter Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons buttermilk
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 tablespoon juice of canned cherries
- pie crusts
- canned cherries

## Directions

1. Cream buttermilk and sugar
2. Add the yolks of 2 eggs beaten
3. Mix in flour with baking powder
4. Flavor with juice of canned cherries
5. Fill crusts and bake
6. Put in fruit from which the juice has been drained
7. Make a frosting of the whites of eggs

title: Winter Salad
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups chopped cabbage
- 1 cup chopped boiled beets
- 1 cup chopped celery
- 1/2 teaspoonful dry mustard
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 tablespoonful horseradish
- 1 tablespoonful brown sugar
- vinegar
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix all together
2. Break an egg in a sauce-pan
3. Add half cup of vinegar and butter size of walnut
4. Stir over fire till thick, do not boil
5. Pour over salad
6. Toss lightly and serve cold

title: Winter Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts green cucumbers
- 2 quarts green tomatoes
- 2 quarts green cabbage
- 2 large cauliflowers
- a pinch of red pepper
- salt
- vinegar and water
- 6 bunches of celery
- 1 gallon vinegar
- 3/4 pound mustard
- 3 teaspoons turmeric
- 5 1/2 cups sugar
- 1 heaping cup flour

## Directions

1. Let cucumbers, tomatoes, cabbage, cauliflowers and red pepper stand over night in salt in separate dishes
2. Drain in the morning and scald in vinegar and water
3. Add 6 bunches of celery, 1 gallon vinegar, 3/4 pound mustard, 3 teaspoons turmeric, 5 1/2 cups sugar, 1 heaping cup flour
4. Mix mustard, flour and turmeric in cold vinegar
5. Put on and scald

title: Winter Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 peck green tomatoes chopped fine
- 2 dozen large onions peeled and chopped
- large red pepper chopped fine
- salt
- pepper
- spice to suit taste
- 1 cup brown sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop green tomatoes, onions, and red pepper fine
2. Season with salt, pepper and spice to suit taste
3. Add 1 cup brown sugar
4. Pour boiling hot vinegar over all
5. Bottle for winter use

title: Woodcock, Snipe, and Other Small Birds
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcock
- snipe
- small birds
- fat pork or bacon
- boiling water
- butter
- giblets
- toast

## Directions

1. Clean and leave whole, the head skinned, the eyes extracted, and the head twisted over the shoulder until the bill pierces the body
2. Bind a thin slice of fat pork or bacon closely about each bird
3. When all are ready lay them upon the grating of your covered roaster
4. Pour a very little boiling water under them
5. Cover and roast fifteen minutes
6. Remove the bacon, wash the birds over with butter, and brown
7. Boil the giblets and pound fine
8. Rub to a paste with butter; season to taste
9. Have ready squares of toast, buttered
10. Wet with the pan-gravy and spread with the paste, laying a bird upon each

title: Worcester Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- scraps of meat
- butter
- onion
- pepper
- salt
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 pint water
- 1 or 2 carrots

## Directions

1. Cut scraps of meat into small pieces
2. Fry a nice brown in a buttered pan
3. Fry an onion or two with it
4. Season with pepper and salt
5. Rub into it 1 tablespoon of flour
6. Add 1 pint of water and 1 or 2 carrots sliced fine
7. Simmer 2 hours

title: Worcestershire Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 bushel ripe tomatoes
- 1 quart cider vinegar
- 10 lemons cut in pieces
- 1 pound salt
- 1 pound brown sugar
- 3/4 pound allspice
- 1 ounce ground cloves

## Directions

1. Cook slowly 2 hours
2. Put through sieve
3. Add juice of 1 lemon
4. Bottle and seal hot

title: Wormwood And Arnica Poultice
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- wormwood
- arnica
- half a pint of cold water
- bran or rye-meal

## Directions

1. Steep the herbs in half a pint of cold water, and when all their virtue is extracted stir in a little bran or rye-meal to thicken the liquid
2. The herbs must not be removed from the liquid
3. This is a useful application for sprains and bruises

title: Xmas Bun
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds best layer raisins (stoned)
- 4 pounds best currants
- 3 1/2 pounds best peel (mixed)
- 1 pound almonds (blanched)
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon pepper
- 1 teaspoon essence lemon
- 3 pounds bread dough, ready for loaves
- 1/2 pound butter

## Directions

1. Take 3 pounds bread dough, ready for loaves, divide in 2 parts
2. Take 1 part and mix with all the fruit
3. Take 1/2 pound butter and mix into other half
4. Line your pan bottom and sides with this
5. Pack in the fruit mixture
6. Wet the edges and put on top crust
7. Prick all over with a fork
8. Bake in a slow oven from 1 1/2 to 2 hours

title: Yankee Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 1/2 cup butter
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup sour milk
- 2 cups chopped raisins
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon cloves
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon soda
- icing sugar

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients

title: Yankee Jelly Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 cup brown syrup
- 1/2 cup buttermilk
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 2 eggs
- lemon filling
- maple cream icing
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/2 cup sweet milk
- butter size of egg
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Mix brown sugar, butter, brown syrup, buttermilk, ginger, soda, cinnamon and eggs
2. Bake in layers
3. Use lemon filling and maple cream icing
4. For icing: combine brown sugar, sweet milk, and butter size of egg
5. Flavor with vanilla and let boil until it begins to thread
6. Beat until it begins to cool

title: Yeast For Four-Hour Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of potatoes
- 2 quarts of water
- 2 cups of flour
- 2 quarts cold water
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1/4 cup salt
- 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes

## Directions

1. Peel and slice 2 quarts of potatoes, boil in 2 quarts of water
2. When cooked, scald 2 cups of flour with water of potatoes and add 2 quarts cold water
3. Add 1 cup brown sugar and 1/4 cup salt, along with mashed potatoes
4. Add 2 White Swan Yeast Cakes dissolved in a cup of warm water
5. Set in a warm place, stirring 3 or 4 times
6. For making each loaf use a cup of yeast and 1 of warm water

title: Yeast For Use In Making Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 medium-sized potatoes
- 3 tablespoons flour
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup lukewarm water
- 3 White Swan Yeast Cakes
- 1 quart cold water
- 3 parts hot water

## Directions

1. Pare and boil potatoes, mash well
2. There should be 1 quart water on them when done
3. Put the flour in a crock, sugar and salt, scald with 1 pint boiling water
4. Add the quart of hot water from the potatoes, with 1 quart cold water
5. Soak yeast cakes in lukewarm water, then add to the mixture when about cold
6. Let it rise all night
7. Keep in a cool place
8. It will keep from 3 weeks to a month

title: Yeast Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 cups flour
- 1 pint boiled milk
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1/2 White Swan Yeast Cake
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Boil milk and when cold add 1/2 cup butter, 1/2 cup sugar, 1 egg and 1/2 White Swan Yeast Cake to 7 cups flour
2. Set to rise at 8 a.m.
3. At 3 p.m. roll out about 1 inch in thickness
4. Cut with large cutter
5. Dip in melted butter
6. Fold over and rise till time to bake
7. Bake in quick oven

title: Yeast Rolls
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 cups flour
- 1 pint boiled milk
- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 1/2 White Swan Yeast Cake

## Directions

1. When milk is cold, add butter, sugar, egg and White Swan Yeast Cake
2. Set to rise at 8 a.m.
3. At 3 p.m. roll out about 1 inch in thickness
4. Cut with large cutter
5. Dip in melted butter
6. Fold over and rise till time to bake
7. Bake in quick oven

title: Yeast Rusks
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint sweet milk
- 1 teacup yeast or 1 compressed yeast cake
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoonful soda
- A little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Add butter, sugar, soda, little nutmeg to milk and yeast mixture
2. Let rise
3. Knead and mould into shape
4. Let rise and bake
5. When done wet top with eggs, sprinkle with sugar, and return to oven again for a moment
6. Serve hot

title: Yellow Pickle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 large green peppers
- 2 quarts or 4 large cucumbers
- 2 quarts green tomatoes
- 2 quarts small onions
- 4 cauliflowers
- 2 heads celery
- 1 cup mustard seed
- 2 tablespoonfuls mustard
- 2.5 cups flour
- 3.5 cups sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls turmeric powder
- 1 gallon cider vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop finely peppers, slice cucumbers, green tomatoes, and onions
2. Break in pieces four cauliflowers and chop two heads of celery
3. Cover with salt and let stand twenty-four hours
4. Drain and add mustard-seed
5. Mix mustard, flour, sugar, turmeric powder, one pint cider vinegar for dressing
6. Put on to scald three and a half quarts cider vinegar
7. When scalding add mixture and vegetables
8. Leave on stove to scald and get soft
9. Do not boil

title: Yellow Pickle
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large cauliflowers
- 60 small cucumbers
- 1 qt. string beans
- 4 green peppers
- 2 cups sugar
- ½ cup flour
- 6 tablespoonfuls mustard
- 3 qts. cider vinegar
- 1 oz. turmeric
- 2 qts. white onions (pickles)
- 3 qts. green tomatoes

## Directions

1. Cut up tomatoes, cutting out all imperfections
2. Cut up with them the four peppers, throwing away the seeds
3. Put these in a strong brine for an hour, then drain and put on to cook till tender in fresh, hot water
4. In another kettle put the cauliflowers, broken into small pieces
5. In another kettle put the peeled onions
6. When all are well done drain
7. Put in the vinegar in a large kettle
8. Take the mustard, flour and turmeric, and stir into a smooth paste, with a little of the vinegar
9. Stir into the rest of the vinegar and bring it to a boil
10. Put all the ingredients into this liquid
11. Cook slowly an hour or more, stirring often
12. It should be thick and of an even consistency

title: Yellow Sandwiches
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of eggs
- annatto
- pepper
- salt
- thin buttered bread
- fringed yellow tissue paper mat

## Directions

1. Pound yolks of eggs well
2. Color deeply with a trifle of annatto
3. Season with pepper and salt
4. Spread on thin buttered bread
5. Serve on plate covered with a fringed yellow tissue paper mat

title: Yellow Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla or 1/8 teaspoon vanilla and 1 tablespoon brandy

## Directions

1. beat eggs until very light
2. add sugar gradually and continue beating

title: Yorkshire Parkin
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flour
- 1 pound Scotch oatmeal
- 2 pounds syrup
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 pound butter
- 1 ounce ginger
- 1 teaspoon carbonate soda
- a little milk if required
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Bake in a buttered tin and cut into squares

title: Yorkshire Pork-Pie
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- lean pork
- butter
- paste
- minced onion
- hard-boiled eggs
- pepper
- salt
- powdered sage
- flour
- puff-paste
- white of an egg

## Directions

1. Chop lean pork somewhat coarsely; butter a pudding-dish and line with a good paste.
2. Put in the pork interspersed with minced onion and hard-boiled eggs, cut into bits.
3. Sprinkle with pepper, salt, and powdered sage.
4. Now and then dust with flour and drop in a bit of butter.
5. When all the meat is in, dredge with flour and stick small pieces of butter quite thickly all over it.
6. Cover with puff-paste, cut a slit in the middle of the crust.
7. Bake half an hour for each pound of meat.
8. When it begins to brown, wash the crust with the white of an egg. It will give a fine gloss to it as well as preserve the juices.

title: Yorkshire Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- four eggs, white and yolks beaten separately
- one teaspoonful of salt
- two teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- two cups of flour
- drippings from the dripping-pan

## Directions

1. Sift baking powder through flour
2. Mix milk, eggs, salt, and flour mixture very smooth, about the consistency of cream
3. Regulate your time when you put in your roast, so that it will be done half an hour or forty minutes before dishing up
4. Take it from the oven, set it where it will keep hot
5. Take two common biscuit tins, dip some of the drippings from the dripping-pan into these tins
6. Pour half of the pudding into each
7. Set them into the hot oven, and keep them in until the dinner is dished up
8. Take these puddings out at the last moment and send to the table hot

title: Yorkshire Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- Pint new milk
- Two eggs
- Five tablespoons flour
- Pinch of salt
- Lots of melted dripping

## Directions

1. Stir milk and flour to batter, put in salt, add well-beaten eggs.
2. Have a shallow tin pan with lots of melted dripping boiling hot.
3. Pour in batter, bake half an hour in hot oven.
4. English cooks set the cooked pudding under the roast and allow it to catch some of the dripping just before serving.

title: Yorkshire Pudding
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 pt. of milk
- 4 tablespoonfuls flour sifted twice
- 1 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Put flour and salt into a bowl, break eggs into it.
2. Mix well, and add milk by degrees, beating till well mixed.
3. Cook twenty to thirty minutes in heat of cake oven.

title: Yorkshire Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint milk
- 2 eggs
- 6 ounces flour
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Beat eggs
2. add little milk, all flour and salt, then rest of milk
3. Bake in greased pan and serve with roast beef

title: Yorkshire Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 pint milk
- 6 ounces flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- beef dripping

## Directions

1. Beat eggs light
2. add part of milk, all flour and salt, then the rest of the milk
3. When ready to cook take plenty of the beef dripping in which the meat was roasted, place in another pan
4. When boiling hot add the pudding and cook in hot oven
5. When a nice brown remove, and after it cools a minute cut in squares and serve

title: Yorkshire Pudding with Roast Beef and Browned Gravy
body:
## Ingredients

- fat (suet or butter)
- beef
- 2 eggs
- 1 pint of milk
- pinch of salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- flour
- water

## Directions

1. Place in pan enough fat (suet or butter) to cook the meat without adding water
2. Allow 1 hour to every 4 lbs. of beef
3. When sufficiently cooked, remove from pan
4. Pour into the hot grease a batter made of 2 eggs, 1 pint of milk, a pinch of salt, 2 teaspoons baking powder and flour to make a thin batter
5. Return to oven and bake 20 minutes
6. Remove from pan immediately it is done and serve with the gravy, which has been made of the dripping by adding flour and water

title: Young Boiled Beets
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young beets
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. After washing them, boil three-quarters of an hour, scrape, slice, and pour over them a tablespoonful of butter, two of vinegar, and a little pepper and salt.

title: Young Carrots à la Parisienne
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young carrots
- butter
- parsley
- white sugar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil for five minutes
2. Take up and rub off the skins with a coarse cloth
3. Return to the fire and cook until tender
4. Slice lengthwise, making three pieces of a medium-sized carrot, two of a small
5. Have hot in a frying-pan a tablespoonful of butter for each cupful of the carrots
6. When it bubbles lay in the slices
7. Sauté on both sides, quickly
8. Just before taking them up sprinkle with chopped parsley
9. Dish dry
10. Stew over them a little white sugar, pepper, and salt
11. Serve very hot

title: Young Turnips
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young turnips
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter.
2. Cook half an hour, or until tender, but not broken, in boiling, salted water.
3. Drain, still without breaking, and dish.
4. Sprinkle with pepper and salt, then butter plentifully and serve.

title: Young Turnips (Fried)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- pepper
- salt
- flour
- cottolene
- beaten egg
- cracker-dust

## Directions

1. Pare and slice crosswise a quarter of an inch thick.
2. Lay in ice-cold water half an hour, then cook tender, but not too soft, in boiling water without salt.
3. Drain, lay upon a soft cloth until dry and lukewarm, sprinkle with pepper and salt, flour, and fry in hot cottolene; or, dip in beaten egg, then in cracker-dust, and fry.

title: Young Turnips (Stewed)
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- young turnips
- salt
- 1 cupful milk
- soda
- 1 tablespoonful butter
- flour
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter, or slice.
2. Boil fifteen minutes in hot, salted water, drain and cover with a cupful of milk that has been heated in a separate vessel with a tiny bit of soda.
3. When they simmer again stir in a tablespoonful of butter rolled in half as much flour, pepper and salt to taste, and stew gently fifteen minutes more.
4. Serve in a deep, covered dish, and very hot.

title: Yum Yum
author: Grace E. Denison
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. brown sugar
- Butter size of egg
- Water to moisten

## Directions

1. Cook until it becomes brittle when dropped into cold water
2. Then pull until creamy white
